docs.title: IMML
docs.text:
IMML is a declarative language for Bittensor incentive-mechanism design, description, and generation — a QML-style surface where you compose a mechanism from typed primitives, and the one per-subnet thing (the scoring metric) is an explicit hole.

It is derived inductively from a corpus of 189 real Bittensor subnets: every one round-trips through the language losslessly, and the language compiles to a runnable validator scaffold.

  • :material-book-open-variant: Guides

    What IMML is, how to author a mechanism, and how mechanisms were reverse-engineered from real subnets.

  • :material-code-braces: Language reference

    The grammar, the ~50 reusable primitives, the four combinators, and the metric type system.

  • :material-view-gallery: Examples

    All 189 corpus subnets, each lifted to IMML — filter by archetype, language, or burn status.

  • :material-tools: Toolchain

    lift · compile · validate · coverage · canonicalize · generate.

docs.title: The loop
docs.text:
Extract ─▶ Lift ─▶ IMML (.imml) ─▶ Compile ─▶ IR (YAML) ─▶ Generate ─▶ validator.py
 (faithful)         (declarative)              (= the schema)          (runnable scaffold)

docs.title: At a glance
docs.text: Corpus coverage 189 / 189 subnets, all schema-valid Round-trip fidelity 100% (structural signature) Structurally expressible 95.8% — the rest are explicit extern holes Reusable primitives ~50 (9 aggregators, 5 smoothers, 16 guards, 9 ground-truth sources, …) Combinators 4 (pipeline · multiplex · gate/product · overlay)

docs.title: A mechanism in IMML
docs.text:
Mechanism {
    id: pairwiseArena

    netuid: 42
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: model_weights

    @burn {
        uid: 0
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Pipeline {
        score: Metric {
            kind: win_rate
            family: classification_quality
            specific: pairwise_win_rate
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: LlmJudgment {
            trust_model: adversarial
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "360 blocks"
        }
    }
}

docs.title: 404-GEN Competition System
docs.text:
Subnet 404-Repo__404-gen-subnet netuid 17 Archetype gated Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners submit open-source AI 3D-generation solutions (a GitHub repo + commit + Docker image producing Three.js modules). When a round opens, miners use a published seed and prompts to generate 3D models and upload them to their CDNs. Validators render the outputs to multi-view PNGs and run VLM-based DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: LlmJudgment, DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: ProofOfWork, CodeInspection, LivenessCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: vlm_judgment"]
  M1["Metric: vlm_judgment"]
  M2["Metric: vlm_judgment"]
  M3["Metric: vlm_judgment"]
  M4["Metric: vlm_judgment"]
  M5["Metric: win_rate"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  G --> M5
  GT --> M5
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  M5 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: m404GenCompetitionSystem

    netuid: 17
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [source_code, content_link, compute_service]

    @burn {
        uid: "199"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        ProofOfWork {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, cumulative_score }

    Gated {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: vlm_judgment
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: vlm_judgment
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: vlm_judgment
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: vlm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: vlm_judgment
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: win_rate
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: oracle
            },
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: gated
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_round
            tempo: "set_weights_interval_sec (configurable); inner loop 60s"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: single
            count: 1
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Winner-stays-in competition. On-chain weights go almost entirely to a single leader
  hotkey; the residual (1.0 - leader_weight) goes to the subnet owner UID. When a leader defends, its
  weight decays toward a floor each round, so the owner share grows over time — i.e. an increasing, dynamic
  burn to the owner UID rather than a fixed split.
subnet:
  name: 404-GEN Competition System
  owner_repo: 404-Repo__404-gen-subnet
  netuid: 17
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners submit open-source AI 3D-generation solutions (a GitHub repo + commit + Docker image
    producing Three.js modules). When a round opens, miners use a published seed and prompts to generate
    3D models and upload them to their CDNs. Validators render the outputs to multi-view PNGs and run
    VLM-based pairwise duels; the round winner becomes/defends the global leader. The single leader receives
    nearly all on-chain weight under a winner-stays-in format with a proof-of-work (audit) model.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
  - content_link
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: duel_prompt_match_penalty
  metric_kind: vlm_judgment
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Stage 1 of the multi-stage duel judge. For 4 near-front angles, a VLM (GLM-4.6V-Flash) assigns
    each of the two models a 0-10 penalty (0 = perfect match to reference, 10 = completely wrong), evaluated
    both AB and BA. A weighted, split-aware aggregator either declares a per-duel winner or routes to
    later stages.
- name: duel_best_view_penalty
  metric_kind: vlm_judgment
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Stage 2A. DINOv3 embeddings pick each side's highest-similarity rendered view vs the prompt;
    the VLM compares those two best views (AB+BA) producing 0-10 penalties.
- name: duel_artifact_penalty
  metric_kind: vlm_judgment
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Stage 2B. VLM rates rendering quality on a 2x2 grid render per side (bounding box, color/material
    accuracy, surface quality, geometric detail) as a 0-10 penalty.
- name: duel_checklist_score
  metric_kind: vlm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Stage 2C. VLM decomposes the prompt into a 4-7 item feature checklist, then verifies each model's
    grid; per-item yes/partial/no scores (2/1/0) plus a +3 main-object bonus.
- name: side_guard_garbage_votes
  metric_kind: vlm_judgment
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Stage 4 anti-gaming guard. 4 side angles per side classified OK/GARBAGE; K-of-4 garbage votes
    flag a side and can demote a winning-but-flagged side to a draw.
- name: match_margin
  metric_kind: win_rate
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Per match (a set of per-prompt duels), margin = score / number_of_duels. A challenger must reach
    win_margin against the leader/timeline winner to qualify and advance.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: Vision-language model (GLM-4.6V-Flash served via operator vLLM) performs pairwise duels
    comparing two rendered 3D models against a reference image across multiple gated stages.
  source_identifier: glm-4.6v-flash (judge-service/judge_service/judges/multi_stage.py MODEL)
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: DINOv3 embeddings (image-distance-service) provide best-view selection and verify that regenerated
    outputs match submissions.
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Published per-round random seed plus selected prompts drive deterministic generation; winners
    are audited by regenerating their outputs with the miner's own Docker image on serverless GPUs (proof-of-work
    verification).
  source_identifier: rounds/{n}/seed.json, prompts.txt; generation-orchestrator regeneration
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Verification regenerates 128 prompts (4 batches of 32) with the same seed; failure discards the
    candidate timeline.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: gated
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '199'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: 'WeightsService._calculate_weights: if no leader (or leader is the owner) the owner UID gets
      weight 1.0; otherwise owner_weight = 1.0 - leader_weight and weights are set to [owner_uid, leader_uid]
      = [owner_weight, leader_weight]. The owner share is the dynamic residual and grows as a defending
      leader''s weight decays toward weight_floor.'
  notes: Round winner is decided by the judge-service via pairwise duels; round-manager updates leader.json.
    New leader enters at weight 1.0; a defending leader's weight decays by config.weight_decay each round
    down to config.weight_floor (finalize_round.py _compute_leader_transition). Only one non-owner UID
    receives weight at a time.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_round
  method: A standalone weights-setter loop polls the competition git repo's leader.json, resolves the
    active leader hotkey to a metagraph UID at the current block, and sets a two-UID weight vector [owner_uid,
    leader_uid]. Leader weight is whatever leader.json records for the active transition (1.0 for a fresh
    winner, decayed otherwise).
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: set_weights_interval_sec (configurable); inner loop 60s
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA in the weight setter. set_weights is called with wait_for_inclusion=False and wait_for_finalization=False;
      3 confirmation blocks are polled only to verify the update landed. Temporal smoothing of leader
      weight is via the per-round weight_decay, not an EMA.
anti_gaming:
- kind: proof_of_work
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Candidate winners are audited by regenerating their outputs with the miner's own Docker
    image on serverless GPUs (4xH200) using the round seed; regenerated outputs are compared to submissions.
    If verification fails the timeline is discarded and alternatives evaluated.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Each returned JavaScript module is statically analyzed and executed in a sandbox before
    rendering during verification.
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Stage 4 side guard: per-angle OK/GARBAGE VLM classification (K-of-4 garbage threshold,
    S4_K_THRESHOLD=3) flags sides whose non-front views are unmodeled/replaced; a flagged winning side
    is stepped down to a draw.'
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: '_find_new_leader: if a round winner''s repo and commit match the current leader''s, no
    transition occurs (the same submission cannot re-take leadership).'
sub_competitions:
  structure: single
  stages:
  - name: qualification
    role: qualifier
    advancement_rule: Every miner duels the current base leader; a miner qualifies if match.margin >=
      win_margin.
  - name: timeline
    role: main
    advancement_rule: Qualified miners are run as a timeline of pairwise matches; a challenger beating
      the current timeline winner by win_margin becomes the new local leader and is sent to audit.
  count: 1
  notes: The repo also ships Competitions/competition0..2.md describing successive competition definitions,
    but the on-chain mechanism is a single winner-stays-in ladder per round.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - cumulative_score
  status_values:
  - leader
  - qualified
  - rejected
  - local_leader
  reset_policy: Leader state (leader.json) is append-only across rounds; per-round match matrices, winner,
    and audit/rejection sets are recomputed each round.
  notes: leader.json is an append-only transition log (hotkey, repo, commit, docker, weight, effective_block).
    Per-round, rejected_hotkeys and qualified lists track miner status.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - Competitions/competition0.md
  - shared/subnet_common/competition/config.py
  - weights-setter/weights_service.py
  completeness: high
provenance:
  confidence_overall: high
  extracted_by: im-extract (Opus 4.8)
  extractor_notes: 'Production numeric thresholds (win_margin, weight_decay, weight_floor, round timing)
    are NOT in the source tree: they live in an external competition git repo''s config.json (require_competition_config
    reads config.json from a GitHub ref). The values seen in round-manager/tests/test_finalize_round.py
    (win_margin=0.05, weight_decay=0.2, weight_floor=0.5) are test fixtures only and are therefore left
    null, not adopted.'
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: weights-setter/settings.py
    line_ref: '9'
    quote: 'netuid: int = Field(default=17, description="Netuid of the Subtensor network")'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 1-3
    quote: '# 404-GEN Competition System ... running on Bittensor Subnet 17.'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 12-17
    quote: 'The competition follows a winner-stays-in format with a proof-of-work model: Miners generate
      ... Validators collect ... VLM judges ... Validators audit.'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: weights-setter/weights_service.py
    line_ref: 211-220
    quote: return WeightsResult(uids=[self._subnet_owner_uid, leader_uid], weights=[owner_weight, leader_weight])
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/dynamic
    source_path: weights-setter/weights_service.py
    line_ref: '215'
    quote: owner_weight = 1.0 - leader_weight
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: weights-setter/settings.py
    line_ref: '32'
    quote: 'subnet_owner_uid: int = Field(default=199, ge=0, description="UID of the network owner")'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: round-manager/round_manager/finalize_round.py
    line_ref: 229-241
    quote: 'decayed_weight = round(max(config.weight_floor, current_leader.weight - config.weight_decay),
      10) ... return current_leader.model_copy(update={"weight": decayed_weight ...'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: round-manager/round_manager/finalize_round.py
    line_ref: 260-266
    quote: return LeaderEntry(hotkey=round_result.winner_hotkey, ... weight=1.0, effective_block=effective_block)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: weights-setter/weights_service.py
    line_ref: 155-162
    quote: res, error_msg = await subtensor.set_weights(wallet=self._wallet, netuid=self._netuid, weights=weights.weights,
      uids=weights.uids, wait_for_inclusion=False, wait_for_finalization=False)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0
    source_path: judge-service/judge_service/judges/multi_stage.py
    line_ref: 7-10
    quote: Stage 1 - Prompt match (always runs). 4 front-hemisphere angles, each evaluated AB and BA =
      8 VLM calls. A weighted, split-aware aggregator either declares a winner or routes to Stage 2.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1
    source_path: judge-service/judge_service/judges/multi_stage.py
    line_ref: 13-16
    quote: 'Best view: DINOv3 picks the highest-similarity rendered view per side; the VLM compares those
      two views (AB+BA = 2 calls).'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/3
    source_path: judge-service/judge_service/judges/multi_stage.py
    line_ref: 971-973
    quote: base = sum(S2_CHECKLIST_MATCH_SCORES.get(c.match, 0) for c in result.checks) return base +
      (S2_CHECKLIST_MAIN_BONUS if result.main_object_correct else 0)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/5

docs.title: 404-GEN (three-gen-subnet)
docs.text:
Subnet 404-Repo__three-gen-subnet netuid 17 Archetype gated Language python Mechanism status full_burn Task Miners generate 3D assets (3D Gaussian Splatting / .spz files) from a text prompt or a prompt image. The validator renders the submitted asset to images and scores them on image quality (DINOv2 classifier), prompt-image alignment (CLIP), and inter-view similarity (SSIM/LPIPS), combining them into a DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: ReferenceModel, OrganicTraffic"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: RateLimit, DeterministicCheck, LivenessCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: vlm_judgment"]
  M1["Metric: similarity"]
  M2["Metric: similarity"]
  M3["Metric: similarity"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: m404GenThreeGenSubnet

    netuid: 17
    lang: python
    status: full_burn
    submission: content_link

    @burn {
        uid: "199"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @guards {
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, reputation, cooldown, cumulative_score }

    Gated {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: vlm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            ReferenceModel {
                trust_model: oracle
            },
            OrganicTraffic {
                trust_model: unknown
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: gated
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.05
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "100 blocks (weight_set_interval)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: full_burn
mechanism_status_notes: The validator's run loop (validator.py `run`) calls `self._burn_all()` on every
  metagraph sync, which sets weight 1.0 to a single hard-coded UID (199). The fully-documented scoring/weight
  distribution in `_set_weights()` (rank sigmoid over per-miner glicko*observation rewards) exists in
  the code but is NOT invoked anywhere in the active loop. So the live mechanism is a full burn to UID
  199 while the documented scoring pipeline is dormant.
subnet:
  name: 404-GEN (three-gen-subnet)
  owner_repo: 404-Repo__three-gen-subnet
  netuid: 17
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners generate 3D assets (3D Gaussian Splatting / .spz files) from a text prompt or a prompt
    image. The validator renders the submitted asset to images and scores them on image quality (DINOv2
    classifier), prompt-image alignment (CLIP), and inter-view similarity (SSIM/LPIPS), combining them
    into a fidelity score; only assets above a quality threshold are accepted.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  submission_format_other: ''
scoring_signals:
- name: image_quality_assessment
  metric_kind: vlm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.75
  normalization: none
  notes: DINOv2-small based quality classifier (`QualityClassifierModel`), raw sigmoid output averaged
    over rendered views. Weighted 0.75 in `_compute_final_score`.
- name: prompt_alignment
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.2
  normalization: none
  notes: CLIP text-vs-image (or image-vs-image) cosine alignment of rendered views to the prompt. Weighted
    0.2 in final score; acts as a gate (final_score forced to 0.0 if alignment < 0.3).
- name: ssim_similarity
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.025
  normalization: none
  notes: Structural similarity (SSIM) across rendered views, passed through sigmoid(slope=35, shift=0.83),
    weighted 0.025 in final score.
- name: lpips_similarity
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.025
  normalization: none
  notes: 1 - LPIPS perceptual distance across views, multiplied by sigmoid(slope=30, shift=0.7) of itself,
    weighted 0.025 in final score.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: reference_model
  description: No labelled ground truth; correctness is judged by pretrained reference models (DINOv2
    quality classifier from HF repo 404-Gen/validation, CLIP alignment model) plus SSIM/LPIPS metrics.
  source_identifier: 404-Gen/validation (quality_scorer.safetensors); open_clip CLIP; torchmetrics SSIM/LPIPS
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Prompts come from synthetic datasets and organic public-API traffic.
- kind: organic_traffic
  description: Prompts sourced from organic traffic via public API in addition to synthetic prompt datasets.
  source_identifier: validator public API / prompt collector
  trust_model: unknown
  notes: ''
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: gated
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '199'
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: '`_burn_all` sets uids=[199], weights=[1.0]. All emission is directed to a single UID; the
      documented score-based distribution (`_set_weights`) is not called.'
  notes: 'Documented (dormant) per-asset aggregation: final_score = 0.75*iqa + 0.2*alignment + 0.025*sigmoid(ssim,35,0.83)
    + 0.025*lpips*sigmoid(lpips,30,0.7), gated to 0 if alignment < 0.3. Per-miner reward (dormant) = number
    of in-window observations * glicko rating; weights then distributed by a rank sigmoid (strip_sigmoid)
    over qualifying miners.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: 'Live: `_burn_all` calls subtensor.set_weights(uids=[199], weights=[1.0]) once per metagraph
    sync, rate-limited by weight_set_interval blocks. Documented/dormant `_set_weights` would build rewards
    from per-miner fidelity-EMA * glicko rating, mask rewards > 5.0, apply a rank sigmoid and normalize
    to sum-to-one before emit.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (weight_set_interval)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.05
    window: null
    notes: 'Per-miner fidelity_score is an EMA: fidelity = (1-alpha)*prev + alpha*new, with moving_average_alpha
      default 0.05. Feeds the dormant reward calculation, not the live burn.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: Per-miner task cooldown (default 300s) and throttle period; repeated cooldown violations
    beyond a threshold add extra cooldown penalty (cooldown_violation_penalty).
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: Submissions below quality_threshold (default 0.6) are rejected via _process_task_failure
    and incur a cooldown_penalty (default 600s); empty results rejected.
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: rejection
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: Miner must sign each submission (license-consent declaration + submit_time + prompt + hotkeys);
    invalid signatures are rejected. Resubmissions arriving too quickly are dropped to prevent double
    rewards.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: A duels/glicko rating subsystem exists (DuelRatings, glicko2) feeding the dormant reward, but
    duels are disabled by default (--duels.disabled default True).
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - reputation
  - cooldown
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Per-miner observations expire after a 4h window; fidelity EMA resets to 1.0 and cooldowns
    clear when a new hotkey replaces the previous one at a UID (reset_data).
  notes: MinerData tracks fidelity_score (EMA), a 4h deque of task-finish observations, cooldown_until,
    cooldown_violations. Glicko2 ratings (rating/rd/vol) tracked per UID in DuelRatings.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/running_validator.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / Claude Opus 4.8
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: neurons/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 459-463
    quote: 'if self.metagraph_sync.should_sync(): ... self.metagraph_sync.sync(self.miners, self.ratings)
      self._burn_all()'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: neurons/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 489-496
    quote: result, msg = self.subtensor.set_weights( wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.config.netuid, uids=[199,],
      weights=[1.0], ...)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 3-3
    quote: '# 404—GEN | SUBNET 17'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: validation/engine/validation_engine.py
    line_ref: 71-106
    quote: 'def validate_image_to_gs(...) / def validate_text_to_gs(...): Function that validates the
      input 3D data generated using provided prompt'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: validation/engine/validation_engine.py
    line_ref: 54-59
    quote: 0.75 * validation_results.iqa + 0.2 * validation_results.alignment + 0.025 * sigmoid(torch.tensor(validation_results.ssim),
      35, 0.83) + 0.025 * validation_results.lpips * sigmoid(torch.tensor(validation_results.lpips), 30,
      0.7)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: validation/engine/validation_engine.py
    line_ref: 51-52
    quote: 'if validation_results.alignment < 0.3: final_score = 0.0'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind
    source_path: validation/engine/models/quality_model.py
    line_ref: 16-18
    quote: A quality classifier model that uses DinoNet for image quality assessment.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: validation/engine/metrics/quality_scorer.py
    line_ref: 20-20
    quote: 'self, repo_id: str = "404-Gen/validation", quality_scorer_model: str = "quality_scorer.safetensors"'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/alpha
    source_path: neurons/validator/config.py
    line_ref: 127-132
    quote: --validation.moving_average_alpha ... help="Alpha parameter for fidelity factor update." default=0.05
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator/miner_data.py
    line_ref: 73-74
    quote: self.fidelity_score = prev_fidelity_score * (1 - moving_average_alpha) + moving_average_alpha
      * fidelity_score

  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator/miner_data.py
    line_ref: 105-111
    quote: Calculate the reward for the miner. It is calculated by multiplying fidelity score by the number

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: neurons/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 520-535
    quote: 'def strip_sigmoid(x, n): return np.array(0.003 + 0.005 / (1 + np.exp(-0.06 * (x - n / 2)))
      ...) reward_mask = rewards > 5.0 ... processed_weights = final_probs / np.sum(final_probs)'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval

  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description

  unresolved:

  extractor_notes: Implementation language is Python. The dominant live behaviour is a full burn to UID

docs.title: Affine
docs.text:
Subnet AffineFoundation__affine netuid 120 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners submit reasoning/LLM models (committed on-chain, served via Subnet 64 / Chutes) that are evaluated on a set of RL environments. Validators look for the model that Pareto-dominates the frontier across all environments. A single "champion" model holds the crown and receives all weight; challeng DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: PlagiarismDetection, Deduplication, ChallengePeriod"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: reward"]
  M1["Metric: win_rate"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: affine

    netuid: 120
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: [model_weights, commitment_hash]

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        PlagiarismDetection {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        ChallengePeriod {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, performance_ledger, cumulative_score, registration_age }

    Multiplex<tournament> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: reward
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: win_rate
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: gated
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "180 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: tournament
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: 'Scoring is winner-take-all: the champion miner is assigned weight 1.0 and every
  other miner 0.0 (champion_challenge._assign_weights). On chain, the WeightSetter applies a dynamic validator_burn_percentage
  (fetched per-iteration from the backend API, default 0.0 if config fetch fails) plus the accumulated
  weight of "system miners" (uid <= 0 or uid > 1000), all redirected to UID 0. So a variable fraction
  of emission is burned/redirected to UID 0 while the rest concentrates on a single champion. The exact
  live burn fraction is API-driven and not a source constant.'
subnet:
  name: Affine
  owner_repo: AffineFoundation__affine
  netuid: 120
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners submit reasoning/LLM models (committed on-chain, served via Subnet 64 / Chutes) that
    are evaluated on a set of RL environments. Validators look for the model that Pareto-dominates the
    frontier across all environments. A single "champion" model holds the crown and receives all weight;
    challengers must dominate the champion on common tasks to dethrone it (winner-take-all).
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: env_score
  metric_kind: reward
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  normalization_other: ''
  notes: Per-environment average score over completed tasks (MinerData.env_scores). Raw scores are normalized
    to [0,1] via per-environment (min,max) ranges in ScorerConfig.ENV_SCORE_RANGES before comparison.
    Signals are NOT additively weighted; they feed a Pareto dominance comparison on common tasks, so weight
    is null.
- name: pareto_dominance
  metric_kind: win_rate
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Per pair, a challenger "wins" an env if its avg score on common tasks exceeds the incumbent by
    a margin; dominance requires winning all envs (strict Pareto, WIN_MIN_DOMINANT_ENVS=1 with not-worse
    tolerance in others). Gated/comparative, not additively weighted.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Models are evaluated against RL environments (e.g. coding / program abduction tasks such
    as DED-V2, ABD-V2, agentgym:sciworld) packaged as pre-built Docker images. Scores per task are collected
    as samples.
  source_identifier: RL environments (DED-V2, ABD-V2, agentgym:sciworld)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Environments enumerated dynamically; only agentgym:sciworld has an explicit score range in source
    (ENV_SCORE_RANGES). README references program abduction and coding tasks.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: gated
  normalization: none
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: 'WeightSetter.process_weights redirects to UID 0: (a) burn_percentage of all weight (validator_burn_percentage
      fetched from API per iteration; default 0.0) and (b) the accumulated weight of system miners (uid
      <= 0 or uid > 1000). Fraction is dynamic/API-driven, not a source constant.'
  notes: 'champion_challenge._assign_weights: champion (weight_uid) gets 1.0, all others 0.0. The Scorer
    docstring states "The champion gets 100% weight. All other miners get 0%." Champion is resolved/dethroned
    via Pareto comparison on common tasks; dethrone needs CHAMPION_DETHRONE_MIN_CHECKPOINT(=10) reached
    and the most recent comparison a win.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Validator service waits for a weight submission window every WEIGHT_SET_INTERVAL_BLOCKS (default
    180) blocks, fetches the latest weights and validator_burn_percentage from the backend API, then calls
    subtensor.set_weights with up to 3 retries. Off-chain champion-challenge scoring (separate scorer
    service) produces the weights the API serves.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 180 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of the final reward in source. Weights are set directly (champion=1.0).
      Temporal stability comes from champion persistence/dethrone gating, not an EMA on the on-chain weight
      vector.
anti_gaming:
- kind: plagiarism_detection
  enforcement: penalty
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: 'AntiCopyDetector flags model copies via two independent cosine signals (hidden_states
    cosine and logprob top-3 cosine). Defaults: suspicious thresholds 0.99, cheat thresholds 0.995, min_tasks
    30. A ''suspicious''/''cheat'' status against the alleged source tightens the Pareto margin by PARETO_SUSPICIOUS_MARGIN_MULTIPLIER
    (1.5) in pairwise elimination, making the copy easier to terminate.'
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: elimination
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: 'Pairwise Pareto filter: among non-champion miners with >= PARETO_MIN_WINDOWS(5) x window_size
    common tasks, the earlier-registered (incumbent) miner terminates a strictly Pareto-dominated later
    miner (likely copy). Termination is permanent.'
- kind: challenge_period
  enforcement: elimination
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: Champion challenges are checkpoint-gated. First CHAMPION_WARMUP_CHECKPOINTS(2) checkpoints
    don't count. A challenger is terminated after CHAMPION_TERMINATION_TOTAL_LOSSES(4) total or CHAMPION_TERMINATION_CONSECUTIVE_LOSSES(3)
    consecutive post-warmup losses, or immediately on a loss at/after dethrone CP(10).
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: System miners (uid <= 0 or uid > 1000) participate in scoring but their weight is redirected
    to UID 0 rather than paid out, so they cannot earn emission.
sub_competitions:
  structure: tournament
  stages:
  - name: pairwise_pareto_filter
    role: qualifier
    advancement_rule: Non-champion miners compared pairwise; strictly Pareto-dominated (and copy-flagged)
      miners are permanently terminated. Survivors proceed to champion challenges.
  - name: champion_challenge
    role: boss
    advancement_rule: Each surviving challenger is compared against the champion on common tasks at each
      new checkpoint. Reaching CHAMPION_DETHRONE_MIN_CHECKPOINT(10) and winning the most recent comparison
      qualifies a challenger to dethrone; earliest-registered qualifier (geo-mean tiebreaker) becomes
      the new champion and all state resets.
  count: null
  notes: 'Winner-take-all single-champion ladder: one reigning champion holds 100% weight; challengers
    must out-dominate it to take the crown.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - performance_ledger
  - cumulative_score
  - registration_age
  status_values:
  - sampling
  - terminated
  - champion
  reset_policy: On a successful dethrone (champion change), all non-terminated miners' challenge counters
    (wins/losses/checkpoints) reset to sampling state. Terminated miners are never revived (permanent).
  notes: 'Per-miner persisted challenge state: challenge_consecutive_wins, total_wins, total_losses, consecutive_losses,
    checkpoints_passed, status, termination_reason. Identity is hotkey+revision. first_block (registration
    age) breaks ties and sets pairwise incumbency. Champion identity persisted in system_config with since_block.'

docs.title: AlphaCore
docs.text:
Subnet AlphaCoreBittensor__alphacore netuid None Archetype gated Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners are autonomous DevOps agents that interpret a task prompt (today: Terraform/GCP infrastructure-as-code tasks), execute automation in their own environment, and submit a workspace ZIP artifact (base64-encoded). Validators score the submission by running it in a Firecracker sandbox Validation A DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: Other"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, LivenessCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: latency"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: alphaCore

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: content_link

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: 0.9
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Gated {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: Other {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: gated
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "tempo (round_manager.tempo) blocks per epoch"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Default config burns 90% of emission to BURN_UID (BURN_AMOUNT_PERCENTAGE=0.9)
  every epoch, splitting the remaining 10% among the top-k (k=1) positive-scoring miners. If no miner
  has a positive score the validator emits a burn-only allocation (100% weight to BURN_UID). Scoring via
  the external Validation API is implemented and active; emission is heavily burn-weighted.
subnet:
  name: AlphaCore
  owner_repo: AlphaCoreBittensor__alphacore
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners are autonomous DevOps agents that interpret a task prompt (today: Terraform/GCP infrastructure-as-code
    tasks), execute automation in their own environment, and submit a workspace ZIP artifact (base64-encoded).
    Validators score the submission by running it in a Firecracker sandbox Validation API which checks
    machine-checkable "invariants" against outputs (e.g. terraform.tfstate), returning a score in [0,1].
    Final per-miner score optionally blends the API score with a relative latency score.'
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  submission_format_other: ''
scoring_signals:
- name: api_invariant_score
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.8
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Score in [0,1] returned by the external Validation API after checking task invariants in a Firecracker
    sandbox. Per-miner api_avg = sum of task scores / task count. Combined weight is API_SCORE_WEIGHT=0.8
    (normalized against latency weight). Gating: if api_avg <= 0, the final combined score is forced to
    0 regardless of latency (fail-closed).'
- name: latency_score
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: min_max
  notes: 'Relative latency score in [0,1]. LATENCY_SCORE_WEIGHT=0.1. Default mode is winner_take_all:
    among miners tied at the highest API score, the single fastest gets latency_score 1.0 and all others
    0.0; an alternate "relative" mode uses score=(1-normalized_delta)**gamma with LATENCY_SCORE_GAMMA=1.0.
    Latency scoring is enabled only when LATENCY_SCORING_ENABLED is true (default true in testing profile,
    false in production).'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: other
  kind_other: sandbox_invariant_checker
  description: Firecracker-backed sandbox Validation API that processes untrusted miner ZIP submissions
    and verifies task "invariants" (machine-checkable requirements) against outputs such as terraform.tfstate,
    returning a score 0..1.
  source_identifier: VALIDATION_API_ENDPOINT (default http://127.0.0.1:8888)
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: No exact enum fit; used kind=other (sandbox_invariant_checker). The sandbox is validator-side
    and trusted; miner inputs are treated as adversarial/untrusted.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: gated
  normalization: l1
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 0.9
    dynamic: false
    notes: BURN_AMOUNT_PERCENTAGE default 0.9 (90%) sent to BURN_UID (default UID 0) every epoch via _emit_top_k_weights;
      remaining 0.1 split among top-k (k=1) positive-scoring uids (per_uid = (1 - burn_pct)/len(top_uids)).
      When total score <= 0, _emit_burn_only_weights assigns weight 1.0 entirely to BURN_UID.
  notes: Per-round final score blends api (0.8) and latency (0.1) weights, normalized to sum to 1 (api_w/=total_w,
    lat_w/=total_w). Settlement updates EMA scores; emission selects top-k by EMA score and applies the
    burn split. raw_weights = scores / L1-norm before on-chain processing.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: 'Custom epoch-aware emitter (_maybe_emit_weights): once per epoch, inside the weights window
    (block_in_epoch >= WEIGHTS_EMIT_BLOCK_OFFSET, default 0) and after at least WEIGHTS_MIN_TASKS_BEFORE_EMIT
    (default 10) tasks. If sum(scores) <= 0 -> burn-only (100% to BURN_UID); else top-k (k=1) split: burn_pct
    to BURN_UID, (1-burn_pct) to top positive scorer.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: tempo (round_manager.tempo) blocks per epoch
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: 'update_scores applies EMA: self.scores = alpha*scattered_rewards + (1-alpha)*self.scores with
      alpha = config.neuron.moving_average_alpha (default 0.1). Settlement clips per-round scores to [0,1]
      and excludes BURN_UID and non-positive scores before the EMA update.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Submissions are validated in a locked-down Firecracker microVM sandbox with strict egress
    controls (net_checks=False on the validator path); the validator never trusts miner-supplied task.json
    and uses its own remembered per-round invariants. If invariants are missing the score is 0; if api
    score is 0 the final combined score is forced to 0 (fail-closed).
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: StartRoundSynapse handshake (HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=90) probes miner liveness; dead
    miners are skipped. MIN_RESPONSE_RATE (default 0.5) and MAX_MINERS_TO_QUERY bound participation.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: self.scores is a per-uid EMA (moving average) vector persisted to state.npz and updated each
    settlement. Burn UID and non-positive scores are excluded from EMA updates.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - VALIDATOR.md
  - VALIDATOR-API.md
  - MINER.md

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight

  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid

  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/notes
    source_path: subnet/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 520-529
    quote: np.savez(self.config.neuron.full_path + "/state.npz", step=self.step, scores=self.scores, hotkeys=self.hotkeys,)
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/notes
    source_path: subnet/validator/settlement/mixin.py
    line_ref: 64-69
    quote: 'if np.isfinite(score) and score > 0.0: if int(uid) != int(BURN_UID): positive_scores[int(uid)]
      = score'
  - claim_field: /documentation/primary_docs
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 9-12
    quote: '[`VALIDATOR-QUICKSTART.md`] ... [`VALIDATOR.md`] ... [`VALIDATOR-API.md`] ... [`MINER.md`]'
  unresolved:
  - 'subnet.netuid: not hard-coded; --netuid default is 1 (placeholder) and ALPHACORE_NETUID env overrides
    it. No canonical netuid in source.'
  - 'ground_truth_sources[0].kind: no exact enum fit (sandbox invariant checker is an oracle-style deterministic
    verifier of machine-checkable invariants). Used kind=other with kind_other=sandbox_invariant_checker;
    trust_model=oracle. Schema stress.'
  - 'Two unused/placeholder reward paths exist: subnet/validator/rewards.py::compute_rewards returns all-zero
    rewards, and subnet/validator/finalization/weights.py::set_validator_weights plus settlement/rewards.py::wta_rewards/apply_burn_mechanism
    are not wired into the live _maybe_emit_weights emission path. The active path is neurons/validator.py
    _emit_top_k_weights / _emit_burn_only_weights.'
  - 'k for top-k weight split: hard-coded default k=1 in _emit_top_k_weights signature; not a config constant.'
composition:
  shape: gated
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: BitSota
docs.text:
Subnet AlveusLabs__SN94-BitSota netuid 94 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners evolve machine-learning algorithms (expressed in a DSL) via genetic programming and submit those that beat the current State-of-the-Art (SOTA) threshold on a fixed CIFAR-10 binary classification evaluation pipeline. Validators re-evaluate submitted algorithms deterministically and, when an al DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, CommitReveal"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, RateLimit, CodeInspection"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: bitSota

    netuid: 94
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: source_code

    @burn {
        uid: "5Ef5EsPQoMVmJ8rYectQ26BEvscvATEGm365bcQjo1Y6bxGr"
        fraction: 0.9
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, reputation }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: Metric {
            kind: accuracy
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            CommitReveal {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: gated
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "alignment_mod: 360 blocks (config); epoch_length: 100"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: 'The shipped capacitorless config burns 90% of weight to a burn hotkey and pays
  10% to the latest accepted SOTA winner (burn_share: 0.9). When no winner exists, 100% goes to burn.
  The code''s hardcoded default reward_mode is "capacitor" (EVM contract voting), but the example/committed
  validator configs select capacitorless sticky_burnsplit. Scoring (CIFAR-10 binary DSL evaluation) is
  real but only gates who, if anyone, receives the 10% winner share.'
subnet:
  name: BitSota
  owner_repo: AlveusLabs__SN94-BitSota
  netuid: 94
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners evolve machine-learning algorithms (expressed in a DSL) via genetic programming and
    submit those that beat the current State-of-the-Art (SOTA) threshold on a fixed CIFAR-10 binary classification
    evaluation pipeline. Validators re-evaluate submitted algorithms deterministically and, when an algorithm
    beats SOTA, direct the winner share of on-chain weight to its miner; the remainder is burned.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: cifar10_binary_median_accuracy
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Validator runs the algorithm over a deterministic suite of task_count tasks and takes the median
    per-task score (np.median(scores)). The algorithm "passes" if score >= sota_threshold. Used as a gate/winner-selection
    signal, not as an additive weight component, so weight is null.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Deterministic CIFAR-10 binary task suite (also MNIST binary and scalar linear regression
    registered) sampled with fixed seeds. task_count defaults to 128, task_seed defaults to 1337, default_task_type
    cifar10_binary, default_input_dim 16.
  source_identifier: core/evaluations.py TASK_REGISTRY; validator_hyperparams.json
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Validators do not trust miner-reported scores; they re-run algorithms with deterministic per-task
    RNG seeds and use their own evaluated scores.
- kind: commit_reveal
  description: Relay service (relay.bitsota.com) finalizes SOTA events via validator votes; sticky/windowed
    weight managers fetch finalized SOTA events (miner_hotkey, start_block, end_block) to identify the
    winner.
  source_identifier: validator/relay_client.py; validator/sota_schedule.py
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: In winner_source 'local' mode the validator uses its own locally-evaluated best miner instead
    of relay-finalized events.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: gated
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: 5Ef5EsPQoMVmJ8rYectQ26BEvscvATEGm365bcQjo1Y6bxGr
    fraction: 0.9
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'burn_share: 0.9 in capacitorless sticky_burnsplit mode; winner_share defaults to 1 - burn_share
      = 0.1. burn_hotkey example 5Ef5EsPQoMVmJ8rYectQ26BEvscvATEGm365bcQjo1Y6bxGr. If no winner exists,
      burn receives 1.0. In the windowed (non-sticky) capacitorless manager the winner receives full weight
      (1.0) during the reward window and burn 1.0 otherwise.'
  notes: 'Sticky burn-split: weights are {burn_hotkey: burn_share, winner_hotkey: winner_share}; weights
    change only when the accepted SOTA winner changes.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Validator background loop sets on-chain weights via subtensor.set_weights once per chain rate-limit
    window. should_set_weights() gates on (current_block - last_update[uid]) > epoch_length. Sticky burn-split
    assigns burn_share to burn_hotkey and winner_share to the latest SOTA winner.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 'alignment_mod: 360 blocks (config); epoch_length: 100'
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of the final weights; weights are set discretely and change only when
      the winner changes (sticky) or the reward window flips (windowed).
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Validators independently re-evaluate submitted algorithms on a deterministic task suite
    rather than trusting miner-reported scores (verify_solution_quality returns score >= sota_threshold).
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'SOTA threshold ratchets upward: each new accepted algorithm raises the bar so the next
    submission must beat it (progressive improvement). Also IP/address request rate limiting in validator
    config.'
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: elimination
  description: 'Blacklisting: if a miner''s claimed score differs from the validator''s re-evaluated score
    by more than 10%, validators vote to blacklist; after multiple votes the relay rejects the miner''s
    submissions (per docs/rewards.md).'
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: TASK_REGISTRY supports cifar10_binary (default), mnist_binary, and scalar_linear, selected per
    submission via task_type; cifar10_binary is the documented/default validator benchmark. Direct vs
    pool participation modes also exist but only direct mining feeds the on-chain SOTA winner mechanism.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - reputation
  status_values:
  - active_winner
  - burn_default
  - blacklisted
  reset_policy: Winner changes (and prior winner stops earning) when a new SOTA event is accepted or a
    better local candidate is found; min_winner_improvement (default 0.0) gates replacement of the local
    winner.
  notes: 'On-chain: validator tracks the current SOTA winner hotkey (sticky) or the active windowed event.
    Off-chain pool mining tracks a reputation ledger converted to RAO at hourly epoch boundaries (documentation
    only).'
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/rewards.md
  - docs/validation.md
  - validator_config.yaml.example
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: validator_config.yaml.example
    line_ref: '9'
    quote: 'netuid: 94'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: validator/bittensor_validation.py
    line_ref: '5'
    quote: import bittensor as bt
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '14'
    quote: 'Miners: Evolve ML algorithms using genetic programming and self-improving methods on our fixed
      CIFAR-10 binary evaluation pipeline'
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: core/evaluations.py
    line_ref: 220-221
    quote: 'algorithm_dsl = solution_data.get("algorithm_dsl") if not algorithm_dsl:'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind
    source_path: core/evaluations.py
    line_ref: '366'
    quote: score = float(np.median(scores)) if scores else task.get_baseline_fitness()
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: core/evaluations.py
    line_ref: '394'
    quote: return score >= sota_threshold, score
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description
    source_path: core/hyperparams.py
    line_ref: 141-148
    quote: '"task_count": 128, "task_seed": 1337, "default_task_type": "cifar10_binary", "default_input_dim":
      16,'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/notes
    source_path: docs/rewards.md
    line_ref: 95-96
    quote: Validators don't trust miner-reported scores. They re-run algorithms and use their own evaluated
      scores for voting. This prevents cheating.
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/1/description
    source_path: validator/capacitorless_sticky_weight_manager.py
    line_ref: 282-283
    quote: event_id = int(e["id"]) miner_hotkey = str(e["miner_hotkey"])
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: validator/capacitorless_sticky_weight_manager.py
    line_ref: 308-314
    quote: 'if not winner_hotkey or winner_hotkey == self.burn_hotkey: desired_scores = {self.burn_hotkey:
      1.0} else: desired_scores = {self.burn_hotkey: float(self.burn_share), str(winner_hotkey): float(self.winner_share)}'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction
    source_path: validator_config.yaml.example
    line_ref: burn_share
    quote: 'burn_share: 0.9'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: validator_config.yaml.example
    line_ref: burn_hotkey
    quote: 'burn_hotkey: "5Ef5EsPQoMVmJ8rYectQ26BEvscvATEGm365bcQjo1Y6bxGr"'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: validator/capacitorless_sticky_weight_manager.py
    line_ref: 49-51
    quote: winner_share_f = (float(winner_share) if winner_share is not None else 1.0 - burn_share_f)
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: docs/rewards.md
    line_ref: '55'
    quote: 'Validator sets on-chain weights: 90% burn_hotkey, 10% winner_hotkey'
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status_notes
    source_path: neurons/validator_node.py
    line_ref: '72'
    quote: reward_mode = str(config.get("reward_mode", "capacitor")).strip().lower()
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: validator/bittensor_validation.py
    line_ref: 97-104
    quote: result = self.subtensor.set_weights(wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.metagraph.netuid, uids=uint_uids,
      weights=uint_weights,
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/cadence
    source_path: validator/bittensor_validation.py
    line_ref: 35-38
    quote: 'def should_set_weights(self) -> bool: return (self.subtensor.get_current_block() - self.metagraph.last_update[self.uid])
      > self.config.epoch_length'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: validator_config.yaml.example
    line_ref: alignment_mod
    quote: 'alignment_mod: 360'
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/notes
    source_path: validator/capacitorless_sticky_weight_manager.py
    line_ref: 16-21
    quote: Always assigns `burn_share` of weight to `burn_hotkey`. Assigns `winner_share` of weight to
      the most recently accepted SOTA winner
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: docs/rewards.md
    line_ref: 98-99
    quote: If your claimed score differs from validator's score by more than 10%, validators vote to blacklist
      you.
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/notes
    source_path: core/evaluations.py
    line_ref: 31-35
    quote: 'TASK_REGISTRY = { DEFAULT_TASK_TYPE: CIFAR10BinaryTask, "mnist_binary": MNISTBinaryTask, "scalar_linear":
      ScalarLinearRegressionTask, }'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: validator_config.yaml.example
    line_ref: mode
    quote: 'mode: "sticky_burnsplit"'
  unresolved:
  - 'Which reward_mode runs in production on netuid 94 is not pinned by the repo: the hardcoded code default
    is "capacitor" (EVM contract voting) but example and committed configs use capacitorless sticky_burnsplit.
    The 90/10 split is taken from the shipped example config (burn_share: 0.9), not a hardcoded constant
    in scoring code.'
  - Pool-mining reputation -> RAO conversion (evaluator_weight, evolver_weight, tolerance_ratio, consensus_threshold,
    repetition_penalty, epoch budget, pool fee, 5% per-miner cap) is described in docs/rewards.md with
    no concrete numeric constants located in source; it is off-chain and not the on-chain incentive, left
    unmodeled.
  - 'SOTA threshold value is fetched at runtime from relay.bitsota.com (/sota_threshold); only default_threshold:
    0.0 is in config.'
composition:
  shape: multiplex
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Aurelius Protocol
docs.text:
Subnet Aurelius-Protocol__Aurelius-Protocol netuid 37 Archetype gated Language python Mechanism status full_burn Task A miner is a Bittensor axon that serves a library of operator-authored ethical-dilemma scenario configs (JSON). When a validator queries with a ScenarioConfigSynapse, the miner returns the next config in round-robin order, stamped with a work_id and signed by the miner's hotkey. The validator runs t DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: LlmJudgment, ReferenceModel"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, RateLimit, Collateral"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  M1["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: aureliusProtocol

    netuid: 37
    lang: python
    status: full_burn
    submission: [content_link, commitment_hash]

    @burn {
        uid: "200"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Collateral {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, cooldown, reputation }

    Gated {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: oracle
            },
            ReferenceModel {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: gated
            normalization: sum_to_one
            min_weight_floor: 0.1
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "TEMPO_BLOCKS = 360 blocks (~4320s); weight_interval default 300s (~25 blocks)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: full_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Operational defaults set burn_mode=True and burn_percentage=1.0 (DEFAULT_BURN_MODE
  = True, DEFAULT_BURN_PERCENTAGE = 1.0 in constants.py). When burn_mode is True, _set_weights routes
  100% of weight to BURN_UID (UID 200) and the documented 8-stage scoring pipeline does not influence
  on-chain weights. The full scoring path (classifier-graduated weight) exists and runs, but only contributes
  to on-chain weights when an operator or the remote Central API flips burn_mode off / lowers burn_percentage.
subnet:
  name: Aurelius Protocol
  owner_repo: Aurelius-Protocol__Aurelius-Protocol
  netuid: 37
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: A miner is a Bittensor axon that serves a library of operator-authored ethical-dilemma scenario
    configs (JSON). When a validator queries with a ScenarioConfigSynapse, the miner returns the next
    config in round-robin order, stamped with a work_id and signed by the miner's hotkey. The validator
    runs the config through an 8-stage pipeline (version, schema, work-id, work-token balance, rate limit,
    novelty, classifier gate, Concordia generative-agent simulation, plus optional gatekeeper/semantic
    coherence) and sets on-chain weight graduated by classifier confidence. Accepted scenarios are simulated
    through Concordia; transcripts form training data for moral-reasoning benchmarks (MoReBench).
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  - commitment_hash
  submission_format_other: ''
scoring_signals:
- name: classifier_confidence
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: min_max
  notes: The only signal that produces a non-zero graduated weight. The Central API classifier returns
    a confidence score; compute_weight maps a score above threshold linearly into [WEIGHT_MIN=0.1, 1.0]
    via (score - threshold) / (1 - threshold). Below threshold => WEIGHT_FAIL (0.0). Default classifier_threshold
    = 0.5; custom tension_archetype bumps threshold by DEFAULT_CUSTOM_ARCHETYPE_THRESHOLD_BUMP = 0.1.
- name: pipeline_gate_pass
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Gating signal, not additive. The 8-stage pipeline short-circuits on first failure (version, schema,
    verify_work_id, work_token balance, rate_limit, novelty, classifier, simulate, optional gatekeeper,
    optional semantic_coherence). Any stage failure yields weight=WEIGHT_FAIL (0.0) regardless of classifier
    confidence.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: Central-API-hosted classifier scores scenario configs for quality; an optional remote-prompt
    LLM "gatekeeper" rubric judges whether the Concordia transcript is a valid moral-reasoning outcome;
    optional semantic coherence check via the configured LLM provider.
  source_identifier: Central API (https://new-collector-api-production.up.railway.app)
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Classifier threshold, novelty threshold, gatekeeper prompt, and other knobs are fetched from
    the Central API remote config and cached ~5 min.
- kind: reference_model
  description: Concordia (google-deepmind/concordia) generative-agent simulation run in a sandboxed Docker
    container; the resulting transcript is the payload that determines the miner's weight.
  source_identifier: ghcr.io/aurelius-protocol/aurelius-concordia
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Simulation uses an OpenAI-compatible LLM (default deepseek-chat).
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: gated
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: 0.1
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '200'
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'DEFAULT_BURN_MODE = True and DEFAULT_BURN_PERCENTAGE = 1.0. In burn_mode, weights are np.array([1.0])
      to BURN_UID (200). When burn_mode is off, burn_percentage still carves a fixed slice: miner weights
      are normalized to sum to 1.0 then scaled by (1 - burn_pct), and burn_pct is appended to UID 200.
      burn_percentage is bounded (0.0, 1.0) in remote config.'
  notes: 'Non-burn path: each passing miner''s graduated weight (result.weight) is multiplied by a validator
    consistency multiplier, then all miner weights are normalized to sum to (1 - burn_pct). min_weight_floor
    (0.1) is WEIGHT_MIN, the minimum weight a passing submission receives before normalization.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: 'Main loop sets weights when blocks_since_last >= (weight_interval // 12). DEFAULT_WEIGHT_INTERVAL
    = 300s => ~25 blocks. Burn_mode: single weight 1.0 to UID 200. Otherwise per-passing-miner graduated
    weight * consistency multiplier, normalized to (1 - burn_pct), burn slice appended. Weights run through
    process_weights_for_netuid before subtensor.set_weights(mechid=0). Subtensor''s own per-subnet weights
    rate limit defers most attempts.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: TEMPO_BLOCKS = 360 blocks (~4320s); weight_interval default 300s (~25 blocks)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA on the final reward in this repo. Cross-validator aggregation is left to Bittensor
      Yuma consensus. A per-validator consistency multiplier (rate - floor)/(1 - floor), with consistency_floor
      default 0.4, attenuates low-agreement validators but is not temporal smoothing.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Novelty check (stage 5) embeds the config and queries the Central API FAISS novelty index;
    configs too similar (novelty_threshold default 0.88) are rejected. Embedding is added to the index
    before token deduction; fail-closed if indexing fails to prevent duplicate resubmission bypass.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Per-hotkey rate limiter (DEFAULT_RATE_LIMIT_PER_UID_PER_TEMPO = 1 per TEMPO_SECONDS window),
    persisted across restarts.
- kind: collateral
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Work-token economy: each accepted submission costs DEFAULT_WORK_TOKEN_COST = 1.0 alpha
    (raised from 0.01 to mitigate Sybil). Balance checked in stage 3 and deducted only after the full
    pipeline passes (stage 8); zero-balance miners are rejected before simulation.'
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Work-id verification (stage 2b): validator recomputes sha256(config_json + hotkey + time_ns
    + nonce) and rejects on mismatch. Freshness window (work_id_freshness_seconds default 300s) rejects
    stale work ids; the work_id is signed by the miner hotkey.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Self-validation prevention: validator never sets weight for its own hotkey. Version gate
    rejects miners below remote min_miner_version. Schema validation enforces scenario structure and a
    max config size (DEFAULT_MAX_CONFIG_SIZE = 65536 bytes).'
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Ramp-up: for the first RAMP_UP_TEMPOS (3) tempos a validator skips miners with validation_counts
    < MIN_VALIDATIONS_FOR_WEIGHT (2). Concordia simulation runs sandboxed (capped CPU/RAM, egress allowlisted).'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Single mechanism. Scenarios carry one of 9 tension_archetype enum values (or "custom"), but these
    are content tags within one task, not separate scored competitions; "custom" only raises the classifier
    threshold.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - cooldown
  - reputation
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Results retained RESULT_RETENTION_BLOCKS (= TEMPO_BLOCKS = 360 blocks) after the recording
    cycle; results for hotkeys unseen for 3 tempos are evicted. Validation counts persisted to disk for
    ramp-up survival.
  notes: 'Per-hotkey: latest PipelineResult (self.results), validation_counts (ramp-up), last-seen time,
    and rate-limiter window. Off-chain in the Central API: work-token balance and a per-miner work-token
    ledger; novelty index of prior submissions; validator consistency / agreement_rate.'
documentation:
