docs.title: Aurelius
docs.text:
Subnet Aurelius-Protocol__aurelius-whitepaper netuid 37 Archetype multiplex Language none_docs_only Mechanism status unknown Task Miners contribute one of two innovation types: (a) configuration innovation — defining environmental scenarios (agent parameters, interaction conditions, narrative context) for a multi-agent world model; and (b) world-model innovation — improving the world-model infrastructure itself. Validators run DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: LlmJudgment, Other"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Other, Deduplication, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  M1["Metric: similarity"]
  M2["Metric: other"]
  M3["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: Other"]
  PUB["publish: Unknown"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: aurelius

    netuid: 37
    lang: none_docs_only
    status: unknown
    submission: [source_code, other]

    @guards {
        Other {
            enforcement: other
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: other
        }
    }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: generative_reward
                specific: alignment_signal
                raw: "alignment_signal_per_token"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                raw: "behavioral_delta_between_conditions"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: adversarial
            },
            Other {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: Other {
            composition: gated
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: Unknown {
            cadence: undocumented
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: unknown
mechanism_status_notes: Whitepaper-only repository (no source code, config, or weight-setting call site).
  The document describes phase-one intentions for Aurelius (Bittensor Subnet 37) but provides no implementation,
  so the operative on-chain reality (active scoring vs. burn) cannot be determined from this repo.
subnet:
  name: Aurelius
  owner_repo: Aurelius-Protocol__aurelius-whitepaper
  netuid: 37
  implementation_lang: none_docs_only
task:
  summary: 'Miners contribute one of two innovation types: (a) configuration innovation — defining environmental
    scenarios (agent parameters, interaction conditions, narrative context) for a multi-agent world model;
    and (b) world-model innovation — improving the world-model infrastructure itself. Validators run the
    orchestrated world model, score submissions against a "DLV" (Dual Life Value) signal and secondary
    dimensions on a Pareto frontier, and reach consensus on which submissions are rewarded. The corpus
    output ("aenes" = atomic units of moral reasoning) is periodically used to retrain the base model.'
  submission_format:
  - source_code
  - other
  submission_format_other: Scenario/orchestrator configurations and world-model infrastructure improvements
    submitted by miners; openly visible and forkable.
scoring_signals:
- name: dlv_signal_quality
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Apex selection pressure and a gate: "A configuration that fails to produce sufficient DLV reasoning
    is rejected regardless of performance on other dimensions." Measured by a judge model (LLM-as-judge)
    against DLV heuristics: self-awareness, other-awareness, tension recognition, deliberation, coherence.
    No numeric threshold given in the whitepaper.'
- name: novelty
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Secondary (Pareto) dimension. "Novelty is measured across perspectives, strategies, outcomes,
    and corpus-level redundancy. New aenes should surface reasoning samples the corpus does not already
    contain." No weight given; combined via Pareto dominance, not an additive weight.
- name: token_efficiency
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: alignment_signal_per_token
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Secondary (Pareto) dimension. "Total alignment signal per token, including tokens from both the
    world model and the agent." No numeric value given.
- name: immersion_effectiveness
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: behavioral_delta_between_conditions
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Secondary (Pareto) dimension proxying epistemic opacity. Measured as the delta in agent behavior
    between control (explicit game framing) and immersive (first-person, consequential framing) conditions.
    "Environments with strong deltas are selected for."
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: Validators feed an agent's observations, reasoning, and actions to a judge model that evaluates
    the aene against DLV heuristics. Aurelius mitigates gaming via judge ensembles, cross-validation,
    and continuous human-in-the-loop calibration on sampled batches.
  source_identifier: ''
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Acknowledged as LLM-as-judge with known limitations (gameable, can reward surface patterns).
    Heuristics "designed to be calibrated over time."
- kind: other
  kind_other: external_moral_reasoning_benchmarks
  description: Corpus effectiveness validated through fine-tuning experiments against external moral reasoning
    benchmarks; key signal is out-of-distribution generalization. This is a corpus-level validation, not
    a per-submission ground truth.
  source_identifier: ''
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: References [12,13] (MoReBench, MACHIAVELLI).
aggregation:
  method: other
  method_other: Pareto-frontier ranking gated by a DLV threshold. "Ranking of miner submissions operates
    on a Pareto frontier. Any submission that passes the DLV threshold is rewarded if no other beats it
    on all secondary dimensions simultaneously."
  composition: gated
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn mechanism described in the whitepaper.
  notes: DLV signal quality acts as a hard gate; among DLV-passing submissions, ranking is by Pareto dominance
    over novelty, token efficiency, and immersion effectiveness. World-model changes carry a higher consensus
    bar than configuration changes. No numeric thresholds, temperatures, or weights given.
weight_setting:
  cadence: undocumented
  method: Validator consensus adjudicates which aenes/submissions enter the corpus and are rewarded; "Validator
    consensus adjudicates aenes entering the corpus." No on-chain weight-setting call, tempo, or smoothing
    is described.
  on_chain_call: unknown
  tempo_or_interval: null
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No reward smoothing/EMA described in the whitepaper.
anti_gaming:
- kind: other
  kind_other: judge_ensembles_and_human_calibration
  enforcement: other
  enforcement_other: mitigation
  description: '"Aurelius mitigates this through judge ensembles, cross-validation, and continuous human-in-the-loop
    calibration on sampled batches." Addresses LLM-as-judge gaming.'
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Novelty is measured including corpus-level redundancy; new aenes should surface reasoning
    the corpus does not already contain, penalizing redundant submissions.
- kind: other
  kind_other: cryptographic_anchoring
  enforcement: other
  enforcement_other: tamper_evidence
  description: Corpus is batched and hashed into Merkle trees periodically anchored to Bitcoin; "The corpus
    cannot be modified after the fact without detection." Provides tamper-evidence/provenance rather than
    scoring enforcement.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: configuration
    role: main
    advancement_rule: 'Configuration scoring: validators receive configurations from miners, deploy them,
      and score results; consensus decides which configurations produce high-quality aenes.'
  - name: world_model
    role: main
    advancement_rule: 'World-model scoring: improvements scored against quality, efficiency, and stability.
      "World model changes affect the entire system, thus a higher bar is set for consensus."'
  count: 2
  notes: Two parallel miner contribution tracks (configuration innovation and world-model innovation),
    each with a corresponding validator scoring role.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: Agents are explicitly stateless ("Agents are stateless; by design they do not learn during interaction
    or carry context between sessions"). No persistent per-miner ledger/reputation state is described
    for the incentive mechanism.
documentation:
  status: whitepaper_only
  source_version: Part 1 (phase-one implementation)
  primary_docs:
  - Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
  - README.md
  completeness: low
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / Opus 4.8
  confidence_overall: low
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
    line_ref: p.6 (5.1 Bittensor)
    quote: 'Aurelius is Subnet 37 on Bittensor. The subnet consists of two roles: miners and validators.'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 1-19
    quote: This repository contains the official whitepaper (part 1) for the Aurelius protocol.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
    line_ref: p.6 (5.2 Miners)
    quote: 'Configuration innovation: is defining environmental scenarios. ... World model innovation:
      is improving the world model infrastructure itself.'
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
    line_ref: p.7 (5.4 Evolutionary Iteration)
    quote: All miner contributions are openly visible and forkable. A high quality miner submission can
      be forked, modified, and resubmitted by any other miner.
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/name
    source_path: Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
    line_ref: p.7 (6.1 Signal Hierarchy)
    quote: DLV signal quality is the apex selection pressure. A configuration that fails to produce sufficient
      DLV reasoning is rejected regardless of performance on other dimensions.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind
    source_path: Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
    line_ref: p.8 (6.2 DLV Signal Quality)
    quote: Validators feed an agent's observations, reasoning, and actions to a judge model. The judge
      evaluates the aene against DLV heuristics ... Self-awareness ... Other-awareness ... Tension recognition
      ... Deliberation ... Coherence.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/name
    source_path: Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
    line_ref: p.8 (6.3 Secondary Signals)
    quote: Novelty. ... Novelty is measured across perspectives, strategies, outcomes, and corpus-level
      redundancy.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/name
    source_path: Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
    line_ref: p.9 (6.3 Secondary Signals)
    quote: Token efficiency. Total alignment signal per token, including tokens from both the world model
      and the agent.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/3/name
    source_path: Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
    line_ref: p.9 (6.3 Secondary Signals)
    quote: Immersion effectiveness measures how well epistemic opacity is achieved in practice. ... The
      delta in behavior between conditions indicates the strength of the immersion. Environments with
      strong deltas are selected for.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method_other
    source_path: Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
    line_ref: p.8 (6.1 Signal Hierarchy)
    quote: Ranking of miner submissions operates on a Pareto frontier. Any submission that passes the
      DLV threshold is rewarded if no other beats it on all secondary dimensions simultaneously.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/kind
    source_path: Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
    line_ref: p.8 (6.2 DLV Signal Quality)
    quote: This is LLM-as-judge. It has known limitations. ... Aurelius mitigates this through judge ensembles,
      cross-validation, and continuous human-in-the-loop calibration on sampled batches.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/1/kind_other
    source_path: Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
    line_ref: p.9 (6.4 Validation)
    quote: Corpus effectiveness is validated through fine-tuning experiments against external moral reasoning
      benchmarks. The key signal is out-of-distribution generalization.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
    line_ref: p.6 (5.3 Validators)
    quote: They determine which configurations produce high quality aenes and which do not. Validator
      consensus adjudicates aenes entering the corpus.
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/kind_other
    source_path: Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
    line_ref: p.11 (8.2 Cryptographic Anchoring)
    quote: The Aurelius corpus is batched and hashed into separate Merkle trees. The roots are periodically
      anchored to Bitcoin. The corpus cannot be modified after the fact without detection.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/structure
    source_path: Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
    line_ref: p.6-7 (5.3 Validators)
    quote: Validators run the world model. They have two roles mapping to miner contributions. Configuration
      scoring ... World model scoring ... World model changes affect the entire system, thus a higher
      bar is set for consensus.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/tracked
    source_path: Aurelius_A_Peer-to-Peer_Alignment_Protocol.pdf
    line_ref: p.5 (4.3 Agent Design)
    quote: Agents are stateless; by design they do not learn during interaction or carry context between
      sessions.
    confidence: medium
  unresolved:
  - No source code, config, or weight-setting call site exists in the repo (whitepaper-only); on-chain
    mechanism (set_weights cadence, EMA smoothing, burn) is undocumented.
  - 'No numeric constants given: DLV threshold, Pareto weights, judge-ensemble size, calibration batch
    fractions, retrain cadence are all unspecified.'
  - mechanism_status cannot be verified as active/burn — no validator implementation present.
  - Whether weight setting uses standard set_weights or commit_reveal is not stated.
  extractor_notes: Docs-only (whitepaper) extraction. All evidence quotes are from the 12-page PDF whitepaper
    (page/section refs in line_ref since the PDF has no line numbers) and the README. No code-derived
    fields could be filled.
composition:
  shape: multiplex

  extern_count: 2

docs.title: MANTIS
docs.text:
Subnet Barbariandev__MANTIS netuid 123 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status unknown Task Miners submit encrypted feature embeddings ("signals") for a set of market-prediction challenges (e.g. ETH/BTC/FX binary direction, barrier-hit, LBFGS volatility/quantile paths, multi-asset range breakout, cross-sectional return and funding-rate ranking). Validators sample each miner's committed pay DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData, CommitReveal"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CommitReveal, Deduplication, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: other"]
  M1["Metric: accuracy"]
  AGG["aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: mantis

    netuid: 123
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: [signals, content_link, commitment_hash]

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: 0.45
    }
    @guards {
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { registration_age }

    Multiplex<multi_asset> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: predictive_contribution
                specific: saliency_coefficient
                raw: "predictive contribution (absolute meta-model logistic coefficient |beta_j|) of a miner's signal to forecasting the realized forward price/return outcome"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            MarketData {
                trust_model: oracle
            },
            CommitReveal {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma {
            normalization: sum_to_one
            decay_rate: 0.15
            min_weight_floor: 0.0001
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "360 blocks (WEIGHT_SET_INTERVAL); weight calc every 1000 blocks (WEIGHT_CALC_INTERVAL)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_asset
            count: 13
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
subnet:
  name: MANTIS
  owner_repo: Barbariandev__MANTIS
  netuid: 123
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners submit encrypted feature embeddings ("signals") for a set of market-prediction challenges
    (e.g. ETH/BTC/FX binary direction, barrier-hit, LBFGS volatility/quantile paths, multi-asset range
    breakout, cross-sectional return and funding-rate ranking). Validators sample each miner's committed
    payload every few blocks, decrypt it after a timelock maturation window, and store (embedding, price)
    pairs. Periodically a walk-forward scoring pass computes each hotkey's salience — its measured contribution
    / predictive value to a fitted logistic meta-model of the realized forward price outcome — for each
    challenge. Per-challenge salience is aggregated across challenges by weight, EMA-smoothed, and turned
    into on-chain weights. Reward is the predictive value of a miner's signal.
  submission_format:
  - signals
  - content_link
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: salience_contribution
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: predictive contribution (absolute meta-model logistic coefficient |beta_j|) of a
    miner's signal to forecasting the realized forward price/return outcome
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: other
  notes: 'Normalization: per-challenge salience is normalized to sum-to-one over hotkeys, then a challenge-weighted
    average is taken across challenges, then re-normalized to sum-to-one. Computed by model.multi_salience
    dispatching per challenge loss_func. Binary/xsec use a walk-forward ElasticNet/L2-logistic meta-model
    on out-of-sample base-model predictions; importance = |beta_j|. LBFGS blends a classifier path (beta_j^2)
    and a Q-path 75/25. HITFIRST uses |beta_up| + |beta_down|.'
- name: feature_selection_auc
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Gating signal: per-miner L2-logistic AUC on a held-out half (binary) or univariate AUC (xsec).
    Only miners with AUC > 0.5 are selected into the meta-model (top_k, default 50/20).'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: Realized spot asset prices (and funding rates) fetched from an external price service and
    published to a Cloudflare R2 JSON endpoint; forward outcome is the price/return blocks_ahead later.
    Labels are binary (return > eps), barrier-hit, or cross-sectional rank vs median.
  source_identifier: 'config.PRICE_DATA_URL = https://pub-ba8c1b8edb8046edaccecbd26b5ca7f8.r2.dev/latest_prices.json
    (price_service.py: Polygon spot + OKX/HL/CoinGlass funding)'
  trust_model: oracle
- kind: commit_reveal
  description: Miner payloads are dual-path encrypted (X25519+ChaCha20-Poly1305 owner path; Drand IBE
    timelock path). Validators can only decrypt after the specified Drand round, so predictions are hidden
    until maturation. Payload commit is an on-chain URL pointing to the ciphertext.
  source_identifier: 'config: DRAND_BEACON_ID=quicknet, ALG_LABEL_V2=x25519-hkdf-sha256+chacha20poly1305+drand-tlock'
  trust_model: trusted
aggregation:
  method: exponential_decay_ema
  normalization: sum_to_one
  decay_rate: 0.15
  min_weight_floor: 0.0001
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 0.45
    notes: 'BURN_PCT applied in validator.py calc_worker: all UID weights scaled by (1-BURN_PCT) and BURN_PCT
      added to UID 0. config.BURN_PCT = 0.45 (README table inconsistently lists 0.30; code value 0.45
      is authoritative).'
  notes: decay_rate is the EMA alpha (0.15) applied across weight-setting intervals to the normalized
    weight vector. min_weight_floor is the fixed 0.0001 weight given to each young UID (hotkey age < 36000
    blocks); mature UIDs share the remaining mass proportional to salience. Per-challenge salience is
    normalized sum-to-one, multiplied by challenge weight, and averaged.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Weight calc runs in a background thread when current_block % WEIGHT_CALC_INTERVAL (1000) ==
    0 and at least LAG*2+1 steps exist; computed/EMA'd weights are saved to disk and then pushed on-chain
    when current_block % WEIGHT_SET_INTERVAL (360) == 0.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 360 blocks (WEIGHT_SET_INTERVAL); weight calc every 1000 blocks (WEIGHT_CALC_INTERVAL)
anti_gaming:
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Timelock (Drand IBE) + owner X25519 encryption hides predictions until a maturation round;
    a SHA-256 binding hash over (hotkey, round, owner_pk, ephemeral_pk) is used as AAD to prevent replay,
    relay, and substitution. Payloads sampled every SAMPLE_EVERY blocks but only scored after decryption.
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: L2 regularization splits coefficient mass among correlated/clone miners (n clones each
    get ~w/n). LBFGS path applies a uniqueness penalty suppressing miners with >85% argmax overlap with
    higher-ranked peers; TRADE-MIX uses dedup_cosine_threshold 0.95.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Stale-signal filtering: in iter training, when price is unchanged for more than MAX_UNCHANGED_TIMESTEPS
    (15) consecutive samples those rows are skipped; funding/xsec challenges zero out miner columns whose
    temporal std < 1e-4 before pooling.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Commit URL validation in cycle.get_miner_payloads: host must be *.r2.dev or *.r2.cloudflarestorage.com,
    object key must be exactly the hotkey (single path segment), payload <= 25 MB. Invalid commits are
    dropped.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_asset
  stages: []
  count: 13
  notes: '13 challenges in config.CHALLENGES, each with its own ticker, dim, blocks_ahead, loss_func and
    weight: binary direction (ETH/CADUSD/NZDUSD/CHFUSD/XAGUSD, w=1), ETH-HITFIRST (w=2.5), ETH-LBFGS (w=3.5),
    BTC-LBFGS-6H (w=2.875), MULTI-BREAKOUT (w=5.0), XSEC-RANK (w=3.0), FUNDING-XSEC (w=4.0), TRADE-MIX
    (sized to ~15% of emissions). Salience aggregated by weight.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - registration_age
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: Hotkey "first block" (first non-zero submission) is read from the DB; UIDs younger than young_threshold
    = 36000 blocks are treated as "young" and assigned a fixed 0.0001 weight instead of salience-proportional
    weight, until they mature. No persistent debt/score ledger beyond the SQLite (embedding, price) history
    used for scoring.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: dormant
mechanism_status_notes: 'Validator main loop computes local SLA scores via SLAScorer, but SLAScorer is
  a stub (score_orchestrator returns SLAScore(effective_multiplier=1.0)). The actual on-chain weight vector
  is taken from BeamCore''s persisted snapshot via subnet_core_client.get_recommended_weights(epoch);
  local scores are only submitted to BeamCore, not used to set weights. The detailed exposure*quality*confidence*penalty
  formula in services/weight_calculator.py is a port of BeamCore''s formula and is only exercised in the
  README''s optional custom-scoring path, not the running validator. No emission burn is present; SLA-failing
  orchestrators have rewards redirected to Orchestrator UID #1 (subnet-owned treasury), not a null/burn
  UID.'
subnet:
  name: BEAM
  owner_repo: Beam-Network__beam
  netuid: 105
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners run "orchestrators" (and the workers they coordinate) that relay/transfer data chunks
    across a decentralized bandwidth network. Workers move object bytes directly between storage connectors
    and submit Proof-of-Bandwidth (PoB) evidence (signed timing, bytes relayed, claimed bandwidth, canary
    proof) to the off-repo BeamCore service. Validators fetch unverified proofs from BeamCore, verify
    signature/timing/bandwidth/canary, and orchestrators are rewarded based on measured delivery performance
    (throughput, success rate, uptime, latency) rather than promises.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: bandwidth_score
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.4
  normalization: none
  notes: Quality component, coefficient BANDWIDTH_WEIGHT=0.40 in weight_calculator.py. Validator README
    documents the SLA-formula bandwidth coefficient as 0.30 (a different, simpler formula); the two in-repo
    formulas disagree.
- name: compliance_score
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: payment_compliance
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.25
  normalization: none
  notes: 'COMPLIANCE_WEIGHT=0.25. Also acts as a hard gate: below MIN_COMPLIANCE_FOR_EMISSIONS=0.5 the
    orchestrator receives zero weight.'
- name: verification_rate
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: proof_verification_rate
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.2
  normalization: none
  notes: VERIFICATION_WEIGHT=0.20. Also scales exposure (effective work = claimed * verification_rate,
    clamped to [0,1]).
- name: spot_check_rate
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: spot_check_pass_rate
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.15
  normalization: none
  notes: SPOT_CHECK_WEIGHT=0.15.
- name: exposure
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Multiplicative factor (not additive). Blended routing share of verified work: EXPOSURE_BYTES_WEIGHT=0.70
    * bytes_share + EXPOSURE_PROOFS_WEIGHT=0.30 * proofs_share, over verification-rate-discounted effective
    values.'
- name: confidence
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: work_volume_confidence_ramp
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Multiplicative sqrt ramp on verified work: 0.70*min(sqrt(eff_proofs/10),1) + 0.30*min(sqrt(eff_bytes/1e9),1).
    Thresholds CONFIDENCE_PROOF_THRESHOLD=10, CONFIDENCE_BYTES_THRESHOLD=1_000_000_000.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: 'Proof-of-Bandwidth: workers sign timing/bytes/canary evidence of an actual chunk relay.
    Validator independently recomputes bandwidth from timing (bytes*8 / duration), checks timing sanity
    (>1ms, end>start), canary format, and worker hotkey signature.'
  source_identifier: proof_of_bandwidth
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Proofs are fetched from the off-repo BeamCore service (subnet_core_client.get_unverified_proofs);
    BeamCore (PostgreSQL) holds authoritative execution state.
- kind: other
  kind_other: offchain_coordination_service
  description: BeamCore V2 (separate repo, github.com/Beam-Network/beamcore_v2) is the authoritative coordination
    service. The live validator fetches the recommended weight vector and orchestrator scores from BeamCore
    rather than computing them locally.
  source_identifier: beamcore_v2
  trust_model: trusted
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'No emission burn. SLA-violating orchestrators have a fraction of reward redirected to Orchestrator
      UID #1 (subnet-owned treasury), governed by sla_score.penalty_redirect_percent (a stub returns 0.0
      in-repo).'
  notes: 'Documented in-repo formula (weight_calculator.compute_weights): raw = exposure * quality_final
    * confidence * penalty * effective_compliance, then normalized to sum 1 across orchestrators and converted
    to uint16 (MAX_WEIGHT_UINT16=65535). quality_final = quality_raw * trust_multiplier, where trust_multiplier
    multiplies by TRUST_PENALTY=0.5 per failed gate (compliance<0.5 or verification<0.5; both -> 0.25).
    It is a weighted_sum of the four quality signals, gated by trust and compliance, then multiplied by
    exposure and confidence — i.e. hybrid additive+multiplicative. The LIVE validator instead normalizes
    a vector pulled from BeamCore (get_recommended_weights).'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Async main loop. _maybe_set_weights sets weights only after blocks_between_weights (default
    100, ~20 min) have passed since last set. The weight vector is the BeamCore persisted snapshot (uids,
    weights) from get_recommended_weights for the current/latest data epoch; on-chain via bittensor/Fiber
    set_weights, or timelocked commit-reveal when CommitRevealWeightsEnabled is true for the netuid.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (blocks_between_weights, ~20 min)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.3
    window: null
    notes: config.weight_alpha=0.3 ("EMA smoothing factor") and BANDWIDTH_EMA_ALPHA=0.3 are defined. The
      EMA at validator.py:2123 (score = alpha*score + (1-alpha)*old) applies only to legacy per-connection
      scores, NOT to the BeamCore weight vector actually set on chain. Recorded as the configured smoother;
      effectively unused on the live path.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Validator re-verifies each PoB: recomputed bandwidth must be within 50% of claimed (ratio
    in [0.5,2.0]) and below physical 100 Gbps limit; transfer duration must be >1ms and end>start; canary
    proof must be 64 hex chars / valid hex. Failing proofs are rejected.'
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  description: When CommitRevealWeightsEnabled is set for the subnet, weights are committed via time-locked
    commit-reveal (subtensor.set_weights with wait_for_revealed_execution).
- kind: other
  kind_other: signature_authentication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Worker PoB submissions must carry a valid hotkey signature over "{task_id}:{worker_hotkey}:{start}:{end}:{bytes}";
    PoB main path verifies the orchestrator/miner signature via verify_hotkey_signature before scoring.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Weight setting is rate-limited to once per blocks_between_weights (default 100 blocks)
    per validator.
- kind: other
  kind_other: outflow_traffic_selling_penalty
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Outflow penalty deters traffic-selling: top 25% sellers (OUTFLOW_PENALTY_PERCENTILE=0.75)
    are penalized down to a 0.25 multiplier (OUTFLOW_MAX_PENALTY=0.25, i.e. up to 75% traffic reduction).
    Defined as tunable defaults in weight_calculator.py (part of the dormant in-repo formula).'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: README mentions BeamCore PRISM tracks orchestrators in "qualifying" vs "qualified" pools with
    prism_final_score, but that logic lives in the off-repo BeamCore service and is not implemented in
    this repository.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cooldown
  - performance_ledger
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: New orchestrators get a NEW_ORCHESTRATOR_GRACE_PERIOD_HOURS=24 grace window and default
    compliance=1.0 (no penalty) before their first epoch; PoB verification stats reset each epoch.
  notes: Validator keeps rolling 24h SLA metrics, per-orchestrator PoB verification stats (reset per epoch),
    payment/fraud penalty multipliers, and a grace period for new orchestrators. Authoritative orchestrator
    state lives in BeamCore PostgreSQL.

docs.title: BetterTherapy
docs.text:
Subnet BetterTherapy__BetterTherapy-Subnet netuid 102 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status dormant Task Miners run an LLM ("AI Doctor Twin" / therapist) that answers mental-health prompts. A validator generates a mental-health question and a base answer from Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, queries miners via an InferenceSynapse, and (as documented) scores each miner's text response on quality (GPT-4 LLM-as-jud DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: RateLimit, Honeypot"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  M1["Metric: latency"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: betterTherapy

    netuid: 102
    lang: python
    status: dormant
    submission: api_response

    @guards {
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Honeypot {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, reputation }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: LlmJudgment {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "100 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: dormant
mechanism_status_notes: 'The README documents a quality (LLM-judge) + response-time scoring mechanism,
  and the scoring code exists (evals/eval.py judge, forward.py per-miner score computation). But in the
  live forward() path the score-update call is commented out (forward.py lines 292-295) and immediately
  overridden by hardcoded reward_scores=[1], rewarded_miner_ids=[0] which is passed to update_scores.
  The result is that only UID 0 receives reward every step; the documented per-miner scoring never reaches
  the on-chain weights. Treated as dormant: the scoring code is present but the invoked path does not
  run it.'
subnet:
  name: BetterTherapy
  owner_repo: BetterTherapy__BetterTherapy-Subnet
  netuid: 102
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners run an LLM ("AI Doctor Twin" / therapist) that answers mental-health prompts. A validator
    generates a mental-health question and a base answer from Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, queries miners via
    an InferenceSynapse, and (as documented) scores each miner's text response on quality (GPT-4 LLM-as-judge,
    0-1) plus a response-time bonus. The documented total score is 0.7*quality + 0.3*time-bonus, but the
    live code bypasses this and rewards only UID 0.
  submission_format:
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: response_quality
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.7
  normalization: none
  notes: GPT-4 LLM-as-judge scores each therapist response 0-1 vs a base response; "A score of 0.7 means
    the response is as good as the base response." Clamped to [0,1], scaled *100, weighted 0.7. quality_score
    = reward*100*0.7. DOCUMENTED ONLY; not applied in the live weight path.
- name: response_time
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Bucketed time bonus, gated on quality > 0.2: <10s=>100, <20s=>50, <30s=>20, else 0; weighted
    0.3. DOCUMENTED ONLY; not applied in the live weight path.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: GPT-4 used as judge ("strict and fair judge for therapy responses") to score miner responses
    against a base answer generated by the validator's local Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
  source_identifier: gpt-4
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Both batch (evals/batch.py) and synchronous (evals/eval.py) judge paths use judge_model="gpt-4".
    Base response produced by --model.name default meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: Not a chain burn UID per se, but the live forward() hardcodes all reward to UID 0 (rewarded_miner_ids=[0],
      reward_scores=[1]), so post-L1-normalization effectively the entire weight vector concentrates on
      UID 0. Documented additive scoring (0.7 quality + 0.3 time) is bypassed.
  notes: set_weights normalizes raw scores by their L1 norm (np.linalg.norm ord=1) then passes through
    process_weights_for_netuid. Documented per-miner total_score = bounded_score*100*0.7 + response_time_score*0.3
    is summed per miner in forward.py but the update_scores call using it is commented out.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: EMA of per-miner scores (alpha=0.1) maintained in self.scores; at sync/epoch boundary set_weights
    L1-normalizes self.scores, runs process_weights_for_netuid, converts to uint16 and calls subtensor.set_weights
    with version_key=spec_version.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: 'update_scores: scores = alpha*scattered_rewards + (1-alpha)*scores, alpha = config.neuron.moving_average_alpha
      (default 0.1). epoch_length default 100 (12s blocks).'
anti_gaming:
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: filter_uids caps miners per coldkey and per IP at max_per_key=15, dedups ip:port, and excludes
    axons not serving. Limits sybil concentration when querying.
- kind: honeypot
  enforcement: elimination
  description: A judge score of -1 for a miner triggers blacklist_hotkey (coldkey+hotkey) against the
    pool mining backend, removing that miner. Blacklisted hotkeys are filtered out in filter_uids.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - reputation
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: On resync_metagraph, scores for replaced hotkeys are zeroed (self.scores[uid]=0). State
    (step, scores, hotkeys) persisted to state.npz via save_state/load_state.
  notes: self.scores is an EMA moving average over metagraph.n. Blacklist of misbehaving coldkeys/hotkeys
    is maintained via the external pool-mining backend (get_blacklisted_hotkeys).
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8
  extracted_at: '2026-06-06'
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: BetterTherapy/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 292-314
    quote: '# self.update_scores(reward_scores, rewarded_miner_ids) ... reward_scores = [1] ... rewarded_miner_ids
      = [0] ... self.update_scores(reward_scores, rewarded_miner_ids)'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: BetterTherapy/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 18-39
    quote: import bittensor as bt
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid

    quote: Replace `<your_netuid>` with your subnet ID (e.g., `354` (`test`) and `102`(`finney`)).
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /task/summary

    quote: You are a compassionate mental health assistant. Generate both a mental health question and

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format

      deserialize=True, timeout=500,)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind

    quote: score each therapist's response on a scale from 0 to 1. ... A score of 0.7 means the response

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1
    source_path: BetterTherapy/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 216-240
    quote: 'if (...response_time... < 10): response_time_score = 100 ... total_score = (bounded_score

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes

    quote: 'Miners receive response time bonuses only if their quality reward exceeds 0.2 ... Under 10

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0
    source_path: evals/eval.py
    line_ref: 9-44
    quote: 'self.judge_model = judge_model ... "content": "You are a strict and fair judge for therapy
