    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 70-79
    quote: self.evals = OpenAILLMAsJudgeEval(api_key=api_key, judge_model="gpt-4")
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization
    source_path: BetterTherapy/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 262-269
    quote: norm = np.linalg.norm(self.scores, ord=1, axis=0, keepdims=True) ... raw_weights = self.scores
      / norm
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: BetterTherapy/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 311-314
    quote: reward_scores = [1] ... rewarded_miner_ids = [0] ... self.update_scores(reward_scores, rewarded_miner_ids)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: BetterTherapy/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 237-263
    quote: total_score = (bounded_score * 100 * 0.7 + response_time_score * 0.3) ... miner_scores[miner_uid]
      = (miner_scores.get(miner_uid, 0.0) + total_score)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: BetterTherapy/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 298-306
    quote: result, msg = self.subtensor.set_weights(wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.config.netuid, uids=uint_uids,
      weights=uint_weights, ... version_key=self.spec_version,)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/alpha
    source_path: BetterTherapy/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 385-386
    quote: 'alpha: float = self.config.neuron.moving_average_alpha ... self.scores = alpha * scattered_rewards
      + (1 - alpha) * self.scores'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/alpha
    source_path: BetterTherapy/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 242-247
    quote: --neuron.moving_average_alpha ... default=0.1,
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: BetterTherapy/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 88-93
    quote: --neuron.epoch_length ... how often we set weights, measured in 12 second blocks ... default=100,
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0
    source_path: BetterTherapy/utils/uids.py
    line_ref: 32-56
    quote: 'def filter_uids(bt_obj, max_per_key: int = 15, ...): ... if ip_counts[ip_address] >= max_per_key:'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1
    source_path: BetterTherapy/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 189-201
    quote: 'if score == -1: bt.logging.info(f"Blacklisting miner {miner_uid}") blacklist_hotkey(...)'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/tracked
    source_path: BetterTherapy/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 344-387
    quote: Performs exponential moving average on the scores based on the rewards received from the miners.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: BetterTherapy/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 327-330
    quote: 'for uid, hotkey in enumerate(self.hotkeys): if hotkey != self.metagraph.hotkeys[uid]: self.scores[uid]
      = 0  # hotkey has been replaced'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /documentation/status
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 9-55
    quote: The BetterTherapy subnet implements a sophisticated incentive mechanism that rewards miners
      based on both response quality and response time.
    confidence: high
  unresolved:
  - '/subnet/netuid: README gives 102 for finney but also references netuid 10 and 354(test); exact mainnet
    netuid ambiguous.'
  - 'aggregation.method: documented as additive quality+time per miner, but live path bypasses it; chosen
    weighted_average as the documented intent.'
  - Whether the commented-out update_scores / hardcoded UID-0 reward reflects the deployed production
    state or a debugging stub left in source could not be confirmed from the repo.
  extractor_notes: 'Two judge implementations exist: synchronous OpenAILLMAsJudgeEval (evals/eval.py,
    used by reward.py/get_rewards) and OpenAIBatchLLMAsJudgeEval (evals/batch.py, used by the live forward()).
    The live forward() computes per-miner total_score (quality*0.7 + time*0.3) and accumulates into miner_scores,
    but the update_scores call consuming those scores is commented out and replaced by reward_scores=[1],
    rewarded_miner_ids=[0]; hence mechanism_status: dormant.'
composition:
  shape: pipeline
  overlays:
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: BitKoop
docs.text:
Subnet BitKoopLabs__BitKoop netuid 16 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners find and submit working discount coupon codes for online stores. Each submitted coupon is validated (via first-party coupon APIs / TLS Notary proofs) and, if it works, marked VALID. A miner is rewarded a flat amount of points per valid coupon; the normalized share of points across miners beco DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: Other, CommitReveal"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, RateLimit, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: bitKoop

    netuid: 16
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: content_link

    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, cumulative_score, cooldown }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            Other {
                trust_model: oracle
            },
            CommitReveal {
                trust_model: oracle
            }
        ]
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: min_max
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "1 hour"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: 'Live scoring is active: valid coupons award flat points, scores are normalized
  by the max, and weights are set on-chain hourly. Container scoring is stubbed (returns empty dict).
  When no miner weights are available the validator falls back to a single hardcoded weight (uid 207 =
  1.0, the subnet owner), but this is a degenerate fallback, not a standing burn.'
subnet:
  name: BitKoop
  owner_repo: BitKoopLabs__BitKoop
  netuid: 16
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners find and submit working discount coupon codes for online stores. Each submitted coupon
    is validated (via first-party coupon APIs / TLS Notary proofs) and, if it works, marked VALID. A miner
    is rewarded a flat amount of points per valid coupon; the normalized share of points across miners
    becomes the on-chain weight.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  submission_format_other: ''
scoring_signals:
- name: valid_coupon_points
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.8
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Each valid coupon awards a flat 100 points (calculate_coupon_points returns 100). Per-miner coupon
    points are multiplied by coupon_weight (0.8). Coupons must have status VALID and not be deleted to
    count.
- name: container_points
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.2
  normalization: min_max
  notes: 'Container scoring is multiplied by container_weight (0.2) in the combined score, but get_container_points()
    is stubbed and returns {} (TODO: implement actual container scoring logic), so this signal currently
    contributes nothing.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: other
  kind_other: first_party_coupon_api
  description: Coupon validity is determined by calling first-party coupon-check APIs (e.g. Shopify store
    coupon-check endpoints); a coupon is treated as valid only if the API reports it applicable (ok=true
    and applicable=true).
  source_identifier: store coupon-check API (api_url)
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Schema enum has no value for a live first-party API check; used 'other' with kind_other.
- kind: commit_reveal
  description: TLS Notary (TLSN) cryptographic proofs of real HTTP exchanges, judged by a local verifier,
    substantiate coupon claims without a browser.
  source_identifier: tlsn-http-verifier (tlsn_verifier_url default http://127.0.0.1:8080/verify)
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Used as proof-of-claim rather than a classic commit-reveal; mapped to nearest enum value.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: min_max
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'No standing burn. Degenerate fallback only: if there are no node weights to set, the validator
      assigns weight 1.0 to hardcoded uid 207 (subnet owner).'
  notes: total_points[miner] = coupon_weight * coupon_pts + container_weight * container_pts. score =
    min(1.0, round(total_points / max_points, 4)) where max_points is the highest total over all miners.
    If max_points == 0 all scores are 0.0.
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: set_weights computes normalized scores via WeightCalculatorService.calculate_weights(), maps
    miner hotkeys to metagraph node ids, and calls weights.set_node_weights. Runs at most once per set_weights_interval
    (skips if last set was within the interval).
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 1 hour
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of the final weights; each cycle recomputes scores from the current set
      of valid coupons.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: deduplicate_coupons_by_site groups coupons by (site_id, code) and keeps only the earliest
    one for each duplicate, so the same code on the same site cannot be double-counted across miners.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: rejection
  description: max_coupons_per_site_per_miner = 8 caps active codes per miner per site; each website has
    a limited number of shared coupon slots (typically 15), first come first served. A submit_window (2
    minutes) gates submissions.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Coupons are validated against first-party coupon APIs and/or TLSN proofs; only coupons
    the validator confirms working become VALID and earn points. Stale VALID coupons are rechecked (recheck_interval
    / delta_points 7 days) and flipped to INVALID if they stop working.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - cumulative_score
  - cooldown
  status_values:
  - INVALID
  - VALID
  - PENDING
  - EXPIRED
  - USED
  - DELETED
  - DUPLICATE
  reset_policy: Coupon points are recomputed each cycle from current VALID coupons (no persistent cumulative
    ledger of weight). INVALID coupons can be rechecked after recheck_interval (1 day) cooldown, moving
    them back to PENDING.
  notes: State is per-coupon (CouponStatus enum) rather than per-miner; miner score is the aggregate over
    their currently-VALID coupons.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/validating.md
  - docs/mining.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: subnet_validator/constants.py
    line_ref: 29-32
    quote: 'NETWORK_TO_NETUID = { constants.FINNEY_NETWORK: 16, constants.FINNEY_TEST_NETWORK: 368, }'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: subnet_validator/services/weight_calculator_service.py
    line_ref: '29'
    quote: 'class WeightCalculatorService:'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '14'
    quote: BitKoop's discount codes are sourced directly from its network of incentivized miners ... Every
      coupon is tested, verified, and scored by independent validators
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: subnet_validator/settings.py
    line_ref: '32'
    quote: 'coupon_weight: float = 0.8'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: subnet_validator/services/weight_calculator_service.py
    line_ref: 53-57
    quote: 'def calculate_coupon_points(self, coupon: Coupon) -> int: """Award a flat 100 points per valid
      coupon.""" return 100'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/weight
    source_path: subnet_validator/settings.py
    line_ref: '33'
    quote: 'container_weight: float = 0.2'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: subnet_validator/services/weight_calculator_service.py
    line_ref: 136-142
    quote: 'Stubbed container points calculation. TODO: Implement actual container scoring logic ... return
      {}'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description
    source_path: docs/validating.md
    line_ref: 159-161
    quote: A coupon is treated as valid only if the API reports it as applicable (e.g., ok=true and applicable=true).
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/1/description
    source_path: docs/validating.md
    line_ref: 167-171
    quote: TLS Notary (TLSN) provides cryptographic proofs of HTTP exchanges without running a browser.
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: subnet_validator/services/weight_calculator_service.py
    line_ref: 150-178
    quote: score = min(1.0, round((0.8 * coupon_points + 0.2 * container_points) / MAX_POINTS, 4)) ...
      score = min(1.0, round(total_points[miner] / max_points, 4))
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: subnet_validator/services/weight_calculator_service.py
    line_ref: 164-178
    quote: total_points[miner] = ( self.coupon_weight * coupon_pts + self.container_weight * container_pts
      ) ... score = min(1.0, round(total_points[miner] / max_points, 4))
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: subnet_validator/tasks/set_weights.py
    line_ref: 131-138
    quote: 'No node weights to set, fallback to hardcoded weights to subnet owner ... node_id_to_weight
      = { 207: 1.0, }'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: subnet_validator/tasks/set_weights.py
    line_ref: 140-150
    quote: result = weights.set_node_weights( substrate=metagraph.substrate, keypair=keypair, node_ids=...,
      node_weights=..., netuid=settings.netuid, ... )
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: subnet_validator/settings.py
    line_ref: '29'
    quote: 'set_weights_interval: timedelta = timedelta(hours=1)  # Run every hour'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/cadence
    source_path: subnet_validator/tasks/set_weights.py
    line_ref: 60-68
    quote: 'if ( last_set_weights_time > time.time() - settings.set_weights_interval.total_seconds() ):
      logger.debug("Skipping weight calculation because it was already run recently")'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: subnet_validator/services/weight_calculator_service.py
    line_ref: 78-79
    quote: Group coupons by site and code, keeping only the earliest one for each duplicate.
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: subnet_validator/settings.py
    line_ref: 18-20
    quote: 'max_coupons_per_site_per_miner: int = ( 8  # Maximum coupons per miner per site )'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 75-76
    quote: Success sets status to VALID; failure sets to INVALID ... Another task rechecks VALID coupons
      whose last_checked_at is older than ~1 day, potentially flipping them to INVALID
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/status_values
    source_path: subnet_validator/constants.py
    line_ref: 7-14
    quote: 'class CouponStatus(enum.IntEnum): INVALID = 0 VALID = 1 PENDING = 2 EXPIRED = 3 USED = 4 DELETED
      = 5 DUPLICATE = 6'
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: subnet_validator/settings.py
    line_ref: '21'
    quote: 'recheck_interval: timedelta = timedelta(days=1)'
  - claim_field: /documentation/primary_docs
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 65-69
    quote: '## Scoring Formula - Points: - Each valid coupon: 100 points - Combined score = coupon_weight
      * coupon_points - Normalization: min(1.0, round(points / MAX, 4))'
  unresolved:
  - Exact container scoring logic (signal stubbed, not implemented).
  - min_weight_stake (1000.0) role in weight setting not traced to a use site.
  - Whether the hardcoded fallback uid 207 corresponds to a sustained burn in practice.
composition:
  shape: pipeline
  overlays:
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: GAS — Generative Adversarial Subnet (BitMind)
docs.text:
Subnet BitMind-AI__bitmind-subnet netuid 34 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Two parallel tracks. Discriminative miners submit AI-generated-content detection models (image, video, audio), evaluated on cloud infrastructure. Generative miners run a server that produces synthetic media on demand; they are rewarded by a base reward for content that passes validator verification DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, RealEngagement"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: LivenessCheck, Deduplication, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: win_rate"]
  M2["Metric: composite"]
  M3["Metric: accuracy"]
  M4["Metric: accuracy"]
  M5["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  G --> M5
  GT --> M5
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  M5 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: gasGenerativeAdversarialSubnetBitMind

    netuid: 34
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: [model_weights, compute_service, content_link]

    @burn {
        uid: "5HjBSeeoz52CLfvDWDkzupqrYLHz1oToDPHjdmJjc4TF68LQ"
        fraction: 0.6
    }
    @guards {
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, other }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: win_rate
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            RealEngagement {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: multiplicative
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.5
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: 360
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: The live validator hardcodes a fixed weight budget that sends 60% of emission
  to the burn UID (burn_pct = .6) and only 20% to generator miners plus 20% to a video escrow hotkey;
  image and audio escrow allocations are set to 0.0. Discriminator rewards (get_discriminator_rewards
  / sn34_score) are documented but are NOT computed in the validator's update_scores path — only generator
  base_reward * fool-multiplier scores flow on-chain, with discriminator emission delivered to escrow
  hotkeys rather than per-UID scoring. Hence partial_burn with a largely dormant discriminator scoring
  path.
subnet:
  name: GAS — Generative Adversarial Subnet (BitMind)
  owner_repo: BitMind-AI__bitmind-subnet
  netuid: 34
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Two parallel tracks. Discriminative miners submit AI-generated-content detection models (image,
    video, audio), evaluated on cloud infrastructure. Generative miners run a server that produces synthetic
    media on demand; they are rewarded by a base reward for content that passes validator verification
    checks, multiplied by an adversarial "fool rate" multiplier reflecting how often their content fools
    discriminators in benchmark runs.
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
  - compute_service
  - content_link
scoring_signals:
- name: generator_pass_rate
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: pass_rate from verification stats; base reward = pass_rate * volume taper (min(verified,10) +
    log2 of overflow). Combined multiplicatively with the fool multiplier, so no additive weight.
- name: generator_fool_rate
  metric_kind: win_rate
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: fool_rate = total_fooled / (total_fooled + total_not_fooled), scaled by a log sample-size multiplier
    (capped at 2.0) and clamped to [0, 2.0]. Multiplies the generator base reward.
- name: generator_model_tier_multiplier
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: sqrt(model_price / baseline_price) averaged over a miner's verified submissions; rewards use
    of higher-cost generation models. Multiplies the base reward.
- name: discriminator_binary_mcc
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.75
  normalization: none
  notes: binary_score_weight default 0.75 (config scoring.binary-weight). Part of get_discriminator_rewards,
    which is NOT invoked in the live update_scores path.
- name: discriminator_multiclass_mcc
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.25
  normalization: none
  notes: multiclass_score_weight default 0.25 (config scoring.multiclass-weight). Part of get_discriminator_rewards,
    not invoked in the live weight-setting path.
- name: sn34_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Documented discriminator metric: geometric mean of normalized MCC and normalized Brier score,
    sqrt(MCC_norm * Brier_norm) with alpha=1.2, beta=1.8. Described only in docs/Incentive.md; not present
    in the repo''s reward code (computed in external cloud benchmark / gasbench).'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Benchmark datasets per modality (image/video/audio) mixing real, synthetic and semi-synthetic
    content, including public gasbench datasets plus non-public holdout datasets refreshed each round.
  source_identifier: gasbench image/video/audio_datasets.yaml + holdout datasets
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Datasets refresh weekly; holdout data mitigates overfitting.
- kind: real_engagement
  description: 'Adversarial fool counts: whether generator-submitted content fooled discriminator models
    in benchmark runs (fooled_count / not_fooled_count from the benchmark API).'
  source_identifier: benchmark API (https://gas.bitmind.ai), get_benchmark_results
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: ''
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: l1
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: 5HjBSeeoz52CLfvDWDkzupqrYLHz1oToDPHjdmJjc4TF68LQ
    fraction: 0.6
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'Hardcoded burn_pct = .6 in validator.set_weights. Remaining budget: video_pct=.2, image_pct=.0,
      audio_pct=.0, generator_pct=.2. Video/image/audio allocations go to fixed escrow hotkeys; only the
      generator_pct portion is distributed per-UID by L1 normalization of EMA scores.'
  notes: Generator reward = base_reward * fool_multiplier. Per-UID scores are L1-normalized (np.linalg.norm
    ord=1) over active generator UIDs then scaled by generator_pct (.2). Burn and escrow UIDs receive
    fixed fractions.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: 'on_block_interval(epoch_length): update_scores() computes EMA of generator rewards, then set_weights
    builds a fixed budget (burn .6 / video .2 / image .0 / audio .0 / generator .2), L1-normalizes generator
    scores, assigns escrow/burn UIDs their fixed fractions, and calls subtensor.set_weights.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 360
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.5
    window: null
    notes: 'update_scores: self.scores = alpha * reward_arr + (1 - alpha) * self.scores with alpha = 0.5
      (hardcoded). Config also exposes scoring.moving-average-alpha default 0.05, but that value is not
      used in this generator-scoring EMA path. epoch_length default 360 (12s blocks).'
anti_gaming:
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: Generators not seen within max_inactive_hours (24h) are filtered to 0 reward, and update_scores
    hard-zeroes EMA scores for hotkeys not in the liveness set.
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: 'Duplicate detection on generated media: CLIP embedding cosine-similarity threshold 0.96
    and global perceptual-hash Hamming threshold 4 (verification.* config).'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: 'Temporal tampering check on videos: perceptual-hash frame-jump Hamming threshold 30, flag
    tampered when fraction of jump frames exceeds 0.10.'
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: 'Volume taper on base reward: first 10 verified samples count linearly, beyond 10 log2
    takes over so high-throughput miners cannot farm volume indefinitely.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: discriminative_mining
    role: main
    advancement_rule: 'Winner-take-all per modality per round: highest sn34_score discriminator per modality
      (image/video/audio) wins the round''s alpha reward (per docs/Incentive.md).'
    multipliers: {}
  - name: generative_mining
    role: main
    advancement_rule: 'Continuous per-epoch scoring: base reward (pass rate x volume x model tier) times
      fool-rate multiplier.'
    multipliers: {}
  count: 2
  notes: Two parallel tracks (discriminative + generative) across three modalities (image/video/audio).
    Discriminator track is documented as per-round winner-take-all but its scoring is not exercised in
    the validator's on-chain weight path.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - other
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: EMA score persisted to scores.npy across restarts; hard-reset to 0 when a generator hotkey
    falls outside the 24h liveness window.
  notes: self.scores numpy vector, EMA-updated, saved/loaded with one backup. The 'other' state kind is
    the 24h liveness window that gates whether a generator's EMA score is retained or hard-zeroed.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: SN34 (mainnet netuid 34)
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/Incentive.md
  - docs/Validating.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  confidence_overall: medium
  extracted_by: im-extract / Claude Opus 4.8
  extractor_notes: Live on-chain weight path (validator.set_weights + update_scores) only scores generators;
    discriminator rewards (get_discriminator_rewards, sn34_score) are documented and present in rewards.py
    but not wired into update_scores, and discriminator emission is delivered to fixed escrow hotkeys.
    60% burn is hardcoded.
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: gas/config.py
    line_ref: '5'
    quote: MAINNET_UID = 34
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 4-5
    quote: GAS<br><small>Generative Adversarial Subnet</small> ... Bittensor SN34
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 31-35
    quote: Discriminative Mining | Submit AI-generated content detection models ... Generative Mining
      | Run a server that generates synthetic media on demand | Base reward for valid content × multiplier
      for fooling discriminators
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: gas/evaluation/rewards.py
    line_ref: 246-247
    quote: "volume = min(verified_count, 10) + max(0.0, math.log2(max(1, verified_count - 9)))\n     \
      \       base_reward = pass_rate * volume"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: gas/evaluation/rewards.py
    line_ref: 356-372
    quote: "fool_rate = total_fooled / total_count ... base_reward = fool_rate * sample_size_multiplier\n\
      \                    rewards[uid] = max(0, min(2.0, base_reward))"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: gas/evaluation/rewards.py
    line_ref: 118-121
    quote: "total += math.sqrt(price / _GENERATOR_BASELINE_PRICE)\n    return total / len(model_names)"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/3/weight
    source_path: gas/config.py
    line_ref: 356-360
    quote: --scoring.binary-weight ... default=0.75
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/4/weight
    source_path: gas/config.py
    line_ref: 363-368
    quote: --scoring.multiclass-weight ... default=0.25
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/5/notes
    source_path: docs/Incentive.md
    line_ref: 121-129
    quote: sn34_{score} = \sqrt{MCC_{norm} \cdot Brier_{norm}} ... With default parameters \alpha = 1.2
      and \beta = 1.8
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction
    source_path: neurons/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 207-211
    quote: "burn_pct      = .6\n                video_pct     = .2\n                image_pct     = .0\n\
      \                audio_pct     = .0\n                generator_pct = .2"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: neurons/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 46-47
    quote: '"burn": "5HjBSeeoz52CLfvDWDkzupqrYLHz1oToDPHjdmJjc4TF68LQ"'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization
    source_path: neurons/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: '240'
    quote: norm[active_uids] = np.linalg.norm(self.scores[active_uids], ord=1)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/composition
    source_path: neurons/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 312-316
    quote: "rewards = {\n            uid: generator_base_rewards.get(uid, 0)\n            * reward_multipliers.get(uid,\
      \ DEFAULT_FOOL_RATE_FALLBACK)"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/cadence
    source_path: neurons/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 175-176
    quote: "@on_block_interval(\"epoch_length\")\n    async def set_weights(self, block):"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: gas/config.py
    line_ref: 47-52
    quote: --epoch-length ... how often we set weights, measured in 12 second blocks ... default=360
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: gas/utils/metagraph.py
    line_ref: 58-67
    quote: "result, message = subtensor.set_weights(\n                wallet=wallet,\n               \
      \ netuid=netuid,\n                uids=processed_weight_uids,"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/alpha
    source_path: neurons/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 337-338
    quote: "alpha = 0.5\n        self.scores = alpha * reward_arr + (1 - alpha) * self.scores"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: neurons/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 292-294
    quote: 'max_inactive_hours = 24  # Hours of inactivity before generator rewards are zeroed'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: gas/config.py
    line_ref: 454-466
    quote: --verification.embedding-dup-threshold ... default=0.96 ... --verification.global-dup-hamming-threshold
      ... default=4
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: gas/config.py
    line_ref: 440-452
    quote: --verification.temporal-phash-jump-threshold ... default=30 ... --verification.temporal-tamper-ratio
      ... default=0.10
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/3/description
    source_path: gas/evaluation/rewards.py
    line_ref: 244-247
    quote: "First 10 count linearly; beyond 10, log₂ takes over so high-\n            # throughput miners\
      \ still see gains but can't farm volume indefinitely."
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/stages/0/advancement_rule
    source_path: docs/Incentive.md
    line_ref: 146-150
    quote: Each round is winner-take-all -- only the top-scoring discriminator for each modality receives
      the alpha reward for that round.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: neurons/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 342-351
    quote: "if hotkey not in generator_liveness and self.scores[uid] > 0:\n                        self.scores[uid]\
      \ = 0"
    confidence: high
  unresolved:
  - sn34_score alpha=1.2/beta=1.8 and the Brier-based discriminator scoring are documented only; the computation
    lives in external gasbench/cloud, not in this repo.
  - get_discriminator_rewards exists in rewards.py but is not invoked in validator update_scores; whether/where
    it is used on-chain is unresolved from this repo.
  - Per-modality escrow distribution (video_escrow/image_escrow/audio_escrow) maps modality emission to
    fixed hotkeys; the downstream split among discriminator miners is not in this repo.
  - scoring.moving-average-alpha default 0.05 in config is unused in the generator EMA path (which hardcodes
    0.5); its intended use is unresolved.
composition:
  shape: multiplex
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Bitsec Subnet v2 (Security Agent)
docs.text:
Subnet Bitsec-AI__sandbox netuid None Archetype gated Language python Mechanism status mixed Task A miner submits a security-audit agent (an agent.py program) that, given a smart-contract project's source files, produces a list of vulnerability findings (title, description, severity, type). The central platform executes each submitted agent over benchmark projects and scores its findings against DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CodeInspection"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: composite"]
  M1["Metric: accuracy"]
  M2["Metric: accuracy"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: bitsecSubnetV2SecurityAgent

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: source_code

    @burn {
        uid: "burn hotkey from platform top-with-burn payload"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }

    Gated {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: oracle
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: gated
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default; ~12s blocks)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: On-chain weight-setting does NOT score miners locally. The validator fetches a
  "top-with-burn" payload from the central bitsec.ai platform and assigns ALL emission to a single selected
  top-agent hotkey plus a burn hotkey, split by burn_fraction (default burn percentage 100 => full burn
  when no agents are returned). The actual competitive scoring (ScaBench security-finding F1 scorer in
  validator/scorer.py) runs off-chain on the platform; the in-repo template reward.py is unused dummy
  boilerplate.
subnet:
  name: Bitsec Subnet v2 (Security Agent)
  owner_repo: Bitsec-AI__sandbox
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: A miner submits a security-audit agent (an agent.py program) that, given a smart-contract project's
    source files, produces a list of vulnerability findings (title, description, severity, type). The
    central platform executes each submitted agent over benchmark projects and scores its findings against
    an expected vulnerability set; the best-performing agent is selected as the "top agent" and receives
    the non-burned emission fraction.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: finding_match_f1
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: validator/scorer.py (ScaBenchScorerV2) computes detection_rate (recall), precision, and f1_score
    from true/false positives/negatives of matched findings. Matching of an expected vulnerability to
    a tool finding is decided by an LLM judge (MiniMax-M2.5-TEE via the Chutes inference proxy), with
    a default confidence_threshold of 0.75 (or strict no-confidence mode). This scoring runs off-chain
    on the platform; it does not appear in the on-chain weight-setting path.
- name: detection_rate
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: detection_rate = true_positives / total_expected (recall), one of the ScoringResult fields produced
    by score_project.
- name: precision
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: precision = true_positives / (true_positives + false_positives), a ScoringResult field.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: A benchmark JSON file mapping project_id -> expected vulnerabilities (title, description,
    severity, type). Tool/agent findings are matched one-by-one against these expected entries.
  source_identifier: ScaBench benchmark JSON (validator/scorer.py --benchmark)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Loaded from a benchmark file; expected_findings = entry['vulnerabilities'].
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: The expected<->found match decision itself is delegated to an LLM judge over the Chutes
    inference proxy; it returns found/decision + confidence used to count true positives.
  source_identifier: MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2.5-TEE via {api_url}/inference
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: system='You are a precise vulnerability matcher. Be strict.'
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: gated
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: burn hotkey from platform top-with-burn payload
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: burn_fraction = clamp(burn.percentage/100, 0, 1), default percentage 100. The burn hotkey's
      UID receives burn_fraction; the first matching agent hotkey receives agent_fraction = 1 - burn_fraction.
      When no agents are present or percentage is 100, the result is a full burn.
  notes: split_top_agent_scores zeroes a metagraph-sized vector then adds burn_fraction to the burn UID
    and agent_fraction to a single selected agent UID. set_weights then L1-normalizes self.scores (raw_weights
    = scores / ||scores||_1). Only one agent hotkey is ever credited (loop breaks on first match), so
    it is effectively winner-take-all among miners.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Forward loop calls update_top_miner_scores() (fetches platform top-with-burn payload and overwrites
    self.scores via split_top_agent_scores). On sync(), should_set_weights() fires once block - last_update
    > epoch_length, then set_weights() L1-normalizes scores and emits.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default; ~12s blocks)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: The base BaseValidatorNeuron.update_scores implements an EMA with neuron.moving_average_alpha
      default 0.1, but the live path (update_top_miner_scores) assigns self.scores = new_scores directly
      and bypasses that EMA.
anti_gaming:
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: README describes the platform /upload/agent endpoint validating that the uploaded agent.py
    is Python and uses only stdlib (a simple regex check) before storing it.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: 'Single competition: agents are ranked by their off-chain score and one top agent is selected.
    No tiered/ladder structure is present in code.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: resync_metagraph zeroes scores for replaced hotkeys, but no persistent per-miner ledger or status
    enum is maintained; live scores are overwritten each forward from the platform payload.
documentation:
  status: sparse
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/bitsec.ai (external)
  completeness: low
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '3'
    quote: '# **Bitsec Subnet v2**'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 25-32
    quote: import bittensor as bt ... from validator.manager import SandboxManager
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 26-34
    quote: execute-agent Run the miner agent script locally on a single project. ... Agent file is in
      miner/agent.py
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '50'
    quote: 'Endpoint: /upload/agent (POST) – Accepts a single agent.py file upload.'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/name
    source_path: validator/scorer.py
    line_ref: 722-725
    quote: f1_score = ((2 * precision * detection_rate / (precision + detection_rate)) ...
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind
    source_path: validator/scorer.py
    line_ref: 266-272
    quote: 'Check if an expected vulnerability exists in the tool findings. Returns: (found_match, matched_finding,
      justification, confidence, decision)'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/name
    source_path: validator/scorer.py
    line_ref: '719'
    quote: detection_rate = (true_positives / len(expected_findings)) if expected_findings else 0.0
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/name
    source_path: validator/scorer.py
    line_ref: 720-722
    quote: precision = ((true_positives / (true_positives + false_positives)) if (true_positives + false_positives)
      > 0 else 0.0)
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: validator/scorer.py
    line_ref: 75-79
    quote: self.confidence_threshold = self.config.get("confidence_threshold", 0.75) ... self.model_id
      = self.config.get("model", CHUTES_MODELS)
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: validator/scorer.py
    line_ref: 811-813
    quote: 'with open(args.benchmark, "r") as f: benchmark = json.load(f)'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description
    source_path: validator/scorer.py
    line_ref: 868-872
    quote: 'if entry.get("project_id") == project_id or entry.get("id") == project_id: expected_findings
      = entry.get("vulnerabilities", [])'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/1/source_identifier
    source_path: validator/scorer.py
    line_ref: 62-65
    quote: MODELS = [ "MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2.5-TEE", ] CHUTES_MODELS = f"{','.join(MODELS)}:throughput"
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/1/notes
    source_path: validator/scorer.py
    line_ref: '377'
    quote: system = "You are a precise vulnerability matcher. Be strict."
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: validator/top_agents.py
    line_ref: 32-44
    quote: 'for agent in agent_entries: ... new_scores[agent_uid] += agent_fraction; selected_agent_hotkey
      = agent_hotkey; break'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: validator/top_agents.py
    line_ref: 16-31
    quote: burn_percentage = burn_entry.get("percentage", 100) ... burn_fraction = min(max(float(burn_percentage)
      / 100.0, 0.0), 1.0) ... new_scores[burn_uid] += burn_fraction
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: validator/platform_client.py
    line_ref: 158-161
    quote: 'def get_top_agents(self): endpoint = "agents/top-with-burn/"'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization
    source_path: template/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 241-248
    quote: norm = np.linalg.norm(self.scores, ord=1, axis=0, keepdims=True) ... raw_weights = self.scores
      / norm
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: template/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 44-45
    quote: await loop.run_in_executor(None, self.update_top_miner_scores)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: template/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 277-285
    quote: result, msg = self.subtensor.set_weights(wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.config.netuid, uids=uint_uids,
      weights=uint_weights, ...)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: template/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 84-88
    quote: '"--neuron.epoch_length", ... help="The default epoch length (how often we set weights, measured
      in 12 second blocks).", default=100'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/cadence
    source_path: template/base/neuron.py
    line_ref: 164-169
    quote: return ((self.block - self.metagraph.last_update[self.uid]) > self.config.neuron.epoch_length
      and self.neuron_type != "MinerNeuron")
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes
    source_path: template/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 366-369
    quote: 'alpha: float = self.config.neuron.moving_average_alpha; self.scores = (alpha * scattered_rewards
      + (1 - alpha) * self.scores)'
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 75-89
    quote: new_scores, selected_agent_hotkey, burn_hotkey, burn_fraction = split_top_agent_scores(...)
      ... self.scores = new_scores
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '50'
    quote: Validate it's Python, uses only stdlib (simple regex check). Store in local folder with metadata
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/notes
    source_path: template/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 308-311
    quote: 'for uid, hotkey in enumerate(self.hotkeys): if hotkey != self.metagraph.hotkeys[uid]: self.scores[uid]
      = 0'
  - claim_field: /documentation/status
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '24'
    quote: Refer to the [Bitsec docs](https://docs.bitsec.ai) for the latest documentation.
  unresolved:
  - subnet.netuid — not pinned in repo (config default netuid=1 is the template placeholder, not the production
    Bitsec netuid).
  - Exact burn.percentage value in production — only the default 100 and the clamp formula are in code;
    the live value comes from the platform payload.
  - How the platform ranks/orders agent_entries before returning them (the off-chain selection of which
    agent is 'top') is not in this repo.
  - Whether the off-chain ScaBench F1 score is the sole basis for top-agent selection vs. other platform-side
    criteria.
composition:
  shape: gated
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Bitsec Subnet
docs.text:
Subnet Bitsec-AI__subnet netuid 60 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status unknown Task Miners receive a Solidity smart-contract code sample (a "challenge") and return a PredictionResponse: a boolean indicating whether vulnerabilities were found, plus a list of detected vulnerabilities each carrying a category, severity, line ranges, description, and fix/exploit code. Validators genera DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: similarity"]
  AGG["aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: bitsecSubnet

    netuid: 60
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: signals

    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: oracle
            }
        ]
        aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma {
            normalization: sum_to_one
            decay_rate: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "neuron.epoch_length default 100 (12-second blocks); validator main loop sleeps 600s per step"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
subnet:
  name: Bitsec Subnet
  owner_repo: Bitsec-AI__subnet
  netuid: 60
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners receive a Solidity smart-contract code sample (a "challenge") and return a PredictionResponse:
    a boolean indicating whether vulnerabilities were found, plus a list of detected vulnerabilities each
    carrying a category, severity, line ranges, description, and fix/exploit code. Validators generate
    challenges that are either secure (clean code) or vulnerable (an LLM injects a labeled vulnerability
    into clean code). Miners are rewarded for correctly predicting the vulnerable/secure label and for
    matching the expected vulnerability categories.'
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: prediction_match
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: reward() returns 0.0 if the miner's boolean `prediction` does not equal the expected prediction;
    this gates all further scoring.
- name: vulnerability_category_jaccard
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Jaccard score (intersection over union) of the SET of vulnerability `category` values between
    the expected response and the miner response. Exact-equality of the full vulnerability lists returns
    1.0; empty category union returns 1.0. Line ranges and descriptions are TODO/unused.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Clean Solidity codebases (samples/clean-codebases/*.sol) and labeled vulnerability descriptions
    (samples/vulnerabilities/*.md) shipped in the repo. A challenge is built by randomly picking a clean
    .sol file and, with ~80% probability, using an LLM to inject a randomly chosen labeled vulnerability
    into it; the injected vulnerability's PredictionResponse is the ground-truth `expected_response`.
  source_identifier: samples/clean-codebases/*.sol ; samples/vulnerabilities/*.md
  trust_model: trusted
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: The vulnerable challenge code and its expected vulnerability labels are produced by an
    LLM (chat_completion) that injects a vulnerability into clean code, returning NewlyVulnerableCode
    {code, vulnerability_info}. The LLM-generated vulnerability_info serves as ground truth.
  source_identifier: bitsec/utils/data.py:create_challenge_with_inputs
  trust_model: oracle
aggregation:
  method: exponential_decay_ema
  normalization: sum_to_one
  decay_rate: 0.1
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    notes: ''
  notes: 'Per-step rewards (np.array of reward() values) are folded into self.scores via EMA: scores =
    alpha*rewards + (1-alpha)*scores with alpha = moving_average_alpha (default 0.1). At weight-setting
    time scores are L1-normalized (raw_weights = scores / ||scores||_1) before process_weights_for_netuid
    and conversion to uint16.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: L1-normalize EMA scores, run process_weights_for_netuid, convert to uint16 uids/weights, then
    subtensor.set_weights with version_key=spec_version, wait_for_inclusion on first set.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: neuron.epoch_length default 100 (12-second blocks); validator main loop sleeps 600s
    per step
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Boolean prediction gate: reward() returns 0.0 whenever the miner''s `prediction` flag
    does not match the expected vulnerable/secure label, so guessing categories without matching the label
    yields zero reward.'
- kind: other
  kind_other: data_augmentation_noise
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'After LLM injection, challenge code is passed through add_comment_noise_simple, which
    prepends a misleading "// TODO: Fix security vulnerability" comment as noise to make the challenge
    harder. forward() docstring also references random data augmentation.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Per-uid EMA score (self.scores) persists across steps and is saved/loaded from state.npz.
    A uid's score is zeroed when its hotkey is replaced in the metagraph (resync_metagraph).
  notes: Validator also tracks a seen_miners set, but only for logging new-connection notices, not for
    scoring.
documentation:
  status: code_authoritative
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: low
provenance:
  confidence_overall: medium
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  extractor_notes: 'reward.py contains an unreachable second block after the first `return score` (lines
    92-132): a 1-5 integer LLM-judge scoring scheme and a more detailed tuple return. It is dead code
    in the current control flow — the live reward path is the Jaccard category score only. Recorded here
    because it documents intended (but inactive) LLM-judging plagiarism/similarity controls.'
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: bitsec/utils/config.py
    line_ref: '71'
    quote: parser.add_argument("--netuid", type=int, help="Subnet netuid", default=60)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '1'
    quote: Bitsec Subnet (Bittensor mainnet 60 / testnet 209)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: bitsec/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 20-22
    quote: 'import numpy as np

      from typing import List

      import bittensor as bt'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: bitsec/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 38-47
    quote: 'Get a code sample. 50/50 chance of: A. SECURE (label = 0): No vulnerability injected. B. VULNERABLE
      (label = 1): Inject a vulnerability into the code.'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: bitsec/protocol.py
    line_ref: 199-209
    quote: 'class PredictionResponse(pydantic.BaseModel): ... prediction: bool ... vulnerabilities: List[Vulnerability]'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: bitsec/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 57-58
    quote: "if response.prediction != expected_response.prediction:\n        return 0.0"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/metric_kind
    source_path: bitsec/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 35-36
    quote: "# Jaccard score of the vulnerabilities\n    score = jaccard_score(expected_response, response)"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: bitsec/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 78-85
    quote: "category_intersection = category_expected.intersection(category_response)\n    category_union\
      \ = category_expected.union(category_response) ... score = len(category_intersection) / len(category_union)"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description
    source_path: bitsec/utils/data.py
    line_ref: 209-225
    quote: secure_filename = _get_random_filename(SECURE_CODE_DIR, ".sol") ... vulnerability_filename
      = _get_random_filename(VULNERABILITIES_DIR, ".md")
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: bitsec/utils/data.py
    line_ref: 13-15
    quote: 'SAMPLE_DIR = os.path.join(...)

      VULNERABILITIES_DIR = ''vulnerabilities''

      SECURE_CODE_DIR = ''clean-codebases'''
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/1/description
    source_path: bitsec/utils/data.py
    line_ref: 156-176
    quote: 'class NewlyVulnerableCode(pydantic.BaseModel): code: str; vulnerability_info: PredictionResponse
      ... response = chat_completion(prompt, ..., response_format=NewlyVulnerableCode)'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: bitsec/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 385-386
    quote: "alpha: float = self.config.neuron.moving_average_alpha\n        self.scores: np.ndarray =\
      \ alpha * scattered_rewards + (1 - alpha) * self.scores"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/decay_rate
    source_path: bitsec/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 215-220
    quote: '"--neuron.moving_average_alpha", ... help="Moving average alpha parameter, how much to add
      of the new observation.", default=0.1'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: bitsec/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 252-259
    quote: norm = np.linalg.norm(self.scores, ord=1, axis=0, keepdims=True) ... raw_weights = self.scores
      / norm
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: bitsec/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 288-296
    quote: result, msg = self.subtensor.set_weights(wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.config.netuid, uids=uint_uids,
      weights=uint_weights, ... version_key=self.spec_version,)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: bitsec/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 82-87
    quote: '"--neuron.epoch_length", ... help="The default epoch length (how often we set weights, measured
      in 12 second blocks).", default=100'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: bitsec/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 57-58
    quote: "if response.prediction != expected_response.prediction:\n        return 0.0"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: bitsec/utils/data.py
    line_ref: 189-190

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: bitsec/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 328-330
    quote: "for uid, hotkey in enumerate(self.hotkeys):\n            if hotkey != self.metagraph.hotkeys[uid]:\n\
      \                self.scores[uid] = 0  # hotkey has been replaced"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /documentation/status
    source_path: README.md

    quote: The subnet incentivizes miners to develop models and workflows to find and fix code vulnerabilities.
    confidence: medium

      \ response_format=Score)"

  unresolved:
  - 'min_weight_floor / burn allocation: no floor or burn fraction present in code.'
  - 'scoring_signal weights: reward() returns the raw Jaccard score directly; there is no explicit per-signal
    weighting constant to cite.'
  - 'source_version: no version tag found (__version__ = ''0.0.0'' is a template placeholder).'

    path.
composition:

  overlays:

  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Candles Subnet
docs.text:
Subnet CandlesTAO__candles netuid 31 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners predict cryptocurrency candle outcomes for the TAO-USD pair over hourly, daily, and weekly intervals: each prediction supplies a candle color (red/green = down/up), a closing price, and a confidence. Validators score the prediction once the interval closes against real market data fetched fro DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, Deduplication, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: regression_error"]
  M2["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: candlesSubnet

    netuid: 31
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: signals

    @burn {
        uid: "147"
        fraction: 0.8
    }
    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, cumulative_score, registration_age }

    Multiplex<multi_asset> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: regression_error
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: target_band
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: MarketData {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: none
            decay_rate: 0.032
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: continuous
            tempo: "INCENTIVE_SCORING_INTERVAL minutes (default 30)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_asset
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: _apply_hardcoded_weights() unconditionally assigns 80% of the weight vector to
  hardcoded UID 147 and spreads the remaining 20% proportionally among others on every set_weights call.
  The merit-based scoring therefore governs only ~20% of emission. UID 147 is not a known burn UID label
  in code, so this is captured as a fixed-fraction emission redirect (partial_burn).
subnet:
  name: Candles Subnet
  owner_repo: CandlesTAO__candles
  netuid: 31
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners predict cryptocurrency candle outcomes for the TAO-USD pair over hourly, daily, and
    weekly intervals: each prediction supplies a candle color (red/green = down/up), a closing price,
    and a confidence. Validators score the prediction once the interval closes against real market data
    fetched from CoinDesk. Rewards combine color accuracy (weighted 60%) and price proximity (weighted
    40%), modulated by the miner''s stated confidence.'
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: color_accuracy
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.6
  normalization: none
  notes: _calculate_color_score returns 1.0 if predicted candle color equals actual color, else 0.0. Combined
    into base_score with a 0.6 weight.
- name: price_proximity
  metric_kind: regression_error
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.4
  normalization: none
  notes: _calculate_price_score computes percentage_error = abs(pred-actual)/actual, then converts to
    a 0..1 score via exp(-percentage_error * 10). Combined into base_score with a 0.4 weight. Higher final
    score is better (lower error -> higher score).
- name: confidence_weight
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: target_band
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Confidence (default 0.5 if absent) modulates the base score: if base_score>0.5 the score is
    boosted by (confidence-0.5)*0.2, otherwise it is penalized by (confidence-0.5)*0.2. Acts as a multiplicative/gated
    adjustment, not an additive signal, hence weight null.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: Actual OHLC candle data for the closed interval, fetched per interval from the CoinDesk
    price API via PriceClient, used as the actual color and actual close price for scoring.
  source_identifier: CoinDesk OHLC API (provider=coindesk); symbol TAO-USD
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Requires COINDESK_API_KEY env var; validator raises if unset. actual_ohlc.color and actual_ohlc.close
    are the references for color and price scoring.
aggregation:
  method: exponential_decay_ema
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: 0.032
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '147'
    fraction: 0.8
    dynamic: false
    notes: _apply_hardcoded_weights forces target_weight_ratio=0.8 of the weight to target_uid=147; the
      remaining 0.2 is distributed proportionally to the merit-based raw weights among the other UIDs.
      Not labeled a burn address in code, but functions as a fixed emission redirect.
  notes: 'Per-prediction final_score = clip(base_score +/- (confidence-0.5)*0.2). Per miner, daily scores
    are averaged (total_score/prediction_count). Then _calculate_historical_decay_score aggregates up
    to 31 daily scores: today gets decay 1.0, older days get decay exp(-0.032*days_ago) (=0 past 31 days),
    summed and divided by the number of effective days. The composition is hybrid: weighted-sum of color/price
    + gated confidence + temporal decay aggregation.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: continuous
  method: Background asyncio task _incentive_scoring_and_set_weights_async runs every 60s; when the current
    UTC minute is divisible by INCENTIVE_SCORING_INTERVAL (default 30) it scores closed intervals, runs
    update_scores (EMA), then calls set_weights_async. A second background loop _periodic_weight_setting
    also checks should_set_weights every 60s. Raw weights are L1-normalized, IP-deduplicated, then 80%
    hardcoded to UID 147 before process_weights_for_netuid.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: INCENTIVE_SCORING_INTERVAL minutes (default 30)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: update_scores applies self.scores = alpha*scattered_rewards + (1-alpha)*self.scores, where
      alpha = config.neuron.moving_average_alpha (argparse default 0.1). This is the final-reward EMA,
      distinct from the within-aggregation 31-day decay (aggregation.decay_rate).
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'enforce_unique_miner_ip=True: _filter_uids_by_unique_ip keeps only the lowest UID per
    IP when querying, and _apply_ip_deduplication_to_weights zeroes weights of duplicate-IP miners (keeping
    the highest-weighted UID per IP). IP 0.0.0.0 is exempted.'
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Within an interval, PredictionBatchScorer tracks (prediction_id, miner_uid) keys and skips
    duplicate predictions from the same miner for the same prediction_id.
- kind: other
  kind_other: interval_id_whitelist
  enforcement: rejection
  description: parse_responses ignores any miner prediction whose interval_id was not in the validator's
    original request set (allowed_ids), preventing injection of unrequested intervals.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_asset
  stages: []
  count: 3
  notes: 'Three concurrent prediction horizons are requested and scored independently: hourly, daily,
    and weekly intervals (TimeInterval enum). Not separate reward ladders but parallel interval types
    whose scores are pooled per miner. Treated as multi_asset/multi-horizon; closest enum.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - cumulative_score
  - registration_age
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: On hotkey change for a UID, detect_new_hotkeys clears self.scores[uid]=0 and calls sqlite_storage.clear_miner_history
    for the old hotkey.
  notes: SQLite storage retains up to 31 days of historical daily scores per miner (get_historical_daily_scores,
    days=31) used for decay aggregation. _get_days_since_registration converts blocks-since-last-update
    to days (BLOCKS_PER_DAY=7200), capped 1..31, used as a fallback denominator when SQLite history is
    absent.
documentation:
  status: code_authoritative
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - validator.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 215-217
    quote: '| Network  | netuid |

      | mainnet  | 31     |

      | testnet  | 357    |'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '7'
    quote: Miners compete to predict cryptocurrency price movements (candle colors and values), while
      validators score predictions against real market data.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: candles/core/scoring/prediction_scorer.py
    line_ref: 126-127
    quote: "# Weight color prediction more heavily (60%) than price proximity (40%)\n        base_score\
      \ = (color_score * 0.6) + (price_score * 0.4)"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind
    source_path: candles/core/scoring/prediction_scorer.py
    line_ref: '86'
    quote: return 1.0 if predicted_color == actual_color else 0.0
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/normalization
    source_path: candles/core/scoring/prediction_scorer.py
    line_ref: 107-110
    quote: "percentage_error = abs(predicted_price - actual_price) / actual_price\n        # Convert to\
      \ score using exponential decay\n        score = math.exp(-percentage_error * 10)  # 10 is a scaling\
      \ factor"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: candles/core/scoring/prediction_scorer.py
    line_ref: 132-137
    quote: "if base_score > 0.5:\n            weighted_score = base_score + (confidence - 0.5) * 0.2\n\
      \        else:\n            weighted_score = base_score - (confidence - 0.5) * 0.2"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: candles/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 61-68
    quote: "api_key = os.getenv(\"COINDESK_API_KEY\")\n        ...\n        self.price_client = PriceClient(api_key=api_key,\
      \ provider=\"coindesk\")"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/kind
    source_path: candles/core/scoring/batch_scorer.py
    line_ref: 49-53
    quote: "actual_ohlc = (\n                    await self.prediction_scorer.price_client.get_price_by_interval(\n\
      \                        self.prediction_scorer.symbol, interval_id\n                    )\n   \
      \             )"
