docs.title: Casino TAO
docs.text:
Subnet Casino-TAO__casino-tao-subnet netuid None Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners place native-TAO bets on the TAO Casino smart contract deployed on Bittensor EVM, after linking their Bittensor coldkey to an EVM address. Validators query the contract for each miner's betting volume over a 7-day window, apply time-decay weights (most recent days weighted higher), and reward DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: volume"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: casinoTao

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: other

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        Other {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, other }

    Pipeline {
        score: Metric {
            kind: volume
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: MarketData {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "360 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: Documented mechanism rewards miners proportional to time-decayed betting volume
  on an EVM smart contract. When no miner has any betting volume, forward() explicitly runs a burn path
  that assigns the full reward (1.0) to UID 0. So live behavior is full-burn-to-UID-0 whenever betting
  activity is absent, and proportional volume scoring otherwise.
subnet:
  name: Casino TAO
  owner_repo: Casino-TAO__casino-tao-subnet
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners place native-TAO bets on the TAO Casino smart contract deployed on Bittensor EVM, after
    linking their Bittensor coldkey to an EVM address. Validators query the contract for each miner's
    betting volume over a 7-day window, apply time-decay weights (most recent days weighted higher), and
    reward each miner proportional to its share of total weighted volume. Miners produce no synapse response;
    the rewarded artifact is on-chain betting activity.
  submission_format:
  - other
  submission_format_other: on-chain betting volume on the TAO Casino EVM smart contract (no synapse response)
scoring_signals:
- name: time_decayed_betting_volume
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Per-miner weighted volume is each miner's betting volume over 7 days with daily decay weights
    [1.0, 0.85, 0.70, 0.55, 0.40, 0.25, 0.10] (today..6 days ago). Reward = miner's weighted volume /
    total weighted volume across all miners (volume share).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: TAO Casino smart contract on Bittensor EVM. Validator queries the contract via RPC for
    each miner's EVM address (linked from the miner's coldkey via a signed wallet-mapping) to obtain daily
    betting volumes.
  source_identifier: TAO Casino contract 0x1e0bA1BcF31feFf1a9dc16FE3E0A203E3439DB47 via https://lite.chain.opentensor.ai
    (chain id 964)
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: EVM address mapping is established via POST /api/wallet-mapping with coldkey signature verification.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: When active_count == 0 (no miner has betting volume), forward() sets reward_array[0] = 1.0
      ("running burn code"), assigning the entire weight to UID 0. Otherwise no burn; rewards are pure
      volume share.
  notes: Reward is computed in calculate_volume_rewards as raw_volumes / total_volume (volume share, sums
    to 1). The within-window time-decay weights are applied earlier (apply_time_decay / get_miner_volume),
    not as an aggregation decay_rate; aggregation itself is a plain proportional normalization. set_weights
    then L1-normalizes the EMA scores (raw_weights = scores / ||scores||_1).
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: EMA scores are L1-normalized (raw_weights = self.scores / norm where norm is the L1 norm), passed
    through process_weights_for_netuid, converted to uint16, and emitted via subtensor.set_weights with
    version_key = spec_version.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 360 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: update_scores applies scores = alpha * scattered_rewards + (1 - alpha) * scores with alpha
      = config.neuron.moving_average_alpha, default 0.1. Epoch/weight-commit interval is 360 blocks (~72
      min), default --neuron.epoch_length 360, WEIGHT_COMMIT_INTERVAL = 360.
anti_gaming:
- kind: other
  kind_other: coldkey-to-EVM wallet mapping requires cryptographic signature proof of coldkey ownership
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Wallet mappings (coldkey -> EVM address) require cryptographic proof of coldkey ownership
    (README Security: "Wallet mappings require cryptographic proof of coldkey ownership"), preventing
    a miner from claiming another participant''s betting volume.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - other
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: On hotkey replacement (resync_metagraph), scores[uid] is zeroed and miner_volumes, miner_daily_volumes,
    miner_evm_addresses for that UID are cleared.
  notes: 'self.scores is an EMA per UID (moving_average_alpha). miner_volumes and miner_daily_volumes
    hold the latest queried volumes; snapshots of scores+volumes are persisted to SQLite on each successful
    weight commit. The "other" state_kinds are: per-UID weighted betting volume, per-UID daily volume
    arrays, and per-UID coldkey-to-EVM address mapping.'
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/validator.md
  - docs/miner.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '20'
    quote: Miners earn rewards by placing bets on the TAO Casino smart contract, with rewards distributed
      based on time-decayed betting volume over a 7-day window.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind
    source_path: casinotao/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 61-62
    quote: "# Reward proportional to volume share\n    rewards = raw_volumes / total_volume"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: casinotao/core/const.py
    line_ref: 16-18
    quote: '# Time decay weights for 7 days (index 0 = today, index 6 = 6 days ago)

      # Most recent activity gets highest weight

      TIME_DECAY_WEIGHTS = [1.0, 0.85, 0.70, 0.55, 0.40, 0.25, 0.10]'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: casinotao/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 55-62
    quote: "total_volume = raw_volumes.sum()\n...\n    rewards = raw_volumes / total_volume"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/enabled
    source_path: casinotao/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 186-191
    quote: "bt.logging.info(\"No miners with betting volume - running burn code\")\n        reward_array\
      \ = np.zeros(self.metagraph.n, dtype=np.float32)\n        reward_array[0] = 1.0"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: casinotao/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: '189'
    quote: reward_array[0] = 1.0
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: casinotao/core/const.py
    line_ref: 12-14
    quote: 'CASINOTAO_CONTRACT_ADDRESS = "0x1e0bA1BcF31feFf1a9dc16FE3E0A203E3439DB47"

      BITTENSOR_EVM_RPC = "https://lite.chain.opentensor.ai"

      BITTENSOR_EVM_CHAIN_ID = 964'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: casinotao/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 283-290
    quote: "norm = np.linalg.norm(self.scores, ord=1, axis=0, keepdims=True)\n...\n        raw_weights\
      \ = self.scores / norm"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: casinotao/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 319-327
    quote: "result, msg = self.subtensor.set_weights(\n            wallet=self.wallet,\n            netuid=self.config.netuid,\n\
      \            uids=uint_uids,\n            weights=uint_weights,"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: casinotao/core/const.py
    line_ref: 23-24
    quote: '# Weight commit interval in blocks (360 blocks = ~72 minutes)

      WEIGHT_COMMIT_INTERVAL = 360'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/alpha
    source_path: casinotao/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 204-209
    quote: "\"--neuron.moving_average_alpha\",\n        type=float,\n        help=\"Moving average alpha\
      \ parameter, how much to add of the new observation.\",\n        default=0.1,"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/kind
    source_path: casinotao/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 431-434
    quote: "alpha: float = self.config.neuron.moving_average_alpha\n        self.scores: np.ndarray =\
      \ (\n            alpha * scattered_rewards + (1 - alpha) * self.scores\n        )"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '195'
    quote: '**Signature Verification**: Wallet mappings require cryptographic proof of coldkey ownership'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: casinotao/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 371-376
    quote: "self.scores[uid] = 0  # hotkey has been replaced\n                # Casino TAO: Also clear\
      \ volume data for replaced hotkeys\n                self.miner_volumes.pop(uid, None)"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/notes
    source_path: casinotao/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 53-54
    quote: "This queries the database for wallet mappings that were registered\n    via the POST /api/wallet-mapping\
      \ endpoint."
    confidence: high
  unresolved:
  - 'subnet.netuid: no fixed netuid in source (default --netuid 1 is a generic template default, not the
    Casino TAO assignment).'
  - 'ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier: get_miner_volume / contract.py (queried module) was not
    read in full; the exact 7-day daily-volume query method on-chain is inferred from forward.py and reward.py,
    not the contract client internals.'
  - Whether daily time-decay is applied in get_miner_volume (contract) or via apply_time_decay (reward.py)
    at runtime is ambiguous; apply_time_decay exists but forward.py uses the already-weighted volume returned
    by get_miner_volume.
composition:
  shape: pipeline
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: CheckerChain SN87 — AI-powered trustless crypto review platform (tRCM)
docs.text:
Subnet CheckerChain__checkerchain-subnet netuid 87 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners run an LLM to predict a product's Trust Score (0-100) plus a short review, quality keywords, and a sentiment for products fetched from the CheckerChain dApp. Validators compare each prediction against the platform-provided ground-truth trustScore: predictions deviating more than 10% earn 0, o DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: RealEngagement"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, Honeypot, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: regression_error"]
  M1["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  M2["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  M3["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: checkerChainSn87AiPoweredTrustlessCryptoReviewPlatformTRcm

    netuid: 87
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: signals

    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Honeypot {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, status_enum }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: regression_error
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: RealEngagement {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "epoch_length = 100 (12-second blocks); forward sleeps 25 min per cycle"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Validator computes per-miner rewards from an LLM analysis of each review (sentiment,
  keyword verification, coherence, score accuracy), keeps the top 90% of miners, EMA-blends into self.scores,
  and emits L1-normalized weights via set_weights. No burn / no-op path observed in the weight-setting
  code.
subnet:
  name: CheckerChain SN87 — AI-powered trustless crypto review platform (tRCM)
  owner_repo: CheckerChain__checkerchain-subnet
  netuid: 87
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners run an LLM to predict a product''s Trust Score (0-100) plus a short review, quality
    keywords, and a sentiment for products fetched from the CheckerChain dApp. Validators compare each
    prediction against the platform-provided ground-truth trustScore: predictions deviating more than
    10% earn 0, otherwise an LLM analyzes the response and awards points for score accuracy, sentiment,
    keyword verification, and coherence.'
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: score_accuracy
  metric_kind: regression_error
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 40.0
  normalization: none
  notes: 'analysis_result["score_accuracy"] capped at 40 (accuracy_score = min(..., 40)). LLM rubric:
    40 within 2%, 30 within 6%, 20 within 8%, 10 within 10%, 0 beyond 10%. A hard gate also returns 0.0
    when deviation > 10% before the LLM is called.'
- name: sentiment_score
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 20.0
  normalization: none
  notes: sentiment_score = 20 if analysis_result["sentiment"] != "unknown" else 5. sentiment == "malicious"
    returns -math.inf (blacklist sentinel).
- name: keyword_verification_score
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 5.0
  normalization: none
  notes: keyword_score = min(analysis_result["keyword_verification_score"], 5); LLM rubric 0-5.
- name: coherence_score
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 20.0
  normalization: none
  notes: coherence_score = min(analysis_result["coherence_score"], 20); LLM rubric 0-20.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: real_engagement
  description: Product trustScore (0-100) produced by CheckerChain's tRCM consensus of human reviewers,
    fetched from the CheckerChain dApp / API and passed as `actual` to the reward function.
  source_identifier: checkerchain.com / fetch_products()
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: reviewed_product.trustScore is the authoritative target. When trustScore == 0 the validator short-circuits
    and pays a flat 100/len(responses) to every responder.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: ''
  notes: 'Per-prediction perf_score = accuracy_score + sentiment_score + keyword_score + coherence_score
    (additive, each capped). Gating overlays: deviation>10% => 0.0; malicious => -inf (blacklist). After
    per-miner rewards, only the top 90% by reward are kept (keep_count = ceil(0.9*N)), the rest set to
    0. Per-product rewards accumulate into rewards[idx] across reward_items. In set_weights the scores
    vector is L1-normalized (raw_weights = scores / ||scores||_1) before emission.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: 'update_scores() does EMA: scores = alpha*scattered_rewards + (1-alpha)*scores. set_weights()
    L1-normalizes scores, runs process_weights_for_netuid, converts to uint16 and calls subtensor.set_weights
    with version_key=spec_version. should_set_weights() fires when blocks-since-last-update > epoch_length
    and neuron is a validator.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: epoch_length = 100 (12-second blocks); forward sleeps 25 min per cycle
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: config default neuron.moving_average_alpha = 0.1. update_to_last_scores() falls back to last_scores
      when there is nothing to reward this cycle.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'filter_duplicate_predictions: predictions matching to 3 decimal places (int(pred*1000)/1000)
    are grouped and only one randomly-chosen miner per identical value is kept.'
- kind: honeypot
  enforcement: elimination
  description: LLM sentiment == "malicious" (prompt injection / manipulation) returns -math.inf, and the
    miner is added to the blacklist (add_or_update_blacklisted_miner). Blacklisted hotkeys are excluded
    from future uid selection in get_filtered_uids.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Hard deviation gate: get_deviation_percentage(prediction, actual) > 10 returns reward
    0.0 before any LLM analysis.'
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: get_filtered_uids caps miners per coldkey at max_per_key=15, limiting sybil concentration
    per coldkey.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'get_stake_score maps stake in [500, 2000] to [0,1] (1.0 above 2000). NOTE: defined in
    reward.py but not observed wired into the final reward() return; recorded as present-but-unused.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - status_enum
  status_values:
  - blacklisted
  reset_policy: scores zeroed for a uid when its hotkey is replaced in the metagraph (resync_metagraph);
    blacklist is a persistent DB table of malicious miners.
  notes: self.scores is an EMA-accumulated per-uid score vector persisted to state.npz (load_state/ save_state);
    last_scores enables fallback. Blacklisted miners stored in DB and filtered out.

docs.title: Platform (PlatformNetwork multi-challenge)
docs.text:
Subnet CortexLM__platform netuid None Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners submit to one or more independent challenges, each of which lives in its own repository and Docker image and owns its scoring logic, submissions, and SQLite state. Each challenge returns per-hotkey weights via a standard internal get_weights contract. The master platform aggregates these acro DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: Other"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: platformPlatformNetworkMultiChallenge

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: other

    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: Metric {
            kind: other
            raw: "Opaque per-hotkey weight emitted by each external challenge's GET /internal/v1/get_weights; the underlying metric is challenge-defined and not visible in this repo."
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: other
        }
        groundTruth: Other {
            trust_model: unknown
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "360 seconds (master.epoch_interval_seconds default)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: 'This repo is a multi-challenge MASTER orchestrator only. It does not itself score
  miners: each challenge runs in its own external Docker image/repo (not in this tree) and exposes GET
  /internal/v1/get_weights returning hotkey->weight. The master normalizes each challenge''s weights,
  applies fixed per-challenge emission percentages, aggregates a weighted sum, maps hotkeys to UIDs, and
  calls set_weights. No burn logic exists. The actual scoring signals / ground truth are delegated to
  challenge repos that are not part of this codebase, so the operative scoring is undocumented here.'
subnet:
  name: Platform (PlatformNetwork multi-challenge)
  owner_repo: CortexLM__platform
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners submit to one or more independent challenges, each of which lives in its own repository
    and Docker image and owns its scoring logic, submissions, and SQLite state. Each challenge returns
    per-hotkey weights via a standard internal get_weights contract. The master platform aggregates these
    across active challenges using fixed emission percentages and submits final per-UID weights to Bittensor.
    What a miner produces and how it is scored is defined entirely by each external challenge, not by
    this repo.
  submission_format:
  - other
  submission_format_other: Per-challenge / challenge-defined. The master is challenge-agnostic; each external
    challenge image defines its own submission type and scoring.
scoring_signals:
- name: per_challenge_hotkey_weight
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: Opaque per-hotkey weight emitted by each external challenge's GET /internal/v1/get_weights;
    the underlying metric is challenge-defined and not visible in this repo.
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: l1 / sum-to-one across the challenge's hotkeys (value / total)
  notes: Each challenge's raw weights are L1-normalized (sum-to-one) by normalize_weights in master/aggregator.py
    before being scaled by the challenge emission share. Non-finite and non-positive weights are dropped
    by _clean_weights.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: other
  kind_other: External challenge container. Ground truth/scoring is owned by each independent challenge
    repo+image and is not present in this codebase; the master only consumes the get_weights output over
    an authenticated internal HTTP contract.
  description: '"A challenge is an independent repository and Docker image. It owns its logic, public
    routes, submissions, scoring data, and SQLite schema."'
  source_identifier: GET /internal/v1/get_weights
  trust_model: unknown
  notes: Per-challenge shared token authenticates the internal endpoint. A signed-weights fallback (HMAC-SHA256)
    can supply cached weights when a challenge is unavailable.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn / no emission-to-owner logic anywhere in the master pipeline.
  notes: 'aggregate_challenge_weights: emissions = normalize_emissions (each active challenge''s emission_percent
    divided by total active emission_percent); for each ok challenge, hotkey_scores[hotkey] += emission
    * normalized_weight. Hotkeys with no UID in the metagraph are dropped, then uid_scores are L1-normalized
    to sum to one. A challenge whose get_weights fails (and has no usable fallback) is skipped (contributes
    zero).'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: 'MasterWeightService.run_epoch: read metagraph hotkey->uid cache, collect get_weights from each
    active challenge (with capability check and optional signed fallback), aggregate, then WeightSetter.set_weights(final.uids,
    final.weights). Empty weights are skipped; with no subtensor it is a dry run.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 360 seconds (master.epoch_interval_seconds default)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No temporal smoothing/EMA of the final weights in the master; weights are recomputed fresh
      each epoch from current challenge get_weights output.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: _clean_weights drops any hotkey weight that is non-finite or <= 0 before normalization;
    unknown hotkeys (not in metagraph hotkey_to_uid) are ignored when mapping to UIDs.
- kind: other
  kind_other: Per-challenge shared-token authentication on the internal get_weights endpoint plus HMAC-SHA256
    signed-weights fallback with max-age expiry; protects weight provenance rather than miner-side gaming.
  enforcement: rejection
  description: FallbackWeightClient verifies the HMAC signature of cached weights and rejects payloads
    older than max_age_seconds; the master blocks /internal/* from the public proxy.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Multiple independent challenges run concurrently, each its own scoring mechanism with a fixed
    emission_percent share; the master blends their normalized weights. Number of challenges is registry-driven
    (not a fixed constant in this repo).
per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: The master holds no per-miner ledger; it recomputes from challenge get_weights each epoch. Any
    per-miner state lives inside each external challenge's SQLite, outside this repo.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/architecture.md
  - docs/validator.md
  - docs/challenges.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '11'
    quote: '[![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.12-blue.svg)]'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '25'
    quote: A master validator orchestrates independent challenge containers, exposes a registry and public
      challenge proxy, collects `get_weights` from each active challenge, normalizes emissions, maps hotkeys
      to UIDs, and submits final weights to Bittensor.
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format_other
    source_path: docs/challenges.md
    line_ref: '7'
    quote: A challenge is an independent repository and Docker image. It owns its logic, public routes,
      submissions, scoring data, and SQLite schema.
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind_other
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 143-154
    quote: '"GET /internal/v1/get_weights" ... `get_weights` returns hotkey weights: { "weights": { "5F...hotkey":
      1.0 } }'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/normalization
    source_path: src/platform_network/master/aggregator.py
    line_ref: 21-26
    quote: 'def normalize_weights(raw): cleaned = _clean_weights(raw); total = sum(cleaned.values());
      if total <= 0: return {}; return {hotkey: value / total for ...}'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: src/platform_network/master/aggregator.py
    line_ref: 9-18
    quote: 'if not math.isfinite(weight): continue; if weight <= 0: continue'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/notes
    source_path: docs/challenges.md
    line_ref: '17'
    quote: The internal endpoint is authenticated with a per-challenge shared token mounted by the master.
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: src/platform_network/master/aggregator.py
    line_ref: 44-49
    quote: 'emission = emissions.get(result.slug, 0.0); for hotkey, weight in normalize_weights(result.weights).items():
      hotkey_scores[hotkey] += emission * weight'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization
    source_path: src/platform_network/master/aggregator.py
    line_ref: 60-65
    quote: 'total = sum(uid_scores.values()); if total > 0: normalized = {uid: value / total for ...}'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: src/platform_network/master/aggregator.py
    line_ref: 29-34
    quote: 'active = {r.slug: max(float(r.emission_percent), 0.0) for r in results if r.ok}; total = sum(active.values());
      ... return {slug: value / total for slug, value in active.items()}'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/enabled
    source_path: src/platform_network/master/aggregator.py
    line_ref: 37-71
    quote: aggregate_challenge_weights ... return FinalWeights(uids=..., weights=..., hotkey_weights=...)
      [no burn/owner allocation present]
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: src/platform_network/master/service.py
    line_ref: 65-76
    quote: 'async def run_epoch(...): hotkey_to_uid = self.metagraph_cache.get(); results = await self.collect_weights(...);
      final = aggregate_challenge_weights(results, hotkey_to_uid); self.weight_setter.set_weights(final.uids,
      final.weights)'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: src/platform_network/bittensor/weight_setter.py
    line_ref: 23-30
    quote: return self.subtensor.set_weights(wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.netuid, uids=uids, weights=weights,
      wait_for_inclusion=False, wait_for_finalization=False)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: src/platform_network/config/settings.py
    line_ref: '21'
    quote: 'epoch_interval_seconds: int = 360'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: src/platform_network/master/aggregator.py
    line_ref: 13-17
    quote: 'if not math.isfinite(weight): continue; if weight <= 0: continue; cleaned[str(hotkey)] = weight'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: src/platform_network/master/weight_fallback.py
    line_ref: 84-88
    quote: 'if not verify_payload(...): raise WeightFallbackError("invalid fallback weight signature");
      age = ...; if age > self.max_age_seconds: raise WeightFallbackError("fallback weights are too old")'
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/structure
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '156'
    quote: The master normalizes each challenge, applies fixed emission percentages, ignores unknown hotkeys,
      and sets a challenge contribution to zero if it fails.
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction
    source_path: src/platform_network/schemas/challenge.py
    line_ref: '29'
    quote: 'emission_percent: Decimal = Field(default=Decimal("0"), ge=0)'
  unresolved:
  - /subnet/netuid — network.netuid defaults to 0 in settings.py; no real netuid is committed.
  - Actual scoring metric/ground truth per challenge — lives in external challenge repos not in this tree.
  - /aggregation/burn_allocation — emission_percent values per challenge are registry-driven, not constants
    in this repo; no burn UID/fraction exists.
  - Whether any challenge applies temporal smoothing/EMA — defined inside external challenge code, not
    visible here.
composition:
  shape: multiplex
  overlays:
  - guards
  extern_count: 1

docs.title: Compute Subnet (Lium.io)
docs.text:
Subnet Datura-ai__compute-subnet netuid 51 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status unknown Task Miners contribute GPU-equipped executor machines to a decentralized GPU-rental marketplace. Each cycle a validator SSHes into each executor, probes its hardware specs, runs a GraVal-style device-info-seeded matrix-multiplication challenge to prove the GPU is genuine, runs a VerifyX network/storage/m DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: HardwareProbe, MarketData"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: HardwareValidation, Collateral, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: hardware_spec"]
  M1["Metric: hardware_spec"]
  M2["Metric: hardware_spec"]
  M3["Metric: throughput"]
  M4["Metric: hardware_spec"]
  M5["Metric: uptime"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  G --> M5
  GT --> M5
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  M5 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: computeSubnetLiumIo

    netuid: 51
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: compute_service

    @burn {
        fraction: 0.91
    }
    @guards {
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Collateral {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, status_enum, reputation }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: hardware_spec
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: hardware_spec
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: hardware_spec
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: hardware_spec
                direction: target_band
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            HardwareProbe {
                trust_model: adversarial
            },
            MarketData {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: Proportional {
            normalization: sum_to_one
            decay_rate: 0.3
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "tempo (TEMPO=360 blocks per epoch); set when last_update >= tempo"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
subnet:
  name: Compute Subnet (Lium.io)
  owner_repo: Datura-ai__compute-subnet
  netuid: 51
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners contribute GPU-equipped executor machines to a decentralized GPU-rental marketplace.
    Each cycle a validator SSHes into each executor, probes its hardware specs, runs a GraVal-style device-info-seeded
    matrix-multiplication challenge to prove the GPU is genuine, runs a VerifyX network/storage/memory
    probe, and checks rental status, price, collateral deposit and uptime. A per-executor mining score
    is the GPU-type rate weighted by the miner's share of that GPU type, gated by binary pass/fail checks
    and reduced by multiplicative penalties (sysbox, uptime). 91% of emission is burned; the remaining
    9% is distributed proportionally to mining score.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: gpu_type_rate
  metric_kind: hardware_spec
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Per-GPU-model emission rate (gpu_portion) from GPU_MODEL_RATES; higher-tier data-center GPUs
    (H200, B200, etc.) carry larger rates, many consumer cards are 0.0.
- name: gpu_count_share
  metric_kind: hardware_spec
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: mining_score = score * gpu_portion * gpu_count / total_gpu_count — a miner's share of the total
    GPUs of that model online this cycle. Capped at MAX_GPU_COUNT=14.
- name: graval_matrix_challenge
  metric_kind: hardware_spec
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Binary GPU-authenticity check: validator encrypts a device-info-seeded matrix challenge (DMCompVerify),
    executor must decrypt and return the embedded UUID. UUID mismatch => fail.'
- name: ema_verifyx_download_speed
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: other
  notes: Exponential moving average (alpha=0.3) of VerifyX download speed in Mbps. Below MIN_VERIFYX_EMA_DOWNLOAD_SPEED_MBPS=100.0
    the executor fails; if EMA is missing for an unrented machine, actual_score=0.
- name: gpu_price_within_limit
  metric_kind: hardware_spec
  direction: target_band
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Binary gate: price_per_gpu must not exceed MACHINE_PRICES[gpu_model] * MACHINE_MAX_PRICE_RATE
    (default 2.0). Over the limit => actual_score=0.0.'
- name: uptime
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: uptime_multiplier = 1 - PORTION_FOR_UPTIME + PORTION_FOR_UPTIME * min(1, uptime/UPTIME_REQUIRED_MINUTES);
    UPTIME_REQUIRED_MINUTES = 60*24*14 (14 days). Skipped when SKIP_COLLATERAL_PENALTY or collateral deposited.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: Validator SSHes into the executor and runs device-info-seeded matrix-multiplication (GraVal/DMCompVerify),
    VerifyX network/storage/memory probes, and GPU spec/utilization reads. The probe results are the ground
    truth for hardware authenticity and capacity.
  source_identifier: matrix_validation_service.py / verifyx_validation_service.py
  trust_model: adversarial
- kind: market_data
  description: Rental status and price are sourced from the backend (rented executors API) and per-GPU
    reference prices in MACHINE_PRICES; collateral deposits are read from an on-chain contract.
  source_identifier: MACHINE_PRICES / collateral_contract_service.py
  trust_model: trusted
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  normalization: sum_to_one
  decay_rate: 0.3
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: 0.91
    notes: 'TOTAL_BURN_EMISSION = 0.91 routed to burner UIDs (settings.NEW_BURNERS, equal split when ENABLE_NEW_BURN_LOGIC).
      mining_share = 1 - 0.91 = 0.09 distributed across miners proportional to mining_score. address_or_uid
      null: burner UIDs are runtime config (settings.NEW_BURNERS).'
  notes: 'Per-executor: result.incentive = mining_share * mining_score / total_mining_score. Per-cycle
    incentives accumulate into miner_scores across cycles, then are normalized via process_weights_for_netuid
    at set_weights. decay_rate 0.3 is the VerifyX network-speed EMA alpha, not a score-level temporal
    decay.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Accumulated per-cycle miner_scores (mining incentives + burn shares) are normalized with bittensor
    process_weights_for_netuid, converted to uint weights, and committed via set_weights. Jobs run every
    BLOCKS_FOR_JOB blocks; weights are set when last_update >= tempo.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: tempo (TEMPO=360 blocks per epoch); set when last_update >= tempo
anti_gaming:
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'GraVal/DMCompVerify device-info-seeded matrix challenge proves GPU authenticity: the validator
    encrypts a challenge seeded with gpu uuids/count/model; the executor must decrypt and return the embedded
    UUID. UUID mismatch fails the job (score 0). Also LIB_NVIDIA_ML_DIGESTS attestation and TDX_WHITELIST
    hash checks.'
- kind: collateral
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Miners must deposit a per-GPU-type collateral (REQUIRED_DEPOSIT_AMOUNT, on-chain contract).
    If not deposited and collateral enforced: actual_score=job_score=0. With ENABLE_NO_COLLATERAL the
    deposit becomes a warning; IS_NOT_DEPOSITED_SCORE_MULTIPLIER=0.5 and an uptime ramp apply instead.
    Outdated contract version multiplies score by SCORE_PORTION_FOR_OLD_CONTRACT=0.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'GPU utilization gate: an unrented executor reporting gpu_utilization >= GPU_UTILIZATION_LIMIT
    (5%) or memory utilization > GPU_MEMORY_UTILIZATION_LIMIT (5%) is treated as already-busy/rented and
    rejected from the unrented scoring pool. MAX_GPU_COUNT=14 caps reported GPUs.'
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Price gate: price_per_gpu above MACHINE_PRICES[gpu_model]*MACHINE_MAX_PRICE_RATE (2.0)
    sets actual_score to 0.0, preventing miners from advertising overpriced rentals.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: default_incentive
    role: main
    advancement_rule: 'DefaultIncentive: per-GPU mining_score with 0.91 fixed burn and 0.09 mining share.'
    multipliers: {}
  - name: rental_price_incentive
    role: main
    advancement_rule: Alternative IncentiveFactory algorithm rewarding actual rentals; uses dynamic burn_share
      = TOTAL_BURN_EMISSION - rental_share with per-GPU-type unrented caps (MAX_UNRENTED_GPUS_BY_TYPE).
    multipliers: {}
  count: 2
  notes: Incentive algorithm is selected at runtime via settings.incentive / IncentiveFactory.create.
    The active algorithm in default config is DefaultIncentive; rental_price is an alternative.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - status_enum
  - reputation
  status_values:
  - rented
  - unrented
  reset_policy: miner_scores accumulator persisted to Redis each cycle and reset to {} after a successful
    set_weights. VerifyX download-speed EMA (alpha=0.3) carried across cycles per executor.
  notes: 'Rented vs unrented (idle) status changes scoring path: rented machines always report job_score=1.0
    and skip the missing-EMA exclusion; UNRENTED_MULTIPLIER=1. EMA acts as a slow reputation signal that
    decays toward 0 on repeated probe failures, eventually excluding the executor.'

docs.title: Lium / Compute Subnet (Datura)
docs.text:
Subnet Datura-ai__lium-io netuid 51 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners register GPU executors (machines). Validators send synthetic jobs to verify each executor's GPU model, count, connectivity, collateral and rental state. Miners are rewarded for providing real, verified, available GPU compute — primarily for unrented high-end GPUs whose rental market value dri DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: HardwareProbe, MarketData"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: HardwareValidation, Collateral, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: hardware_spec"]
  M1["Metric: composite"]
  M2["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  SM["smooth: Other"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: liumComputeSubnetDatura

    netuid: 51
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: compute_service

    @burn {
        uid: "NEW_BURNERS uids [187..196] (ENABLE_NEW_BURN_LOGIC=True); legacy BURNERS [4,206,207,208]"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Collateral {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: hardware_spec
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            HardwareProbe {
                trust_model: adversarial
            },
            MarketData {
                trust_model: oracle
            }
        ]
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Other {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "360 blocks (TEMPO); jobs every BLOCKS_FOR_JOB; sync loop every 12s (SYNC_CYCLE)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: TOTAL_BURN_EMISSION = 0.91 is burned every cycle (distributed to fixed burner
  UIDs), leaving only 1 - 0.91 = 0.09 for actual miners under the default algorithm. The active algorithm
  is "rental_price", which can dynamically convert part of the 0.91 burn into a rental_share paid to miners
  with unrented high-end GPUs (capped at 0.91); when no eligible rental supply exists the share is 0 and
  the cycle is effectively a 0.91 burn. So real scoring (hardware/rental incentive) coexists with a large
  dynamic burn — hence mixed.
subnet:
  name: Lium / Compute Subnet (Datura)
  owner_repo: Datura-ai__lium-io
  netuid: 51
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners register GPU executors (machines). Validators send synthetic jobs to verify each executor's
    GPU model, count, connectivity, collateral and rental state. Miners are rewarded for providing real,
    verified, available GPU compute — primarily for unrented high-end GPUs whose rental market value drives
    a dynamic "rental_price" emission share, plus a smaller mining-pool reward for rented executors. Burner
    UIDs absorb the residual (0.91) burn emission.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
  submission_format_other: ''
scoring_signals:
- name: gpu_hardware_validation
  metric_kind: hardware_spec
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Validator runs synthetic jobs to determine gpu_model, gpu_count and a base score/job_score.
    calculate_scores() gates the actual_score: machine price above base_price*MACHINE_MAX_PRICE_RATE,
    missing EMA verifyx download speed, or (for non-excluded GPU types) missing collateral / outdated
    contract version drive actual_score to 0 (gating, not additive).'
- name: rental_price_incentive
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'For unrented eligible GPUs: incentive = rental_share * gpu_count * effective_rate / total_rental_cost,
    where effective_rate = hourly_rate * unrented_cap_multiplier * sysbox_multiplier. Multiplicative/proportional
    within the dynamic rental pool, not an additive weighted signal.'
- name: mining_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Default-pool score for rented / non-eligible GPUs: mining_score = score * gpu_portion * gpu_count
    / total_gpu_count * sysbox_multiplier * uptime_multiplier; incentive = mining_share * mining_score
    / total_mining_score where mining_share = 1 - TOTAL_BURN_EMISSION.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: Synthetic jobs executed on each executor over SSH verify GPU model/count, connectivity
    (port/DinD probes), verifyx download speed, and rental state. Drives score/job_score.
  source_identifier: neurons/validators/src/services/task/score_calculator.py
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Validator-run probes; miners can attempt to fake hardware, hence adversarial.
- kind: market_data
  description: TAO price (CoinGecko) and alpha rate feed epoch_subnet_emission used to convert total rental
    USD/hour cost into the rental emission share.
  source_identifier: incentive/price_provider.py (PriceProvider.get_tao_price / get_alpha_rate)
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Used in _calculate_rental_share; on failure rental_share falls back to 0.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: sum_to_one
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: NEW_BURNERS uids [187..196] (ENABLE_NEW_BURN_LOGIC=True); legacy BURNERS [4,206,207,208]
    fraction: 0.91
    dynamic: true
    notes: TOTAL_BURN_EMISSION=0.91 is the burn pool ceiling. Under rental_price, burn_share = TOTAL_BURN_EMISSION
      - rental_share, so the burn fraction is a dynamic residual that shrinks as eligible rental supply
      grows (rental_share capped at 0.91). With new burn logic, burn_share is split equally across the
      NEW_BURNERS uids.
  notes: 'Two-phase: per-cycle scores (mining incentives + burn scores) are accumulated into miner_scores
    across cycles, then set_weights normalizes via process_weights_for_netuid.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Per sync cycle the validator accumulates cycle_scores (burn scores for burner UIDs + mining/rental
    incentives for miners) into self.miner_scores. When should_set_weights() is true (last_update >= tempo),
    set_weights normalizes accumulated scores with process_weights_for_netuid, applies a positive floor,
    and calls subtensor.set_weights; miner_scores is then reset to {}.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 360 blocks (TEMPO); jobs every BLOCKS_FOR_JOB; sync loop every 12s (SYNC_CYCLE)
  smoothing:
    kind: other
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA. Temporal smoothing is via additive accumulation of per-cycle scores into miner_scores
      until a weight-set, then reset. First post-restart set_weights is skipped (completed_cycles_since_start
      < 1 warm-up).
anti_gaming:
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Synthetic jobs verify GPU model/count and connectivity before any score; spot-tier and
    failed executors get mining_score 0 and are excluded from both pools.
- kind: collateral
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'For non-excluded GPU types, missing collateral sets actual_score and job_score to 0 (unless
    ENABLE_NO_COLLATERAL). Outdated contract version multiplies score by SCORE_PORTION_FOR_OLD_CONTRACT
    = 0. (Note: B200 is in COLLATERAL_EXCLUDED_GPU_TYPES.)'
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: price_per_gpu above base_price * MACHINE_MAX_PRICE_RATE (default 2.0) sets actual_score
    to 0, preventing miners from inflating rental price for higher rental incentive.
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Missing EMA verifyx download speed (probe failed/never measured) on unrented executors
    sets actual_score and job_score to 0.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: default incentive (rented / non-eligible GPUs)
    role: main
    advancement_rule: mining_share * mining_score / total_mining_score
  - name: rental_price incentive (unrented eligible high-end GPUs)
    role: main
    advancement_rule: rental_share * gpu_count * effective_rate / total_rental_cost
  - name: burn pool (fixed burner UIDs)
    role: other
    advancement_rule: burn_share = TOTAL_BURN_EMISSION - rental_share, split across burner uids
  count: 3
  notes: IncentiveFactory selects algorithm from IncentiveConfig.algorithm (default "rental_price"; options
    "default"|"rental_price"). Cap dilution is applied per (base_model, gpu_count_bucket).
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: miner_scores accumulate per cycle and are persisted to Redis (MINER_SCORES_KEY); reset
    to {} immediately after a successful set_weights.
  notes: Executor uptime (minutes) is also tracked in Redis and feeds uptime_multiplier in the default
    mining score when collateral penalty is not skipped.
documentation:

  source_version: null

  - neurons/validators/README.md

  completeness: medium

docs.title: InfiniteVibe (TensorFlix)
docs.text:
Subnet Delta-Compute__infinitevibe netuid 89 Archetype multiplicative Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners promote the subnet by publishing social-media content (Instagram posts/reels, historically YouTube videos) that carries a per-hotkey signature tag, then commit a gist listing their content submissions on-chain. Validators fetch each post's engagement and follower metrics via a platform-tracke DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: RealEngagement, LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Honeypot, PlagiarismDetection, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: engagement"]
  M1["Metric: engagement"]
  M2["Metric: engagement"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: infiniteVibeTensorFlix

    netuid: 89
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [content_link, commitment_hash]

    @guards {
        Honeypot {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        PlagiarismDetection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger }

    Multiplicative {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: engagement
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: engagement
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: engagement
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            RealEngagement {
                trust_model: adversarial
            },
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: oracle
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: multiplicative
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.95
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "set_weights_interval = 1200 seconds (60*20)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Two validator implementations coexist. The base tensorflix/validator.py scores
  by raw engagement rate over the top-5 miners. The operative path is enhanced_validator_v2.py (launched
  by start_growth_validator.sh and described in README.md), which overrides calculate_and_set_weights
  to use the multiplicative growth x engagement formula in new_growth_scoring.py. No emission burn is
  present; weights are normalized over the top-5 positive-scoring miners. Documented and invoked scoring
  match, so status is active.
subnet:
  name: InfiniteVibe (TensorFlix)
  owner_repo: Delta-Compute__infinitevibe
  netuid: 89
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners promote the subnet by publishing social-media content (Instagram posts/reels, historically
    YouTube videos) that carries a per-hotkey signature tag, then commit a gist listing their content
    submissions on-chain. Validators fetch each post's engagement and follower metrics via a platform-tracker
    service, run AI-content and bot detection, and reward authentic follower growth combined with genuine
    engagement. Miners are scored on growth x engagement and only the top-5 receive weight.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: authentic_follower_growth
  metric_kind: engagement
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.7
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: Hourly follower growth rate capped at 1% (max_hourly_growth_rate=0.01); positive
    growth passed through log10(1+growth_percentage)*100, negative growth multiplied by 5 as penalty;
    result scaled by growth_weight=0.7.
  notes: 'From new_growth_scoring.py: growth_score requires >=500 followers and >=12h of history, else
    final_score=0. growth_weight=0.7 of the combined score.'
- name: engagement_multiplier
  metric_kind: engagement
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: engagement_rate=((likes+comments)/current_followers)*100, capped at 8.0; mapped to a 0.5x-2.0x
    multiplier (min_multiplier=0.5, max_multiplier=2.0). Multiplies the growth score; if authentic_growth_score<=0
    final_score=0 (gated on growth).
- name: bot_authenticity_multiplier
  metric_kind: engagement
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: If bot_probability>0.6 (bot_threshold) and bot_confidence>0.5, authenticity_multiplier
    = max(1.0-0.9, 1.0-bot_probability*0.9); else 1.0. max_bot_penalty=0.9 (up to 90% reduction).
  notes: Multiplies the growth score (anti-bot penalty) in new_growth_scoring.py.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: real_engagement
  description: Per-post engagement and follower counts (like_count, comment_count, owner_follower_count)
    fetched from an external platform-tracker microservice that scrapes Instagram/YouTube (via Apify per
    README).
  source_identifier: service_platform_tracker_url (/get_metrics); Apify
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Metrics fetched over HTTP from CONFIG.service_platform_tracker_url. AI-generation score obtained
    from a separate ai-detector service (service_ai_detector_url).
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: AI-generated-content detector service scores each video; metric.ai_score must exceed CONFIG.ai_generated_score_threshold
    (0.3) for the content to count.
  source_identifier: service_ai_detector_url (/detect, mean_ai_generated)
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: kind 'llm_judgment' used as the closest fit for an automated AI-detection model verdict.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn UID or burn fraction present in the weight-setting code.
  notes: enhanced_validator_v2.calculate_and_set_weights sorts miners by growth score, keeps the top-5
    with positive scores, zeros all others, then L1-normalizes (weights_array/=sum). Per-miner score itself
    is multiplicative (growth x engagement x bot-authenticity). If the normalized integer weights sum
    to zero, top-5 hotkeys are each set to the max int weight 65535 (equal weights fallback).
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Async _periodical_task loop calls calculate_and_set_weights every set_weights_interval (60*20
    = 1200s) and submits via subtensor.set_weights with version_key from CONFIG.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: set_weights_interval = 1200 seconds (60*20)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.95
    window: null
    notes: 'Per-content EMA in protocol.py Performance.get_score (alpha=0.95) scores incremental improvement
      between intervals; a failed signature/AI check resets the EMA chain to 0. NOTE: this get_score path
      feeds _hotkey_scores, used by the BASE validator''s weights; the operative enhanced_validator_v2
      recomputes scores from follower-growth history instead and does not EMA-smooth the final reward.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: honeypot
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'check_signature requires the post caption to contain a per-hotkey signature ''Made with
    @infinitevibe.ai on #bittensor --- <last5 of hotkey>'' (CONFIG.get_signature_post). Content without
    the tag is not counted.'
- kind: plagiarism_detection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'AI-content gate: metric.ai_score must exceed CONFIG.ai_generated_score_threshold (0.3)
    via the ai-detector service, else the content is treated as invalid and skipped.'
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: max_hourly_growth_rate=0.01 caps follower growth at 1%/hour to blunt bot-purchase spikes;
    capped_engagement caps engagement_rate at 8.0 to limit engagement gaming.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Bot follower analysis (ValidatorBotDetector / Apify): bot_probability>0.6 with confidence>0.5
    applies up to a 90% authenticity penalty (max_bot_penalty=0.9).'
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Eligibility barriers: min_followers_threshold=500 and min_analysis_period_hours=12; also
    max_submissions_per_hotkey=5 caps submissions counted per miner.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Follower-count history persisted per hotkey in MongoDB (follower_history collection) and
    used for 12h growth deltas. Per-content EMA score chain resets to 0 on any failed signature/AI validation
    interval.
  notes: Submissions and performance metrics stored in MongoDB collections keyed by config version (submissions-/performances-{CONFIG.version}).
    enhanced_validator_v2 adds a follower_history time-series collection.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: config version 0.0.2
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8
  extracted_at: '2026-06-06'
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: tensorflix/config.py
    line_ref: '11'
    quote: 'netuid: int = 89'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: tensorflix/validator.py
    line_ref: 1-13
    quote: import bittensor as bt ... import numpy as np
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: start_growth_validator.sh
    line_ref: 20-26
    quote: python enhanced_validator_v2.py --netuid 89 ... --subtensor.network finney
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: tensorflix/protocol.py
    line_ref: 117-122
    quote: username, gist_id = self.commit.split(":", 1)  url = f"https://gist.githubusercontent.com/{username}/{gist_id}/raw"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 1-3
    quote: InfiniteVibe Validator with Growth-Focused Scoring ... Bittensor subnet 89 with bot detection
      and growth-focused scoring.
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: new_growth_scoring.py
    line_ref: 17-18
    quote: 'self.growth_weight = 0.7      # 70% of score from growth'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/normalization_other
    source_path: new_growth_scoring.py
    line_ref: 71-80
    quote: 'capped_growth_rate = min(hourly_growth_rate, self.max_hourly_growth_rate) ... growth_score
      = math.log10(1 + growth_percentage) * 100 ... else: growth_score = growth_percentage * 5'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: new_growth_scoring.py
    line_ref: 96-105
    quote: engagement_rate = ((likes + comments) / current_followers) * 100 ... capped_engagement = min(engagement_rate,
      8.0) ... engagement_multiplier = min_multiplier + (capped_engagement / 8.0) * (max_multiplier -
      min_multiplier)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/normalization_other
    source_path: new_growth_scoring.py
    line_ref: 83-90
    quote: 'if bot_probability > self.bot_threshold and bot_confidence > 0.5: authenticity_multiplier
      = max(1.0 - self.max_bot_penalty, 1.0 - (bot_probability * self.max_bot_penalty))'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/composition
    source_path: new_growth_scoring.py
    line_ref: 109-113
    quote: 'if authentic_growth_score <= 0: final_score = 0.0  ... else: final_score = authentic_growth_score
      * engagement_multiplier'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: enhanced_validator_v2.py
    line_ref: 311-313
    quote: 'top_5_hotkeys = {hk for hk, _ in sorted_miners[:5] if _ > 0}  # Only include positive scores'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization
    source_path: enhanced_validator_v2.py
    line_ref: 330-332
    quote: 'if np.sum(weights_array) > 0: weights_array /= np.sum(weights_array)'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: enhanced_validator_v2.py
    line_ref: 339-345
    quote: 'if np.sum(uint_weights) == 0: ... for hotkey in top_5_hotkeys: uint_weights.append(65535)'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: tensorflix/config.py
    line_ref: '14'
    quote: 'set_weights_interval: int = Field(60 * 20, description="seconds")'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
