docs.title: SOMA
docs.text:
Subnet DendriteHQ__SOMA netuid 114 Archetype opaque Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners submit a context-compression algorithm (source code, one per hotkey per weekly competition) to the platform. The platform runs the script in a remote sandbox to compress a noise-injected base text at specified compression ratios. A validator then passes the compressed text to an LLM (OpenRout DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: HardwareValidation, DeterministicCheck, StakeWeighting"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  M1["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: Undocumented"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: soma

    netuid: 114
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: source_code

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, reputation }

    Opaque {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: content_quality
                specific: compression_ratio
                raw: "compression_ratio_threshold"
                direction: target_band
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: oracle
            }
        ]
        aggregate: Undocumented {
            composition: other
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "110 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: Validators do not compute weights locally; they fetch a weight vector from the
  platform's /validator/get_best_miners endpoint and set it on-chain. The platform assigns a tiny fixed
  weight per top screener miner (SCREENER_WEIGHT_PER_MINER=0.00002) plus TopMiner.weight to competition
  winners, and the unclaimed residual (1 - screener_used - top_miners_assigned) burns to uid 0. On any
  error (missing metagraph snapshot, no active competition, combined weight > 1) the platform returns
  a burn-only payload [uid 0, weight 1.0]. So actual emission is a mix of small screener allocations,
  winner allocation, and a dynamic burn residual.
subnet:
  name: SOMA
  owner_repo: DendriteHQ__SOMA
  netuid: 114
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners submit a context-compression algorithm (source code, one per hotkey per weekly competition)
    to the platform. The platform runs the script in a remote sandbox to compress a noise-injected base
    text at specified compression ratios. A validator then passes the compressed text to an LLM (OpenRouter),
    has the LLM answer predefined questions using only the compressed representation, and scores those
    answers against ground-truth reference answers. Validators report per-question scores to the platform;
    the platform aggregates scores, selects top screeners / winners, and returns a weight vector that
    validators set on-chain.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: llm_answer_correctness
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: An LLM scores each candidate answer (produced by a second LLM from the compressed text) against
    the reference answer. Allowed discrete scores are {0.0, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0} with verdicts NO_ANSWER/INCORRECT/PARTIAL/CORRECT.
    Per-challenge rollup is the mean of its question scores; the platform stores the mean as the BatchChallengeScore.
- name: compression_ratio_gate
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: compression_ratio_threshold
  direction: target_band
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Before scoring, the platform checks _is_compressed_enough(original, compressed, ratio); challenges
    that fail the compression-ratio check are auto-scored 0.0 with reason "not_compressed_enough" and
    excluded from the validator response. A gate, not an additive signal.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Predefined questions and ground-truth answers generated from a clean base text before noise
    injection; stored on the platform (BatchQuestionAnswer / questions). Miners do not see the clean base
    text or the answers.
  source_identifier: platform challenge dataset (BatchChallenge / BatchQuestionAnswer)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: ''
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: OpenRouter LLM (default model openai/gpt-4.1-mini, temperature 0) both generates candidate
    answers from the compressed text and grades them against references.
  source_identifier: OpenRouter chat/completions
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: ''
aggregation:
  method: undocumented
  composition: other
  composition_other: 'Two-stage: validator computes per-question/per-challenge mean scores and reports
    them; the platform separately selects top-screener miners and competition winners and emits a weight
    vector. The combine step that maps reported scores to final weights lives in platform DB logic (TopMiner.weight,
    fetch_top_screener_ss58_for_competition) not present in this repo.'
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: burn = max(0.0, 1.0 - screener_used - top_miners_assigned), added to uid 0. Full burn-only
      payload [uid 0, weight 1.0] is returned on missing metagraph snapshot, no active competition, or
      combined weight > 1.0.
  notes: Per-screener weight is the fixed SCREENER_WEIGHT_PER_MINER=0.00002 times the number of top screeners
    (TOP_SCREENER_SCRIPTS=0.2 fraction). Winner weights come from TopMiner.weight rows in the DB. Validator-side,
    weights are renormalized to sum to 1.0 before set_weights.
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: 'Validator loop checks current block each tick; when blocks_since_last >= weight_block_interval
    (110), it calls set_weights(): fetches get_best_miners from the platform and writes the returned (uid,
    weight) vector on-chain via subtensor.set_weights.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 110 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No temporal smoothing/EMA of the reward observed in this repo; the platform returns a fresh
      weight vector each call. Validator only renormalizes to sum 1.0.
anti_gaming:
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Miner scripts run in a RemoteSandboxManager with per-task timeouts (SANDBOX_TIMEOUT_PER_TASK_SECONDS=10s);
    failed sandbox tasks get score 0.0 with reason "sandbox_error" and no BatchCompressedText record is
    created.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Compression-ratio enforcement: outputs that are not compressed enough are auto-scored
    0.0 ("not_compressed_enough") before LLM scoring.'
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Validators must meet MIN_VALIDATOR_TOTAL_WEIGHT (default 30000) and MIN_VALIDATOR_ALPHA_WEIGHT
    (default 5000) stake to register and request/score challenges (verify_validator_stake_dep).
- kind: other
  kind_other: miner_ban
  enforcement: elimination
  description: Banned miners are skipped during challenge assignment and scoring is disabled for their
    batches (get_miner_banned_status_for_batch / for_update).
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Validators that report OpenRouter/provider errors are temporarily blocked from fetching
    new assignments (VALIDATOR_OPENROUTER_ERROR_COOLDOWN_SECONDS=600, min 30s).
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: Submission Window
    role: other
    advancement_rule: Miners upload/update one algorithm per hotkey per weekly cycle; only code submitted
      in this window is eligible.
  - name: Screening & Qualification
    role: qualifier
    advancement_rule: Automated validation/integrity checks; top fraction of qualified submissions (TOP_SCREENER_SCRIPTS=0.2)
      advance.
  - name: Competition Phase
    role: main
    advancement_rule: Qualified solutions scored continuously; final rankings computed at end of weekly
      cycle.
  count: null
  notes: Recurring weekly competition cycle (README). First competition is context compression. Scoring
    is evaluated independently at multiple compression levels (docs cite 20%/40%/60%); the documented
    per-level weight w(b)=1/sqrt(b) and the screener-selection logic live in the platform DB layer, not
    in this repo's source.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - reputation
  status_values:
  - working
  reset_policy: Per weekly competition cycle; screeners from the previous competition get a grace window
    (PREVIOUS_COMPETITION_SCREENERS_GRACE_HOURS=4.0).
  notes: Platform tracks per-miner ban status, screener qualification per competition, and TopMiner winner
    rows. "working" is a validator status; miner-level state lives in the platform DB (Validator.current_status
    shown here is for validators).
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/miner/context-compression.md
  - docs/validator/validator-setup.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: validator/config/settings.py
    line_ref: '42'
    quote: netuid = cls._get_int("NETUID", 114)
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: validator/evaluation/llm_scorer.py
    line_ref: 1-16
    quote: from __future__ import annotations ... from validator.evaluation.prompts import ANSWERS_GENERATION_PROMPT,
      ANSWER_SCORING_PROMPT
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: docs/miner/context-compression.md
    line_ref: 54-67
    quote: The compressed text is passed to a language model ... The model answers the predefined questions
      ... Model-generated answers are compared against ground-truth answers
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 60-66
    quote: The miner's responsibility is to design and implement model or algorithm that solves the defined
      problem ... and upload it to the platform
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind
    source_path: validator/evaluation/llm_scorer.py
    line_ref: '44'
    quote: LLM_SCORING_ALLOWED_SCORES = {0.0, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0}
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: mcp_platform/app/api/routes/validator.py
    line_ref: 1527-1535
    quote: BatchChallengeScore( ... score=sum(scores) / len(scores) )
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: mcp_platform/app/api/routes/validator.py
    line_ref: 864-868
    quote: 'if not _is_compressed_enough( original=challenge.challenge_text or "", compressed=compressed_text,
      ratio=ratio, ):'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/1/source_identifier
    source_path: validator/config/settings.py
    line_ref: 83-90
    quote: openrouter_model=os.getenv("OPENROUTER_MODEL", "openai/gpt-4.1-mini"), ... llm_temperature=cls._get_float("LLM_TEMPERATURE",
      0)
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/dynamic
    source_path: mcp_platform/app/api/routes/validator.py
    line_ref: 1969-1971
    quote: burn = max(0.0, 1.0 - screener_used - top_miners_assigned) ... miners_by_uid[0] = miners_by_uid.get(0,
      0.0) + burn
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: mcp_platform/app/api/routes/validator.py
    line_ref: 1604-1605
    quote: 'def _burn_only_payload() -> GetBestMinersUidResponse: return GetBestMinersUidResponse(miners=[MinerWeight(uid=0,
      weight=1.0)])'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: mcp_platform/app/core/config.py
    line_ref: 97-100
    quote: 'screener_weight_per_miner: float = Field( default=0.00002, alias="SCREENER_WEIGHT_PER_MINER",
      )'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval

  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description

docs.title: Djinn Protocol
docs.text:
Subnet Djinn-Inc__djinn netuid 103 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners run TLSNotary provers (and optional peer-notary sidecars) that verify sports betting line availability and produce web attestations. Validators challenge miners with sports queries (executability/odds checks against ESPN + The Odds API ground truth) and dispatch TLSNotary attestation tasks. M DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData, DeterministicDataset, CommitReveal"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: LivenessCheck, CommitReveal, StakeWeighting"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: latency"]
  M2["Metric: accuracy"]
  M3["Metric: uptime"]
  M4["Metric: hardware_spec"]
  M5["Metric: similarity"]
  M6["Metric: accuracy"]
  M7["Metric: latency"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  G --> M5
  GT --> M5
  G --> M6
  GT --> M6
  G --> M7
  GT --> M7
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  M5 --> AGG
  M6 --> AGG
  M7 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: djinnProtocol

    netuid: 103
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: [api_response, signals]

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: 0.8
    }
    @guards {
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, cumulative_score, reputation, registration_age }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: ema
            },
            Metric {
                kind: hardware_spec
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            MarketData {
                trust_model: oracle
            },
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            CommitReveal {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.00193
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "100 blocks (MIN_WEIGHT_INTERVAL)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Live blended sports+attestation scoring drives the miner weight vector, but a
  fixed 80% of total weight is routed to UID 0 (burn) on every weight set via apply_burn(weights, burn_fraction=0.80).
  Miners share the remaining 20%.
subnet:
  name: Djinn Protocol
  owner_repo: Djinn-Inc__djinn
  netuid: 103
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners run TLSNotary provers (and optional peer-notary sidecars) that verify sports betting
    line availability and produce web attestations. Validators challenge miners with sports queries (executability/odds
    checks against ESPN + The Odds API ground truth) and dispatch TLSNotary attestation tasks. Miners
    are rewarded for accuracy on sports queries, response speed, proof coverage, uptime, advertised hardware
    capability, and valid TLSNotary attestation proofs, blended into a single per-miner score.
  submission_format:
  - api_response
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: accuracy
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.35
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Sports challenge: fraction of recent (sliding window of last 20) queries where result matched
    TLSNotary ground truth. W_ACCURACY = 0.35.'
- name: speed
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.25
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Sports challenge response latency, min-max normalized across miners (fastest=1.0, slowest=0.0).
    W_SPEED = 0.25.
- name: coverage
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.15
  normalization: none
  notes: Fraction of recent proof requests (last 20) where miner's TLSNotary proof verified. W_COVERAGE
    = 0.15.
- name: uptime
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.15
  normalization: ema
  notes: EMA-smoothed uptime score, alpha=0.00193 (half-life = 1 Bittensor tempo, 360 blocks). W_UPTIME
    = 0.15. Also gets up to a 10% bonus scaled by notary_reliability inside _compute_sports_scores.
- name: capability
  metric_kind: hardware_spec
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: none
  notes: Resource capability bonus from advertised RAM/CPU/availability/session headroom (capped at 0.5).
    Non-reporting miners get neutral 0.3. W_CAPABILITY = 0.10.
- name: canonical_agreement
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.0
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Agreement with canonical odds median consensus. OBSERVE-ONLY: W_CANONICAL = 0.0, surfaced in
    breakdown but not multiplied into final weight.'
- name: attest_validity
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.6
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Attestation challenge: fraction of recent (last 10) attestations whose TLSNotary proof verified.
    W_ATTEST_VALIDITY = 0.60 within the attestation sub-score.'
- name: attest_speed
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.4
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Attestation latency, min-max normalized independently of sports speed. W_ATTEST_SPEED = 0.40
    within the attestation sub-score.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: Sports betting odds/lines used to construct challenges and check miner answers (real +
    synthetic lines mixed).
  source_identifier: The Odds API
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: 'Module docstring: ''executability checks via The Odds API''.'
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Public game/score data used to resolve sports outcomes and verify line availability.
  source_identifier: ESPN public API
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: 'README: validator picks a live sport from ESPN; resolves outcomes via ESPN game data.'
- kind: commit_reveal
  description: TLSNotary cryptographic web attestation proofs that the validator verifies to confirm a
    miner actually fetched a given web resource.
  source_identifier: TLSNotary
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Proof validity drives both accuracy gating (unverified proofs never counted correct) and the
    attestation validity sub-score.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 0.8
    dynamic: false
    notes: apply_burn assigns burn_fraction (config.bt_burn_fraction = 0.80, hardcoded protocol parameter,
      .env BT_BURN_FRACTION ignored) to UID 0 and scales miner weights into the remaining 0.20. Applied
      every weight set in the epoch loop.
  notes: 'Per-miner score = (1 - W_ATTESTATION_BLEND)*sports + W_ATTESTATION_BLEND*attest, with W_ATTESTATION_BLEND
    = 0.30 (only when attestation data exists; otherwise pure sports). Sports and attestation are each
    internal weighted sums of their signals. The blended score is then multiplied by a sqrt volume-confidence
    factor and several multiplicative gates (challenged-but-never-valid 0.05 penalty, optional reliability/shield/proof-complexity
    amplifiers, bootstrap and circuit-breaker zeroing) — hence composition: hybrid. Final raw scores are
    L1-normalized to sum to 1 across miners (then burn re-scales). Empty epochs use uptime+history(+attestation)
    weights instead.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: compute_weights_detailed(is_active) builds per-miner blended scores, normalizes to sum 1, apply_burn
    routes 80% to UID 0, then set_weights pushes the vector on chain. Gated by should_set_weights (>=
    MIN_WEIGHT_INTERVAL blocks since on-chain last_update).
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (MIN_WEIGHT_INTERVAL)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.00193
    window: null
    notes: No EMA on the final blended reward itself. The only EMA in scoring is on the uptime component
      (MinerMetrics.ema_uptime, _EMA_ALPHA = 0.00193, half-life = 1 tempo / 360 blocks), which survives
      the per-epoch reset. Attestation metrics also accumulate across epochs rather than resetting.
anti_gaming:
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Miners challenged for attestation but never producing a valid proof are multiplied by 0.05
    (95% penalty) in both active and empty epochs unless they have a verified proactive proof.
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: rejection
  description: TLSNotary proofs must verify cryptographically; queries with proof_status 'unverified'
    are never counted correct (R25-18). Proof bytes are capped at MAX_PROOF_BYTES_PER_SESSION = 1_048_576
    so padded wire proofs can't game the proof-complexity amplifier.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'New-miner zero-weight bootstrap (gated by DJINN_FF_NEW_MINER_ZERO_WEIGHT): fresh hotkeys
    held at weight 0 until BOOTSTRAP_MIN_SAMPLES=50 scored samples AND BOOTSTRAP_MIN_AGE_SECONDS=86400
    (24h) age. Also a coldkey-density sybil divisor (DJINN_FF_COLDKEY_DENSITY_WEIGHT) and a volume-confidence
    sqrt shrinkage below VOLUME_TARGET=250 samples.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: elimination
  description: 'Consensus circuit breaker (DJINN_FF_CIRCUIT_BREAKER): miners flagged by a CUSUM tracker
    for sustained deviation from validator consensus are forced to weight 0.'
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Proof-complexity amplifier (DJINN_FF_PROOF_COMPLEXITY_WEIGHT) and notary reliability amplifier
    (DJINN_FF_RELIABILITY_WEIGHT, RELIAB_FLOOR=0.20, gate RELIAB_GATE_MIN_ASSIGNED=5) reward per-UID TLSNotary/notary
    work that a shared-sidecar sybil cluster cannot replicate per UID. Non-notary miners take a 0.5 free-rider
    penalty on attestation score.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: sports_challenge
    role: main
    advancement_rule: scored on accuracy/speed/coverage/uptime/capability; 70% blend weight
  - name: attestation_challenge
    role: main
    advancement_rule: scored on proof validity/speed; 30% blend weight (W_ATTESTATION_BLEND)
  count: 2
  notes: Two independently-scored challenge types blended into one weight. A separate empty-epoch path
    scores uptime+history(+attestation) when no challenges ran.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - cumulative_score
  - reputation
  - registration_age
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Per-epoch sports counters (queries, latencies, proofs, notary duties) reset in reset_epoch()
    after weights are set; consecutive_epochs increments for participants. Attestation metrics, ema_uptime,
    lifetime counters, and notary-pair history persist across resets. All state persisted to SQLite so
    scores survive restarts; metrics reset on hotkey change (new miner on a UID).
  notes: first_seen_at drives the bootstrap window (registration_age); ema_uptime and notary_reliability
    are the reputation signals; lifetime_* counters are the cumulative ledger.

docs.title: Eastworld
docs.text:
Subnet Eastworld-AI__eastworld-subnet netuid 94 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status unknown Task Miners run embodied AI agents in Eastworld's virtual world (SN94). Each validator step the validator fetches an observation/context for one miner UID from the Eastworld server, builds a Synapse (sensor + LLM-summarized perception + inventory + action space), queries the miner's axon for an action (a DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: Other"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: StakeWeighting, LivenessCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: reward"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: eastworld

    netuid: 94
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: api_response

    @guards {
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Pipeline {
        score: Metric {
            kind: reward
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: other
        }
        groundTruth: Other {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "neuron.epoch_length blocks (default 100)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: unknown
mechanism_status_notes: The miner-scoring logic is NOT in this repo. The validator fetches per-UID scores
  from the Eastworld server's Leaderboard API (GET /sn/score) and emits them as L1-normalized chain weights.
  The actual reward computation (how an agent's actions in the virtual world become a numeric score) lives
  server-side and is not visible in the source tree, so the operative on-chain reality (active distribution
  vs. burn) cannot be confirmed from code. The on-chain weight-setting path itself is a straightforward
  proportional distribution of server scores, not a burn, but the upstream scoring is opaque.
subnet:
  name: Eastworld
  owner_repo: Eastworld-AI__eastworld-subnet
  netuid: 94
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners run embodied AI agents in Eastworld's virtual world (SN94). Each validator step the
    validator fetches an observation/context for one miner UID from the Eastworld server, builds a Synapse
    (sensor + LLM-summarized perception + inventory + action space), queries the miner's axon for an action
    (a function call), and submits that action back to the Eastworld server. Agents must perceive, remember,
    ground goals, plan, and reflect to complete quests (e.g. collecting ship components, delivering batteries,
    multi-NPC missions). Scoring is computed server-side; the validator only retrieves cumulative per-miner
    scores from the Eastworld Leaderboard API and turns them into chain weights.
  submission_format:
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: server_score
  metric_kind: reward
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: l1
  notes: 'A single opaque per-UID score returned by the Eastworld server (GET /sn/score, field "score"
    per UID). The validator does not compute it; it only fetches it. The validator L1-normalizes the score
    vector before setting weights. The Synapse context also carries a per-agent "reward: float" field
    (EWContext.reward) passed through to the miner, described as the "Agent''s weighted score", but this
    is informational input to the miner, not a validator-side scoring computation.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: other
  kind_other: server_side_leaderboard_api
  description: The Eastworld server (mainnet.eastworld.ai / testnet.eastworld.ai) is the sole authority
    for miner scores. It runs the virtual-world simulation, evaluates agent actions, and exposes cumulative
    scores via the /sn/score Leaderboard endpoint. Authentication is HTTP Basic Auth signed with the validator
    hotkey.
  source_identifier: https://mainnet.eastworld.ai (/sn/score)
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Trusted centralized oracle; the scoring algorithm is not in the open-source repo.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn logic in the repo. Weights are the server scores divided by their L1 norm.
  notes: raw_weights = self.scores / ||self.scores||_1, where self.scores is the per-UID score vector
    fetched from the Eastworld server (scattered by UID). If the L1 norm is 0 or NaN it is replaced by
    1 to avoid division by zero. The result is passed to process_weights_for_netuid / convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit
    (standard bittensor subnet limit processing) and emitted via subtensor.set_weights. There is a single
    signal, so aggregation is just proportional normalization of one server score per miner.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Every epoch (block - last_set_weights_block > neuron.epoch_length) the validator L1-normalizes
    the fetched server-score vector and calls subtensor.set_weights with version_key=spec_version. Scores
    themselves are refreshed each concurrent_forward via fetch_and_update_scores (GET /sn/score), which
    OVERWRITES self.scores wholesale (no smoothing applied in the live path).
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: neuron.epoch_length blocks (default 100)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: The BaseValidatorNeuron.update_scores method implements an EMA with neuron.moving_average_alpha
      (default 0.1), but the Eastworld Validator does NOT call it. Instead fetch_and_update_scores replaces
      self.scores directly with the server-provided scores each step, so no EMA smoothing is applied to
      the final weights in this subnet.
anti_gaming:
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Before a miner UID returned by the server is queried, check_uid_availability validates
    the UID against the metagraph and the vpermit_tao_limit (neuron.vpermit_tao_limit, default 409600),
    and the validator verifies the API-provided hotkey matches the metagraph hotkey for that UID before
    sending the synapse.
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: On test/local networks, miners that fail or return no action are added to inactive_miners
    and skipped for an increasing interval (interval += 180s up to 1800s). This is a validator-local skip,
    not a chain-weight penalty, and is gated to test/local networks only.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Multiple quest scenarios exist in the virtual world (docs/agent_quest.md) but they are not modeled
    as separate on-chain competitions; all resolve into a single server score per miner.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: self.scores for a UID is zeroed when its metagraph hotkey changes (resync_metagraph).
    The server-side cumulative score reset policy is not visible in the repo.
  notes: The validator keeps self.scores (a per-UID vector) but overwrites it each step from the server.
    It is the server that maintains cumulative per-miner achievement; the docs describe a "longitudinal,
    multidimensional" evaluation and "cumulative miner achievements".

docs.title: EfficientFrontier-SignalPlus
docs.text:
Subnet EfficientFrontier-SignalPlus__EfficientFrontier netuid 53 Archetype gated Language python Mechanism status full_burn Task Miners link a real CEX trading account through the SignalPlus platform and run live trading strategies. A miner node calls the SignalPlus API to fetch account metadata (balance, equity, PnL, drawdown), signs it, and returns it to the validator. The documented reward favors strategies with consistent DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, LivenessCheck, RiskLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: risk_adjusted_ratio"]
  M1["Metric: pnl"]
  M2["Metric: regression_error"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: efficientFrontierSignalPlus

    netuid: 53
    lang: python
    status: full_burn
    submission: [signals, api_response]

    @burn {
        uid: "161"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RiskLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Collateral {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, registration_age, status_enum }

    Gated {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: risk_adjusted_ratio
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: pnl
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: ema
            },
            Metric {
                kind: regression_error
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: MarketData {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: gated
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "sleeps 10 minutes after a successful set_weights; loop gated by should_set_weights()"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: full_burn
mechanism_status_notes: core/validator_forward.py computes per-miner rewards via get_rewards() and logs
  them, but the final action is validator.update_scores(np.array([1.0]), [161]) under a comment "Burn
  100% of the miner's emissions." set_weights() L1-normalizes scores, so a single uid 161 with score 1.0
  receives all weight. The documented scoring model (core/ranking_model.py) is therefore dormant on-chain
  despite still being executed.
subnet:
  name: EfficientFrontier-SignalPlus
  owner_repo: EfficientFrontier-SignalPlus__EfficientFrontier
  netuid: 53
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners link a real CEX trading account through the SignalPlus platform and run live trading
    strategies. A miner node calls the SignalPlus API to fetch account metadata (balance, equity, PnL,
    drawdown), signs it, and returns it to the validator. The documented reward favors strategies with
    consistently positive, time-weighted daily returns and minimal decayed drawdown (a Calmar-like ranking),
    subject to numerous zero-score violation rules. In the live code path, however, all miner emission
    is burned to the owner UID.
  submission_format:
  - signals
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: strategy_score
  metric_kind: risk_adjusted_ratio
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Documented daily Strategy Score = (Weighted Daily % Returns / Maximum Decayed Drawdown) * Risk
    Factor * 10, capped via day_pnl factor. Computed in ScoreModel.calculate_score (core/ranking_model.py)
    and returned by reward() as score_model.score. NOT used to set on-chain weights in the live path (burned).
    Weight is null because it is a single composite magnitude, not an additively weighted signal.
- name: exponentially_weighted_daily_returns
  metric_kind: pnl
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: ema
  notes: 'Numerator of the score: exponentially day-weighted, AUM-multiplied, qualification-gated sum
    of daily return ratios / sum of day weights (calculate_exponentially_weighed_daily_returns).'
- name: max_decayed_drawdown
  metric_kind: regression_error
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Denominator of the score: worst peak-to-trough drawdown, decayed, used as abs(measure_day_detail.decayedDrawDown)
    divisor in calculate_score.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: Real CEX trading-account performance (balance, equity, PnL, drawdown) sourced from the
    SignalPlus platform via its public API, which acts as a trusted intermediary verifying exchange trade
    authenticity.
  source_identifier: t.signalplus.com / SignalPlus API (ReportDataHandler)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: 'Miner fetches from SignalPlus API; data signed (verify256) so the validator can confirm it is
    untampered. README: "SignalPlus will act as a trusted intermediary".'
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: gated
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '161'
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'validator_forward.py: owner_uid = 161; validator.update_scores(np.array([1.0]), [owner_uid])
      under comment "Burn 100% of the miner''s emissions." Only uid 161 receives a non-zero score, so
      after L1 normalization in set_weights it holds 100% of the weight.'
  notes: 'Documented reward share is proportional: Strategy Reward = Strategy Daily Score / Total Daily
    Score of Top-50 Strategies * Total Daily Reward Pool. Composition is gated because many violation
    conditions (min balance, qualification, mmr, observation period) multiply the magnitude to zero. The
    on-chain aggregation is overridden by the full burn.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: scores normalized to weights via L1 norm (raw_weights = self.scores / norm), then process_weights_for_netuid
    + convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit before subtensor.set_weights. In the live path scores = a single
    1.0 on uid 161, so 100% weight goes to the owner.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: sleeps 10 minutes after a successful set_weights; loop gated by should_set_weights()
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: update_scores directly overwrites score with the latest reward ("Directly update score with
      the latest reward"), no EMA/moving-average smoothing.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Validator verifies an asymmetric signature on miner-reported data (verify256 over rawData
    + signature) in reward(); failure returns 0. Also checks the reported hotkey/coldkey match the metagraph
    for the uid (returns 0 on mismatch).
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'is_measure_time_expired: reported measureTime older than 25 hours returns score 0; empty
    response returns 0.'
- kind: risk_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Documented MMR / excessive-risk adjustment: margin usage > 0.8 discounts score *0.5, >
    0.5 discounts *0.8 (core/ranking_model.py calculate_score mmrEnd branches). Documented whitelisted-asset,
    wash-trade, net-withdrawal and min-volume rules zero the day''s positive return (RankingRulesEN.md
    section 3.3).'
- kind: collateral
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Registration fee (documented ~1 TAO / $500 USD) and a minimum 10,000 USDT account equity
    to qualify; 14-day observation period before a strategy is eligible for ranking.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Documented "Top-50" reward eligibility and per-strategy ranking, but no distinct sub-competition
    tracks in code. Not modeled as sub_competitions.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - registration_age
  - status_enum
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Life-to-date records capped at 90 days (documented LTD <= 90 days). Subnet-init period
    of 14 days from subNetCreateTime gives flat scores (0.01 or 1.0).
  notes: ScoreModel carries a per-day performance ledger (list of DayDetailDTO with equity, drawdown,
    index values), an inception/observation age (inTopRank, is_sub_net_init, inObserveTime), and a quit
    flag. This state lives in miner-reported data scored centrally, not in validator-persisted state (save_state/load_state
    are no-ops).

docs.title: FLock OFF
docs.text:
Subnet FLock-io__FLock-subnet netuid None Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners curate and upload conversational instruction-tuning datasets (JSONL) to Hugging Face and register the repo/commit metadata on-chain. Validators train a LoRA adapter on Qwen/Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct using each miner's dataset and measure evaluation loss on a fixed eval dataset. The miner whose da DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: PlagiarismDetection, Deduplication, CommitReveal"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: loss"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: CommitReveal"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: fLockOff

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: [content_link, commitment_hash]

    @burn {
        uid: "subnet owner UID (SubnetOwnerHotkey)"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @guards {
        PlagiarismDetection {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, status_enum, registration_age }

    Pipeline {
        score: Metric {
            kind: loss
            direction: lower_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: rank
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: CommitReveal {
            cadence: daily
            tempo: "blocks_per_epoch default 360; block_threshold 50"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: single
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: 'Daily winner-take-all competition: weights = 1 on the single winning UID (lowest
  eval_loss among the earliest committers within a loss-percentile cutoff), 0 elsewhere. When no active
  competition exists / the dataset commit is unchanged, weights are set entirely on the subnet owner (burn)
  UID. So at any given time emissions go either fully to one miner or fully to the burn UID — hence "mixed"
  rather than continuously active.'
subnet:
  name: FLock OFF
  owner_repo: FLock-io__FLock-subnet
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners curate and upload conversational instruction-tuning datasets (JSONL) to Hugging Face
    and register the repo/commit metadata on-chain. Validators train a LoRA adapter on Qwen/Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct
    using each miner's dataset and measure evaluation loss on a fixed eval dataset. The miner whose dataset
    yields the lowest eval loss (subject to anti-duplication and a loss-percentile + earliest-commit tie-break)
    wins the daily competition and receives all emissions.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: eval_loss
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: train_lora returns the LoRA eval loss on the fixed eval dataset; stored as the miner's raw score
    (eval_loss). select_winner sorts eligible submissions by eval_loss ascending. Invalid / duplicate
    / errored submissions get DEFAULT_RAW_SCORE = 999.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Fixed evaluation dataset downloaded from a Hugging Face repo (flock-io/flock-off-s1-competition,
    eval_commit "main"); validator trains/evaluates LoRA against it.
  source_identifier: flock-io/flock-off-s1-competition
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: eval_commit = "main"; Competition.repo default. Miner datasets are also checked against this
    eval dataset for plagiarism (count_similar must equal full miner dataset, else fallback score).
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: rank
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: subnet owner UID (SubnetOwnerHotkey)
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: In _comp_exists_in_db, when no valid stored competition exists, weights are zeroed and weights[burn_uid]
      = 1, where burn_uid is the subnet owner's UID (get_burn_uid queries SubnetOwnerHotkey). This is
      full burn when no competition is active.
  notes: 'Winner gets new_weights[winner] = 1 (all other UIDs 0). select_winner: take submissions with
    eval_loss not None, keep the top LOSS_THRESHOLD_PCT (0.05) by loss (min 1), then pick the earliest
    commitment_block/timestamp among those. compute_score() (a benchmark-curve scoring fn) exists in validator_utils.py
    but is NOT called by the validator; winner selection uses raw eval_loss directly.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: daily
  method: Competition runs on a daily UTC schedule (submission window starting at 12:00 UTC, validation,
    then rewarding). Weights committed when blocks-to-epoch <= block_threshold (50), using commit-reveal.
  on_chain_call: commit_reveal
  tempo_or_interval: blocks_per_epoch default 360; block_threshold 50
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of weights; each rewarding cycle overwrites self.weights with a fresh
      one-hot winner vector (or burn vector).
anti_gaming:
- kind: plagiarism_detection
  enforcement: penalty
  description: count_similar between miner dataset and the eval dataset must equal the full miner dataset
    length (rows capped at competition.rows=100); otherwise the miner gets DEFAULT_RAW_SCORE (999), effectively
    disqualifying them. Each row must come from the eval dataset.
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Pairwise count_similar between miners; if > DEFAULT_DUPLICATE_COUNT (33), miners are grouped
    as duplicates, sorted by commitment block, and all but the earliest committer get DEFAULT_RAW_SCORE
    (999).
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Weights are submitted via commit-reveal (commit_weights_extrinsic / reveal_weights_extrinsic)
    with a fresh random 8-byte salt per commitment; revealed on a later interval.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Miner on-chain metadata commitment subject to a ~20-minute commit cooldown (README: retries
    every 120s if the 20-minute commit cooldown applies).'
sub_competitions:
  structure: single
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: One daily dataset competition (Competition id "1", repo flock-io/flock-off-s1-competition). Competition
    lifecycle has SUBMISSION / VALIDATION / REWARDING / COMPLETED states but it is a single mechanism,
    not a ladder/tournament of distinct competitions.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - status_enum
  - registration_age
  status_values:
  - submission
  - validation
  - rewarding
  - completed
  reset_policy: Per-competition (daily). A new competition_id (YYYYMMDD) starts each day; scores/submissions
    are recorded per competition_id in scores.db. Earliest commitment_block is used as a tie-break.
  notes: 'ScoreDB persists submissions, eval_loss, commitment_block/timestamp, eligibility, and competition
    status per competition_id. CompetitionState enum: submission/validation/rewarding/completed.'
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:

  completeness: medium
provenance:

docs.title: BitAds
docs.text:
Subnet FirstTensorLabs__BitAds netuid 16 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status mixed Task A proof-of-sale marketing subnet. Miners promote BitAds campaigns across platforms of their choosing and are rewarded for verified (post-webhook) sales: number of verified orders, total revenue in USD, and a low refund ratio. Validators collect per-miner sales statistics over a rolling window (defau DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: RealEngagement"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Other, Other, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: volume"]
  M1["Metric: volume"]
  M2["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: CommitReveal"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: bitAds

    netuid: 16
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: content_link

    @burn {
        uid: "subnet owner hotkey (get_subnet_owner_hotkey); README says UID 0"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        Other {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: volume
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: volume
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: financial_pnl
                specific: refund_rate
                raw: "refund-rate multiplicative penalty"
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: RealEngagement {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: sum_to_one
            min_weight_floor: 0.0001
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: CommitReveal {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "subnet tempo (subtensor.tempo(netuid))"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_brief
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: Validator computes per-miner scores from verified sales/revenue/refunds and aggregates
  them across campaigns by emission_split, but a dynamic creator-burn (sent to subnet owner UID, "burned"
  by the chain) is applied on top. Burn percentage is computed from the sales-to-emissions ratio and can
  range 0-100%. With zero active campaigns or all-zero aggregated scores, the validator sets all weight
  to the subnet owner (full burn fallback).
subnet:
  name: BitAds
  owner_repo: FirstTensorLabs__BitAds
  netuid: 16
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'A proof-of-sale marketing subnet. Miners promote BitAds campaigns across platforms of their
    choosing and are rewarded for verified (post-webhook) sales: number of verified orders, total revenue
    in USD, and a low refund ratio. Validators collect per-miner sales statistics over a rolling window
    (default 30 days) and convert them to weights.'
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  submission_format_other: ''
scoring_signals:
- name: sales_volume
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.15
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: sqrt(sales)/sqrt(P95_sales), capped at 1
  notes: Number of verified orders in the window. Default weight w_sales=0.15 (DEFAULT_W_SALES). Square-root
    normalized against the 95th-percentile of sales across active miners.
- name: revenue_usd
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.85
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: ln(1+rev)/ln(1+P95_rev), capped at 1
  notes: Sum of verified revenue in USD. Default weight w_rev=0.85 (DEFAULT_W_REV). Logarithmically normalized
    against the 95th-percentile of revenue.
- name: refund_rate_penalty
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: refund-rate multiplicative penalty
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: ref_i = min(1, refund_orders_i / max(1, sales_i)); refund_multiplier = 1 - ref_i, applied multiplicatively
    to the base score. Gated/multiplicative, no additive weight.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: real_engagement
  description: Verified (post-webhook) sales, revenue and refund data per miner, fetched by the validator
    from the BitAds storage/stats API (storage.bitads.ai) over the configured window (default 30 days).
  source_identifier: https://storage.bitads.ai (NETWORK_BASE_URLS)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Only webhook-verified sales/revenue and confirmed refunds/chargebacks are counted. Statistics
    come from an off-chain API operated by the subnet, not on-chain data.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: sum_to_one
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: 0.0001
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: subnet owner hotkey (get_subnet_owner_hotkey); README says UID 0
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: Dynamic creator burn. burn_percentage = max(0, (emission_usd - sales_usd*ratio) / emission_usd
      * 100), clamped to [0,100]; emission_usd = emission_in_tao * tao_price_usd; target sales_emission_ratio
      default 1.0. The burned fraction of weight is sent to the subnet owner via apply_creator_burn. Can
      be overridden by a fixed CLI/per-scope value. When no campaigns are active or all aggregated scores
      are zero, all weight goes to the owner (full burn).
  notes: base_i = w_sales*sales_norm + w_rev*rev_norm; score_i = base_i * (1 - ref_i), bounded [0,1].
    Per-campaign weight vectors are normalized, optionally burn-adjusted, then combined across campaigns
    using each campaign's emission_split (normalized to fractions; uniform if none provided), and the
    aggregated vector is normalized to sum 1. min_weight_floor is the pending-miner minimum score (PENDING_MINER_MIN_SCORE=0.0001)
    preserved for miners that only have pending orders. Optional soft-cap (sales<threshold -> *factor)
    is disabled by default (DEFAULT_USE_SOFT_CAP=False, threshold 3, factor 0.30).
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: 'On each loop, if blocks_since_last_update >= tempo, _process_weights() computes and aggregates
    per-campaign scores, applies burn, and publishes a single weight extrinsic via ValidatorScoreSink.
    When subtensor.commit_reveal_enabled(netuid) is true it uses commit_timelocked_weights_extrinsic (commit-reveal);
    otherwise set_weights_extrinsic (set_mechanism_weights). mechid=0. Burn percentage is recalculated
    periodically (docs: daily).'
  on_chain_call: commit_reveal
  tempo_or_interval: subnet tempo (subtensor.tempo(netuid))
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA of the final reward in this repo. The docs mention optional P95 smoothing (alpha in
      [0.3,0.5]) of the normalization reference values, but no smoothing constant is present in the readable
      source; left unresolved.
anti_gaming:
- kind: other
  kind_other: verified-sales / webhook truth requirement
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Only webhook-verified sales/revenue and confirmed refunds count toward score; unverified
    activity is excluded (README "Data integrity"; ground-truth from trusted off-chain API).
- kind: other
  kind_other: refund-rate multiplicative penalty
  enforcement: penalty
  description: High refund ratios multiplicatively reduce score (refund_multiplier = 1 - ref_i), discouraging
    fake or reversed sales.
- kind: other
  kind_other: P95-percentile outlier resistance
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Sales/revenue are normalized against the 95th percentile and compressed via sqrt/ln, limiting
    the dominance of whales and reducing the value of inflating a single metric.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_brief
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Multiple advertising campaigns ("briefs") run concurrently; each has a scope (campaign_id), a
    mech_id, and an emission_split. Per-campaign scores are aggregated into one weight vector by emission_split
    share. Configuration is keyed per mechanism (mech_scope = "mech{id}").
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  status_values:
  - pending (only pending orders -> minimum score)
  - scored (in miner-stats)
  reset_policy: State derives from a rolling window (default DEFAULT_WINDOW_DAYS=30) of fetched miner
    stats; pending-only miners receive PENDING_MINER_MIN_SCORE=0.0001 to avoid deregistration.
  notes: No persistent on-disk ledger in this repo; per-miner sales/revenue/refund stats are fetched each
    cycle from the external stats API over the window.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: 0.0.1 (validator_version example; core via bitads-v3-core PyPI)
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/validating.md
  - docs/mining.md
  - core/burn_calculator.py
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract
  confidence_overall: medium
  extractor_notes: The core scoring function (ScoreCalculator) and apply_creator_burn live in the external
    PyPI package bitads_v3_core, not vendored in this repo, so their exact implementation lines are not
    readable here. The scoring algorithm is, however, fully specified in README.md and its default constants
    are duplicated in core/constants.py, which the validator passes into ScoreCalculator. Burn logic is
    fully readable in core/burn_calculator.py and core/resolvers.py.
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: core/constants.py
    line_ref: 9-12
    quote: "NETUIDS = {\n    NETWORKS[0]: 16,\n    NETWORKS[1]: 368,\n}"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 1-14
    quote: import argparse ... from bittensor.core.subtensor import Subtensor
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '7'
    quote: BitAds is a decentralized, proof of sale marketing network where merchandisers stake or rent
      ALPHA to own marketing bandwidth, and miners earn on verified sales.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: core/constants.py
    line_ref: 31-32
    quote: 'DEFAULT_W_SALES = 0.15

      DEFAULT_W_REV = 0.85'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/normalization_other
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '80'
    quote: sales_norm_i = min(1, sqrt(sales_i) / max(sqrt(P95_sales), epsilon))
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/normalization_other
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '82'
    quote: rev_norm_i = min(1, ln(1 + rev_i) / max(ln(1 + P95_rev), epsilon))
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes

docs.title: Cartha
docs.text:
Subnet General-Tao-Ventures__cartha-cli netuid 35 Archetype opaque Language python Mechanism status unknown Task Cartha is the Liquidity Provider for the 0xMarkets DEX. Miners provide liquidity by locking USDC into Cartha vault contracts on an EVM chain (e.g. Base) for a chosen duration (7-365 days), against a specific trading pair / pool (e.g. BTCUSD). An off-chain "verifier"/validator detects the on-chain Lo DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Collateral, LivenessCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: Undocumented"]
  PUB["publish: Unknown"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: cartha

    netuid: 35
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: other

    @guards {
        Collateral {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum }

    Opaque {
        score: Metric {
            kind: other
            family: financial_pnl
            specific: liquidity_position
            raw: "locked_liquidity_amount"
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: MarketData {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: Undocumented {
            composition: gated
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: Unknown {
            cadence: weekly
            tempo: "weekly (epoch freeze every Friday 00:00 UTC)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_asset
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: unknown
mechanism_status_notes: 'This repository is the miner-side command-line tool (cartha-cli), NOT the validator.
  The actual scoring and weight-setting code lives in a separate repo referenced in a code comment as
  "cartha-validator config.py min_total_assets_usdc". The CLI only documents mechanism facts as surfaced
  to miners: weekly epochs frozen Friday 00:00 UTC, locked-USDC liquidity is what gets scored, and a 100,000
  USDC minimum portfolio threshold below which a miner receives score=0 and no rewards. Whether the live
  validator burns emission, the exact scoring curve, the aggregation, and the on-chain weight cadence
  cannot be determined from this repo. Hence mechanism_status: unknown.'
subnet:
  name: Cartha
  owner_repo: General-Tao-Ventures__cartha-cli
  netuid: 35
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Cartha is the Liquidity Provider for the 0xMarkets DEX. Miners provide liquidity by locking
    USDC into Cartha vault contracts on an EVM chain (e.g. Base) for a chosen duration (7-365 days), against
    a specific trading pair / pool (e.g. BTCUSD). An off-chain "verifier"/validator detects the on-chain
    LockCreated event and includes the miner's locked position in the upcoming weekly reward epoch. Miners
    are rewarded for the USDC liquidity they keep locked; a miner whose total locked portfolio is below
    100,000 USDC receives score=0 and no rewards. The actual scoring/weight computation is performed by
    the separate cartha-validator service, not by this CLI.
  submission_format:
  - other
  submission_format_other: 'On-chain liquidity lock: miner locks USDC into a Cartha vault contract (EIP-712
    signed LockRequest from the verifier), and the verifier detects the LockCreated event to enroll the
    position in the next epoch. No model weights, code, or signals are submitted to validators.'
scoring_signals:
- name: locked_usdc_liquidity
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: locked_liquidity_amount
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Total USDC locked across the miner's vault positions (the "portfolio"). The CLI displays an "Active
    Portfolio (Current Epoch)" and "Verified Portfolio (Next Epoch)" and warns that portfolios below 100,000
    USDC get score=0. The exact mapping from locked amount to validator weight is NOT in this repo (it
    is in cartha-validator). Weight left null because the additive contribution/curve is undocumented
    here.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: On-chain LockCreated events from Cartha vault contracts on an EVM chain (e.g. Base, chain
    id 8453 mainnet / 84532 Base Sepolia testnet). The verifier reads these events to determine each miner's
    locked USDC amount and pool membership for an epoch.
  source_identifier: Cartha vault contracts (EVM); verifier api.cartha.finance
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Ground-truth lock state is read on-chain by the off-chain verifier; the CLI only queries the
    verifier's HTTP API to display status. Lock detection is event-driven (LockCreated).
aggregation:
  method: undocumented
  composition: gated
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn logic present in this repo (CLI only). Burn behaviour of the live validator is unknown.
  notes: 'The only documented gating rule in this repo is a hard minimum-portfolio threshold: miners with
    less than 100,000 USDC total locked receive score=0 and no rewards (gated/fail-closed). The actual
    aggregation across pools/miners and the conversion to on-chain weights is performed by cartha-validator
    and is not present here, hence method: undocumented.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: weekly
  method: Rewards are organised around weekly epochs. The epoch "freezes" each Friday at 00:00 UTC; the
    CLI computes the current epoch start as the most recent Friday 00:00 UTC and the next freeze as +7
    days. Positions verified before the freeze are "Active" (earning rewards in the current frozen epoch);
    positions locked after are "In Next Epoch". The actual set_weights call is made by the cartha-validator,
    not by this CLI.
  on_chain_call: unknown
  tempo_or_interval: weekly (epoch freeze every Friday 00:00 UTC)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No reward smoothing logic in this repo. The CLI only displays per-epoch active vs next-epoch
      status; any temporal smoothing would live in cartha-validator (not present).
anti_gaming:
- kind: collateral
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Minimum-portfolio barrier: a miner must lock at least 100,000 USDC total across pools
    to be eligible for any score/rewards; below this threshold score=0. Liquidity is real on-chain USDC
    locked for a fixed term (7-365 days), making it a capital/collateral requirement rather than a cheap
    signal.'
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Positions are scoped to weekly epochs frozen Friday 00:00 UTC; only positions verified
    before the freeze count toward the current epoch ("Active"), others are deferred to the next epoch.
    Pair states include "revoked" (deregistered or evicted), indicating positions can be evicted.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_asset
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Miners can hold positions across multiple trading-pair pools (e.g. BTCUSD and others listed by
    "cartha vault pools"); the portfolio threshold is evaluated on the total across all pools. No explicit
    tiers/ladder documented.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  status_values:
  - active
  - verified
  - pending
  - revoked
  - unknown
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: 'The CLI''s pair-status command documents a per-miner pair state enum: active (in current frozen
    epoch, earning rewards), verified (has lock proof, not in current active epoch), pending (registered,
    no lock proof yet), revoked (deregistered or evicted), unknown (no pair record). These states are
    maintained by the verifier, not this CLI.'
documentation:
  status: docs_authoritative
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/COMMANDS.md
  - cartha_cli/testnet/README.md
  completeness: low
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8
  extracted_at: '2026-06-06'
  confidence_overall: low
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '3'
    quote: Cartha is the Liquidity Provider for 0xMarkets DEX.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: cartha_cli/config.py
    line_ref: 47-48
    quote: 'network: str = Field("finney", alias="CARTHA_NETWORK")  / netuid: int = Field(35, alias="CARTHA_NETUID")'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: cartha_cli/config.py
    line_ref: 1-9
    quote: '"""Runtime configuration for the Cartha CLI.""" ... from pydantic import Field'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 84-93
    quote: cartha vault lock --coldkey your-wallet --hotkey your-hotkey --pool-id BTCUSD --amount 1000.0
      --lock-days 30 --owner-evm 0xYourEVMAddress --chain 8453 --vault-address 0xVaultAddress
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format_other
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 102-109
    quote: 3. Request a signed LockRequest from the verifier ... 7. The verifier automatically detects
      your lock and adds you to the upcoming epoch
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: cartha_cli/commands/miner_status.py
    line_ref: 39-42
    quote: '# Minimum total portfolio required to receive score/rewards (100k USDC) / # Miners below this
      threshold will receive score=0 and no rewards / # Reference: cartha-validator config.py min_total_assets_usdc
      / MIN_PORTFOLIO_THRESHOLD_USDC = 100_000.0'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: cartha_cli/commands/miner_status.py
    line_ref: 546-548
    quote: Miners with less than 100k USDC total locked will receive [bold]score=0[/] and [bold]no rewards[/]

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/composition
    source_path: cartha_cli/commands/miner_status.py
    line_ref: '540'
    quote: 'if total_portfolio_usdc < MIN_PORTFOLIO_THRESHOLD_USDC:'
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description
    source_path: cartha_cli/testnet/README.md
    line_ref: '202'
    quote: 'Auto-Detection: The verifier automatically detects your `LockCreated` event and adds you to

  - claim_field: /weight_setting/cadence
    source_path: cartha_cli/utils.py
    line_ref: 216-228
    quote: 'def get_next_epoch_freeze_time(...): Calculate the next epoch freeze time (next Friday 00:00
      UTC). ... return current_start + timedelta(days=7)'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: cartha_cli/utils.py

    quote: 'def get_current_epoch_start(...): Calculate the start of the current epoch (Friday 00:00 UTC).
      ... weekday = reference.weekday()  # Monday=0, Friday=4 / days_since_friday = (weekday - 4) % 7'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/status_values
    source_path: cartha_cli/commands/pair_status.py
    line_ref: 67-75
    quote: 1. active - In current frozen epoch, earning rewards / 2. verified - Has lock proof, not in

  extractor_notes: 'cartha-cli is strictly the miner-facing CLI; it talks to an off-chain verifier HTTP
