docs.title: Cartha
docs.text:
Subnet General-Tao-Ventures__cartha-validator netuid 35 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners are USDC liquidity providers who lock USDC into Cartha vaults (principal miners operate vaults; federated miners lock into a principal's vault). A miner's reward is driven by the sum of all their active locked positions, each scored as pool_weight x amount_usdc x lock_boost, where lock_boost DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: Other"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: RiskLimit, Other, LivenessCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: volume"]
  M1["Metric: other"]
  M2["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: cartha

    netuid: 35
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: other

    @burn {
        uid: "subnet owner hotkey (metagraph.owner_hotkey UID)"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        RiskLimit {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }

    Multiplex<multi_asset> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: volume
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: financial_pnl
                specific: lock_duration
                raw: "lock_duration_commitment"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: financial_pnl
                specific: pool_multiplier
                raw: "pool_multiplier"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: Other {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: multiplicative
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: weekly
            tempo: "360 blocks (default_tempo / epoch_length_blocks fallback; tempo from metagraph when available)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_asset
            count: 6
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: Documented scoring is fully implemented (liquidity-weighted per-position scoring),
  and a fixed 24.3902% of weight always goes to the "Cartha's Incentive Pool" hotkey. When no miners meet
  the 100,000 USDC minimum threshold, the remaining 75.6098% is routed to the subnet owner hotkey explicitly
  for EMISSION BURNING. So the live mechanism mixes active scoring, a fixed pool allocation, and a burn
  fallback depending on whether any miners qualify.
subnet:
  name: Cartha
  owner_repo: General-Tao-Ventures__cartha-validator
  netuid: 35
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners are USDC liquidity providers who lock USDC into Cartha vaults (principal miners operate
    vaults; federated miners lock into a principal's vault). A miner's reward is driven by the sum of
    all their active locked positions, each scored as pool_weight x amount_usdc x lock_boost, where lock_boost
    rewards longer lock durations (capped at 365 days). The validator fetches an epoch-frozen miner/position
    snapshot from the Cartha verifier API rather than evaluating any model output.
  submission_format:
  - other
  submission_format_other: On-chain/off-chain USDC liquidity lock positions reported by the Cartha verifier
    API as (hotkey, pool_id, amount, lock_days, expires_at, deregistered_at) entries.
scoring_signals:
- name: locked_usdc_amount
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: amount_usdc = original_amount_usdc frozen at epoch start (6 decimals). Mid-epoch top-ups do not
    count until the next epoch. Multiplicative factor in position_score = pool_weight x amount_usdc x
    lock_boost.
- name: lock_duration_boost
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: lock_duration_commitment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: lock_boost = min(lock_days, max_lock_days) / max_lock_days, with max_lock_days = 365. A 365-day
    lock gives boost 1.0, 182 days gives ~0.5. Multiplicative factor in position_score.
- name: pool_weight
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: pool_multiplier
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Per-pool multiplier looked up from settings.pool_weights, defaulting to 1.0. Currently 1.0 for
    all pools (PRE_DEX equal-weight mode); POST_DEX on-chain allocations exist in pool_weights.py but
    are inactive.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: other
  kind_other: verifier_api_epoch_frozen_snapshot
  description: Cartha verifier API (https://api.cartha.finance) returns the epoch-frozen verified-miner
    list with (hotkey, slot_uid, pool_id, amount, lock_days, expires_at, deregistered_at). This is the
    single source of truth for verified miner positions and frozen liquidity amounts.
  source_identifier: https://api.cartha.finance
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Verifier handles all on-chain (EVM) validation and RPC queries. Legacy on-chain EVM event replay
    (LockCreated/LockUpdated/LockReleased) exists but is used only with --no-use-verified-amounts; verified-amounts
    mode is the default and required mode on mainnet.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: subnet owner hotkey (metagraph.owner_hotkey UID)
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: When miner_total <= 0 (no miners with positive score, e.g. all below the 100,000 USDC minimum),
      the remaining_weight (1.0 - trader_pool_weight = 0.756098) is allocated to the subnet owner hotkey
      UID for EMISSION BURNING (explicitly "not rewards"). Separately, a fixed 0.243902 (24.3902%) is
      always allocated to the trader rewards pool hotkey (5EPdZMZyByHbweNBWRFwYqL5czKGbdeVKTpHqVizp4UyUq94);
      this is a fixed reserved allocation, not a burn.
  notes: Per-miner raw score = sum over active positions of (pool_weight x amount_tokens x boost). Negative
    scores clamped to 0; miners with score 0 (incl. below-threshold) excluded. Miner weights = (score_i
    / sum_score) x remaining_weight where remaining_weight = 1.0 - 0.243902. Trader pool gets fixed 0.243902.
    Weights verified to sum to 1.0.
weight_setting:
  cadence: weekly
  method: Weights computed once per weekly epoch (Friday 00:00 UTC -> Thursday 23:59 UTC), cached for
    the week, and republished every Bittensor epoch (tempo blocks) via subtensor.set_weights with a cooldown
    check (blocks_since_update vs tempo/epoch_length_blocks). version_key = __spec_version__ (1000*major
    + 10*minor + 1*patch). Daily expiry checks re-filter expired pools/deregistered hotkeys and force
    re-publication.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 360 blocks (default_tempo / epoch_length_blocks fallback; tempo from metagraph when
    available)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of the final reward. Weekly cached weights are republished unchanged each
      tempo; scores are recomputed fresh per weekly epoch from the frozen snapshot.
anti_gaming:
- kind: risk_limit
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Minimum total assets threshold: principal miners with total locked USDC < 100,000 (min_total_assets_usdc)
    score 0.0 regardless of lock duration, so all federated miners in that vault also earn nothing for
    the epoch. Prevents dust positions from gaming weight distribution.'
- kind: other
  kind_other: epoch_freeze_snapshot
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Epoch freezing: scoring uses the verifier''s epoch-frozen original_amount_usdc snapshot
    taken at Friday 00:00 UTC. Mid-epoch top-ups (pending_amount) do not count until the next epoch, preventing
    mid-epoch manipulation.'
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Expired pool filtering (pools with expires_at < now excluded) and deregistered-hotkey filtering
    (all positions for deregistered hotkeys scored 0). Deregistered hotkeys fetched from the verifier;
    expiry/deregistration parsed per position in process_entries.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Validator whitelist: only whitelisted validator hotkeys may query verified miners from
    the verifier; non-whitelisted validators are rejected. Validators must also meet the on-chain minimum
    version requirement (weight_versions hyperparameter).'
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_asset
  stages: []
  count: 6
  notes: 'Six market pools across three parent vaults (Cryptos: BTC/USD, ETH/USD; Currencies: EUR/USD,
    GBP/USD, JPY/USD; Commodities: GOLD/USD). Currently all pools have equal weight 1.0 (PRE_DEX); pool
    choice does not differentiate scores until POST_DEX on-chain allocations activate. Treated as multi_asset
    rather than separate competitions because all pools feed one summed per-miner score.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Scores are recomputed fresh each weekly epoch from the frozen verifier snapshot; no persistent
    per-miner ledger or reputation carried across epochs in this validator.
  notes: Weights are cached for the duration of a weekly epoch and recomputed at the next epoch boundary,
    but this is a per-epoch cache, not persistent per-miner state.

docs.title: Graphite
docs.text:
Subnet GraphiteAI__Graphite-Subnet netuid None Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status full_burn Task Miners solve graph optimization problems — primarily variants of the Traveling Salesman Problem (Metric/General TSP, mTSP, constrained multi-depot mTSP with time windows) and a PortfolioReallocation problem — sent as synthetic requests by validators. Miners return a route/solution (or list of portfo DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, MarketData"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, Other, StakeWeighting"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: other"]
  M1["Metric: similarity"]
  M2["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: graphite

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: full_burn
    submission: api_response

    @burn {
        uid: "20"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: other
                raw: "graph_optimization_objective_cost"
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: rank
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            MarketData {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: l1
            min_weight_floor: 0.2
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.01
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "300 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default 300)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: full_burn
mechanism_status_notes: 'The documented scoring mechanism (TSP/mTSP/portfolio/yield benchmark scaling)
  is fully computed in graphite/validator/reward.py and aggregated into a CompositeScore, but BaseValidatorNeuron.set_weights()
  discards all of it: after computing processed_weights it hardcodes `processed_weight_uids = np.arange(256)`
  / `processed_weights = np.zeros(256)` / `processed_weights[20] = 1`, sending 100% of weight to UID 20
  every epoch. The described scoring path is therefore dormant; emission is concentrated on a single UID.'
subnet:
  name: Graphite
  owner_repo: GraphiteAI__Graphite-Subnet
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners solve graph optimization problems — primarily variants of the Traveling Salesman Problem
    (Metric/General TSP, mTSP, constrained multi-depot mTSP with time windows) and a PortfolioReallocation
    problem — sent as synthetic requests by validators. Miners return a route/solution (or list of portfolio
    swaps), which validators score against a benchmark greedy heuristic and against the best solution
    in the queried cohort. A separate 'yield' competition ranks copy-trading leaders by performance metrics
    (Sharpe, PnL, drawdown, etc.).
  submission_format:
  - api_response
  submission_format_other: ''
scoring_signals:
- name: tour_cost
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: graph_optimization_objective_cost
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: 0.6
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Path cost from the problem's cost function (get_tour_distance / get_multi_minmax_tour_distance
    / _tw). scaled_rewards min-max scales finite scores between 0.2 and 1.0 relative to best_score and
    the benchmark/worst reference; solutions worse than the benchmark get 0, solutions matching it get
    a 0.2 floor. This 'synthetic_score' carries weight 0.6 in CompositeScore.composite_score.
- name: portfolio_distribution_similarity
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: For PortfolioReallocation, scaled_portfolio_rewards normalizes swap-count (1/swaps) and objective-similarity,
    combines them as 0.6*swaps + 0.4*objective, then applies the same 0.2-floor / min-max gap scaling
    vs benchmark. Feeds the 'organic_score' channel (weight 0.2 in CompositeScore).
- name: yield_composite
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.2
  normalization: rank
  notes: 'ScoreYieldResponse ranks copy-trade leaders per-field (ordinal rank normalized 0..1) and takes
    a weighted sum with fixed weights historical_daily_pnl=0.1, sharpe_ratio=0.55, max_drawdown=0.1, num_copy_traders=0.1,
    notional_value_of_copy_traders=0.05, volume=0.1. Feeds ''yield_score'' (weight 0.2). NOTE: yield/organic
    request flows are commented out in base/validator.run(); only the synthetic flow is launched.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Validator generates synthetic graph-optimization problems and computes a benchmark solution
    with a greedy/nearest-neighbour solver (BENCHMARK_SOLUTIONS), scoring miners relative to that benchmark
    and to the best miner in the cohort.
  source_identifier: graphite.utils.constants.BENCHMARK_SOLUTIONS
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Benchmark solver chosen per problem_type (e.g. NearestNeighbourSolver for TSP). The benchmark
    acts as the reference floor (0.2) that miners must beat to score above it.
- kind: market_data
  description: Yield/leaderboard performance data for the copy-trading competition is fetched from an
    external taotrader API and signed by a trusted yield provider hotkey.
  source_identifier: api.yield.taotrader.xyz
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: DEFAULT_YIELD_PROVIDER signer 5GNAzXLXdX749B2ZXPzbromP3rgatDFUpFdHsNxrBKBfA1yi; yield flow is
    currently disabled in run().
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: 0.2
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '20'
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: set_weights() overrides the computed weight vector with a one-hot vector putting weight 1 on
      UID 20 (processed_weights[20] = 1), so 100% of emission is routed to a single UID regardless of
      miner scores. Treated here as a full burn of the documented mechanism.
  notes: 'Documented aggregation: CompositeScore.composite_score = organic*0.2 + synthetic*0.6 + yield*0.2
    (with a 0.7 factor applied where yield is NaN); raw_weights = composite / L1-norm(composite). The
    0.2 min_weight_floor is the matched-benchmark floor in scaled_rewards. This documented vector is computed
    but then discarded (see burn_allocation).'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: L1-normalized composite score computed, then overridden to a one-hot vector on UID 20 before
    convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit and subtensor.set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 300 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default 300)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.01
    window: null
    notes: 'update_scores applies EMA per score channel: current = alpha*scattered_rewards + (1-alpha)*current,
      with neuron.moving_average_alpha default 0.01. (Applied to the scores that are then discarded by
      the one-hot override.)'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: is_valid_solution validates each miner's submission against the problem; invalid solutions
    or False (unsolvable claim) are assigned np.inf (TSP) / (1000000, 0) (portfolio), yielding a reward
    of 0.
- kind: other
  kind_other: benchmark_floor
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Miners scoring worse than the greedy benchmark reference get reward 0; only those matching
    or beating it receive >=0.2 via scaled_rewards/scaled_portfolio_rewards.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: get_miner_uids excludes UIDs with validator_permit or stake >= 2000 or invalid (0.0.0.0)
    IP from being treated as miners; vpermit_tao_limit (default 4096) gates availability.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages: []
  count: 3
  notes: 'Three scoring channels combined in CompositeScore: synthetic (graph optimization, 0.6), organic/portfolio
    (0.2), and yield/copy-trading (0.2). Organic and yield request flows are commented out in run(); only
    synthetic is active. All three are moot under the UID-20 burn.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Scores re-initialized from normalized metagraph incentive on load_state (called every
    360*3 blocks); per-hotkey scores zeroed when a hotkey is replaced in resync_metagraph.
  notes: CompositeScore holds per-channel EMA score arrays (organic_score, synthetic_score, yield_score)
    carried across steps.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/validator.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8
  extracted_at: '2026-06-06'
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: graphite/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 577-579
    quote: "processed_weight_uids = np.arange(256)\n        processed_weights = np.zeros(256, dtype=int)\n\
      \        processed_weights[20] = 1"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation
    source_path: graphite/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 577-587
    quote: processed_weights[20] = 1 ... convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit(uids=processed_weight_uids,
      weights=processed_weights)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: graphite/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 592-600
    quote: result, msg = self.subtensor.set_weights(wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.config.netuid, uids=uint_uids,
      weights=uint_weights, ...)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: graphite/base/validator.py

  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: graphite/validator/reward.py

    quote: '"historical_daily_pnl": 0.1, "sharpe_ratio": 0.55, "max_drawdown": 0.1, "num_copy_traders":

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/normalization
    source_path: graphite/validator/reward.py

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0

docs.title: Handshake58 (HS58)
docs.text:
Subnet Handshake58__HS58 netuid 58 Archetype pipeline Language typescript Mechanism status unknown Task Miners run AI-provider endpoints (LLM / image / audio / code / scraping / VPN / multi-modal services) deployed from the provided templates. Agents pay providers per request via the DRAIN payment-channel protocol (signed USDC vouchers settled on Polygon) or the MPP HTTP-402 per-request protocol. Bitt DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData, OrganicTraffic"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: LivenessCheck, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: volume"]
  M1["Metric: uptime"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Other"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: handshake58Hs58

    netuid: 58
    lang: typescript
    status: unknown
    submission: compute_service

    @guards {
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: volume
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            MarketData {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            OrganicTraffic {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: other
        }
        smooth: Other {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: undocumented
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: unknown
mechanism_status_notes: The README documents a live scoring mechanism (60% DRAIN claims, 40% availability,
  7-day rolling window) and a fixed Yuma-consensus emission split (41% miners / 41% validators / 18% subnet
  owner), but the validator implementation that sets weights is in the separate HS58-subnet repo, which
  is not present in this corpus tree. No burn fraction, alpha, or scoring constant is in-source here,
  so the operative reality (active vs burning) cannot be confirmed from this repo.
subnet:
  name: Handshake58 (HS58)
  owner_repo: Handshake58__HS58
  netuid: 58
  implementation_lang: typescript
task:
  summary: Miners run AI-provider endpoints (LLM / image / audio / code / scraping / VPN / multi-modal
    services) deployed from the provided templates. Agents pay providers per request via the DRAIN payment-channel
    protocol (signed USDC vouchers settled on Polygon) or the MPP HTTP-402 per-request protocol. Bittensor
    Subnet 58 validators score providers trustlessly on real on-chain DRAIN ChannelClaimed events (claimed
    USDC volume) and on availability/health-check responses, then set weights so providers earn TAO incentives.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: drain_claims
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.6
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: Scored relative to the top provider over a 7-day rolling window of on-chain ChannelClaimed
    (USDC) events; exact normalization formula is in the (absent) HS58-subnet validator, not in this repo.
  notes: '"60% DRAIN Claims - Real USDC claimed from payment channels (7-day rolling window)". The validator
    scans Polygon ChannelClaimed events.'
- name: availability
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.4
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: Provider must respond to validator health checks with valid wallet proof; exact
    scoring is in the absent HS58-subnet validator.
  notes: '"40% Availability - Provider responds to validator health checks with valid wallet proof".'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: Real on-chain DRAIN ChannelClaimed events (USDC claimed by providers) on the DRAIN Channel
    V2 contract on Polygon mainnet, over a 7-day rolling window.
  source_identifier: polygon:0x0C2B3aA1e80629D572b1f200e6DF3586B3946A8A
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: DRAIN Channel V2 contract address per README Contract Addresses table; the validator scans ChannelClaimed
    events emitted by this contract.
- kind: organic_traffic
  description: Validator health checks against the provider endpoint, requiring a valid wallet proof response
    (availability signal).
  source_identifier: ''
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Availability is measured by the validator probing providers; provider must prove wallet ownership.
    Exact probe logic lives in the absent HS58-subnet repo.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: Documented as scored relative to the top provider; the precise normalization and
    any softmax/sum-to-one step are in the absent HS58-subnet validator.
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn is documented or implemented in this repo. Emission split is the fixed Yuma-consensus
      41/41/18 (miners/validators/subnet owner), not a burn.
  notes: '"60% DRAIN Claims ... 40% Availability". Combined as a weighted sum of the two signals. "The
    validator scores relative to the top provider." The literal combination/normalization code is not
    in this repo.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: undocumented
  method: Validator scans Polygon ChannelClaimed events (7-day rolling window) plus availability health
    checks, computes a per-provider score (60/40), and sets weights on Subnet 58. The implementation is
    in the separate HS58-subnet repo (NEURON_TYPE=validator), not present here; cadence/tempo are not
    stated.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: null
  smoothing:
    kind: other
    kind_other: 7-day rolling window over on-chain claim events (a rolling-volume window, not an EMA of
      the final reward). Window length stated in days, not blocks/rounds.
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: '"7-day rolling window" is documented for the DRAIN-claims signal; whether the final reward
      is additionally EMA-smoothed is unknown (validator code absent).'
anti_gaming:
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Availability component (40%): providers must respond to validator health checks with valid
    wallet proof; failing/unavailable providers lose that portion of score. "Miner auto-registers on handshake58.com,
    validator scores you."'
- kind: other
  kind_other: on_chain_payment_proof
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'The DRAIN-claims signal is grounded in real on-chain USDC ChannelClaimed events: a provider
    must actually deliver inference and claim a real voucher to score. The DRAIN contract requires distinct
    consumer and provider addresses, so a single wallet cannot self-pay. Self-test payments are real economic
    transactions (~$0.13 each), raising the cost of fabricating score.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Three documented provider TYPES (TAO Miners auto-verified, DRAIN Providers, MPP Providers) and
    provider categories (llm/image/audio/code/scraping/vpn/ multi-modal/other), but no tiered/laddered
    scoring competition is documented.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: 'DRAIN-claims score is a 7-day rolling window: claims older than 7 days drop out, so score
    decays unless refreshed by new claims ("No payments = no claims = no score").'
  notes: A per-provider tao_score is maintained and synced on handshake58.com. The underlying storage/state
    machine is in the absent marketplace/HS58-subnet code.
documentation:
  status: docs_only
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/score-keepalive.md
  - docs/thesis.html
  completeness: medium
provenance:

  confidence_overall: low

docs.title: HashTensor (Subnet 16)
docs.text:
Subnet HashTensor__HashTensor_Subnet netuid 16 Archetype multiplicative Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners contribute hashrate to HashTensor's Kaspa (PoW) mining pool via the Kaspa Stratum Bridge. The pool exports per-worker Prometheus metrics (valid shares, share difficulty, uptime). Miners register a Bittensor hotkey -> Kaspa worker mapping with a signature. Validators score each hotkey on "Proo DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: HardwareProbe"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CommitReveal, RateLimit, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: throughput"]
  M1["Metric: uptime"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: hashTensorSubnet16

    netuid: 16
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: compute_service

    @guards {
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }

    Multiplicative {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: HardwareProbe {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: multiplicative
            normalization: min_max
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "3600 seconds (set_weights_interval default 60 min)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Validator computes per-hotkey ratings from Kaspa pool metrics and sets them as
  on-chain weights every set_weights_interval (default 60 min). No emission burn in the weight-setting
  path. The README describes an off-chain Alpha buyback-and-burn funded by pool fees (manual, announced
  on X), which is an economic mechanism on the Alpha token, not an emission/weight burn.
subnet:
  name: HashTensor (Subnet 16)
  owner_repo: HashTensor__HashTensor_Subnet
  netuid: 16
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners contribute hashrate to HashTensor's Kaspa (PoW) mining pool via the Kaspa Stratum Bridge.
    The pool exports per-worker Prometheus metrics (valid shares, share difficulty, uptime). Miners register
    a Bittensor hotkey -> Kaspa worker mapping with a signature. Validators score each hotkey on "Proof
    of Effective Work" (valid_shares x difficulty) penalized by uptime, and set on-chain weights accordingly.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: effective_work
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: min_max
  notes: 'Per worker: valid_shares * min(difficulty, max_difficulty) * difficulty_penalty * share_penalty,
    summed over a hotkey''s workers (compute_effective_work). Normalized by dividing by the max effective_work
    across all hotkeys (work[hotkey]/max_work). difficulty_penalty = exp(-(difficulty-max_difficulty)/max_difficulty)
    when difficulty exceeds max_difficulty (default 16384.0). share_penalty = exp(-excess/allowed_shares)
    when valid_shares exceed allowed (shares_per_minute=20 over the window).'
- name: average_uptime
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Average fractional uptime across a hotkey's workers (uptime_seconds/window_seconds, clamped to
    [0,1]). Applied multiplicatively as (avg_uptime ** uptime_alpha) with uptime_alpha default 2.0, gating
    the normalized effective_work score. Final score clamped to [0.0, 1.0].
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: 'Prometheus endpoint of the HashTensor Kaspa pool (default http://pool.hashtensor.com:9090)
    exporting Kaspa Stratum Bridge counters: ks_valid_share_counter, ks_invalid_share_counter, ks_valid_share_diff_counter,
    ks_miner_uptime_seconds, ks_miner_work_seconds_total, keyed by (wallet, worker).'
  source_identifier: http://pool.hashtensor.com:9090
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Centralized pool-owned Prometheus; metrics filtered to the pool owner wallet (kaspa_pool_owner_wallet).
    Validators trust the pool's reported share/uptime data.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: min_max
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No emission/weight burn in code. README describes an off-chain Alpha buyback-and-burn (target
      90% of each buyback) funded by KAS pool fees, executed manually via Bittensor's native burn extrinsic.
      Not represented in weight setting.
  notes: Final rating = clamp(normalized_effective_work * avg_uptime^alpha, 0, 1). Normalization is max-scaling
    (divide by max work across hotkeys), not sum-to-one; the chain (Yuma) normalizes the submitted weights.
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: set_weights_task runs on a schedule; skips if now - last_set < set_weights_interval (default
    60 min). Computes ratings via Validator.compute_ratings, then calls set_weights, which maps hotkey
    ratings to node weights (0 for unrated nodes) and calls weights.set_node_weights via fiber.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 3600 seconds (set_weights_interval default 60 min)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA on the final reward. The scoring window is a rolling 60-minute Prometheus window (config.window
      default timedelta(minutes=60)); each cycle recomputes from scratch.
anti_gaming:
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Hotkey<->worker registration requires a signature; verify_signature(hotkey, worker, signature)
    uses Keypair.verify and rejects invalid signatures (config.verify_signature default True). Registration
    also checked against registration_time_tolerance (default 1 min).
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Share-rate penalty: if valid_shares exceed allowed_shares (window_minutes * shares_per_minute,
    shares_per_minute=20), an exponential penalty exp(-excess/allowed_shares) is applied to that worker''s
    effective work.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Difficulty cap: difficulty clamped to max_difficulty (default 16384.0) and an exponential
    penalty exp(-(difficulty-max_difficulty)/max_difficulty) applied when a worker reports difficulty
    above the cap.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Single PoW network (Kaspa) at present; README states support for additional PoW coins is planned
    but not implemented in code.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: Ratings recomputed each cycle from a rolling metrics window; no persistent per-miner score/reputation

docs.title: It's AI - Decentralized AI Detection
docs.text:
Subnet It-s-AI__llm-detection netuid 32 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status unknown Task Miners receive a list of texts and return, per word, a predicted probability that the word is AI/LLM-generated (List[List[float]] of probabilities in [0,1]). Validators build a balanced mix of human-written texts (sampled from the Pile and Common Crawl) and AI-generated texts (completions/in-the-mid DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, ReferenceModel"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, DeterministicCheck, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: other"]
  M2["Metric: accuracy"]
  M3["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: itSAiDecentralizedAiDetection

    netuid: 32
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: signals

    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, performance_ledger }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: classification_quality
                specific: fpr_complement
                raw: "false_positive_rate_complement"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: classification_quality
                specific: validity_penalty
                raw: "validity_penalty_multiplier"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            ReferenceModel {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma {
            normalization: softmax
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "100"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
subnet:
  name: It's AI - Decentralized AI Detection
  owner_repo: It-s-AI__llm-detection
  netuid: 32
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners receive a list of texts and return, per word, a predicted probability that the word
    is AI/LLM-generated (List[List[float]] of probabilities in [0,1]). Validators build a balanced mix
    of human-written texts (sampled from the Pile and Common Crawl) and AI-generated texts (completions/in-the-middle
    generations from 30+ open-source LLMs run via Ollama), apply augmentations/adversarial attacks, and
    score each miner's predictions against the per-word human/AI ground-truth labels using F1, a false-positive
    score, and average-precision. The per-step reward is the average of those three metrics, multiplied
    by a 0/1 penalty.
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: f1_score
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: sklearn f1_score(y_true, round(y_pred)). One of three equally-weighted components averaged into
    the reward.
- name: fp_score
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: false_positive_rate_complement
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: FP_score = 1 - FP / len(samples). Penalizes classifying human-written text as AI-generated; chosen
    because false positives are considered more costly.
- name: ap_score
  metric_kind: accuracy
  metric_kind_other: ''
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: average_precision_score(y_true, y_pred) - area summarizing the precision-recall curve, evaluating
    the ranking quality of the predicted probabilities.
- name: penalty
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: validity_penalty_multiplier
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: count_penalty returns 0 if any prediction is <0 or >1, if check-id predictions don't match the
    full-set predictions, or if the miner answered a probe sent with a random (wrong) version; also forced
    to 0 if the miner's out-of-domain F1 < 0.9. miner_reward is multiplied by this penalty.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Human-written texts sampled from the Pile (monology/pile-uncopyrighted, streaming) and
    from Common Crawl 2023 dumps. Sampling is 80:40 Pile:CommonCrawl; Common-Crawl samples are treated
    as the out-of-domain validation set.
  source_identifier: monology/pile-uncopyrighted ; Common Crawl 2023 dumps
  trust_model: trusted
- kind: reference_model
  description: AI-generated texts produced by 30+ open-source LLMs run through Ollama (llama2/3/3.x, qwen,
    command-r, gemma2, mistral, internlm2.5, deepseek, phi4, aya, yi, athene, etc.), using both full text-completion
    and in-the-middle regeneration, with randomized generation parameters. These provide the AI-labeled
    half of the balanced dataset.
  source_identifier: Ollama models in neurons/validator.py / data_generator.py
  trust_model: trusted
aggregation:
  method: exponential_decay_ema
  normalization: softmax
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    notes: ''
  notes: 'Two stages. Per forward step, raw rewards are passed through Softmax(rewards*100) over queried
    miners. Then update_scores applies a per-uid exponential moving average: scores = alpha*reward + (1-alpha)*scores,
    with alpha=moving_average_alpha (0.2) once a uid has counter>=counter_threshold (10) and alpha_early
    (0.3) before that. At weight time the EMA scores are L1-normalized (torch.nn.functional.normalize
    p=1) into raw weights. decay_rate/min_weight_floor not expressed as literal constants.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: set_weights L1-normalizes the EMA scores and emits them via process_weights_for_netuid / convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit.
    Weights are set when (block - metagraph.last_update[uid]) > neuron.epoch_length (default 100 blocks
    of ~12s).
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: '100'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'count_penalty: a random subset of challenges (check_ids, 1-10 items) is re-queried separately;
    if the miner''s predictions for those ids differ (rounded to 2 dp) from the full-set predictions,
    or any prediction is outside [0,1], penalty=0 (reward zeroed for that step).'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Version probe: with probability 0.2 a synapse is sent with a random (in-range but wrong)
    version string; an honest miner running the real code returns empty predictions, so any non-empty
    version_response sets penalty=0.'
- kind: other
  kind_other: data_augmentation_adversarial
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Texts are augmented before being sent (random consecutive-sentence subsampling, char-level
    misspellings, adjective removal) to prevent miners precomputing hashes over the Pile and memorizing
    membership.
- kind: other
  kind_other: out_of_domain_quality_gate
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Common-Crawl (out-of-domain) samples are scored separately; an EMA of each miner's out-of-domain
    F1 is kept (out_of_domain_alpha=0.2) and if it falls below neuron.out_of_domain_min_f1_score (default
    0.9) the miner's penalty is set to 0.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'has_enough_stake gate: a miner whose coldkey alpha stake minus min_alpha_amount is negative
    gets reward 0 for the step (default min_alpha_amount=0).'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Single classification task. Out-of-domain (Common Crawl) vs in-domain (Pile) scoring is a quality
    gate within the one mechanism, not a separate competition.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - performance_ledger
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: On metagraph resync, if a uid's hotkey changed the score is reset to default_score_value
    (0) and its counter reset to 0; otherwise the counter is incremented each resync.
  notes: Persistent per-uid EMA scores (self.scores) and a counter (self.counter) controlling early vs
    steady alpha, plus a per-uid out-of-domain F1 EMA (self.out_of_domain_f1_scores). scores/counter/hotkeys
    are saved to state.pt and reloaded on restart.

docs.title: Distributed Training Subnet
docs.text:
Subnet KMFODA__DistributedTraining netuid None Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners collaboratively pre-train a shared LLM (default global_model_name "llama-4b-ws-4"): each miner runs inner training steps and uploads a pseudo-gradient (gradients.pt) to a Cloudflare R2 bucket. Validators score a miner by applying that miner's pseudo-gradient to the shared model and measuring DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: LivenessCheck, DeterministicCheck, StakeWeighting"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: loss"]
  M1["Metric: loss"]
  M2["Metric: uptime"]
  M3["Metric: uptime"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: distributedTrainingSubnet

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: model_weights

    @guards {
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { reputation, cumulative_score, performance_ledger }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: loss
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: rank
            },
            Metric {
                kind: loss
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: ema
            },
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: rank
            },
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.6
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "epoch_length (default 160 blocks)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: 'Live scoring is implemented and wired into weight-setting: update_total_scores
  combines a train.score (OpenSkill-rated loss improvement) and an all_reduce.score, normalises, and feeds
  total.score into update_scores -> set_weights. No burn allocation or weight-copying short-circuit found
  in the source.'
subnet:
  name: Distributed Training Subnet
  owner_repo: KMFODA__DistributedTraining
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners collaboratively pre-train a shared LLM (default global_model_name "llama-4b-ws-4"):
    each miner runs inner training steps and uploads a pseudo-gradient (gradients.pt) to a Cloudflare
    R2 bucket. Validators score a miner by applying that miner''s pseudo-gradient to the shared model
    and measuring the loss improvement on random unseen data and on the miner''s assigned data, plus whether
    the miner participated successfully in the periodic all-reduce round.'
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
scoring_signals:
- name: train_random_loss_improvement
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.75
  normalization: rank
  notes: Relative loss improvement (before-after)/before from applying the miner's pseudo-gradient on
    a random unseen evaluation block, fed into an OpenSkill PlackettLuce rating; train.random.score is
    the OpenSkill ordinal. Part of train.score which carries TRAIN_SCORE_WEIGHT = 0.75 in update_total_scores.
    weight reflects TRAIN_SCORE_WEIGHT (combined train signal).
- name: train_assigned_loss_gate
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: ema
  notes: Binary indicator (assigned.absolute > random.absolute) smoothed by an EMA with assigned_loss_score_moving_average_alpha
    = 0.05. train.score = max(0, random.score * assigned.score), so this acts multiplicatively (gate)
    rather than additively -> weight null.
- name: all_reduce_participation
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.25
  normalization: rank
  notes: all_reduce.score = 1 if the miner's allreduce_status_dict entry is SUCCESS or NON_PARTICIPATING,
    else 0. Carries ALL_REDUCE_SCORE_WEIGHT = 0.25 in update_total_scores. normalization rank is the closest
    enum to the L1-norm normalisation applied across all miners.
- name: repo_validity
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: train.is_valid (score_repo) checks that the miner's R2 gradients.pt manifest exists and its age
    is below max_upload_interval (MAX_UPLOAD_INTERVAL = 4800 s). total.score is multiplied by repo_valid_score,
    so it is a 0/1 gate -> weight null.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Evaluation data fetched via DatasetLoader.next_pages over fineweb-style pages, seeded by
    block / uid; loss is computed by the shared model with labels=inputs (next-token LM loss).
  source_identifier: DatasetLoader (distributed_training/data/dataset.py)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Both 'random' unseen blocks and the miner's 'assigned' blocks are drawn deterministically from
    the dataset/block seeds; the shared global model provides the reference loss.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn allocation found in reward.py or set_weights.
  notes: 'update_total_scores: normalized_train_score = (0.75 * train.score)/L1(train_scores), normalized_all_reduce_score
    = (0.25 * all_reduce.score)/L1(all_reduce_scores), and total.score = (normalized_train_score + normalized_all_reduce_score)
    * repo_valid_score. Hybrid: train and all_reduce combine additively (weighted), while repo_valid acts
    as a multiplicative gate and the assigned-loss signal gates train.score.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: set_weights normalises self.scores by its L1 norm to raw_weights, runs process_weights_for_netuid
    (quantile exclusion + normalize_max_weight), converts to uint16 and calls subtensor.set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: epoch_length (default 160 blocks)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.6
    window: null
    notes: update_scores applies self.scores = alpha*scattered_rewards + (1-alpha)*self.scores with alpha
      = moving_average_alpha (default 0.6) on total.score before weights are set.
anti_gaming:
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: score_repo / benchmark_uids require a recent R2 gradients.pt manifest (age < MAX_UPLOAD_INTERVAL
    = 4800 s); stale or missing manifest sets train.is_valid False, zeroing total.score.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: score_uids resets a UID's train.random/train.assigned scores to 0 (reset_uid_train_scores)
    on any evaluation error, on a revision with 0 inner steps, or if the score_status all-reduce across
    ranks does not equal world_size.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: vpermit_tao_limit (default 40960) caps the TAO a vpermit validator may be queried with;
    failed_is_alive_counter above threshold zeroes a UID's score in update_scores.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - reputation
  - cumulative_score
  - performance_ledger
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: UidTracker reset on hotkey replacement / metagraph growth (resync_metagraph); per-round
    train scores reset to 0 on evaluation failure (reset_uid_train_scores).
  notes: Per-UID UidTracker holds train (random/assigned scores, openskill_rating mu/sigma, is_valid,
    revision), all_reduce (score, count) and total.score. OpenSkill PlackettLuce ratings (beta=7, tau=0.1)
    persist per UID across rounds.
documentation:
  status: code_authoritative
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: low

  extracted_by: im-extract
  confidence_overall: high

docs.title: Level114
docs.text:
Subnet Level114__level114-subnet netuid 114 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners operate publicly reachable Minecraft servers running the required Level114 plugin set. A collector-center-main service ingests signed server reports (TPS, player counts, plugins, memory, uptime). Validators fetch these reports plus an independent scanner snapshot and score each server on infr DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: OrganicTraffic, HardwareProbe"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, LivenessCheck, CommitReveal"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: throughput"]
  M1["Metric: composite"]
  M2["Metric: engagement"]
  M3["Metric: engagement"]
  M4["Metric: composite"]
  M5["Metric: composite"]
  M6["Metric: engagement"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  G --> M5
  GT --> M5
  G --> M6
  GT --> M6
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  M5 --> AGG
  M6 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: level114

    netuid: 114
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: api_response

    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: engagement
                direction: target_band
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: engagement
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: engagement
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            OrganicTraffic {
                trust_model: adversarial
            },
            HardwareProbe {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.2
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "weight_update_interval default 1200s (20 min); validation_interval default 1440s (24 min)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: 'Validator computes per-server scores (0-1000) and sets weights directly as score/1000
  with no burn allocation. Two mechanisms run concurrently via Bittensor multi-mechanism set_weights (mechid
  0 = Minecraft, mechid 1 = TCL). Note: docs/scoring.md documents primary weights of 40/35/25 (infra/part/rely),
  but the live constants.py uses 20/20/60 — code is authoritative.'
subnet:
  name: Level114
  owner_repo: Level114__level114-subnet
  netuid: 114
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners operate publicly reachable Minecraft servers running the required Level114 plugin set.
    A collector-center-main service ingests signed server reports (TPS, player counts, plugins, memory,
    uptime). Validators fetch these reports plus an independent scanner snapshot and score each server
    on infrastructure (TPS), participation (plugin compliance + player activity), and reliability (player-power
    + TPS stability + recovery). A second mechanism (TCL) scores community/engagement metrics (online
    players, playtime, daily logins, monthly new users). Scores map to on-chain weight = score/1000.
  submission_format:
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: tps_performance
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.2
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Infrastructure component (W_INFRA=0.20). tps_score = clamp(tps_actual,0,20)/IDEAL_TPS, capped
    at 1.0; multiplied by 0.1 if tps_actual < MIN_TPS_THRESHOLD (5.0). W_INFRA_TPS=1.0.
- name: plugin_compliance
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Participation sub-component (W_PART_COMPLIANCE=0.857). 0.6 base if has_required_plugins, plus
    up to 0.4 from bonus plugins (ViaVersion/ViaBackwards/ViaRewind). Combined into participation via
    W_PART=0.20.
- name: player_activity
  metric_kind: engagement
  direction: target_band
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Participation sub-component (W_PART_PLAYERS=0.143). player_count/MAX_PLAYERS_WEIGHT(200), *1.2
    if ratio in [0.2,0.8], *0.8 if ratio>0.95. Requires >=5 players (MIN_PLAYERS_FOR_BONUS).
- name: player_power
  metric_kind: engagement
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: 'Reliability sub-component (W_RELY_PLAYER_POWER=0.90). Cross-server normalized player presence:
    each player''s reported power averaged then split per server, then divided by the max server total.
    Dominant single signal since W_RELY=0.60.'
- name: tps_stability
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Reliability sub-component (W_RELY_STABILITY=0.05). 1 - (coefficient_of_variation / MAX_TPS_COEFFICIENT_OF_VARIATION(0.3))
    over last 20 reports (TPS_STABILITY_WINDOW).
- name: recovery_speed
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Reliability sub-component (W_RELY_RECOVERY=0.05). Penalizes time to recover above RECOVERY_TPS_THRESHOLD(18.0)
    within MAX_RECOVERY_TIME_MINUTES(30).
- name: tcl_engagement
  metric_kind: engagement
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Separate TCL mechanism (mechid 1). Weighted sum of normalized online_players(0.20), avg_playtime_minutes(0.30),
    daily_unique_logins(0.20), monthly_new_users(0.30), each min-max clamped against MAX_VALUES, scaled
    to 0-1000.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: organic_traffic
  description: Signed server reports (TPS, players, plugins, memory, uptime) streamed to the collector-center-main
    service by miners' Minecraft servers; validators fetch historical reports via the collector API.
  source_identifier: collector.level114.io (collector-center-main API)
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Reports carry ed25519 signatures and counter/nonce replay protection (per docs). Validators additionally
    cross-check against an independent scanner snapshot (online status, max_players, player count) and
    zero the score on mismatch.
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: Independent scanner snapshot used to verify server liveness and detect report inflation
    (max_players mismatch, player_count > scanner_players + 5).
  source_identifier: scanner_entry (validator-side)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Server scored 0 if scanner missing, offline, or mismatched with report.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn logic present; weight = clamp(score/1000, 0, 1) per miner.
  notes: raw = W_INFRA*infra + W_PART*part + W_RELY*rely (0.20/0.20/0.60), each component itself a weighted
    sum / gated sum of sub-signals. Normalized to integer 0-1000 via MIN_SCORE..MAX_SCORE. Hard zero-score
    gates (scanner mismatch, missing/stale reports, offline) override the weighted sum. Final per-mechanism
    weights are score/1000, then process_weights_for_netuid applies Bittensor's standard min-stake/max-limit
    processing.
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Per mechanism (mechid), build raw_weights[uid] = clamp(score/1000, 0, 1) for each scored hotkey,
    run process_weights_for_netuid, then subtensor.set_weights with the mechid. Skipped if no positive
    weights. Gated by should_update (update_interval + next_update). Two mechanisms (0=Minecraft, 1=TCL)
    set independently.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: weight_update_interval default 1200s (20 min); validation_interval default 1440s
    (24 min)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.2
    window: null
    notes: 'apply_score_smoothing: smoothed = EMA_ALPHA*new + (1-EMA_ALPHA)*previous with EMA_ALPHA=0.2,
      then clamped so per-update change is within [MIN_SCORE_CHANGE=1, MAX_SCORE_CHANGE=200]; sub-MIN_SCORE_CHANGE
      deltas keep the previous score.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Cross-check report vs independent scanner: if scanner missing/offline, or scanner_max_players
    != report_max_players, or report_player_count > scanner_players + 5, the server is assigned a zero
    score.'
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Reports older than 6h (filter_fresh_reports max_age 6*3600*1000 ms) are dropped; if all
    reports stale or collector returns none, score downgraded to 0.
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Reports carry ed25519 signatures, SHA256 payload hash, and nonce/counter replay protection
    (per docs/scoring.md). Signature failures cap score (docs); validator parses and discards malformed
    reports.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: MAX_PLAYERS_WEIGHT=200 caps player-activity contribution (anti-whale); player_power normalized
    cross-server so concentrating players on one server does not inflate.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: Minecraft (mechid 0)
    role: main
    advancement_rule: infra/participation/reliability server scoring; weight=score/1000
  - name: TCL (mechid 1)
    role: other
    advancement_rule: community engagement metrics scoring; weight=score/1000
  count: 2
  notes: Each mechanism produces its own scores and sets weights independently with its mechid via Bittensor
    multi-mechanism set_weights.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: score_cache holds previous smoothed score per server_id for EMA smoothing; reset to 0
    on scanner mismatch, offline, missing or stale (>6h) reports.
  notes: ScoreCacheEntry(score, raw_score, components, updated_at) per server_id; bounded history deque
    of up to MAX_REPORT_HISTORY=60 reports feeds stability/recovery. Classification thresholds (excellent>=850,
    good>=650, poor<300) are display-only.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
