composition:
  shape: multiplex
  overlays:
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: LogicNet
docs.text:
Subnet LogicNet-Subnet__LogicNet netuid None Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners receive a mathematical / logical reasoning question and return both a reasoning trace (logic_reasoning) and a final answer (logic_answer). The validator rewards each response by a weighted sum of cosine similarity between the miner's reasoning and a validator-generated ground-truth reasoning, DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Honeypot, DeterministicCheck, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: similarity"]
  M1["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  M2["Metric: latency"]
  AGG["aggregate: RankBased"]
  SM["smooth: Sma"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: logicNet

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: signals

    @guards {
        Honeypot {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, reputation }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: LlmJudgment {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: RankBased {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Sma {
            window: 5
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "50 blocks (neuron.epoch_length, in 12s blocks)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: single
            count: 1
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: 'Scoring is live: per-response rewards (similarity + correctness - time penalty)
  feed a rank-based cubic incentive, which is averaged over the last 5 epochs and normalized to set on-chain
  weights. No burn allocation is present in the code. The on-chain set_weights call is active (gated only
  by --neuron.disable_set_weights, default False).'
subnet:
  name: LogicNet
  owner_repo: LogicNet-Subnet__LogicNet
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners receive a mathematical / logical reasoning question and return both a reasoning trace
    (logic_reasoning) and a final answer (logic_answer). The validator rewards each response by a weighted
    sum of cosine similarity between the miner's reasoning and a validator-generated ground-truth reasoning,
    an LLM-judged (or programmatically computed) correctness of the final answer, and a small processing-time
    penalty. Per-epoch mean rewards are ranked and converted to a cubic rank-based incentive that determines
    on-chain weights.
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: similarity
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: none
  notes: Cosine similarity (sentence-transformers all-MiniLM-L6-v2) between validator-generated ground-truth
    text and the miner's logic_reasoning. README documents 0.2, but rewarder.py uses SIMILARITY_WEIGHT
    = 0.3.
- name: correctness
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.7
  normalization: none
  notes: Final-answer correctness in [0,1]. First attempts programmatic numerical comparison (_compare_numerical_answers);
    falls back to LLM judgment (CORRECTNESS_TEMPLATE). Cheat words, template responses, and detected trick
    answers score -1. README documents 0.8, but rewarder.py uses CORRECTNESS_WEIGHT = 0.7.
- name: processing_time_penalty
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: -0.05
  normalization: none
  notes: PROCESSING_TIME_WEIGHT = -0.05 multiplied by min(process_time / timeout, 1). README documents
    -0.1, but rewarder.py uses -0.05.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: Validator self-generates the reference reasoning/ground-truth text for the similarity signal
    by calling an LLM (model_selector task_type=create_task) on the raw question. Correctness is judged
    by an LLM against base_synapse.ground_truth_answer, or computed programmatically for numerical answers.
  source_identifier: model_selector / OpenAI-compatible LLM pool; CORRECTNESS_TEMPLATE
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Questions/ground-truth answers are pulled from an external TaskPoolServer (TASK_POOL_URL).
aggregation:
  method: rank_based
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn allocation found in the reward or weight-setting code.
  notes: Per-response reward = SIMILARITY_WEIGHT*sim + CORRECTNESS_WEIGHT*correct + PROCESSING_TIME_WEIGHT*min(t/timeout,1),
    then rescaled reward/2 + 0.5. Per epoch, each UID's mean reward is computed (negatives floored to
    0), UIDs ranked descending, and a cubic rank function incentive = -1.038e-7*rank^3 + 6.214e-5*rank^2
    - 0.0129*rank - 0.0118 + 1 is applied. Only miners with mean score > 0.3 and rank <= 160 get their
    true-rank incentive; all others receive incentive_formula(250). These incentives are stored per UID;
    on-chain weights are the mean of the last 5 stored scores per UID (get_model_specific_weights), clamped
    to [0,1] and normalized to sum to 1, then scaled by category incentive_weight = 1.0.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: update_scores_on_chain sets validator scores to the per-category normalized mean-of-last-5-scores
    weights (incentive_weight=1.0). set_weights then L1-normalizes scores, runs process_weights_for_netuid,
    and calls subtensor.set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 50 blocks (neuron.epoch_length, in 12s blocks)
  smoothing:
    kind: sma
    alpha: null
    window: 5
    notes: 'On-chain weight per UID is the simple mean of the last NO_OF_RECENT_SCORES = 5 stored incentive
      scores (get_model_specific_weights). Note: base/validator.py also defines an EMA update_scores using
      config.neuron.moving_average_alpha, but the active validator overrides self.scores via update_scores_on_chain,
      so the live path is the 5-sample SMA, not the EMA.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: honeypot
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Cheat-word list fetched from TaskPoolServer (/cheats); any response containing a cheat
    word returns correctness -1. Responses matching the regex {{ answer }} template also return -1.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'LLM-based trick detection (DETECT_TRICK_TEMPLATE): if the cleaned response is judged a
    trick (''yes''), correctness is set to -1.'
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Per-validator request rate limit derived from stake-weighted epoch volume, clamped to [MIN_RATE_LIMIT=2,
    MAX_RATE_LIMIT=80]. reward_scale = clamp(epoch_volume/512, 0, 1).
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Only validators with total_stake >= MIN_STAKE (10000) receive query volume; min_stake gates
    rate-limit allocation.
sub_competitions:
  structure: single
  stages: []
  count: 1
  notes: Single category 'Logic' with incentive_weight 1.0 (init_category). The code is structured to
    support multiple categories but only one is defined.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - reputation
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Scores and reward_logs are kept as a rolling window of the last NO_OF_RECENT_SCORES =
    5 entries. A UID's score is zeroed when its hotkey is replaced (resync_metagraph).
  notes: MinerInfo holds per-UID category, recent scores list, epoch_volume, rate_limit, reward_scale,
    and reward_logs; refreshed each epoch.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/VALIDATOR.md
  - docs/MINER.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: logicnet/validator/rewarder.py
    line_ref: 1-8
    quote: 'import torch

      import openai

      import sympy

      import bittensor as bt'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: logicnet/validator/rewarder.py
    line_ref: '18'
    quote: SIMILARITY_WEIGHT = 0.3
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/weight
    source_path: logicnet/validator/rewarder.py
    line_ref: '19'
    quote: CORRECTNESS_WEIGHT = 0.7
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/weight
    source_path: logicnet/validator/rewarder.py
    line_ref: '20'
    quote: PROCESSING_TIME_WEIGHT = -0.05
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: logicnet/validator/rewarder.py
    line_ref: 118-127
    quote: reward = (SIMILARITY_WEIGHT * similarities[i] + CORRECTNESS_WEIGHT * correctness[i] + PROCESSING_TIME_WEIGHT
      * min(process_times[i] / timeout, 1)) ... reward = reward / 2 + 0.5
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind
    source_path: logicnet/validator/rewarder.py
    line_ref: 415-437
    quote: Calculate cosine similarity between self-generated ground truth and miner responses. ... similarity
      = torch.nn.functional.cosine_similarity(...)
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: logicnet/validator/rewarder.py
    line_ref: 206-218
    quote: 'score = self._compare_numerical_answers(ground_truth_answer, miner_answer) ... else: # Need
      LLM evaluation'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description
    source_path: logicnet/validator/rewarder.py
    line_ref: 443-455
    quote: Generate self-generated ground truth based on the question. ... model, base_url, api_key =
      model_selector(self.model_pool, task_type="create_task")
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: neurons/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 404-408
    quote: 'def incentive_formula(rank): reward_value = -1.038e-7 * rank**3 + 6.214e-5 * rank**2 - 0.0129
      * rank - 0.0118 ... scaled_reward_value = reward_value + 1'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 379-420
    quote: 'final_rewards = [sum(uid_rewards) / len(uid_rewards) ...] ... if score > 0.3 and rank <= 160:
      incentive_rewards.append(incentive_formula(rank)) else: incentive_rewards.append(incentive_formula(250))'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/window
    source_path: logicnet/validator/miner_manager.py
    line_ref: '11'
    quote: NO_OF_RECENT_SCORES = 5
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: logicnet/validator/miner_manager.py
    line_ref: 181-192
    quote: model_specific_weights[int(uid)] = (sum(self.all_uids_info[uid].scores[-NO_OF_RECENT_SCORES:])
      / NO_OF_RECENT_SCORES) ... model_specific_weights = model_specific_weights / tensor_sum
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator/validator.py

  - subnet.netuid — config default --netuid is 1, which is a placeholder default, not the real LogicNet
    netuid; not asserted.
  - aggregation.min_weight_floor — get_on_chain_weights adds 1e-6, but the active path get_model_specific_weights
    has no floor; ambiguous, left null.
  - moving_average_alpha (EMA alpha) — referenced in base/validator.py update_scores but not used on the
    live weight path; no literal default found, left null.

    but have no dedicated schema field.

docs.title: MiaoAI
docs.text:
Subnet MIAOAI-Subnet__MIAOAI_SUBNET netuid 86 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Validators dispatch AI inference tasks (text classification, sentiment analysis, scene understanding, object detection, emotion analysis) to miners. Miners run AI models and return a structured JSON response (e.g. class_id+confidence, sentiment_score+confidence, objects list, description+confidence) DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: StakeWeighting, DeterministicCheck, CodeInspection"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: other"]
  M2["Metric: composite"]
  M3["Metric: latency"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: miaoAi

    netuid: 86
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: api_response

    @guards {
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, cumulative_score, reputation }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                raw: "stake_share"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: ema
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
            min_weight_floor: 0.001
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "tempo * (1/3)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
            count: 5
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: 'Validator computes per-miner quality scores from real task evaluation and sets
  normalized weights on-chain via set_weights. Not a burn loop, but a documented owner-fallback: when
  no miner has a positive current-cycle quality score, ALL weight is assigned to the subnet owner uid
  at owner_default_score (0.2). A server-controlled `marrs` list lets favored miners be assigned a random
  score in [0.8, 1.0] regardless of performance, and non-marrs miners can have scores multiplied by a
  tiny coefficient (default 1e-9) — i.e. live scoring can be overridden by off-chain config.'
subnet:
  name: MiaoAI
  owner_repo: MIAOAI-Subnet__MIAOAI_SUBNET
  netuid: 86
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Validators dispatch AI inference tasks (text classification, sentiment analysis, scene understanding,
    object detection, emotion analysis) to miners. Miners run AI models and return a structured JSON response
    (e.g. class_id+confidence, sentiment_score+confidence, objects list, description+confidence). The
    validator validates the schema of the response and evaluates its correctness against task ground truth,
    producing a per-task quality score in [0,1].
  submission_format:
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: task_quality_score
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.7
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Per-task correctness from _evaluate_response: F1 of detected vs ground-truth objects for object_detection;
    confidence-weighted correctness (0.7 + confidence*0.3 on match) for sentiment/emotion/scene/text-classification;
    0.5 fallback. Averaged over the cycle into the current_cycle quality score, which contributes 0.7
    to the final per-miner weight.'
- name: stake_weight
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: stake_share
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.2
  normalization: none
  notes: stake_weight = (miner_stake / total_non_validator_stake) * 0.2; contributes 0.2 to final score.
- name: historical_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: ema
  notes: Exponentially-weighted average of past quality_score*success_rate*time_factor over the miner's
    performance history; contributes 0.1 to final score. Defaults to alpha=0.95 if history is empty.
- name: response_time_factor
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: time_factor = 1/(1+response_time). Used multiplicatively inside historical score and inside calculate_score,
    though the on-chain set_weights path sums the three weighted terms without an explicit response factor.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Tasks carry a ground_truth field (object list, sentiment label, description string, or
    class id) loaded from the validator's task pool / task data path; evaluation compares miner response
    to it.
  source_identifier: task.ground_truth (task_manager task pool)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: README states validators are provided an Alibaba e-commerce customer-service dialogue dataset
    as a foundational training/eval set. Per-task ground truth supplied by the task definitions.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: 0.001
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'No emission burn. Owner-fallback instead: if no miner has current_quality_score>0, weights
      become all-zero except weights[owner_uid]=owner_default_score (0.2), then set on-chain.'
  notes: final_score = stake_weight + current_quality_score*0.7 + historical_score*0.1 (with historical_score
    defaulted to alpha=0.95 when zero). Only miners with current_quality_score>0 are included. Weights
    below MIN_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD=0.001 are zeroed, then the vector is normalized to sum to one. If everything
    zeros out, all weight goes to the subnet owner uid.
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: set_weights called once blocks_since_last_update and blocks_since_last_weights both exceed weights_interval,
    which is tempo*(1/3). Eval loop advances in eval_interval=25 block steps.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: tempo * (1/3)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: self.alpha=0.95 exists but is used as a default historical_score floor, not as an EMA smoothing
      of the final weight. current_cycle_scores are cleared after a successful set_weights.
anti_gaming:
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Miners below miner_min_stake_dtao (default 100 dTAO) and nodes with axon ip 0.0.0.0 are
    excluded from task allocation and weight setting. Validators below MIN_VALIDATOR_STAKE_DTAO (1000
    dTAO) can be rejected when CHECK_VALIDATOR_STAKE is enabled.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: _validate_response enforces per-task-type response schema (required fields, numeric ranges
    0..1, allowed_classes membership, min_objects). Invalid responses score 0 and are not counted.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Server/database-driven config can blacklist validators (set_weights aborts if validator
    blacklisted) and apply a coefficient (default 1e-9) multiplier to non-`marrs` miners' cycle scores;
    BAD_COLDKEYS have their moving_avg_scores zeroed on restore. The `marrs` allowlist instead forces
    favored miners' low scores up to a random value in [0.8, 1.0], which is a favoritism/centralization
    vector rather than an anti-gaming control.
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages: []
  count: 5
  notes: Five task types (text_classification, sentiment_analysis, scene_understanding, object_detection,
    emotion_analysis) share one scoring pipeline; no separate emission pools per type.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - cumulative_score
  - reputation
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: performance_history capped at last 1000 entries per hotkey; current_cycle_scores cleared
    after a successful set_weights.
  notes: ScoringSystem keeps performance_history (ValidatorPerformance records) and current_cycle_scores
    per hotkey; historical_score is an exp-weighted average over the history.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/running_validator.md
  - docs/running_miner.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '3'
    quote: '# **MIAOAI** ![Subnet 86]'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: miaoai/core/scoring.py
    line_ref: 1-5
    quote: import numpy as np ... from miaoai.core.config import MiaoAIConfig
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: miaoai/core/task_type.py
    line_ref: 5-9
    quote: TEXT_CLASSIFICATION = "text_classification" ... EMOTION_ANALYSIS = "emotion_analysis"
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: miaoai/validator/miaoai_validator.py
    line_ref: 361-433
    quote: 'def _validate_response(self, task_id: str, response: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: miaoai/validator/miaoai_validator.py
    line_ref: 776-780
    quote: current_quality_score * 0.7 +
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: miaoai/validator/miaoai_validator.py
    line_ref: 457-463
    quote: "f1_score = 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)\n                return f1_score"
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/weight
    source_path: miaoai/validator/miaoai_validator.py
    line_ref: '774'
    quote: stake_weight = (stake / total_stake) * 0.2 if total_stake > 0 else 0
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/weight
    source_path: miaoai/validator/miaoai_validator.py
    line_ref: '779'
    quote: historical_score * 0.1
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: miaoai/core/scoring.py
    line_ref: 41-53
    quote: "weights = np.exp(np.linspace(-1, 0, len(history)))\n        weights /= weights.sum()"
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: miaoai/validator/miaoai_validator.py
    line_ref: 771-772
    quote: "if historical_score == 0.0:\n                    historical_score = self.alpha"
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/3/notes
    source_path: miaoai/core/scoring.py
    line_ref: '48'
    quote: time_factor = 1 / (1 + perf.response_time)
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: miaoai/validator/miaoai_validator.py
    line_ref: 776-784
    quote: "final_score = (\n                        stake_weight +\n                        current_quality_score\
      \ * 0.7 +\n                        historical_score * 0.1\n                )"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/min_weight_floor
    source_path: miaoai/validator/miaoai_validator.py
    line_ref: 805-807
    quote: "MIN_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD = 0.001\n\n                    weights = [w if w >= MIN_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD\
      \ else 0.0 for w in weights]"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization
    source_path: miaoai/validator/miaoai_validator.py
    line_ref: 810-811
    quote: "if total_weight > 0:\n                        weights = [w / total_weight for w in weights]"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: miaoai/validator/miaoai_validator.py
    line_ref: 793-801
    quote: weights[owner_uid] = self.config_manager.get_config().owner_default_score
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status_notes
    source_path: miaoai/core/constants.py
    line_ref: '31'
    quote: OWNER_DEFAULT_SCORE = 0.2
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status_notes
    source_path: miaoai/core/scoring.py
    line_ref: 71-75
    quote: "if hotkey not in config.marrs:\n                base_score *= config.coefficient\n       \
      \     else:\n                if base_score < 0.8:\n                    base_score = random.uniform(0.8,\
      \ 1.0)"
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call

      \          wallet=self.wallet,\n                uids=miner_indices,\n                weights=weights,\n\
      \                wait_for_inclusion=True,\n                version_key=VERSION_KEY"
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: miaoai/validator/miaoai_validator.py
    line_ref: '99'
    quote: self.weights_interval = self.tempo *  (1 / 3)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method

  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description

      MIN_MINER_STAKE_DTAO = float(os.getenv("MIN_MINER_STAKE_DTAO", "100.0"))'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description

      \ must be between 0 and 1\")\n                    return False"

    quote: DEFAULT_COEFFICIENT= 0.000000001

docs.title: NullUntilBoot
docs.text:
Subnet NeuralNexusII__NullUntilBoot netuid 31 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status dormant Task No task is defined for miners. The README instructs miners to "standby for new instructions" and to monitor Discord/Twitter for clues. The validator does not evaluate any miner output; it concentrates all weight on a single hardcoded uid (251). DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: nullUntilBoot

    netuid: 31
    lang: python
    status: dormant
    submission: other

    Pipeline {
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: other
            normalization: other
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "200 blocks (block_diff >= 200), polled every 10s"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: dormant
mechanism_status_notes: No reward/scoring function exists. validator.py unconditionally assigns weight
  1.0 to uid 251 and 0.0 to every other uid, then commits via set_weights. There is no metagraph read
  of miner performance, no challenge, and no ground truth. Miners are told to "standby for new instructions".
  This is a placeholder/boot-trap validator concentrating all emission on one uid until the real mechanism
  launches.
subnet:
  name: NullUntilBoot
  owner_repo: NeuralNexusII__NullUntilBoot
  netuid: 31
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: No task is defined for miners. The README instructs miners to "standby for new instructions"
    and to monitor Discord/Twitter for clues. The validator does not evaluate any miner output; it concentrates
    all weight on a single hardcoded uid (251).
  submission_format:
  - other
  submission_format_other: No miner submission is defined or evaluated; miners standby for future instructions.
scoring_signals: []
ground_truth_sources: []
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: other
  composition_other: No composition of signals. A boolean mask (raw_weight_uids == 251) sets weight 1.0
    for one uid and 0.0 for all others, before bittensor's process_weights_for_netuid normalization.
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: Weights passed through bt.utils.weight_utils.process_weights_for_netuid (subtensor
    normalization), then convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit. No subnet-specific normalization.
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'Not a burn/null-uid allocation: all emission is routed to a live uid (251), not a designated
      burn address. Effectively winner-take-all to a single uid rather than a burn.'
  notes: raw_weights[raw_weight_uids == 251] = 1.0; all other entries remain 0.0 (np.zeros).
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Infinite loop polling current block; when (current_block - last_successful_block) >= 200, build
    a weight vector with 1.0 on uid 251 and 0.0 elsewhere, process via process_weights_for_netuid, convert
    with convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit, and call set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 200 blocks (block_diff >= 200), polled every 10s
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null

docs.title: Nuance
docs.text:
Subnet NuanceNetwork__Nuance netuid 23 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners connect a verified X (Twitter) account to their hotkey and submit factual, nuanced posts (and interactions/replies) relevant to constitution-defined topics. Validators fetch the content, use an LLM to filter for quality and positive sentiment, and score posts from verified accounts by an enga DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: LlmJudgment, RealEngagement"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: RateLimit, DeterministicCheck, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: engagement"]
  M1["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  M2["Metric: human_judgment"]
  M3["Metric: engagement"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: nuance

    netuid: 23
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: content_link

    @burn {
        uid: "metagraph.owner_hotkey (owner_hotkey_index)"
        fraction: 0.7
    }
    @guards {
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: engagement
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: human_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: engagement
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: oracle
            },
            RealEngagement {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "EPOCH_LENGTH = 300*12 seconds (3600s)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: A fixed 70% of every epoch's weight (ALPHA_BURN_RATIO = 0.7) is always assigned
  to the subnet owner's hotkey (burn). Only the remaining 30% is distributed by the documented per-category
  score. Additionally, any topic category with no scored interactions assigns its full normalized weight
  to the owner hotkey (further burn). Interaction type weights (reply, quote) are currently both 0.0,
  so only posts from verified accounts contribute non-burn weight.
subnet:
  name: Nuance
  owner_repo: NuanceNetwork__Nuance
  netuid: 23
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners connect a verified X (Twitter) account to their hotkey and submit factual, nuanced posts
    (and interactions/replies) relevant to constitution-defined topics. Validators fetch the content,
    use an LLM to filter for quality and positive sentiment, and score posts from verified accounts by
    an engagement-count base score, a quality bonus, the posting user's influence weight, and the topic
    weight.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
scoring_signals:
- name: post_base_engagement_score
  metric_kind: engagement
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Per-account base score from engagement count: 1 engagement = 1.0, 2 = 1.7, 3+ = 2.0, then divided
    by the engagement count (anti-spam cap). Posts default base score multiplier is 1.0.'
- name: quality_post_bonus
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: LLM QualityAssessor returns 'approve'/not; an approved (quality) post multiplies its base score
    by 1.5 (50% bonus). Multiplicative, not additive.
- name: user_influence_weight
  metric_kind: human_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: rank_multiplier read from the constitution's verified_users list (user_data['weight']) for the
    post's category; final_score = topic_score * rank_multiplier. Multiplicative.
- name: interaction_type_weight
  metric_kind: engagement
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'INTERACTION_TYPE_WEIGHTS = {REPLY: 0.0, QUOTE: 0.0}; interactions currently contribute 0 score.
    Sentiment must be positive (not negative) for an interaction to be ACCEPTED at all.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: LLM (via Chutes) assesses post quality ('approve') and interaction sentiment ('positive'/'neutral'/'negative');
    negative-sentiment interactions are rejected.
  source_identifier: nuance/processing/quality_assessing.py, nuance/processing/sentiment.py
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Prompts and topic/user weights sourced from the external constitution repo.
- kind: real_engagement
  description: Real X (Twitter) posts/interactions fetched via the Datura social provider for verified
    miner accounts; engagement counts drive the base score.
  source_identifier: X (Twitter) via Datura API
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: ''
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: sum_to_one
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: metagraph.owner_hotkey (owner_hotkey_index)
    fraction: 0.7
    dynamic: false
    notes: ALPHA_BURN_RATIO = 0.7 of final weight goes to owner hotkey unconditionally; additionally any
      category with zero total score assigns its whole normalized weight to the owner hotkey.
  notes: 'Per category: scores placed in a per-hotkey vector, normalized to sum to one (divide by category
    sum); empty categories give all weight to owner. Categories combined as a weighted sum using constitution
    topic weights. Final on-chain weight = 0.7 * owner_burn_vector + 0.3 * score_vector.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: score_aggregating loop computes node_scores, normalizes per category, weighted-sums by topic
    weight, combines with 70% owner burn, then calls subtensor.set_weights over all uids. Loops every
    EPOCH_LENGTH seconds.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: EPOCH_LENGTH = 300*12 seconds (3600s)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA on the final reward in code; weights set directly each cycle. A 7-day (SCORING_WINDOW)
      rolling cutoff window bounds which posts/interactions are scored.
anti_gaming:
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Per-account base score capped by engagement count (1.0/1.7/2.0 then divided by count) to
    prevent gaming via spamming engagements.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Account verification: miner must prove X-account ownership via hotkey verification post
    or ''Nuance<username>'' hashtag/marker; only posts from verified accounts are scored.'
- kind: other
  kind_other: llm_quality_and_sentiment_gate
  enforcement: rejection
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: LLM quality assessment and sentiment analysis; non-quality posts get no bonus and negative-sentiment
    interactions are REJECTED (not scored).
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Scoring partitioned by constitution topic/category; each category normalized independently then
    combined by topic weight. Categories are dynamic (from external constitution repo).
per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: Posts/interactions carry a ProcessingStatus (new/accepted/rejected/error) but scoring recomputes
    from scratch each cycle over a 7-day window; no persistent per-miner score ledger drives weights.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/dev/overall_architecture.md
  - docs/validators.md
  - docs/miners.md

docs.title: ORO
docs.text:
Subnet ORO-AI__oro netuid 15 Archetype multiplicative Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners submit Python shopping agents (defining agent_main()) that are run by validators in isolated Docker sandboxes against the ShoppingBench problem suite (product / shop / voucher tasks over 2.5M real products). Each problem is scored for ground-truth accuracy, format compliance, and field matchi DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Honeypot, DeterministicCheck, ChallengePeriod"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: accuracy"]
  M2["Metric: accuracy"]
  M3["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: oro

    netuid: 15
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: source_code

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: 0.75
    }
    @guards {
        Honeypot {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        ChallengePeriod {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, status_enum, registration_age }

    Multiplicative {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: multiplicative
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: continuous
            tempo: "interval_seconds (config.weight_update_interval); WeightSetterThread default 300s"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: single
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: 'Emission is structurally split: the designated top miner receives exactly t_top
  (default 0.25) of normalised emission, while burn uid 0 plus the deregistration- protection "tail" (top-50%
  of last-race finishers) together receive t_burn (default 0.75). The on-chain weight vector therefore
  concentrates ~25% on a single top hotkey and pins a large burn slot (u16 weight at uid 0). Per-problem
  scoring (ProblemScorer + LLM reasoning judge -> blend_final_score) and the race that ranks finishers
  run off-chain in a Backend; validators only fetch the completed-race finishers and the designated top
  hotkey and emit a deterministic vector. When no completed race is available the tick is skipped (no
  weight set). So: active scoring combined with a fixed structural burn — hence "mixed".'
subnet:
  name: ORO
  owner_repo: ORO-AI__oro
  netuid: 15
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners submit Python shopping agents (defining agent_main()) that are run by validators in
    isolated Docker sandboxes against the ShoppingBench problem suite (product / shop / voucher tasks
    over 2.5M real products). Each problem is scored for ground-truth accuracy, format compliance, and
    field matching, then a final outcome score is multiplied by a reasoning-quality coefficient from an
    LLM judge. Agents compete in periodic "races"; the best agent earns the top emission slot.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: rule_score
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: rule_score_reward over the recommended product vs ground truth; the success gate uses rule >=
    1 (plus shop>=1 or budget>=1 for shop/voucher tasks). Feeds success_rate; aggregated as a rate over
    total_problems, not additively weighted.
- name: ground_truth_match
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: ground_truth_reward (gt). compute_aggregate counts gt>=1 into ground_truth_rate. A reported signal
    but not what drives the success gate.
- name: format_score
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Per-step format_reward (PRM) averaged over output steps, with a TIMESTAMP_MISSING_PENALTY=0.5
    multiplier on steps lacking a valid extra_info timestamp. Reported as format_score; not part of the
    success gate.
- name: reasoning_quality
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: LLM judge rates each trajectory on a strict 5-bin decision tree {0.1,0.3,0.5,0.7,0.9} detecting
    genuine reasoning vs regex/pattern-matching. Averaged, then mapped to a multiplicative coefficient
    in [COEFF_FLOOR=0.3, 1.0] (1.0 at quality>=0.80) and multiplied into success_rate to form the final
    score (blend_final_score).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: ShoppingBench problem suite (product/shop/voucher tasks) backed by a search server over
    ~2.5M real products; per-problem ground-truth rewards and voucher constraints are supplied to the
    ProblemScorer.
  source_identifier: ShoppingBench (arXiv:2508.04266); search-server /get_product_raw
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Product documents fetched via HTTP from SEARCH_SERVER_URL. Reward/voucher dicts keyed by query
    are passed into ProblemScorer at construction.
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: An LLM judge (GLM / Gemma class models via Chutes or OpenRouter proxy) scores reasoning-quality
    of each agent trajectory using verified proxy-call logs plus the trajectory text.
  source_identifier: JUDGE_MODELS_BY_PROVIDER (zai-org/GLM-5.1-TEE, google/gemma-4-31B-turbo-TEE, ...)
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Judge prompt explicitly treats the trajectory as untrusted (anti-prompt-injection) and grades
    on verified proxy inference-call counts/token thresholds, not narration.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 0.75
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'compute_pinned_weights: with t_top=0.25, t_burn=0.75 (WeightSetterThread defaults), the top
      miner gets exactly t_top of normalised emission and burn uid 0 + tail together get t_burn. Burn
      slot pinned at U16_MAX (65535); the protection tail''s u16 weight comes out of t_burn''s share,
      so the top miner stays at t_top regardless of N. fraction 0.75 is the t_burn share (burn + tail
      combined), not burn-uid-only. When the top hotkey or all finishers are missing, uid 0 is pinned
      and emission is effectively burned.'
  notes: Off-chain per-problem scoring feeds a race; finishers are ranked by race_score desc (tie-break
    agent_version_id asc). On-chain the designated top miner (GET /v1/public/top, else rank-1 finisher)
    gets the t_top slot; the top-50% of finishers receive a linear taper M, M-1, ..., 1 (deregistration
    protection) within the t_burn share. final per- agent score = success_rate * reasoning_coefficient
    (blend_final_score).
weight_setting:
  cadence: continuous
  method: Background WeightSetterThread loops every interval_seconds (config.weight_update_interval),
    syncs the metagraph, fetches the most recent RACE_COMPLETE race's finishers and the designated top
    hotkey from the Backend, builds a deterministic (uids, u16 weights) vector via build_metagraph_weight_vector,
    and calls subtensor.set_weights with wait_for_inclusion=True. Determinism across validators is load-bearing
    for Yuma consensus (kappa=0.5). No completed race -> tick skipped (no submission). No retries in-tick;
    the next tick retries.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: interval_seconds (config.weight_update_interval); WeightSetterThread default 300s
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA of the on-chain weight vector in source. Temporal stability comes from protecting
      last race's top-50% finishers between races and the decaying score-to-beat threshold for challengers
      (documented in README, threshold constant not in source).
anti_gaming:
- kind: honeypot
  enforcement: penalty
  description: LLM reasoning judge detects regex / pattern-matching agents. Gate 1 caps score at 0.1 if
    the verified proxy log shows 0 inference calls with completion_tokens>=30; the coefficient floor is
    COEFF_FLOOR=0.3 so a zero-reasoning agent earns at most success_rate*0.3. Judge ignores instructions
    embedded in the (untrusted) trajectory.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Per-step format penalty: step_format *= TIMESTAMP_MISSING_PENALTY (0.5) when a step lacks
    a valid positive extra_info.timestamp, discouraging fabricated/replayed steps.'
- kind: challenge_period
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'README: challengers must exceed a decaying score threshold to claim the top spot, "preventing
    trivial improvements from churning the leader." Threshold/decay handled off-chain by the Backend (score-to-beat);
    no constant in this repo.'
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Agents run in isolated Docker sandboxes; only finishers with a non-null race_score (ran
    the race to completion and were scored) are considered. DNF / eliminated / never-executed qualifiers
    (race_score=null) are dropped and get no weight.
sub_competitions:
  structure: single
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: 'A single recurring "race": agents qualify, run, and are ranked by race_score. The most recent
    RACE_COMPLETE race determines the protected top-50% and the top slot. No multi-mechanism / per-task-type
    weight split on chain.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - status_enum
  - registration_age
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: 'Per-race: only finishers (non-null race_score) from the most recent completed race are
    protected; the protected set is recomputed each race from fresh finishers.'
  notes: race_score per qualifier (off-chain). Top-50% finishers keep Emission[uid]>0 between races so
    they survive get_neuron_to_prune (ranks by emission asc, reg_block asc, uid asc) when immunity_period
    expires — i.e. registration age / emission are the relevant per-miner state for deregistration protection.
    The bulk of this state lives in the Backend, not in this repo.

docs.title: Oceans Subnet 66
docs.text:
Subnet Oceans-Subnet__oceans_subnet netuid 66 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners provide liquidity to Bittensor subnet pools. Each epoch a miner is rewarded in proportion to its share of the total liquidity (measured in TAO) it supplies to each target subnet, weighted by a "master weight" vector over subnets derived from sn66 alpha-stake holder votes. The reward for a min DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData, Other"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: StakeWeighting"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: volume"]
  M1["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: oceansSubnet66

    netuid: 66
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: signals

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @guards {
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<multi_asset> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: volume
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            MarketData {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            Other {
                trust_model: crowd
            }
        ]
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: multiplicative
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: continuous
            tempo: "random 10-20 minutes per iteration (neuron_validator.py)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_asset
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: The documented and recommended validator (neurons/neuron_validator.py, the one
  in docs/validator.md) runs the real liquidity-weighted scoring via validator/forward.py and RewardCalculator.
  However the repo also ships neurons/burn_validator.py whose forward() assigns weight 1.0 to UID 0 and
  0.0 to every other UID — a full burn to UID 0. Which one is actually run on-chain cannot be determined
  from the source alone, so the operative reality is mixed.
subnet:
  name: Oceans Subnet 66
  owner_repo: Oceans-Subnet__oceans_subnet
  netuid: 66
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners provide liquidity to Bittensor subnet pools. Each epoch a miner is rewarded in proportion
    to its share of the total liquidity (measured in TAO) it supplies to each target subnet, weighted
    by a "master weight" vector over subnets derived from sn66 alpha-stake holder votes. The reward for
    a miner is the sum over subnets of (miner_LP_in_subnet / total_LP_in_subnet) * subnet_master_weight.
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: liquidity_provided
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Per-subnet liquidity a miner supplies, measured as total TAO value of its positions (lp_amt).
    Within each subnet, a miner''s contribution is lp_amt * (1/total_LP) * w_sub, i.e. its share of total
    subnet liquidity scaled by the subnet''s master weight. Not an additive standalone weight, hence weight:
    null.'
- name: subnet_master_weight
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Per-subnet weight w_sub derived from holder votes (stake-weighted, normalised to sum to 1.0)
    in RewardCalculator._build_master_vector. Acts as a multiplier on each subnet's liquidity share; not
    an additive per-miner signal.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: On-chain liquidity positions of each registered miner across Bittensor subnet pools, converted
    to TAO value, fetched by the LiquidityFetcher.
  source_identifier: validator/forward.py liq_fetcher.fetch_and_store()
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: 'Returns {subnet: {uid: tao}} liquidity map consumed by RewardCalculator.compute.'
- kind: other
  kind_other: stake_weighted_offchain_vote
  description: sn66 alpha-stake holder votes fetched off-chain via the VoteFetcher (vote API), each carrying
    a voter_stake; combined into a stake-weighted normalised per-subnet weight vector.
  source_identifier: validator/vote_fetcher.py VoteFetcher.fetch_and_store()
  trust_model: crowd
  notes: V1/V2 votes are off-chain web votes; roadmap (V3-V5) plans migration to on-chain smart contracts.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'Only in neurons/burn_validator.py: weights = [1.0 if uid == 0 else 0.0 for uid in range(0,256)],
      a full burn of all emission to UID 0. The documented neuron_validator.py path performs no burn —
      it distributes proportionally by liquidity.'
  notes: 'Documented path: reward[uid] += lp_amt * (1/total_lp) * w_sub summed over subnets, then normalised
    so the reward vector sums to 1.0 (uniform fallback if the vector is all zero).'
weight_setting:
  cadence: continuous
  method: BaseValidatorNeuron.set_weights L1-normalises self.scores and emits via subtensor.set_weights.
    neuron_validator.py runs forward() then sleeps a random 10-20 minutes per iteration (no explicit epoch
    detection); burn_validator.py sleeps 300s then sets weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: random 10-20 minutes per iteration (neuron_validator.py)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: 'update_scores applies EMA: self.scores = alpha * scattered + (1 - alpha) * self.scores with
      alpha = 0.1 (base/validator.py).'
anti_gaming:
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Voter influence on the master subnet vector is proportional to voter_stake (stake * weight/sum),
    so vote weight is gated by alpha-stake holdings rather than vote count.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_asset
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Rewards are computed independently per target Bittensor subnet pool (multiple liquidity pools),
    then summed per miner. No staged/tournament structure.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: self.scores persisted via save_state/load_state (state.npz); EMA-decayed each update and
    zeroed for replaced hotkeys on metagraph resync.
  notes: Per-UID EMA score vector maintained in base/validator.py; no debt ledger or status enum.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: v0 / V1 (July 2025)
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/validator.md
  - docs/miner.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: config.py
    line_ref: '25'
    quote: DEFAULT_NETUID = 66
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: validator/rewards.py
    line_ref: '14'

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary

    line_ref: '5'
    quote: Reward(N) = Σ_subnets  ( LP_N,sub / Σ LP_all,sub ) × MasterWeight_sub
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/name
    source_path: validator/rewards.py
    line_ref: 102-103
    quote: "contrib = lp_amt * inv_total_lp * w_sub\n                rewards[uid_int] += contrib"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/name
    source_path: validator/rewards.py
    line_ref: '163'
    quote: 'master_w = {sid: w / total_stake for sid, w in raw.items()}'

  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier

      {uid: tao}}'

  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction

docs.title: BitQuant Subnet
docs.text:
Subnet OpenGradient__BitQuant-Subnet netuid 15 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status active Task A miner runs the BitQuant agent for AI-powered quantitative DeFi analysis. The validator sends a natural-language QuantQuery; the miner forwards it to BitQuant and returns a QuantResponse (response text, signature, proofs, metadata). The response is rewarded by a quality score in [0.0, 1.0] obtained DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: HardwareValidation, StakeWeighting"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: bitQuantSubnet

    netuid: 15
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: api_response

    @guards {
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Pipeline {
        score: Metric {
            kind: llm_judgment
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: LlmJudgment {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length, 12s blocks)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Validators score miner responses via an external BitQuant Agent API and set weights
  from L1-normalized EMA scores. No burn allocation is present in the code. The reward is fully delegated
  to a remote evaluation endpoint; the actual scoring logic lives off-repo behind the API.
subnet:
  name: BitQuant Subnet
  owner_repo: OpenGradient__BitQuant-Subnet
  netuid: 15
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: A miner runs the BitQuant agent for AI-powered quantitative DeFi analysis. The validator sends
    a natural-language QuantQuery; the miner forwards it to BitQuant and returns a QuantResponse (response
    text, signature, proofs, metadata). The response is rewarded by a quality score in [0.0, 1.0] obtained
    from the BitQuant Agent evaluation API.
  submission_format:
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: bitquant_agent_evaluation_score
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Single score in [0.0, 1.0] returned by the BitQuant Agent API endpoint (quant-api.opengradient.ai/api/subnet/evaluate).
    Code clamps the value to max(0.0, min(1.0, score)) and defaults to 0.0 on any failure, None response,
    or empty response content. The exact evaluation logic runs off-repo behind the API.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: The production BitQuant Agent deployment evaluates the miner's response via an HTTP API.
    Validators are explicitly invited to design their own evaluation mechanism.
  source_identifier: https://quant-api.opengradient.ai/api/subnet/evaluate
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Off-chain remote API; the actual scoring model is not in this repository (see https://github.com/OpenGradient/BitQuant).
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn allocation present in the weight-setting code.
  notes: There is a single scoring signal. EMA-smoothed scores are L1-normalized (raw_weights = self.scores
    / norm, norm = L1 norm) before being processed for on-chain emission.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Per-step EMA of API reward scores; on sync, scores are L1-normalized, passed through process_weights_for_netuid
    and convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit, then committed via subtensor.set_weights with version_key=spec_version.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length, 12s blocks)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: update_scores applies scores = alpha * scattered_rewards + (1 - alpha) * scores, with neuron.moving_average_alpha
      default 0.1.
anti_gaming:
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'A TEE GPU remote-attestation framework exists (retrieve_remote_attestation / validate_attestation
    against golden_measurements.json, plus a periodic check), but the hard attestation gate in reward()
    is commented out: "no hard TEE attestation check requirement for now". So it is implemented but not
    currently enforced in scoring.'
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Validator queries require a validator permit / vpermit; neuron.vpermit_tao_limit default
    4096 caps TAO allowed to query a validator with a vpermit (see add_validator_args).
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: On metagraph resync, scores for replaced hotkeys are zeroed; state (step, scores, hotkeys)
    is persisted to state.npz.
  notes: self.scores is a per-UID EMA moving average over float32, updated each step via update_scores.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: quant __version__ 0.1.0; __spec_version__ 100000
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - quant/validator/reward.py
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '5'

    confidence: high

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang

    confidence: high

    quote: Evaluate a miner's response using the BitQuant Agent API and return a reward score.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format

    line_ref: 60-65
    quote: '"quant_response": { "response": response.response, "signature": ..., "proofs": [...], "metadata":

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier

  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization

    line_ref: 234-241
    quote: norm = np.linalg.norm(self.scores, ord=1, axis=0, keepdims=True) ... raw_weights = self.scores

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/enabled

    quote: result, msg = self.subtensor.set_weights(wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.config.netuid, uids=uint_uids_converted,

  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call

  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/kind

    quote: "alpha: float = self.config.neuron.moving_average_alpha\n self.scores = (alpha * scattered_rewards\
      \ + (1 - alpha) * self.scores)"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description

    line_ref: 172-182

docs.title: OpenKaito (Decentralized Embedding, Bittensor SN5)
docs.text:
Subnet OpenKaito__openkaito netuid 5 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners serve a text-embedding model. Each step the validator generates a batch of texts composed of relevant (positive) pairs drawn from a corpus and asks miners to embed all texts into vectors of a requested dimension (1024). Validators evaluate the returned embeddings with the InfoNCE contrastive DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: loss"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: openKaitoDecentralizedEmbeddingBittensorSn5

    netuid: 5
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: signals

    @burn {
        uid: "5FHsM8ywifUgtSXbDQaSD9zzDYq76RqAvf9PnaSVg5q3Lc8G"
        fraction: 0.95
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Pipeline {
        score: Metric {
            kind: loss
            direction: lower_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.2
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default; 12s blocks)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: The validator forward() loads a `burner_config` from GitHub and reallocates `burner_reward_percentage`
  (default 0.95, fallback 0.90) of the total batch reward to a designated burner miner UID, leaving the
  documented InfoNCE text-embedding scoring to distribute only the remaining fraction (~5%) among real
  miners. The documented per-miner scoring still runs, so this is a partial burn rather than a full burn
  or fully dormant mechanism.
subnet:
  name: OpenKaito (Decentralized Embedding, Bittensor SN5)
  owner_repo: OpenKaito__openkaito
  netuid: 5
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners serve a text-embedding model. Each step the validator generates a batch of texts composed
    of relevant (positive) pairs drawn from a corpus and asks miners to embed all texts into vectors of
    a requested dimension (1024). Validators evaluate the returned embeddings with the InfoNCE contrastive
    loss over the known positive pairs; lower loss (better separation of positive vs negative pairs) yields
    a higher score. Legacy/inactive paths also support semantic search and Discord search tasks, but the
    live forward sends the text-embedding task with ~100% probability.
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: infonce_embedding_loss
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: In evaluate_text_embedding, the validator computes loss = info_nce_loss(q_embeddings, a_embeddings)
    and converts it to a score as scores[i] = min(100.0, 1 / loss.item()). Lower InfoNCE loss => higher
    score. top1/top3 recall are also computed but only logged to wandb, not used for the on-chain reward.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Relevant (positive) text pairs are generated from a configured corpus via generate_relevant_pairs
    / generate_text_embedding_synapse; the validator holds the pairwise relevance (q_indices, a_indices)
    used as ground truth for the InfoNCE loss.
  source_identifier: text_embedding_datasets (cached_datasets_from_config(branch='main'))
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: The validator knows which texts are positive pairs because it constructs the batch, so the relevance
    labels are trusted ground truth held by the validator.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: 5FHsM8ywifUgtSXbDQaSD9zzDYq76RqAvf9PnaSVg5q3Lc8G
    fraction: 0.95
    dynamic: false
    notes: burner_reward_percentage of the total batch reward is assigned to the burner miner UID (rewards[burner_idx]
      = total_reward * burner_reward_percentage). Default fallback value in burner_config.fetch_config
      is 0.95; if the GitHub config fetch fails entirely the forward() except-branch defaults to 0.90.
      Remaining (1 - burner_reward_percentage) is split among real miners proportionally to their normalized
      rewards. The fraction and address are fetched from an external GitHub burner_config.json at runtime
      and may differ from these source defaults.
  notes: Per step, raw scores are normalized by rewards = rewards / (rewards.max() + 1e-5), then the burner
    split is applied, then update_scores applies an EMA. set_weights L1-normalizes the EMA scores (torch.nn.functional.normalize(self.scores,
    p=1, dim=0)) before on-chain emit.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: EMA scores are L1-normalized into raw_weights, passed through bt.utils.weight_utils.process_weights_for_netuid
    and convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit, then emitted via subtensor.set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default; 12s blocks)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.2
    window: null
    notes: update_scores does self.scores = alpha * scattered_rewards + (1 - alpha) * self.scores, with
      alpha = config.neuron.moving_average_alpha (default 0.2).
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: In evaluate_text_embedding, responses whose embedding tensor cannot be parsed, whose row
    count != number of query texts, or whose dimension != query.dimensions are given NaN loss / skipped
    (effectively zero score). Other task evaluators (evaluate, evaluate_discord_query_search) apply integrity/uniqueness
    checks (zero_score_mask) but are not on the live path.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: The codebase contains multiple task evaluators (text embedding, keyword/structured search, semantic
    search, Discord search) but the live validator forward sends the text-embedding task with ~100% probability
    (random_number < 1 + 1e-5), so only one mechanism is active.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Scores are persisted to state.pt and reset to 0 for a UID when its hotkey is replaced
    in resync_metagraph.
  notes: self.scores is a per-UID EMA moving average maintained across steps (the only persistent per-miner
    state).
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - quickstart.md
  - docs/query_and_response_api.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '3'
    quote: '# **Decentralized Embedding: Bittensor SN5**'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: quickstart.md
    line_ref: '39'
    quote: python neurons/miner.py --netuid 5 --subtensor.network finney
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 29-31
    quote: 'import bittensor as bt

      import openai

      import torch'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary

    line_ref: 302-328
    quote: "# 100% chance to send text-embedding task\n            if random_number < 1 + 1e-5:\n    \
      \            ...\n                query, q_indices, a_indices = generate_text_embedding_synapse(\n\
      \                    pairs, dimensions=1024\n                )"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 44-46
    quote: 'Miners will receive a batch of texts and embed them.


      For the text embeddings, validators have the pairwise relevance information to evaluate them via

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind

    line_ref: 327-350
    quote: "loss = info_nce_loss(q_embeddings, a_embeddings)\n                ...\n                losses[i]\

    confidence: high

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/kind

    line_ref: 306-326
    quote: "text_embedding_datasets = cached_datasets_from_config(branch=\"main\")[\n                \
      \    \"text_embedding_datasets\"\n                ]\n                ...\n                pairs\
      \ = generate_relevant_pairs(...)\n                query, q_indices, a_indices = generate_text_embedding_synapse(\n\
      \                    pairs, dimensions=1024\n                )"
    confidence: high

    quote: "burner_config = fetch_config(branch=\"main\")\n                burner_miner_address = burner_config[\"\
      burner_miner_address\"]\n                burner_reward_percentage = burner_config[\"burner_reward_percentage\"\
      ]"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction
    source_path: openkaito/utils/burner_config.py
    line_ref: 16-18
    quote: "\"burner_miner_address\": \"5FHsM8ywifUgtSXbDQaSD9zzDYq76RqAvf9PnaSVg5q3Lc8G\",\n        \
      \    \"burner_reward_percentage\": 0.95"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: openkaito/utils/burner_config.py
    line_ref: '17'
    quote: '"burner_miner_address": "5FHsM8ywifUgtSXbDQaSD9zzDYq76RqAvf9PnaSVg5q3Lc8G",'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 449-460
    quote: "rewards[i] = max(\n                                    1e-12, \n                         \
      \           original_rewards[i] / non_burner_total * (total_reward * (1 - burner_reward_percentage))\n\
      \                                )\n                    ...\n                    rewards[burner_idx]\
      \ = total_reward * burner_reward_percentage"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization
    source_path: openkaito/base/validator.py
    line_ref: '225'
    quote: raw_weights = torch.nn.functional.normalize(self.scores, p=1, dim=0).numpy()
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 430-431
    quote: "raw_scores = rewards.clone().detach()\n            rewards = rewards / (rewards.max() + 1e-5)"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/alpha
    source_path: openkaito/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 145-150
    quote: "\"--neuron.moving_average_alpha\",\n            type=float,\n            ...\n           \
      \ default=0.2,"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes
    source_path: openkaito/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 320-323
    quote: "alpha: float = self.config.neuron.moving_average_alpha\n        self.scores: torch.FloatTensor\
      \ = alpha * scattered_rewards + (\n            1 - alpha\n        ) * self.scores.to(self.device)"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call

    quote: "if embeddings.shape[0] != num_of_text:\n                    ...\n                    losses[i]\
      \ = torch.nan\n                    continue\n\n                if embeddings.shape[1] != query.dimensions:\n\
      \                    ...\n                    continue"
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/state_kinds

    quote: "def update_scores(self, rewards: torch.FloatTensor, uids: List[int]):\n        \"\"\"Performs\
