docs.title: Zeus — Environmental Forecasting Subnet
docs.text:
Subnet Orpheus-AI__Zeus netuid 18 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners produce forecasts of future ERA5 environmental variables (2m temperature, 100m u/v wind components, surface solar radiation downwards) over sampled geographic grids at short (through +48h) and long (through +360h / 15 days) horizons. Validators score predictions against held-out ERA5 reanalys DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, DeterministicCheck, CommitReveal"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: regression_error"]
  M1["Metric: regression_error"]
  M2["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: RankBased"]
  SM["smooth: Sma"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: zeusEnvironmentalForecastingSubnet

    netuid: 18
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: signals

    @burn {
        uid: "56"
        fraction: 0.5
    }
    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, registration_age }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: regression_error
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: regression_error
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: rank
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: RankBased {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Sma {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
            count: 8
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: 'Validators score miners on ERA5 forecast accuracy, but set_weights applies a
  configurable burn: burn_percent defaults to 0.5 (50%) and is assigned to BURN_UID 56 on mainnet, with
  the remainder distributed proportionally among miners. On testnet BURN_UID is None so burning is skipped.'
subnet:
  name: Zeus — Environmental Forecasting Subnet
  owner_repo: Orpheus-AI__Zeus
  netuid: 18
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners produce forecasts of future ERA5 environmental variables (2m temperature, 100m u/v wind
    components, surface solar radiation downwards) over sampled geographic grids at short (through +48h)
    and long (through +360h / 15 days) horizons. Validators score predictions against held-out ERA5 reanalysis
    ground truth using latitude- and Europe-weighted RMSE and MAE; the average of RMSE and MAE is the
    per-challenge score, converted into competition ranks.
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: europe_weighted_rmse
  metric_kind: regression_error
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Latitude- and Europe/Germany-region-weighted RMSE between miner prediction and ERA5 output_data
    (zeus/validator/metrics.py custom_rmse). NaN or shape-mismatched predictions are set to inf. Used
    both in the average score and as the rank tie-breaker.
- name: europe_weighted_mae
  metric_kind: regression_error
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Latitude- and region-weighted MAE (custom_mae). NaN set to inf.
- name: average_error_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: rank
  notes: score = (rmse + mae)/2 in calculate_scores; miners are sorted by this and converted to competition
    ranks (miner.score = float(rank)). The rank, not the raw error, drives weights.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: ERA5 reanalysis data from the Climate Data Store (CDS) of Copernicus, streamed in real-time;
    validators evaluate miners on held-out future environmental data.
  source_identifier: https://cds.climate.copernicus.eu/api
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: ERA5 variables and per-variable scoring weights defined in ERA5_DATA_VARS.
aggregation:
  method: rank_based
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '56'
    fraction: 0.5
    dynamic: false
    notes: burn_percent default 0.5 assigned to BURN_UID 56 on mainnet; remaining (1 - burn_percent) scaled
      proportionally across other miners. BURN_UID is None on testnet (burning skipped).
  notes: Per challenge (variable × time-window), compute_min_rank_weights averages each hotkey's last
    window_size ranks; the best miner gets PERCENTAGE_GOING_TO_WINNER (0.95) and the rest receive logarithmically
    decreasing weights summing to 0.05. Per-challenge weight vectors are combined via np.average weighted
    by effective per-challenge weights (TIME_WINDOW_WEIGHTS × ERA5_DATA_VARS), then normalized to sum
    to one before burn is applied.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: For each available challenge (variable × time-window), average each miner's last window_size
    ranks (window_size = min(score_time_window_short=4 or _long=8, max_len)); winner gets 0.95, others
    logarithmically. Combine challenges via weighted average, normalize to sum-to-one, apply burn, then
    process_weights_for_netuid and set_weights on chain.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: null
  smoothing:
    kind: sma
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: Weights are computed from a rolling window of the last window_size ranks per hotkey (score_time_window_short
      default 4, score_time_window_long default 8); miners with fewer than window_size samples are treated
      as rank infinity (weight 0).
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Collusion detection (apply_collusion_penalty): if two miners'' RMSEs are within a threshold
    (metric_similarity < COLLUSION_PENALTY_THRESHOLD: 0.0004 short, 0.00002 long), the newer registration
    (higher block) is penalized (rmse/mae/score set None, shape_penalty True), keeping the oldest. Not
    applied to surface_solar_radiation_downwards.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'should_apply_shape_penalty: predictions that are None, have wrong element count, or are
    non-finite get inf error (sorted last / excluded from scoring).'
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Validation uses a commit–reveal flow (hash phase then prediction reveal phase) so miners
    commit hashed predictions before revealing.
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages: []
  count: 8
  notes: 8 challenges = 4 ERA5 variables × 2 time windows (SHORT_CHALLENGE (0,48), LONG_CHALLENGE (0,360)).
    Each has its own state_key and rank_history; combined via per-variable weights (2m_temperature 0.2,
    100m_u 0.3, 100m_v 0.3, solar 0.2) and time-window weights (short 0.2, long 0.8).
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - registration_age
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: rank_history per hotkey per challenge is pruned to RANK_HISTORY_PRUNE_LEN (1000) most
    recent ranks; weights use only the last window_size ranks.
  notes: Per-challenge rank_history of recent ranks per hotkey drives weighting; registration block (block_at_registration)
    is used to decide which colluding miner to penalize.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/Validating.md
  - docs/Mining.md
  - docs/ScoringChallengesCalculatingWeights.ipynb
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: zeus/validator/constants.py
    line_ref: '10'
    quote: MAINNET_UID = 18
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: zeus/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 19-24
    quote: import math ... import bittensor as bt ... import numpy as np ... import torch
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '30'
    quote: Validators issue both short-range challenges (hourly steps from the current hour through +48
      hours, 49 timesteps) and long-range challenges (the same grid through +360 hours, i.e. 15 days,
      361 timesteps).
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: zeus/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 126-128
    quote: cosine_rmse, europe_weighted_rmse = custom_rmse(output_data, temp_tensor, latitude_weights,
      europe_weight)
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: zeus/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: '148'
    quote: score = (miner.rmse + miner.mae)/2
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: zeus/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 192-195
    quote: ranks = calculate_competition_ranks(rmses) ... miner.score = float(rank)
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: zeus/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 263-266
    quote: weights[best_uid] = PERCENTAGE_GOING_TO_WINNER ... remaining_weight = 1.0 - PERCENTAGE_GOING_TO_WINNER
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction
    source_path: zeus/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 206-210
    quote: '"--neuron.burn_percent", type=float, help="The percentage of the reward to burn.", default=0.5,'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: zeus/base/validator.py
    line_ref: '91'
    quote: self.BURN_UID = 56
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: zeus/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 427-429
    quote: 'if 0 < burn_percent < 1.0 and self.BURN_UID and self.BURN_UID < len(raw_weights): ... raw_weights[self.BURN_UID]
      = burn_percent'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: zeus/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 469-477
    quote: result, msg = self.subtensor.set_weights(wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.config.netuid, uids=uint_uids,
      weights=uint_weights, ...)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/window
    source_path: zeus/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 182-194
    quote: '"--neuron.score_time_window_short" ... default=4, ... "--neuron.score_time_window_long" ...
      default=8,'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: zeus/validator/collusion.py
    line_ref: 28-31
    quote: Identifies collusion. If two miners are too similar, the NEWER registration (higher UID) is
      penalized, keeping the OLDEST (lower UID).
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: zeus/validator/constants.py
    line_ref: '62'
    quote: 'COLLUSION_PENALTY_THRESHOLD = {SHORT_CHALLENGE: 0.0004, LONG_CHALLENGE: 0.00002}'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: zeus/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 46-51
    quote: 'if prediction.numel() != np.prod(correct_shape): ... shape_penalty = True elif not torch.isfinite(prediction).all():
      ... shape_penalty = True'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '37'
    quote: Validation uses a commit–reveal flow; see the Validator Guide for phases and timing.
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/notes
    source_path: zeus/validator/constants.py
    line_ref: 64-69
    quote: 'ERA5_DATA_VARS: Dict[str, float] = {"2m_temperature": 0.2, "100m_u_component_of_wind": 0.3,
      "100m_v_component_of_wind": 0.3, "surface_solar_radiation_downwards": 0.2}'
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/notes
    source_path: zeus/validator/constants.py
    line_ref: 106-109
    quote: 'TIME_WINDOW_WEIGHTS: Dict[Tuple[int, int], float] = {SHORT_CHALLENGE: 0.2, LONG_CHALLENGE:
      0.8,}'
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: zeus/validator/constants.py
    line_ref: '61'
    quote: RANK_HISTORY_PRUNE_LEN = 1000
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: zeus/validator/constants.py
    line_ref: '111'
    quote: PERCENTAGE_GOING_TO_WINNER = 0.95
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '23'
    quote: The datasource for this subnet consists of ERA5 reanalysis data from the Climate Data Store
      (CDS) of the European Union's Earth observation programme (Copernicus).
  unresolved:
  - 'Weight-setting cadence/tempo: set_weights is gated by should_set_weights() and database scoring readiness
    in forward.py; no explicit block tempo constant found.'
  - Europe/Germany region weight magnitudes (EUROPE_WEIGHT=1.5, GERMANY_WEIGHT=2.5) feed custom RMSE/MAE
    but their exact effect on final scores is not separately modeled here.
composition:
  shape: multiplex
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Platform (PlatformNetwork)
docs.text:
Subnet PlatformNetwork__platform netuid None Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task The repo itself defines no single miner task. It is a multi-challenge Bittensor validator platform: a master validator orchestrates independent challenge containers, each of which owns its own logic, submissions, and scoring. Each challenge exposes a standard GET /internal/v1/get_weights returning a DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: Other"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, Other, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: other"]
  M1["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: platformPlatformNetwork

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [api_response, other]

    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: other
                raw: "Opaque per-hotkey weight returned by each challenge's get_weights endpoint; the master does not know how it was computed (challenge-internal scoring lives out of tree)."
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                raw: "Fixed per-challenge emission share (Decimal, >= 0) configured at challenge registration; determines how much of total emission each challenge's normalized weights contribute."
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: Other {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: 360
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: The master orchestrator computes and submits weights on-chain every epoch (run_epoch
  -> aggregate_challenge_weights -> weight_setter.set_weights). The miner-facing scoring that produces
  each challenge's hotkey weights is delegated to per-challenge containers in separate repositories and
  is therefore out of tree.
subnet:
  name: Platform (PlatformNetwork)
  owner_repo: PlatformNetwork__platform
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'The repo itself defines no single miner task. It is a multi-challenge Bittensor validator
    platform: a master validator orchestrates independent challenge containers, each of which owns its
    own logic, submissions, and scoring. Each challenge exposes a standard GET /internal/v1/get_weights
    returning a hotkey -> weight map. The master normalizes each challenge''s weights, applies fixed per-challenge
    emission percentages, maps hotkeys to UIDs, and submits final aggregated weights to Bittensor. What
    a miner produces and what is rewarded depends on the specific challenge.'
  submission_format:
  - api_response
  - other
  submission_format_other: Per-challenge; the master only consumes a hotkey->weight map returned by each
    challenge's internal get_weights API. The underlying miner submission type is defined by each challenge
    repo (out of tree).
scoring_signals:
- name: challenge_hotkey_weight
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: Opaque per-hotkey weight returned by each challenge's get_weights endpoint; the master
    does not know how it was computed (challenge-internal scoring lives out of tree).
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  normalization_other: ''
  notes: 'Within a challenge the raw hotkey weights are sum-normalized to one (normalize_weights: value/total
    over finite, strictly-positive weights). Non-finite and non-positive weights are dropped.'
- name: challenge_emission_percent
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: Fixed per-challenge emission share (Decimal, >= 0) configured at challenge registration;
    determines how much of total emission each challenge's normalized weights contribute.
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  normalization_other: ''
  notes: 'normalize_emissions: emission_percent of each OK challenge is clamped to >=0 and divided by
    the sum across active challenges; a failed challenge contributes nothing.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: other
  kind_other: Per-challenge internal API (GET /internal/v1/get_weights) returning trusted hotkey weights;
    the ground truth that produced them is challenge-specific and out of this tree.
  description: The master treats each challenge's get_weights response as authoritative for that challenge.
    Responses are authenticated with a per-challenge bearer token; the master ignores unknown hotkeys
    and zeroes a challenge that fails.
  source_identifier: GET {internal_base_url}/internal/v1/get_weights
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: ChallengeClient retries (default 3) with backoff and a 10s timeout; on failure the challenge
    result is marked ok=False (and optionally served from a signed fallback store).
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn/UID-zero allocation found in the master aggregator. Emission for a failed challenge
      is simply excluded from the normalized emission split.
  notes: 'aggregate_challenge_weights: for each OK challenge, hotkey_scores[hotkey] += emission_share
    * normalized_challenge_weight. Hotkeys not present in the metagraph hotkey->uid map are dropped. uid_scores
    are then divided by their total so final weights sum to one. UIDs are emitted sorted ascending.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: MasterWeightService.run_epoch refreshes the hotkey->uid metagraph cache, collects per-challenge
    weights, aggregates them, and calls WeightSetter.set_weights with the final uid/weight lists.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 360
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of the final reward found in the master. epoch_interval_seconds default
      is 360 and metagraph_cache_ttl_seconds default is 300.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: _clean_weights drops any challenge-reported weight that is non-finite or <= 0 before normalization,
    so negative/NaN/Inf weights cannot influence the result.
- kind: other
  kind_other: capability_check_and_failure_isolation
  enforcement: rejection
  description: A challenge that fails its capability check or whose get_weights call errors (and has no
    valid signed fallback) is marked ok=False and contributes zero to both the emission split and the
    hotkey scores, isolating its failure.
- kind: other
  kind_other: unknown_hotkey_filtering
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Hotkeys returned by a challenge that are not present in the metagraph hotkey->uid map are
    ignored when accumulating uid scores.
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Fallback weights are served as an HMAC-SHA256-signed payload; FallbackWeightClient rejects
    payloads with an invalid signature or older than max_age_seconds (default 600).
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages: []

docs.title: Poker44 — Poker Bot Detection Subnet
docs.text:
Subnet Poker44__Poker44-subnet netuid 126 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Validators query miners with DetectionSynapse(chunks=...) — each chunk is a homogeneous list of poker hand payloads that is entirely human or entirely bot. Miners return one risk_score per chunk. Validators round predictions and score them against the known per-chunk human/bot labels using a reward DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: LivenessCheck, RiskLimit, Deduplication"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: accuracy"]
  M2["Metric: composite"]
  M3["Metric: throughput"]
  M4["Metric: latency"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: CommitReveal"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: poker44PokerBotDetectionSubnet

    netuid: 126
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: signals

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: 0.97
    }
    @guards {
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RiskLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: other
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: other
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, performance_ledger, reputation }

    Multiplex<tiered> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: gated
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.05
        }
        publish: CommitReveal {
            cadence: continuous
            tempo: "300s poll_interval between forward cycles"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: tiered
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Validator burns 97% of emissions to UID 0 (BURN_FRACTION = 0.97); only the remaining
  3% follows the scoring/winner allocation. In the live provider_runtime path, on-chain weights are read
  from the backend competition settlement vector, and before the first daily winner is settled the backend
  fallback typically returns UID 0 (100% burn). The local reward/scoring code is fully implemented and
  exercised but its output only governs the 3% keep slice (or is bypassed entirely when backend settlement
  weights are present).
subnet:
  name: Poker44 — Poker Bot Detection Subnet
  owner_repo: Poker44__Poker44-subnet
  netuid: 126
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Validators query miners with DetectionSynapse(chunks=...) — each chunk is a homogeneous list
    of poker hand payloads that is entirely human or entirely bot. Miners return one risk_score per chunk.
    Validators round predictions and score them against the known per-chunk human/bot labels using a reward
    that combines average precision and bot recall, gated by a hard human-safety penalty on false-positive
    rate. The goal is objective, reproducible detection of bots in online poker.
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: ap_score
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.65
  normalization: none
  notes: average_precision_score(y_true, y_pred) over the reward window; contributes 0.65 to base_score.
    Zero when no positive labels present.
- name: bot_recall
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.35
  normalization: none
  notes: tp / positive_count; contributes 0.35 to base_score (base_score = 0.65*ap_score + 0.35*bot_recall).
- name: human_safety_penalty
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Multiplicative gate: (1 - fpr)**2, forced to 0.0 when fpr >= 0.10. Final reward = base_score
    * human_safety_penalty. Not an additive signal.'
- name: coverage_rate
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: len(scores)/expected_chunk_count, tracked per cycle and reported in the competition score payload;
    does not directly enter the reward formula.
- name: latency_mean_seconds
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Mean dendrite process_time over the window; reported in competition score payload but does not
    enter the reward formula.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Per-chunk human/bot labels derived from Poker44 benchmark-table hands. batch.is_human determines
    label (0=human, 1=bot). Production batches are fetched from the central eval API via the provider_runtime
    dataset provider.
  source_identifier: ProviderRuntimeDatasetProvider / api.poker44.net /internal/eval/*
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Validators do not run their own tables; canonical batches come from the central platform backend.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: gated
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 0.97
    dynamic: false
    notes: BURN_FRACTION = 0.97 burned to UID_ZERO (0); KEEP_FRACTION = 1.0 - 0.97 = 0.03 distributed
      to scored miners. In WINNER_TAKE_ALL=False (default) podium mode the 0.03 keep is split across the
      top-3 positive-reward miners with fixed shares [0.5, 0.3, 0.2]. If no miner has positive reward,
      UID 0 gets 100%.
  notes: 'Local path: _select_weight_targets ranks reward_map and (WINNER_TAKE_ALL=False) forms a podium
    of up to 3 positive-reward miners with fixed splits [0.5,0.3,0.2] scaled by KEEP_FRACTION, plus BURN_FRACTION
    to UID 0. Final raw_weights are then L1-normalized in set_weights (raw_weights = scores / l1-norm).
    When the backend provides a competition settlement vector, set_weights uses that vector instead (with
    the same 97/3 burn/keep convention).'
weight_setting:
  cadence: continuous
  method: Each forward cycle computes windowed rewards, selects podium/burn targets, and EMA-updates self.scores.
    set_weights resolves either the backend competition settlement vector or the local L1-normalized scores,
    processes them via process_weights_for_netuid, and submits on chain. Cycles sleep poll_interval (default
    300s) between ticks.
  on_chain_call: commit_reveal
  tempo_or_interval: 300s poll_interval between forward cycles
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.05
    window: null
    notes: update_scores applies self.scores = alpha*scattered_rewards + (1-alpha)*self.scores with alpha
      = config.neuron.moving_average_alpha (default 0.05). Rewards themselves are computed over a rolling
      reward_window (default 40 samples) in _compute_windowed_rewards before EMA.
anti_gaming:
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: _compute_windowed_rewards assigns reward 0.0 (and metrics fpr=1.0) to any miner with fewer
    than `window` prediction/label samples, so insufficiently responsive miners earn nothing until they
    accumulate a full window.
- kind: risk_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Hard human-safety penalty in reward(): human_safety_penalty = max(0, 1-fpr)**2, and is
    forced to 0.0 when fpr >= 0.10, zeroing the reward of any miner that misclassifies humans as bots
    above the 10% false-positive threshold.'
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: other
  enforcement_other: logging/observability only
  description: _record_served_chunk_fingerprints tracks chunk fingerprints across cycles and warns when
    chunks are reused; does not directly alter scoring.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: other
  enforcement_other: observability metadata only
  description: Optional model_manifest attached to responses feeds suspicion/compliance registries (evaluate_manifest_suspicion
    / evaluate_manifest_compliance). README states this does not change validator scoring or on-chain
    set_weights.
sub_competitions:
  structure: tiered
  stages:
  - name: podium
    role: main
    advancement_rule: Top-3 miners by reward (sorted by -reward, then uid), restricted to those with strictly
      positive reward.
    multipliers:
      rank_1: 0.5
      rank_2: 0.3
      rank_3: 0.2
      scaled_by_keep_fraction: 0.03
  count: 3
  notes: Default mode is WINNER_TAKE_ALL=False, giving a 3-slot podium. A documented target settlement
    model is winner-take-all (per README and WINNER_TAKE_ALL flag), but that flag is False in the committed
    constants.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - performance_ledger
  - reputation
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: On metagraph resync, scores[uid] is zeroed when a hotkey is replaced. Rolling prediction/label/coverage/latency
    buffers accumulate per uid; rewards use the last `window` entries.
  notes: EMA self.scores persisted to state.npz; prediction_buffer/label_buffer/ coverage_buffer/latency_buffer
    hold rolling per-uid history; model_manifest, compliance, and suspicion registries persisted as JSON
    per uid.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/validator.md
  - docs/miner.md
  - docs/training-benchmark.md
  completeness: medium

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/stages/0/multipliers
    source_path: poker44/validator/constants.py
    line_ref: '10'
    quote: 'WINNER_TAKE_ALL = False            # Distribute emissions across a fixed podium'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: poker44/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 537-539
    quote: 'for uid, hotkey in enumerate(self.hotkeys): if hotkey != self.metagraph.hotkeys[uid]: self.scores[uid]
      = 0  # hotkey has been replaced'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 84-91
    quote: validators read the canonical competition weight vector from the backend. ... `97%` is burned
      to `uid 0`, and the remaining `3%` follows the backend-provided winner vector. Before the first
      settlement exists, the backend returns its explicit fallback vector (typically `uid 0`, which keeps
      the burn at `100%`).
    confidence: high
  unresolved:
  - 'reward_window default 40 vs 20: neurons/validator.py sets self.reward_window from POKER44_REWARD_WINDOW
    default 40, while _compute_windowed_rewards uses getattr default 20; effective live window depends
    on env.'
  - Exact backend competition settlement weight algorithm lives server-side (api.poker44.net) and is not
    in this repo.
  - Whether on-chain submission ends up as commit-reveal or classic set_weights depends on RPC availability
    at runtime (multiple fallbacks in set_weights).
composition:
  shape: multiplex
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Polaris Cloud Subnet
docs.text:
Subnet PolarisCloud-AI__polarisvalidator netuid 49 Archetype multiplicative Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners contribute GPU/CPU compute resources (containers / rented machines) to a decentralized compute network. Validators score each miner's resources by a proof-of-work compute score combined with uptime reliability and active container counts; only resources with PoW >= 0.03 participate at all. DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: HardwareProbe"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: ProofOfWork, StakeWeighting, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: hardware_spec"]
  M1["Metric: uptime"]
  M2["Metric: throughput"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: polarisCloudSubnet

    netuid: 49
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: compute_service

    @burn {
        uid: "44"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        ProofOfWork {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, cooldown, status_enum }

    Multiplicative {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: hardware_spec
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: HardwareProbe {
            trust_model: unknown
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: multiplicative
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "max(tempo, WeightsSetRateLimit)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: Compute/uptime scoring is implemented and gated at PoW >= 0.03, but neurons/utils/validator_utils.py
  routes a 90% bonus plus all CPU scores and penalty losses to a hardcoded UID '44' before weights are
  normalized and set, redirecting a large share of emissions to a single owner-controlled UID.
subnet:
  name: Polaris Cloud Subnet
  owner_repo: PolarisCloud-AI__polarisvalidator
  netuid: 49
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners contribute GPU/CPU compute resources (containers / rented machines) to a decentralized
    compute network. Validators score each miner's resources by a proof-of-work compute score combined
    with uptime reliability and active container counts; only resources with PoW >= 0.03 participate at
    all.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
  submission_format_other: ''
scoring_signals:
- name: compute_pow_score
  metric_kind: hardware_spec
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: 75th-percentile log scaling with soft cap (logarithmic normalization to MAX_SCORE)
  notes: 'Raw PoW (proof-of-work) compute score is used directly as a multiplier (compute_multiplier =
    scaled_compute_score). Gated: PoW < SCORE_THRESHOLD (0.03) excludes the resource entirely.'
- name: uptime_reliability
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: uptime_score = (uptime_percent / 100) * 10 contributes to the additive base score; also drives
    a tiered uptime multiplier (1.0 / 1.05 / 1.10 / 1.15).
- name: container_activity
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: container_score = min(effective_container_count, MAX_CONTAINERS) * 0.5; also drives a rented-machine
    bonus multiplier (base 1.08, +0.01/container, cap 1.20).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: Miner compute resources are discovered via Polaris API endpoints and scored by a proof-of-work
    compute score (CPU/GPU specs) plus measured uptime and active container counts.
  source_identifier: neurons/utils/api_utils.py (reward_mechanism, _get_cached_miners_data)
  trust_model: unknown
  notes: Scores derive from API-reported miner data and PoW verification; the validator fetches cached
    miner data and verifies hotkeys before scoring.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: sum_to_one
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '44'
    fraction: 0.9
    dynamic: true
    notes: 'validator_utils.process_miners adds to UID 44: a 90% bonus computed as total_rewarded_scores
      * 0.90, PLUS all CPU scores collected from miners, PLUS penalty losses. uid_44_total = penalty_collection
      + cpu_collection + 0.90*rewarded. The 0.90 fraction is fixed; the absolute amount is a computed
      residual of the rewarded pool, hence dynamic.'
  notes: Final per-resource score = base_score(uptime+container) * (tempo/3600)*10 * compute_multiplier(PoW)
    * uptime_multiplier * rented_machine_bonus, then 75th/80th percentile logarithmic normalization to
    MAX_SCORE=500. Per-resource scores are summed per miner_uid in aggregate_rewards, then the UID-44
    redirect logic is applied.
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Scores are decayed by score_decay (0.9), merged via max(old, new) per UID, filtered to non-zero,
    normalized to sum to 1.0, individual weights capped at 0.1 (10%) and renormalized, then set via subtensor.set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: max(tempo, WeightsSetRateLimit)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: self.scores *= self.score_decay (0.9) each weight update before merging new results via max();
      not a standard EMA alpha. After a successful set_weights, scores are reset to zero.
anti_gaming:
- kind: proof_of_work
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Resources with PoW (pog_score) < SCORE_THRESHOLD (0.03) are skipped entirely - no scoring,
    no bonuses. PoW >= 0.03 is the absolute participation requirement.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Alpha over-selling detector (AlphaOverSellingDetector) flags UIDs whose stake/emission
    ratio breaches thresholds and applies tiered score reductions (low 0.2 / moderate 0.4 / high 0.6 /
    extreme 0.8) for a duration; penalty losses are redirected to UID 44.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Weights are only set when blocks since last update >= max(tempo, WeightsSetRateLimit);
    per-UID weight capped at 0.1 then renormalized.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - cooldown
  - status_enum
  status_values:
  - active
  - inactive
  - verified
  - rejected
  reset_policy: self.scores reset to zeros after a successful set_weights; per-UID score zeroed on hotkey
    change during metagraph resync. Alpha penalties expire after a duration (base 12h moderate, scaled)
    tracked in active_penalties by end_block.
  notes: Validator maintains self.scores (np.float32 array), decayed by 0.9 each cycle and merged via
    max(). Alpha penalties carry per-UID end_block cooldowns.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: Documentation Version 2.1 (Last Updated 2025-08-12)
  primary_docs:
  - REWARD_MECHANISM_README.md
  - README.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '1'
    quote: '# 🚀 **Polaris Cloud Subnet (NETUID 49)**'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: neurons/utils/api_utils.py
    line_ref: 34-39
    quote: 'SCORE_THRESHOLD = 0.03 ... MAX_SCORE = 500.0  # Maximum normalized score'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 11-14
    quote: 'decentralized compute network where: - **Miners** contribute GPU/CPU resources and earn rewards
      based on performance ... **Quality Control** ensures only high-performance resources participate
      (PoW ≥ 0.03)'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: neurons/utils/api_utils.py
    line_ref: 190-195
    quote: 'compute_multiplier = scaled_compute_score  # Direct PoW score as multiplier ... final_score
      = tempo_scaled_score * compute_multiplier * uptime_multiplier * rented_machine_bonus'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: neurons/utils/api_utils.py
    line_ref: '179'
    quote: 'uptime_score = (uptime_percent / 100) * 10  # 0-10 scale'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: neurons/utils/api_utils.py
    line_ref: '182'
    quote: 'container_score = min(effective_container_count, MAX_CONTAINERS) * 0.5  # 0-10 scale (max
      20 containers)'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: REWARD_MECHANISM_README.md
    line_ref: 82-84
    quote: 'if pog_score < SCORE_THRESHOLD:  # 0.03 ... continue  # Skip this resource entirely - no processing,
      no bonuses'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: neurons/utils/alpha_penalty.py
    line_ref: 455-458
    quote: '''low'': 0.2,      # 20% reduction

      ''moderate'': 0.4,  # 40% reduction

      ''high'': 0.6,      # 60% reduction

      ''extreme'': 0.8    # 80% reduction'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 252-253
    quote: "if weights_array.sum() > 0:\n    weights_array = weights_array / weights_array.sum()"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: neurons/utils/api_utils.py
    line_ref: 188-195
    quote: tempo_scaled_score = base_score * (tempo / 3600) * 10 ... final_score = tempo_scaled_score
      * compute_multiplier * uptime_multiplier * rented_machine_bonus
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction
    source_path: neurons/utils/validator_utils.py
    line_ref: 234-236
    quote: 'total_rewarded_scores = sum(final_rewards.values())

      uid_44_90_percent_bonus = total_rewarded_scores * 0.90'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: neurons/utils/validator_utils.py
    line_ref: 242-256
    quote: uid_44_total = uid_44_penalty_collection + uid_44_cpu_collection + uid_44_90_percent_bonus
      ... final_rewards['44'] = uid_44_total
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: '49'
    quote: self.score_decay = 0.9
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 286-294
    quote: 'max_allowed_weight = 0.1  # 10% maximum per UID ... weights_list = [w / total_capped for w
      in capped_weights]'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 318-326
    quote: "result = self.subtensor.set_weights(\n    self.wallet,\n    netuid=self.config.netuid,\n \
      \   uids=uids_tensor,\n    weights=weights_tensor, ... version_key=9010000"
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: '171'
    quote: effective_rate_limit = max(self.tempo, weights_rate_limit)
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: '331'
    quote: self.scores = np.zeros(self.metagraph.n, dtype=np.float32)
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: neurons/utils/api_utils.py
    line_ref: 43-48
    quote: 'UPTIME_MULTIPLIER_TIERS = { "excellent": {"threshold": 95.0, "multiplier": 1.15}, "good":
      {"threshold": 85.0, "multiplier": 1.10}, "average": {"threshold": 70.0, "multiplier": 1.05}, "poor":
      {"threshold": 0.0, "multiplier": 1.0} }'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: neurons/utils/api_utils.py
    line_ref: 51-55
    quote: 'RENTED_MACHINE_BONUS = { "base_multiplier": 1.08, "container_scaling": 0.01, "max_bonus":
      1.20 }'
  - claim_field: /documentation/source_version
    source_path: REWARD_MECHANISM_README.md
    line_ref: 383-384
    quote: "**Documentation Version**: 2.1  \n**Last Updated**: 2025-08-12"
  unresolved:
  - EMA alpha and SMA window are not used; smoothing is score_decay=0.9 with a max() merge, so smoothing.alpha/window
    left null.
  - aggregation.temperature/decay_rate/min_weight_floor have no literal source values (no softmax temperature
    or geometric decay in code).
  - Per-signal additive weights are null because signals combine multiplicatively, not via an additive
    weighted sum.

composition:
  shape: multiplicative
  overlays:

  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: RedTeam Subnet (by Innerworks)
docs.text:
Subnet RedTeamSubnet__RedTeam netuid None Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners submit Docker-packaged code solutions (e.g. a bot.py for the Humanize Behaviour challenge) to subnet challenges. Validators run each submission in a challenge Docker container, score it on challenge-specific criteria, and compare it against past and same-day submissions for originality. Highe DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, CommitReveal"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: PlagiarismDetection, Deduplication, CommitReveal"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: composite"]
  M1["Metric: other"]
  M2["Metric: similarity"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Other"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: Unknown"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: redTeamSubnetByInnerworks

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: source_code

    @burn {
        uid: "subnet owner hotkey (metagraph.owner_hotkey)"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        PlagiarismDetection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, cooldown }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: softmax
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                raw: "owner_burn_allocation"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            CommitReveal {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Other {}
        publish: Unknown {
            cadence: daily
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Final onchain score is a fixed 50/50 split between challenge performance and an
  "alpha burn" component that assigns weight 1.0 to the subnet owner's hotkey. When a challenge has no
  valid submissions its incentive weight is redistributed to alpha burn, and if no challenge has any valid
  submissions all weight goes to burn (fallback / full_burn regime). So at least 50% is always burned.
subnet:
  name: RedTeam Subnet (by Innerworks)
  owner_repo: RedTeamSubnet__RedTeam
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners submit Docker-packaged code solutions (e.g. a bot.py for the Humanize Behaviour challenge)
    to subnet challenges. Validators run each submission in a challenge Docker container, score it on
    challenge-specific criteria, and compare it against past and same-day submissions for originality.
    Higher-quality, original solutions earn higher scores; plagiarized/duplicate submissions are rejected
    or zeroed.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: challenge_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: softmax
  notes: Per-challenge performance score produced by each ChallengeManager (abstract get_challenge_scores;
    concrete scoring lives in external per-challenge images). Combined across challenges by their challenge_incentive_weight.
    README states a softmax normalizes scores. CHALLENGE_SCORES_WEIGHT default 0.5.
- name: alpha_burn
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: owner_burn_allocation
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Burn component: assigns score 1.0 to the subnet owner''s hotkey (metagraph.owner_hotkey). ALPHA_BURN_WEIGHT
    default 0.5; increased by redistributed weight from challenges with no valid submissions.'
- name: comparison_similarity
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Plagiarism/originality gate, not an additive reward. Max comparison score >= 0.6 causes the
    submission''s comparison validation to be skipped (treated as high similarity); README: similarity
    above 60% rejected, 100% similarity = zero score.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Challenge inputs generated/seeded inside the challenge Docker container; each miner submission
    is run against these inputs to produce scoring logs.
  source_identifier: challenge container (e.g. selenium/standalone-chrome for Humanize Behaviour)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Inputs come from seed_inputs plus container-generated challenge inputs (start_challenge).
- kind: commit_reveal
  description: Miners commit encrypted submissions; commits are subject to a 24h cooldown and time-on-time
    check before scoring.
  source_identifier: MinerChallengeCommit / encrypted_commit
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: COMMIT_COOLDOWN default 86400s; is_commit_on_time requires commit >= 24h old.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: subnet owner hotkey (metagraph.owner_hotkey)
    fraction: 0.5
    dynamic: true
    notes: 'ALPHA_BURN_WEIGHT default 0.5 (fixed floor). Dynamic because unused challenge incentive weight
      is added: alpha_burn_weight = ALPHA_BURN_WEIGHT + weights_to_redistribute * CHALLENGE_SCORES_WEIGHT.
      If no challenge has valid submissions, all weight goes to burn.'
  notes: Per-challenge scores are normalized (exclude_same_miner produces scores summing to 1), multiplied
    by each challenge's challenge_incentive_weight, summed, then combined 50/50 with the burn score. final_scores
    = challenge_scores * CHALLENGE_SCORES_WEIGHT + alpha_burn_scores * alpha_burn_weight.
weight_setting:
  cadence: daily
  method: get_onchain_scores combines normalized challenge scores (weight CHALLENGE_SCORES_WEIGHT) and
    alpha burn scores (weight ALPHA_BURN_WEIGHT) into final onchain scores. Scoring occurs once per day
    at SCORING_HOUR (default 14 UTC). The actual subtensor.set_weights call site is not present in this
    repository.
  on_chain_call: unknown
  tempo_or_interval: null
  smoothing:
    kind: other
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: README describes a time-based decay of historical scores ("Older submissions decay over ~10-15
      days") and _get_challenge_scores docstring mentions "applies time-based decay to historical scores",
      but no decay-rate constant is present in this repo's source; concrete decay lives in external per-challenge
      scoring. Decay kind/rate unresolved.
anti_gaming:
- kind: plagiarism_detection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Submissions compared against past and same-day submissions. Max comparison score >= 0.6
    skips comparison validation (high similarity). README: similarity above 60% => rejection; 100% similarity
    => zero score.'
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: exclude_same_miner keeps only the best-scoring submission among miners deemed the same
    entity (shared IP, Docker username, or overlapping coldkeys); other UIDs in the group are zeroed.
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Commit cooldown (COMMIT_COOLDOWN default 24h) and is_commit_on_time gate prevent rapid
    resubmission; submissions must be >= 24h old to be scored.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: MIN_VALIDATOR_STAKE default 10,000 required for validators. _get_alpha_stake_scores uses
    sqrt of alpha stake aggregated per coldkey (present but not used in the final get_onchain_scores path).
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Multiple independent challenges (e.g. Humanize Behaviour) each managed by a ChallengeManager
    with its own challenge_incentive_weight; scores combined across challenges. Number of active challenges
    not fixed in this repo.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - cooldown
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Miner state per challenge keyed by UID; a hotkey change on a UID resets that UID's MinerChallengeInfo.
    Each new commit immediately stops rewards from the previous submission (README).
  notes: ChallengeManager.miner_states maps uid -> MinerChallengeInfo (latest_commit); a bounded heap
    tracks up to max_unique_commits unique commits. Miners not in metagraph are pruned.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/miner/concepts/incentive.md
  - docs/challenges/humanize_behaviour/README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: src/redteam_core/validator/miner_manager.py
    line_ref: 238-250

docs.title: Solidity-Audit
docs.text:
Subnet ReinforcedAIAudits__solidity-audit netuid 92 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task A miner is a thin client that, given a Solidity contract, returns a JSON audit report listing vulnerabilities (vulnerability_class, from_line/to_line, description, test_case, fixed_lines). The validator generates a task (random text, valid contract, vulnerable, or hybrid contract enriched with a kno DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: ReferenceModel, LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CommitReveal, Deduplication, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: latency"]
  M2["Metric: composite"]
  M3["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Sma"]
  PUB["publish: CommitReveal"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: solidityAudit

    netuid: 92
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: api_response

    @guards {
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            ReferenceModel {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: oracle
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: other
        }
        smooth: Sma {
            window: 24
        }
        publish: CommitReveal {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "3600 seconds (CYCLE_TIME default)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
            count: 4
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Validator computes per-miner scores from the audit-report comparison and time
  score every cycle and pushes them on-chain via set_weights/commit_weights. No burn allocation or emission-burn
  code path was found in the validator.
subnet:
  name: Solidity-Audit
  owner_repo: ReinforcedAIAudits__solidity-audit
  netuid: 92
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: A miner is a thin client that, given a Solidity contract, returns a JSON audit report listing
    vulnerabilities (vulnerability_class, from_line/to_line, description, test_case, fixed_lines). The
    validator generates a task (random text, valid contract, vulnerable, or hybrid contract enriched with
    a known vulnerability), sends it to miners, and rewards reports whose vulnerability class matches
    the injected/expected one (accounting for synonyms), plus line precision/recall for hybrid tasks,
    faster response time, and quality of the description/test_case/fixed_lines fields as judged by a model
    server.
  submission_format:
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: report_class_match
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.95
  normalization: none
  notes: 'validate_reports_by_reference: 1.0 for a valid_contract with no reported vulns; for vulnerable
    tasks, score from matching the task vulnerability_class (is_synonyms) decayed by a coefficient over
    excess (false-positive) vuln classes; weighted by WEIGHT_ONLY_SCORE = 0.95.'
- name: response_time
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.05
  normalization: min_max
  notes: _calculate_time_score = min_time / max(min_time, result.time) (min_time floored at 60s), gated
    by report_score/WEIGHT_ONLY_SCORE and scaled by WEIGHT_TIME = 0.05.
- name: hybrid_line_f1
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'For HYBRID tasks the base score is blended with an F1 over reported vs vulnerable line ranges,
    penalized by a sigmoid of the ratio of missed healthy-code lines: score = (score + f1*(1 - missed_lines_penalty))
    / 2.'
- name: extra_fields_quality
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: none
  notes: Model server /estimate_response returns description_score, test_case_score, fixed_lines_score;
    each converted to a sigmoid penalty and combined with WEIGHT_DESCRIPTION=0.5, WEIGHT_TEST_CASE=0.3,
    WEIGHT_FIXED_LINES=0.2 into a penalty_factor, then mixed with the base score using WEIGHT_EXTRA_FIELDS=0.5.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: reference_model
  description: Validator generates the task contract and injects/knows the expected vulnerability class
    and line range ahead of time (via the model server, e.g. Claude 3.7 Sonnet for random templates);
    miner reports are compared against this known vulnerability.
  source_identifier: MODEL_SERVER /{task_type} and validate_reports_by_reference
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: The known vulnerability class/lines act as the ground truth for matching.
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: Model server /estimate_response scores the description, test_case and fixed_lines fields
    of matching reports.
  source_identifier: MODEL_SERVER /estimate_response
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Used only for the extra-fields penalty.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: Per-miner mean of buffered cycle scores, then multiplied by U16_MAX (65535) and
    rounded to produce the integer weight vector; on-chain normalization is left to Yuma consensus.
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn allocation found in validator code.
  notes: Final per-cycle reward = base_score (report + time) combined multiplicatively with the LLM extra-fields
    penalty. ScoresBuffer keeps up to MAX_BUFFER=24 recent scores per uid; ScoresBuffer.scores() emits
    round(mean*65535).
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Each validate() cycle adds a score per miner to ScoresBuffer, then set_weights averages the
    buffer per uid and submits. Cycle gated by CYCLE_TIME (VALIDATOR_SEND_REQUESTS_EVERY_X_SECS, default
    3600s) or a fixed minute.
  on_chain_call: commit_reveal
  tempo_or_interval: 3600 seconds (CYCLE_TIME default)
  smoothing:
    kind: sma
    alpha: null
    window: 24
    notes: ScoresBuffer is a fixed-length deque (MAX_BUFFER=24); set_weights uses the simple mean of the
      buffered scores per uid.
anti_gaming:
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  description: If CommitRevealWeightsEnabled is true on chain, weights are submitted via commit_weights
    rather than set_weights.
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Anti-duplicate-submission via Unique Network NFTs: each miner must mint an NFT with encrypted
    proof of work; check_tokens verifies the NFT validator, task and miner_info match and that report
    vuln classes equal those in the NFT, else no score is assigned.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Responses must pass signature verification (response.verify()) and NFT collection ownership
    (check_nft_collection_ownership); failures yield a None response scored 0.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: penalty
  description: False positives (excess vulnerability classes beyond the matching one) decay the base score
    via _calculate_asymptotic_decay_coefficient.
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages: []
  count: 4
  notes: Tasks are drawn from TaskType {hybrid_task, vulnerable (implied), random_task, valid_contract}
    with selection weights [65, 20, 5, 10]; scoring branches on task type (valid_contract rewards empty
    reports; hybrid adds line F1).
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ScoresBuffer per-uid deque (maxlen 24); reset for a uid when its hotkey changes (clear_scores_for_old_hotkeys).
    State persisted via relayer storage.
  notes: Inactive miners get a 0 appended (except a brand-new miner is not punished more than once).
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: config/base.py
    line_ref: '15'
    quote: NETWORK_UID = 92 if NETWORK_TYPE == "mainnet" else 222
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 1-9
    quote: 'import dataclasses

      import json

      import logging

      import math'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 84-85
    quote: The validator receives a completely random contract from the LLM, enriches it with vulnerabilities,
      ensures that the contract remains valid ... and knows the type of the vulnerability in advance.
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '55'
    quote: the response must be delivered as a single JSON object. For each vulnerability found, a separate
      JSON object with a description is formed.
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: '34'
    quote: WEIGHT_ONLY_SCORE = 0.95
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 452-453
    quote: "report_score = (\n                cls.validate_reports_by_reference(result.response, task_map[miner.uid])\
      \ * cls.WEIGHT_ONLY_SCORE\n            )"
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/weight
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: '33'
    quote: WEIGHT_TIME = 0.05
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 405-410
    quote: "def _calculate_time_score(cls, result: MinerResult, min_time: float) -> float:\n        ...\
      \ return min_time / max(min_time, result.time)"
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 639-641
    quote: f1_score = 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall) ... score = (score + f1_score *
      (1 - missed_lines_penalty)) / 2
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/3/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 676-680
    quote: "total_penalty = (\n                description_penalty * cls.WEIGHT_DESCRIPTION\n        \
      \        + test_case_penalty * cls.WEIGHT_TEST_CASE\n                + fixed_lines_penalty * cls.WEIGHT_FIXED_LINES\n\
      \            )"
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/3/weight
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: '38'
    quote: WEIGHT_EXTRA_FIELDS = 0.5
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 613-620
    quote: matching_vulns = {v for v in vulnerabilities_found if is_synonyms(task.vulnerability_class,
      v)}
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/1/source_identifier
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 528-531
    quote: "chunk = create_session().post(\n                    f\"{Config.MODEL_SERVER}/estimate_response\"\
      ,"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization_other
    source_path: neurons/base.py
    line_ref: 96-97
    quote: "score = sum(scores) / len(scores)\n            prepared_scores.append(round(score * int(self.U16_MAX)))"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/composition
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 685-687
    quote: "penalty_scores = [scores[i] * max(0.0, penalties.get(x, 1.0)) for i, x in enumerate(miners)]\n\
      \        base_score_weight = 1 - cls.WEIGHT_EXTRA_FIELDS\n        return [x * base_score_weight\
      \ + penalty_scores[i] * cls.WEIGHT_EXTRA_FIELDS ...]"
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 386-389
    quote: "if commitment_enabled:\n                    result, error = client.commit_weights(self.hotkey,\
      \ self.config.net_uid, weights_dict)\n                else:\n                    result, error =\
      \ client.set_weights(self.hotkey, self.config.net_uid, weights_dict)"
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: config/base.py
    line_ref: '29'
    quote: CYCLE_TIME = int(os.getenv("VALIDATOR_SEND_REQUESTS_EVERY_X_SECS", "3600"))
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/window
    source_path: config/base.py
    line_ref: '31'
    quote: MAX_BUFFER = int(os.getenv("VALIDATOR_BUFFER", "24"))
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 386-387
    quote: "if commitment_enabled:\n                    result, error = client.commit_weights(self.hotkey,\
      \ self.config.net_uid, weights_dict)"
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '76'
    quote: Each miner is required to generate an NFT containing encrypted proof of their work. This proof
      can only be verified using the private key of the requesting validator.
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 207-217
    quote: 'if not response.verify(): ... if not self.check_nft_collection_ownership(response.collection_id,
      response.ss58_address): ... if not self.check_tokens(response, task):'
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py

    quote: task_type = random.choices(list(TaskType), [65, 20, 5, 10])[0]
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: neurons/validator.py

    quote: "for uid, key in old_hotkeys.items():\n            if key != new_hotkeys[uid]:\n          \
      \      self._buffer_scores.reset(uid)"
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status

  - 'Whether a separate ''vulnerable'' TaskType exists: TaskType enum in protocol.py shows hybrid_task/random_task/valid_contract;
