docs.title: Web Agents Subnet (Autoppia / Infinite Web Arena)
docs.text:
Subnet autoppia__autoppia_web_agents_subnet netuid 36 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners submit metadata pointing at a GitHub repo (agent name, image, GitHub URL). The validator clones and runs that repo inside a sandbox as a web agent exposing a POST /act endpoint. The validator generates synthetic web tasks (Infinite Web Arena), executes the agent's returned actions in a fresh DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, StakeWeighting, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: composite"]
  M1["Metric: accuracy"]
  M2["Metric: latency"]
  M3["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: webAgentsSubnetAutoppiaInfiniteWebArena

    netuid: 36
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: source_code

    @burn {
        uid: "5"
        fraction: 0
    }
    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        RiskLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, status_enum, cooldown }

    Multiplex<tiered> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: financial_pnl
                specific: token_cost
                raw: "dollar_cost_of_tokens"
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: gated
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_round
            tempo: "1800 blocks (round = 5 epochs x 360 blocks); season = 100 epochs"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: tiered
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: 'Default BURN_AMOUNT_PERCENTAGE=0.0 ⇒ the single season winner takes the full
  weight (winner-take-all is operative). Burn is a configurable dial: any fraction is sent to BURN_UID
  and the remainder to the winner; at 1.0 consensus is skipped and everything burns. There is also a hard
  burn-all fallback when no eligible winner / no positive rewards exist. So operative reality ranges from
  active WTA to full burn depending on env config.'
subnet:
  name: Web Agents Subnet (Autoppia / Infinite Web Arena)
  owner_repo: autoppia__autoppia_web_agents_subnet
  netuid: 36
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners submit metadata pointing at a GitHub repo (agent name, image, GitHub URL). The validator
    clones and runs that repo inside a sandbox as a web agent exposing a POST /act endpoint. The validator
    generates synthetic web tasks (Infinite Web Arena), executes the agent's returned actions in a fresh
    browser, and runs executable tests to determine task completion. A task is binary-solved when eval_score
    >= 1.0; solved tasks are then shaped by execution time and token cost. Per-round/season the highest-rewarding
    miner is selected winner-take-all.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: eval_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Binary task success gate: eval_score >= 1.0 counts as solved, otherwise the task reward is forced
    to 0.0. eval_score itself comes from IWA executable tests (HTML verification, backend event testing,
    visual/LLM-based evaluation) via AsyncStatefulEvaluator. Acts as a multiplicative gate, not an additive
    term, hence weight null.'
- name: eval_score_weight
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.7
  normalization: none
  notes: EVAL_SCORE_WEIGHT=0.7. For a solved task the base reward contributes EVAL_SCORE_WEIGHT * 1.0.
- name: time_component
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: min_max
  notes: TIME_WEIGHT=0.1. time_penalty = min(execution_time / TASK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, 1.0) (TASK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=180.0);
    time_component = 1 - time_penalty. Lower execution time ⇒ higher component.
- name: cost_component
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: dollar_cost_of_tokens
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: 0.2
  normalization: min_max
  notes: COST_WEIGHT=0.2. cost_penalty = min(token_cost / REWARD_TASK_DOLLAR_COST_NORMALIZATOR, 1.0) (REWARD_TASK_DOLLAR_COST_NORMALIZATOR=0.05
    USD); cost_component = 1 - cost_penalty.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Synthetic web tasks generated by the Infinite Web Arena (IWA) on auto- generated demo websites,
    with executable tests (HTML string/DOM checks, backend event testing, visual assessment, LLM-based
    DOM evaluation, hybrid) determining completion. The same N tasks are reused every round within a season
    and only change when a new season starts.
  source_identifier: autoppia_iwa (AsyncStatefulEvaluator / IWA benchmark)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Mix of deterministic (HTML/backend-event) and model-based (visual/LLM) checks; final eval_score
    per task is reduced to a binary solved gate (>= 1.0) in calculate_reward_for_task.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: gated
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '5'
    fraction: 0.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'BURN_UID=5, BURN_AMOUNT_PERCENTAGE default 0.0 (no burn by default). When burn_pct > 0: winner
      gets (1 - burn_pct) and BURN_UID gets burn_pct. When burn_pct >= 1.0 consensus is skipped and all
      weight burns. Additional hard burn-all fallback (_burn_all) when there are no valid rewards or no
      eligible winner; that fallback puts weight 1.0 on BURN_UID.'
  notes: 'Per task: calculate_reward_for_task gives 0 if unsolved, else EVAL_SCORE_WEIGHT*1 + TIME_WEIGHT*time_component
    + COST_WEIGHT*cost_component. Rewards are averaged across tasks, aggregated across validators via
    consensus commitments, then a single season winner is chosen (season-best persistence with LAST_WINNER_BONUS_PCT
    overtake margin) and final on-chain weights are winner-take-all (final_rewards_array[winner_uid] =
    1.0), L1-normalized in set_weights.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_round
  method: 'Final per-round weights are winner-take-all: the resolved season winner gets 1.0 (split with
    BURN_UID if burn enabled), passed through update_scores (EMA) then L1-normalized (raw_weights = scores
    / ||scores||_1) and process_weights_for_netuid before subtensor.set_weights.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 1800 blocks (round = 5 epochs x 360 blocks); season = 100 epochs
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: 'update_scores applies EMA: scores = alpha*scattered_rewards + (1-alpha)*scores, alpha = neuron.moving_average_alpha
      (default 0.1). Since final rewards are already winner-take-all (1.0 on one uid), this EMA blends
      the on-chain score toward the new winner each round.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Same-solution detection: cosine similarity over bag-of-words of agent action sequences;
    groups with sim >= SAME_SOLUTION_SIM_THRESHOLD=0.90 are penalized by multiplying scores by SAME_SOLUTION_PENALTY
    (hard-coded 0.0, i.e. zeroed when active).'
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Per-round evaluation limited to top N miners by stake (MAX_MINERS_PER_ROUND_BY_STAKE=30
    in prod), and miners must meet MIN_MINER_STAKE_ALPHA=100.0 alpha stake to be evaluated.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Anti-sybil caps: MAX_MINERS_PER_COLDKEY=2 and MAX_MINERS_PER_REPO=2 unique active miners
    (repo cap per season). Unchanged repo commits are not re-evaluated within a season.'
- kind: risk_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Per-task USD cost cap MAX_TASK_DOLLAR_COST_USD=0.05; after MAX_OVER_COST_TASKS_BEFORE_FORCED_ZERO_SCORE=10
    tasks exceeding the cap, a miner's score is forced to zero.
sub_competitions:
  structure: tiered
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: 'Season/round hierarchy: a Season = 100 epochs containing 20 rounds of 5 epochs (~6h) each. The
    same N tasks are reused each round; per-season winner persistence (season-best reward per miner) decides
    the on-chain winner, with a challenger needing to beat the reigning leader by > LAST_WINNER_BONUS_PCT
    (5%) to dethrone. Not a true bracketed competition, so ''tiered''.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - status_enum
  - cooldown
  status_values:
  - handshake_valid
  - reused
  - evaluated
  reset_policy: Season-best competition history resets per season (best_by_miner keyed by season). Repo
    caps reset when a new season starts; unchanged commits skip re-evaluation until a new season or new
    commit.
  notes: _season_competition_history tracks per-miner best consensus reward in the season, best round,
    and snapshots; current_winner_uid persists across rounds. Optional adaptive evaluation cooldown (ENABLE_EVALUATION_COOLDOWN,
    default False) waits more rounds for worse/non-responding miners.

docs.title: Babelbit
docs.text:
Subnet babelbit__babelbit_subnet netuid 59 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners run low-latency predictive-translation models that predict the completion of an utterance step-by-step as it is revealed. Each prediction is scored against the revealed ground-truth utterance by a blend of lexical (edit-distance) and calibrated semantic (sentence-embedding cosine) similarity, DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, ReferenceModel"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: StakeWeighting, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: similarity"]
  M1["Metric: similarity"]
  M2["Metric: latency"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  SM["smooth: Other"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: babelbit

    netuid: 59
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [api_response, model_weights]

    @burn {
        uid: "248"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<tiered> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            ReferenceModel {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Other {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: 100
        }
        tracks {
            structure: tiered
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Validator (babelbit/cli/validate.py) fetches per-miner scores from the Babelbit-operated
  submit API and splits emissions between a Qualifying (main) tier paid proportionally to scores and a
  winner-take-all Arena tier. Default split is 80% arena / 20% main (BB_ARENA_INCENTIVE_PERCENT=80.0).
  When no scores are available the validator reuses last weights and, after BB_MAX_SKIPPED_WEIGHT_EPOCHS
  rounds, falls back to a single default/burn UID (BB_DEFAULT_FALLBACK_UID=248) — a degenerate-state burn,
  not a steady-state one. The reference scorer (score_dialogue.py) lives in-repo but the authoritative
  scoring is performed by Babelbit-operated services and only the resulting scores are consumed here.
subnet:
  name: Babelbit
  owner_repo: babelbit__babelbit_subnet
  netuid: 59
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners run low-latency predictive-translation models that predict the completion of an utterance
    step-by-step as it is revealed. Each prediction is scored against the revealed ground-truth utterance
    by a blend of lexical (edit-distance) and calibrated semantic (sentence-embedding cosine) similarity,
    multiplied by an earliness factor that rewards getting the meaning right before the utterance is finished.
    The dialogue score is the mean over utterances of the best earliness-weighted similarity achieved.
    Miners compete in a 20% Qualifying tier (paid proportionally) and an 80% winner-take-all Arena tier.
  submission_format:
  - api_response
  - model_weights
scoring_signals:
- name: semantic_similarity
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Calibrated semantic similarity s = clamp01((cos_raw - b)/(1 - b)) where cos_raw is the cosine
    between normalized prediction and ground-truth embeddings (mxbai-embed-large-v1 truncated to EMBED_DIM)
    and b is a baseline cosine estimated from random GT pairs. Combined with lexical via weight (1 - lex_weight);
    not an independent additive weight, hence null.
- name: lexical_similarity
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Character-level similarity 1 - edit_distance/max(len). Combined with semantic via lex_weight
    (CLI default 0.5; score_jsonl default 0.0). U_step = (lex*lex_weight + sem*(1-lex_weight)) * earliness.
- name: earliness
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: earliness_s = 1.0/(s+1) for step index s; multiplicative gate on the similarity blend so earlier
    correct predictions score higher. Per-utterance score is the max U_step over steps; dialogue score
    is the mean of those.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Revealed ground-truth utterance text for each utterance/step, emitted as 'revealed' / 'utterance_complete'
    events in the dialogue JSONL log; the last 'complete' event's 'ground_truth' field (EOF-stripped)
    is the target.
  source_identifier: dialogue_run_*.jsonl ('ground_truth' field)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Challenges are served by the Babelbit utterance engine (BB_UTTERANCE_ENGINE_URL, default https://api.babelbit.ai).
- kind: reference_model
  description: Sentence-transformer embedding model used to compute semantic cosine similarity for scoring.
  source_identifier: mixedbread-ai/mxbai-embed-large-v1
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: EMBEDDER_NAME default; truncate_dim=EMBED_DIM (default 64).
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '248'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: No steady-state burn fraction. compute_weights routes the main share to burn_uid only if there
      are no positive main scores, and falls back to [1.0]->burn_uid when no scores are available after
      MAX_NO_SCORE_ROUNDS. Default burn/fallback UID is BB_DEFAULT_FALLBACK_UID=248.
  notes: 'compute_weights: main_fraction = 1 - arena_fraction distributed proportionally to positive main
    scores; arena_fraction assigned entirely to the arena winner UID (winner-take-all). Final vector renormalized
    to sum 1 (_normalize_weight_vector). arena_fraction default 0.8 (main 0.2).'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Validator loop runs only when block % tempo == 0. It fetches main and arena scores from the
    submit API (/v1/get_scores), picks the max-score hotkey per mode, and calls compute_weights to build
    the (uids, weights) vector, then submits via retry_set_weights (signer HTTP /set_weights, falling
    back to a subtensor-gateway set_weights_and_confirm).
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100
  smoothing:
    kind: other
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA of the reward. The only temporal carry-over is reuse of the previous weight vector
      (last_weights) on rounds where the API returns no scores; otherwise weights are recomputed fresh
      each epoch.
anti_gaming:
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Arena eligibility is gated: a miner must have won at least one qualifying challenge in
    the last 7 days (rolling 7x24h window). There are 7 arena slots ordered by number of wins then by
    score. (README 5.2; eligibility computed by Babelbit-operated services, not in this repo.)'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Validator requests to the submit API and miners are Bittensor-signed (sign_message / bt_header_*
    headers); set_weights submissions go through a signer service. Miners self-reported as cheating are
    excluded from the qualifying share (README 4).
sub_competitions:
  structure: tiered
  stages:
  - name: Qualifying (main / round1)
    role: qualifier
    advancement_rule: win >=1 qualifying challenge in rolling 7-day window; top 7 by wins then score advance
      to Arena
  - name: Arena (round2)
    role: final
    advancement_rule: winner_take_all_per_challenge
  count: 2
  notes: Split controlled by BB_ARENA_INCENTIVE_PERCENT (default 80.0). 'round1' normalizes to 'main'/'qualifying';
    'round2' to 'arena'.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Arena eligibility uses a rolling 7-day (7x24h) window of qualifying wins; validator's
    no_score_rounds counter resets when the active challenge UID changes.
  notes: Win history and per-miner scores are persisted/aggregated by Babelbit services (Postgres-backed)
    and exposed to the validator via the submit API; this repo only reads the resulting scores.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: 0.1.0
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: medium

  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8

    confidence: high

  - claim_field: /task/summary

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes

docs.title: ComputeHorde
docs.text:
Subnet backend-developers-ltd__ComputeHorde netuid 12 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status unknown Task ComputeHorde is Bittensor's decentralized GPU-compute subnet (netuid 12). Miners run "executors" (dockerized GPU instances of declared hardware classes, e.g. spin_up-4min.gpu-24gb) that perform compute jobs. Validators earn per-block "allowance" (executor-seconds, proportional to executor count and DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: OrganicTraffic, ReferenceModel, HardwareProbe"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CommitReveal, Other, Collateral"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: throughput"]
  M1["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: CommitReveal"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: computeHorde

    netuid: 12
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: compute_service

    @burn {}
    @guards {
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: other
        }
        Collateral {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, cooldown, reputation }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                raw: "fixed score per successfully completed, non-cheated organic job"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            OrganicTraffic {
                trust_model: adversarial
            },
            ReferenceModel {
                trust_model: oracle
            },
            HardwareProbe {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            normalization: other
        }
        publish: CommitReveal {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "722 blocks (DYNAMIC_COMMIT_REVEAL_WEIGHTS_INTERVAL; cycle length)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
subnet:
  name: ComputeHorde
  owner_repo: backend-developers-ltd__ComputeHorde
  netuid: 12
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: ComputeHorde is Bittensor's decentralized GPU-compute subnet (netuid 12). Miners run "executors"
    (dockerized GPU instances of declared hardware classes, e.g. spin_up-4min.gpu-24gb) that perform compute
    jobs. Validators earn per-block "allowance" (executor-seconds, proportional to executor count and
    validator stake) and spend allowance blocks to pay miners for ORGANIC jobs forwarded from other subnets'
    validators via a Facilitator. The current scoring rewards miners by the executor-seconds of finished
    organic jobs that were properly paid for with valid allowance blocks. SYNTHETIC jobs (validator-generated)
    test executor availability and hardware authenticity; organic results are cross-validated against
    a Trusted Miner. Scores per coldkey are split across hotkeys (main hotkey gets the largest share),
    with a "dancing" bonus for changing the main hotkey between cycles to discourage weight-copying.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: allowance_paid_executor_seconds
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: other
  notes: 'Per-executor-class batch normalization: score/total * executor_class_weight. calculate_allowance_paid_job_scores
    gives each miner score equal to executor_seconds_cost of organic jobs that finished in the cycle and
    were validated as paid with fresh, unspent, sufficient allowance blocks (scores[executor_class][miner]
    += job_cost). Invalid spendings (CannotSpend / ErrorWhileSpending) score nothing.'
- name: organic_job_completion
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: fixed score per successfully completed, non-cheated organic job
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Legacy/alternative organic scoring in calculations.py (score_organic_jobs / calculate_organic_scores):
    each COMPLETED, not-cheated, not-on-trusted-miner organic job contributes DYNAMIC_ORGANIC_JOB_SCORE
    (default 1.0). The README''s allowance-based engine is the active path; this fixed-per-job path is
    documented in the scoring README as the organic-job contribution.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: organic_traffic
  description: Real user/cross-subnet jobs forwarded into ComputeHorde via the Facilitator gateway; completing
    these paid organic jobs is the primary scored activity.
  source_identifier: Facilitator (organic requests from other subnets' validators)
  trust_model: adversarial
- kind: reference_model
  description: A separate GPU 'Trusted Miner' (same code as a regular miner, not registered in the metagraph,
    only accepts the associated validator's tasks) pre-runs part of validation tasks to establish expected
    results, against which miners' organic results are cross-checked for cheating.
  source_identifier: Trusted Miner
  trust_model: oracle
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: Synthetic validator-generated jobs benchmark/test executors to confirm declared hardware
    classes and availability; jobs are matched to advertised hardware (e.g. A6000 for A6000-required tasks).
  source_identifier: synthetic jobs (SyntheticJobBatch)
  trust_model: adversarial
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  normalization: other
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    notes: 'apply_dancing_burners directs DYNAMIC_BURN_RATE fraction of incentive to DYNAMIC_BURN_TARGET_SS58ADDRESSES
      (weights *= (1 - burn_rate)), with DYNAMIC_BURN_PARTITION controlling the primary burner''s share
      when multiple are registered. Defaults: DYNAMIC_BURN_RATE = 0.0 and DYNAMIC_BURN_PARTITION = 0.0
      (burning disabled by default; activated via dynamic config).'
  notes: 'Normalization (other): per executor class, (miner_score / total_executor_class_score) * executor_class_weight,
    summed across executor classes per hotkey; final on-chain weights produced by bittensor.utils.weight_utils.process_weights
    with min_allowed_weights / max_weight_limit. Executor-class weights default DYNAMIC_EXECUTOR_CLASS_WEIGHTS
    = "spin_up-4min.gpu-24gb=98,always_on.llm.a6000=1,always_on.test=0,always_on.cpu.8c.16gb=1". Dancing
    bonus multiplier (1 + DYNAMIC_DANCING_BONUS, default 0.1) applied to a coldkey total when its main
    hotkey changed vs the previous cycle; main hotkey then gets MAIN_HOTKEY_SHARE (default 0.8) of the
    coldkey score, others split the remainder.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Celery task set_scores scores the current cycle (722-block cycles), normalizes per executor
    class, applies dancing burners, maps hotkeys->uids, and submits weights via the Pylon client (p_client.identity.put_weights).
    Commit-reveal gating via CommitRevealInterval when enabled.
  on_chain_call: commit_reveal
  tempo_or_interval: 722 blocks (DYNAMIC_COMMIT_REVEAL_WEIGHTS_INTERVAL; cycle length)
anti_gaming:
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Commit-Reveal weights: validators post hidden weights and reveal them in the next epoch,
    making copying of current weights impossible. DYNAMIC_COMMIT_REVEAL_WEIGHTS_ENABLED default True,
    DYNAMIC_COMMIT_REVEAL_WEIGHTS_INTERVAL default 722 blocks; commit/reveal windows computed by CommitRevealInterval.'
- kind: other
  kind_other: executor dancing (random GPU reallocation across UIDs + dancing bonus)
  enforcement: other
  description: 'Executor Dancing: miners move GPUs across multiple UIDs and change their declared main
    hotkey between cycles; changing it triggers a 10% (DYNAMIC_DANCING_BONUS) boost, encouraging variance
    to further reduce effectiveness of copying old weights.'
- kind: collateral
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Validators can require miners to deposit collateral (DYNAMIC_MINIMUM_COLLATERAL_AMOUNT_WEI,
    default 1e16 wei = 0.01 TAO) to be eligible for organic jobs. Cheating miners are slashed (DYNAMIC_COLLATERAL_SLASH_AMOUNT_WEI,
    default 5e15 wei = 0.005 TAO) via the collateral contract.
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Jobs are matched to advertised hardware (executor classes); synthetic jobs test executor
    availability and hardware authenticity, and unconfigured executor classes are excluded from scoring
    (weight defaults to 0.0).
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Organic results are cross-validated against a Trusted Miner that pre-runs validation tasks
    to establish expected results; jobs flagged cheated (job.cheated) are excluded from scoring and trigger
    temporary blacklisting (DYNAMIC_JOB_CHEATED_BLACKLIST_TIME_SECONDS, default 4 hours).
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Allowance is minted per miner-validator-executorClass per block and validators must spend
    valid, fresh, unspent, sufficient allowance blocks to pay for a job; overspending, stale, or double-spent
    blocks void the incentive (CannotSpend), enforcing fair usage spread across miners.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: organic_jobs
    role: main
    advancement_rule: Real cross-subnet jobs paid with valid allowance blocks; scored by executor-seconds
      (the primary incentivized activity).
  - name: synthetic_jobs
    role: qualifier
    advancement_rule: Validator-generated jobs that test executor availability and hardware authenticity;
      gate miner participation rather than being the primary reward path in the current allowance engine.
  count: 2
  notes: The scoring README describes scoring across two job types (organic and synthetic) combined per
    executor class; the README's active "Big Picture" describes scoring driven by paid organic-job runtime.
    Treated as a multi-mechanism (organic vs synthetic) structure.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - cooldown
  - reputation
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Temporary blacklists expire after DYNAMIC_JOB_FAILURE_BLACKLIST_TIME_SECONDS / DYNAMIC_JOB_CHEATED_BLACKLIST_TIME_SECONDS
    (each default 4 hours). Main hotkey declarations (MinerMainHotkey) are recorded per cycle_start and
    compared between cycles for the dancing bonus.
  notes: Per-miner persistent state includes MinerMainHotkey (coldkey -> main hotkey per cycle), collateral
    deposits (reputation/eligibility for organic jobs), and time-bounded blacklists after failed or cheated
    organic jobs.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - validator/app/src/compute_horde_validator/validator/scoring/README.md
  - validator/app/src/compute_horde_validator/validator/collateral/README.md
  - docs/synthetic-jobs-flow.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  extractor_notes: Large multi-service Django repo. The active scoring path is the allowance/organic-runtime
    engine (scoring/engine.py + scoring/calculations.calculate_allowance_paid_job_scores), confirmed by
    the README "Big Picture". A second documented organic path (fixed DYNAMIC_ORGANIC_JOB_SCORE per job
    in calculations.py) and synthetic-job scoring are also present; exact combination/weighting of organic

docs.title: InfiniteHash (subnet auction incentive system)
docs.text:
Subnet backend-developers-ltd__InfiniteHash netuid None Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners are Bitcoin hashrate providers. Each validation window they post on-chain bid commitments encoding (hashrate, price) pairs. Validators run an integer linear program that maximizes purchased PH/s under a budget derived from subnet emissions and consensus prices (TAO/USDC, ALPHA/TAO, HASHP/USDC DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: RealEngagement, MarketData"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: LivenessCheck, CommitReveal, RiskLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: throughput"]
  M1["Metric: throughput"]
  AGG["aggregate: ConvexOptimization"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: CommitReveal"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: infiniteHashSubnetAuctionIncentiveSystem

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: commitment_hash

    @burn {
        uid: "subnet owner earliest-registered neuron (burn UID)"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        RiskLimit {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            RealEngagement {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            MarketData {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: ConvexOptimization {
            composition: gated
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: CommitReveal {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "6 validation windows/epoch; windows of 60 blocks (window 5 = 61)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Live mechanism is the validator-managed auction (mechanism 1), mirrored onto mechanism
  0 ("Legacy mechanism 0 scheduling disabled. Auction weights are mirrored to both mechanisms from the
  auction pipeline."). Unused auction budget (total_budget_alpha - total_spent_alpha) is dynamically redirected
  to the subnet owner's earliest-registered neuron (burn UID) and included in weight normalization, so
  a variable fraction of emissions is burned each epoch.
subnet:
  name: InfiniteHash (subnet auction incentive system)
  owner_repo: backend-developers-ltd__InfiniteHash
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners are Bitcoin hashrate providers. Each validation window they post on-chain bid commitments
    encoding (hashrate, price) pairs. Validators run an integer linear program that maximizes purchased
    PH/s under a budget derived from subnet emissions and consensus prices (TAO/USDC, ALPHA/TAO, HASHP/USDC),
    selecting auction winners subject to a per-bid max price multiplier. Winners must then deliver the
    committed hashrate, verified against Luxor mining-pool scraping snapshots. Delivered winners are paid
    in proportion to an ALPHA-denominated payment; unused budget is burned to the subnet owner. Underdelivering
    hotkeys are banned from future auctions.
  submission_format:
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: delivered_hashrate_payment_alpha
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Per delivered winner, payment_alpha = delivered_hashrate * hashp_usdc * price_multiplier * (window_blocks
    / blocks_per_day) / alpha_usdc. Summed across 6 windows per hotkey; final weight = payment / total_payments_alpha.
    Only winners with delivered != False contribute.
- name: delivery_compliance
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Gating check: delivered_ph (from Luxor samples) must be >= DELIVERY_THRESHOLD_FRACTION (0.1)
    * total_commitment to count as delivered. Failing winners are excluded from payment and their hotkeys
    banned. (Docs prose says "at least 95%", but the code constant is 0.1.)'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: real_engagement
  description: Observed delivered hashrate from Luxor mining-pool scraping snapshots over the validation
    window, compared against committed hashrate. If validator scraping has gaps > 7 blocks the underdelivery
    check is skipped and all winners assumed delivered.
  source_identifier: Luxor scraping snapshots (LUXOR_SUBACCOUNT_NAME)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: ''
- kind: market_data
  description: 'Price consensus over miner/validator price commitments: TAO/USDC, ALPHA/TAO, HASHP/USDC
    FP18 prices used for the auction budget and ALPHA payment normalization.'
  source_identifier: compute_price_consensus (TAO_USDC, ALPHA_TAO, HASHP_USDC)
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Aggregated across commitments; banned hotkeys excluded from contributing bids.
aggregation:
  method: convex_optimization
  composition: gated
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: subnet owner earliest-registered neuron (burn UID)
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: total_burn_alpha = total_budget_alpha - total_spent_alpha is added to payments_alpha[burn_hotkey]
      and included in the L1 weight normalization, so the burned fraction is the residual of unspent auction
      budget each epoch.
  notes: Winner selection solves an ILP (PuLP/CBC) maximizing purchased hashrate under a PH/s budget.
    Final on-chain weights normalize summed ALPHA payments to sum-to-one (weights = payment / total_payments_alpha),
    then to u16 via division by max weight.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: calculate_auction_weights runs only in validation window 0 of the next epoch, after all 6 windows
    of the previous validation epoch are processed; weights committed and revealed for both mechanism
    0 and mechanism 1. Celery beat triggers calculate_auction_weights and set_auction_weights every 1
    minute (gated to window 0).
  on_chain_call: commit_reveal
  tempo_or_interval: 6 validation windows/epoch; windows of 60 blocks (window 5 = 61)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: commit_timelocked_mechanism_weights (CRV3, commit_reveal_version=4); falls back to set_mechanism_weights
      if commit-reveal is disabled. No EMA on final weights.
anti_gaming:
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: elimination
  description: Delivery verification against Luxor snapshots; hotkeys whose delivered hashrate falls below
    DELIVERY_THRESHOLD_FRACTION (0.1) of commitment in any bid are added to BannedMiner and excluded from
    future auctions until ban expires/cleared.
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Bids, price feeds and ban bitmaps are on-chain commitments; weights published via timelocked
    commit/reveal (CRV3) so validator weights are not front-runnable.
- kind: risk_limit
  enforcement: rejection
  description: _build_worker_items discards bids priced more than MAX_PRICE_MULTIPLIER (1.05) above the
    consensus price before the ILP, capping how far above market a bid can be.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Ban consensus (compute_ban_consensus) aggregates ban bitmaps published by other validators
    so excluded hotkeys are honored network-wide.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages: []
  count: 2
  notes: Two on-chain mechanisms (IDs 0 and 1). Mechanism 0 legacy hashrate scoring is disabled; the auction
    pipeline (mechanism 1) computes weights and mirrors the same normalized weights onto both mechanisms.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  status_values:
  - banned
  reset_policy: Ban recorded in BannedMiner (banned_at, epoch_start, window_number, reason); repeat offense
    updates the ban timestamp. Ban honored until it expires or is manually cleared.
  notes: Banned hotkeys' commitments are filtered out of future auctions.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: commit 1c1d576
  primary_docs:
  - docs/subnet_auction_incentive_system.md

  completeness: medium

  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes

docs.title: TPN - Tao Private Network
docs.text:
Subnet beyond-stake__tpn-subnet netuid 65 Archetype multiplicative Language mixed Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners run "mining pools" that coordinate worker nodes offering WireGuard / SOCKS5 VPN connections across many geographic locations. The validator samples a pool's workers, validates each WireGuard and SOCKS5 connection (egress IP must match the claimed worker IP), measures response latency, and geo DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: HardwareProbe, Other"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: LivenessCheck, DeterministicCheck, CodeInspection"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: volume"]
  M1["Metric: uptime"]
  M2["Metric: latency"]
  M3["Metric: similarity"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: tpnTaoPrivateNetwork

    netuid: 65
    lang: mixed
    status: partial_burn
    submission: compute_service

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: 0.8
    }
    @guards {
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: other
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, status_enum }

    Multiplicative {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: volume
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },

                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]

            HardwareProbe {
                trust_model: adversarial
            },

                trust_model: oracle
            }
        ]
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: multiplicative
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "360 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: A fixed 80% of emission is burned to UID 0 on every weight-set (BURN_WEIGHT =
  0.8, BURN_UID = 0, applied in process_weights_for_netuid). The remaining 20% is distributed across mining
  pools by the composite worker-pool score computed in the JS validator container.
subnet:
  name: TPN - Tao Private Network
  owner_repo: beyond-stake__tpn-subnet
  netuid: 65
  implementation_lang: mixed
task:
  summary: Miners run "mining pools" that coordinate worker nodes offering WireGuard / SOCKS5 VPN connections
    across many geographic locations. The validator samples a pool's workers, validates each WireGuard
    and SOCKS5 connection (egress IP must match the claimed worker IP), measures response latency, and
    geolocates the egress. A mining pool is rewarded linearly on pool size, multiplied by stability (up
    fraction), performance (latency), and the square root of geographic completeness. Workers themselves
    run no Bittensor neuron; only mining-pool miners receive emissions and distribute them to workers
    as they see fit.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: size_score
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: workers.length * stability_fraction — number of up workers in the pool, already penalised by
    the stability fraction.
- name: stability_score
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: stability_fraction = successes.length / selected_workers.length; entered into the composite as
    stability_fraction (a 0..1 multiplier).
- name: performance_score
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: performance_score = min(100 / (median_test_length_s / 20), 100); enters composite as performance_fraction
    = performance_score / 100. 20s is treated as a "good" test length (s_considered_good = 20).
- name: geo_score
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: geo_completeness_fraction = countries_in_pool / total_unique_countries across all pools; enters
    composite as sqrt(geo_completeness_fraction). metric_kind 'similarity' chosen as nearest fit for "geographic
    diversity / completeness fraction"; no exact enum exists.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: 'Validator actively probes each sampled worker: tests its WireGuard tunnel and SOCKS5 proxy
    by checking the observed egress IP matches the claimed worker IP (mismatch => status ''cheat''), and
    times the round trip.'
  source_identifier: validate_and_annotate_workers (score_workers.js)
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Egress IP mismatch is explicitly flagged as 'cheat'; outdated node versions and pool-membership
    mismatches cause failure.
- kind: other
  kind_other: ip_geolocation_database
  description: Worker country_code is resolved via IP geolocation (IP2Location LITE and MaxMind, configured
    via IP2LOCATION_DOWNLOAD_TOKEN and a MaxMind license key) to compute geographic completeness.
  source_identifier: ip_geodata (geolocation/helpers.js); README setup
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: External geolocation providers; used for geo_score country attribution.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 0.8
    dynamic: false
    notes: BURN_WEIGHT = 0.8 assigned to BURN_UID = 0 inside process_weights_for_netuid; remaining 0.2
      distributed across pools.
  notes: 'Per-pool composite: score = size_score * performance_fraction * sqrt(geo_completeness_fraction)
    * stability_fraction (multiplicative). The validator neuron then L1-normalises the EMA scores (raw_weights
    = self.scores / ||scores||_1) and emits via process_weights_for_netuid, which removes UID 0, scales
    the normalised weights by (1 - 0.8) and appends burn_weight 0.8 to UID 0.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: EMA scores are L1-normalised, processed through process_weights_for_netuid (burn carve-out +
    max-weight normalisation), converted to uint16 and set on chain. Retried up to 20 times on failure.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 360 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: 'update_scores: self.scores = alpha * scattered_rewards + (1 - alpha) * self.scores, with alpha
      = config.neuron.moving_average_alpha (default 0.1).'
anti_gaming:
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Each sampled worker must pass an active WireGuard + SOCKS5 connectivity test; failures
    mark the worker 'down', lowering stability_fraction and size_score.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Egress IP must match claimed worker IP; on egress_ip_mismatch the worker is marked 'cheat'
    (status_from_failure_code). Worker must also broadcast the expected mining-pool URL or it fails the
    membership check.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: score_node_version checks both pools and workers run a current version; an outdated version
    throws and excludes the pool/worker from scoring (partnered pools exempt).
- kind: other
  kind_other: random_sampling
  enforcement: other
  enforcement_other: statistical_sampling
  description: Workers are randomly subsampled per Cochran's formula (cochrane_sample_size, 99% confidence,
    5% margin) so a pool cannot game by showing only its best workers.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - status_enum
  status_values:
  - up
  - down
  - cheat
  reset_policy: On hotkey replacement at a UID the validator zeroes that UID's score (resync_metagraph).
    Worker-level status (up/down/cheat) is annotated per validation run.
  notes: Validator keeps a per-UID EMA score (self.scores) persisted to state.npz. Worker test_result.status
    takes up | down | cheat.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - federated-container/SPECIFICATION.md
  - federated-container/modules/scoring/score_mining_pools.js
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '1'
    quote: '# TPN - Tao Private Network'
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 203-204
    quote: Use netuid 65 for mainnet, 279 for testnet
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 1-9
    quote: The TPN subnet coordinates miners that offer VPN connections in a wide variety of geographic
      locations. ... Workers ... provide VPN connections ... Miners ... offer the VPN connections that
      workers provide and are given subnet emissions
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '32'
    quote: 'Emissions for miners on this subnet are based linearly on your worker pool size and geographic
      uniqueness. In principle: ` amount of workers * geographic diversity * slowness penalty`.'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: federated-container/modules/scoring/score_mining_pools.js
    line_ref: '258'
    quote: const size_score = workers.length * stability_fraction
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: federated-container/modules/scoring/score_mining_pools.js
    line_ref: 253-255
    quote: let stability_fraction = 0 ... stability_fraction = successes.length / selected_workers.length
      ... const stability_score = stability_fraction * 100
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: federated-container/modules/scoring/score_mining_pools.js
    line_ref: 283-285
    quote: const s_considered_good = 20 ; const performance_score = Math.min( 100 / ( median_test_length_s
      / s_considered_good ), 100 ) ; const performance_fraction = performance_score / 100
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/3/notes
    source_path: federated-container/modules/scoring/score_mining_pools.js
    line_ref: 291-293
    quote: const geo_completeness_fraction = round_number_to_decimals( countries_in_pool.length / total_countries,
      4 ) ; const geo_score = round_number_to_decimals( geo_completeness_fraction * 100, 2 )
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: federated-container/modules/scoring/score_mining_pools.js
    line_ref: '298'
    quote: const score = size_score * performance_fraction * Math.sqrt( geo_completeness_fraction ) *
      stability_fraction
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: sybil/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 236-243
    quote: norm = np.linalg.norm(self.scores, ord=1, axis=0, keepdims=True) ... raw_weights = self.scores
      / norm
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction
    source_path: sybil/base/consts.py
    line_ref: 1-2
    quote: 'BURN_UID = 0

      BURN_WEIGHT = 0.8'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/enabled
    source_path: sybil/base/validator.py

    quote: "burn_uid=BURN_UID,\n            burn_weight=BURN_WEIGHT,"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes

    line_ref: 250-253
    quote: final_weights = normalized_weights * (1 - burn_weight) ; final_weights = np.append(final_weights,
      burn_weight) ; final_weight_uids = np.append(non_zero_weight_uids, burn_uid)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/alpha

      + (1 - alpha) * self.scores )'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/alpha
    source_path: sybil/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 183-188
    quote: '"--neuron.moving_average_alpha" ... "Moving average alpha parameter, how much to add of the
      new observation." ... default=0.1,'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: sybil/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 84-89
    quote: '"--neuron.epoch_length" ... "The default epoch length (how often we set weights, measured

  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call

    line_ref: 276-284
    quote: response = self.subtensor.set_weights( wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.config.netuid, uids=uint_uids,
      weights=uint_weights ...
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/cadence
    source_path: sybil/base/neuron.py
    line_ref: 168-171
    quote: return ( (self.block - self.metagraph.last_update[self.uid]) > self.config.neuron.epoch_length
      and self.neuron_type != "MinerNeuron" )
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description

  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/1/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 125-126
    quote: Make an account at https://lite.ip2location.com/ ... Make an account at https://www.maxmind.com
      and generate a license key
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/3/description
    source_path: federated-container/modules/math/samples.js
    line_ref: 12-25
    quote: export function cochrane_sample_size( { uptime_confidence_fraction=.99, expected_proportion_up=.99,
      error_margin=.05, node_count } )
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: federated-container/modules/scoring/score_mining_pools.js
    line_ref: 186-187
    quote: 'const { version_valid, version } = await score_node_version({...}) ; if( !version_valid )
      throw new Error( `Mining pool ... is running an outdated version: ${ version }` )'
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/status_values
    source_path: federated-container/modules/scoring/score_workers.js
    line_ref: 237-275
    quote: 'failure_code == ''egress_ip_mismatch'' ? ''cheat'' : ''down'' ... test_result.status = ''up'''
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: sybil/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 313-315
    quote: 'for uid, hotkey in enumerate(self.hotkeys): if hotkey != self.metagraph.hotkeys[uid]: self.scores[uid]
      = 0  # hotkey has been replaced'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 188-193
    quote: 'The miner consists out of two components: 1. A miner docker container that is managed through
      `docker` 2. A miner neuron that is managed through `pm2`'
  unresolved:
  - '/aggregation/method: no top-level enum cleanly matches "L1-normalised proportional share of a multiplicative
    composite"; ''proportional'' chosen as nearest fit. The true cross-miner allocation is L1 normalisation
    of EMA scores.'
  - '/scoring_signals/*/weight: signals combine multiplicatively (size * perf_frac * sqrt(geo_frac) *
    stability_frac), so no additive per-signal weight exists; all left null.'
  - '/aggregation/burn_allocation: whether the 80% burn is currently live on netuid 65 in production cannot
    be confirmed from source alone; the constant BURN_WEIGHT=0.8 is the only evidence. mechanism_status
    set to partial_burn on that basis.'
composition:
  shape: multiplicative
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Polaris (Polaris Node Manager / Compute)
docs.text:
Subnet bigideaafrica__polaris netuid None Archetype pipeline Language none_docs_only Mechanism status unknown Task Per the distribution docs, Polaris is an installer / node-manager for running compute (GPU mining) nodes on the Bittensor network. A miner registers a machine, the manager detects and reports its hardware, self-validates against the server, and the machine participates as a rentable compute resource DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: HardwareProbe"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: HardwareValidation"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: hardware_spec"]
  M1["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: Other"]
  PUB["publish: Unknown"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: polarisPolarisNodeManagerCompute

    netuid: -
    lang: none_docs_only
    status: unknown
    submission: [compute_service, other]

    @guards {
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, registration_age }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: hardware_spec
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                raw: "performance_benchmark / self-validation score (CPU/GPU scoring), formula undocumented"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: HardwareProbe {
            trust_model: unknown
        }
        aggregate: Other {
            normalization: none
        }
        publish: Unknown {
            cadence: other
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
subnet:
  name: Polaris (Polaris Node Manager / Compute)
  owner_repo: bigideaafrica__polaris
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: none_docs_only
task:
  summary: Per the distribution docs, Polaris is an installer / node-manager for running compute (GPU
    mining) nodes on the Bittensor network. A miner registers a machine, the manager detects and reports
    its hardware, self-validates against the server, and the machine participates as a rentable compute
    resource. The docs state that only NVIDIA GPUs (24GB+ VRAM) qualify for rewards/incentives; AMD GPUs
    and CPUs do not earn rewards. The actual scoring / weight-setting algorithm is NOT described anywhere
    in these distribution READMEs — they are install/marketing docs with no validator code.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
  - other
  submission_format_other: 'hardware_spec / registered machine: a node registers its detected GPU/CPU
    hardware specs for reward qualification'
scoring_signals:
- name: gpu_reward_qualification
  metric_kind: hardware_spec
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Docs state rewards require an NVIDIA GPU with 24GB+ VRAM (48GB+ recommended); AMD GPUs and CPUs
    are explicitly excluded from rewards. This is the only reward-relevant signal the docs reveal. No
    scoring formula or weighting is documented.
- name: self_validation_benchmark
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: performance_benchmark / self-validation score (CPU/GPU scoring), formula undocumented
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: v2 docs mention a "Self-Validation System", "built-in CPU/GPU scoring", "performance benchmarking",
    and "tier qualification status", but give no metric definition, units, or aggregation. Recorded as
    a documented-but-opaque signal.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: The node manager automatically detects and reports system specifications (Python-based
    automatic remote hardware detection, v2.0.1) and runs a self-validation / server-authentication step.
    Used to gate reward eligibility on GPU type/VRAM.
  source_identifier: ''
  trust_model: unknown
aggregation:
  method: other
  method_other: Not documented. Distribution READMEs describe no raw-score-to-weight aggregation.
  normalization: none
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    notes: No burn mechanism documented.
  notes: No aggregation algorithm is described in any version README; this is a docs-only download repo.
weight_setting:
  cadence: other
  cadence_other: Not documented in the distribution READMEs.
  method: ''
  on_chain_call: unknown
  tempo_or_interval: null
anti_gaming:
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Reward eligibility is gated on hardware: only NVIDIA GPUs (24GB+ VRAM) qualify for rewards;
    AMD GPUs and CPUs do not. A "Self-Validation System" performs automated server authentication and
    performance benchmarking before a machine qualifies. Enforcement details and thresholds beyond the
    VRAM minimum are not documented.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Docs mention a "free tier" (CPU/testing, no rewards) versus a reward-earning GPU tier, plus "tier
    qualification status", but no formal multi-stage competition structure is described.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - registration_age
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: Docs reference real-time status tracking, "tier qualification status", per-UID multi-machine
    registration, and activity logging, implying some persistent per-miner state. The concrete state model
    (status enum values, ledgers, reset policy) is not documented.
documentation:
  status: docs_only
  source_version: v3.0.2
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - v3.0.2/README.md
  - v3.0.2/linux/README.md
  - v1/README.md
  completeness: low
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: low
  extractor_notes: 'DOCS-ONLY repo: this is the Polaris distributions/installer repository (binary download
    READMEs), not the validator/subnet source. The READMEs are marketing and install instructions; they
    contain NO incentive-mechanism algorithm, scoring formula, weight-setting logic, or constants. The
    only incentive-relevant facts are the GPU reward-qualification rules and vague references to "self-validation",
    "scoring", and "tier qualification". All code-derived numeric fields are left null. Most enum fields
    that have no documentary basis are set to "other" or "unknown" rather than guessed.'
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 1-3
    quote: Polaris Distributions — This repository contains distribution packages for the Polaris Node
      Manager application, organized by version.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: v1/README.md

    quote: a tool designed to simplify the setup and management of Polaris mining nodes on the Bittensor
      network.
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /documentation/source_version
    source_path: README.md

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/metric_kind
    source_path: README.md

    quote: 'Self-Validation System: Automated server authentication and performance benchmarking'

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes

    line_ref: 305-307

  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/kind
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '319'
    quote: v2.0.1 ✅ - SSH overhaul, Python-based hardware detection, resource deletion verified
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/kind
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 277-283
    quote: 'CPU Support: CPUs are accepted for testing purposes only. No Rewards: CPU processing does
      not qualify for incentives or rewards. Free Tier: CPU users are welcome to participate in the free

  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/tracked
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 53-55
    quote: 'Multi-Machine Management: Register and manage multiple mining rigs under a single UID. Real-time

    source_path: README.md

  unresolved:

  - aggregation.decay_rate / min_weight_floor — no numeric constants documented anywhere.
  - weight_setting.cadence / on_chain_call / tempo_or_interval — no on-chain weight-setting described.
  - scoring_signals[*].weight — no relative weighting of signals documented.
  - self_validation_benchmark — metric definition, units, scoring formula, and thresholds undocumented.
  - gpu_reward_qualification — exact reward function beyond the 24GB VRAM / NVIDIA-only gate is undocumented.
  - subnet.netuid — Bittensor netuid is not stated in the distribution docs.
  - per_miner_state status_values / reset_policy — concrete per-miner state model not documented.
  - burn_allocation — presence/absence of any burn not documented; assumed disabled, not confirmed.
extensions:
  note: This is the Polaris *distributions* repo (installer binaries + download READMEs), separate from

docs.title: Bitcast — The Decentralized Creator Economy
docs.text:
Subnet bitcast-network__bitcast netuid None Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status unknown Task Miners are YouTube content creators (or agencies running up to 5 accounts) who publish videos satisfying brand-defined "briefs". Validators obtain temporary OAuth tokens to read each account's YouTube Analytics. A video is eligible only if its transcript and description are LLM-judged to satisfy an DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: RealEngagement, LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, RateLimit, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: engagement"]
  M1["Metric: engagement"]
  M2["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: bitcastTheDecentralizedCreatorEconomy

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: content_link

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: engagement
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: engagement
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            RealEngagement {
                trust_model: oracle
            },
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: oracle
            }
        ]
        aggregate: Proportional {
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: other
            tempo: "VALIDATOR_STEPS_INTERVAL = 240 (interpreted as 4 hours)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_brief
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
subnet:
  name: Bitcast — The Decentralized Creator Economy
  owner_repo: bitcast-network__bitcast
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners are YouTube content creators (or agencies running up to 5 accounts) who publish videos
    satisfying brand-defined "briefs". Validators obtain temporary OAuth tokens to read each account's
    YouTube Analytics. A video is eligible only if its transcript and description are LLM-judged to satisfy
    an active brief (plus a unique-identifier and publish-date check). Each eligible video's score is
    the curve-difference of its 7-day moving-average YouTube Premium revenue (estimatedRedPartnerRevenue;
    for non-YPP accounts, estimatedMinutesWatched converted to revenue via a fixed multiplier), capped
    at the channel's historical median, then multiplied by a per-format scaling factor and a per-brief
    boost. Per-account video limits apply per brief format (FIFO, oldest videos kept). Brief USD targets
    are converted to on-chain weights against the subnet's total daily USD miner emissions.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
scoring_signals:
- name: youtube_premium_revenue
  metric_kind: engagement
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: other
  notes: 'Primary signal for YPP accounts: 7-day moving average of estimatedRedPartnerRevenue (YouTube
    Premium revenue), median-capped, fed through a diminishing-returns curve. normalization is custom
    (curve difference + median cap), not a listed enum.'
- name: estimated_minutes_watched
  metric_kind: engagement
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: other
  notes: For non-YPP accounts (and YPP accounts with zero revenue and min_stake), revenue is estimated
    from estimatedMinutesWatched * YT_NON_YPP_REVENUE_MULTIPLIER (0.00005) before the curve is applied.
- name: brief_content_match
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Gating (binary) signal, not a magnitude: an LLM judges whether a video''s transcript + description
    satisfy an active brief. Only matching videos receive a revenue-based score.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: real_engagement
  description: YouTube Analytics API metrics (estimatedRedPartnerRevenue, estimatedMinutesWatched, averageViewPercentage,
    views) pulled per-video and per-channel via validator-held OAuth tokens. Revenue is the 7-day moving
    average with a 3-day reward delay.
  source_identifier: YouTube Analytics API (estimatedRedPartnerRevenue, estimatedMinutesWatched)
  trust_model: oracle
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: An LLM (Chutes or OpenRouter provider) evaluates whether the video transcript and description
    satisfy each active brief's requirements; also screens for prompt injection.
  source_identifier: LLM_PROVIDER (chutes|openrouter)
  trust_model: oracle
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  normalization: sum_to_one
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: null
    notes: Unclaimed emissions accrue to UID 0 (burn). _sum_to_final_rewards sets rewards[uid 0] = max(1.0
      - other_sum, 0.0) so any emissions not claimed by miners go to UID 0. A configurable SUBNET_TREASURY_PERCENTAGE
      (default 0) is then moved from UID 0 to the subnet treasury UID (default 106). No literal burn fraction
      exists; it is residual.
  notes: Per-video USD targets = adjusted_curve_difference * scaling_factor * boost_factor. Summed per
    brief across a miner's accounts, divided by total daily USD emissions to get raw weights, each brief
    capped at its 'cap' (default 1.0), then globally normalized so the total sums to <= 1.0. Final per-miner
    reward = sum across briefs.
weight_setting:
  cadence: other
  cadence_other: every VALIDATOR_STEPS_INTERVAL (4 hours) forward pass
  method: forward() runs only when step % VALIDATOR_STEPS_INTERVAL == 0 (240 = 4 hours); scores are L1-normalized
    (raw_weights = scores / ||scores||_1) then passed through process_weights_for_netuid and convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit
    before set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: VALIDATOR_STEPS_INTERVAL = 240 (interpreted as 4 hours)
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Channel vetting: channel must be YPP member OR meet min alpha-stake threshold (YT_MIN_ALPHA_STAKE_THRESHOLD=1000);
    channel age >= YT_MIN_CHANNEL_AGE (21 days); YT_MIN_SUBS (100) <= subs <= YT_MAX_SUBS (500000); averageViewPercentage
    >= YT_MIN_CHANNEL_RETENTION (10%); total minutes watched >= YT_MIN_MINS_WATCHED (1000) over YT_LOOKBACK
    (90) days. Failing channels score 0.'
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Per-account video limits per brief: dedicated briefs reward up to 2 videos/account, ad-read
    up to 5, integration per-brief via max_count. Excess videos zeroed via FIFO (oldest kept). YT_MAX_VIDEOS=75
    overall cap. Each brief also has an emissions ''cap'' (default 1.0); briefs exceeding cap are scaled
    down.'
- kind: other
  kind_other: median revenue cap (anti fake-engagement lookback)
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Each video''s daily revenue/minutes is capped at the channel''s median daily value over
    the T-60 to T-30 window (YT_SCORE_CAP_START_DAYS=60, YT_SCORE_CAP_END_DAYS=30). README: caps the 7-day
    average at the median daily revenue from the previous month so YouTube''s ~1-month fake-engagement
    audit is factored in. A diminishing-returns curve (sqrt(v)/(1+0.1*sqrt(v))) further dampens linear-scaling
    exploitation.'
- kind: other
  kind_other: llm-judged brief eligibility + unique-identifier + prompt-injection screen
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Brief eligibility is LLM-judged on transcript + description; a unique-identifier string
    must appear in the description (check_brief_unique_identifier) and the publish date must fall in the
    brief's date range (with YT_VIDEO_RELEASE_BUFFER=3 day buffer). A separate prompt-injection check
    (check_prompt_injection) screens description/transcript.
- kind: other
  kind_other: lifetime deduction threshold
  enforcement: penalty
  description: A per-format lifetime deduction (YT_LIFETIME_DEDUCTION=100 USD dedicated, 25 USD ad-read)
    is subtracted from the lifetime score total by shifting the curve down by (lifetime_deduction / scaling_factor)
    and clamping at zero before differencing.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_brief
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Multiple brand briefs run concurrently, each fetched from the briefs server (BITCAST_BRIEFS_ENDPOINT).
    Each brief has its own format (dedicated|ad-read|integration), scaling factor (YT_SCALING_FACTOR_DEDICATED=1800,
    YT_SCALING_FACTOR_AD_READ=400; integration uses the ad-read factor), boost multiplier, max_count,
    and emissions cap. A video matches at most one brief (highest priority selected). Brief count is dynamic
    from the server, not a literal constant.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: No persistent cross-cycle per-miner ledger is set in the reward path; each forward pass re-queries
    YouTube Analytics over rolling windows. The validator keeps an EMA-like self.scores via update_scores,
    but no debt/status/cooldown ledger was found in the scoring code.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: 2.6.1
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - bitcast/validator/README.md
  - bitcast/miner/README.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  confidence_overall: high
