docs.title: Bitrecs
docs.text:
Subnet bitrecs__bitrecs-subnet netuid 122 Archetype multiplicative Language python Mechanism status full_burn Task Miners are e-commerce recommenders: given an input SKU, a store catalog, and the validator's prompt, a miner returns an array of recommended product SKUs (with name, price, reason) drawn from the store catalog. Validators score each response for validity and quality (signed/timely response, SKUs in DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: Other, Other"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, LivenessCheck, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: other"]
  M1["Metric: reward"]
  M2["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  M3["Metric: other"]
  M4["Metric: other"]
  M5["Metric: similarity"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  G --> M5
  GT --> M5
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  M5 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: bitrecs

    netuid: 122
    lang: python
    status: full_burn
    submission: api_response

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, status_enum }

    Multiplicative {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: classification_quality
                specific: validity_gate
                raw: "deterministic_validity_gate"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: reward
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: cryptographic_auth
                specific: proof_of_inference
                raw: "cryptographic_proof_of_inference"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: model_architecture
                specific: model_rarity
                raw: "model_rarity_multiplier"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            Other {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            Other {
                trust_model: oracle
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: multiplicative
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.2
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "epoch_length blocks (default 100); EPOCH_TEMPO=360"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: full_burn
mechanism_status_notes: 'The documented scoring pipeline (base reward + reasoning f_score bonus + verified-proof
  / rarity / consensus multipliers, EMA over scores) is fully implemented and live, but EMISSION_CONTROL_ENABLED=True
  with EMISSION_CONTROL_RATE=1.0 routes 100% of emission weight to EMISSION_CONTROL_TARGET_UID=0 inside
  emission_control_scores() before weights are normalized and set on chain. Net effect at the captured
  constants: full burn to UID 0.'
subnet:
  name: Bitrecs
  owner_repo: bitrecs__bitrecs-subnet
  netuid: 122
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners are e-commerce recommenders: given an input SKU, a store catalog, and the validator''s
    prompt, a miner returns an array of recommended product SKUs (with name, price, reason) drawn from
    the store catalog. Validators score each response for validity and quality (signed/timely response,
    SKUs in catalog, no dupes/query leakage, correct count, latency above r_limit), then award a base
    reward modulated by an optional reasoning f_score bonus, an optional verified-inference proof bonus,
    a model-rarity bonus, and a per-round consensus bonus for the elected miner.'
  submission_format:
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: response_validity
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: deterministic_validity_gate
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: A long sequence of pass/fail gates in reward(); any failure returns score 0.0 (e.g. Invalid_Validator_Hotkey,
    Timeout, Invalid_Signature, Invalid_SKU, Duplicate_Results, Query_In_Results, Invalid_Num_Results,
    Invalid_Axon_Time).
- name: base_reward
  metric_kind: reward
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Flat BASE_REWARD = 0.80 awarded once all validity gates pass.
- name: reasoning_f_score
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: When REASONING_SCORING_ENABLED, f_score (capped at max_f_score) is added to BASE_REWARD and the
    sum multiplied by REASONING_BONUS_MULTIPLIER=1.025. Missing report => BASE_REWARD/4; f_score<=0 =>
    BASE_REWARD/2. f_score/rank come from an external ReasoningReport synced from the verified-inference
    server.
- name: verified_proof_bonus
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: cryptographic_proof_of_inference
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: If a valid verified_proof is present and verifies (Ed25519 + SKU/hash/TTL checks via verify_proof_with_recs),
    score is multiplied by VERIFIED_BONUS_MULTIPLIER=1.15.
- name: model_rarity_bonus
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: model_rarity_multiplier
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: On verified proof, if the model's RarityReport.bonus >= 1.0 the verified multiplier is further
    multiplied by that bonus value (bonus is an external server-supplied float).
- name: consensus_bonus
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Per round, top responses are sorted and the most-similar top_k examined; if entity and model
    diversity thresholds are met, a winner is chosen via secrets.choice and its reward multiplied by CONSENSUS_BONUS_MULTIPLIER=1.05
    (single elected miner per request).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: other
  kind_other: validator_constructed_query_plus_catalog
  description: The validator supplies the ground-truth BitrecsRequest (query SKU, num_results, and the
    store catalog parsed from context) against which each miner response is validated.
  source_identifier: 'ground_truth: BitrecsRequest'
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Catalog SKUs (CatalogValidator) are the authority for valid recommendations.
- kind: other
  kind_other: external_reasoning_and_rarity_server
  description: ReasoningReport (f_score, rank) and RarityReport (rarity, tier, bonus) are fetched from
    the Bitrecs verified-inference server (VERIFIED_INFERENCE_URL).
  source_identifier: https://verified.bitrecs.ai
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Off-chain server supplies the bonus multipliers and reasoning f_scores.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: emission_control_scores(target_uid=0) sets new_target_score = EMISSION_CONTROL_RATE * total_score
      with EMISSION_CONTROL_RATE=1.0, giving UID 0 all weight and scaling every other UID's residual to
      (1-1.0)=0. Applied in set_weights when EMISSION_CONTROL_ENABLED.
  notes: Per-response score is built multiplicatively (base reward * reasoning/verified/rarity/ consensus
    multipliers), optionally scaled by a difficulty_decay in [0.9,1.0] when DIFFICULTY_SCORING_ENABLED
    (currently False). Scores are EMA-accumulated per UID, then in set_weights emission control is applied
    and the score vector is L1-normalized into raw_weights.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: set_weights() applies emission control, computes L1 norm of the per-UID EMA score vector to
    get raw_weights, runs process_weights_for_netuid + convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit, and calls subtensor.set_weights
    with version_key=spec_version. Triggered when (block - last_update[uid]) > neuron.epoch_length (should_set_weights),
    plus a backup weights_sync every BACKUP_WEIGHT_SYNC_INTERVAL=7200s.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: epoch_length blocks (default 100); EPOCH_TEMPO=360
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.2
    window: null
    notes: 'update_scores uses default_alpha = neuron.moving_average_alpha (default 0.2): score = alpha*reward
      + (1-alpha)*score for non-zero rewards; for zero rewards a gentle decay (1-low_alpha)*score with
      low_alpha = default_alpha/2 = 0.1.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: reward() enforces signature validity, validator/miner hotkey match, response success, timestamp
    freshness (verify_time, PERMITTED_CLOCK_DIFF_SECONDS=300), exactly one model, correct rec count, empty
    context, schema validity, SKUs in catalog, no duplicate SKUs, and that the query SKU is not echoed
    in results. Any failure => 0.0 reward.
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Axon and dendrite process_time must each be >= r_limit (default 1.0s); too-fast responses
    are rejected (Invalid_Axon_Time / Invalid_Dendrite_Time).
- kind: other
  kind_other: ip_entity_throttling
  enforcement: penalty
  description: get_rewards counts responses per axon IP; if max IP share >= BATCH_ENTITY_THRESHOLD=0.20
    those IPs are flagged as entities. When REWARD_ENTITIES is False they get 0 reward (Entity_No_Reward).
    REWARD_ENTITIES is currently True so the zeroing is disabled.
- kind: other
  kind_other: suspect_miner_decay
  enforcement: penalty
  description: decay_suspects multiplies each suspect miner's score by SUSPECT_MINER_DECAY=0.980 each
    update when that uid was not in the current batch.
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Verified inference proofs are Ed25519-signed and bound to a response hash and TTL; verify_proof_with_recs
    rejects expired/mismatched/invalid-signature proofs.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Optional reasoning, verified-inference, and difficulty subsystems are feature-flagged in constants
    (REASONING_SCORING_ENABLED=True, DIFFICULTY_SCORING_ENABLED=False, CONVERSION_SCORING_ENABLED=False)
    but are bonuses within one mechanism, not separate competitions.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - status_enum
  status_values:
  - suspect_miner
  reset_policy: On hotkey replacement (resync_metagraph) the UID's EMA score is zeroed; metagraph growth
    extends the score array with zeros. No explicit clearing of suspect_miners shown.
  notes: self.scores is a per-UID EMA vector persisted to state.npz. suspect_miners is a tracked set whose
    members are decayed by SUSPECT_MINER_DECAY when not queried in a batch.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: 0.8.9
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/running_validator.md
  - docs/verified_inference.md
  - docs/releases/0.8.5.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8
  extracted_at: '2026-06-06'
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 29-31
    quote: '## Subnet

      - Testnet: 296

      - Mainnet: 122'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: bitrecs/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 23-27
    quote: 'import numpy as np

      import bittensor as bt'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 74-75
    quote: miners produce arrays of product SKUs from a given input SKUs, a catalog of store inventory
      and prompting
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/weight
    source_path: bitrecs/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 40-40
    quote: BASE_REWARD = 0.80
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes

docs.title: Bitrecs V2
docs.text:
Subnet bitrecs__bitrecs-v2 netuid 296 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Bitrecs V2 is a prompt-evolution subnet. A miner produces an artifact.yaml (a system prompt, user prompt, model, provider and sampling temperature) submitted as a single GitHub gist plus an on-chain commitment. The artifact is run against a rotating set of ecommerce recommendation evaluations in a s DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, CodeInspection, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: bitrecsV2

    netuid: 296
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: [content_link, commitment_hash]

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, registration_age }

    Multiplex<tiered> {
        score: Metric {
            kind: accuracy
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: rank
            normalization: sum_to_one
            temperature: 1
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "30 blocks before epoch (~6 min); poll every 300s"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: tiered
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Only a single winner-take-all miner ever receives non-burn weight, and even that
  miner's weight is capped at MINER_EMISSION_PORTION (0.10) times a time-decay factor; the remaining burn_weight
  = 1 - miner_weight is assigned to uid 0. With MINER_EMISSION_PORTION = 0.10 at least 90% of emission
  is burned each epoch, and decay only increases the burn fraction over time. A dedicated set_weights_burn_only()
  path (uids=[0], weights=[1.0]) fires when MINER_EMISSION_PORTION <= 0.
subnet:
  name: Bitrecs V2
  owner_repo: bitrecs__bitrecs-v2
  netuid: 296
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Bitrecs V2 is a prompt-evolution subnet. A miner produces an artifact.yaml (a system prompt,
    user prompt, model, provider and sampling temperature) submitted as a single GitHub gist plus an on-chain
    commitment. The artifact is run against a rotating set of ecommerce recommendation evaluations in
    a sandboxed Affine/Docker environment; each run yields a success-rate score per environment. Rewards
    flow to the single miner whose artifact non-dominantly leads the epsilon-Pareto frontier under winner-take-all
    subset scoring.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: environment_success_rate
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Per evaluation environment, the eval container returns a `score` (success rate in [0,1]) and
    a `sample_size`. df_to_miner_scores aggregates by taking the max score per hotkey+task_name (best
    of retries). These per-environment scores feed Pareto dominance and WTA subset scoring; they are not
    additively weighted, so weight is null.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Rotating set of challenging ecommerce evaluation problems (SKU/recommendation tasks). The
    validator pulls the current evaluation set via get_current_eval_set_id() and runs the miner artifact
    against problem environments inside the bitrecs-evals Docker container, which returns a per-task success-rate
    score. SKUs are injected dynamically at test time.
  source_identifier: ghcr.io/bitrecs/bitrecs-evals:main (EVAL_CONTAINER_TAG); evaluation set served by
    BITRECS_PLATFORM_URL
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Scores are produced by a centralized off-chain platform/eval container, not reproducible on-chain.
    The eval set rotates and is held by the platform.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: rank
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: 1.0
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: burn_weight = 1 - miner_weight, where miner_weight = MINER_EMISSION_PORTION (0.10) * decay_factor
      for the single WTA winner; uids=[0, winner], weights=[burn_weight, miner_weight]. The burn fraction
      is therefore a dynamic residual (>= 0.90, rising as decay shrinks miner_weight). When MINER_EMISSION_PORTION
      <= 0 the validator sets uids=[0], weights=[1.0] (full burn).
  notes: 'Pipeline (scoring/engine.calculate_scores -> scoring/wta + scoring/pareto): (1) compute_pareto_frontier
    keeps only epsilon-non-dominated miners (epsilon = 2*std-error per env, clamped MIN_EPSILON 0.005..
    MAX_EPSILON 0.05); (2) compute_subset_scores_with_priority awards each non-empty environment subset
    to one winner (LINEAR subset_weight = |subset|) using score-first ranking with first-commit tie-break
    against per-miner thresholds; (3) scores_to_weights applies softmax (temperature 1.0, min_weight 0.0);
    (4) only the single max-weight UID is taken on chain. softmax is computed but only the argmax UID
    is used.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: calculate_scores_loop polls every SET_WEIGHTS_INTERVAL_SECONDS (300s) and sets weights once
    per epoch when blocks_until_next_epoch <= BLOCKS_BEFORE_EPOCH_TO_SET_WEIGHTS (30 blocks, ~6 min) and
    the epoch has not yet been set. set_weights_onchain assigns uids=[0, winner_uid] with weights=[burn_weight,
    MINER_EMISSION_PORTION*decay_factor], retrying up to 3 times.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 30 blocks before epoch (~6 min); poll every 300s
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: 'No EMA/SMA smoothing of the final reward in this code: weights are recomputed and set fresh
      each epoch via subtensor.set_weights. Temporal effects come only from the per-miner age decay factor,
      not from reward smoothing.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'epsilon-Pareto dominance: a copy ties (no dominance either way) and cannot enter/displace
    the frontier; a miner must strictly improve beyond epsilon on at least one environment (and be no
    worse within epsilon on all) to dominate. Designed so copying earns nothing.'
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: elimination
  description: Artifact policy forbids hardcoding evaluation-set SKUs/item names/descriptions into the
    prompt; violations result in a hotkey ban ("revoke your hotkey and rewards"). Only select jinja {{varname}}
    variables permitted; system/user prompt token caps (5000 / 10000).
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: rejection
  description: One submission per hotkey; gists must have a single commit and be < 24 hours old or they
    are rejected. Miners make an on-chain commitment to begin evaluation. Miners use a fresh hotkey.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Two-stage screener pipeline (screener_1, screener_2) gates artifacts before they reach
    the validator set group, with SCREENER_1_THRESHOLD / SCREENER_2_THRESHOLD / PRUNE_THRESHOLD score
    gates (threshold values are environment-configured, not hardcoded).
sub_competitions:
  structure: tiered
  stages:
  - name: screener_1
    role: qualifier
    advancement_rule: pass SCREENER_1_THRESHOLD
  - name: screener_2
    role: qualifier
    advancement_rule: pass SCREENER_2_THRESHOLD
  - name: validator
    role: main
    advancement_rule: winner-take-all over epsilon-Pareto frontier; PRUNE_THRESHOLD removes weak artifacts
  count: 3
  notes: 'EvaluationSetGroup (screener_1, screener_2, validator) forms a tiered evaluation pipeline: artifacts
    pass two screener stages (basic evals + thresholds) before entering the validator set group whose
    WTA result drives on-chain weights. Set group is derived from the validator hotkey.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - registration_age
  status_values:
  - screening_1
  - screener_2
  - validator
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: 'Each miner artifact carries a status and progresses through evaluation_set_group stages. Per-miner
    first_block (registration age) drives the emission decay factor: full emission within a 3-day grace
    period, then 5% reduction per day past grace down to a 25% floor (calculate_decay_factor with GRACE_PERIOD_DAYS=3,
    DECAY_FACTOR=0.05, DECAY_FLOOR=0.25). first_block also breaks WTA ties in favor of the earlier committer.'
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/validator_setup.md
  - docs/miner_setup.md
  - docs/artifact_policy.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: docs/validator_setup.md
    line_ref: '63'
    quote: NETUID=296
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: scoring/engine.py
    line_ref: 1-16
    quote: import os ... from scoring.constants import MINER_EMISSION_PORTION, GRACE_PERIOD_DAYS, DECAY_FACTOR,
      DECAY_FLOOR
  - claim_field: /task/summary

    quote: Bitrecs V2 is a prompt evolution subnet which rewards miners who optimize an artifact.yaml,
      an object containing a prompt, model, temperature and other parameters against a rotating set of
      challenging ecommerce evaluations. Miners submit artifacts via the CLI by making an onchain commitment
      to begin evaluation.
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: docs/miner_setup.md

      ... grouped = df.groupby([''hotkey'', ''task_name'']).agg({''score'': ''max'', ''uid'': ''first''})'

    line_ref: 23-25
    quote: Bitrecs V2 employs a winnter take all (WTA) scoring engine ... using epsilon-Pareto dominance
      to identify non-dominated miners on the frontier rewarding genuine improvements ... apply linear
      decay factors over time (with a 3-day grace period and 5% daily reduction to a 25% floor).
  unresolved:
  - '/aggregation/min_weight_floor: scores_to_weights default min_weight=0.0; not configured to a nonzero
    floor at the call site.'
  - '/weight_setting/smoothing/alpha: no EMA/SMA of final reward in this repo (off-chain platform may
    smooth scores; not in source).'
  - 'Screener gate values (SCREENER_1_THRESHOLD, SCREENER_2_THRESHOLD, PRUNE_THRESHOLD): env-configured,
    no literal in source.'
  - '/scoring_signals/0/weight: per-env scores are not additively weighted (consumed by Pareto + WTA subset
    logic), so no numeric weight exists.'
composition:
  shape: multiplex
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: SWE-Rizzo (Gen42 / Code)
docs.text:
Subnet brokespace__code netuid None Archetype gated Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners develop SWE (software engineering) pipelines / "logic" — Python code that takes a repository and a GitHub issue and returns a patch. The logic is served by the miner over a LogicSynapse, transferred into a Docker container with the target repo + issue, executed, and the resulting patch is eva DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CodeInspection, DeterministicCheck, Deduplication"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: sweRizzoGen42Code

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: source_code

    @guards {
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, performance_ledger, status_enum }

    Gated {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                raw: "api_cost_penalty"
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: gated
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: continuous
        }
        tracks {
            structure: single
            count: 1
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Operative scoring runs through coding/finetune (SWE-Bench competition). The template
  files coding/validator/reward.py and coding/rewards/reward.py contain dummy "response == query*2" logic
  and are NOT the live mechanism. Live weights come from update_scores() (top-10 finetune trackers) and
  set_weights() (winner-take-all on top 10 with score_spreading). No explicit emission burn found in source.
subnet:
  name: SWE-Rizzo (Gen42 / Code)
  owner_repo: brokespace__code
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners develop SWE (software engineering) pipelines / "logic" — Python code that takes a repository
    and a GitHub issue and returns a patch. The logic is served by the miner over a LogicSynapse, transferred
    into a Docker container with the target repo + issue, executed, and the resulting patch is evaluated
    by the SWE-Bench harness (resolved tests pass/fail). Score is the average resolution rate across SWE-Bench
    tasks, adjusted down by LLM API cost.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: swebench_resolution
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Per-task binary: SWEBenchTask.score returns 1 if the SWE-Bench eval report marks the instance
    "resolved", else 0 (score_patch). A patch that touches a test file scores 0. tracker.raw_score = mean
    of per-task scores.'
- name: llm_cost_penalty
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: api_cost_penalty
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'adjust_score_by_cost: if avg LLM cost per task <= ideal_cost (0.5) no penalty; otherwise penalty_factor
    = 1.0 - min(0.9, (llm_cost - 0.5)/0.5) multiplies the raw score. Multiplicative gate, not an additive
    signal.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: SWE-Bench tasks generated from SWEFullDataset; each task runs the SWE-Bench eval harness
    (make_test_spec / get_eval_report) inside a per-instance Docker image to determine whether the miner's
    patch resolves the issue.
  source_identifier: SWEFullDataset / swebench harness; coding/datasets/swefull.py
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: 'Tasks rotate: every ~1.5 days (10800 blocks) 50 of 100 tasks are replaced (FinetunePipeline.update_tasks).'
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: gated
  normalization: sum_to_one
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No emission-burn allocation found in source. Blacklisted coldkeys are zeroed (set_weights),
      and all-but-top-10 scores are set to 0, but emissions are not routed to a burn UID in the read code.
  notes: 'update_scores selects trackers from the latest competition: top 10 by score (rest 0); if more
    than 10 tie at max_score, distribute across distinct-logic groups. Scores within max_score-0.17 are
    kept, all below threshold set to 0 (finetune_scores[finetune_scores < threshold] = 0). set_weights
    then normalizes self.scores to sum to 1 (np.divide by sum), applies score_spreading, and keeps only
    the top-10 uids (all others zeroed) before process_weights_for_netuid.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: continuous
  method: Validator run loop calls forward() each step (with a 5-min sleep), then sync() which may call
    set_weights(). set_weights normalizes self.scores to sum-to-one, passes through score_spreading(scores,
    divisions, 0.002, 0.0025, kurtosis_factor=0.5, divisions=randint(2,9)), zeroes all but top-10 uids,
    then process_weights_for_netuid + convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: null
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: update_scores docstring says "exponential moving average" but the implementation overwrites
      self.scores = finetune_scores each cycle (no EMA alpha blend); prior scores are reused only via
      stored tracker state. No EMA alpha constant present in source.
anti_gaming:
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Miner logic runs in a sandboxed Docker container restricted to an import allowlist (ALLOWED_MODULES,
    ALLOWED_IMPORTS), a disallowed list (DISALLOWED_IMPORTS = ["zlib"]) and a character cap (NUM_ALLOWED_CHARACTERS
    = 1000000). Invalid models/logic get score 0.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Patches that touch any file with "test" in its name score 0 (SWEBenchTask.score) — prevents
    miners from editing the test suite to pass.
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'logic_similar dedup: trackers whose logic matches an already-graded logic reuse the previous
    score rather than being re-evaluated; in update_scores, when >10 trackers tie at max_score, duplicates
    of the same logic are grouped and only a subset get max_score, the rest get score - 0.01.'
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: BLACKLISTED_COLDKEYS (one hard-coded coldkey "blacklisted for repeatedly exploiting") are
    excluded from logic gathering and have their scores set to 0 in set_weights.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'should_evaluate: a tracker is skipped if it already has >=6 evaluations in the last 7
    days (blocks_in_7_days = 7*24*60*60//12), limiting re-grading frequency.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: single
  stages: []
  count: 1
  notes: Single SWE-Bench competition keyed by COMPETITION_ID = 7 (constants.py), with a COMPETITION_END_DATE
    = "2025-01-29". Trackers/tasks are namespaced per competition id.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - performance_ledger
  - status_enum
  status_values:
  - valid
  - invalid
  - scoring_in_progress
  - scoring_in_queue
  reset_policy: Tracker state persisted in trackers_{COMPETITION_ID}.pkl and validator state.npz (scores,
    finetune_results). Models/trackers cleared every 14400*3 blocks (~5 days) in forward(); score=0 forces
    re-evaluation. On hotkey replacement (resync_metagraph) the uid's score is zeroed.
  notes: TrackingInfo carries hotkey, uid, logic, score, raw_score, avg_cost, score_timestamps, block.
    ModelStore tracks per-logic validity and scoring status flags.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: COMPETITION_ID 7; IMAGE_VERSION 2.0; COMPETITION_END_DATE 2025-01-29
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/validators/swe.md
  - docs/validators/quickstart.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  confidence_overall: medium
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  extractor_notes: Live mechanism is the finetune SWE-Bench competition (coding/finetune/*, coding/validator/forward.py,
    coding/base/validator.py). The top-ranked locate.py reward anchors (coding/validator/reward.py, coding/rewards/reward.py)
    are inherited template stubs (dummy query*2) and were intentionally NOT used as the mechanism. score_spreading
    is imported from an external `scoring_utils` module not present in the repo, so its internal transform
    is unresolved.
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 69-71
    quote: Miners are to develop SWE-Bench pipelines that will be evaluated... The code for their pipeline
      must be a python script that takes in a repository and a git issue and returns a completed patch
      for the issue.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: coding/finetune/tracker.py
    line_ref: 37-55
    quote: synapse = LogicSynapse() ... TrackingInfo(logic=synapse.logic, ...)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0
    source_path: coding/tasks/swe.py
    line_ref: 251-254
    quote: 'if result[1][instance["instance_id"]]["resolved"]: return 1 else: return 0'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: coding/tasks/swe.py
    line_ref: 517-519
    quote: 'if any("test" in file.file_name for file in changed_files.files): ... return 0'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1
    source_path: coding/finetune/pipeline.py
    line_ref: 517-518
    quote: tracker.raw_score = sum(scores) / len(scores); tracker.score = adjust_score_by_cost(tracker.raw_score,
      tracker.avg_cost)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: coding/finetune/pipeline.py
    line_ref: 80-91
    quote: ideal_cost = 0.5 ... penalty_factor = 1.0 - min(0.9, (llm_cost - ideal_cost) / ideal_cost);
      adjusted_score = score * penalty_factor
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0
    source_path: coding/tasks/swe.py
    line_ref: 204-211
    quote: report = get_eval_report(test_spec=test_spec, prediction=pred, test_log_path=test_output_path,
      include_tests_status=True)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/notes
    source_path: coding/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 30-32
    quote: 'if self.last_task_update + 10800 < self.block: # every 1.5 days replace 50(half) the tasks  FinetunePipeline.update_tasks(self.config,
      50, 100)'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: coding/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 288-292
    quote: top_10_uids = np.argsort(weighted_scores)[-10:] ... weighted_scores[~np.isin(np.arange(len(weighted_scores)),
      top_10_uids)] = 0
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: coding/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 446-447
    quote: 'threshold = max_score - 0.17  # within 0.18 of max score  finetune_scores[finetune_scores
      < threshold] = 0'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization
    source_path: coding/base/validator.py
    line_ref: '276'
    quote: raw_weights = np.divide(self.scores, np.sum(self.scores, axis=0))
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: coding/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 280-287
    quote: weighted_scores = score_spreading(current_scores, divisions, 0.002, 0.0025, kurtosis_factor=0.5,
      divisions=np.random.randint(2, 9))
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: coding/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 317-325
    quote: result, msg = self.subtensor.set_weights(wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.config.netuid, uids=uint_uids,
      weights=uint_weights, ... version_key=self.spec_version)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/cadence
    source_path: coding/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: '83'
    quote: sleep(60*5)
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes
    source_path: coding/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: '456'

    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0
    source_path: coding/constants.py
    line_ref: 67-70
    quote: DISALLOWED_IMPORTS = ["zlib",]  NUM_ALLOWED_CHARACTERS = 1000000
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1
    source_path: coding/tasks/swe.py
    line_ref: 517-519
    quote: print("Miner is not allowed to touch test files, returning a score of 0") return 0
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2
    source_path: coding/validator/reward.py

  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/3

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/4
    source_path: coding/finetune/pipeline.py

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: unknown
mechanism_status_notes: Miner-only repo. README documents a two-factor scoring model (lease revenue 100%
  plus a 169-hour availability multiplier) but the actual scoring/weight computation lives on the validator,
  which is not in this repository. Whether scoring is live or burning emissions cannot be determined from
  the available source.
subnet:
  name: ByteLeap Compute Network (Miner)
  owner_repo: byteleapai__byteleap-Miner
  netuid: 128
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners aggregate worker hardware resources and act as the Bittensor network interface for the
    ByteLeap distributed compute platform (SN128). Each miner manages up to 100 workers over WebSocket,
    reports their status and system info to validators via encrypted heartbeats, and executes cryptographic
    compute challenges (CPU/GPU matrix multiplication with Merkle-tree commitments and proofs) routed
    from the validator. Per the README, miners earn rewards through active compute leases (lease revenue)
    and an availability multiplier; the scoring itself is performed by the validator (separate repo).
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
  - commitment_hash
  extensions:
    detail: 'Two-phase challenge protocol: Phase 1 miner sends CommitmentData (Merkle root per computation
      unit); Phase 2 miner submits ProofData (row hashes + Merkle proofs) for validator verification.'
scoring_signals:
- name: lease_revenue
  metric_kind: reward
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: 'README "Score Components (Weighted)": "Lease Revenue (100%): Active compute rentals generate
    the primary score". Idle workers score zero on this component. Documentation claim only; no computation
    present in this repo.'
- name: availability_multiplier
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'README: "Availability Multiplier: Based on 169-hour online presence". Described as a multiplier
    (not additively weighted), hence weight null. Documentation claim only; the 169-hour figure appears
    only in the README, not in any code constant.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: Workers report system/hardware info (CPU, memory, disk, GPU) and online status via heartbeats;
    miners run CPU/GPU matrix-multiplication challenges whose results are committed via Merkle roots and
    proven on request.
  source_identifier: neurons/shared/challenges (cpu_matrix, gpu_matrix)
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Challenge-response with Merkle commitments and ECDSA signatures (GPU) defends against spoofed
    compute. Actual verification/scoring runs on the validator (not in this repo).
aggregation:
  method: undocumented
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn information present in this miner-only repo.
  notes: README states final score "sums all worker performance (capped at 100)" and that lease revenue
    (100%) is combined with an availability multiplier, implying an additive-then- multiplicative (hybrid)
    composition. The exact aggregation method is computed by the validator and is not in this repository,
    hence 'undocumented'.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: No per-miner persistent scoring state in this repo. The miner tracks worker connections and a
    passive validator blacklist (failure threshold 5, TTL 600s) but no score ledger.
anti_gaming:
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Cryptographic compute challenges (CPU/GPU matrix multiplication) with Merkle-tree commitment/proof
    verification and ECDSA signatures for GPU results validate that a worker actually possesses the claimed
    hardware. README: "Challenges target only unleased workers".'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Matrix challenges use dynamic matrix sizing with variance (base_matrix_challenge.py calculate_dynamic_matrix_size,
    "dynamic variance for anti-gaming") so challenges are not precomputable.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'config/miner_config.yaml requires non-whitelisted validators to hold validator_min_stake_tao:
    10000; a validator whitelist and passive blacklist (threshold 5 failures, ttl 600s) gate which validators
    the miner serves.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
documentation:
  status: docs_only
  source_version: 0.0.6
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - config/miner_config.yaml
  completeness: low
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8
  extracted_at: '2026-06-06'
  confidence_overall: low
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: config/miner_config.yaml
    line_ref: '4'
    quote: 'netuid: 128'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: requirements.txt
    line_ref: null
    quote: 'Python repo (33 code files); README: ''Python 3.8+''.'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 1-3
    quote: ByteLeap Miner - Bittensor SN128 Compute Network ... resource aggregation component of the
      ByteLeap distributed compute platform.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 3-30
    quote: Miners connect to the Bittensor network (SN128), aggregate worker resources, and earn rewards
      through active compute leases and computational challenges. ... Maximum 100 workers per miner
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: neurons/shared/protocols.py
    line_ref: 348-423
    quote: 'Phase 1: Commitment data sent from Miner to Validator ... Phase 2: Proof data sent from Miner
      to Validator.'
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 16-26
    quote: 'Lease Revenue (100%): Active compute rentals generate the primary score ... Idle workers score
      zero on this component'
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1

Subnet chutesai__chutes netuid None Archetype opaque Language python Mechanism status unknown Task On the chutes.ai compute platform, miners contribute GPU hardware and run "chutes" (containerized FastAPI-style applications, e.g. vLLM/SGLang/diffusion model servers) on top of docker images. flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: unknown
mechanism_status_notes: The Bittensor weight/emission incentive mechanism is not present in this repository.
  This is the client SDK/CLI only; per README the validator/API code is in rayonlabs/chutes-api and miner
  code in rayonlabs/chutes-miner. No set_weights call, scoring function, or emission/burn logic exists
  in this tree (grep for set_weights|emission|incentive|burn|reward|consensus|yuma returns nothing). The
  operative reality of on-chain weight setting cannot be determined from this repo.
subnet:
  name: Chutes (chutes.ai)
  owner_repo: chutesai__chutes
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: On the chutes.ai compute platform, miners contribute GPU hardware and run "chutes" (containerized
    FastAPI-style applications, e.g. vLLM/SGLang/diffusion model servers) on top of docker images. Users
    (authenticated via Bittensor hotkey signature) deploy and call these applications and are billed per
    GPU-hour. The GraVal library validates that miners' claimed GPUs are authentic (VRAM checks, device-seeded
    matrix multiplications). How miner contribution translates into on-chain validator weights/emissions
    is NOT defined in this repository.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals: []
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: GraVal graphics-card validation library used as middleware inside each chute to verify
    the GPUs miners claim to run are authentic (VRAM capacity checks, matrix multiplications seeded by
    device information, filesystem checks, device-info challenges, pings). This is a hardware authenticity
    probe; the README does not describe how its results feed validator weights.
  source_identifier: GraVal
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Quoted from README only. The repo references GraVal as middleware but does not contain the scoring/weight
    logic that would consume its results.
aggregation:
  method: undocumented
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    notes: No burn or emission-routing logic present in this repository.
  notes: No aggregation of miner scores into weights exists in this client SDK. Validator aggregation
    is in the separate chutes-api repo and is undocumented here.
weight_setting:
  cadence: undocumented
  method: ''
  on_chain_call: unknown
  tempo_or_interval: null
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    notes: No weight-setting / smoothing code in this repository.
anti_gaming:
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: rejection
  description: GraVal hardware-validation library runs as middleware within each chute to ensure miner
    GPUs are authentic/correct (VRAM capacity checks, device-seeded matrix multiplications, filesystem
    checks, device-info challenges, pings). Per README it decrypts validator traffic and performs additional
    validation steps. Enforcement consequence (rejection vs scoring penalty) is not specified in this
    repo; 'rejection' chosen as the closest documented effect (failing GPUs would not be accepted as authentic).
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Platform access requires a Bittensor wallet + hotkey; authentication is performed via Bittensor
    hotkey signatures (HOTKEY_HEADER / SIGNATURE_HEADER / NONCE_HEADER). A registration and (historically)
    a developer deposit / minimum balance acted as an economic barrier to entry. This gates platform participation
    but is not an on-chain weight mechanism.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none

per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  notes: No per-miner scoring state is tracked in this client SDK. Any validator-side miner ledger would
    live in the separate chutes-api repo.

docs.title: CoinMetrics Precog
docs.text:
Subnet coinmetrics__precog netuid 55 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status unknown Task Miners produce continuous high-frequency price forecasts for a fixed set of crypto assets (BTC, ETH, TAO). Every 5 minutes the validator queries each miner for, per asset, a point prediction of the price one hour into the future and an interval estimate (a [min, max] band) the asset is expected to t DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: RateLimit, StakeWeighting"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: regression_error"]
  M1["Metric: regression_error"]
  AGG["aggregate: RankBased"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: coinMetricsPrecog

    netuid: 55
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: signals

    @guards {
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<multi_asset> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: regression_error
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: rank
            },
            Metric {
                kind: regression_error
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: rank
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: MarketData {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: RankBased {
            normalization: sum_to_one
            decay_rate: 0.8
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: continuous
            tempo: "weights_rate_limit hyperparam (blocks_since_last_update >= weights_rate_limit)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_asset
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
subnet:
  name: CoinMetrics Precog
  owner_repo: coinmetrics__precog
  netuid: 55
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners produce continuous high-frequency price forecasts for a fixed set of crypto assets (BTC,
    ETH, TAO). Every 5 minutes the validator queries each miner for, per asset, a point prediction of
    the price one hour into the future and an interval estimate (a [min, max] band) the asset is expected
    to trade in over that hour. One hour later the validator evaluates the point prediction against the
    CoinMetrics 1-second reference rate and the interval against the observed price path, ranks miners
    per task, and converts ranks into rewards.
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: point_error
  metric_kind: regression_error
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: 0.166
  normalization: rank
  notes: Per-asset relative point error abs(prediction - actual)/actual, evaluated at eval_time against
    the CoinMetrics ReferenceRateUSD. Missing/absent predictions receive np.inf (worst). Errors are ranked
    ascending (lower error = better rank). The 0.166 weight is the per-asset 'point' weight from constants.TASK_WEIGHTS
    (same value for btc/eth/tao_bittensor).
- name: interval_score
  metric_kind: regression_error
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.166
  normalization: rank
  notes: Per-asset interval score = inclusion_factor * width_factor, where inclusion_factor = fraction
    of observed 1s prices inside the predicted [min,max] band, and width_factor = (effective_top - effective_bottom)/(pred_max
    - pred_min). Higher is better; ranks are computed on negated scores. The 0.166 weight is the per-asset
    'interval' weight from constants.TASK_WEIGHTS. metric_kind set to regression_error as the closest
    fit (it is a composite inclusion/width score over a forecast band, not classification accuracy or
    pnl).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: CoinMetrics Reference Rate (ReferenceRateUSD) fetched at 1-second frequency via the coinmetrics-api-client
    for the predicted hour window [prediction_time, eval_time]. Used both for the point-prediction actual
    price and for the observed price path that the interval is scored against.
  source_identifier: coinmetrics_api
  trust_model: oracle
aggregation:
  method: rank_based
  normalization: sum_to_one
  decay_rate: 0.8
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    notes: ''
  notes: Each of the 6 tasks (point + interval for each of 3 assets) is scored and ranked independently.
    Per task, ranks are converted to weights by get_average_weights_for_ties with base_weights = decay
    ** arange(n), decay = 0.8 (a hardcoded local in calc_rewards, NOT the EMA alpha) — i.e. a geometric
    rank-decay where rank 0 gets weight 1, rank 1 gets 0.8, etc., with ties averaged. The 6 per-task weight
    vectors are combined into final_rewards by a weighted sum using constants.TASK_WEIGHTS (each 0.166,
    nominally summing to ~1.0 across the 6 tasks). final_rewards then feed the per-UID moving average
    (EMA) before weight-setting. normalization set to sum_to_one because the task weights are documented
    to "sum to 1.0 across all tasks".
weight_setting:
  cadence: continuous
  method: 'Validator runs an async loop. Every weights_rate_limit blocks (when blocks_since_last_update
    >= hyperparameters.weights_rate_limit), it emits the per-UID moving_average_scores as weights. The
    moving average is updated each query cycle (~every 5 minutes per prediction_interval) via the EMA:
    ma = (1 - moving_average_alpha)*ma + moving_average_alpha*reward, with moving_average_alpha default
    0.05. If all weights are zero, a uniform vector ([1]*n) is emitted. Weights are converted via convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit
    and pushed with subtensor.set_weights using version_key = __spec_version__.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: weights_rate_limit hyperparam (blocks_since_last_update >= weights_rate_limit)
anti_gaming:
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Weight-setting is gated by the subnet's weights_rate_limit hyperparameter; the validator
    only calls set_weights once blocks_since_last_update >= weights_rate_limit. This is a chain-side cadence
    limit rather than an explicit anti-gaming control.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Miner UIDs are filtered by check_uid_availability against neuron.vpermit_tao_limit (default
    4096 TAO) when building available_uids; only available UIDs are queried and scored.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_asset
  stages: []
  count: 3
  notes: Three supported assets (tao_bittensor, btc, eth), each with two independent tasks (point + interval),
    for 6 ranked tasks total. Per-asset weights live in constants.TASK_WEIGHTS (each task 0.166). No ladder/tournament/probation
    structure.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: MinerHistory per UID stores recent predictions/intervals; clear_old_predictions drops
    entries older than 24h (run hourly). moving_average_scores is reset to 0 for a UID when its hotkey
    is replaced or on --reset_state. State persisted to state.pt via pickle.
  notes: Per-UID MinerHistory holds submitted predictions and intervals keyed by timestamp (used at eval
    time one hour later). Per-UID moving_average_scores is the EMA-smoothed cumulative reward that is
    emitted as weights.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/Release Notes.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8[1m]

      Please see the [Incentive mechanism Documentation] ... For more information, feel free to investigate

    source_path: precog/constants/__init__.py
    line_ref: 7-9

  unresolved:
  - 'Exact weights_rate_limit value: read from chain hyperparameters at runtime (subtensor.get_subnet_hyperparameters),
    not a literal in the repo, so tempo cannot be pinned to a block count.'
  - 'burn_allocation: no burn/null-UID routing found in the code; left disabled rather than asserted absent.'
  - EVALUATION_WINDOW_HOURS=6 constant is defined but its role is not exercised in calc_rewards (which
    uses PREDICTION_FUTURE_HOURS=1 for the prediction window); its effect on scoring is unresolved.
  - 'min_weight_floor: no explicit floor found; if-all-zero fallback emits uniform [1]*n weights but that
    is not a per-miner floor.'
  extractor_notes: 'Two distinct decay constants exist and must not be conflated: (1) decay=0.8 in calc_rewards
    is the geometric rank-to-weight base used by get_average_weights_for_ties (recorded as /aggregation/decay_rate);
    (2) moving_average_alpha=0.05 is the EMA alpha applied to per-UID scores before weight-setting (recorded
    in /weight_setting/method). Aggregation is fundamentally rank_based per task, then a fixed-weight
    linear combine across the 6 asset-task vectors, then EMA smoothing — so it is a hybrid the schema
    cannot fully express in a single ''method'' enum.'

docs.title: CoLaborative Distributed INcentivized Training (coldint, SN29)
docs.text:
Subnet coldint__coldint_validator netuid 29 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners commit (on-chain metadata pointing to a Hugging Face repo) a causal language model. Validators download each model, compute per-sample cross-entropy losses on a shared evaluation set drawn from Fineweb-edu-2, and reward models that achieve the lowest loss across samples. Lower loss relative t DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, DeterministicCheck, CodeInspection"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: loss"]
  M1["Metric: win_rate"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: coLaborativeDistributedINcentivizedTrainingColdintSn29

    netuid: 29
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [model_weights, commitment_hash]

    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, status_enum, cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: loss
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: win_rate
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: sum_to_one
            decay_rate: 0.995
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.5
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "WEIGHT_SET_MIN_INTERVAL = 35*60 seconds (min interval)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Validator distributes all miner emissions to evaluated models by win-rate (REWARDS_MAX_FRACTION
  = 1.0; no burn UID in code). Up to that whole fraction may be diverted to hall-of-fame bounties fetched
  from a remote JSON, but the default code path scores live models. No emission burn observed in the source.
subnet:
  name: CoLaborative Distributed INcentivized Training (coldint, SN29)
  owner_repo: coldint__coldint_validator
  netuid: 29
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners commit (on-chain metadata pointing to a Hugging Face repo) a causal language model.
    Validators download each model, compute per-sample cross-entropy losses on a shared evaluation set
    drawn from Fineweb-edu-2, and reward models that achieve the lowest loss across samples. Lower loss
    relative to peers (with an age advantage for older models) yields a higher win rate, which determines
    the model's weight. Additional rewards (bounties) can be earned for code/insight contributions via
    a hall-of-fame list.
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: per_sample_cross_entropy_loss
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'compute_losses_regular() uses torch CrossEntropyLoss(reduction=''sum'') per batch. Per-sample
    losses feed compute_wins(): for each sample the model with the lowest advantage-adjusted loss wins
    one point.'
- name: win_rate
  metric_kind: win_rate
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: win_fractions = wins / n_samples (validation.py compute_wins). Model weights derived as win_rate
    ** WEIGHT_SKEW_FACTOR then normalized.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Fineweb-edu-2 text samples loaded via dataset.SubsetFineWebEdu2Loader; models are scored
    on next-token prediction cross-entropy over these samples. The same sample set is shared across evaluated
    models.
  source_identifier: HuggingFaceFW/fineweb-edu-score-2
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: 'README: "initial competition rewards miners for improving model score on the Fineweb-edu-2 dataset".
    Competition list is fetched from a remote competitions.json on the coldint/sn29 GitHub.'
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: 0.995
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn UID in code. REWARDS_MAX_FRACTION = 1.0 caps the share that can be diverted to hall-of-fame
      bounty recipients (reward_weights), but that is a redistribution to other hotkeys, not an emission
      burn.
  notes: 'Per competition: model_weights = win_rate ** weight_skew_factor (default WEIGHT_SKEW_FACTOR
    = 1.2), normalized to sum 1. update_weights(): new_weights[uid] = (1 - reward_sum) * cparams[''reward'']
    * weight, plus hall-of-fame reward_weights. decay_rate 0.995 = REWARDS_DECAY_FACTOR / advantage_decay_per_epoch
    (both 0.995 per epoch); the win advantage uses advantage_initial 0.004 decayed at 0.995 per 361-block
    epoch.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: update_weights() builds new_weights from competition win-rate weights plus hall-of-fame bounties,
    normalizes to sum 1, then EMA-blends with previous weights. try_set_weights() pushes via btlite.set_weights_retry_btlib,
    gated by WEIGHT_SET_MIN_INTERVAL.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: WEIGHT_SET_MIN_INTERVAL = 35*60 seconds (min interval)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.5
    window: null
    notes: self.weights = weight_alpha * self.weights + (1 - weight_alpha) * new_weights, with constants.weight_alpha
      = 0.5. First non-zero update skips EMA.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: retrieve_metadata() keeps only the oldest commitment when the same model label appears
    for multiple hotkeys (copies discarded by block). get_uid_metadata ignores a UID serving a copy of
    a model first seen by an earlier hotkey/block.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: check_and_compute_losses validates model constraints (validate_model_constraints), rejects
    vocabulary-size mismatch between tokenizer and model, and a fraction of cached losses (SAMPLE_CHECK_FRACTION
    = 0.02, max SAMPLE_CHECK_MAX_N = 10) are recomputed to verify cache consistency. Model hash is verified
    unless IGNORE_MODEL_HASH.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Models with current and absolute win-rate below defaults['discard_winrate'] (0.02) are
    marked discarded and not re-downloaded; advantage_initial (0.004) gives earlier-committed models a
    small loss advantage, discouraging trivial copy-then-resubmit improvements.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Competitions are defined dynamically by a remote competitions.json (constants.COMPETITIONS_URL).
    run_step iterates self.competitions; each has its own reward fraction (cparams['reward']), pool_size
    (DEFAULT_POOL_SIZE 7), pend_size (DEFAULT_PEND_SIZE 3), tokenizer and constraints. Exact set/count
    is not in the repo (fetched at runtime).
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - status_enum
  - cumulative_score
  status_values:
  - pool
  - pending
  - discarded
  reset_policy: discarded_commitments persisted in validator_state.json; a discarded label is removed
    if the UID later appears among top miners. State is reset if the stored version major.minor differs
    from constants.__spec_version__.
  notes: cstate per competition tracks uids_pool, uids_pending (with priority) and uids_weight. eval_state
    caches a loss matrix per model index.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: 1.0.17
  primary_docs:
  - README.md

provenance:

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/direction

    line_ref: 131-135
    quote: 'if win_uid is None or loss < win_loss_adv: win_uid = uid ... wins[win_uid] += 1'
    confidence: high

    source_path: README.md

    quote: '[Fineweb-edu-2 dataset](https://huggingface.co/datasets/HuggingFaceFW/fineweb-edu-score-2)'
    confidence: high

      REWARDS_MAX_FRACTION    = 1.0'

  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/alpha

docs.title: Compelle
docs.text:
Subnet compelle__compelle-validator netuid 82 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Compelle is an adversarial-persuasion subnet. Miners commit a written debate strategy on chain (inline text or a revisioned GitHub gist reference). Each epoch the validator pairs miners in Pro/Con LLM debates over a single shared topic (motion), runs the debaters and a judge panel via Chutes-hosted DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: RateLimit, CommitReveal, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: win_rate"]
  AGG["aggregate: Softmax"]
  SM["smooth: Other"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: compelle

    netuid: 82
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [commitment_hash, content_link]

    @guards {
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, reputation, registration_age }

    Multiplex<tournament> {
        score: Metric {
            kind: win_rate
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: softmax
        }
        groundTruth: LlmJudgment {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: Softmax {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
            temperature: 100
        }
        smooth: Other {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "tempo_blocks=360 (tournament/topic bucket); epoch loop sleeps epoch_seconds=4320s"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: tournament
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Live scoring drives weights. Each epoch the validator runs a Swiss-pairing LLM
  debate tournament across eligible miners, updates Elo, derives softmax weights from Elo and calls set_weights.
  A tiny EPS_BUDGET=0.001 fraction is reserved for placeholder ("epsilon") miners by registration-age;
  the remaining 0.999 is scaled across real miners. No burn UID. Epsilon-only epochs (no real miners)
  skip set_weights entirely to preserve the rate-limit window rather than burning.
subnet:
  name: Compelle
  owner_repo: compelle__compelle-validator
  netuid: 82
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Compelle is an adversarial-persuasion subnet. Miners commit a written debate strategy on chain
    (inline text or a revisioned GitHub gist reference). Each epoch the validator pairs miners in Pro/Con
    LLM debates over a single shared topic (motion), runs the debaters and a judge panel via Chutes-hosted
    LLMs, scores outcomes with Elo, and converts Elo to weights. The miner whose strategy wins more debates
    earns higher Elo and thus higher weight.
  submission_format:
  - commitment_hash
  - content_link
  extensions:
    commitment_detail: The on-chain commitment is either inline strategy text (<=65536 bytes) or a 'gist:<id>/<revision>'
      reference to an immutable GitHub gist revision. Modeled as commitment_hash (on-chain commitment)
      + content_link (gist URL fetched off-chain).
scoring_signals:
- name: debate_win_elo
  metric_kind: win_rate
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: softmax
  notes: Sole scoring signal. Pro/Con debate outcomes (win/loss/draw) update an Elo rating per hotkey
    via standard Elo (k_factor=32, initial=1000, expected = 1/(1+10**((rb-ra)/400))). Per-epoch weights
    derived from Elo via softmax with temperature=100. Win counts also drive Swiss pairing.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: Debate winners are decided either by concession (a debater starts a message with the Greek
    delta symbol and >=50 chars) or by a judge. The judge is a majority vote across a configured panel
    of LLM models (judge_panel); when the panel can't reach a clear majority it falls back to a single
    judge with primary+fallback model chain.
  source_identifier: 'Chutes LLM API (judge_panel: Qwen3-235B, DeepSeek-TNG-R1T2, MiniMax-M2.5)'
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: 'Non-deterministic: debater and judge calls use non-zero temperature (debate temp=0.6, judge
    temp=0.3 + 0.2*attempt). Validators converge over many epochs via Yuma consensus rather than per-epoch
    agreement.'
aggregation:
  method: softmax
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: 100.0
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn UID. EPS_BUDGET=0.001 of weight is reserved for placeholder ("epsilon") miners distributed
      by registration age; real miners share the residual (1 - EPS_BUDGET). This is a placeholder allocation,
      not an emission burn.
  notes: 'Elo.weights(temperature=100) softmaxes ratings: e[k]=exp((v-max_r)/temp), weight=e[k]/sum(e).
    assign_weights() then scales real-miner softmax weights by (1 - EPS_BUDGET) when any placeholder miners
    exist, and distributes EPS_BUDGET across placeholders proportional to age (floored at EPS_AGE_FLOOR=50).'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: 'Once per epoch loop iteration: build Elo softmax weights for real miners, add age-weighted
    epsilon allocation for placeholders, then call set_weights. Pre-checks chain WeightsRateLimit (LastUpdate
    + rate_limit) and skips if within the window. Re-queries WeightsVersionKey per attempt; up to 3 attempts
    with 5s backoff. bittensor 10.x handles commit-reveal automatically (wait_for_revealed_execution=False).'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: tempo_blocks=360 (tournament/topic bucket); epoch loop sleeps epoch_seconds=4320s
  smoothing:
    kind: other
    kind_other: persistent_elo
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: 'No EMA/SMA on the final weight vector. Smoothing is implicit via persistent cumulative Elo
      carried across epochs (elo_state.json): ratings accumulate across tournaments rather than being
      recomputed fresh each epoch, so weights move gradually. Tournament idempotency: one tournament per
      tempo (last_completed_tempo.txt); within the same tempo the cached Elo is reused.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Chain WeightsRateLimit pre-checked before set_weights; calls within the window are skipped.
    Tournament is run at most once per tempo (current_tempo <= last_tempo => skip).
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Strategies referenced via gist must pin an immutable revision: regex ^gist:([0-9a-f]{20,40})/([0-9a-f]{40})$.
    Mutable ''gist:<id>'' refs are rejected so a miner cannot swap their committed strategy after the
    fact. Multi-file gists rejected (non-deterministic file selection).'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Eligibility window: a commitment is only eligible if 0 <= commitment_block - registration_block
    <= ELIGIBILITY_WINDOW_BLOCKS (200). ''vdata:''-prefixed (validator-data) commitments are excluded
    from real scoring. Stale Elo for deregistered or structurally-malformed-gist hotkeys is pruned each
    tournament.'
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Judge and debater system prompts include prompt-injection hardening (treat strategy/transcript
    as opaque data, ignore embedded override attempts) and an anti-fabrication rule that penalizes invented
    statistics, citations, quotes, and events.
sub_competitions:
  structure: tournament
  stages:
  - name: Swiss tournament round
    role: group
    advancement_rule: 'Swiss pairing: round 1 seeds top-half-by-Elo vs bottom-half; rounds 2+ pair within
      score groups, preferring unplayed opponents, with per-player Pro/Con color balancing and content-addressed
      coin-flip tiebreaks. swiss_rounds=4 default.'
  count: null
  notes: Single per-epoch Swiss tournament over one shared topic. No multi-track / ladder structure; one
    mechanism. Topic chosen by tempo_index % len(topics).
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - reputation
  - registration_age
  status_values:
  - real
  - epsilon
  - ineligible
  reset_policy: Elo persisted in data/elo_state.json across epochs/restarts. Entries pruned when a hotkey
    deregisters (absent from metagraph) or its current gist commitment is structurally malformed; a repaired
    commitment restarts at initial_rating=1000. Registration age used to apportion the epsilon placeholder
    budget (floored at EPS_AGE_FLOOR=50).
  notes: 'tier classification: real (eligible, non-epsilon, non-vdata) / epsilon (placeholder commitment
    text ''epsilon'') / ineligible.'
documentation:

  completeness: high
