docs.title: SubVortex
docs.text:
Subnet eclipsevortex__SubVortex netuid 7 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners each run a Bittensor subtensor node. The validator challenges each miner by connecting to its subtensor and fetching the current block, scoring the miner on availability (verified + unique IP), latency (normalized request time with a distance-based tolerance), reliability (Wilson-normalized s DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: HardwareProbe"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, LivenessCheck, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: uptime"]
  M1["Metric: latency"]
  M2["Metric: win_rate"]
  M3["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: subVortex

    netuid: 7
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: compute_service

    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, performance_ledger }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: win_rate
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                raw: "geographic_distribution"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: HardwareProbe {
            trust_model: adversarial
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: other
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "weights_rate_limit (subnet hyperparameter)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: 'Weights are set directly from the per-miner EMA moving_scores array (main.py:
  weights = self.moving_scores); no burn / UID-routing is present in the weight-setting path. The documented
  4-criteria scoring drives weights.'
subnet:
  name: SubVortex
  owner_repo: eclipsevortex__SubVortex
  netuid: 7
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners each run a Bittensor subtensor node. The validator challenges each miner by connecting
    to its subtensor and fetching the current block, scoring the miner on availability (verified + unique
    IP), latency (normalized request time with a distance-based tolerance), reliability (Wilson-normalized
    success/attempt ratio), and global distribution (geographic spread). These four scores combine into
    a weighted average final score that feeds an EMA moving score used to set on-chain weights.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: availability
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 8.0
  normalization: none
  notes: 1.0 if miner is verified and has no IP conflict, else 0.0 (AVAILABILITY_FAILURE_REWARD). In compute_final_score
    the weight is 3 instead of 8 when the miner is verified but its subtensor is not synced.
- name: latency
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 7.0
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Process time is scaled by a distance-based tolerance (1 - distance/MAX_DISTANCE), converted to
    a relative latency score (1 - process_time/baseline_latency), then min-max normalized across verified
    miners so best=1, worst=0. 0.0 if not verified or IP conflict.
- name: reliability
  metric_kind: win_rate
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 3.0
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: wilson_score_interval (lower/upper bound midpoint, z=0.6744897501960817)
  notes: Ratio of challenge_successes/challenge_attempts normalized via Wilson score interval; returns
    0.5 when total attempts is 0.
- name: distribution
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: geographic_distribution
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 2.0
  normalization: none
  notes: 1 / (# of verified, non-conflicting miners in that miner's country), else 0.0 if not verified
    or IP conflict.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: Validator establishes a direct connection to the miner's subtensor node and retrieves the
    current block; success, response time, and the miner's reported country/IP drive all four scores.
  source_identifier: miner subtensor node (block fetch)
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: ''
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: divide by sum of the four signal weights (availability+latency+reliability+distribution)
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn or UID-routing in the weight-setting path; weights = moving_scores.
  notes: final score = (avail_w*avail + lat_w*lat + rel_w*rel + dist_w*dist) / (avail_w + lat_w + rel_w
    + dist_w). Suspicious miners' final score is multiplied by a penalty_factor (0 if none provided).
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: should_set_weights gates on min stake and the subnet weights_rate_limit (next_block = validator_last_update
    + weights_rate_limit + 1). Weights are the EMA moving_scores processed through process_weights_for_netuid,
    then set via subtensor.set_weights with retries.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: weights_rate_limit (subnet hyperparameter)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: moving_scores[uid] = alpha*score + (1-alpha)*moving_scores[uid] for non-suspicious miners;
      suspicious miners' moving score is multiplied by penalty_factor. Code comment says alpha=0.1 replaces
      ~10% of the previous weight.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Multiple miners sharing the same IP all receive 0 for availability, latency, and distribution
    (1 miner = 1 IP enforced via ip_occurrences).
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: A miner must be verified (miner and subtensor operational and accessible) or it scores
    0 on availability/latency/distribution.
- kind: other
  kind_other: suspicious_uid_list_penalty
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Miners present in the suspicious UID list (matched by uid + hotkey) have their final and
    moving scores multiplied by a penalty_factor in ]0,1[, or set to 0 when no penalty factor is provided.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - performance_ledger
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Reliability challenge statistics (successes/attempts) are reset every 3 epochs (RELIABILLITY_RESET
    = 1080 in constants.py). Moving scores persist across challenges in saved state.
  notes: Persistent per-uid moving_scores (EMA) saved to disk via save_state/load_state, plus per-miner
    challenge_successes/challenge_attempts counters used by the Wilson reliability score.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - docs/features/incentive_mechanism.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8
  extracted_at: '2026-06-06'
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: subvortex/validator/neuron/src/settings.py
    line_ref: '18'
    quote: 'netuid: int = 7'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: docs/features/incentive_mechanism.md
    line_ref: 1-6
    quote: 'SubVortex''s incentive mechanism will score miners based on multiple criteria of their subtensor
      node: - Availability ... - Latency ... - Reliability ... - Global distribution'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: subvortex/core/constants.py
    line_ref: '24'
    quote: AVAILABILITY_WEIGHT = 8
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: subvortex/validator/neuron/src/score.py
    line_ref: 60-71
    quote: score = (1.0 if can_compute_availability_score(miner, has_ip_conflicts) else AVAILABILITY_FAILURE_REWARD)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/weight
    source_path: subvortex/core/constants.py
    line_ref: '25'
    quote: LATENCY_WEIGHT = 7
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/normalization
    source_path: subvortex/validator/neuron/src/score.py
    line_ref: 160-201
    quote: scaled_distance = distance / MAX_DISTANCE ... relative_latency_score = 1 - (process_time /
      baseline_latency) ... score = (score - min_score) / (max_score - min_score)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/weight
    source_path: subvortex/core/constants.py
    line_ref: '26'
    quote: RELIABILLITY_WEIGHT = 3
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/normalization_other
    source_path: subvortex/validator/neuron/src/bonding.py
    line_ref: 23-46
    quote: 'def wilson_score_interval(successes, total): ... z = 0.6744897501960817 ... wilson_score =
      (max(0, lower_bound) + min(upper_bound, 1)) / 2'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/3/weight
    source_path: subvortex/core/constants.py
    line_ref: '27'
    quote: DISTRIBUTION_WEIGHT = 2
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/3/notes
    source_path: subvortex/validator/neuron/src/score.py
    line_ref: '240'
    quote: score = 1 / count if count > 0 else 0
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: subvortex/validator/neuron/src/score.py
    line_ref: 254-265
    quote: numerator = ((availability_weight * miner.availability_score) + (LATENCY_WEIGHT * miner.latency_score)
      + (RELIABILLITY_WEIGHT * miner.reliability_score) + (DISTRIBUTION_WEIGHT * miner.distribution_score))
      ... score = numerator / denominator if denominator != 0 else 0
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: subvortex/validator/neuron/src/score.py
    line_ref: 250-252
    quote: availability_weight = (3 if miner.verified and not miner.sync else AVAILABILITY_WEIGHT)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/alpha
    source_path: subvortex/validator/neuron/src/challenge.py
    line_ref: 342-404
    quote: 'alpha: float = 0.1 ... self.moving_scores[uid] = (alpha * miner.score + (1 - alpha) * self.moving_scores[uid]
      if not miner.suspicious else self.moving_scores[uid] * miner.penalty_factor)'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: subvortex/validator/neuron/src/weights.py
    line_ref: 68-72
    quote: next_block = validator_last_update + (weights_rate_limit) + 1 ; return next_block <= block
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: subvortex/validator/neuron/src/weights.py
    line_ref: 104-113
    quote: success, message = subtensor.set_weights(wallet=wallet, netuid=settings.netuid, uids=uids_proceed.tolist(),
      weights=weights_proceed.tolist(), ...)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: subvortex/validator/neuron/src/main.py
    line_ref: 347-359
    quote: 'if must_set_weight: weights = self.moving_scores ... set_weights(... weights=weights, ...)'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: subvortex/validator/neuron/src/score.py
    line_ref: 267-272
    quote: 'if miner.suspicious: penalty_factor = miner.penalty_factor or 0 ... score = penalty_factor
      * score'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: subvortex/validator/neuron/src/challenge.py
    line_ref: 372-376
    quote: has_ip_conflicts = miner_ip_occurences > 1
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: subvortex/core/constants.py
    line_ref: '30'
    quote: 'RELIABILLITY_RESET = 1080 # Reset challenge statistics every 3 epochs'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/notes
    source_path: subvortex/validator/neuron/src/challenge.py
    line_ref: '419'
    quote: save_state(path=self.config.neuron.full_path, moving_scores=self.moving_scores)
    confidence: high
  unresolved:
  - weights_rate_limit is a runtime subnet hyperparameter, not a literal in the repo; exact tempo unknown.
  - min_stake threshold for setting weights is passed in at runtime; no literal value found in source.
  extractor_notes: Active 4-signal weighted-average mechanism with EMA smoothing (alpha=0.1) over per-uid
    moving_scores, set directly on chain. No burn path observed.
composition:
  shape: pipeline
  overlays:
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 1

docs.title: Allways
docs.text:
Subnet entrius__allways netuid 7 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners are cross-chain swap market-makers. They post exchange-rate pairs (currently BTC <-> TAO) and collateral, then fulfill swap orders natively on each chain. Per direction, the miner posting the best rate for the swapper (highest TAO/BTC in the canonical btc->tao direction, lowest in the reverse DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData, CommitReveal"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Collateral, LivenessCheck, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: volume"]
  M1["Metric: win_rate"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: allways

    netuid: 7
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [signals, commitment_hash]

    @burn {
        uid: "53"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        Collateral {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, status_enum }

    Multiplex<multi_asset> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: volume
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: win_rate
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            MarketData {
                trust_model: adversarial
            },
            CommitReveal {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: multiplicative
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "epoch_length blocks (config.neuron.epoch_length)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_asset
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Validators compute per-miner rewards from a crown-time event replay each scoring
  window; any undistributed pool is recycled to RECYCLE_UID (subnet owner UID 53). Recycling is a residual-of-distribution,
  not a fixed burn fraction; during a contract halt the full pool recycles. So this is "active" scoring
  with a dynamic owner-recycle residual rather than a fixed burn.
subnet:
  name: Allways
  owner_repo: entrius__allways
  netuid: 7
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners are cross-chain swap market-makers. They post exchange-rate pairs (currently BTC <->
    TAO) and collateral, then fulfill swap orders natively on each chain. Per direction, the miner posting
    the best rate for the swapper (highest TAO/BTC in the canonical btc->tao direction, lowest in the
    reverse) holds the "crown" for that interval and earns reward proportional to how many blocks they
    held the crown over the scoring window, scaled by their all-time swap success rate. Validators independently
    verify fulfillments and vote outcomes through a smart contract that enforces collateral and slashing.
  submission_format:
  - signals
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: crown_time_share
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Per direction-pool, a miner's share = (crown blocks held) / (total crown blocks). Crown is held
    by the miner posting the best rate at each instant, provided they are on the metagraph, active, not
    busy, and rate > 0; ties at the same rate split credit evenly. reward[uid] += pool * share * success_rate**SUCCESS_EXPONENT.
    (scoring.py:120-122)
- name: success_rate
  metric_kind: win_rate
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: All-time completed/(completed+timed_out) over the credibility window (~30 days). Zero-outcome
    miners default to 1.0 (optimistic). Applied multiplicatively as success_rate ** SUCCESS_EXPONENT (exponent
    literal = 3), a gating/penalty factor not an additive weight. (scoring.py:136-144)
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: Miner-committed exchange rate pairs (rate_events) posted on chain and stored by the validator;
    the best rate per direction determines crown holding.
  source_identifier: rate_events (validator state.db); commitments on chain
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Rates are normalized to RATE_SIG_FIGS=5 significant figures at post and parse so scoring buckets
    and consensus hashes agree. (constants.py:28-30)
- kind: commit_reveal
  description: Swap fulfillment outcomes (completed vs timed_out) verified end-to-end by validators against
    both chains and voted through the smart contract; these form the swap_outcomes credibility ledger
    feeding success_rate.
  source_identifier: swap_outcomes (validator state.db); contract votes
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Validators verify FULFILLED swaps and vote confirm_swap; the contract enforces collateral and
    slashing. (forward.py:76-80; README Architecture)
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '53'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: 'Undistributed pool recycles to RECYCLE_UID (53, "Subnet owner UID"): rewards[recycle_uid]
      += max(0.0, 1.0 - distributed). On a contract halt the full pool recycles (build_halted_rewards
      sets rewards[recycle_uid]=1.0). Not a fixed fraction — it is the residual of what crown-time scoring
      distributed. (scoring.py:124-126, 55-64; constants.py:63)'
  notes: Per direction-pool reward = pool * share * success_rate**SUCCESS_EXPONENT, summed across both
    direction pools (DIRECTION_POOLS each 0.04). pool and share combine multiplicatively with the success_rate
    gate; shares sum to the pool, and the residual recycles to the owner UID. (scoring.py:102-126; constants.py:55-60)
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Final self.scores (built from crown-time rewards) are L1-normalized (scores / ||scores||_1),
    then process_weights_for_netuid + emit. If scores are empty/NaN (cold start, RPC failure, all dereg'd)
    the full pool is routed to RECYCLE_UID instead of bittensor's uniform 1/n fallback. set_weights is
    called from neuron.sync() when should_set_weights() is true.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: epoch_length blocks (config.neuron.epoch_length)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 1.0
    window: null
    notes: update_scores does scores = alpha*new + (1-alpha)*old with alpha = config.neuron.moving_average_alpha,
      defaulting to SCORING_EMA_ALPHA = 1.0 (instantaneous, no smoothing across passes). (validator.py:275-276;
      config.py:94-99; constants.py:53). Note scoring itself runs every SCORING_WINDOW_BLOCKS=1200 (~4h)
      inside forward (forward.py:85-86).
anti_gaming:
- kind: collateral
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Miners post collateral; the smart contract manages collateral and slashing. Below-floor
    miners get auto-deactivated on chain (fee/slash paths) or kicked via vote_deactivate; collateral-floor
    invariants are trusted to the contract's active flag. (scoring.py:90-94, 322-325; README Architecture)
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Crown is only credited while a miner is active and not busy per the per-block event replay;
    busy (reserved) miners do not earn a new interval. Failed swaps reduce success_rate, which is cubed
    (exponent 3) into reward. (scoring.py:231-247, 136-144)
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Failed reservations trigger a cooldown (RESERVATION_COOLDOWN_BLOCKS=150, multiplier 2)
    and MAX_RESERVATIONS_PER_ADDRESS=1. (constants.py:66-68)
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_asset
  stages: []
  count: 2
  notes: 'Two direction pools scored independently then summed: (''tao'',''btc'') and (''btc'',''tao''),
    each with pool fraction 0.04. Crown direction sort flips (lower_rate_wins) for the reverse direction.
    (constants.py:55-58; scoring.py:102-122, 253-257)'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - status_enum
  status_values:
  - active
  - busy
  - halted
  reset_policy: rate_events pruned older than SCORING_WINDOW_BLOCKS; swap_outcomes (all-time credibility
    ledger) pruned older than CREDIBILITY_WINDOW_BLOCKS (~30 days) and persisted across restarts. (scoring.py:67-76;
    constants.py:52-54; README)
  notes: swap_outcomes is the all-time credibility ledger driving success_rate; active and busy flags
    are reconstructed per-block from contract events during replay. (scoring.py:99-100, 182-201; README
    Validator Storage Layout)
documentation:
  status: code_authoritative
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: allways/constants.py
    line_ref: '4'
    quote: NETUID_FINNEY = 7
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 1-9
    quote: '# Allways ... Trustless native transactions across independent assets — Bittensor Subnet 7
      (SN7).'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 50-53
    quote: 'Miners: Post exchange rate pairs and collateral, fulfill swap orders; Validators: Monitor
      swaps, verify on-chain transactions, vote on outcomes'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: allways/validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 120-122
    quote: share = blocks / total; sr = success_rate(...); rewards[uid] += pool * share * (sr**SUCCESS_EXPONENT)
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: allways/validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 136-144
    quote: All-time success rate. Zero-outcome miners default to 1.0 (optimistic). ... return completed
      / total
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: allways/validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 1-5
    quote: Reward per miner is ``pool * share * success_rate ** SUCCESS_EXPONENT``; unclaimed pool recycles
      to ``RECYCLE_UID``.
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/dynamic
    source_path: allways/validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 124-126
    quote: recycle_uid = RECYCLE_UID if RECYCLE_UID < n_uids else 0; distributed = float(rewards.sum());

composition:
  shape: multiplex
  overlays:

docs.title: Gittensor
docs.text:
Subnet entrius__gittensor netuid 74 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners register a GitHub fine-grained personal access token (PAT) with validators and contribute merged pull requests (and discovered/solved issues) to whitelisted open-source repositories. Validators authenticate GitHub account ownership via the PAT (or the das-github-mirror service), fetch the min DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: OrganicTraffic"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, DeterministicCheck, ChallengePeriod"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: composite"]
  M1["Metric: composite"]
  M2["Metric: composite"]
  M3["Metric: composite"]
  M4["Metric: composite"]
  M5["Metric: composite"]
  M6["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  G --> M5
  GT --> M5
  G --> M6
  GT --> M6
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  M5 --> AGG
  M6 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: gittensor

    netuid: 74
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: source_code

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: 0.45
    }
    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        ChallengePeriod {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Collateral {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, reputation }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: OrganicTraffic {
            trust_model: adversarial
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: multiplicative
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.7
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "VALIDATOR_STEPS_INTERVAL=120 steps (~2h per scoring round); VALIDATOR_WAIT=60s loop"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: 'Live code blends four hardcoded emission pools (gittensor/validator/forward.py
  blend_emission_pools): OSS contributions OSS_EMISSION_SHARE=0.30, issue discovery ISSUE_DISCOVERY_EMISSION_SHARE=0.10,
  a flat 0.15 to a treasury UID (111), and RECYCLE_EMISSION_SHARE=0.45 routed flat to UID 0 (RECYCLE_UID).
  The recycle UID also absorbs any unclaimed share when a pool is empty, so a large fixed fraction (>=0.45)
  is permanently recycled/burned alongside live PR scoring — hence partial_burn. NOTE: the forward() docstring
  claims 30%/30%/15%/25% but the actual constants used by the code are 0.30/0.10/0.15/0.45; the constants
  are authoritative.'
subnet:
  name: Gittensor
  owner_repo: entrius__gittensor
  netuid: 74
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners register a GitHub fine-grained personal access token (PAT) with validators and contribute
    merged pull requests (and discovered/solved issues) to whitelisted open-source repositories. Validators
    authenticate GitHub account ownership via the PAT (or the das-github-mirror service), fetch the miner's
    PRs over a rolling 35-day window, and score each merged PR by an AST/tree-sitter token-based base
    score scaled by code density, then multiplied by repo weight, linked-issue, label, open-PR-spam, time-decay,
    credibility, and review-quality multipliers. Per-miner scores are summed, normalized to sum to 1,
    and blended into four hardcoded emission pools.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: token_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'AST/tree-sitter token score over a PR''s file diff (structural nodes like functions/classes
    + leaf tokens). Density-scaled SOURCE token score feeds the base score: initial_base_score = MERGED_PR_BASE_SCORE(25)
    * source_density when source token score >= MIN_TOKEN_SCORE_FOR_BASE_SCORE(5), else 0 (mirror/scoring.py
    calculate_base_score_for_pr_files).'
- name: contribution_bonus
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Cross-category bonus added to base score: bonus_percent = min(1.0, total_score / CONTRIBUTION_SCORE_FOR_FULL_BONUS(1500));
    bonus = bonus_percent * MAX_CONTRIBUTION_BONUS(25).'
- name: repo_weight_multiplier
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Per-repository weight from master_repositories.json; DEFAULT_REPO_WEIGHT=0.01 fallback. Multiplicative
    on the PR score (resolve_repo_weight).
- name: time_decay_multiplier
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Sigmoid time decay from merge timestamp (datetime_utils.calculate_time_decay): sigmoid = 1/(1+exp(0.4*(days_since_merge-10))),
    floored at TIME_DECAY_MIN_MULTIPLIER=0.05, with a 12h grace period (TIME_DECAY_GRACE_PERIOD_HOURS).
    Rolling 35-day window (PR_LOOKBACK_DAYS).'
- name: issue_multiplier
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Flat multiplier from best valid linked issue: MAINTAINER_ISSUE_MULTIPLIER=1.66 if a valid issue
    author is OWNER/MEMBER/COLLABORATOR, else STANDARD_ISSUE_MULTIPLIER=1.33, else 1.0 (scoring.py calculate_issue_multiplier).'
- name: review_quality_multiplier
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: max(0.0, 1.0 - REVIEW_PENALTY_RATE(0.15) * changes_requested_count) per maintainer CHANGES_REQUESTED
    review (calculate_review_quality_multiplier).
- name: credibility_multiplier
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: merged_count / (merged_count + max(0, closed_count - mulligan)); also gates eligibility at MIN_CREDIBILITY=0.80
    (credibility.py).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: organic_traffic
  kind_other: ''
  description: Real merged pull requests / closed issues on whitelisted public GitHub repositories, fetched
    via the GitHub GraphQL API (legacy PAT path) or the das-github-mirror service (mirror.gittensor.io).
    Account ownership is proven by the miner's GitHub PAT.
  source_identifier: github_api / mirror.gittensor.io
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: GitHub is the external oracle for merge state, authorship, reviews, and labels; miners are adversarial
    (sybil/self-merge/spam controls applied).
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: sum_to_one
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 0.45
    dynamic: false
    notes: RECYCLE_EMISSION_SHARE=0.45 routed flat to RECYCLE_UID=0 in blend_emission_pools; recycle UID
      additionally absorbs unclaimed shares of any empty pool (OSS 0.30 and/or issue 0.10), so effective
      burn can exceed 0.45. A separate flat 0.15 (ISSUES_TREASURY_EMISSION_SHARE) goes to treasury UID
      111.
  notes: Per-PR earned_score = base_score * product(repo, issue, label, spam, decay, cred, review) multipliers
    (classes.py calculate_final_earned_score), summed per miner (finalize_miner_scores), collateral deducted,
    then normalize_rewards_linear divides each miner's total_score by the sum of all totals (proportional,
    sum to 1).
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  cadence_other: ''
  method: update_scores applies an EMA (alpha * new + (1-alpha) * old) to per-UID scores each forward
    round (every VALIDATOR_STEPS_INTERVAL=120 steps ~ stated 2h); set_weights L1-normalizes self.scores
    and calls subtensor.set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: VALIDATOR_STEPS_INTERVAL=120 steps (~2h per scoring round); VALIDATOR_WAIT=60s loop
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    kind_other: ''
    alpha: 0.7
    window: null
    notes: 'neuron.moving_average_alpha default 0.70 in update_scores (neurons/base/validator.py): self.scores
      = alpha*scattered + (1-alpha)*self.scores.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: detect_and_penalize_miners_sharing_github zeroes total_score (and token/base totals) for
    UIDs sharing the same GitHub account; penalized UIDs are blacklisted and their scores set to 0 (inspections.py).
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Self-merge rejected unless external approval exists; PRs merged to non-acceptable base
    branches or from acceptable source branches are rejected; maintainer-authored PRs excluded (_should_skip_merged_mirror_pr).
- kind: challenge_period
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Eligibility gate (credibility.py check_eligibility): requires MIN_VALID_MERGED_PRS=5 valid
    merged PRs (token_score>=5) and MIN_CREDIBILITY=0.80 credibility before a miner receives any score.'
- kind: collateral
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Open PRs reserve collateral = base*multipliers*OPEN_PR_COLLATERAL_PERCENT(0.20), deducted
    from a miner's earned score (calculate_open_pr_collateral_score).
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Excessive-open-PR spam multiplier is binary 1.0/0.0: 0 if open PRs exceed a dynamic threshold
    (base EXCESSIVE_PR_PENALTY_BASE_THRESHOLD=10 + floor(token_score/300), capped MAX_OPEN_PR_THRESHOLD=30)
    (calculate_pr_spam_penalty_multiplier).'
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  structure_other: ''
  stages:
  - name: OSS contributions (PR scoring)
    role: main
    advancement_rule: 'eligibility gate: >=5 valid merged PRs and credibility >=0.80'
    multipliers: {}
  - name: Issue discovery
    role: other
    advancement_rule: mirror-only; stricter gate MIN_VALID_SOLVED_ISSUES=7, MIN_ISSUE_CREDIBILITY=0.80
    multipliers: {}
  count: 2
  notes: Two scored competitions (OSS contributions 30%, issue discovery 10%) plus two flat allocations
    (treasury UID 111 15%, recycle UID 0 45%) blended in blend_emission_pools.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - reputation
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: EMA-smoothed self.scores persisted to state.npz; blacklisted/penalized UIDs reset to 0.
    PR scoring uses a rolling 35-day lookback window (PR_LOOKBACK_DAYS), so contributions naturally age
    out.
  notes: Credibility (merged/(merged+closed-mulligan)) acts as a reputation-like ratio gating eligibility;
    an evaluation_cache stores last-successful evaluations for GitHub-API-failure fallback.
documentation:
  status: code_authoritative
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: medium

  - Token weights (per-AST-node-type scoring weights) come from a token_config JSON loaded at runtime;
    literal values not present in the read source.
  - Exact GitHub repo whitelist and per-repo weights (master_repositories.json) are dynamic/external;
    DEFAULT_REPO_WEIGHT=0.01 fallback is the only literal.
  - Precise on-chain weight-setting cadence (chain rate limit / tempo) is governed by bittensor process_weights_for_netuid
    and the chain, not a literal in this repo.
  extractor_notes: 'Code-authoritative Python validator. Detailed scoring docs are external (docs.gittensor.io/scoring.html,
    not in repo). Notable discrepancy: forward.py docstrings state 30/30/15/25 emission split but the
    imported constants are 0.30/0.10/0.15/0.45 — constants are what execute, so they are used here.'
composition:
  shape: multiplex
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Bittensor Brain Subnet
docs.text:
Subnet frkrueger__bittensor-brain-subnet netuid None Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status unknown Task Miners verify prediction-market statements: given a statement, a miner returns a True/False determination with a confidence score (0.0-1.0), supporting evidence, an explanation of reasoning, and a methodology description. Optionally, miners also cross-validate other miners' verification results, ret DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, Other"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: composite"]
  M2["Metric: composite"]
  M3["Metric: similarity"]
  M4["Metric: composite"]
  M5["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Sma"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  G --> M5
  GT --> M5
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  M5 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: bittensorBrainSubnet

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: api_response

    @guards {
        Other {
            enforcement: other
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            Other {
                trust_model: crowd
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Sma {
            window: 10
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "epoch_length = 100 blocks (config.validator.epoch_length default 100)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: unknown
mechanism_status_notes: Reference implementation. load_statements() and load_ground_truth() are explicit
  "placeholder implementation" stubs with hardcoded examples; there is no real config source, no commit-reveal,
  and no burn logic. No evidence the validator runs on a live subnet, so operative reality cannot be determined
  from the source.
subnet:
  name: Bittensor Brain Subnet
  owner_repo: frkrueger__bittensor-brain-subnet
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners verify prediction-market statements: given a statement, a miner returns a True/False
    determination with a confidence score (0.0-1.0), supporting evidence, an explanation of reasoning,
    and a methodology description. Optionally, miners also cross-validate other miners'' verification
    results, returning is_valid plus an alternative result. Validators score miners on accuracy (vs ground
    truth when available), evidence quality, explanation quality, and methodology soundness.'
  submission_format:
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: accuracy_score
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.6
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Verification reward, ground-truth branch: if response.result.is_true matches ground_truth[''is_true''],
    accuracy_score = response.result.confidence; else accuracy_score = 1.0 - confidence. Weight 0.6 in
    the composite. In the no-ground- truth branch this is replaced by consensus_agreement (majority vote,
    weight 0.4).'
- name: evidence_quality
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.25
  normalization: min_max
  notes: min(1.0, len(response.result.evidence) / 5.0). Weight 0.25 in the ground-truth branch; 0.3 in
    the no-ground-truth branch. A length-based proxy for evidence quality.
- name: explanation_quality
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.15
  normalization: min_max
  notes: min(1.0, len(response.result.explanation) / 500.0). Weight 0.15 (both verification branches).
    Length-based proxy.
- name: consensus_agreement
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.4
  normalization: none
  notes: 'No-ground-truth branch only: 1.0 if response.result.is_true == majority-vote consensus, else
    0.0. Weight 0.4.'
- name: methodology_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.15
  normalization: min_max
  notes: 'No-ground-truth branch only: min(1.0, len(response.result.methodology) / 300.0). Weight 0.15.
    Length-based proxy.'
- name: validation_quality_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.6
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Cross-validation reward (calculate_validation_reward): quality_score is 0.9 for a correct validation
    verdict (is_valid & ver_reward>0.7, or not is_valid & ver_reward<0.3), 0.1 for an incorrect verdict,
    0.5 otherwise. Combined as 0.6*quality + 0.3*explanation + 0.1*alternative. Only used when validator.run_validation
    is set.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Hardcoded ground-truth dictionary mapping statement IDs to {is_true, source}. Explicitly
    a placeholder; "In a real validator, this would load from a database or file." When absent for a statement,
    scoring falls back to miner consensus (majority vote).
  source_identifier: load_ground_truth() placeholder dict
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Three example statements with is_true=True and a news/source URL each. No live data feed implemented.
- kind: other
  kind_other: miner_consensus_majority_vote
  description: 'Fallback when no ground truth is available for a statement: consensus = majority vote
    of miners'' is_true predictions; miners are rewarded for agreeing with consensus.'
  trust_model: crowd
  notes: No-ground-truth branch of calculate_verification_reward.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn logic present in the source.
  notes: Per-signal scores combine as a weighted sum inside each reward function. Across rounds, set_weights()
    averages each miner's last 10 rewards from rewards_history, then normalizes the weight vector to sum
    to one (weights = weights / weights.sum()).
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Average of each miner's last 10 recorded rewards (per-UID rewards_history), then L1-normalize
    to sum to one, scale to uint16 (int(weight*65535)), and submit.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: epoch_length = 100 blocks (config.validator.epoch_length default 100)
  smoothing:
    kind: sma
    alpha: null
    window: 10
    notes: Simple rolling mean over the last 10 reward entries per miner (recent_rewards = self.rewards_history[uid][-10:]).
      Window is in reward-entry count, not blocks. Not an EMA.
anti_gaming:
- kind: other
  kind_other: cross_validation
  enforcement: other
  enforcement_other: scoring_signal
  description: Validators may ask other miners to validate a miner's verification result (is the verification
    correct?), returning is_valid and optionally an alternative result. Documented as "Cross-validation
    to prevent cheating". Gated by the validator.run_validation flag. Not a hard gate/penalty; it produces
    a separate reward signal.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: verification
    role: main
    advancement_rule: ''
  - name: validation
    role: other
    advancement_rule: ''
  count: 2
  notes: 'Two distinct scored tasks: verification (always run) and validation/cross-validation (optional,
    behind validator.run_validation). Rewards from both are appended to the same per-miner rewards_history
    and averaged together at weight-setting time.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: No explicit reset; rewards_history grows unbounded but only the last 10 entries per UID
    are used at weight-setting time.
  notes: self.rewards_history is a dict mapping uid -> list of (block, reward) tuples, appended each step
    for both verification and (optional) validation rewards.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: low

subnet:

  netuid: null

  summary: 'Academia is described as a Bittensor subnet "incubation academy": builders submit ideas, research,

    line_ref: 75-80
    quote: Submit ... Analyze ... Review ... Build ... Validate ... Graduate - mature projects transition
      into dedicated subnets
    confidence: low
  - claim_field: /documentation/status
    source_path: ROADMAP.md
    line_ref: 138-142
    quote: '## 2026 Roadmap ### Q2 2026 - Foundation and launch readiness'
    confidence: high
  unresolved:
  - /scoring_signals — no scoring signals, metrics, or weights are defined anywhere in the source (vision/roadmap
    only).
  - /aggregation/method — no aggregation formula, ranking, or constants are documented.
  - /aggregation/temperature, /aggregation/decay_rate, /aggregation/min_weight_floor — no numeric constants
    exist in the repo.
  - /aggregation/burn_allocation — burn behavior unknown; no validator code or hyperparameters present.
  - /weight_setting/cadence, /weight_setting/on_chain_call, /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval — no weight-setting
    code or cadence is documented.
  - /weight_setting/smoothing — no EMA/smoothing parameters are documented.
  - /subnet/netuid — no netuid is stated; cannot confirm the subnet is live on chain.
  - mechanism_status — set to 'unknown'; cannot determine whether emissions are scored, burned, or dormant,
    as no validator/runtime code exists.
  - /ground_truth_sources/0/trust_model and rubric — the AI-agent evaluation rubric, model, and trust
    assumptions are not specified.
  - /anti_gaming — only planned 2026 deliverables; no implemented enforcement, penalties, or thresholds.
  - /sub_competitions/stages roles and advancement_rules between Mechanism 0 and Mechanism 1 are not specified.
  extractor_notes: 'Repo is docs-only: README.md (32 lines), ROADMAP.md (314 lines), and logo.png. There
    is no validator, neuron, reward, or config code. Everything about the incentive mechanism is a 2026
    plan ("Academia AI Agent Team", "multiple incentive mechanism architecture", "earn emissions based
    on real performance"), so all code-derived fields are left null/empty and listed in unresolved. confidence_overall
    is low.'
composition:
  shape: opaque
  overlays:
  - guards
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Merit
docs.text:
Subnet fx-integral__merit netuid 73 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status unknown Task Miners register a hotkey on Merit (netuid 73) and are rewarded for active participation across OTHER Bittensor subnets. A miner produces no artifact for Merit itself; instead it must (a) be registered and earning incentive on as many other subnets as possible under the same hotkey, and (b) keep a pu DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: Other, HardwareProbe"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Other, Other, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: other"]
  M1["Metric: uptime"]
  AGG["aggregate: RankBased"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: merit

    netuid: 73
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: [compute_service, other]

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: 0.75
    }
    @guards {
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, status_enum }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: cross_subnet_aggregate
                specific: cross_subnet_incentive
                raw: "Mean of the miner hotkey's on-chain incentive value across all other active subnets (netuid 0 and 73 excluded), scaled by 100000 to form the BMPS."
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            Other {
                trust_model: oracle
            },
            HardwareProbe {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: RankBased {
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "360 blocks (TEMPO); weights set when blocks_since_last_update >= TEMPO-2"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
subnet:
  name: Merit
  owner_repo: fx-integral__merit
  netuid: 73
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners register a hotkey on Merit (netuid 73) and are rewarded for active participation across
    OTHER Bittensor subnets. A miner produces no artifact for Merit itself; instead it must (a) be registered
    and earning incentive on as many other subnets as possible under the same hotkey, and (b) keep a public
    IPv4 axon live that answers TOTP-secured liveness pings. The validator reads the on-chain metagraph
    of every other active subnet, looks up the miner's hotkey, averages its incentive across all those
    subnets, and scales it into a Bittensor Miner Participation Score (BMPS). A failed liveness ping zeroes
    the score.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
  - other
  submission_format_other: The scored "submission" is the miner's pre-existing on-chain incentive footprint
    across other subnets (cross-subnet participation), plus a live axon that answers PingSynapse with
    a valid TOTP token. Nothing is submitted to Merit directly.
scoring_signals:
- name: cross_subnet_avg_incentive
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: Mean of the miner hotkey's on-chain incentive value across all other active subnets
    (netuid 0 and 73 excluded), scaled by 100000 to form the BMPS.
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: avg_incentive = sum(incentives across other subnets) / (num other subnets); bmps = avg_incentive
    * 100000. Missing hotkey entries count as incentive 0.0.
- name: liveness_ping
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Binary gate, not an additive signal. A miner that fails the TOTP-secured ping after retries (or
    has an invalid public IPv4 / port 0) has its BMPS set to 0.0.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: other
  kind_other: 'Cross-subnet on-chain reference: the incentive values in the live metagraphs of all OTHER
    active Bittensor subnets, fetched via subtensor.get_all_metagraphs_info().'
  description: For each Merit hotkey, the validator looks up the same hotkey in every other active subnet's
    metagraph and reads its incentive; these are averaged. The "correct answer" is therefore the chain's
    own incentive state on other subnets.
  source_identifier: subtensor.get_all_metagraphs_info()
  trust_model: oracle
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: 'TOTP-secured liveness probe: validator opens a TCP connection to the miner axon and sends
    PingSynapse; the miner replies with a TOTP token seeded from the sha256 of its hotkey, which the validator
    verifies (valid_window=1).'
  source_identifier: PingSynapse / pyotp TOTP
  trust_model: adversarial
aggregation:
  method: rank_based
  normalization: sum_to_one
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 0.75
    notes: UID 0 always receives burner_weight (default 0.75 = 75%). Only miners with score > 0.0 share
      the remaining 25%. burner_weight is a default argument to _calculate_normalized_weights, not a config
      constant.
  notes: Miners with BMPS > 0 are ranked descending; each rank r gets an S-curve reward (-1.038e-7*r^3
    + 6.214e-5*r^2 - 0.0129*r - 0.0118 + 1), clamped at >= 0. Rewards are normalized over their sum and
    scaled to the 25% non-burner share. BMPS itself is used only to rank and gate (>0), not proportionally;
    the polynomial S-curve determines the actual weight per rank.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Once blocks_since_last_update >= TEMPO-2, the validator builds (uid, score) pairs from the saved
    BMPS state, inserts burner UID 0, and calls _calculate_normalized_weights to produce a weight vector
    summing to ~1.0 (75% to UID 0, 25% S-curve-distributed across ranked positive-score miners), then
    submits.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 360 blocks (TEMPO); weights set when blocks_since_last_update >= TEMPO-2
anti_gaming:
- kind: other
  kind_other: cross_subnet_incentive_averaging
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Reward is averaged across ALL other active subnets, so registering on a single subnet to
    maximize one incentive is diluted by the division over total active subnets. Rewards broad multi-subnet
    participation.
- kind: other
  kind_other: totp_liveness_verification
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Miners reply to PingSynapse with a TOTP token seeded from sha256(hotkey), verified with
    pyotp valid_window=1. Invalid/missing token or non-PingSynapse response fails the ping; failure sets
    BMPS to 0.0. Requires accurate clocks (ntpsec recommended).
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Public IPv4 validation: miners whose axon IP is not a global public IPv4 or whose port
    is 0 are skipped and assigned BMPS 0.0 (ipaddress.IPv4Address(...).is_global).'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - status_enum
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: BMPS state (.merit_state.json) is rebuilt each evaluation round and cleared after weights
    are set (_clear_state). A separate health file (.merit_health.json) and per-epoch result JSONs (capped
    at MAX_EPOCH_FILES=48) record ping success and last-ping timestamps over time.
  notes: state holds per-hotkey BMPS; latest_ping_success / latest_ping_times track liveness. valid_miners
    is the set of hotkeys that passed the latest ping round.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - whitepaper.md
  - docs/validator_setup.md

docs.title: NIOME (Privacy-Safe Genomic Intelligence)
docs.text:
Subnet genomesio__subnet-niome netuid 55 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status full_burn Task Validators fetch a synthetic genomic simulation task (paired-end FASTQ reads plus expected variant count) from the NIOME backend and broadcast it to miners. Each miner runs a variant-calling / generative genomics pipeline and returns a VCF file of called variants. Validators score the miner VCF agai DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, StakeWeighting"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: accuracy"]
  M2["Metric: accuracy"]
  M3["Metric: latency"]
  AGG["aggregate: RankBased"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: niomePrivacySafeGenomicIntelligence

    netuid: 55
    lang: python
    status: full_burn
    submission: content_link

    @burn {
        uid: "5DJ5fT174AY8GzbYHnamYQCJd4cTcj2Zf7ogUvBhry1KfYVd"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: RankBased {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: other
            tempo: "INTERVAL_BLOCKS = 2400 (FETCHING_BLOCK=0, VALIDATION_BLOCK=750, WEIGHT_SET_BLOCK=900)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: full_burn
mechanism_status_notes: BURNING_RATE is hard-coded to 1.0. In set_weights, miner weights are scaled by
  (1 - BURNING_RATE) = 0 and the owner UID receives the full BURNING_RATE = 1.0. The forward() loop additionally
  branches to a burn-only path (set_weights([], "") then subtensor.set_weights) whenever BURNING_RATE
  == 1.0, so the genomic VCF scoring pipeline does not currently influence on-chain emissions.
subnet:
  name: NIOME (Privacy-Safe Genomic Intelligence)
  owner_repo: genomesio__subnet-niome
  netuid: 55
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Validators fetch a synthetic genomic simulation task (paired-end FASTQ reads plus expected
    variant count) from the NIOME backend and broadcast it to miners. Each miner runs a variant-calling
    / generative genomics pipeline and returns a VCF file of called variants. Validators score the miner
    VCF against a held-out ground-truth VCF using depth-weighted precision, recall and F1.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  submission_format_other: ''
scoring_signals:
- name: weighted_precision
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Depth-weighted precision tp_w / (tp_w + fp_w + 1e-9); variants weighted by a difficulty heuristic
    (variant_weight: 0.3/0.6/1.0 by read depth). Contributes 0.3 to the final score.'
- name: weighted_recall
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: none
  notes: Depth-weighted recall tp_w / (tp_w + fn_w + 1e-9). Contributes 0.3 to final score.
- name: weighted_f1
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.4
  normalization: none
  notes: Depth-weighted F1 = 2*p*r/(p+r+1e-9). Contributes 0.4 to final score (final_score = 0.4*f1 +
    0.3*p + 0.3*r).
- name: response_time
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: response_time is recorded and passed to final_score(), but the time-penalty term (score2) is
    commented out, so response time currently does NOT affect the score.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Held-out truth VCF and reference FASTA fetched from the NIOME backend per task, normalized
    via bcftools and compared as exact variant sets.
  source_identifier: https://niome-api.genomes.io/api/tasks/ground_truth
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Ground truth (truth_vcf, ref) is downloaded from the centralized genomes.io backend; depth map
    derived from a BAM built by aligning task reads with bwa/samtools.
aggregation:
  method: rank_based
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: 5DJ5fT174AY8GzbYHnamYQCJd4cTcj2Zf7ogUvBhry1KfYVd
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: final_weights = full_miner_weights * (1 - BURNING_RATE); owner gets += BURNING_RATE. With BURNING_RATE
      = 1.0 the owner receives 100% of emissions.
  notes: 'SCORING_SYSTEM = "top": process_scores_top keeps only the top TOP_MINER_COUNT (10) positive-scoring
    miners and assigns weights from SCORE_DISTRIBUTION [0.6, 0.2, 0.05, 0.04, 0.03, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02,
    0.01, 0.01] (renormalized), ranked by descending final_score. A "linear" proportional alternative
    exists but is not selected.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: other
  cadence_other: block-phased within a fixed INTERVAL_BLOCKS cycle
  method: 'Phased loop keyed off chain block: fetch miners'' VCFs at FETCHING_BLOCK offset, run validation/scoring
    at VALIDATION_BLOCK offset, within each INTERVAL_BLOCKS cycle since BASE_BLOCK_NUMBER. set_weights
    builds a normalized weight vector then applies burn.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: INTERVAL_BLOCKS = 2400 (FETCHING_BLOCK=0, VALIDATION_BLOCK=750, WEIGHT_SET_BLOCK=900)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/moving average of the final reward; scores are recomputed fresh each cycle and converted
      directly to weights.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Miner responses whose non-header variant line count != task.expected_variant_count are
    skipped (continue) and never scored. Axons with ip == '0.0.0.0' are also skipped.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Validator task/ground-truth requests to the backend are signed with the validator hotkey

sub_competitions:

  stages: []
  count: null

per_miner_state:

  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: Per-cycle scoring only; self.scores array is reset to zero on hotkey replacement in resync_metagraph

docs.title: NeuralAI (3D Asset Generator)
docs.text:
Subnet goneuralai__neural-subnet netuid 46 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status active Task A miner receives a text prompt and generates a 3D mesh object, returning it (plus a preview image) to the validator. The validator renders the mesh from multiple axes and scores it on how well the rendered images and preview match the prompt (semantic similarity), how well the rendered views match t DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, DeterministicCheck, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: similarity"]
  M1["Metric: similarity"]
  M2["Metric: similarity"]
  M3["Metric: similarity"]
  M4["Metric: latency"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: neuralAi3DAssetGenerator

    netuid: 46
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [content_link, api_response]

    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: LlmJudgment {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default 100, ~12s blocks)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: 'Validators score miner 3D outputs via an external validation endpoint and set
  weights on chain. No burn UID or burn fraction is present in the source. Weight emphasis is steep: per-miner
  moving-average scores are raised to the 8th power and gated at >0.4 before normalization, so emission
  concentrates heavily on the top miners.'
subnet:
  name: NeuralAI (3D Asset Generator)
  owner_repo: goneuralai__neural-subnet
  netuid: 46
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: A miner receives a text prompt and generates a 3D mesh object, returning it (plus a preview
    image) to the validator. The validator renders the mesh from multiple axes and scores it on how well
    the rendered images and preview match the prompt (semantic similarity), how well the rendered views
    match the preview (CLIP image similarity), and overall image quality. Generation quality is rewarded;
    generation speed can be optionally weighted in.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: preview_prompt_semantic_similarity
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.25
  normalization: none
  notes: 'S0 = semantic similarity between prompt vector and preview-image description vector (only computed
    if quality Q0 > 0.15). Contributes 0.25 to total_score. Acts as a gate too: if S0 < 0.23 the score
    is 0.'
- name: render_prompt_semantic_similarity
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: none
  notes: S_geo = geometric mean of outlier-filtered semantic similarities between the prompt and the rendered-image
    descriptions. Contributes 0.5 to total_score.
- name: render_preview_clip_similarity
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: none
  notes: R_geo = geometric mean of outlier-filtered CLIP similarities between the preview image and each
    rendered image. Contributes 0.3 to total_score.
- name: image_quality
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: none
  notes: Q_geo = geometric mean of outlier-filtered per-image quality scores of the pre-render ('before')
    images. Contributes 0.1 to total_score.
- name: generation_time
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Process time per miner is normalized (x/max)^2 across miners; final score blends validation score
    with (1 - time_score) using validator.time_rate. Default time_rate is 0, so by default speed has no
    effect.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: External validation endpoint that renders the miner mesh, uses CLIP/text-similarity models
    and an image-vision model, and gates on whether the output looks like a real object. Image-to-text
    descriptions are produced via Anthropic Claude 3.5 Haiku.
  source_identifier: validation/validation_endpoint.py (http endpoint, default http://127.0.0.1:8094)
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: 'Uses CLIP (image), text-similarity, quality, and image-vision (Claude) models. README: ''Claude
    API key is utilized for generating image-to-text outputs through Anthropic Claude 3.5 Haiku service.'''
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: l1
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn UID or burn fraction present in source.
  notes: 'Per-response total_score = S0*0.25 + S_geo*0.5 + R_geo*0.3 + Q_geo*0.1 (note weights sum to
    1.15, not 1). Hard gates zero the score: image not a real object => 0; S0<0.23 => 0; total_score<0.35
    => 0. Across miners, validation scores are normalized by (x/max)^2 (normalize()). final = s*(1-time_rate)
    + time_rate*(1-t) when a time score exists. EMA-updated base_scores are then transformed in set_weights
    as score**8 if score>0.4 else 0, and L1-normalized (raw_weights = scores / ||scores||_1).'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: base_scores (EMA of per-step rewards) are gated and steepened (score**8 if >0.4 else 0), then
    L1-normalized, processed via process_weights_for_netuid, converted to uint16 and emitted with set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default 100, ~12s blocks)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: update_scores applies base_scores[i] = alpha*(0 if reward<0.1 else reward) + (1-alpha)*base_scores[i],
      with alpha = neuron.moving_average_alpha (default 0.1). base_scores below 4e-2 are zeroed.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Real-object gate: an image-vision model checks whether the rendered/preview images look
    like a real 3D object; if not, score returns 0.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Threshold gates: S0 < 0.23 returns 0; total_score < 0.35 returns 0; rewards below 0.1
    are treated as 0 in the EMA update; base_scores below 4e-2 zeroed.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Outlier filtering (IQR-based, threshold 1.1) on the per-image similarity/quality lists
    before taking geometric means, removing anomalous scores.
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Non-serving / vpermit-failing UIDs are given zero scores in update_scores (check_serving_axon
    + vpermit_tao_limit default 85000).
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: vpermit_tao_limit gating on which axons count as serving.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Two synapse paths exist (synthetic vs organic) but they share the same scoring; organic only
    returns the top miner's response and does not separately set weights.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: base_scores persisted to state.npz and reloaded; a UID's score is zeroed when its hotkey
    changes in resync_metagraph; scores below 4e-2 are zeroed each update.
  notes: base_scores is an EMA moving-average vector over metagraph.n, saved/loaded across runs.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: 2.5.8
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - validation/README.md
  - docs/running_on_mainnet.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:

  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: validation/validation_endpoint.py
    line_ref: '92'
    quote: total_score = S0 * 0.25 + S_geo * 0.5 + R_geo * 0.3 + Q_geo * 0.1
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: validation/validation_endpoint.py
    line_ref: 63-66
    quote: 'S0 = self.text_similarity_model.compute_semantic_similarity(prompt_vector, prev_img_vector)[0]

    line_ref: 345-347
    quote: 'if hotkey != self.metagraph.hotkeys[uid]: self.scores[uid] = 0  # hotkey has been replaced'
  - claim_field: /documentation/source_version
    source_path: neuralai/__init__.py
    line_ref: '23'
    quote: __version__ = "2.5.8"
  unresolved:
  - No burn/recycle UID or fraction present in source; mechanism_status set to active by absence of burn.
  - Total scoring weights (0.25+0.5+0.3+0.1=1.15) do not sum to 1; this is the literal code and is not
    a normalized weighted average.
  - compute_semantic_similarity / compute_clip_similarity / compute_quality model internals not read (separate
    model files); metric ranges assumed [0,1] only from gating thresholds.
composition:
  shape: pipeline
  overlays:
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

The validator collects per-source telemetry counters from each worker, computes deltas over a time window, and rewards the volume of successful work (e.g. retur DataflowIMMLIR (YAML)
