schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Validator computes per-platform success-only scores from TEE worker telemetry,
  normalizes per platform, weighted-sums by emission_weight, applies a kurtosis curve, and sets weights
  on-chain via fiber set_node_weights. No burn allocation is present in the code.
subnet:
  name: Subnet 42 (Gopher)
  owner_repo: gopher-lab__subnet-42
  netuid: 42
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners run a TEE (Intel SGX) job worker that scrapes/indexes data across several platforms
    (Twitter/X, TikTok, web, Reddit, LinkedIn, LLM). The validator collects per-source telemetry counters
    from each worker, computes deltas over a time window, and rewards the volume of successful work (e.g.
    returned tweets, transcriptions, videos, pages, items, profiles). Errors are not penalized; only success
    metrics are scored.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: twitter_tweets
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.7
  normalization: min_max
  notes: success_metrics=["tweets"] (raw field twitter_returned_tweets); platform emission_weight 0.7.
    Per-platform raw success summed then min-max normalized across nodes.
- name: twitter_apify_followers
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.05
  normalization: min_max
  notes: success_metrics=["followers"] (twitter_returned_followers); emission_weight 0.05.
- name: tiktok_transcriptions
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.05
  normalization: min_max
  notes: success_metrics=["transcriptions"] (tiktok_transcription_success); emission_weight 0.05.
- name: tiktok_search_videos
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.05
  normalization: min_max
  notes: success_metrics=["videos"] (tiktok_returned_videos); emission_weight 0.05.
- name: web_pages
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.05
  normalization: min_max
  notes: success_metrics=["pages","scraped_pages"]; emission_weight 0.05.
- name: reddit_items
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.05
  normalization: min_max
  notes: success_metrics=["items"] (reddit_returned_items); emission_weight 0.05.
- name: linkedin_profiles
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.05
  normalization: min_max
  notes: success_metrics=["profiles"] (linkedin_returned_profiles); emission_weight 0.05.
- name: llm_processed_items
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.0
  normalization: min_max
  notes: emission_weight=0.0 -- "Not scored currently, but tracked for metrics visibility". success_metrics=["processed_items"].
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: real_engagement
  description: Per-source success/error counters reported by each miner's TEE job worker, fetched over
    the network via TEETelemetryClient. The active indexer source and required worker version are fetched
    from the MASA TEE API (tee-api.masa.ai). Telemetry from unexpected sources/versions is rejected.
  source_identifier: https://github.com/gopher-lab/tee-worker ; MASA_TEE_API (https://tee-api.masa.ai)
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Counters are self-reported by the worker; scoring guards against multi-source amplification and
    enforces the active worker version.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: min_max
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn allocation present in weight calculation.
  notes: 'Per platform: raw success score = sum of success_metrics; min-max normalized across nodes to
    [0,1]; multiplied by platform.emission_weight; summed across platforms (total_weighted_score). The
    combined scores are then passed through apply_kurtosis_custom (sigmoid on z-scored values with tanh
    boost, renormalized to [0,1]) before being used as on-chain weights. Platform emission_weights are
    validated to sum to 1.0.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: set_weights_loop runs every WEIGHTS_LOOP_CADENCE_SECONDS (half a weight-setting period). set_weights
    checks blocks_since_last_update vs min_interval_to_set_weights and waits if the minimum interval has
    not elapsed; then computes deltas and weights and submits up to 3 attempts.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (BLOCKS_PER_WEIGHT_SETTING) * 12s; loop cadence = period/2
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of the final reward in code; deltas computed over the telemetry window
      (TELEMETRY_EXPIRATION_HOURS default 8h) then scored fresh each cycle.
anti_gaming:
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: _validate_source_id enforces the active worker version (fail closed if the record's worker_version
    != active_worker_version) and requires the expected active indexer source to be present; otherwise
    the record is skipped.
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: aggregate_telemetry_stats_without_validation only counts the active indexer source; if
    the active source is unknown it aggregates only when exactly one source is present, to avoid multi-source
    amplification.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Delta calculation clamps per-field deltas to max(0, end-start) and detects worker restarts
    via boot_time changes, splitting into chunks and summing, to prevent counter-reset inflation.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages: []
  count: 8
  notes: Eight platform configs (twitter, twitter-apify, tiktok-transcription, tiktok-search, web, reddit,
    linkedin, llm) each scored independently and combined by emission_weight. llm has emission_weight
    0.0 (tracked, not scored).
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Telemetry stored per hotkey in TelemetryStorage; old entries cleaned after TELEMETRY_EXPIRATION_HOURS
    (default 8h). Scoring uses delta between oldest and latest records within the retained window each
    cycle.
  notes: No persistent score/EMA carried across cycles in code; state is the retained raw telemetry counters
    per hotkey.
documentation:
  status: docs_authoritative
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - https://developers.gopher-ai.com/docs/subnet/intro
  - README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: validator/config.py
    line_ref: '11'

    confidence: high

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: validator/scorer.py
    line_ref: 242-244
    quote: telemetry_result = await telemetry_client.execute_telemetry_sequence(routing_table=self.validator.routing_table)
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight

docs.title: Gradients on Demand (G.O.D)
docs.text:
Subnet gradients-ai__G.O.D netuid 56 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status unknown Task Miners submit open-source training repositories (Docker container + training scripts). Validators execute these on standardized cloud GPU infrastructure (H100/A100) for text (Instruct/DPO/GRPO), image (SDXL/Flux), and environment (RL) tasks. The subnet is 100% tournament-based: miners compete in wee DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: ReferenceModel, DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: HardwareValidation, Other, Collateral"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: loss"]
  M1["Metric: other"]
  M2["Metric: other"]
  M3["Metric: loss"]
  AGG["aggregate: TournamentBracket"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: gradientsOnDemandGOD

    netuid: 56
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: [source_code, compute_service]

    @burn {
        uid: "5GU4Xkd3dCGTU3s8VLcHGc5wsD5M8XyxDca5yDQhYm1mVXFu"
    }
    @guards {
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Collateral {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, cumulative_score, reputation }

    Multiplex<tournament> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: loss
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: rank
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: generative_reward
                specific: grpo_reward
                raw: "grpo_reward"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: rank
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: generative_reward
                specific: rl_environment_reward
                raw: "rl_environment_reward"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: rank
            },
            Metric {
                kind: loss
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: rank
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            ReferenceModel {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: TournamentBracket {
            normalization: sum_to_one
            decay_rate: 0.3
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: continuous
            tempo: "WeightsSetRateLimit hyperparam (loop sleeps until updated >= rate limit)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: tournament
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
subnet:
  name: Gradients on Demand (G.O.D)
  owner_repo: gradients-ai__G.O.D
  netuid: 56
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners submit open-source training repositories (Docker container + training scripts). Validators
    execute these on standardized cloud GPU infrastructure (H100/A100) for text (Instruct/DPO/GRPO), image
    (SDXL/Flux), and environment (RL) tasks. The subnet is 100% tournament-based: miners compete in weekly
    tournaments (group stage -> knockout -> boss round) and are scored on the quality of the model their
    training method produces (test loss for most tasks, GRPO/environment score where higher is better).'
  submission_format:
  - source_code
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: test_loss
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: rank
  notes: Primary ranking signal for Instruct/DPO and image tasks. calculate_miner_ranking_and_scores sorts
    by test_loss (lower is better); top result gets FIRST_PLACE_SCORE=3, bottom 25% get SCORE_PENALTY=-1
    when pool > MIN_IDEAL_NUM_MINERS_IN_POOL (8).
- name: grpo_score
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: grpo_reward
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: rank
  notes: For GRPO tasks the test_loss field carries the GRPO reward and is sorted in reverse (higher is
    better) per is_grpo_task branch.
- name: environment_score
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: rl_environment_reward
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: rank
  notes: Environment (RL) tasks scored higher-is-better; evaluation runs 250 episodes post-training to
    determine final scores.
- name: image_eval_loss
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: rank
  notes: 'Image tasks use a weighted diffusion loss: DIFFUSION_TEXT_GUIDED_EVAL_WEIGHT (0.25) * text_guided_avg
    + 0.75 * no_text_avg.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: reference_model
  description: Validators evaluate the miner-trained model against a held-out test dataset on standardized
    hardware; submissions equal to the original base model_id are marked non-finetuned and scored 0.
  source_identifier: held-out test_data split per task
  trust_model: trusted
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Boss round uses proven historical tasks from the database with at least 2 successful quality
    scores (MIN_SUCCESSFUL_SCORES_FOR_HISTORICAL_TASK=2), comparing tournament miners against best historical
    scores from general miners.
  source_identifier: historical tournament tasks (DB)
  trust_model: trusted
aggregation:
  method: tournament_bracket
  normalization: sum_to_one
  decay_rate: 0.3
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: 5GU4Xkd3dCGTU3s8VLcHGc5wsD5M8XyxDca5yDQhYm1mVXFu
    fraction: null
    notes: Burn weight = 1.0 - text_tournament_weight - image_tournament_weight - environment_tournament_weight;
      all undistributed tournament weight is also routed to the EMISSION_BURN_HOTKEY. Base burn is 0.65
      (1 - 0.15 - 0.10 - 0.10) and rises when champion performance is weak. No fixed fraction; computed
      dynamically.
  notes: 'decay_rate 0.3 is TOURNAMENT_SIMPLE_DECAY_BASE: within-tournament rank weights follow weight[rank]=0.3^(rank-1),
    normalized to sum to 1 via exponential_decline_mapping. Champion tournament-type weight = base (0.15
    text / 0.10 image / 0.10 env) + emission boost, capped at MAX (0.48 text / 0.32 image / 0.20 env).
    Text/image/environment tournaments scored separately.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: continuous
  method: get_node_weights_from_tournament_audit_data builds per-tournament-type weights with performance
    boost ((perf_diff-0.05)*2.0 above 0.05 threshold) minus time-based decay (0.0033/day since EMISSION_TIME_DECAY_START_DATE),
    capped at MAX weights; applies dual weight pools (champions vs non-champions) ranked by exponential
    decay; remainder to burn; 0.0001 participation weight per active participant.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: WeightsSetRateLimit hyperparam (loop sleeps until updated >= rate limit)
anti_gaming:
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Validators execute miners' training in Docker containers on standardized infrastructure
    with --network none isolation, fixed memory (24g) and CPU (8 nano_cpus) limits, and dynamic GPU allocation
    via get_tournament_gpu_requirement (H100 1X/2X/4X/8X by model size).
- kind: other
  kind_other: code_obfuscation_detection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Training repositories are checked for obfuscation during tournament registration; the validator
    runs anti_obfuscation detection (OBFUSCATION_DETECTION_PATH). Obfuscated code is disallowed.
- kind: collateral
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Participation requires sufficient tournament fee balance: text/env 0.20 TAO (200,000,000
    RAO), image 0.15 TAO (150,000,000 RAO) per tournament.'
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Duplicate submission repos are deduplicated before evaluation; repos matching the original
    base model_id are marked non-finetuned and scored 0.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Tournaments require minimums to proceed: MIN_MINERS_FOR_TOURN=8 (text/image), MIN_MINERS_FOR_ENV_TOURN=5
    (environment). Failed training retried up to MAX_TRAINING_ATTEMPTS=2.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: tournament
  count: 3
  stages:
  - name: Text/Image group stage
    role: group
    advancement_rule: top_8_advance
    multipliers:
      DPO_gpu: 3
      GRPO_gpu: 2
  - name: Text/Image knockout stage
    role: knockout
    advancement_rule: single_elimination_top_8 (1 task per pair; Instruct/DPO/GRPO or SDXL/Flux)
    multipliers:
      DPO_gpu: 3
      GRPO_gpu: 2
  - name: Final / boss round
    role: boss
    advancement_rule: challenger must win majority (4+ of 6 tasks) vs defending champion's best historical
      scores; champion retains title otherwise
    multipliers:
      DPO_gpu: 3
      GRPO_gpu: 2
  - name: Environment group rounds (R1-R4)
    role: group
    advancement_rule: R1 top8 -> R2 top2 -> R3 boss+2 -> R4 boss vs 1 contender; contender must win 4/6
      tasks at progressive threshold to dethrone boss
    multipliers:
      env_gpu: 2
  notes: 'Three separate tournaments scored independently: Text (Thursday 14:00 UTC), Image (Thursday
    14:00 UTC), Environment (Monday 14:00 UTC). Text/Image use group -> knockout -> boss; Environment
    uses 4 group rounds (no knockout). GPU multipliers (DPO 3x, GRPO 2x, env 2x) scale model-size-based
    GPU allocation, not score. Progressive championship threshold: base 0.10 (text/image) / 0.05 (env),
    exponential decay 0.8 per win, floor 0.03.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - cumulative_score
  - reputation
  status_values:
  - PENDING
  - ACTIVE
  - COMPLETED
  - SUCCESS
  - FAILURE
  - ELIMINATED
  reset_policy: Tournament participants are eliminated when they lose a round; defending champion carried
    as base contestant into next tournament. Champion reign accrues time-based emission decay (0.0033/day)
    from first_championship_time.
  notes: Per-tournament round/training statuses (TaskStatus, TrainingStatus). Defending champion tracked
    across tournaments via first_championship_time and consecutive_wins for decay and progressive threshold.

docs.title: FakeNews Subnet
docs.text:
Subnet gutenberg-team__fakenews-subnet netuid None Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status active Task A validator fetches a real news article, then uses LLMs to generate paraphrased ("real", label 0) and fabricated ("fake", label 1) variants. Miners receive the articles (and, in version 0, the original) and must return a fake-probability in [0.0, 1.0] for each. Miner predictions are rounded to binar DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: StakeWeighting, DeterministicCheck, LivenessCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: accuracy"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: fakeNewsSubnet

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: signals

    @guards {
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, cumulative_score }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: LlmJudgment {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: single
            count: 1
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Validator computes accuracy-based rewards and sets them directly on chain via
  subtensor.set_weights after L1 normalization of EMA scores. No burn allocation or burn UID is present
  anywhere in the code. Rewards are gated to zero for miners whose coldkey alpha stake is below a daily-increasing
  minimum threshold.
subnet:
  name: FakeNews Subnet
  owner_repo: gutenberg-team__fakenews-subnet
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: A validator fetches a real news article, then uses LLMs to generate paraphrased ("real", label
    0) and fabricated ("fake", label 1) variants. Miners receive the articles (and, in version 0, the
    original) and must return a fake-probability in [0.0, 1.0] for each. Miner predictions are rounded
    to binary and scored against the ground-truth labels; reward is a blend of long- and short-window
    classification accuracy over the miner's stored prediction history.
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: long_term_accuracy
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: none
  notes: accuracy_score over the last _LONG_TERM_WINDOW=300 stored predictions (results), scaled by window_k
    = available/window when history is shorter than the window. Weighted by _LONG_ALPHA=0.5.
- name: short_term_accuracy
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: none
  notes: accuracy_score over the last _SHORT_TERM_WINDOW=20 stored predictions, scaled by window_k. Weighted
    by (1 - _LONG_ALPHA) = 0.5. Predictions are np.round of the probability before comparison to labels;
    entries equal to -1 (missing/invalid) are excluded.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: 'Ground-truth labels are deterministic by construction: the validator itself generates
    the fake (label 1) and paraphrased real (label 0) articles from a real source article using OpenAI
    prompts (weak/strong fake, weak/strong original). The label is the prompt''s LABEL_PROBABILITY, not
    a judgment of the miner output.'
  source_identifier: fakenews/validator/task/fakenews_detection_no_original.py
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Real source articles are fetched from a private news API (NewsAPIClient). Labels are assigned
    at generation time, so the comparison against miner predictions is deterministic.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn logic present. set_weights normalizes scores by their L1 norm and emits them directly;
      if the norm is zero it is replaced by ones (no burn fallback).
  notes: Per-miner reward = sum over tasks of task.REWARD_WEIGHT * (0.5*accuracy_long + 0.5*accuracy_short).
    Only one task is registered (FakenewsDetectionNoOriginal, REWARD_WEIGHT=1, FORWARD_PROBABILTY=1; validator
    asserts both sum to 1.0). Rewards feed an EMA into self.scores, which is then L1-normalized in set_weights.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: set_weights() L1-normalizes the EMA score vector (raw_weights = scores / ||scores||_1), runs
    process_weights_for_netuid, converts to uint16, and calls subtensor.set_weights with version_key=spec_version.
    Triggered when (block - metagraph.last_update[uid]) > neuron.epoch_length and step != 0 and not disable_set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: update_scores applies scores[uids] = alpha*rewards + (1-alpha)*scores[uids] with alpha = neuron.moving_average_alpha
      (default 0.1). Before the EMA, rewards are multiplied by has_enough_stake[uids] (0/1 stake gate).
anti_gaming:
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: resync_metagraph computes a per-coldkey minimum alpha threshold via calculate_minimum_miner_alpha()
    = 150 + 6*days_since(2025-03-20); miners whose coldkey alpha stake is below this have has_enough_stake=0,
    which zeroes their reward before the EMA update. Stake is consumed per-hotkey within a coldkey.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: '_normalize_miner_probs penalizes malformed responses: if the response length does not
    match the labels, or a value is non-numeric/NaN/Inf/out-of-range, the prediction is forced to abs(label-1)
    (i.e. the wrong answer), counting as an incorrect classification.'
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Performance history is keyed by (uid, hotkey); if a UID's hotkey changes the miner's history
    is reset (reset_miner_history), preventing inheritance of a prior miner's accuracy.
sub_competitions:
  structure: single
  stages: []
  count: 1
  notes: README describes a roadmap of 6 escalating tasks, but only one task (FakenewsDetectionNoOriginal)
    is registered in the validator at extraction time.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: PerformanceTracker stores up to STORE_LAST_N_PREDICTIONS_DEFAULT=500 recent predictions/labels
    per UID in a deque. History is reset when a UID is new or its hotkey changes. self.scores EMA is zeroed
    for replaced hotkeys in resync_metagraph.
  notes: Both the rolling prediction/label history (per UID) and the EMA score vector (self.scores, persisted
    to state.npz / *_performance_history.pkl) are maintained across steps.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/VALIDATOR.md
  - docs/MINER.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: fakenews/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 17-25
    quote: 'import math

      import numpy as np

      from fakenews.validator.performance_tracker import PerformanceTracker'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 104-118
    quote: 'Using LLMs, validators create two different versions of the article: 1. A paraphrased version
      ... 2. A fake news article ... 0: Reliable ... 1: Fake news. Miners should score each version of
      the article on a scale from 0 to 1.'
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: fakenews/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 49-50
    quote: 'responses (list[list[float]]): Miner responses in the range [0.0, 1.0].'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: fakenews/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 29-32
    quote: '_LONG_ALPHA: Final[float] = (0.5  # Weight for long term performance, 1 - _LONG_ALPHA is the
      weight for short term performance'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes

docs.title: Harnyx
docs.text:
Subnet harnyx__harnyx netuid 67 Archetype gated Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Harnyx (SN67) is a deep-research subnet. Miners submit Python agent scripts that implement a query entrypoint; given a research-style query (plain text), the script must return a plain-text answer produced under a tight tool budget. Validators execute each miner script inside sandboxed containers DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: ReferenceModel"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, CodeInspection, ChallengePeriod"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  M1["Metric: accuracy"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: harnyx

    netuid: 67
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: source_code

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        ChallengePeriod {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, cumulative_score }

    Gated {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: ReferenceModel {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: gated
            normalization: other
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "weights_rate_limit hyperparam; 30s poll interval"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: single
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Live scoring drives champion selection, but miner emission is hard-capped at 10%
  of total weight (MAX_MINER_EMISSION_FRACTION = 0.10) and scaled by the champion's benchmark score; the
  remainder is routed to owner uid=0, which the README describes as burning that share of miner emission.
  If no eligible benchmark score exists for the newest champion, all miner emission is burned for that
  round. So at most 10% of emission can ever reach miners, and frequently the full share is burned --
  live scoring with a structurally large/dynamic burn.
subnet:
  name: Harnyx
  owner_repo: harnyx__harnyx
  netuid: 67
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Harnyx (SN67) is a deep-research subnet. Miners submit Python agent scripts that implement
    a `query` entrypoint; given a research-style query (plain text), the script must return a plain-text
    answer produced under a tight tool budget. Validators execute each miner script inside sandboxed containers
    against a batch of tasks, where each task is one query plus one stronger reference answer generated
    by a more expensive model. Validators score the miner response against the reference answer and report
    scored runs to a central platform, which aggregates across validators, selects a champion via a dethroning
    cascade, and computes the on-chain weight vector.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: comparison_score
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Pairwise LLM judge compares the miner response against the task's reference answer, run twice
    with the answer order swapped; miner_wins counts how often the miner is preferred, and comparison_score
    = miner_wins / 2.0 (so 0.0, 0.5, or 1.0). total_score = comparison_score (ScoreBreakdown enforces
    total_score == comparison_score). The judge system prompt treats the miner answer as untrusted and
    only credits independently-retrieved `validated_citations`.
- name: benchmark_correctness
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Separate benchmark path: a strict LLM "is_correct" judge over a sampled set of benchmark items
    (BENCHMARK_SAMPLE_SIZE = 20); mean_total_score = correct_item_count / terminal_item_count. This benchmark
    score does NOT contribute additively to champion ranking; it gates and scales how much emission the
    selected champion receives (benchmark_score * 0.10).'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: reference_model
  description: Each task carries a reference answer generated by a more expensive model than the typical
    miner budget allows; the pairwise scoring judge treats reference_answer as canonical ground truth.
  source_identifier: task.reference_answer (platform-generated)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Tasks are mixed across factual recall, explanation, comparison, and synthesis. Scoring and the
    benchmark correctness judge are themselves LLM-based (provider/model configured per validator).
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  method_other: ''
  composition: gated
  composition_other: ''
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: 'Champion weight vector is {champion_uid: 1.0} before emission scaling; final on-chain
    vector is L1-normalized to sum to 1.0 (WeightSubmission requires sum == 1.0 +/- 0.01) after benchmark
    scaling splits weight between champion and owner uid=0.'
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: 'Owner uid=0 receives 1.0 - miner_fraction, where miner_fraction = benchmark_score * 0.10 (MAX_MINER_EMISSION_FRACTION
      = 0.10, OWNER_UID = 0). So owner/burn share is at least 90% and is 100% when benchmark_score is
      0.0 or no eligible benchmark score exists. README: owner uid=0 "burns that share of miner emission".'
  notes: 'Champion selection is a dethroning cascade, not highest-score-wins: starting from the incumbent
    champion, challengers are compared in batch order; a challenger only dethrones the incumbent if its
    aggregate total beats the incumbent by score_margin_required = 0.20, or it is non-regressing and materially
    cheaper (COST_REDUCTION_REQUIRED = 0.20) or faster (TIME_REDUCTION_REQUIRED = 0.20, min 1000.0 ms).
    Per-task scores are aggregated across validators by median, then summed across tasks into an artifact
    total.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: A tempo-aware WeightWorker polls every 30s (DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL) and submits only when the
    chain-owned cadence window is open (weight_submission_cadence.can_submit, gated by weights rate limit
    / blocks since last update). It fetches the platform-computed champion weights via get_champion_weights()
    and submits them; the platform computes the actual weight vector.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: weights_rate_limit hyperparam; 30s poll interval
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of the reward observed. Champion persistence across rounds (incumbent
      carry-over via the dethroning cascade) provides temporal hysteresis instead of numeric smoothing.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Pairwise judge payload bounds and de-duplicates validated citations (_MAX_RENDERED_CITATIONS
    = 200; identical citation payloads dropped) and explicitly does not give citation credit for URLs/source-like
    structures appearing inside the untrusted miner answer_text.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Miners submit Python agent scripts that validators execute inside sandboxed containers
    ("sandbox/ runtime run by validators"), isolating untrusted miner code.
- kind: challenge_period
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Dethroning cascade: an incumbent champion is only replaced when a challenger clears a
    score_margin_required = 0.20 margin (or is non-regressing and materially cheaper/faster), so marginal/gamed
    score deltas inside the tolerance band do not displace the incumbent.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: single
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: A single global competition for one champion artifact per the batch/dethroning cascade; no ladders,
    tournaments, or multi-track structure observed.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - cumulative_score
  status_values:
  - queued
  - running
  - completed
  - partial_success
  - failed
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: 'The incumbent champion (champion_uid / champion_artifact_id) persists across batches and is
    carried into the next selection as the initial incumbent. Benchmark run state enum (BenchmarkRunState:
    queued/running/completed/partial_success/failed) gates emission eligibility (EMISSION_BENCHMARK_STATES
    = {completed, partial_success}). Per-artifact aggregate totals are cumulative across tasks within
    a batch.'
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - validator/README.md
  - packages/commons/README.md
  completeness: high

docs.title: HashiChain
docs.text:
Subnet hashi115__hashichain netuid None Archetype pipeline Language none_docs_only Mechanism status unknown Task Per the README, miners operate as "Solver Nodes" running distributed AI models inside Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) to simulate potential agent interactions in secure sandboxes, and calculate the probability of a successful "match" between private agent intents ("Proof of Entanglement"). No DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  M0["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: full_burn
mechanism_status_notes: 'The only validator implementation (validator.py) sets 100% weight on UID 0 every
  tempo and contains no miner scoring, querying, or reward logic at all. Its own docstring describes it
  as "Simplest possible Bittensor subnet validator. Simply burns everything to UID 0." Chi is a vibe-coding
  subnet template, not a live incentive mechanism: the repo ships a knowledge/ corpus of reference YAML
  for an agent to author a mechanism from, but no scoring is implemented.'
subnet:
  name: Chi
  owner_repo: helionlink__Chi
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Chi is "A Vibe-Codable Bittensor Subnet Template" (README) intended to be cloned into Cursor
    and extended by an AI agent against a knowledge/ corpus. As shipped there is no defined miner task
    and no scoring: the validator queries nothing and rewards nothing. It simply sets 100% of weight on
    UID 0 (burn) once per tempo. Any actual incentive task is left to the user to vibe-code.'
  submission_format:
  - other
  submission_format_other: None defined. The shipped validator performs no miner query or submission collection;
    there is no submission format in the implemented code.
scoring_signals:
- name: none_implemented
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: No scoring signal — validator sets a fixed weight of 1.0 on UID 0 with no miner evaluation
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: No metric is computed. validator.py hardcodes uids = [0] and weights = [1.0]; there is no reward
    function, no miner querying, and no per-miner state.
ground_truth_sources: []
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: other

    combined
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false

      0. The module docstring states it "Simply burns everything to UID 0."
  notes: Not an aggregation of signals — the validator unconditionally assigns full weight to UID 0.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Once per subnet tempo the validator calls subtensor.set_weights with uids=[0], weights=[1.0].
    Tempo is read from subtensor.get_subnet_hyperparameters(netuid).tempo; weights are reset when current_block
    - last_weight_block >= tempo.

  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null

sub_competitions:

per_miner_state:

docs.title: Omron / Subnet 2 (Proof of Inference)
docs.text:
Subnet inference-labs-inc__omron-subnet netuid 2 Archetype gated Language rust Mechanism status active Task Miners receive input data from validators, generate predictions using custom AI models compiled into zero-knowledge circuits, and return the outputs together with a zk-proof. Validators verify the cryptographic proof and score miners on verified-inference throughput (rate of accepted proofs times co DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: Other"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, Other, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: throughput"]
  M1["Metric: latency"]
  M2["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: CommitReveal"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: omronSubnet2ProofOfInference

    netuid: 2
    lang: rust
    status: active
    submission: [api_response, signals]

    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, performance_ledger, reputation, cooldown }

    Gated {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: cryptographic_auth
                specific: zk_proof
                raw: "zk_proof_cryptographic_validity"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: Other {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: gated
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: CommitReveal {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "100 blocks (WEIGHT_RATE_LIMIT_BLOCKS); chain Tempo default 360"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Validators commit on-chain weights derived from miner zk-proof throughput (compute_throughput_weights),
  not from the EMA score map maintained by update_score. The subnet owner UID is hard-boosted to 0.8 of
  total weight when present, with all other miners scaled to share the remaining 0.2. No explicit emission-burn
  allocation was found in the source.
subnet:
  name: Omron / Subnet 2 (Proof of Inference)
  owner_repo: inference-labs-inc__omron-subnet
  netuid: 2
  implementation_lang: rust
task:
  summary: Miners receive input data from validators, generate predictions using custom AI models compiled
    into zero-knowledge circuits, and return the outputs together with a zk-proof. Validators verify the
    cryptographic proof and score miners on verified-inference throughput (rate of accepted proofs times
    concurrent capacity) and proof response time. Higher sustained verified throughput earns higher on-chain
    weight.
  submission_format:
  - api_response
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: verified_throughput
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Per-miner throughput = rate * capacity from the performance tracker snapshot; raised to PERFORMANCE_CURVE_POWER
    (5.0) then L1-normalized across miners. This is the signal that is actually committed on-chain as
    weights. Requires at least PERFORMANCE_MIN_SAMPLES (100) recorded samples or weight is 0.
- name: response_time
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: In update_score, response_time is min-max normalized against max/min_response_time and passed
    through a normalized tangent curve; response_time_metric = 1 - curve(normalized). Feeds the EMA score
    map (decay 0.4 / recovery 0.1) which is persisted but is NOT the on-chain weight source in the throughput
    path.
- name: proof_verification
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: zk_proof_cryptographic_validity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Gating signal: a miner response only counts (verified=true) if its zk-proof passes verification.
    Unverified responses decay the EMA score and accrue strikes; only verified, sampled proofs build up
    performance/throughput.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: other
  kind_other: zk_proof_verification
  description: Validators verify the cryptographic integrity of the miner's returned zero-knowledge proof
    against the expected circuit, confirming the source model and integrity of the prediction rather than
    checking the end result against a labeled dataset.
  source_identifier: crates/sn2-validator/src/validator_loop/verification.rs
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Proof validity is the oracle; verification is sampled per hotkey per tempo (VERIFICATION_SAMPLES_PER_TEMPO
    = 20) plus forced verification for PoW / customer RWR / external-hash / API dslice requests.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: gated
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'No emission-burn allocation found. The closest fixed redistribution is the subnet-owner boost:
      when the owner UID is present its weight is set to 0.8 and every other miner''s weight is multiplied
      by 0.2 before u16 scaling.'
  notes: raw_weight(uid) = (rate*cap)^5 for miners with >=100 samples, else 0; skiplisted and coldstart
    miners get 0. Weights are L1-normalized, then the IP-region cap zeroes excess miners per /16-region
    (max 25% of miners per region, lowest-weight ones dropped) and re-normalizes, then the owner boost
    is applied. Final floats are scaled by u16::MAX into the on-chain weight vector.
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: compute_throughput_weights builds the (uid, weight) vector from the throughput snapshot; weights
    are only recomputed/committed once blocks_since_last_update >= WEIGHT_RATE_LIMIT_BLOCKS (100). The
    vector is timelock-encrypted (bittensor_drand::generate_commit) and submitted as a commit_timelocked_weights
    extrinsic with version_key WEIGHTS_VERSION (11003).
  on_chain_call: commit_reveal
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (WEIGHT_RATE_LIMIT_BLOCKS); chain Tempo default 360
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: 'EMA-style update in update_score: on verified responses score moves toward effective_max at
      RATE_OF_RECOVERY (0.1); on unverified it decays toward 0 at RATE_OF_DECAY (0.4). NOTE: this EMA
      score map is persisted and used by the PoW path, but the throughput weight path that is committed
      on-chain does not apply this EMA. Recorded here as the documented smoothing constant.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'zk-proof verification: a response without a valid proof is treated as unverified, does
    not build throughput, decays the EMA score, and can earn a strike. Verification is sampled per hotkey
    per tempo and forced for PoW/customer/external requests.'
- kind: other
  kind_other: strike_skiplist
  enforcement: penalty
  description: RsvManager records strikes for misbehaving hotkeys; reaching VERIFICATION_STRIKES_REQUIRED
    (3) strikes within VERIFICATION_STRIKES_WINDOW_BLOCKS (7200) adds the hotkey to a skiplist for VERIFICATION_SKIPLIST_TEMPOS
    (5) tempos, during which its weight is forced to 0 in compute_throughput_weights.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: New hotkeys are in a coldstart window of VERIFICATION_COLDSTART_BLOCKS (1800) blocks during
    which they are excluded (weight 0) from the throughput weight vector. Hotkey changes also reset performance
    and decay the score.
- kind: other
  kind_other: ip_region_concentration_cap
  enforcement: penalty
  description: apply_ip_region_cap groups miners by /16 IP region and caps each region to IP_REGION_CAP_FRACTION
    (0.25) of total miners; the lowest-weight miners in an over-saturated region are zeroed and remaining
    weights re-normalized, limiting Sybil/IP concentration.
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Weights are committed as timelock-encrypted (drand) commitments via commit_timelocked_weights,
    revealed after RevealPeriodEpochs, preventing weight-copying within the reveal window.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Single mechanism. Separate request types exist (regular inference, ProofOfWeights, customer Rwr)
    but they feed the same throughput/score state, not distinct competitions.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - performance_ledger
  - reputation
  - cooldown
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Scores are persisted to disk (scores.json) and pruned to active UIDs via sync_uids; zeroed
    for non-queryable UIDs. On hotkey change for a UID, performance is reset and the score is decayed.
    Strikes expire outside the 7200-block window; skiplist entries expire after 5 tempos; coldstart clears
    after 1800 blocks.
  notes: ScoreManager holds a per-UID EMA score map; PerformanceTracker holds a rolling throughput/capacity
    window (PERFORMANCE_WINDOW_SIZE 2000); RsvManager holds strikes/skiplist/coldstart per hotkey.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: crates/sn2-types/src/constants.rs
    line_ref: '13'
    quote: 'pub const DEFAULT_NETUID: u16 = 2;'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '23'
    quote: The miner and validator are native Rust binaries communicating over both HTTP and QUIC
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 37-39
    quote: Generate predictions using custom, verifiable AI models that have been converted into zero-knowledge
      circuits; Return the generated content to the requesting validator for validation and distribution
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: crates/sn2-validator/src/scoring.rs
    line_ref: 141-148
    quote: if count >= PERFORMANCE_MIN_SAMPLES { let throughput = rate * cap as f64; throughput.powf(PERFORMANCE_CURVE_POWER)
      } else { 0.0 }
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: crates/sn2-types/src/constants.rs
    line_ref: 20-21
    quote: 'pub const PERFORMANCE_CURVE_POWER: f64 = 5.0; pub const PERFORMANCE_MIN_SAMPLES: usize = 100;'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: crates/sn2-validator/src/scoring.rs
    line_ref: 53-61
    quote: let raw = (response_time - min_response_time) / (max_response_time - min_response_time); ...
      RESPONSE_TIME_WEIGHT * (1.0 - normalized_tangent_curve(response_time_normalized))
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: crates/sn2-validator/src/scoring.rs
    line_ref: 47-51
    quote: let rate_of_change = if verified { RATE_OF_RECOVERY } else { RATE_OF_DECAY };
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 47-48
    quote: Confirm that miners are acting faithfully, by verifying the authenticity of the miner's returned
      zero-knowledge proof
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/notes
    source_path: crates/sn2-types/src/constants.rs
    line_ref: '23'
    quote: 'pub const VERIFICATION_SAMPLES_PER_TEMPO: u64 = 20;'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization
    source_path: crates/sn2-validator/src/scoring.rs
    line_ref: 150-155
    quote: 'let total: f64 = raw_weights.iter().sum(); if total > 0.0 { for w in &mut raw_weights { *w
      /= total; } }'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: crates/sn2-validator/src/scoring.rs
    line_ref: 159-166
    quote: if let Some(owner) = owner_uid { ... for w in &mut raw_weights { *w *= 0.2; } raw_weights[idx]
      = 0.8; }
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: crates/sn2-validator/src/scoring.rs
    line_ref: 168-171

docs.title: Subnet 2 — Proof of Inference (Inference Labs)
docs.text:
Subnet inference-labs-inc__subnet-2 netuid 2 Archetype multiplex Language rust Mechanism status active Task Miners run custom AI models compiled into zero-knowledge circuits, receive input data from validators, perform verified inference and return the outputs together with a zk-proof of inference. Validators verify the proof and reward miners primarily on verified throughput (proving rate x capacity) and DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CommitReveal, DeterministicCheck, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: throughput"]
  M1["Metric: latency"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  PUB["publish: CommitReveal"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: subnet2ProofOfInferenceInferenceLabs

    netuid: 2
    lang: rust
    status: active
    submission: api_response

    @guards {
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, cumulative_score, cooldown, status_enum }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: gated
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: CommitReveal {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "rate-limited: >= 100 blocks since last update (Tempo default 360)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Validators compute and commit per-miner weights from a throughput-based curve
  (throughput^5, normalized, IP-region capped, with a subnet-owner boost) via timelocked commit-reveal.
  No burn/null-UID allocation is present in the weight-setting path. Note a separate per-UID ScoreManager
  (decay/recovery EMA on verified response time) and PoW-derived scores are maintained in state, but the
  weights actually committed on-chain are derived from compute_throughput_weights, not from that EMA score
  map.
subnet:
  name: Subnet 2 — Proof of Inference (Inference Labs)
  owner_repo: inference-labs-inc__subnet-2
  netuid: 2
  implementation_lang: rust
task:
  summary: Miners run custom AI models compiled into zero-knowledge circuits, receive input data from
    validators, perform verified inference and return the outputs together with a zk-proof of inference.
    Validators verify the proof and reward miners primarily on verified throughput (proving rate x capacity)
    and on response time, not on prediction correctness.
  submission_format:
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: verified_throughput
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Committed weights derive from throughput = rate * capacity raised to PERFORMANCE_CURVE_POWER
    (5.0), then L1-normalized across miners. Only counted once a miner has at least PERFORMANCE_MIN_SAMPLES
    (100) samples; otherwise raw weight is 0.
- name: response_time_metric
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: ScoreManager.update_score normalizes response_time to [0, 0.99] via min/max, passes it through
    a normalized tangent curve, and moves a per-UID score toward effective_max = (1/n) * (1 - tangent(norm)).
    This EMA-like score is maintained in state but is NOT the source of the committed throughput weights;
    recorded as a distinct signal.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Validators verify the miner's returned zero-knowledge proof of inference (cryptographic
    integrity / source-model fingerprint) using the sn2-verify Expander backend; scoring is based on proof
    validity, proving throughput and response time rather than prediction accuracy.
  source_identifier: sn2-verify (Expander backend)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Output consistency between miner-reported outputs and the validator's run is also checked (OutputConsistency::Consistent/Diverged).
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: gated
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'No burn or null-UID allocation found in the weight-setting path. A subnet-owner UID boost
      exists instead: when an owner UID is present, all other miners'' normalized weights are multiplied
      by 0.2 and the owner UID weight is set to 0.8.'
  notes: raw_weight[uid] = (rate*cap)^5 for miners with >=100 samples, else 0; then divided by the sum
    (L1 normalize). Skiplisted and coldstart miners get 0. IP-region cap zeroes the lowest-weight miners
    in any region holding more than floor(n * 0.25) miners, then re-normalizes. Final weights are scaled
    to u16 (w * u16::MAX).
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Throughput weights computed in update_weights, then committed as timelocked (commit-reveal)
    weights. Skipped when blocks_since_last_update < WEIGHT_RATE_LIMIT_BLOCKS (100) or when all weights
    are zero.
  on_chain_call: commit_reveal
  tempo_or_interval: 'rate-limited: >= 100 blocks since last update (Tempo default 360)'
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: The committed throughput weights are not temporally smoothed across rounds; they are recomputed
      from the current throughput snapshot each eligible cycle.
anti_gaming:
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Weights are submitted via commit_timelocked_weights (timelocked drand commit-reveal, COMMIT_REVEAL_VERSION
    4) so they cannot be copied before reveal.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'RSV verification: proof verification failures record a strike; reaching VERIFICATION_STRIKES_REQUIRED
    (3) within VERIFICATION_STRIKES_WINDOW_BLOCKS (7200) skiplists the hotkey for VERIFICATION_SKIPLIST_TEMPOS
    (5) tempos. Skiplisted and coldstart miners receive weight 0.'
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Coldstart: newly seen hotkeys are excluded (weight 0) for VERIFICATION_COLDSTART_BLOCKS
    (1800) blocks before they can earn weight.'
- kind: other
  kind_other: ip_region_concentration_cap
  enforcement: penalty
  enforcement_other: cap_and_redistribute
  description: 'IP-region cap: at most floor(n * IP_REGION_CAP_FRACTION=0.25) miners per /16-style IP
    region keep weight; excess (lowest-weight) miners in a saturated region are zeroed and weights re-normalized.
    Mitigates sybil/co-location concentration.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: 'Multiple parallel scoring paths feed validator state: verified-inference throughput scoring
    (committed), a response-time decay/recovery score, and PoW (proof-of-weights) derived scores via apply_pow_scores.
    Only the throughput path produces the committed on-chain weights.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - cumulative_score
  - cooldown
  - status_enum
  status_values:
  - skiplisted
  - coldstart
  - active
  reset_policy: Per-UID scores persisted to disk; sync_uids drops stale UIDs and inserts new ones at 0.0.
    Skiplist entries expire after VERIFICATION_SKIPLIST_TEMPOS tempos; strike/coldstart records pruned
    by last-seen retention windows.
  notes: ScoreManager holds a HashMap<uid, f64> updated by decay (0.4) / recovery (0.1). RsvManager tracks
    skiplist, strikes (sliding window), coldstart first-seen, last_seen, and per-tempo sample budget.
    PerformanceTracker tracks a windowed throughput snapshot (rate, capacity, count).
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/README.md
  completeness: medium

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes

    quote: 'pub const PERFORMANCE_CURVE_POWER: f64 = 5.0;

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/metric_kind

    quote: let response_time_metric = RESPONSE_TIME_WEIGHT * (1.0 - normalized_tangent_curve(response_time_normalized));
      let calculated_score_fraction = response_time_metric.clamp(0.0, 1.0); let effective_max = maximum_score
      * calculated_score_fraction;
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/normalization
    source_path: crates/sn2-validator/src/scoring.rs
    line_ref: 53-58
    quote: "let raw = (response_time - min_response_time) / (max_response_time - min_response_time);\n\
      \            raw.clamp(0.0, MAXIMUM_RESPONSE_TIME_DECIMAL)"

  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization

      \ &mut raw_weights {\n                *w /= total;"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: crates/sn2-validator/src/scoring.rs
    line_ref: 159-166
    quote: "if let Some(owner) = owner_uid {\n            if let Some(idx) = uids.iter().position(|&u|\
      \ u == owner) {\n                for w in &mut raw_weights {\n                    *w *= 0.2;\n \
      \               }\n                raw_weights[idx] = 0.8;"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: crates/sn2-validator/src/scoring.rs
    line_ref: 137-148

      \                }\n                let (rate, cap, count) = snap.get(&uid).copied().unwrap_or((0.0,\
      \ 1, 0));\n                if count >= PERFORMANCE_MIN_SAMPLES {"
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call

    quote: "pub async fn commit_timelocked_weights(\n        &self,\n        ...\n        let tx = subxt::dynamic::tx(\n\
      \            PALLET,\n            \"commit_timelocked_weights\","
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/cadence

docs.title: Cacheon (SN14)
docs.text:
Subnet latent-to__cacheon netuid 14 Archetype gated Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners build a containerized LLM inference server (V1 arena: Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct), push it to a public registry, and commit the image reference + manifest digest on-chain. Validators pull the image, run it on the same GPU hardware against a pinned vLLM baseline, and score speed improvement (TTFT an DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: ReferenceModel, DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, ChallengePeriod, CommitReveal"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: latency"]
  M2["Metric: throughput"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: cacheonSn14

    netuid: 14
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [commitment_hash, compute_service]

    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        ChallengePeriod {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, cumulative_score, reputation }

    Gated {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            ReferenceModel {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: gated
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: continuous
            tempo: "36s poll interval (CACHEON_POLL_INTERVAL_S)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: single
            count: 1
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: 'Winner-take-all king mechanism is live in code: weights are a one-hot vector
  on the current king''s UID (validator/chain.py build_winner_take_all_weights / set_weights, called from
  validator/loop.py run_once). No burn UID is wired in; before any non-DQ''d evaluation produces a king
  the loop skips set_weights entirely. Mechanism status assessed from source only (no live-chain check).'
subnet:
  name: Cacheon (SN14)
  owner_repo: latent-to__cacheon
  netuid: 14
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners build a containerized LLM inference server (V1 arena: Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct), push it
    to a public registry, and commit the image reference + manifest digest on-chain. Validators pull the
    image, run it on the same GPU hardware against a pinned vLLM baseline, and score speed improvement
    (TTFT and throughput) after a greedy-decoding correctness gate. The single fastest correct server
    becomes "king" and takes all subnet emission until dethroned.'
  submission_format:
  - commitment_hash
  - compute_service
  submission_format_other: ''
scoring_signals:
- name: correctness_gate
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Hard pass/fail gate evaluated FIRST. First-mismatch greedy-token agreement check vs baseline
    with a TP-noise logprob sanity allowance (baseline token in miner top-5 and top-1/baseline logprob
    gap <= 0.5). Fail => score 0.0. Gating, not additive — modeled as weight null.
- name: ttft_improvement
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Relative time-to-first-token improvement vs baseline: max(0, (baseline_ttft - miner_ttft) /
    baseline_ttft), using median TTFT across prompts. Floored at 0.'
- name: throughput_improvement
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Relative throughput (tokens/s) improvement vs baseline: max(0, (miner_tps - baseline_tps) /
    baseline_tps), using median throughput across prompts. Floored at 0.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: reference_model
  description: Pinned vLLM baseline server (default image vllm/vllm-openai:latest) run on the same hardware
    as the miner; baseline TTFT/throughput and greedy baseline tokens are the reference the miner is scored
    against.
  source_identifier: vllm/vllm-openai:latest
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Baseline image/digest set via CACHEON_BASELINE_IMAGE / CACHEON_BASELINE_DIGEST.
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Block-hash-seeded prompt set sampled deterministically from PG19 (Project Gutenberg books)
    paired with instruction templates; same block hash always produces the same prompt set.
  source_identifier: emozilla/pg19
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Output capped at MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS=256 per prompt; deterministic per block hash.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: gated
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn allocation in code. When there is no king (no non-DQ'd eval yet) the loop skips set_weights
      rather than burning.
  notes: Per-miner score = 0.5*max(0,ttft_improvement) + 0.5*max(0,throughput_improvement), gated to 0.0
    on correctness failure or duplicate-of-king DQ. The single highest scorer is "king" and receives a
    one-hot weight (1.0); all others get 0.0. A challenger must STRICTLY EXCEED a decaying defender-advantage
    threshold king_score*(1+epsilon) to dethrone (see anti_gaming).
weight_setting:
  cadence: continuous
  method: One-hot weight vector with all mass on the reigning king's UID (build_winner_take_all_weights),
    pushed each loop tick via subtensor.set_weights with wait_for_inclusion / wait_for_finalization. version_key
    tags the mechanism version so Yuma consensus only trust-weights validators that agree on it.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 36s poll interval (CACHEON_POLL_INTERVAL_S)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of the final weight; weights are set directly to the king.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: elimination
  description: 'duplicate_of_king DQ: an incoming eval whose Docker manifest digest equals the current
    king''s, with a different hotkey and a strictly later commit_block, is rewritten to score 0.0 / disqualified
    (reason ''duplicate_of_king''). Byte-identical copies can never tie or dethrone — earliest-block-wins.'
- kind: challenge_period
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Decaying defender''s advantage: a challenger must strictly exceed king_score*(1+epsilon)
    where epsilon = KING_EPSILON_INITIAL (0.01) * linear decay to 0 over KING_EPSILON_DECAY_BLOCKS (50400
    blocks ~ 7 days). Suppresses noise-driven churn near crowning; after the window any strict improvement
    dethrones.'
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Miners commit (image, digest) on-chain via set_reveal_commitment; validators read revealed
    commitments. Commitments must parse as JSON with a valid Docker image reference and a sha256:<64hex>
    digest, else they are skipped/pre-rejected.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Correctness gate: first-mismatch greedy-token agreement vs the vLLM baseline with a logprob
    sanity allowance (baseline token in miner top-5, top-1/baseline logprob gap <= 0.5); failing the gate
    (or non-greedy decoding) zeroes the score.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: single
  stages: []
  count: 1
  notes: 'Single winner-take-all arena (V1: Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct on 4x H200 or equivalent). Documented
    roadmap implies future arenas but only one is implemented.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - cumulative_score
  - reputation
  status_values:
  - king
  - evaluated
  - disqualified
  - precheck_failed
  reset_policy: Throne is cleared if the king's UID hotkey changes on chain (deregistration / UID recycling).
    Evaluations are keyed by (hotkey, commit_block) so a re-commit triggers re-eval; completed evals are
    append-only.
  notes: ValidatorState persists king (KingRecord), per-(hotkey,commit_block) EvaluationRecord history,
    precheck_failures, and a king-history.jsonl audit log on dethronement.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: V1
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - https://cacheon.ai/docs/evaluation/scoring
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: validator/config.py
    line_ref: '15'
    quote: 'NETUID: int = int(os.environ.get("CACHEON_NETUID", "14"))'
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '3'
    quote: '# Cacheon (SN14)'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 1-9
    quote: '"""Correctness checking and scoring for containerized evaluation.'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 19-21
    quote: Miners submit containerized inference servers. Validators evaluate them against a vLLM baseline
      on the same hardware. The fastest correct server takes all emission.
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '25'
    quote: commit the image reference and image digest on-chain.
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 80-93
    quote: First-mismatch correctness gate for TP-safe greedy decoding. ... If the baseline token is in
      the miner's top-5 with a small gap, the flip is explainable by TP noise -> pass. Otherwise -> fail.
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 210-214
    quote: ttft_imp = max(0.0, (med_bl_ttft - med_mn_ttft) / med_bl_ttft)
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 215-218
    quote: tps_imp = max(0.0, (med_mn_tps - med_bl_tps) / med_bl_tps)
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/weight
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: '220'
    quote: score = 0.5 * ttft_imp + 0.5 * tps_imp
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 34-35
    quote: "if not correctness_pass:\n    score = 0.0"
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: validator/config.py
    line_ref: 51-54
    quote: 'BASELINE_IMAGE: str = os.environ.get("CACHEON_BASELINE_IMAGE", "vllm/vllm-openai:latest")'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/1/source_identifier
    source_path: validator/prompts.py

    quote: 'DATASET_NAME: str = "emozilla/pg19"'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/1/notes

  - claim_field: /aggregation/method

  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: validator/state.py
    line_ref: 11-15
    quote: A miner's score is 0.5 * max(0, ttft_improvement) + 0.5 * max(0, throughput_improvement) ...
      The king is the miner with the highest score. Disqualified runs store score 0.0 and cannot take

  - claim_field: /aggregation/composition
