docs.title: TAOHash
docs.text:
Subnet latent-to__taohash netuid 14 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners contribute Bitcoin (and Bitcoin Cash) proof-of-work mining hashrate to the subnet's unified pool. A validator-side proxy records each miner's submitted shares; validators score a miner by the fiat (USD) value of the BTC/BCH block-reward share those shares represent over each evaluation window DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: HardwareProbe"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: ProofOfWork, Deduplication, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: throughput"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: taoHash

    netuid: 14
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: compute_service

    @burn {
        uid: "burn_uid (SubnetOwnerHotkey UID)"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        ProofOfWork {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, registration_age }

    Multiplex<multi_asset> {
        score: Metric {
            kind: throughput
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: HardwareProbe {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "weights_interval = tempo; eval_interval = 25 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_asset
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: 'Scoring is active: validators rank miners by the USD-denominated value of the
  BTC shares they contributed in each evaluation window. However weight distribution is capped by the
  alpha actually available to distribute, and any residual weight (and the full weight when no miners
  are mining) is assigned to the subnet-owner burn UID. In practice emissions are largely burned unless
  miners contribute enough share value.'
subnet:
  name: TAOHash
  owner_repo: latent-to__taohash
  netuid: 14
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners contribute Bitcoin (and Bitcoin Cash) proof-of-work mining hashrate to the subnet's
    unified pool. A validator-side proxy records each miner's submitted shares; validators score a miner
    by the fiat (USD) value of the BTC/BCH block-reward share those shares represent over each evaluation
    window. Higher total share value over the period yields a higher on-chain weight (and thus more alpha
    emission). Miners also earn direct BTC payouts off-chain via the TIDES protocol, independent of weights.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: share_value_fiat
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Per miner, per coin: share_value_fiat = (share_value / coin_difficulty) * block_reward * coin_price.
    share_value is the difficulty-weighted work the pool recorded for that worker; block_reward is BLOCK_REWARDS[coin]
    (3.125 for btc and bch); coin_price is the CoinGecko USD price. Summed across the window via add_score,
    then multiplied by the coin payout_factor (PAYOUT_FACTOR=0.015) in get_weighted_scores, then summed
    across coins (btc, bch) into final_scores.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: Miner mining contributions (shares / share_value / hashrate) for a time range, retrieved
    from the subnet's mining-pool proxy API (ProxyPool) rather than directly from miners. Coin price from
    CoinGecko; network difficulty from a network-stats lookup.
  source_identifier: ProxyPool.get_miner_contributions_timerange; CoinPriceAPI('coingecko'); get_current_difficulty
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: The proxy pool API is operated by the subnet; validators need proxy credentials from subnet staff.
    Share values are taken as authoritative (already computed by the pool from actual work done).
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: burn_uid (SubnetOwnerHotkey UID)
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: If total miner value is zero, weights[burn_uid]=1.0 (full burn). Otherwise weights are score/total_value,
      but if total_value <= value_to_dist (the alpha available to distribute = blocks_to_set_for * (1
      - OWNER_TAKE=0.18) * SPLIT_WITH_MINERS=0.5 * alpha_price) each weight is scaled down by total_value/value_to_dist
      and the remaining (1 - sum_weights) is added to weights[burn_uid]. Burn fraction is a computed residual,
      not a fixed constant.
  notes: final_scores[uid] = sum over coins of (raw_score[uid] * payout_factor). Weights are proportional
    to final_scores, capped by value_to_dist, normalized so weights sum to 1 (residual to burn UID; if
    sum exceeds 1 weights are renormalized).
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Each weights_interval (== subnet tempo), set_weights consolidates per-coin weighted scores into
    final_scores, computes proportional weights capped by distributable alpha with residual to the burn
    UID, calls subtensor.set_weights, then resets per-coin scores for the next cycle. Evaluation of share
    value polls roughly every 10 minutes (eval_interval = 25 blocks).
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: weights_interval = tempo; eval_interval = 25 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: Scores are reset every cycle (reset_scores after each successful set_weights), so no EMA/SMA
      of the final reward is applied across cycles. A field self.alpha = 0.8 is assigned in the validator
      __init__ but is never referenced anywhere in the weight/score code (dead variable); not treated
      as an active smoothing constant.
anti_gaming:
- kind: proof_of_work
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Reward is grounded in actual Bitcoin/Bitcoin-Cash proof-of-work shares recorded by the
    pool proxy; value scales with real difficulty-weighted work done, so claiming reward requires real
    hashrate.
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: In get_metrics_timerange, when two hotkeys map to the same pool worker_id, only the hotkey
    with the earlier block_at_registration keeps the worker's contributions; the later duplicate is dropped.
- kind: other
  kind_other: coldkey_blocklist
  enforcement: penalty
  description: A hardcoded BAD_COLDKEYS list zeroes a miner's scores across all coins on state restore
    (and replaced/duplicate hotkeys have their scores reset to 0.0).
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_asset
  stages: []
  count: 2
  notes: Two coins are scored in parallel (SUPPORTED_COINS = ['btc', 'bch']); each has its own EvaluationMetrics
    with a per-coin payout_factor, and the per-coin weighted scores are summed into a single final score
    before weighting.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - registration_age
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Per-coin scores accumulate within an evaluation window and are reset to zero after each
    successful set_weights. Scores are also zeroed on hotkey replacement, on duplicate-worker resolution
    (loser dropped), and for BAD_COLDKEYS on state restore.
  notes: block_at_registration is tracked per hotkey and used to resolve duplicate pool workers (earlier
    registration wins).
documentation:

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/name

docs.title: Leadpoet
docs.text:
Subnet leadpoet__leadpoet netuid 71 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners source high-quality sales prospect ("lead") records and submit them via a TEE gateway; multiple validators run automated quality/reputation checks and consensus-approve leads. Miners are rewarded proportional to the sum of the reputation scores (0-48) of their approved leads over a rolling 30 DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: RealEngagement, CommitReveal"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CommitReveal, Deduplication, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: composite"]
  M1["Metric: accuracy"]
  M2["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  M3["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Sma"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: leadpoet

    netuid: 71
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: [content_link, model_weights, commitment_hash]

    @burn {
        uid: "UID 0 (hotkey 5FNVgRnrxMibhcBGEAaajGrYjsaCn441a5HuGUBUNnxEBLo9)"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, reputation, status_enum }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            RealEngagement {
                trust_model: oracle
            },
            CommitReveal {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: gated
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Sma {
            window: 30
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "360 blocks (submission at block 345)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: The live validator weight-setting in neurons/validator.py distributes a fixed
  20% sourcing pool to miners by rolling-30-epoch rep score, a 5% qualification champion share, a 71%
  fulfillment pool, and a 4% fulfillment leaderboard bonus, with the remainder (unused sourcing under
  threshold, deregistered-miner shares, unused fulfillment pool, unfilled leaderboard slots) burned to
  UID 0. With fulfillment disabled (ENABLE_FULFILLMENT defaults to "false") the 71% + 4% fulfillment-flavored
  allocation burns, so a large fraction of emission routes to burn by default. A separate older reward.py
  module (45/45/10 K/S/C split) is marked DEPRECATED in-code and is not the active path.
subnet:
  name: Leadpoet
  owner_repo: leadpoet__leadpoet
  netuid: 71
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners source high-quality sales prospect ("lead") records and submit them via a TEE gateway;
    multiple validators run automated quality/reputation checks and consensus-approve leads. Miners are
    rewarded proportional to the sum of the reputation scores (0-48) of their approved leads over a rolling
    30-epoch window. Two additional sub-mechanisms exist: qualification models (AI models that curate
    leads to ICPs, with a single "champion" earning a share) and fulfillment (commit-reveal competition
    on client ICP requests, winning leads earn 0.1% of emission for 30 epochs plus an all-time top-3 leaderboard
    bonus).'
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  - model_weights
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: lead_reputation_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Per-approved-lead reputation score, max 48 = Wayback(6) + SEC(12) + WHOIS/DNSBL(10) + GDELT(10)
    + Companies House(10). Per-miner sourcing score is the sum of effective_rep_score over approved leads;
    effective_rep_score = base_rep_score + icp_adjustment, floored at 0.
- name: lead_validity_consensus
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Gating signal: only consensus-approved (VALID) leads contribute rep score. Denied leads contribute
    0 (early return in accumulate_miner_weights).'
- name: qualification_model_score
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Qualification models scored against 100 ICPs; a single champion earns the 5% champion share.
    A challenger must beat the champion by CHAMPION_DETHRONING_THRESHOLD_POINTS (default 10 absolute points)
    to dethrone.
- name: fulfillment_intent_score
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Fulfillment leads scored through a 3-tier pipeline (ICP fit, data accuracy, intent scoring with
    LLM relevance + time decay; min threshold 5.0). Top num_leads winners per request each earn 0.1% of
    emission for 30 epochs.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: real_engagement
  description: Automated reputation/validity checks against external public services (Wayback Machine,
    SEC EDGAR, GDELT, WHOIS/DNSBL, UK Companies House) plus email (TrueList) and LinkedIn/company verification
    (ScrapingDog).
  source_identifier: validator_models/automated_checks.py; validator_models/checks_repscore.py
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Multi-validator consensus weighted by stake and validator trust approves leads.
- kind: commit_reveal
  description: Fulfillment requests use a commit (hashed lead data) then reveal flow; hashes must match
    the revealed lead JSON or the submission is rejected.
  source_identifier: Leadpoet/utils/hashing.py; POST /fulfillment/commit, /fulfillment/reveal
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Commit-reveal prevents miners copying each other's leads within an epoch.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: gated
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: UID 0 (hotkey 5FNVgRnrxMibhcBGEAaajGrYjsaCn441a5HuGUBUNnxEBLo9)
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: total_burn_share = BASE_BURN_SHARE(0.0) + unused_sourcing_share + dereg_burn + unused_fulfillment
      + leaderboard_burn. With fulfillment disabled (default), the 71% fulfillment pool and 4% leaderboard
      bonus burn; sourcing share below SOURCING_FLOOR_THRESHOLD (125000 leads/30 epochs) is proportionally
      burned. Burn is assigned to UID 0.
  notes: 'Final per-UID weights: UID0=total_burn_share; champion=0.05 (if active and registered); fulfillment
    miners=per-miner pct from 71% pool; leaderboard top-3 = 2.5%/1.0%/0.5%; sourcing miners split effective_sourcing_to_miners
    by rep_score / registered_rolling_total. Weights must sum to 1.0.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: At block >=345 of each 360-block epoch the primary validator builds per-UID weights from rolling-30-epoch
    rep scores plus champion/fulfillment/leaderboard shares and burn, then calls subtensor.set_weights.
    Epoch length 360 blocks (~72 min). MAX_SOURCING_SHARE = 1 - champion(0.05 if active) - 0.71 - 0.04.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 360 blocks (submission at block 345)
  smoothing:
    kind: sma
    alpha: null
    window: 30
    notes: Rewards weighted by a rolling 30-epoch (ROLLING_WINDOW=30) history of summed rep scores and
      approved lead counts; not an EMA but a fixed-window rolling sum over epochs [current-30, current-1].
anti_gaming:
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Fulfillment uses commit (hashed leads) then reveal; revealed leads must match committed
    hashes or are rejected. Consensus validation uses commit/reveal so validators cannot copy each other's
    decisions.
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Fulfillment leads deduplicated by company before winner selection; sourcing rejects duplicate
    submissions (counted against daily rejection limit).
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Server-side daily limits: 1000 submission attempts/day and 200 rejections/day (reset 12:00
    AM UTC); exceeding the rejection limit blocks further submissions.'
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: elimination
  description: Qualification models scanned on upload and at runtime in a sandbox (ALLOWED_LIBRARIES allowlist);
    blocked patterns (eval/exec/__import__, stripping negative LLM assessments, fabricating dates/evidence,
    bypassing LLM verification) lead to model ban and hotkey blacklist; banned hotkeys' sourcing scores
    set to -100000.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Lead approval consensus is weighted by validator stake and validator trust (v_trust); majority
    agreement required.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Automated checks (email format/deliverability, name-in-email match, domain age >=7 days,
    MX/SPF/DMARC, DNSBL, source URL domain matches claimed source) reject low-quality or fabricated leads
    before scoring.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: sourcing
    role: main
    advancement_rule: rep-score weighted; 20% sourcing pool split by rolling-30-epoch rep score
  - name: qualification
    role: other
    advancement_rule: single champion model earns 5% share; challenger must beat by 10 points to dethrone
  - name: fulfillment
    role: other
    advancement_rule: commit-reveal ICP requests; top num_leads winners earn 0.1%/30 epochs from 71% pool
      + 4% top-3 leaderboard bonus
  count: 3
  notes: Three parallel incentive mechanisms share the emission via the fixed split in the weight-setting
    code. Fulfillment leaderboard awards all-time top-3 winners 2.5%/1.0%/0.5%.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - reputation
  - status_enum
  status_values:
  - approve
  - deny
  - banned
  - champion
  reset_policy: Per-epoch miner_scores tracked in validator_weights file; weight calculation uses a rolling
    30-epoch sum from validator_weights_history (pruned, default keep 50 epochs). Banned hotkeys penalized
    to -100000.
  notes: miner_scores = sum of effective_rep_score per miner per epoch; approved_lead_count tracked per
    epoch for threshold scaling. Champion status persisted in a local qualification champion JSON.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: Subnet 71 (netuid 71)
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: leadpoet_canonical/constants.py
    line_ref: '46'
    quote: DEFAULT_NETUID = 71
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 3614-3620
    quote: "result = self.subtensor.set_weights(\n                netuid=self.config.netuid,\n       \
      \         wallet=self.wallet,\n                uids=uids,\n                weights=normalized_weights,"
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 203-210
    quote: 'Miners earn rewards based on the quality and validity of leads they submit, with rewards weighted
      entirely by a rolling 30-epoch history ... Formula: miner_reward ∝ Σ(rep_score for all approved
      leads from that miner)'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: validator_models/automated_checks.py
    line_ref: '54'
    quote: 'MAX_REP_SCORE = 48  # Wayback (6) + SEC (12) + WHOIS/DNSBL (10) + GDELT (10) + Companies House
      (10) = 48'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 2958-2961
    quote: "# NEW FORMAT: Use addition with floor at 0 (for normal leads)\n                icp_adjustment\
      \ = int(is_icp_multiplier)\n                effective_rep_score = max(0, rep_score + icp_adjustment)"
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 2943-2944
    quote: "if decision != \"approve\":\n                return"
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/3/notes
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 614-616
    quote: 'Tier 3 (Intent Scoring) ... Minimum threshold: 5.0 ... Each winning lead earns 0.1% of emission
      for 30 epochs.'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 3114-3115
    quote: "UID_ZERO = 0  # LeadPoet revenue UID\n            EXPECTED_UID_ZERO_HOTKEY = \"5FNVgRnrxMibhcBGEAaajGrYjsaCn441a5HuGUBUNnxEBLo9\""
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 3476-3482
    quote: "total_burn_share = (\n                BASE_BURN_SHARE\n                + unused_sourcing_share\n\
      \                + dereg_burn\n                + unused_fulfillment\n                + leaderboard_burn\n\
      \            )"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 3556-3558
    quote: "miner_proportion = rep_score / registered_rolling_total\n                    miner_weight\
      \ = effective_sourcing_to_miners * miner_proportion"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/composition
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 3121-3138
    quote: BASE_BURN_SHARE = 0.0 ... CHAMPION_SHARE = 0.05 ... FULFILLMENT_POOL_SHARE = 0.71 ... LEADERBOARD_BONUS_SHARE
      = 0.04 ... Both active → 20% sourcing, 5% champion, 71% fulfillment pool, 4% leaderboard bonus
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: leadpoet_canonical/constants.py
    line_ref: 20-24
    quote: 'EPOCH_LENGTH = 360

      ...

      WEIGHT_SUBMISSION_BLOCK = 345'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 3079-3080
    quote: "if blocks_into_epoch < 345:\n                return False"
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 3288-3293
    quote: "MAX_SOURCING_SHARE = (\n                1.0\n                - (CHAMPION_SHARE if champion_active\
      \ else 0.0)\n                - FULFILLMENT_POOL_SHARE\n                - LEADERBOARD_BONUS_SHARE\n\
      \            )"
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/window
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: '3156'
    quote: ROLLING_WINDOW = 30
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/window
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 3980-3981
    quote: "start_epoch = current_epoch - window\n            end_epoch = current_epoch - 1"
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: '3282'
    quote: ff_enabled = os.environ.get("ENABLE_FULFILLMENT", "false").lower() == "true"
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status_notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 3284-3286
    quote: "when\n            # fulfillment is disabled, the full 4% burns rather than rerouting\n   \
      \         # to sourcing, mirroring how the 71% fulfillment pool itself burns."
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/dynamic
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 3362-3369
    quote: "if rolling_lead_count >= SOURCING_FLOOR_THRESHOLD:\n                effective_sourcing_share\
      \ = MAX_SOURCING_SHARE\n            else:\n                effective_sourcing_share = (rolling_lead_count\
      \ / SOURCING_FLOOR_THRESHOLD) * MAX_SOURCING_SHARE"
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 607-609
    quote: Miners source leads matching the ICP, then submit hashed lead data (commit). This prevents
      other miners from copying your leads. ... Hashes must match or the submission is rejected.
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description

    quote: "BANNED HOTKEY SOURCING PENALTY\n            # Fetch banned hotkeys from Supabase and set their\
      \ scores to -100,000"

    quote: 'Consensus requirement: Majority validator agreement, weighted by stake and v_trust, is required
      for lead approval'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes

    quote: '_DEFAULT_CHAMPION_DETHRONING_THRESHOLD_POINTS = 10  # Must score 10+ absolute points higher
      to dethrone'
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 3132-3135
    quote: "LEADERBOARD_BONUS_SHARE = 0.04   # 4% total: 2.5 + 1.0 + 0.5\n            LEADERBOARD_TOP1_PCT\
      \     = 0.025 # 2.5% to all-time #1\n            LEADERBOARD_TOP2_PCT     = 0.010 # 1.0% to all-time\
      \ #2\n            LEADERBOARD_TOP3_PCT     = 0.005 # 0.5% to all-time #3"

    source_path: neurons/validator.py

  unresolved:
  - Sourcing-floor proportional burn fraction is dynamic (rolling_lead_count/125000); no single fixed
    burn fraction exists.
  - Exact qualification model scoring formula (how the 100-ICP score is computed) not read in detail;
    only the champion share (5%) and dethroning threshold (10 pts) captured.
  - Per-validator emission (calculate_emissions in reward.py, reputation>15 gated) appears to belong to
    the deprecated reward.py path, not the active validator.py weight-setting; not modeled.
composition:

  overlays:

  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Apex (Subnet 1)
docs.text:
Subnet macrocosm-os__apex netuid 1 Archetype opaque Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners compete within Competitions, each made of multiple Rounds, by submitting Python-based algorithms through the Apex CLI. Validators (via an off-chain orchestrator) evaluate each submission against per-competition benchmarks / baseline scores and reward miners whose solutions perform best. Concr DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: RateLimit, StakeWeighting"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: Undocumented"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: apexSubnet1

    netuid: 1
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: source_code

    @burn {
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, status_enum }

    Opaque {
        score: Metric {
            kind: composite
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: other
        }
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: Undocumented {
            composition: other
            normalization: other
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "WEIGHT_SUBMIT_INTERVAL = 60*21 seconds (21 minutes) on mainnet"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Per-miner weights are computed by an off-chain orchestrator API (apex.api.macrocosmos.ai
  /validator/global_miner_scores); the validator in this repo only relays them to chain via set_weights.
  A per-competition burn_factor reduces emissions to miners (effective miner emission = (1 - burn_factor)
  * incentive_weight per the CLI dashboard), with DEFAULT_BASE_BURN_RATE = 0.9. The actual scoring/burn
  computation lives server-side and is NOT in this repository, so the live split is only partially observable
  here.
subnet:
  name: Apex (Subnet 1)
  owner_repo: macrocosm-os__apex
  netuid: 1
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners compete within Competitions, each made of multiple Rounds, by submitting Python-based
    algorithms through the Apex CLI. Validators (via an off-chain orchestrator) evaluate each submission
    against per-competition benchmarks / baseline scores and reward miners whose solutions perform best.
    Concrete competitions present in the repo include RL Battleship, Iota Simulator, energy arbitrage,
    matrix compression, and tron.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: competition_benchmark_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: Per-competition score relative to a baseline_score / score_to_beat, computed off-chain
    by the orchestrator; exact transform not present in repo.
  notes: Each CompetitionRecord carries baseline_score, baseline_raw_score, score_to_beat and top_score_value.
    The mapping from these to per-miner weights is computed server-side and not in this repository.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Per-competition benchmark / simulation environment that scores a miner's submitted Python
    algorithm (e.g. energy arbitrage simulation with fixed physical constants, RL battleship game environment,
    iota distributed-training simulator).
  source_identifier: shared/competition/src/competition/*
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Scoring is executed by the orchestrator against these task definitions; submissions are graded
    vs a baseline_score and score_to_beat.
aggregation:
  method: undocumented
  composition: other
  composition_other: 'Off-chain: per-competition top-score-relative scoring combined with a per-competition
    incentive_weight allocation and a burn_factor; the combination formula is not present in this repo.'
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: Validator passes raw orchestrator weights through bt.utils.weight_utils.process_weights_for_netuid
    before set_weights; final normalization is bittensor-default.
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: Each competition has a burn_factor (Optional, set by orchestrator) and a base_burn_rate defaulting
      to DEFAULT_BASE_BURN_RATE = 0.9. CLI computes miner emissions as (1 - burn_factor) * incentive_weight,
      so burn_factor is a dynamic per-competition residual computed off-chain; the literal live value
      is not in this repo (only the 0.9 default floor).
  notes: OWNER_UID = 248 is defined in common settings. Validators that receive no orchestrator weights
    fall back to copy_weights_from_chain (stake-weighted average of existing chain weights).
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: weight_loop fetches per-miner weights from the orchestrator (/validator/global_miner_scores),
    converts MinerScore.weight by uid, runs process_weights_for_netuid, and calls subtensor.set_weights.
    On empty/failed fetch it copies stake-weighted weights from chain instead.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: WEIGHT_SUBMIT_INTERVAL = 60*21 seconds (21 minutes) on mainnet
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA on the final reward in this repo; the validator relays orchestrator weights verbatim
      (after process_weights_for_netuid). Any smoothing would be server-side.
anti_gaming:
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: SCORE_THRESHOLD = 0.01 in common settings gates low scores. Submissions carry a submission_fee_usd
    (Decimal) paid to SUBMISSION_FEE_ADDRESS, a fixed coldkey, verified on-chain — a monetary barrier
    to spam submissions.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Fallback copy_weights_from_chain weights chain weights by validator stake (normalized_stakes
    dot valid_weights) restricted to validator_permit holders.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: 'The subnet is organized as multiple independent Competitions, each with its own incentive_weight
    (direct decimal emission allocation 0.0-1.0), baseline, burn_factor and multiple Rounds. Competition
    task packages present in repo: battleship, rl_battleship, iota_simulator, energy_arbitrage, matrix_compression,
    tron. The set and weighting of active competitions is managed off-chain.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - status_enum
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: burn_factor_reset_at marks when a competition's burn factor / top scorer was last reset
    (age of top scorer).
  notes: Per-competition top score (top_score_value, top_scorer_hotkey, score_to_beat) and round state
    are tracked off-chain; this repo only models the API responses (CompetitionRecord / CompetitionDetailsResponse).
documentation:
  status: sparse
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - src/validator/README.md
  completeness: low
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: low
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '9'
    quote: '# **Bittensor Subnet 1: Apex**'
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: shared/common/src/common/settings.py
    line_ref: '20'
    quote: NETUID = int(os.getenv("NETUID", 1))
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: src/validator/src/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 1-6
    quote: 'import bittensor as bt

      import numpy as np'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '23'
    quote: Each pursuit of the best solution takes place within a **Competition**, which consists of multiple
      **Rounds** of evaluation where participants (miners) submit their Python-based algorithms through
      the Apex CLI and earn rewards based on their performance.
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '23'
    quote: submit their Python-based algorithms through the Apex CLI
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: shared/common/src/common/models/api/competition.py
    line_ref: 40-57
    quote: "baseline_score: float\n    baseline_raw_score: float\n    incentive_weight: float\n    burn_factor:\
      \ float"
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: src/validator/src/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 95-123
    quote: 'scores: dict | None = await ValidatorAPIClient.get_global_miner_scores(hotkey=self.wallet.hotkey)
      ... weights = {int(m.uid): m.weight for m in subnet_scores.miner_scores} ... await self.set_weights(weights=weights)'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: src/validator/src/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 313-320
    quote: success, response = self.subtensor.set_weights(
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: src/validator/src/validator/settings.py
    line_ref: '5'
    quote: 'WEIGHT_SUBMIT_INTERVAL: int = 10 if (MOCK or not BITTENSOR) else 60 * 21  # submit weight
      every 21 minutes'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization_other
    source_path: src/validator/src/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 296-305
    quote: ) = bt.utils.weight_utils.process_weights_for_netuid(
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/enabled
    source_path: shared/common/src/common/models/api/competition.py
    line_ref: '43'
    quote: 'burn_factor: float'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: shared/common/src/common/settings.py
    line_ref: '45'
    quote: DEFAULT_BASE_BURN_RATE = 0.9
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status_notes
    source_path: src/cli/src/cli/dashboard/screens/competition_detail_screen.py
    line_ref: 222-223
    quote: "if comp.burn_factor is not None and comp.state == \"active\":\n            emissions_percent\
      \ = (1 - comp.burn_factor) * comp.incentive_weight * 100"
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: src/validator/src/validator/validator_api_client.py
    line_ref: 101-107
    quote: 'response: SubnetScores = await cls.orchestrator_request(method="GET", path="/validator/global_miner_scores",
      hotkey=hotkey)'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: shared/common/src/common/settings.py
    line_ref: 42-49
    quote: SCORE_THRESHOLD = float(os.getenv("SCORE_THRESHOLD", "0.01")) ... SUBMISSION_FEE_ADDRESS
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: src/validator/src/validator/validator.py
    line_ref: 341-345
    quote: valid_indices = np.where(self.metagraph.validator_permit)[0] ... normalized_stakes = valid_stakes
      / np.sum(valid_stakes) stake_weighted_average = np.dot(normalized_stakes, valid_weights)
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/structure
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 31-33
    quote: '**[RL Battleship]** ... **[Iota Simulator]**'
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/notes
    source_path: src/cli/src/cli/dashboard/screens/competition_detail_screen.py
    line_ref: '221'
    quote: '# Calculate % emissions - incentive_weight is now a direct decimal allocation (0.0-1.0)'
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: shared/common/src/common/models/api/competition.py

  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes

  unresolved:
  - Exact orchestrator scoring formula mapping baseline_score/score_to_beat to per-miner weight (computed
    server-side, not in repo).
  - Live burn_factor value and how it is computed per competition (only DEFAULT_BASE_BURN_RATE = 0.9 floor
    is in repo).
  - How incentive_weight is allocated across competitions (set off-chain).
  - Any temporal smoothing / EMA on scores (would be server-side; not observable).

composition:
  shape: opaque

docs.title: Data Universe (SN13)
docs.text:
Subnet macrocosm-os__data-universe netuid 13 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners scrape and store real-time social-media / web data (X/Twitter, Reddit, YouTube transcripts) into local DataEntityBuckets keyed by source, time bucket and label, and advertise a compressed MinerIndex. Validators periodically sample and verify the data (and S3 uploads / on-demand requests) and DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: RealEngagement"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, DeterministicCheck, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: volume"]
  M1["Metric: other"]
  M2["Metric: other"]
  M3["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: dataUniverseSn13

    netuid: 13
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: [content_link, api_response]

    @burn {
        uid: "subnet owner hotkey UID (resolved via get_subnet_owner_hotkey)"
        fraction: 0.7
    }
    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { reputation, cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: volume
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: data_freshness
                specific: data_desirability
                raw: "data_desirability_weight"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: data_freshness
                specific: freshness
                raw: "freshness_decay"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: classification_quality
                specific: validation_pass_rate
                raw: "validation_pass_rate_multiplier"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: ema
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: RealEngagement {
            trust_model: adversarial
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "every >20 minutes (dt.timedelta(minutes=20)) between weight sets"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: A fixed 70% of emission is redirected to the subnet owner UID (constants.EMISSION_CONTROL_PERCENTAGE
  = 0.70) via utils.apply_burn_to_weights, with the remaining 30% distributed across miners by the documented
  data-scoring mechanism. The scoring code is live (set_weights runs every ~20 minutes), so this is partial_burn
  rather than full_burn.
subnet:
  name: Data Universe (SN13)
  owner_repo: macrocosm-os__data-universe
  netuid: 13
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners scrape and store real-time social-media / web data (X/Twitter, Reddit, YouTube transcripts)
    into local DataEntityBuckets keyed by source, time bucket and label, and advertise a compressed MinerIndex.
    Validators periodically sample and verify the data (and S3 uploads / on-demand requests) and reward
    miners by the volume of scorable bytes, weighted by data desirability, freshness (age), duplication
    across miners, and a credibility multiplier earned by passing validation.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: scorable_bytes
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Core raw score: raw_score = data_type_scale_factor * time_scalar * scorable_bytes, summed over
    a miner''s data entity buckets. scorable_bytes already discounts data that is duplicated across many
    miners. Contributes via the P2P component (score * P2P_REWARD_SCALE * p2p_cred) which is additionally
    scaled by P2P_REWARD_SCALE = 0.05 and capped at the S3+OD service components.'
- name: data_desirability_scale_factor
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: data_desirability_weight
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Per-(source,label) multiplier from the Dynamic Desirability lookup. Desirable labels score more
    highly; unspecified labels get the default_scale_factor (0.3). Negative scale factors can penalize
    undesirable data. Reddit weight 0.6 / X weight 0.4 in the static backup lookup; docs/scoring.md cites
    Reddit 0.55 / X 0.35 / YouTube 0.10.
- name: data_age_time_scalar
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: freshness_decay
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Linear age depreciation: data from now scored 1.0, data at max_age_in_hours scored 0.5, older
    than max_age scored 0.0. Formula: 1.0 - (age_in_hours / (2 * max_age_in_hours)). max_age = DATA_ENTITY_BUCKET_AGE_LIMIT_DAYS
    (30) * 24 hours.'
- name: miner_credibility
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: validation_pass_rate_multiplier
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: ema
  notes: 'Per-miner credibility in [0,1], EMA of the fraction of validated bytes that passed checks (cred_alpha
    = 0.15). Applied as a multiplier raised to the power _CREDIBILITY_EXP = 2.5: final P2P/S3 components
    use cred ** 2.5. Starting credibility 0. Separate S3 credibility (s3_cred_alpha 0.30) and on-demand
    credibility (EMA alpha 0.02) gate the S3 and OD score components.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: real_engagement
  description: Validators verify miner-reported data by re-scraping a sample of each miner's index from
    the original platforms (X/Twitter, Reddit, YouTube), checking correctness/existence and schema, and
    tracking pass rate as credibility.
  source_identifier: x.com / reddit / youtube scrapers
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Validators 'remain suspicious of Miners' and periodically sample-verify the MinerIndex against
    the live source platforms.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: subnet owner hotkey UID (resolved via get_subnet_owner_hotkey)
    fraction: 0.7
    dynamic: false
    notes: apply_burn_to_weights sets the owner UID weight to burn_percentage (0.70) and rescales all
      other miners' weights to sum to (1 - 0.70). burn_percentage = constants.EMISSION_CONTROL_PERCENTAGE
      = 0.70.
  notes: 'Final score is reward-proportional: miner_reward = (miner_score / total_network_score) * total_reward_pool.
    get_scores_for_weights combines P2P, S3 and OD components (each gated by its own credibility, with
    caps: S3 <= 2x OD, P2P <= S3+OD), then raw_weights = normalize(scores, p=1) (L1). ''hybrid'' composition
    because components are additive but each is gated multiplicatively by a credibility and then capped.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: L1-normalized capped scores, then 70% burn applied to owner UID, then process_weights_for_netuid,
    then subtensor.set_weights. Gated on completing EVALUATION_ON_STARTUP (10) evaluation cycles after
    startup.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: every >20 minutes (dt.timedelta(minutes=20)) between weight sets
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA is applied to the final per-miner reward before weight-setting; scores are L1-normalized
      directly. EMAs exist upstream on credibility (cred_alpha 0.15, s3_cred_alpha 0.30, ondemand_alpha
      0.3 / ondemand_cred_alpha 0.02), not on the final reward vector.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Duplication Factor: data stored by many miners is worth less; value decreases proportional
    to the number of miners storing it. Reflected in scorable_bytes (which discounts shared data).'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Validators periodically re-scrape and verify a sample of each miner's reported data against
    the live source platforms; failed checks drive credibility (a cred**2.5 multiplier) down, heavily
    penalizing miners who misrepresent their MinerIndex.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: rejection
  description: MIN_EVALUATION_PERIOD = 60 minutes between re-evaluations of a miner; data entity bucket
    size limit (128 MB) and per-miner bucket count limits cap reportable index size.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: P2P index scoring
    role: main
    advancement_rule: scorable_bytes * desirability * age, scaled by P2P_REWARD_SCALE=0.05, capped at
      S3+OD
  - name: S3 upload validation
    role: main
    advancement_rule: effective_size = total_size_bytes * coverage^2; S3 boost share, capped at 2x OD
      component
  - name: On-demand (OD) requests
    role: main
    advancement_rule: EMA of recent on-demand rewards, gated by on-demand credibility
  count: 3
  notes: Three parallel reward channels (P2P MinerIndex, S3 upload validation, on-demand request serving),
    each gated by its own credibility EMA, combined additively with caps in get_scores_for_weights.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - reputation
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Scores/credibility persisted via save_state/load_state; STATE_VERSION (currently 9) bumps
    trigger full or partial resets of S3/OD boosts and credibility when exploits are found.
  notes: 'Per-miner persistent state: scores, miner_credibility, scorable_bytes, s3_boosts, s3_credibility,
    ondemand_boosts, ondemand_credibility, effective_sizes.'

docs.title: Finetuning (Subnet 37)
docs.text:
Subnet macrocosm-os__finetuning netuid 37 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status unknown Task Miners fine-tune LLMs locally, publish the best model to Hugging Face, and commit that model's metadata to the Bittensor chain (one model per miner at a time). Validators download each miner's model and score it on a competition-specific set of evaluation tasks (synthetic MMLU multiple-choice, word- DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, DeterministicDataset, DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Other, Deduplication, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: loss"]
  M2["Metric: loss"]
  M3["Metric: accuracy"]
  M4["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: finetuningSubnet37

    netuid: 37
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: [model_weights, commitment_hash]

    @guards {
        Other {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, registration_age }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: loss
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: loss
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                raw: "weighted_sum_of_normalized_task_scores"
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: other
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            normalization: other
            decay_rate: 0.9
            min_weight_floor: 0.18
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "~once per hour (set_weights loop sleeps 60*60s); SYNC_BLOCK_CADENCE=360 blocks; chain_update_cadence=20min"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
subnet:
  name: Finetuning (Subnet 37)
  owner_repo: macrocosm-os__finetuning
  netuid: 37
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners fine-tune LLMs locally, publish the best model to Hugging Face, and commit that model's
    metadata to the Bittensor chain (one model per miner at a time). Validators download each miner's
    model and score it on a competition-specific set of evaluation tasks (synthetic MMLU multiple-choice,
    word-sorting reference loss, FineWeb text cross-entropy loss, synthetic IFEval rule-following, and
    reasoning-trace reference loss). Within a competition, models are compared pairwise with an epsilon
    (earlier-block) advantage, and the model(s) with the highest win rate receive that competition's emission
    share. The subnet runs several competitions (e.g. B7_MULTI_CHOICE, INSTRUCT_8B, DISTILLED_REASONING_3B)
    over time, each with its own model-size/architecture constraints and reward percentage.
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
  - commitment_hash
  extensions:
    publication: Model uploaded to Hugging Face (public repo); model metadata/hash committed to the Bittensor
      chain.
scoring_signals:
- name: synthetic_mmlu_deviation
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: none
  notes: 'MULTIPLE_CHOICE method: fraction of incorrect answers on a synthetic MMLU-like multiple-choice
    dataset sourced from Subnet 1 (Prompting). Raw score is the ratio of incorrect answers (0 = correct,
    1 = incorrect), so lower is better. Weight shown is for the active INSTRUCT_8B post-B7-sunset schedule
    (0.75 in the earlier schedule).'
- name: word_sorting_reference_loss
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: 0.05
  normalization: other
  notes: REFERENCE_LOSS on a word-sorting task; normalized via INVERSE_EXPONENTIAL with ceiling 40.0 (taoverse
    normalize_score). Cross-entropy on the reference portion.
- name: fineweb_text_loss
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: 0.35
  normalization: other
  notes: 'TEXT_LOSS: average cross-entropy on a FineWeb-edu sample; normalized via INVERSE_EXPONENTIAL
    with ceiling 20.0. A reasonable-output sanity check returns inf (worst) if outputs overlap/repeat
    too much.'
- name: if_eval_v2
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: none
  notes: 'IF_EVAL: fraction of rules NOT satisfied across two responses; per-response score is 1 - (correct_ratio**2).
    Models with >10% duplicated responses across prompts are forced to score 1 (worst). Lower is better.'
- name: composite_weighted_task_score
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: weighted_sum_of_normalized_task_scores
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: other
  notes: score_model() returns score = sum over eval tasks of (normalized_score * task_weight), with task
    weights renormalized to sum to 1. This per-model composite score is then used as the 'loss' input
    to the pairwise win comparison. Lower composite is better. Task set and weights vary by competition/schedule
    block.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Synthetic MMLU-like multiple-choice questions sourced from the Prompting subnet (Subnet
    1) via WandB; also synthetic IFEval-style rule-following prompts.
  source_identifier: SYNTHETIC_MMLU / SYNTHETIC_IF_EVAL (Subnet 1 Prompting, via WandB)
  trust_model: trusted
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: FineWeb-edu text sample for cross-entropy (TEXT_LOSS) and generated word-sorting task for
    REFERENCE_LOSS.
  source_identifier: FINEWEB (HuggingFaceFW/fineweb-edu-score-2) / WORD_SORTING
  trust_model: trusted
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Reasoning-trace datasets for the DISTILLED_REASONING_3B competition (math/code step-by-step
    reasoning), scored via REFERENCE_LOSS.
  source_identifier: SYNTHETIC_1_SFT (PrimeIntellect/SYNTHETIC-1-SFT-Data) / CODEFORCES_COTS
  trust_model: trusted
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  normalization: other
  decay_rate: 0.9
  min_weight_floor: 0.18
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    notes: No deliberate burn/null-UID routing observed in the active validator. Per-competition weight
      goes entirely to the winning model(s); emission then split across competitions by reward_percentage.
  notes: Per competition, step_weights = 1 for the uid(s) with the highest epsilon-adjusted pairwise win
    rate and 0 for all others (winner-take-all), NOT the win-ratio/softmax described in the docs (see
    provenance.extractor_notes). These per-competition weight vectors are fed into a CompetitionTracker
    that maintains an exponential moving average with alpha=ALPHA=0.90, then merged across active competitions
    weighted by each competition's reward_percentage. Any merged competition weight below MIN_WEIGHT_THRESHOLD
    (0.18) is zeroed to aid vtrust. The pairwise win uses an epsilon (LinearDecay, e.g. 0.05 -> 0.01)
    advantage favoring the model committed at the earlier block.
  extensions:
    epsilon_func: LinearDecay(start=0.05, end=0.01, decay_blocks varies per competition, e.g. 7200*1..7200*5)
    competition_reward_split: Each Competition has a reward_percentage; schedule asserts they sum to 1.0.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: EMA (alpha 0.90) of per-competition winner-take-all weight vectors, merged across active competitions
    by reward_percentage, with a 0.18 min-weight threshold; committed on chain roughly once per hour by
    a dedicated set_weights thread.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: ~once per hour (set_weights loop sleeps 60*60s); SYNC_BLOCK_CADENCE=360 blocks; chain_update_cadence=20min
anti_gaming:
- kind: other
  kind_other: epsilon_earlier_block_advantage
  enforcement: barrier
  description: _is_win applies an epsilon (LinearDecay) discount to the earlier-committed model's loss,
    so a copied/newer model must beat the original by epsilon percent to win. This deters copying publicly
    available models without genuine improvement.
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'IF_EVAL penalizes models that produce too-similar responses to different prompts: if >10%
    of samples have response similarity > 0.6, the task score is forced to 1 (worst). TEXT_LOSS also returns
    inf if generated outputs overlap/repeat excessively.'
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Chain metadata may only be updated every ~20 minutes (chain_update_cadence); models have
    an eval_block_delay before being scored. One model per miner at a time.
- kind: other
  kind_other: model_size_and_architecture_constraints
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Each competition enforces max (and sometimes min) parameter size, allowed architectures,
    sequence length, tokenizer, and a max HF repo byte size (MAX_HUGGING_FACE_BYTES ~15.1GB); non-conforming
    models are not valid entries.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: B7_MULTI_CHOICE
    role: other
    advancement_rule: highest epsilon-adjusted pairwise win rate wins the competition share
    multipliers:
      reward_percentage_initial: 0.75
      status: sunset at block 4675163
  - name: INSTRUCT_8B
    role: main
    advancement_rule: highest epsilon-adjusted pairwise win rate wins the competition share
    multipliers:
      reward_percentage_initial: 0.25
      reward_percentage_after_b7_sunset: 1.0
      status: scheduled to sunset at block 5158632
  - name: DISTILLED_REASONING_3B
    role: main
    advancement_rule: highest epsilon-adjusted pairwise win rate wins the competition share
    multipliers:
      reward_percentage: 1.0
      param_range: 3.2B-3.4B
  count: 3
  notes: Competitions are scheduled by block via COMPETITION_SCHEDULE_BY_BLOCK; multiple may be active
    simultaneously with reward_percentages summing to 1.0. Each competition has its own eval task set/weights
    and model constraints. Roles 'other'/'main' are approximations; the schema has no 'competition track'
    role.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - registration_age
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: VALIDATOR_STATE_VERSION (currently 13) bump causes validators to start from fresh state.
    Competition weights carried as an EMA (alpha 0.90) in the CompetitionTracker.
  notes: The CompetitionTracker keeps a moving-average weight per uid per competition across eval loops;
    uid_to_block records the commit block used for the epsilon earlier-block advantage. No explicit status
    enum / debt ledger.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: 3.1.0

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/2

docs.title: Mainframe (Folding / Protein-Folding, SN25)
docs.text:
Subnet macrocosm-os__folding netuid 25 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners run molecular-dynamics protein-folding simulations (OpenMM) on a PDB challenge assigned by validators, and upload their trajectory/checkpoint files. The miner that reaches the lowest (most negative) potential energy for a given protein job wins the bulk of the reward; remaining valid miners g DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: ReferenceModel"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, Deduplication, CodeInspection"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: loss"]
  AGG["aggregate: RankBased"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: mainframeFoldingProteinFoldingSn25

    netuid: 25
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [content_link, signals]

    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { reputation, cumulative_score, performance_ledger }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: Metric {
            kind: loss
            direction: lower_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: ReferenceModel {
            trust_model: adversarial
        }
        aggregate: RankBased {
            composition: gated
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: set_weights L1-normalizes the EMA score vector and emits it directly via bt.utils.weight_utils.
  No burn UID, burn fraction, or emission-burn branch appears in the weight-setting code; validator scoring
  is live.
subnet:
  name: Mainframe (Folding / Protein-Folding, SN25)
  owner_repo: macrocosm-os__folding
  netuid: 25
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners run molecular-dynamics protein-folding simulations (OpenMM) on a PDB challenge assigned
    by validators, and upload their trajectory/checkpoint files. The miner that reaches the lowest (most
    negative) potential energy for a given protein job wins the bulk of the reward; remaining valid miners
    get a linearly decreasing share. Jobs come in synthetic (SyntheticMD) and organic (OrganicMD) flavors.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: reported_energy
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: The simulated potential energy of the folded protein (kJ/mol). Lower (more negative) is better.
    The validator re-runs/validates checkpoints and takes the median of the final ENERGY_WINDOW_SIZE checked
    energies; the argmin energy across miners defines best_loss / best_hotkey. This is a gated/ranking
    signal (not an additively-weighted one), hence weight null.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: reference_model
  description: Validators independently re-simulate a subset of the miner's reported checkpoints in OpenMM
    and compare the recomputed energy to the miner's reported energy; a percent difference above ANOMALY_THRESHOLD
    invalidates the submission. Gradient and XML-checkpoint thresholds also gate validity.
  source_identifier: OpenMM molecular-dynamics re-simulation (validator-side)
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: 'Re-computation is probabilistic: validation runs with probability get_validation_probability(credibility);
    high-credibility miners are checked less often (skip path reuses reported energy).'
aggregation:
  method: rank_based
  composition: gated
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn UID or burn fraction in set_weights; raw_weights are the L1-normalized EMA scores emitted
      directly.
  notes: 'Per job (MDReward.get_rewards): the best miner (argmin energy / stored best_hotkey) receives
    top_reward = TOP_SYNTHETIC_MD_REWARD (0.80); the remaining valid non-zero miners share (1 - top_reward)
    via divide_decreasing, a linearly decreasing allocation sorted smallest-energy-first. If no/one miner
    replies the best miner gets reward 1. Final per-job reward is multiplied by job.priority (SyntheticMD)
    or job.priority * 10 (OrganicMD). Cross-job aggregation onto chain weights is an EMA in update_scores,
    then L1-normalized in set_weights.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: sync() calls weight_setter() -> set_weights() when should_set_weights() is true (>= epoch_length
    blocks since last update). Validator scores are zeroed, L1-normalized (torch.nn.functional.normalize
    p=1), processed via process_weights_for_netuid, converted, and emitted with subtensor.set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: 'update_scores does a per-uid EMA: scores = alpha*reward + (1-alpha)*scores, where alpha =
      neuron.positive_alpha (default 0.10) when reward > 0, else neuron.negative_alpha (default 0.03).
      Two distinct alphas, so alpha left null.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Validator re-simulates miner checkpoints in OpenMM; if the recomputed median energy differs
    from the reported energy by more than ANOMALY_THRESHOLD (1%), the submission is marked invalid (reason
    'energy_difference_too_large'). GRADIENT_THRESHOLD_FINAL/INTERMEDIATE and XML_CHECKPOINT_THRESHOLD
    further gate validity.
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Energies within DIFFERENCE_THRESHOLD (1e-6) of an already-seen energy are marked is_duplicate
    and excluded from the energies dict, preventing copy-of-the-winner submissions from being rewarded.
    TOP_K=5 unique valid energies are collected per job.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'credibility_pipeline: if reason == ''state-checkpoint'' (checkpoint-file exploit detected)
    the miner''s chain score is halved (scores[uid] *= 0.5). Per-task credibility is updated via EMA with
    CREDIBILITY_ALPHA_POSITIVE 0.15 / CREDIBILITY_ALPHA_NEGATIVE 0.25.'
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Only non-validator UIDs are queried/rewarded (get_all_miner_uids with vpermit_tao_limit,
    default 20000); validator permit UIDs have their scores forced to 0 before weight setting.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: SyntheticMD
    role: main
    advancement_rule: Validator-created synthetic PDB folding jobs; final reward = per-job reward * job.priority.
    multipliers:
      final: rewards * priority
  - name: OrganicMD
    role: main
    advancement_rule: Externally-submitted (organic) folding jobs; final reward = per-job reward * priority
      * organic_priority_multiplier (10).
    multipliers:
      final: rewards * priority * 10
      organic_priority_multiplier: 10
  count: 2
  notes: REWARD_REGISTRY maps job_type -> reward class (SyntheticMD / OrganicMD). Same MDReward.get_rewards
    logic; differ only in calculate_final_reward multiplier.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - reputation
  - cumulative_score
  - performance_ledger
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: MinerRegistry credibility per task starts at STARTING_CREDIBILITY (0.50) and is updated
    by EMA each job. On hotkey replacement (resync_metagraph) the miner is reset and chain score zeroed.
    EMA chain scores persist across epochs in state.pt.
  notes: Credibility drives get_validation_probability (lower credibility -> always validated; higher
    -> exponentially less likely, exp(-4.605*(cred-0.5))).
documentation:
  status: code_authoritative
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - documentation/gjp/README.md
  - documentation/api/README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: folding/utils/config.py
    line_ref: '36'
    quote: parser.add_argument("--netuid", type=int, help="Subnet netuid", default=25)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '37'
    quote: Subnet 25, Mainframe (formerly known as Protein-Folding) is the first decentralized science
      subnet on Bittensor.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '37'
    quote: its focus is on using global computing power to simulate protein molecular dynamics via OpenMM
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/direction
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 420-421
    quote: best_index = np.argmin(energies); best_loss = energies[best_index].item()
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/name
    source_path: folding/validators/reward.py
    line_ref: 132-134
    quote: 'sorted_dict = dict(sorted(all_miner_logs.items(), key=lambda item: item[1]["reported_energy"]))'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: folding/rewards/md_rewards.py
    line_ref: 41-46
    quote: rewards[best_index] = top_reward ... If no miners reply, we want *all* reward to go to the
      top miner. rewards[best_index] = 1
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: folding/rewards/linear_reward.py
    line_ref: 8-9
    quote: 'weights = [number_of_elements - i for i in range(number_of_elements)]  # [n, n-1, n-2, ...,
      1]'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: folding/utils/constants.py
    line_ref: '24'
    quote: TOP_SYNTHETIC_MD_REWARD = 0.80
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/enabled
    source_path: folding/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 146-148
    quote: raw_weights = (torch.nn.functional.normalize(self.scores, p=1, dim=0).to("cpu").numpy())
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: folding/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 177-185
    quote: result = self.subtensor.set_weights(wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.config.netuid, uids=uint_uids,
      weights=uint_weights, ...)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes
    source_path: folding/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 257-259
    quote: self.scores = (scattered_alpha * scattered_rewards + (1 - scattered_alpha) * self.scores)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes
    source_path: folding/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 356-367
    quote: '"--neuron.positive_alpha", ... default=0.10 ... "--neuron.negative_alpha", ... default=0.03'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: folding/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 52-57
    quote: '"--neuron.epoch_length", ... "how often we set weights, measured in 12 second blocks", default=100'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: folding/validators/reward.py
    line_ref: 213-221
    quote: 'if percent_diff > c.ANOMALY_THRESHOLD: ... reason = "energy_difference_too_large"'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: folding/validators/reward.py
    line_ref: 225-228
    quote: is_duplicate = any(abs(median_energy - energy) < c.DIFFERENCE_THRESHOLD for energy in unique_energies)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 392-396
    quote: 'if reason == "state-checkpoint": ... self.scores[uid] = 0.5 * self.scores[uid]'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: folding/registries/miner_registry.py
    line_ref: '26'
    quote: 'overall_credibility: float = c.STARTING_CREDIBILITY'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/notes
