docs.title: IOTA (Incentivized Orchestrated Training Architecture)
docs.text:
Subnet macrocosm-os__iota netuid 9 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners are nodes in a data- and pipeline-parallel LLM pretraining pipeline. The orchestrator distributes model layers across heterogeneous miners and streams activations between them; miners compete to process as many activations as possible during the training stage, periodically upload local weigh DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, DeterministicCheck, HardwareValidation"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: throughput"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: iotaIncentivizedOrchestratedTrainingArchitecture

    netuid: 9
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: compute_service

    @burn {
        uid: "209"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, status_enum }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: Metric {
            kind: throughput
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "1260 seconds (60*21)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: 'The validator does not compute scores locally: it fetches orchestrator-computed
  global miner scores via the orchestrator API (/validator/global_miner_scores) and copies them onto the
  chain. The actual scoring/spot-check logic is dynamically fetched as zipped "validator code" from the
  orchestrator at runtime, so it is not present in this repo. A dynamic burn factor is applied (subnet
  burn_factor = 1 - sum(all miner weights)); when no global weights are received the validator falls back
  to copying stake-weighted weights from the chain.'
subnet:
  name: IOTA (Incentivized Orchestrated Training Architecture)
  owner_repo: macrocosm-os__iota
  netuid: 9
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners are nodes in a data- and pipeline-parallel LLM pretraining pipeline. The orchestrator
    distributes model layers across heterogeneous miners and streams activations between them; miners
    compete to process as many activations as possible during the training stage, periodically upload
    local weights, and merge activations via a variant of Butterfly All-Reduce. Validators spot-check
    miners to ensure work was actually performed.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: miner_raw_score
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: raw_score is documented as "sum(scores within time window)" per miner; the per-activation scoring
    that produces these is computed by the orchestrator (off-repo). total_score = raw_score * multiplier.
    Concrete scoring formula and the multiplier value are not present in this repository.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Training data is the FineWeb dataset; pretraining loss/work is the underlying training
    signal. Activations carry BLAKE3 hash chains and a fraction are spot-checked against S3-verifiable
    uploads.
  source_identifier: HuggingFaceFW/fineweb
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: DATASET_NAME = "HuggingFaceFW/fineweb" in shared/common settings. Spot-check verification logic
    runs on the orchestrator.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '209'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: Subnet burn_factor is documented as "1 - sum(all miner weights)" (a dynamic residual). Per-run
      burn_factor also exists. Burn is realized as weight assigned to OWNER_UID (209). FALLBACK_BURN_FACTOR
      = 0.8 is used only as a fallback constant; the active per-run/subnet burn fraction is computed by
      the orchestrator and not a fixed literal in this repo.
  notes: Final per-miner weight is documented as run_weight * (1 - run's burn rate) * run's incentive_perc;
    run_weight = total_score / sum of total_scores for the run. The validator L1-normalizes the received
    scores (torch normalize p=1) before process_weights_for_netuid.
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: weight_setting_step fetches global miner scores from the orchestrator, sums per-uid weights
    into a dict, L1-normalizes to a tensor, runs bt.utils.weight_utils.process_weights_for_netuid, then
    calls subtensor.set_weights. If no global weights are returned it falls back to copy_weights_from_chain
    (stake-weighted average of on-chain validator weights).
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 1260 seconds (60*21)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No local temporal smoothing of the final reward in this repo; the validator copies orchestrator-provided
      scores each interval. WEIGHT_SUBMIT_INTERVAL = 60*21 seconds.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Validators spot-check miners by re-validating activations/weights against thresholds:
    COSINE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = 0.9, ACTIVATION_MAGNITUDE_THRESHOLD = 0.8, OPTIMIZER_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD
    = 0.8 (validator settings).'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Every activation carries a BLAKE3 hash chain; producer/consumer hashes are submitted to
    the orchestrator for auditability. A configurable fraction of P2P activations (SPOT_CHECK_RATE, default
    0.01) are randomly uploaded to S3 for verification (documented in docs/p2p_design_doc.md; constant
    not present in this repo's Python).
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Miners answer attestation challenges (registration / weights / merged_partitions) with
    signed/enclave attestation payloads, supporting delay-suspect runtime checks.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: 'Incentive is allocated across multiple concurrent training runs: each RunIncentiveAllocation
    has an incentive_weight (fraction of incentive for the run) and a per-run burn_factor; miner scores
    are weighted within their assigned run.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - status_enum
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: Per-miner MinerScore carries raw_score, total_score, run_weight, weight and an assigned run_id;
    miners progress through status states (e.g. uploading_weights). Detailed status enum and reset policy
    are managed orchestrator-side.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/p2p_design_doc.md
  - docs/VALIDATOR_README.md
  - https://arxiv.org/abs/2507.17766
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: shared/common/src/common/settings.py
    line_ref: '22'
    quote: NETUID = int(os.getenv("NETUID", "9"))
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: src/validator/src/validator/utils/weight_setting.py
    line_ref: 1-8
    quote: import torch / import numpy as np / import bittensor as bt
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 19-23
    quote: The orchestrator distributes model layers across heterogeneous miners and streams activations
      between them. ... Miners compete to process as many activations as possible in the training stage.
      ... Validators spot-check miners to ensure that work was performed as required.
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: shared/common/src/common/models/api_models.py
    line_ref: 210-215
    quote: '# The raw score for the miner sum(scores within time window) / raw_score: float / # Calculated
      by: sum(scores within time window) * multipler / total_score: float'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: shared/common/src/common/settings.py
    line_ref: '61'
    quote: DATASET_NAME = "HuggingFaceFW/fineweb"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization
    source_path: src/validator/src/validator/utils/weight_setting.py

    quote: raw_weights = torch.nn.functional.normalize(scores, p=1, dim=0)
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes

    line_ref: 217-223
    quote: 'run_weight: total_score / all total_scores for the run; weight Calculated by: run_weight *
      (1 - run''s burn rate) * run''s incentive_perc'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/dynamic
    source_path: shared/common/src/common/models/api_models.py

    quote: '# Overall burn factor calculated for the subnet / # Calculated by: 1 - sum(all miner weights)

  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes

  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description

    not in this repo).

    constant).
  - Per-run incentive_weight / incentive_perc literal values (assigned by orchestrator at runtime).

docs.title: Mainframe (Subnet 25, formerly Protein-Folding)
docs.text:
Subnet macrocosm-os__mainframe netuid 25 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners run molecular-dynamics protein-folding simulations (OpenMM) drawn from a Global Job Pool and post their solutions to S3. The objective is to find the lowest free-energy configuration of a specified protein system; reported energy (analogous to loss) is the quality metric. Validators recompute DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, DeterministicCheck, CodeInspection"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: loss"]
  M1["Metric: reward"]
  AGG["aggregate: RankBased"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: mainframeSubnet25FormerlyProteinFolding

    netuid: 25
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [content_link, signals]

    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { reputation, cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: loss
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: reward
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: RankBased {
            composition: gated
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: 'Synthetic and organic molecular-dynamics jobs are scored live: validators evaluate
  miner energies, run a credibility-gated validation, and update an EMA scores tensor that becomes on-chain
  weights. No burn/owner-allocation logic is present in the source.'
subnet:
  name: Mainframe (Subnet 25, formerly Protein-Folding)
  owner_repo: macrocosm-os__mainframe
  netuid: 25
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners run molecular-dynamics protein-folding simulations (OpenMM) drawn from a Global Job
    Pool and post their solutions to S3. The objective is to find the lowest free-energy configuration
    of a specified protein system; reported energy (analogous to loss) is the quality metric. Validators
    recompute/validate the energy of submitted checkpoints. For each challenge the top-K (K=5) unique
    miners are ranked, with 80% of the challenge reward going to the top miner and the remaining 20% distributed
    in linearly decreasing fashion to the rest.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: reported_energy
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Miners are ranked by their reported (validator-checked) free energy; lower energy is better.
    Validator takes the median of the last ENERGY_WINDOW_SIZE (10) energy steps as the final energy. Only
    the top-K (5) valid, non-duplicate energies receive reward.
- name: credibility
  metric_kind: reward
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Per-miner, per-task credibility (EMA, starting 0.50) determines the probability that the validator
    re-validates a submission: validation_probability = 1 if cred <= 0.5 else exp(-4.605*(cred-0.5)).
    Acts as a gating/trust signal rather than an additive score.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Protein structures pulled from RCSB / PDBe and simulated deterministically via OpenMM;
    energy is recomputable so both miners and validators can verify a configuration's energy.
  source_identifier: rcsb | pdbe (--protein.input_source)
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Energy minimization is deterministic given the system config and seed, so the ground truth is
    the recomputed energy of the submitted checkpoint.
aggregation:
  method: rank_based
  composition: gated
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn or owner-allocation logic found in source.
  notes: 'Per challenge: best miner gets top_reward = TOP_SYNTHETIC_MD_REWARD = 0.80; remaining (1 - 0.80
    = 0.20) is split among the other non-zero miners via divide_decreasing (weights n, n-1, ..., 1 scaled
    to sum to 0.20). Ranking is done over the top-K=5 valid, non-duplicate energies (sorted ascending).
    Final per-challenge rewards are multiplied by job.priority (and an extra organic_priority_multiplier=10
    for organic jobs). The final on-chain weights are an L1 normalization (normalize p=1) of the EMA scores
    tensor.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: EMA scores tensor (per-miner) accumulated from per-job computed_rewards, then L1-normalized
    (torch.nn.functional.normalize p=1) and passed through bittensor process_weights_for_netuid / convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit
    before subtensor.set_weights. Validator-permit UIDs are zeroed before normalization.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: 'Asymmetric EMA on the scores tensor: scores = alpha*reward + (1-alpha)*scores, with alpha
      = neuron.positive_alpha (default 0.10) when reward > 0 else neuron.negative_alpha (default 0.03).
      These are CLI defaults, not hard-coded constants. The credibility EMA uses separate constants CREDIBILITY_ALPHA_POSITIVE=0.15
      / CREDIBILITY_ALPHA_NEGATIVE=0.25.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Duplicate energies are rejected: a submission whose median energy is within DIFFERENCE_THRESHOLD
    (1e-6) of an already-counted unique energy is flagged is_duplicate and receives no reward. Each miner
    uses a separate random seed to force unique exploration.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Validator re-runs the simulation and compares its energy to the miner's reported energy;
    if percent difference exceeds ANOMALY_THRESHOLD (1) the run is marked invalid (reason "energy_difference_too_large")
    and scored zero.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'State/checkpoint exploit detection: if the credibility pipeline sees reason "state-checkpoint",
    the miner''s score is halved (scores[uid] = 0.5 * scores[uid]).'
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Credibility-gated validation: low-credibility miners are validated with probability 1,
    high-credibility miners less often (exp decay), starting credibility 0.50; invalid runs push credibility
    down via the negative alpha.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Mechanism is per-job-type via REWARD_REGISTRY / EVALUATION_REGISTRY (SyntheticMD, OrganicMD).
    Organic jobs apply a 10x priority multiplier. README lists DFT as a planned future task ("Coming soon...").
    Currently molecular dynamics is the active task.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - reputation
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: On hotkey replacement the miner's score is zeroed and miner_registry.reset() reinitializes
    its credibility to STARTING_CREDIBILITY (0.50).
  notes: MinerRegistry tracks per-task credibility (EMA), credibility_over_time, and overall_credibility
    (mean across tasks). The validator also maintains a persistent EMA scores tensor per UID.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - documentation/gjp/README.md
  - documentation/api/README.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: folding/utils/config.py
    line_ref: '36'
    quote: parser.add_argument("--netuid", type=int, help="Subnet netuid", default=25)
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '32'
    quote: Subnet 25, Mainframe (formerly known as Protein-Folding) is the first decentralized science
      subnet on Bittensor.
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 138-142
    quote: miners compete to provide protein configurations that coincide with the lowest energy (analogous
      to loss). ... The top-K miners (currently, K = 5) are ranked, where 80% of the reward for that challenge
      is given to the top miner in the batch, and the remaining 20% is distributed to the (K-1) miners.
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/direction
    source_path: folding/validators/reward.py
    line_ref: 132-134
    quote: 'sorted_dict = dict(sorted(all_miner_logs.items(), key=lambda item: item[1]["reported_energy"]))'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: folding/validators/reward.py
    line_ref: 257-261
    quote: energies[uid] = np.median(miner_registry.registry[uid].logs["checked_energies"]["final"][-c.ENERGY_WINDOW_SIZE
      :])
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: folding/registries/miner_registry.py
    line_ref: 132-137
    quote: validation_probability = (1 if miner_credibility <= 0.5 else math.exp(-4.605 * (miner_credibility
      - 0.5)))
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: folding/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 178-184
    quote: parser.add_argument("--protein.input_source", ... default="rcsb", choices=["rcsb", "pdbe"])
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: folding/rewards/md_rewards.py
    line_ref: 41-78
    quote: rewards[best_index] = top_reward ... rewards_per_miner = divide_decreasing(amount_to_distribute=1
      - top_reward, number_of_elements=num_reminaing_miners)
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: folding/utils/constants.py
    line_ref: '24'
    quote: TOP_SYNTHETIC_MD_REWARD = 0.80
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: folding/rewards/linear_reward.py
    line_ref: 9-16
    quote: weights = [number_of_elements - i for i in range(number_of_elements)] ... scaling_factor =
      amount_to_distribute / total_weight
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: folding/rewards/md_rewards.py
    line_ref: 117-119
    quote: organic_priority_multiplier = 10 ; return rewards * self.priority * organic_priority_multiplier
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization
    source_path: folding/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 146-148
    quote: raw_weights = (torch.nn.functional.normalize(self.scores, p=1, dim=0).to("cpu").numpy())
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: folding/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 177-185
    quote: result = self.subtensor.set_weights(wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.config.netuid, uids=uint_uids,
      weights=uint_weights, ...
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: folding/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 52-57
    quote: parser.add_argument("--neuron.epoch_length", ... help="The default epoch length (how often
      we set weights, measured in 12 second blocks).", default=100)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes
    source_path: folding/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 249-258
    quote: alpha = (self.config.neuron.positive_alpha if rewards[ii] > 0 else self.config.neuron.negative_alpha)
      ... self.scores = (scattered_alpha * scattered_rewards + (1 - scattered_alpha) * self.scores)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes
    source_path: folding/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 355-367
    quote: '"--neuron.positive_alpha", ... default=0.10) ... "--neuron.negative_alpha", ... default=0.03)'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: folding/validators/reward.py
    line_ref: 225-229
    quote: is_duplicate = any(abs(median_energy - energy) < c.DIFFERENCE_THRESHOLD for energy in unique_energies)
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: folding/utils/constants.py
    line_ref: '25'
    quote: 'DIFFERENCE_THRESHOLD = 1e-6  # The threshold for 2 energy values to be considered equal'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: folding/validators/reward.py
    line_ref: 209-221
    quote: 'percent_diff = (abs((median_energy - reported_energy) / reported_energy) * 100) ... if percent_diff
      > c.ANOMALY_THRESHOLD: ... reason = "energy_difference_too_large"'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 392-396
    quote: 'if reason == "state-checkpoint": ... self.scores[uid] = 0.5 * self.scores[uid]'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/3/description
    source_path: folding/utils/constants.py
    line_ref: '21'
    quote: STARTING_CREDIBILITY = 0.50
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/notes
    source_path: folding/registries/miner_registry.py
    line_ref: 112-127
    quote: alpha = (c.CREDIBILITY_ALPHA_POSITIVE if cred > 0 else c.CREDIBILITY_ALPHA_NEGATIVE) ... miner.overall_credibility
      = round(mean(all_credibilities), 3)
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: folding/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 208-211
    quote: 'if hotkey != self.metagraph.hotkeys[uid]: self.scores[uid] = 0  # hotkey has been replaced
      ; self.miner_registry.reset(miner_uid=uid)'
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/notes
    source_path: folding/rewards/md_rewards.py
    line_ref: 122-125
    quote: 'REWARD_REGISTRY = {"SyntheticMD": SyntheticMDReward, "OrganicMD": OrganicMDReward}'
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/notes
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 152-157
    quote: '### Density Functional Theory (DFT) ... Coming soon...'
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 441-451
    quote: 'model: BaseReward = REWARD_REGISTRY[job.job_type](priority=job.priority) ... job.computed_rewards
      = reward_event.rewards.numpy().tolist()'
  unresolved:
  - Exact value of job.priority for synthetic jobs (store.py defaults priority to 1 via event.get('priority',
    1); the live per-job priority assigned by the GJP is not in this repo).
  - 'EMA alpha/window not fixed constants: weight-setting alphas are CLI-configurable (positive_alpha
    0.10 / negative_alpha 0.03 defaults), so no single canonical value.'
composition:
  shape: multiplex
  overlays:

  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Pretraining (SN9)
docs.text:
Subnet macrocosm-os__pretraining netuid 9 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status full_burn Task Miners train Foundation Models on the Falcon Refined Web dataset (and additional datasets per competition) and periodically publish model weights to Hugging Face, committing the model metadata to the Bittensor chain. Validators download each committed model and continuously evaluate its loss on rand DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CommitReveal, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: loss"]
  AGG["aggregate: Other"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: pretrainingSn9

    netuid: 9
    lang: python
    status: full_burn
    submission: [model_weights, commitment_hash]

    @burn {
        uid: "subnet owner UID (SubnetOwnerHotkey -> get_burn_uid())"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @guards {
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { registration_age, cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: Metric {
            kind: loss
            direction: lower_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: Other {
            composition: rank
            normalization: softmax
            temperature: 0.01
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.5
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: continuous
            tempo: "set_weights_interval blocks (waits set_weights_interval * BLOCK_TIME between sets)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: full_burn
mechanism_status_notes: The archived validator (TempValidator) assigns 100% of weight to the subnet-owner
  burn UID; the documented loss/win-rate/softmax/EMA mechanism is present but commented out (dormant).
subnet:
  name: Pretraining (SN9)
  owner_repo: macrocosm-os__pretraining
  netuid: 9
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners train Foundation Models on the Falcon Refined Web dataset (and additional datasets per
    competition) and periodically publish model weights to Hugging Face, committing the model metadata
    to the Bittensor chain. Validators download each committed model and continuously evaluate its loss
    on randomly sampled pages; the designed incentive rewards the lowest loss across the most batches.
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: validation_loss
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Per-batch text loss on sampled dataset pages. Converted to pairwise wins via iswin(), where the
    earlier-committed model gets an epsilon advantage (epsilon decays linearly over time).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Randomly sampled pages from large pretraining corpora; the network "acts like a continuous
    benchmark". Multiple datasets are weighted within a competition (e.g. Falcon, FineWeb, Stack v2, FineMath).
  source_identifier: falcon_refined_web (+ fineweb, stack_v2, finemath per COMPETITION_SCHEDULE_BY_BLOCK)
  trust_model: trusted
aggregation:
  method: other
  method_other: 'DESIGNED: epsilon-adjusted pairwise loss comparison -> per-uid win rate -> softmax over
    win rates with temperature, smoothed by an EMA. ACTIVE (archived snapshot): scoring is bypassed and
    100% of weight is assigned to the subnet-owner burn UID.'
  composition: rank
  normalization: softmax
  temperature: 0.01
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: subnet owner UID (SubnetOwnerHotkey -> get_burn_uid())
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: The archived validator is a TempValidator whose run loop sets uids=[burn_uid], weights=[1.0],
      i.e. a full emission burn. The historical loss/win-rate/softmax mechanism remains in the file but
      is commented out.
  notes: 'DESIGNED aggregation: epsilon-adjusted pairwise win rate -> softmax(win_rate / temperature).
    EMA smoothing of the final reward (alpha 0.5) is recorded under weight_setting.smoothing.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: continuous
  method: 'Designed: validators continuously re-evaluate models and set weights from smoothed win rates.
    Active: the TempValidator loops, computes the burn UID, and calls set_weights([burn_uid],[1.0]) every
    set_weights_interval blocks.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: set_weights_interval blocks (waits set_weights_interval * BLOCK_TIME between sets)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.5
    window: null
    notes: Documented EMA over win-rate scores (constants.alpha = 0.5).
anti_gaming:
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Models are committed to chain as metadata (hotkey + HF repo + block); only committed models
    are evaluated. Earlier commitment earns an epsilon loss advantage, discouraging copying a leader's
    weights (a copy commits later and is penalized).
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Model constraints enforced per competition (parameter-size band, sequence_length, max_bytes)
    via MODEL_CONSTRAINTS_BY_COMPETITION_ID; out-of-spec models are not valid.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: B3_MODEL competition
    role: main
    advancement_rule: share of subnet weight set by COMPETITION_SCHEDULE_BY_BLOCK
    multipliers: {}
  - name: B14_MODEL competition
    role: main
    advancement_rule: share of subnet weight set by COMPETITION_SCHEDULE_BY_BLOCK
    multipliers: {}
  count: null
  notes: Multiple model-size competitions run concurrently with per-dataset weights; subnet weights are
    the merged values across active competitions (per the commented competition_tracker logic).
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - registration_age
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: block-of-commitment recorded for epsilon advantage; EMA smooths scores over time
  notes: Softmax temperature = 0.01 (constants.temperature); EMA alpha = 0.5 (constants.alpha).
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/validator.md
  - docs/miner.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8
  extracted_at: '2026-06-05'
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 25-31
    quote: Bittensor subnet 9 rewards miners for producing pretrained Foundation-Models on the Falcon
      Refined Web dataset. It acts like a continuous benchmark whereby miners are rewarded for attaining
      the best losses on randomly sampled pages
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0
    source_path: pretrain/validation.py
    line_ref: 39-72
    quote: 'def iswin(loss_i, loss_j, ...): # Adjust loss based on timestamp and epsilon. ... return loss_i
      < loss_j'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/alpha
    source_path: constants/__init__.py
    line_ref: '543'
    quote: alpha = 0.5
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/temperature
    source_path: constants/__init__.py
    line_ref: 544-546
    quote: '# validator scoring exponential temperature

      temperature = 0.01'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method_other
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 1160-1164
    quote: '# Compute softmaxed weights based on win rate.

      # step_weights = torch.softmax(model_weights / constants.temperature, dim=0)'
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 1649-1662
    quote: uids = [burn_uid] ... weights = [1.0] ... success, message = self.subtensor.set_weights(self.wallet,
      self.config.netuid, uids, weights, ...)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '37'
    quote: Finding the best model and delta at the earliest block ensures the most incentive.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions
    source_path: constants/__init__.py
    line_ref: 194-285
    quote: 'COMPETITION_SCHEDULE_BY_BLOCK: List[Tuple[int, List[Competition]]] = [ ... MODEL_CONSTRAINTS_BY_COMPETITION_ID_TMP[CompetitionId.B3_MODEL]
      ... CompetitionId.B14_MODEL ...'
    confidence: high
  unresolved:
  - 'Active vs documented divergence is now captured by mechanism_status: full_burn (resolved in schema
    1.0.0).'
  extractor_notes: 'Strong stress case: the operative mechanism (full burn) differs from the documented/designed
    mechanism (epsilon-adjusted pairwise win-rate -> softmax -> EMA). Captured the designed mechanism
    in structured fields and the active burn in aggregation.burn_allocation. Numeric constants (alpha=0.5,
    temperature=0.01) taken verbatim from constants; softmax weighting cited from commented code + README,
    hence medium confidence on method_other.'
composition:
  shape: multiplex
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Subnet 1: Apex
docs.text:
Subnet macrocosm-os__prompting netuid 1 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status unknown Task Apex is a competition-based subnet. Miners submit Python-based algorithms (a "solution file", optionally with binary artifacts) to a specific Competition via the Apex CLI, paying an on-chain submission fee. Each Competition runs multiple Rounds. An off-chain orchestrator (apex.api.macrocosmos.ai) ex DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Collateral, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: RankBased"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: subnet1Apex

    netuid: 1
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: source_code

    @burn {}
    @guards {
        Collateral {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, status_enum, registration_age }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: Metric {
            kind: other
            raw: "per-competition scalar score from sandboxed execution of the submitted algorithm (e.g. simulated total_profit for energy_arbitrage; fewer turns for RL Battleship)"
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: rank
        }
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: RankBased {
            normalization: other
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: continuous
            tempo: "WEIGHT_SUBMIT_INTERVAL = 60*21 (every 21 minutes) in production"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
subnet:
  name: 'Subnet 1: Apex'
  owner_repo: macrocosm-os__prompting
  netuid: 1
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Apex is a competition-based subnet. Miners submit Python-based algorithms (a "solution file",
    optionally with binary artifacts) to a specific Competition via the Apex CLI, paying an on-chain submission
    fee. Each Competition runs multiple Rounds. An off-chain orchestrator (apex.api.macrocosmos.ai) executes
    each submission in sandboxes against the competition''s deterministic, seed-generated challenge instances
    and produces a single scalar score per submission, forming a per-competition leaderboard. Validators
    in this repo do NOT score: they poll the orchestrator for "global miner scores" (already a weight
    per UID) and submit them on-chain. Concrete competitions include energy arbitrage (score = simulated
    total profit, higher is better), RL Battleship (sink ships in as few turns as possible, lower is better),
    and an Iota distributed-training simulator. Per-competition emissions are scaled by an incentive_weight
    and reduced by a burn_factor, with a base (minimum) burn rate.'
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: competition_score
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: per-competition scalar score from sandboxed execution of the submitted algorithm
    (e.g. simulated total_profit for energy_arbitrage; fewer turns for RL Battleship)
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: rank
  notes: 'The orchestrator produces one scalar score per submission and ranks miners into a leaderboard
    (MinerRankRecord.rank / top_scorer). The semantics of the raw score are competition-specific: for
    energy_arbitrage higher total_profit is better; for RL Battleship fewer turns is better. The orchestrator
    that computes the raw score and converts it to a leaderboard/weight is a remote API not present in
    this repo, so the cross-competition normalization beyond "rank-based leaderboard with a top_scorer"
    is unresolved.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Each competition generates challenge instances deterministically from a seed (and a hidden_seed
    not visible to the submitted policy). Scoring is the objective outcome of running the submitted algorithm
    against these generated instances inside a sandbox (e.g. simulated profit accumulated over the rollout
    for energy_arbitrage).
  source_identifier: competition.<name> challenge generators (e.g. energy_arbitrage/python/challenge.py
    generate_instance)
  trust_model: trusted
aggregation:
  method: rank_based
  normalization: other
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    notes: Per-competition emissions = (1 - burn_factor) * incentive_weight; the remainder is burned.
      burn_factor has a configured floor base_burn_rate (DEFAULT_BASE_BURN_RATE = 0.9), shown in the CLI
      as "min burn". The exact live burn_factor value and the recipient of the burned emission are set
      by the off-chain orchestrator (not in this repo), so fraction/address are unresolved.
  notes: 'Normalization is "other": per-competition incentive_weight is a direct decimal allocation (0.0-1.0)
    scaled by (1 - burn_factor); the per-UID weights are provided by the orchestrator. Validators receive
    an already-computed per-UID weight from the orchestrator and submit it via set_weights; on failure
    they copy a stake-weighted average of existing on-chain weights.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: continuous
  method: Validator weight_loop polls the orchestrator (get_global_miner_scores) for a SubnetScores object
    containing {uid, weight} per miner, then calls set_weights. If no scores are available or on timeout/error
    it falls back to copy_weights_from_chain (stake-weighted average of validator weights). Loops every
    WEIGHT_SUBMIT_INTERVAL seconds.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: WEIGHT_SUBMIT_INTERVAL = 60*21 (every 21 minutes) in production
anti_gaming:
- kind: collateral
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Submitting requires an on-chain submission fee (amount_rao / fee_usd) paid via transfer_keep_alive
    to SUBMISSION_FEE_ADDRESS; the orchestrator verifies the payment receipt before accepting the submission.
    This acts as an anti-spam / sybil barrier on submissions.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Submitted algorithms run in sandboxes (SandboxRunRules) and receive only a restricted PolicyView,
    not the full Challenge, so they cannot access the seed or RT-price generation; the hidden_seed used
    to drive the rollout is withheld from the policy. Challenge instances are deterministic from seeds.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: 'The subnet runs multiple independent Competitions in parallel, each with its own package (pkg),
    process/competition type (ptype/ctype), deterministic challenge, leaderboard, incentive_weight and
    burn_factor. Each Competition is divided into Rounds (RoundRecord.round_number). Observed competitions
    in-repo: energy_arbitrage, battleship, rl_battleship, iota_simulator, tron, matrix_compression. The
    exact set and per-competition incentive_weight values are owned by the off-chain orchestrator and
    not pinned in this repo.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - status_enum
  - registration_age
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: burn_factor_reset_at marks when the burn factor was reset (tied to a new top scorer /
    "Age" of the current top scorer). Per-miner best score and rank persist across submissions within
    a competition.
  notes: Per miner the orchestrator tracks best score, leaderboard rank, top_scorer flag, submission version,
    submissions_count and join_date (MinerRankRecord/SubmissionRankMiner). A miner is "top_scorer" if
    its current submission is the competition winner. Submissions must beat a score_to_beat to take the
    top spot.
documentation:
  status: docs_authoritative
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/sandbox-startup-config.md
  - https://docs.macrocosmos.ai/subnets/new-subnet-1-apex/incentive-mechanism
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract
  confidence_overall: medium
  extractor_notes: This is the newer "Apex" competition architecture, not the classic SN1 prompting/reward-model
    LLM-inference subnet described in the task brief. The actual scoring engine (orchestrator) is a remote
    API (apex.api.macrocosmos.ai) and is NOT in this repo; only the validator (which forwards orchestrator
    weights on-chain), the miner-facing CLI, the per-competition challenge simulators, and shared API
    models are present. Reward-pipeline / leaderboard construction internals are therefore inferred from
    data models and CLI render logic, not from the scoring source.
  unresolved:
  - Exact per-competition incentive_weight values and the live burn_factor (orchestrator-owned, not in
    repo).
  - The recipient address/UID of burned emissions and the precise burn fraction beyond the 0.9 base_burn_rate
    floor.
  - 'Cross-competition aggregation: how per-competition leaderboards combine into the single per-UID global
    weight returned to validators.'
  - 'Decay rate / min weight floor: no such constant present in this repo.'
  - Whether any LLM/reference-model judging is used (none found in this repo; competitions are deterministic
    simulations/games).
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md

docs.title: Hone (ARC-AGI-2 Benchmarking)
docs.text:
Subnet manifold-inc__hone netuid 5 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners expose an /info endpoint pointing to a git repository containing an ARC-AGI-2 solver. Validators submit the repo to a Sandbox Runner that clones, builds a Docker image, runs a prep phase (internet on) and an isolated inference phase (internet off) over held-out ARC problems, then computes met DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: RateLimit, DeterministicCheck, StakeWeighting"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  AGG["aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: honeArcAgi2Benchmarking

    netuid: 5
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: source_code

    @burn {
        uid: "251"
        fraction: 0.95
    }
    @guards {
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, cooldown }

    Pipeline {
        score: Metric {
            kind: accuracy
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
            decay_rate: 0.8
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "cycle_duration+5 blocks (default CYCLE_DURATION=30 => 35 blocks)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: set_weights() always allocates BURN_PERCENTAGE (default 0.95) to BURN_UID (default
  251) and distributes only MINER_PERCENTAGE (default 0.05) to qualifying top miners. If no miner meets
  the accuracy floor, 100% is burned. So the live mechanism is a 95% burn / 5% scored split, escalating
  to full burn when no miner qualifies.
subnet:
  name: Hone (ARC-AGI-2 Benchmarking)
  owner_repo: manifold-inc__hone
  netuid: 5
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners expose an /info endpoint pointing to a git repository containing an ARC-AGI-2 solver.
    Validators submit the repo to a Sandbox Runner that clones, builds a Docker image, runs a prep phase
    (internet on) and an isolated inference phase (internet off) over held-out ARC problems, then computes
    metrics. Miners are scored solely on exact_match_rate (fraction of ARC problems solved exactly); the
    top miners above an accuracy floor split a small non-burned reward fraction via exponential rank decay.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: exact_match_rate
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Only exact_match_rate is used to score and rank miners. The sandbox also computes partial_correctness,
    grid_similarity and efficiency (and a constants.py SCORING_WEIGHTS map exact_match=0.5/partial=0.3/grid=0.15/efficiency=0.05),
    but the validator's calculate_scores_and_update_leaderboard only reads exact_match_rate.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Held-out ARC-AGI-2 problems with expected outputs, generated daily by the sandbox runner
    and never exposed to miners. exact_match is computed as predicted_output == expected_output per problem.
  source_identifier: sandbox_runner synthetics / miner_current_dataset.json
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Validation dataset with expected outputs never exposed to miners (README Security Notes).
aggregation:
  method: exponential_decay_ema
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: 0.8
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '251'
    fraction: 0.95
    dynamic: false
    notes: BURN_PERCENTAGE default 0.95 to BURN_UID default 251 (both env-overridable). Miners share the
      remaining 0.05. If no miner qualifies, all_weights[BURN_UID]=1.0 (100% burn).
  notes: Top-N qualifying miners (TOP_MINERS_COUNT default 5) are ranked by avg exact_match_rate; each
    rank r gets weight exp(-0.8*r), normalized to sum to one, then scaled by MINER_PERCENTAGE (0.05).
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Every weights cycle (gated by weights_interval_blocks = cycle_duration+5 and an on-chain can_set_weights
    rate-limit check) the validator computes top-N exponential weights, sets BURN_PERCENTAGE on the burn
    UID and distributes MINER_PERCENTAGE across qualifying miners.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: cycle_duration+5 blocks (default CYCLE_DURATION=30 => 35 blocks)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA on final weights. Scoring uses a lookback window score_window_blocks = cycle_duration*4
      over recent DB results, but weights are recomputed fresh each cycle, not smoothed.
anti_gaming:
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: rejection
  description: MAX_SUBMISSIONS_PER_DAY (default 1) limits evaluations per miner per day; identical repo+branch+commit
    reuse cached scores (no redundant evaluation).
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Inference phase runs with network disabled (network_mode: none), dropped capabilities
    (CAP_SYS_ADMIN, CAP_NET_ADMIN, etc.) and no-new-privileges; held-out validation dataset never exposed
    to miners.'
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Validators must have sufficient stake and pass can_set_weights rate limiting before weights
    are accepted on chain (README Troubleshooting: "Weights not setting - Ensure sufficient stake").'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - cooldown
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Recent results kept for RETENTION_DAYS (default 30); leaderboard holds current top-N and
    removes miners that drop out of the top N. Daily submission limit resets per day.
  notes: A leaderboard (top performers) and per-miner query results are persisted in PostgreSQL; scoring
    aggregates over a recent block window.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: validator/.version (1.0.0 per README /info example)
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 206-213
    quote: '"If miners qualify: 95% burn + 5% distributed to top miners (exponential) - If no miners qualify:
      100% burn" / BURN_PERCENTAGE = float(os.getenv("BURN_PERCENTAGE", "0.95"))'
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: common/constants.py
    line_ref: '3'
    quote: NETUID_MAINNET = 5
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 1-6
    quote: from typing import Dict, List, Tuple / from common.chain import can_set_weights / import os
      / import math
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 26-30
    quote: '"Miners expose an HTTP endpoint (/info) ... Validators fetch miner info, submit jobs to a
      Sandbox Runner ... clones the miner''s repo, builds a Docker image, runs prep ... and inference
      ... then calculates metrics ... Validators aggregate exact_match_rate scores"'
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 26-26
    quote: Miners expose an HTTP endpoint (/info) that returns a pointer to their solution repository
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/name
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 14-14
    quote: Calculate scores based on exact_match_rate only
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: common/constants.py
    line_ref: 19-24
    quote: 'SCORING_WEIGHTS = {"exact_match": 0.5, "partial_correctness": 0.3, "grid_similarity": 0.15,
      "efficiency": 0.05}'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description
    source_path: sandbox_runner/utils/metrics.py
    line_ref: 110-110
    quote: exact_match = predicted_output == expected_output
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/notes
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 699-699
    quote: Validation dataset (with expected outputs) never exposed to miners
  - claim_field: /aggregation/decay_rate
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 161-165
    quote: 'decay_factor = 0.8 / for rank in range(len(top_miners)): exponential_weights.append(math.exp(-decay_factor
      * rank))'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 167-168
    quote: total_weight = sum(exponential_weights) / normalized_weights = [w / total_weight for w in exponential_weights]
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 211-213
    quote: BURN_UID = int(os.getenv("BURN_UID", "251")) / BURN_PERCENTAGE = float(os.getenv("BURN_PERCENTAGE",
      "0.95")) / MINER_PERCENTAGE = 1.0 - BURN_PERCENTAGE
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 225-230
    quote: 'if not has_qualifying_miners: all_weights[BURN_UID] = 1.0 ... BURN MODE - No miners meet minimum
      requirements'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 232-239
    quote: all_weights[BURN_UID] = BURN_PERCENTAGE / normalized_miner_weight = (weight / miner_weight_sum)
      * MINER_PERCENTAGE
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 265-265
    quote: result = chain.set_weights(uids=all_uids, weights=all_weights)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/cadence
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 282-297
    quote: '"Check if the validator can set weights based on rate limiting" / can_set = can_set_weights(chain.substrate,
      chain.netuid, chain.validator_uid)'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: validator/config.py
    line_ref: 98-101
    quote: 'def weights_interval_blocks(self) -> int: "Minimum blocks between weight settings" return
      self.cycle_duration + 5'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes
    source_path: validator/config.py
    line_ref: 103-106
    quote: 'def score_window_blocks(self) -> int: "Look back window for scoring (e.g., last 4 cycles)"
      return self.cycle_duration * 4'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: validator/config.py
    line_ref: 84-84
    quote: 'max_submissions_per_day: int = int(os.getenv("MAX_SUBMISSIONS_PER_DAY", "1"))'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 696-698
    quote: '"Inference phase runs with network disabled (network_mode: none) - Capabilities dropped: CAP_SYS_ADMIN,
      CAP_NET_ADMIN, CAP_SYS_MODULE, CAP_SYS_PTRACE, CAP_SYS_RAWIO - No new privileges flag enabled"'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 633-633
    quote: Weights not setting - Ensure sufficient stake - Check rate limiting (must wait between weight
      updates)
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/state_kinds
    source_path: validator/scoring.py
    line_ref: 150-159
    quote: '"remove miners from leaderboard who are no longer in top N" / for hotkey in leaderboard_hotkeys
      - top_hotkeys: ... await db.remove_from_leaderboard(hotkey)'
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: validator/config.py

docs.title: Targon
docs.text:
Subnet manifold-inc__targon netuid 4 Archetype multiplex Language go Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners run confidential-compute (CC/PPCIE) GPU and CPU nodes (NVIDIA Hopper/Blackwell via Intel TDX, AMD EPYC SEV-SNP) that serve secure attestable inference hardware. Each miner reports node IPs and a per-GPU-hour bid price. Validators collect remote attestations from each node and verify them via DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: HardwareProbe"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: HardwareValidation, Deduplication, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: other"]
  M1["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: targon

    netuid: 4
    lang: go
    status: partial_burn
    submission: compute_service

    @burn {
        uid: "uids 28, 15, 243"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: hardware_authenticity
                specific: remote_attestation
                raw: "confidential_compute_remote_attestation_pass"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                raw: "demand_driven_auction_emission_share"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: HardwareProbe {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: gated
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "360 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: 'Miner emissions are split proportionally to public demand via off-chain auctions
  (tower API). Any emission not allocated to winning auction bids is burned across three randomized burn
  keys (uids 28, 15, 243). Per miner.md: "The remainder of emissions not allocated to auctions is burned."'
subnet:
  name: Targon
  owner_repo: manifold-inc__targon
  netuid: 4
  implementation_lang: go
task:
  summary: Miners run confidential-compute (CC/PPCIE) GPU and CPU nodes (NVIDIA Hopper/Blackwell via Intel
    TDX, AMD EPYC SEV-SNP) that serve secure attestable inference hardware. Each miner reports node IPs
    and a per-GPU-hour bid price. Validators collect remote attestations from each node and verify them
    via the tower attestation service; verified nodes enter demand-driven auctions keyed by hardware type.
    Emission is paid as the percentage of the USD emission pool a miner's winning bids represent; unallocated
    emission is burned.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: attestation_validity
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: confidential_compute_remote_attestation_pass
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Gating signal. A node only enters the auction if its remote attestation verifies via the tower
    /verify-attestation endpoint (attestResponse.Valid) and the nonce matches; duplicate CVM node ids
    are rejected. Nodes below the auction's min_cluster_size GPU count are excluded.
- name: auction_bid_payout
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: demand_driven_auction_emission_share
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Per verified node, per-miner USD payout = min(pool/nodes, MaxPrice) where pool = TargetPrice
    * TargetCards for that auction. Miner incentive (emission share) = (perMiner * 1.2 * count) / (EmissionPool
    * 100). This share is multiplied by U16MAX (65535) and floored to produce the on-chain weight.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: Remote attestation of confidential-compute GPU/CPU hardware. Validator requests an attestation
    quote from each miner node (/api/v1/evidence) and verifies it against the tower attestation service
    (/api/v1/verify-attestation). GPU/switch remote attestation results carried in NVCCResponse.
  source_identifier: tower.targon.com (verify-attestation, auctions APIs)
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: 'The tower service is an off-chain trusted oracle: it returns the auction definitions (target
    cards/price, max price, min cluster size), the burn distribution, the tao price, and the attestation
    verdict. Auction allocation reflects "public demand".'
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: gated
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: uids 28, 15, 243
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: forBurn = max(U16MAX - sumScores, 0); distributed across burn keys by c.BurnDistribution (uid
      -> percent, supplied by the tower auction API). Remaining rounding remainder added to the last burn
      key. burnKeys = [28, 15, 243], "randomized across 3 keys for WC combat".
  notes: Per-uid payout percentages are summed across auctions, scaled by U16MAX and floored. No on-chain
    normalization is done by the validator ("we are sending weights via the bittensor sdk which is going
    to normalize anyways"). The 1.2 multiplier and the *100 / EmissionPool divisor are literal in getWeights;
    the emission pool itself is computed as (alphaOut/1e9)*0.41*360*taoPrice*movingPrice.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: On the block where getBlocksFrom(h)==1 within each tempo interval (tempo=360), the validator
    computes auction payouts via getWeights and POSTs uids/weights to the local weights-api, which calls
    subtensor.set_weights with wait_for_finalization. Skipped if no miner nodes or within 180 blocks of
    validator startup.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 360 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of the final weights in the validator; scores are recomputed each interval
      from current verified nodes and auction state (resetState clears state after each set).
anti_gaming:
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Remote confidential-compute attestation is mandatory; a node's hardware must verify via
    the tower service (attestResponse.Valid) and the attestation nonce must match. Failed or invalid attestation
    excludes the node from the auction.
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Duplicate CVM node ids are rejected: if c.NodeIds[userData.CVMID] is already seen, the
    node errors with "duplicate node id found" and is not counted, preventing one physical node from being
    claimed by multiple miners/IPs.'
- kind: other
  kind_other: minimum_cluster_size_requirement
  enforcement: rejection
  description: For auctions with MinClusterSize != 0, a node must report at least that many GPU cards
    or it is marked with an error ("Node reporting N cards, M required") and excluded.
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Multiple independent auctions keyed by hardware type (e.g. SEV-CPU-AMD-EPYC-V4, TDX-NVCC-NVIDIA-H200),
    each with its own target_cards, target_price, max_price and min_cluster_size. A node is routed to
    its auction by userData.AuctionName. Auction set is supplied dynamically by the tower API, so the
    count is not fixed in source.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: 'State is rebuilt each interval: resetState(c) runs after every setWeights; VerifiedNodes,
    MinerErrors, EmissionPool and auction data are recollected per interval.'
  notes: No persistent per-miner ledger/reputation affecting scoring; verified-node and error maps are
    per-interval working state, not accumulated history.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/miner/miner.md
  - docs/validator/validator.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: targon/internal/callbacks/weights.go
    line_ref: 1-16
    quote: package callbacks ... import ( ... "targon/internal/setup" "targon/internal/targon" )
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: weights-api/main.py
    line_ref: '27'
    quote: NETUID = int(os.getenv("NETUID", "4"))
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: docs/miner/miner.md
    line_ref: 291-292
    quote: '"nodes": [{"ip":"0.0.0.0", "price": 120}, {"ip":"0.0.1.1", "price": 220}]'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: targon/internal/cvm/cvm.go
    line_ref: 171-176
    quote: if !attestResponse.Valid { return nil, utils.Wrap("attestation invalid", attestResponse.Error)
      } return &attestRes.UserData, nil
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: targon/internal/callbacks/weights.go
    line_ref: 122-125
    quote: if auc.MinClusterSize != 0 && auc.MinClusterSize > len(*c.VerifiedNodes[uid][n.IP].GPUCards)
      { c.MinerErrors[uid][n.IP] = fmt.Sprintf("Node reporting %d cards, %d required" ...) continue }
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: targon/internal/callbacks/weights.go

docs.title: Masa Subnet 42 (Real-Time Data Scraping)
docs.text:
Subnet masa-finance__masa-bittensor netuid 42 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners extract recent/trending tweets from X (Twitter) using the Masa SDK in response to validator-issued keyword queries. Validators check each returned batch for structural validity, ID validity, deduplication, query-keyword match, recency (timestamp within ~yesterday), and existence via masa-ai's DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: OrganicTraffic"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, DeterministicCheck, LivenessCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: volume"]
  AGG["aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: masaSubnet42RealTimeDataScraping

    netuid: 42
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: api_response

    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, performance_ledger }

    Pipeline {
        score: Metric {
            kind: volume
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: zscore
        }
        groundTruth: OrganicTraffic {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "epoch_length default 100 blocks; set_weights gated on >100 blocks since last_update"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: 'Active volume-based scoring: validators query miners for tweets, validate them,
  accumulate per-miner tweet volume across a rolling window, score via a kurtosis (normal-CDF z-score)
  curve, EMA the scores, and set_weights on chain. No burn logic present in source.'
subnet:
  name: Masa Subnet 42 (Real-Time Data Scraping)
  owner_repo: masa-finance__masa-bittensor
  netuid: 42
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners extract recent/trending tweets from X (Twitter) using the Masa SDK in response to validator-issued
    keyword queries. Validators check each returned batch for structural validity, ID validity, deduplication,
    query-keyword match, recency (timestamp within ~yesterday), and existence via masa-ai's TweetValidator.
    Each miner's reward is driven by its accumulated volume of valid, unique tweets over a rolling window
    of tempos.
  submission_format:
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: valid_tweet_volume
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: zscore
  notes: Accumulated count of valid, unique tweets per miner over the volume_window of tempos. Reward
    = stats.norm.cdf((volume - mean)/std_dev) (kurtosis-based score). Only volume that passes all validation
    checks is counted.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: organic_traffic
  description: Trending Twitter queries fetched via masa_ai TrendingQueries (top 10), with fallback keywords
    ["crypto","btc","eth"]; tweets are validated for existence against the live Twitter/X API through
    masa-ai's TweetValidator.
  source_identifier: masa_ai.tools.validator (TrendingQueries, TweetValidator)
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Tweet existence/authenticity is checked against the real Twitter API (404 handling); queries
    derive from real-time trending topics.
aggregation:
  method: exponential_decay_ema
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn logic found in source.
  notes: Per-round rewards are the normal-CDF z-score of each miner's windowed volume (single miner =>
    reward 1). These are EMA'd into self.scores (update_scores), then passed raw to process_weights_for_netuid
    which applies max-weight normalization and sum-to-one normalization before emit. With one miner the
    reward list is [1].
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: 'self.scores (EMA of per-round rewards) -> process_weights_for_netuid (max-weight + sum-to-one
    normalization) -> convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit -> subtensor.set_weights. Gated by should_set_weights:
    requires >=150 scored UIDs on mainnet and >100 blocks since last update (except first run).'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: epoch_length default 100 blocks; set_weights gated on >100 blocks since last_update
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: 'update_scores: scores[mask] = alpha*reward + (1-alpha)*scores[mask], alpha = config.neuron.moving_average_alpha
      (default 0.1). Volume itself is summed over a rolling window of volume_window=6 tempos before scoring.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Within-batch duplicate detection (unique tweet IDs must equal batch size) and cross-history
    dedup via tweets_by_uid: previously seen tweet IDs are subtracted so only new unique tweets add to
    volume.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: strict_tweet_id_validation (pure ASCII digits, no leading zeros, no zero-width chars),
    query-keyword match across tweet fields, and timestamp recency (>= yesterday) checks; any failure
    invalidates the whole batch.
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: masa-ai TweetValidator validates 3 randomly sampled tweets against the live Twitter API
    (requires >=2/3 successful validations); 404s count as failures.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Miners selected for scoring are those with metagraph.validator_trust == 0; validators require
    minimum stake / vpermit to be queried.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - performance_ledger
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: On hotkey replacement (resync_metagraph), the miner's score is zeroed, its recent windowed
    volumes set to 0, and tweets_by_uid reset to empty set.
  notes: 'Per-miner persistent state: self.scores (EMA), self.volumes (rolling window of per-tempo volume),
    self.tweets_by_uid (set of seen tweet IDs, capped 100,000). State saved to state.pt; scores can be
    rebuilt from scores.log.'
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/RELEASE_NOTES.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '3'
    quote: 'We are subnet #42 on mainnet and #165 on testnet.'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang

    confidence: high

    line_ref: 585-594

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/normalization
    source_path: masa/validator/scorer.py
    line_ref: 195-199
    quote: 'def kurtosis_based_score(self, volume, mean, std_dev, scale_factor=1.0): ... z_score = (volume
      - mean) / std_dev return stats.norm.cdf(z_score) * scale_factor'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: masa/validator/scorer.py
    line_ref: 64-76
    quote: window_size = min(self.validator.volume_window, len(volumes)) ... miner_volumes[miner_uid_str]
      += vol
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: masa/validator/forwarder.py
    line_ref: '41'
    quote: from masa_ai.tools.validator import TrendingQueries, TweetValidator
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description
    source_path: masa/validator/forwarder.py
    line_ref: 269-277
    quote: trending_queries = TrendingQueries().fetch() ... self.validator.keywords = ["crypto", "btc",

  - claim_field: /aggregation/method

    quote: 'if len(valid_miner_uids) == 1: rewards = [1] else: ... reward = self.kurtosis_based_score(volume,

  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call

  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method

  - claim_field: /weight_setting/cadence

      ... return False'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
