docs.title: Natix StreetVision (SN72)
docs.text:
Subnet natixnetwork__streetvision-subnet netuid 72 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status full_burn Task Miners run an image-classification model that predicts whether an image contains roadwork / construction-site elements (label 1.0 = roadwork, 0.0 = no roadwork), returning a probability in [0,1]. Validators score the classification quality over a sliding window. (Currently the subnet is halted and t DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, Other, StakeWeighting"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: accuracy"]
  M2["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: natixStreetVisionSn72

    netuid: 72
    lang: python
    status: full_burn
    submission: signals

    @burn {
        uid: "subnet owner UID (owner_hotkey index in metagraph)"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: classification_quality
                specific: validity_gate
                raw: "validity_gate"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.05
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: full_burn
mechanism_status_notes: 'The live validator is halted. forward() early-returns ("Subnet halted: skipping
  challenge forward pass.") so no miners are queried or scored, and set_weights() bypasses all (commented-out)
  score normalization to direct 100% weight (uint16 65535) to the subnet owner''s UID, burning all miner
  emissions. The documented classification scoring (MCC + accuracy with reward-curve exponent) still exists
  in the code but is dead/dormant.'
subnet:
  name: Natix StreetVision (SN72)
  owner_repo: natixnetwork__streetvision-subnet
  netuid: 72
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners run an image-classification model that predicts whether an image contains roadwork /
    construction-site elements (label 1.0 = roadwork, 0.0 = no roadwork), returning a probability in [0,1].
    Validators score the classification quality over a sliding window. (Currently the subnet is halted
    and this task is not executed.)
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: mcc_window_100
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Matthews correlation coefficient over the last 100 predictions (clamped to >=0). Half of the
    per-step reward: reward = 0.5*metrics_100["mcc"] + 0.5*metrics_10["accuracy"]. Dormant (forward()
    halted).'
- name: accuracy_window_10
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: none
  notes: Accuracy over the last 10 predictions. Other half of the per-step reward. Dormant.
- name: validity_penalty
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: validity_gate
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Multiplicative gate: compute_penalty(y_pred) returns 0.0 if prediction is outside [0,1] else
    1.0, multiplied into the reward. A prediction of -1 (no response) yields reward 0.0 directly. Dormant.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Labeled roadwork image dataset; the true label (1.0 roadwork / 0.0 not) is known to the
    validator and compared against miner predictions. Challenges also sourced from an API and synthetic
    image caches.
  source_identifier: natix-network-org/roadwork (HuggingFace)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: 'IMAGE_DATASETS["Roadwork"] = [{"path": "natix-network-org/roadwork"}]. Synthetic t2i/i2i caches
    also used. Ground-truth path is dormant while halted.'
aggregation:
  method: exponential_decay_ema
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: subnet owner UID (owner_hotkey index in metagraph)
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: set_weights() directs the full weight vector (uids=[owner_uid], weights=[65535]) to the owner
      UID, burning all miner emissions. Owner's 18% cut and validator dividends are chain-level and unaffected.
  notes: 'Documented (dormant) aggregation: per-step reward = (0.5*MCC100 + 0.5*accuracy10) * validity_penalty,
    then raised to REWARD_CURVE_EXPONENT (3.0); scores updated via EMA (alpha 0.05). Live aggregation
    is full burn.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: 'Halted/burn: 100% weight to owner UID. Documented path (commented out) would normalize EMA
    scores (L1 norm) and call process_weights_for_netuid.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: null
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.05
    window: null
    notes: update_scores applies scores = alpha*scattered_rewards + (1-alpha)*scores with moving_average_alpha
      default 0.05. Dormant while halted.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: compute_penalty rejects out-of-range predictions (y_pred<0 or >1 -> 0.0); a -1 (non-response)
    yields reward 0.0.
- kind: other
  kind_other: hotkey_change_reset
  enforcement: penalty
  description: If a UID's hotkey changes, its performance history is reset (reset_miner_history), wiping
    accumulated MCC/accuracy windows.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: vpermit_tao_limit (default 40096) gates which validators may query; in update_scores validator
    UIDs above the limit are zeroed from rewards.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Per-UID sliding deques of last 100 predictions/labels (MinerPerformanceTracker), persisted
    to image_miner_performance_tracker.pkl. Reset on hotkey change.
  notes: Metrics computed over window 100 (MCC) and window 10 (accuracy). Dormant while forward() is halted.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - docs/incentive.md
  - docs/architecture.md
  - README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: natix/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 335-353
    quote: '# Subnet is halted: burn all miner rewards by directing 100% weight to the subnet owner''s
      UID. ... result, msg = self.subtensor.set_weights(..., uids=[owner_uid], weights=[65535], ...)'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: natix/validator/scoring/reward.py
    line_ref: 41-55
    quote: 'label: The true label (1.0 for roadwork, 0.0 for no roadwork) ... responses: List of responses
      from the miners'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: natix/validator/scoring/reward.py
    line_ref: 87-90
    quote: reward = 0.5 * metrics_100["mcc"] + 0.5 * metrics_10["accuracy"] reward *= compute_penalty(pred_prob)
      reward = reward ** REWARD_CURVE_EXPONENT
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: natix/validator/scoring/performance_tracker.py
    line_ref: 78-80
    quote: mcc = max(0, matthews_corrcoef(labels, predictions)) ... metrics_100 = tracker[modality].get_metrics(uid,
      window=100)
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: natix/validator/scoring/reward.py
    line_ref: 80-81
    quote: metrics_100 = tracker[modality].get_metrics(uid, window=100) metrics_10 = tracker[modality].get_metrics(uid,
      window=10)
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: natix/validator/scoring/reward.py
    line_ref: 37-38
    quote: bad = (y_pred < 0.0) or (y_pred > 1.0); return 0.0 if bad else 1.0
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: natix/validator/scoring/reward.py
    line_ref: 84-85
    quote: 'if pred_prob == -1: reward = 0.0'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: natix/constants.py
    line_ref: 41-45
    quote: '"Roadwork": [ {"path": f"{HUGGINGFACE_REPO}/roadwork"} ]'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction

  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes

  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes

      + (1 - alpha) * self.scores'

      challenge forward pass."); return'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description

  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description

docs.title: Nepher Robotics Subnet
docs.text:
Subnet nepher-ai__nepher-subnet netuid 49 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners train robotics control policies locally and submit signed agents (a best_policy.pt checkpoint plus a task module) to a tournament backend. Validators download each eligible agent and run it against standardized Isaac Lab environments in a sandboxed Docker container, producing a score that is DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: StakeWeighting, Deduplication, CodeInspection"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: reward"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: nepherRoboticsSubnet

    netuid: 49
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: [model_weights, source_code]

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: other
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, registration_age }

    Multiplex<tournament> {
        score: Metric {
            kind: reward
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_tournament
            tempo: 1800
        }
        tracks {
            structure: tournament
            count: 5
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: Winner-take-all on chain only during the (episodic) reward period; during contest,
  submit, evaluation, review, and any idle/no-tournament time the validator burns 100% on UID 0 (with
  an optional 1% to the preliminary leader). The actual per-agent scoring and stake-weighted aggregation
  are performed by an off-repo tournament backend API, so the ranking math is not present in this source
  tree.
subnet:
  name: Nepher Robotics Subnet
  owner_repo: nepher-ai__nepher-subnet
  netuid: 49
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners train robotics control policies locally and submit signed agents (a best_policy.pt checkpoint
    plus a task module) to a tournament backend. Validators download each eligible agent and run it against
    standardized Isaac Lab environments in a sandboxed Docker container, producing a score that is submitted
    back to the backend. The single approved tournament winner receives all on-chain weight during the
    reward period; everything else burns.
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: agent_evaluation_score
  metric_kind: reward
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Each agent is run in a sandbox container against Isaac Lab environments and produces a single
    scalar 'score' returned by the sandbox runner and submitted to the backend (result['score']). The
    metric semantics (episodic task reward) are defined inside the agent/task module and the off-repo
    eval harness, not in this tree.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Standardized Isaac Lab simulation environments executed via eval-nav inside a no-network,
    no-wallet sandbox container. Acts as the reference environment for scoring submitted policies.
  source_identifier: Isaac Lab environments (eval-nav)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Sandbox has GPU access for Isaac Sim but no wallet, no Docker socket, and no network access except
    whitelisted domains.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: Outside the reward period (contest, submit, evaluation, review, idle/no-tournament) the validator
      burns 100% on UID 0; optionally 1% (LEADER_WEIGHT_FRACTION) is split to the preliminary leaderboard
      leader with the remaining 99% burned. If no winner is approved or the winner hotkey is absent from
      the metagraph, weight burns on UID 0.
  notes: Stake-weighted averaging of validator scores, descending rank, ties broken by earliest submission
    time, is documented as occurring in the off-repo backend; only the winner-takes-all weight assignment
    is implemented in this repo.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_tournament
  method: During the reward period the validator queries the approved winner hotkey from the tournament
    API, maps it to a metagraph UID, and sets all weight (1.0) to that UID via subtensor.set_weights,
    refreshing every WEIGHT_SET_INTERVAL (1800s / 30 min). A SHA-256 weight-hash dedup check skips redundant
    commits within DEDUP_WINDOW (900s). Outside the reward period weights burn on UID 0 every BURN_INTERVAL
    (1800s).
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 1800
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No on-chain weight smoothing; weight setting is winner-takes-all per refresh with wait_for_inclusion=True,
      wait_for_finalization=False.
anti_gaming:
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: other
  enforcement_other: aggregation_weighting
  description: Validator scores for an agent are aggregated by stake-weighted averaging in the backend
    (simple average fallback if no validator has stake). Documented in docs/incentive_mechanism.md; not
    implemented in this repo.
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Only the latest agent per miner is scored; eligible-miner list is snapshotted from the
    metagraph and locked before evaluation. On-chain weight commits are deduplicated via a SHA-256 weight
    hash within a 900s window across validators sharing a hotkey.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: elimination
  description: Admin team reviews aggregated scores and verifies the top submission for cheating before
    approving a winner; if not approved, all weight burns on UID 0.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Pre-flight model weight validation loads best_policy.pt in an isolated subprocess with
    a 120s timeout (MODEL_VALIDATION_TIMEOUT) and rejects files larger than 2048 MB (MAX_POLICY_SIZE_MB)
    or that fail to load.
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Evaluation runs inside a sandboxed Docker container with no wallet, no Docker socket, and
    no network access (whitelisted domains only); network/sandbox-escape errors are classified and reported
    to the backend.
sub_competitions:
  structure: tournament
  stages:
  - name: Contest Period
    role: qualifier
    advancement_rule: submit signed agent during contest/submit window
  - name: Submit Period
    role: qualifier
    advancement_rule: registered in metagraph AND submitted agent; list snapshotted and locked
  - name: Evaluation Period
    role: main
    advancement_rule: agent scored in Isaac Lab sandbox; score submitted to backend
  - name: Review Period
    role: knockout
    advancement_rule: admin reviews aggregated scores and approves winner (no cheating)
  - name: Reward Period
    role: final
    advancement_rule: approved winner receives all on-chain weight
  count: 5
  notes: Each tournament proceeds through five sequential block-bounded periods; emissions are directed
    to the winner only during the Reward Period. Period boundaries are defined by Bittensor block numbers
    converted to timestamps by the backend.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - registration_age
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Eligibility re-evaluated per tournament; eligible-miner list snapshotted and locked during
    the submit period.
  notes: Eligibility requires both on-chain registration (present in metagraph) and an agent submission
    during the contest/submit period. A miner deregistered before/during evaluation or reward still receives
    its reward (hotkey-based emission is mandatory).
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: Subnet 49 (Nepher)
  primary_docs:
  - docs/incentive_mechanism.md
  - README.md
  - docs/validator-guide.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: config/common_config.yaml
    line_ref: '9'
    quote: 'subnet_uid: 49'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 13-15
    quote: miner/    validator/ (submit)  (evaluate + set weights)
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: docs/incentive_mechanism.md
    line_ref: '20'
    quote: Validators download each eligible agent, install its task module, and run it against standardized
      Isaac Lab environments via `eval-nav`. Scores are submitted back to the tournament backend.
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 80-90
    quote: 'best_policy/ best_policy.pt  # Trained policy (required) ... source/ <task_module>/'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind
    source_path: validator/evaluation/agent_evaluator.py
    line_ref: '303'
    quote: 'logger.info(f"Sandbox evaluation score: {result.get(''score'', ''N/A'')}")'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description
    source_path: validator/evaluation/agent_evaluator.py
    line_ref: 270-276
    quote: 'The sandbox container: - Has GPU access for Isaac Sim - Has NO wallet access - Has NO Docker
      socket - Has NO network access - Can only write to the output directory'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: docs/incentive_mechanism.md
    line_ref: 28-30
    quote: '### Winner-Takes-All  Only a single top-performing miner receives weight (and thus emissions)
      per tournament.'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: validator/reward/weight_setter.py
    line_ref: '51'
    quote: 'BURN_UID = 0  # UID to burn to when no winner'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: validator/reward/weight_setter.py
    line_ref: '52'
    quote: 'LEADER_WEIGHT_FRACTION = 0.01  # 1% emission to preliminary leader'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: docs/incentive_mechanism.md
    line_ref: '38'
    quote: Outside the reward period — during contest, submit period, evaluation, and review — validators
      set weight to UID 0 (burn).
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: docs/incentive_mechanism.md
    line_ref: '34'
    quote: Validator scores are aggregated using stake-weighted averaging. ... Rankings are determined
      by aggregated score descending, with ties broken by earliest submission time.
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: validator/reward/weight_setter.py
    line_ref: '168'
    quote: await self._set_weights(winner_uid, metagraph)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: validator/reward/weight_setter.py
    line_ref: 302-309
    quote: success, message = subtensor.set_weights(wallet=wallet, netuid=netuid, uids=uids, weights=weights,
      wait_for_inclusion=True, wait_for_finalization=False,)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval

  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description

docs.title: NASChain
docs.text:
Subnet neuronlogic__NASChain netuid 31 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners run Neural Architecture Search (NAS) to design small neural networks that maximize test-set accuracy while minimizing the number of parameters and FLOPs. Miners upload their best models to Hugging Face and commit the metadata on-chain. Validators download and evaluate each model, identify the DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CodeInspection, Deduplication, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: other"]
  M2["Metric: throughput"]
  AGG["aggregate: Softmax"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: nasChain

    netuid: 31
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [model_weights, commitment_hash]

    @guards {
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, status_enum }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: model_architecture
                specific: parameter_count
                raw: "parameter_count"
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: Softmax {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: softmax
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length, ~12s blocks)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Active Pareto-based scoring. Validators evaluate downloaded miner models, compute
  the Pareto-optimal set over (accuracy, params, flops), then assign exponential rewards to the Pareto
  front and set weights directly (no EMA smoothing — the moving-average alpha is commented out in update_scores).
  No burn allocation found in the source.
subnet:
  name: NASChain
  owner_repo: neuronlogic__NASChain
  netuid: 31
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners run Neural Architecture Search (NAS) to design small neural networks that maximize test-set
    accuracy while minimizing the number of parameters and FLOPs. Miners upload their best models to Hugging
    Face and commit the metadata on-chain. Validators download and evaluate each model, identify the Pareto-optimal
    architectures over (accuracy, params, flops), retrain Pareto candidates to detect test-set leakage,
    and reward miners whose models lie on the Pareto-optimal front.
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: accuracy
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Validator-measured test-set accuracy. Models below vali_config.min_accuracy (70.0) are excluded
    from the Pareto front. Used both as a Pareto objective and as norm_accuracy in the exponential reward
    combined_score.
- name: params
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: parameter_count
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Number of model parameters. A Pareto objective (minimized) and contributes via -0.25 * norm_params
    to the combined_score.
- name: flops
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Floating-point operations of the model. Models with flops >= vali_config.max_flops (280000000)
    are excluded from the Pareto front. Contributes via -0.5 * norm_flops to the combined_score.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Validators evaluate miner models on a fixed test set with deterministic (fixed-seed) training/evaluation.
    Pareto candidates are retrained by validators with a standard train/valid split to detect test-set
    leakage (disqualified if validator-measured accuracy is lower than the miner-claimed accuracy).
  source_identifier: vali_trainer.py / vali_config.py
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Validator hardware fixed to RTX 4090 with fixed seed for deterministic results.
aggregation:
  method: softmax
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: softmax
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn allocation found in source.
  notes: combined_score = norm_accuracy - 0.25*norm_params - 0.5*norm_flops, computed only over the rewarded
    (Pareto-optimal) models; reward = exp(combined_score) / sum(exp(combined_score)). This is a softmax
    over a weighted-sum combined score with the literal coefficients 0.25 and 0.5 (temperature is implicitly
    1, not a named constant). Only models flagged reward==True (the Pareto front) participate.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: raw_weights = L1-normalize(self.scores); processed via bt.utils.weight_utils.process_weights_for_netuid
    then convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit; set on chain with subtensor.set_weights(version_key=self.spec_version).
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length, ~12s blocks)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: update_scores zeros self.scores and scatters the current rewards directly; the EMA moving-average
      update (alpha * scattered + (1-alpha)*scores) is commented out in source.
anti_gaming:
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: elimination
  description: 'Test-set leakage check: Pareto top models have weights reinitialized and are retrained
    by the validator on a standard train-test split. If the submitted model''s accuracy is lower than
    the validator''s retrained accuracy, the miner is disqualified.'
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Duplicate architectures (same accuracy, params, flops) on the Pareto front are deduplicated
    via filter_pareto_by_commit_date / filter_pareto_by_lowest_block: only the oldest commit date (or
    lowest block / lowest ext_idx) keeps pareto=True; others are set to non-Pareto.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: File-size limit (vali_config.max_download_file_size = 5.5*1024*1024); models exceeding
    the limit are rejected. Models must be loadable via torch.jit.load (TorchScript) to be evaluated.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - status_enum
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: eval_frame persisted to state/state.csv; scores zeroed each step before scattering current
    rewards; scores for replaced hotkeys are reset to 0 in resync_metagraph.
  notes: Per-UID eval_frame DataFrame tracks uid, params, flops, accuracy, score, pareto (bool), reward
    (bool), commit, commit_date, block, ext_idx, hf_account, evaluate, vali_evaluated.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '105'
    quote: python neurons/miner.py --netuid 31 --wallet.name <wallet_coldkey_name>
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: src/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 330-347
    quote: 'def find_pareto(df, vali_config):'
  - claim_field: /task/summary

    quote: The objective of the NAS is to minimize the number of parameters while maximizing accuracy

  - claim_field: /task/submission_format

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/direction

  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description

    line_ref: '38'
    quote: Models selected as top models due to their high accuracy on the test set will have their weights
      reinitialized and retrained with a standard train-test split by the validators.
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: src/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: '557'
    quote: rewarded_models['combined_score'] = rewarded_models['norm_accuracy'] - 0.25 * rewarded_models['norm_params']
      - 0.5 * rewarded_models['norm_flops']
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: src/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 560-564
    quote: exp_scores = np.exp(rewarded_models['combined_score'].to_numpy())
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/enabled
    source_path: src/base/validator.py

  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: src/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 329-337
    quote: result, msg = self.subtensor.set_weights(
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: src/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 308-314
    quote: ) = bt.utils.weight_utils.process_weights_for_netuid(
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: src/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 73-78
    quote: '"--neuron.epoch_length", type=int, help="The default epoch length (how often we set weights,
      measured in 12 second blocks).", default=100,'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/cadence
    source_path: src/base/neuron.py
    line_ref: 174-178
    quote: (self.block - self.metagraph.last_update[self.uid]) > self.config.neuron.epoch_length
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes
    source_path: src/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 398-409
    quote: 'self.scores = torch.zeros_like(self.scores) ... # alpha: float = self.config.neuron.moving_average_alpha'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '38'
    quote: If the submitted model's accuracy is lower than the accuracy achieved by retraining by the
      validator, the miner will be disqualified.
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: src/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 402-411
    quote: oldest_row_index = group['commit_date'].idxmin() ... df.loc[... & (df.index != oldest_row_index),
      'pareto'] = False
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: model/vali_config.py
    line_ref: '9'
    quote: self.max_download_file_size = 5.5*1024*1024
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/notes
    source_path: src/base/validator.py

    quote: '''uid'': pd.Series(dtype=''int''), ... ''score'': pd.Series(dtype=''float''), ''pareto'':
      pd.Series(dtype=''bool''), ''reward'': pd.Series(dtype=''bool''),'

  unresolved:
  - Exact dataset identity (test/train set) is not named in the read source files; described only via
    vali_trainer.py / vali_config.py references in README.

    exists, so /aggregation/temperature left null.
  - min/max_parameters in vali_config are 0 (unset); whether a param bound is enforced elsewhere not confirmed.
  - spec_version / version_key literal value is in src/__init__.py __spec_version__ tuple but exact integer
    not transcribed.

docs.title: Numinous
docs.text:
Subnet numinouslabs__numinous netuid 6 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners submit Python forecasting agent code (agent_main(event_data) -> {prediction: float in 0..1}). Validators execute the agent in an isolated sandbox once per binary event; the prediction is reused across 24h intervals until the event cutoff. On resolution the prediction is scored by Brier score; DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CodeInspection, RateLimit, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: probabilistic_forecast"]
  M1["Metric: pnl"]
  M2["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: numinous

    netuid: 6
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: source_code

    @guards {
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { registration_age, cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: probabilistic_forecast
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: pnl
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: MarketData {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: other
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "weights_rate_limit blocks (fetched from chain)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: On-chain weights are sourced from a centralized off-chain API (NuminousClient.get_weights
  -> aggregated_weight); the validator only re-normalizes and pushes them. The in-repo scoring.py computes
  a reverse-EMA-weighted Brier score into a local DB but is not the documented on-chain weight source.
  The full winner-takes-all per-pool emission split (global + (topic,metric) pools) is documented but
  computed off-chain and not present in this repo's code.
subnet:
  name: Numinous
  owner_repo: numinouslabs__numinous
  netuid: 6
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners submit Python forecasting agent code (agent_main(event_data) -> {prediction: float
    in 0..1}). Validators execute the agent in an isolated sandbox once per binary event; the prediction
    is reused across 24h intervals until the event cutoff. On resolution the prediction is scored by Brier
    score; lower is better. Rewards are winner-takes-all per pool (one global leaderboard plus K (topic,
    metric) domain pools).'
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: brier_score
  metric_kind: probabilistic_forecast
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: (prediction - outcome)^2 on binary events. Computed in scoring.py as (rema_prediction - outcome)^2
    where rema_prediction is a reverse-power-decay weighted average of per-interval predictions. Predictions
    clipped to [0.01, 0.99]; missing / failed runs imputed to 0.5. README/docs describe winner-takes-all
    on the average Brier over a rolling window of ~100 (docs say last 101) events.
- name: pnl
  metric_kind: pnl
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Documented domain metric (Sport x PNL pool): simulates a $1 trade vs the Polymarket price at
    forecast time. Documentation only; no implementing code found in repo.'
- name: reasoning
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Documented Reasoning pool metric: 70/30 weighted average of a rubric quality score and an implied-probability
    Brier score, with a substantial emissions share (docs: 25% Information track, 20% Signals track).
    Documentation only; rubric/extractor not in this repo''s code.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: Binary event outcomes (outcome = 0 or 1). Events sourced from LLM-generated geopolitical/political
    triggers and Polymarket markets (midprice 0.2-0.8 when received). Some geopolitical/finance events
    resolve against the Polymarket price (or a reference-LLM forecast) rather than the final binary outcome.
  source_identifier: Polymarket; Numinous event platform
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Outcome is delivered by the centralized event platform; the validator reads it from the events
    table (event.outcome) when scoring.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: other
  composition_other: 'Documented as winner-takes-all per pool: emissions split across one global pool
    (alpha_0 * E) and K (topic, metric) pools (alpha_(t,r) * E), each pool fully awarded to its argmin-rank
    miner. On-chain, the validator instead consumes pre-aggregated per-miner weights from the API and
    only re-normalizes them to sum to one.'
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn logic present in repo code.
  notes: Within scoring.py, per-interval predictions are combined into a single rema_prediction by a reverse
    power-law decay weight w(i) = 1 - (i/(n_intervals-1))**exponent with exponent default 2 (older intervals
    down-weighted), then a single Brier score is taken. The final on-chain weight vector is the API's
    aggregated_weight values divided by their sum (renormalize_weights).
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Validator fetches aggregated per-miner weights from the Numinous API (NuminousClient.get_weights
    -> aggregated_weight), merges onto the current metagraph (missing miners filled 0.0), runs sanity
    checks, renormalizes to sum-to-one, applies bittensor process_weights (min_allowed_weights / max_weight_limit),
    and submits via subtensor.set_weights. Gated by the chain weights_rate_limit (in blocks).
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: weights_rate_limit blocks (fetched from chain)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: 'No EMA over final weights in repo. A rolling scoring window exists upstream of weighting:
      scoring.py uses event.run_days_before_cutoff intervals; docs describe a rolling window of ~100/101
      events.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Agent execution and code are entirely visible to the subnet protocol; submitted code is
    verified against the registered wallet/hotkey before execution. Code size limited to 2MB.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Code can be updated at most once every 3 days; submitted code activates at the next 00:00
    UTC; only agents activated before an event is broadcast forecast that event.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Sandbox enforces a 240s timeout, no internet (gateway-only), and resource limits. Caching
    requires no dynamic timestamps/random seeds so executions match across validators. Predictions clipped
    to [0.01, 0.99]; failed/timed-out/missing runs imputed to 0.5 (Brier 0.25).
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Miners participate in multiple independent tracks; identity is (miner_uid, hotkey, track) and
    each track has its own agents, predictions, scores and weight pools, scored independently. Tracks
    defined in neurons/validator/models/track.py. Docs additionally describe one global pool plus K (topic,
    metric) domain pools (e.g. Information / Signals tracks; Sport x PNL, geopolitical, finance pools),
    but the pool split is computed off-chain.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - registration_age
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: 7-day immunity period after registration during which a miner cannot be deregistered and
    is scored only once; after immunity, the miner with the lowest (best inverse) Brier score may be deregistered
    when a new miner registers.
  notes: 'Scoring tracks per-miner registration time (miner_registered_minutes); miners registered after
    the scoring window started are excluded. Average Brier over a rolling window (docs: ~100/101 events)
    determines ranking.'
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: 2.1.9
  primary_docs:
  - docs/scoring-system.md
  - docs/architecture.md
  - docs/subnet-rules.md
  - README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: neurons/validator/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 30-31
    quote: '"--netuid", type=int, help="Subnet netuid", choices=[6, 155]'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: neurons/validator/tasks/scoring.py
    line_ref: 1-8
    quote: 'import copy

      import math

docs.title: OMEGA Labs Any-to-Any (SN21)
docs.text:
Subnet omegalabsinc__omegalabs-anytoany-bittensor netuid 21 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners train and upload a voice-to-voice (any-to-any, currently speech) model to Hugging Face and commit its metadata on-chain. Validators pull the model, run it through a VoiceBench evaluation (CommonEval, WildVoice, IfEval, AdvBench) plus a Voice MOS quality eval inside a Docker container via a sc DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, ChallengePeriod, CodeInspection"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  M1["Metric: similarity"]
  AGG["aggregate: Softmax"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: omegaLabsAnyToAnySn21

    netuid: 21
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: [model_weights, commitment_hash]

    @burn {
        uid: "111"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        ChallengePeriod {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { reputation, registration_age }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: LlmJudgment {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: Softmax {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
            temperature: 0.01
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "every 20 minutes (UTC minute % 20 == 0)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: single
            count: 1
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Active two-pool incentive (run_baseline_incentive). The 75% "above baseline" pool
  is burned to UID 111 whenever no submitted model beats the dynamic baseline; the 25% below-baseline
  pool is distributed by reputation+score softmax. So in practice emissions partially burn to UID 111
  when no model clears the baseline.
subnet:
  name: OMEGA Labs Any-to-Any (SN21)
  owner_repo: omegalabsinc__omegalabs-anytoany-bittensor
  netuid: 21
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners train and upload a voice-to-voice (any-to-any, currently speech) model to Hugging Face
    and commit its metadata on-chain. Validators pull the model, run it through a VoiceBench evaluation
    (CommonEval, WildVoice, IfEval, AdvBench) plus a Voice MOS quality eval inside a Docker container
    via a scoring API, and produce a combined score. Weights are set so that models above a dynamic performance
    baseline share the larger emissions pool.
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: voicebench_overall
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.7
  normalization: none
  notes: VoiceBench overall score (weighted average across CommonEval, WildVoice, IfEval, AdvBench datasets),
    LLM-judge based. Combined into the model score with weight VOICEBENCH_WEIGHT=0.7.
- name: voice_mos
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: none
  notes: Voice MOS (mean opinion score) audio-quality metric. Combined into the model score with weight
    VOICE_MOS_WEIGHT=0.3. metric_kind approximated as similarity/quality (no exact enum).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: VoiceBench evaluation over fixed datasets (CommonEval, WildVoice, IfEval, AdvBench) scored
    via LLM-based judging; plus a Voice MOS quality model. A per-competition dynamic "baseline" score
    is fetched from the SN21 API to split miners into pools.
  source_identifier: VoiceBench datasets (commoneval, wildvoice, ifeval, advbench); SN21 API baseline-score
    endpoint
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Scoring runs in a Docker container behind a scoring API; baseline and reputations are fetched
    from https://sn21-api.omegatron.ai.
aggregation:
  method: softmax
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: 0.01
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '111'
    fraction: 0.75
    dynamic: true
    notes: When no UID scores above the dynamic baseline, the entire 75% above-baseline pool is assigned
      to UID 111 (BURN_UID=111). Otherwise the 75% pool is distributed by score-softmax among up to PLAYERS_IN_ABOVE_BASELINE=3
      models, and the 25% pool by a reputation/score blend among up to PLAYERS_IN_BELOW_BASELINE=10 models.
  notes: 'Two-pool system: above-baseline pool weight = 0.75 * softmax(score/temperature); below-baseline
    pool weight = 0.25 * softmax(0.7*reputation + 0.3*score). Final weights normalized to sum to 1. temperature=0.01
    from constants. (A separate legacy _try_win_rate_competition path uses EMA alpha=0.9 and per-competition
    reward_percentage, but the live weight thread calls run_baseline_incentive which overwrites self.weights
    directly.)'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Weight thread loops every minute and sets weights only when the UTC minute is a multiple of
    20 (:00, :20, :40). It calls run_baseline_incentive() to compute the two-pool weights, then subtensor.set_weights().
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: every 20 minutes (UTC minute % 20 == 0)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: The active run_baseline_incentive path assigns self.weights = weights directly with no EMA.
      The adjust_for_vtrust interpolation in the weight setter is commented out. (constants.alpha=0.9
      EMA exists only in the unused _try_win_rate_competition method.)
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Models with duplicate model hashes are detected; the UID with the newer block is penalized
    to score 0 (the older-block submission keeps the score). Implemented in _try_win_rate_competition
    and also in run_step duplicate-preference logic.
- kind: challenge_period
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Models must be old enough before evaluation: model_age must exceed constants.MIN_AGE (currently
    MIN_AGE=0 seconds), enforced via is_model_old_enough, intended to prevent evaluating models that saw
    OMEGA data-subnet data.'
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Submitted Hugging Face repos are size-capped (MAX_HUGGING_FACE_BYTES = 18 GiB) and a percent
    improvement threshold (PERCENT_IMPROVEMENT=10) is referenced for beating the prior top model (the
    improvement gate is present but commented out at the call site).
sub_competitions:
  structure: single
  stages: []
  count: 1
  notes: COMPETITION_SCHEDULE contains a single competition (competition_id="v4", reward_percentage=1.0).
    run_baseline_incentive uses COMPETITION_SCHEDULE[0] only.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - reputation
  - registration_age
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: Per-hotkey reputation is fetched from the SN21 API (default 0.5 when missing) and used to weight
    the 25% below-baseline pool. Model block (upload age) is tracked and used for tie-breaking, duplicate
    penalties, and the min-age gate.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/README_VALIDATOR_LOGIC.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: constants/__init__.py
    line_ref: '22'

  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: neurons/validator.py

  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: docs/README_VALIDATOR_LOGIC.md
    line_ref: 99-112
    quote: run_baseline_incentive() → Pulls all scores from vali_api ... Calculates two-pool incentive
      system ... Sets weights on Bittensor chain
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: '1117'
    quote: hf_repo_id = model_i_metadata.id.namespace + "/" + model_i_metadata.id.name
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: constants/__init__.py
    line_ref: '74'
    quote: 'VOICEBENCH_WEIGHT = 0.7  # Weight of VoiceBench score in final model score'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/weight
    source_path: constants/__init__.py
    line_ref: '75'
    quote: 'VOICE_MOS_WEIGHT = 0.3  # Weight of VoiceMOS score in final model score'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind
    source_path: neurons/docker_inference_voicebench.py
    line_ref: '119'
    quote: combined_score = (voicebench_score * VOICEBENCH_WEIGHT) + (voice_mos_score * VOICE_MOS_WEIGHT)
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description
    source_path: constants/__init__.py
    line_ref: 96-104
    quote: '''commoneval'': [''test''], ''wildvoice'': [''test''], ''ifeval'': [''test''], ''advbench'':
      [''test'']'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/temperature
    source_path: constants/__init__.py
    line_ref: '53'
    quote: temperature = 0.01
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: '805'
    quote: score_softmax = torch.softmax(score_vec/ constants.temperature, dim=0)
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: '807'
    quote: weights[uid] = 0.75 * score_softmax[i]
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 811-812
    quote: "weights[111] = 0.75\n            bt.logging.warning(\"No UIDs above baseline, burning 75%\
      \ (assigning to UID 111)\")"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 818-821
    quote: "W_REP = 0.7\n            rep_softmax = torch.softmax((W_REP*rep_vec+(1-W_REP)*score_vec),\
      \ dim=0)\n            for i, uid in enumerate(below_baseline):\n                weights[uid] = 0.25\
      \ * rep_softmax[i]"
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 810-812
    quote: "# Burn 75%: assign all to UID 111 (if exists)\n            weights[111] = 0.75"
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 910-917
    quote: "subtensor.set_weights(\n                    netuid=self.config.netuid,\n                 \
      \   wallet=self.wallet,\n                    uids=self.metagraph.uids,\n                    weights=self.weights,"
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: neurons/validator.py

    quote: 'if minutes % 20 == 0 or self.config.immediate:'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method

  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes

  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description

      \ = 0\n                            uids_penalized_for_hash_duplication.add(uid2)"
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description

docs.title: OMEGA Labs Any-to-Any / Multimodal Dataset (SN24)
docs.text:
Subnet omegalabsinc__omegalabs-bittensor-subnet netuid 24 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners contribute multimodal data to build a large dataset. For YouTube video tasks, miners scrape YouTube clips matching a validator topic query and submit VideoMetadata with ImageBind embeddings (video/audio/description); they are scored on query relevance, description relevance, novelty, and desc DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: ReferenceModel, OrganicTraffic, Other"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, LivenessCheck, Deduplication"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: similarity"]
  M1["Metric: similarity"]
  M2["Metric: similarity"]
  M3["Metric: composite"]
  M4["Metric: similarity"]
  M5["Metric: composite"]
  M6["Metric: composite"]
  M7["Metric: similarity"]
  M8["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  G --> M5
  GT --> M5
  G --> M6
  GT --> M6
  G --> M7
  GT --> M7
  G --> M8
  GT --> M8
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  M5 --> AGG
  M6 --> AGG
  M7 --> AGG
  M8 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: omegaLabsAnyToAnyMultimodalDatasetSn24

    netuid: 24
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: [content_link, signals]

    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            ReferenceModel {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            OrganicTraffic {
                trust_model: adversarial
            },
            Other {
                trust_model: oracle
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.3
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "neuron.epoch_length blocks (default 100, 12s blocks)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: Three reward tracks are normalized and summed by percentage weights (YOUTUBE_REWARDS_PERCENT,
  FOCUS_REWARDS_PERCENT, AUDIO_REWARDS_PERCENT). In the checked-in constants AUDIO_REWARDS_PERCENT = 0.0
  and FOCUS_REWARDS_PERCENT = 1, so YOUTUBE_REWARDS_PERCENT = 1.0 - 1 - 0.0 = 0.0 by default, meaning
  the ImageBind YouTube and audio scoring paths receive ~0 weight unless the live focus-rewards-percent
  API returns a smaller value. The focus percent is fetched at runtime from {api_root}/api/focus/get_rewards_percent
  and falls back to the constant; the YouTube/audio scoring code is present but its on-chain weight share
  is governed by these live/percentage splits, so the operative mechanism differs from the full documented
  scoring. No explicit burn/null-UID routing was found in the source.
subnet:
  name: OMEGA Labs Any-to-Any / Multimodal Dataset (SN24)
  owner_repo: omegalabsinc__omegalabs-bittensor-subnet
  netuid: 24
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners contribute multimodal data to build a large dataset. For YouTube video tasks, miners
    scrape YouTube clips matching a validator topic query and submit VideoMetadata with ImageBind embeddings
    (video/audio/description); they are scored on query relevance, description relevance, novelty, and
    description detail. For audio tasks, miners submit podcast audio clips scored on diarization, length,
    audio quality, and query relevance. Separately, the Focus Videos marketplace lets users record task-completion
    videos (Ω Focus app) which are scored off-chain; miners buy these to boost their focus score share.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: query_relevance
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.3
  normalization: none
  notes: Mean of video-query cosine, audio-query cosine, and a text-only similarity model score; scaled
    by QUERY_RELEVANCE_SCALING_FACTOR = 1.3 in the YouTube aggregate score.
- name: description_relevance
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.7
  normalization: none
  notes: Weighted blend of video-description cosine (VIDEO_RELEVANCE_WEIGHT = 0.65) and audio-description
    cosine (0.35), then scaled by unique-token length and DESCRIPTION_RELEVANCE_SCALING_FACTOR = 0.7.
- name: novelty_score
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: true_novelty = min(local cosine novelty, global Pinecone novelty via API). Videos with novelty
    < DIFFERENCE_THRESHOLD (0.1) are filtered out rather than additively weighted; acts as a gate/dedup,
    not an additive term.
- name: description_length_boost
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Unique-token count scales description score between MIN_LENGTH_BOOST_TOKEN_COUNT=100 and MAX_LENGTH_BOOST_TOKEN_COUNT=300
    with DESCRIPTION_LENGTH_WEIGHT=0.35; too-short descriptions are penalized.
- name: audio_diarization_inverse_der
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.6
  normalization: none
  notes: inverse_der scaled by DIARIZATION_SCALING_FACTOR = 0.6 in audio total_score.
- name: audio_length_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: none
  notes: total_audio_length / (NUM_AUDIOS * MAX_AUDIO_LENGTH_SECONDS); scaled by AUDIO_LENGTH_SCALING_FACTOR
    = 0.1.
- name: audio_quality_total_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.2
  normalization: none
  notes: Weighted sum of speech_content, speaker_dominance, background_noise, unique_speakers_error sub-scores;
    scaled by AUDIO_QUALITY_SCALING_FACTOR=0.2.
- name: audio_query_relevance
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: none
  notes: Mean audio-query cosine; scaled by AUDIO_QUERY_RELEVANCE_SCALING_FACTOR = 1.0 - 0.6 - 0.1 - 0.2
    = 0.1.
- name: focus_points_percentage
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Per-hotkey focus_points_percentage fetched from the validator API (/api/focus/miner_purchase_scores);
    used directly as the focus reward (no EMA). Off-chain scoring (DeepSeek completion x boosted multiplier
    per docs).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: reference_model
  description: ImageBind v2 (Meta, Omega Labs fork) produces the unified video/audio/text embeddings the
    validator recomputes to spot-check and to measure relevance.
  source_identifier: imagebind (v2=True)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Validator re-embeds a random sampled submission and compares to submitted embeddings.
- kind: organic_traffic
  description: Source content is scraped from YouTube (videos/podcasts); validator downloads a random
    clip via proxy to verify the submission is real.
  source_identifier: youtube
  trust_model: adversarial
- kind: other
  kind_other: centralized_validator_api
  description: Global novelty scores, focus_points_percentage, and the live focus rewards percent all
    come from the Omega validator API (sn24-api.omegatron.ai), which is authoritative for focus scoring
    and global dedup.
  source_identifier: https://sn24-api.omegatron.ai
  trust_model: oracle
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No explicit burn / null-UID routing found in source. Emission can be effectively withheld from
      the YouTube/audio tracks when their percentage is 0, but this is a track weight split, not a documented
      burn.
  notes: In set_weights, each of the three per-track score vectors (scores, focus_scores, audio_score_arr)
    is L1-normalized (torch.nn.functional.normalize p=1) and scaled by its percentage (YOUTUBE/FOCUS/AUDIO_REWARDS_PERCENT),
    then summed into raw_weights. Within the YouTube track the per-video signals are an additive weighted
    sum divided by num_videos; novelty/stuffing act as gates.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Per-track L1-normalized score vectors scaled by reward percentages and summed; raw_weights passed
    through process_weights_for_netuid then convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit before subtensor.set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: neuron.epoch_length blocks (default 100, 12s blocks)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.3
    window: null
    notes: YouTube scores (update_scores) and audio scores (update_audio_scores) use EMA with neuron.moving_average_alpha
      (default 0.3). Focus scores (update_focus_scores) explicitly do NOT use an EMA; they are scattered
      in directly each step.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: is_valid_youtube_id check; fake video IDs return FAKE_VIDEO_PUNISHMENT = -5.0.
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'With CHECK_PROBABILITY = 0.1 the validator downloads a random submitted clip via proxy
    and re-embeds it; if recomputed embeddings are not similar (SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD 0.95 / strict allclose)
    the miner gets FAKE_VIDEO_PUNISHMENT = -5.0. Audio: byte-equality check of submitted vs downloaded
    audio.'
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Cosine-similarity dedup within batch (> SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD 0.95) and against global Pinecone
    index; novelty < DIFFERENCE_THRESHOLD (0.1) videos are filtered, leaving MIN_SCORE if none remain.
- kind: other
  kind_other: token_stuffing_detection
  enforcement: penalty
  description: unstuff.is_stuffed and check_extraneous_chunks reject token-stuffed descriptions; high-confidence
    stuffing returns STUFFED_DESCRIPTION_PUNISHMENT = -5.0.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: youtube_video
    role: main
    advancement_rule: weighted by YOUTUBE_REWARDS_PERCENT (= 1 - focus - audio)
  - name: audio_podcast
    role: main
    advancement_rule: weighted by AUDIO_REWARDS_PERCENT (0.0 in checked-in constants)
  - name: focus_videos
    role: main
    advancement_rule: weighted by FOCUS_REWARDS_PERCENT (live API; constant default 1)
  count: 3
  notes: Three parallel reward tracks combined by percentage split at weight-setting time, not a ladder/tournament.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: scores/focus_scores reset to 0 when a hotkey is replaced in the metagraph; state persisted
    to state.pt (scores, focus_scores, audio_score_arr, hotkeys).
  notes: YouTube and audio scores are EMA moving averages persisted across steps; focus scores are overwritten
    each step from the API (no EMA).
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/scoring.md
  - docs/focus_video_scoring_and_embeddings.md

  completeness: high

docs.title: Templar Crusades
docs.text:
Subnet one-covenant__crusades netuid 3 Archetype gated Language python Mechanism status full_burn Task Miners host a single train.py at a public URL and commit the URL on-chain (timelock-encrypted). Validators download the code, run it inside a Docker/ Basilica GPU container against a fixed benchmark model and dataset, and score it by Model FLOPs Utilization (MFU). The single highest-MFU finished sub DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: ReferenceModel, LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CodeInspection, DeterministicCheck, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: throughput"]
  M1["Metric: reward"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: templarCrusades

    netuid: 3
    lang: python
    status: full_burn
    submission: [source_code, content_link, commitment_hash]

    @burn {
        uid: "1"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @guards {
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Collateral {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, cumulative_score }

    Gated {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: reward
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            ReferenceModel {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: gated
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "100 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: single
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: full_burn
mechanism_status_notes: hparams.json ships burn_rate=1.0 and burn_uid=1, so the live validator routes
  100% of emissions to burn_uid; the documented winner-takes-all MFU scoring runs (submissions are still
  evaluated) but receives 0 weight under the shipped config.
subnet:
  name: Templar Crusades
  owner_repo: one-covenant__crusades
  netuid: 3
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners host a single train.py at a public URL and commit the URL on-chain (timelock-encrypted).
    Validators download the code, run it inside a Docker/ Basilica GPU container against a fixed benchmark
    model and dataset, and score it by Model FLOPs Utilization (MFU). The single highest-MFU finished
    submission (leaderboard rank 1, above an adaptive threshold) is rewarded winner-takes-all; everything
    else (and, under the shipped burn_rate, all of it) goes to burn_uid.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
  - content_link
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: mfu
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Model FLOPs Utilization is the primary metric. Per submission, MFU is taken as statistics.median_low
    over the successful evaluation runs. Rejected below min_mfu floor and above max_plausible_mfu (anti-cheat)
    at eval time.
- name: success_rate_gate
  metric_kind: reward
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Gate, not an additive signal: a submission whose success rate across runs is below min_success_rate
    is marked FAILED_EVALUATION and gets no score.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: reference_model
  description: 'Fixed benchmark training task: train the benchmark model on the benchmark dataset for
    a set number of steps; correctness verified against a reference run via loss comparison and final
    weight verification.'
  source_identifier: Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B on HuggingFaceFW/fineweb
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: benchmark_model_name, benchmark_dataset_name from hparams.json. README top line describes Qwen2.5-3B;
    shipped hparams use Qwen2.5-7B.
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: LLM-based exploit detection scans miner code before evaluation; fail-open (never blocks
    on LLM errors).
  source_identifier: OpenRouter / anthropic/claude-opus-4.6 (EXPLOIT_DETECTION_MODEL)
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Security/anti-exploit gate, not a scoring oracle.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: gated
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '1'
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: burn_rate=1.0 in hparams.json => burn_weight=1.0, winner_weight=0.0. Code computes winner_weight
      = 1.0 - burn_rate and burn_weight = burn_rate; with no finished/valid winner, 100% goes to burn_uid
      regardless.
  notes: Distribution is winner_weight=(1-burn_rate) to leaderboard rank 1 and burn_weight=burn_rate to
    burn_uid. Leaderboard rank-1 selection uses an adaptive threshold (base_threshold) that is set to
    the improvement on a new leader and decays decay_percent per decay_interval_blocks toward base.
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Two-UID weight vector [burn_uid, winner_uid] = [burn_rate, 1-burn_rate], normalized to sum 1.0.
    If winner==burn_uid or no valid winner, single UID burn_uid gets weight 1.0.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA on the final reward; weights are recomputed each cycle. The adaptive threshold has its
      own decay but that gates leaderboard membership, not the emitted weight vector.
anti_gaming:
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Static security scanner (security_defs.py) blocks forbidden imports, monkey-patching, timer
    tampering, etc.; plus LLM-based exploit detection (check_code_for_exploits) marks submissions FAILED_EVALUATION
    on exploit.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Weight verification against a reference run (weight_relative_error_max), loss comparison
    (max_loss_difference), min_params_changed_ratio, and timer_divergence_threshold; MFU floor (min_mfu)
    and ceiling (max_plausible_mfu) reject implausible results.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: rejection
  description: min_blocks_between_commits (360) enforced per hotkey; first-committer-wins on duplicate
    code URLs; max_evaluated_urls cap on tracked URLs.
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Code URL committed timelock-encrypted (set_reveal_commitment, reveal_blocks); downloaded
    code hash must match the committed 32-char SHA256 truncation.
- kind: collateral
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Submission payment: miner must transfer_stake fee_rao worth of alpha to burn_uid''s coldkey
    and embed the payment extrinsic ref; unverified payment => FAILED_EVALUATION. payment.enabled=true,
    fee_rao=50000000 in shipped hparams.'
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Evaluation runs in a controlled Docker/Basilica A100 container; SSRF protection on code
    download (private-IP/redirect blocking, size/content checks).
sub_competitions:
  structure: single
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Single MFU competition. Competition version derived from __version__ (major*100+minor); state
    resets on version change. Not modeled as distinct sub-competitions.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - cumulative_score
  status_values:
  - EVALUATING
  - FINISHED
  - FAILED_EVALUATION
  reset_policy: State (last_processed_block, evaluated_code_urls) reset when COMPETITION_VERSION changes.
    Adaptive threshold + previous_winner tracked in DB across cycles.
  notes: Per-submission records hold MFU score, tokens/sec, success flag; leaderboard winner + adaptive
    threshold persisted as validator state.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: 0.19.1
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/Validator.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / Claude Opus 4.8
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: hparams/hparams.json
    line_ref: '2'
    quote: '"netuid": 3,'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: src/crusades/config.py
    line_ref: '1'
    quote: '"""Configuration management for templar-crusades (Chi/Affinetes Architecture)."""'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 1-8
    quote: 'URL-Based Architecture: 1. Reads code URL commitments from blockchain (timelock decrypted)
      2. Downloads miner''s train.py from the committed URL 3. Evaluates via affinetes (Docker locally
      or Basilica remotely) 4. Sets weights based on MFU (Model FLOPs Utilization) scores'
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 100-123
    quote: Host your `train.py` at any URL that returns raw code
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/name
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 972-975
    quote: '# MFU is the primary metric now  mfu_scores = [e.mfu for e in successful_evals]  median_mfu
      = statistics.median_low(mfu_scores)'

    quote: 'success_rate = len(successful_evals) / len(all_evals) if all_evals else 0 min_success_rate
      = hparams.min_success_rate  if success_rate < min_success_rate: ... FAILED_EVALUATION'

  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/1/source_identifier
