docs.title: oneoneone (subnet 111)
docs.text:
Subnet oneoneone-io__subnet-111 netuid 111 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners scrape user-generated content (reviews from platforms like Google Maps/Yelp, and X/tweets) in response to synthetic validation tasks issued every ~20 minutes. Validators query up to MAX_MINER_COUNT miners with a GenericSynapse carrying a type_id/metadata task, measure per-miner response time, DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: OrganicTraffic"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, LivenessCheck, StakeWeighting"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: volume"]
  M1["Metric: latency"]
  M2["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: oneoneoneSubnet111

    netuid: 111
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: api_response

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: 0.25
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: volume
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: data_freshness
                specific: freshness
                raw: "data_freshness"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: OrganicTraffic {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "100 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Validator hard-codes a 25% emission burn to UID 0 (burner_uid=0, burner_weight=0.25)
  before distributing the remaining 75% proportionally to miner scores. The documented three-component
  scoring (speed/volume/recency) is delegated to an out-of-repo Node.js scoring endpoint and is active
  for the non-burned 75%.
subnet:
  name: oneoneone (subnet 111)
  owner_repo: oneoneone-io__subnet-111
  netuid: 111
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners scrape user-generated content (reviews from platforms like Google Maps/Yelp, and X/tweets)
    in response to synthetic validation tasks issued every ~20 minutes. Validators query up to MAX_MINER_COUNT
    miners with a GenericSynapse carrying a type_id/metadata task, measure per-miner response time, and
    reward each miner's returned data on a composite of volume (50%), speed (30%) and recency (20%); miners
    that fail structural validation or spot-check verification get zero.
  submission_format:
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: volume_score
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: min_max
  notes: '"Volume Score (50%) - Number of items returned: volume_score = miner_item_count / max_item_count_across_miners".
    Normalized against the maximum item count across all miners.'
- name: speed_score
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: min_max
  notes: '"Speed Score (30%) - Processing efficiency: speed_score = fastest_response_time / miner_response_time".
    Rewards faster response times.'
- name: recency_score
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: data_freshness
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.2
  normalization: min_max
  notes: '"Recency Score (20%) - Freshness of most recent item: recency_score = (miner_most_recent_date
    - oldest_most_recent_date) / date_range".'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: organic_traffic
  description: 'Spot-check verification: randomly selects 3 items from each miner''s response and verifies
    them against live platform data (Google Maps live data, Desearch/Gravity for X tweets), checking exact
    matches of key fields. Actual scoring/validation logic runs in an out-of-repo Node.js endpoint (/score-responses).'
  source_identifier: http://{VALIDATOR_NODE_HOST}:{VALIDATOR_NODE_PORT}/score-responses
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Python reward.py merely POSTs miner responses to the Node.js scoring endpoint and returns its
    scores; the scoring math itself is not in this repo (only documented in README).
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 0.25
    dynamic: false
    notes: burner_uid=0, burner_weight=0.25 (25%). In _calculate_weights_with_burner the burner UID receives
      burner_weight and the remaining 1.0 - burner_weight is split across miners proportional to score.
  notes: Final per-miner score is the weighted sum 0.5*volume + 0.3*speed + 0.2*recency (composite computed
    by the Node.js endpoint), then EMA-smoothed across rounds, then normalized so non-burner weights share
    the remaining 75%.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Scores (EMA moving average of composite rewards) are normalized with a fixed 25% burn to UID
    0; the remaining 75% is allocated proportional to each miner's score via _calculate_weights_with_burner,
    then process_weights_for_netuid and convert_weights_and_uids_for_emit before subtensor.set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: 'update_scores: "alpha: float = self.config.neuron.moving_average_alpha; self.scores = alpha
      * scattered_rewards + (1 - alpha) * self.scores". moving_average_alpha default 0.1.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Spot-check verification randomly selects 3 items per miner response and verifies them against
    live platform data, checking exact matches of key fields; spot-check failures and failed structural
    validation cause zero score / disqualification.
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: elimination
  description: Disqualification when response time >= synapse timeout (120 seconds); timed-out or empty
    responses yield zero score.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Validators require a minimum stake / vpermit (VALIDATOR_MIN_STAKE = 1.024e3 * 25; --neuron.vpermit_tao_limit
    default 4096) to participate.
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Synthetic tasks have a type_id (e.g. Google Maps reviews, X/tweets); a random job type is chosen
    per round. The same speed/volume/recency scoring is applied per task type; no separately-weighted
    competition tracks are defined in this repo.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: On hotkey replacement the corresponding score is zeroed (resync_metagraph sets self.scores[uid]
    = 0 when a hotkey changes); state persisted to state.npz.
  notes: EMA moving-average scores per UID are kept across rounds in self.scores and saved/loaded via
    save_state/load_state (state.npz).
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 232-239
    quote: Mainnet (Subnet 111) ... btcli subnet register --no_prompt --wallet.name "miner" --wallet.hotkey
      "default" --netuid "111"
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: oneoneone/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 37-44
    quote: 'def get_rewards(self, type_id: str, metadata: Dict[str, Any], responses: List[List[Dict[str,
      Any]]], ...) -> np.ndarray:'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 390-394
    quote: 'Challenge Generation: Validator selects job type (e.g., Google Maps reviews, Yelp reviews)
      ... Miner Selection: Up to 50 miners randomly chosen ... Response Window: Miners have 120 seconds
      to complete the task'
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: oneoneone/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 126-142
    quote: response = await self.dendrite(axons=[axon], synapse=GenericSynapse(type_id=type_id, metadata=metadata,
      timeout=timeout), deserialize=True, timeout=timeout,)
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 427-428
    quote: "# Volume Score (50%) - Number of items returned\n    volume_score = miner_item_count / max_item_count_across_miners"
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/weight
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 430-431
    quote: "# Speed Score (30%) - Processing efficiency\n    speed_score = fastest_response_time / miner_response_time"
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/weight
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 433-434
    quote: "# Recency Score (20%) - Freshness of most recent item\n    recency_score = (miner_most_recent_date\
      \ - oldest_most_recent_date) / date_range"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/composition
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 436-437
    quote: "# Final composite score\n    final_score = (0.5 * volume_score) + (0.3 * speed_score) + (0.2\
      \ * recency_score)"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction
    source_path: oneoneone/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 65-66
    quote: "self.burner_uid = 0  # UID to burn emissions to\n        self.burner_weight = 0.25  # Percentage\
      \ of weight to burn (25%)"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: oneoneone/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 298-320
    quote: 'remaining_weight = 1.0 - self.burner_weight ... if uid == self.burner_uid: weights.append(self.burner_weight)
      elif total_other_scores > 0: weights.append((score / total_other_scores) * remaining_weight)'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: oneoneone/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 284-292
    quote: result, msg = self.subtensor.set_weights(wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.config.netuid, uids=uint_uids,
      weights=uint_weights, ...)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: oneoneone/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 70-75
    quote: --neuron.epoch_length ... help="The default epoch length (how often we set weights, measured
      in 12 second blocks).", default=100,
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/alpha
    source_path: oneoneone/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 197-202
    quote: --neuron.moving_average_alpha ... help="Moving average alpha parameter, how much to add of
      the new observation.", default=0.1,
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/kind
    source_path: oneoneone/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 396-397
    quote: "alpha: float = self.config.neuron.moving_average_alpha\n        self.scores: np.ndarray =\
      \ alpha * scattered_rewards + (1 - alpha) * self.scores"
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 483-487
    quote: 'Spot Check System: Randomly selects 3 items from each miner''s response - Verifies against
      live data depending on the type - Checks for exact matches of key data fields'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 476-477
    quote: 'Disqualification Conditions: ... Response time ≥ synapse timeout (120 seconds)'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: oneoneone/config.py
    line_ref: 32-33
    quote: '# Validator minimum stake

      VALIDATOR_MIN_STAKE = 1.024e3 * 25'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: oneoneone/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 67-69
    quote: validator_url = (f"http://{VALIDATOR_NODE_HOST}:{VALIDATOR_NODE_PORT}/score-responses")
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: oneoneone/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 339-341
    quote: 'for uid, hotkey in enumerate(self.hotkeys): if hotkey != self.metagraph.hotkeys[uid]: self.scores[uid]
      = 0  # hotkey has been replaced'
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/structure
    source_path: oneoneone/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 96-98
    quote: "type_id = task[\"typeId\"]\n    metadata = task[\"metadata\"]\n    timeout = task[\"timeout\"\
      ]"
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: oneoneone/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 254-255
    quote: "# Calculate weights with burner control\n        raw_weights = self._calculate_weights_with_burner(uids,\
      \ scores)"
  unresolved:
  - Actual scoring math (speed/volume/recency computation, spot-check, structural validation, disqualification)
    lives in an out-of-repo Node.js endpoint (/score-responses); only the README documents the formula.
    The literal constants 0.5/0.3/0.2 are quoted from README pseudocode, not from executed code in this
    repo.
  - No explicit softmax/temperature/decay; aggregation is plain weighted sum + EMA + proportional renormalization.
    min_weight_floor and decay_rate not present.
composition:
  shape: multiplex
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 1

docs.title: EvolAI
docs.text:
Subnet openevolai__evolai netuid 47 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners publish open HuggingFace language models (model name must contain "evolai") in the "transformer" or "mamba2" track and register them on-chain via commitment metadata. Validators download each model and measure cross-entropy loss and reasoning quality on a deterministic per-epoch challenge set DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CodeInspection, PlagiarismDetection, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: loss"]
  M1["Metric: reward"]
  M2["Metric: risk_adjusted_ratio"]
  M3["Metric: accuracy"]
  M4["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: evolAi

    netuid: 47
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: model_weights

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        PlagiarismDetection {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, cumulative_score, registration_age }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: loss
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: ema
            },
            Metric {
                kind: reward
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: ema
            },
            Metric {
                kind: risk_adjusted_ratio
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: ema
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: ema
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: ema
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "600 seconds (weight worker); EPOCH_BLOCKS=360 for eval epochs"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: 'Production weight-setting uses the loss/DPO-margin ProgressTracker score raised
  to WEIGHT_EXPONENT, split equally per active track. Emissions are burned to UID 0 only dynamically:
  a per-round emission_scale in [emission_floor, 1.0] decays from the last time a miner was improving/near-frontier,
  and the burn UID receives (1 - effective_budget). When miners are active, scale is 1.0 (no burn beyond
  budget cap); when the subnet stagnates, emission decays toward UID 0. A separate interview-style scoring.py
  module (Validity + Questions + Interview - Loop penalties) exists but is NOT the path used by the validator
  weight worker.'
subnet:
  name: EvolAI
  owner_repo: openevolai__evolai
  netuid: 47
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners publish open HuggingFace language models (model name must contain "evolai") in the
    "transformer" or "mamba2" track and register them on-chain via commitment metadata. Validators download
    each model and measure cross-entropy loss and reasoning quality on a deterministic per-epoch challenge
    set: a per-token base CE on ground-truth responses, a thinking-augmented CE, DPO-style margins (CE
    of model-generated vs ground-truth response), and binary side-quest accuracy. Lower loss / better
    reasoning that genuinely improves over time is rewarded.'
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
scoring_signals:
- name: absolute_quality
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: ema
  notes: DPO-base absolute = min(1.0, exp(gamma * ema_dpo_base)) where dpo_base_margin = CE(G_base|q)
    - CE(T|q) (always <= 0). Falls back to exp(-gamma*ema_base_ce) for miners without DPO history. Combined
    with think_gain into quality = absolute*(0.60+0.40*think_gain), then weighted by (w_abs + w_think)
    = 0.75. Weight is non-additive (entangled with think_gain), so left null.
- name: think_gain
  metric_kind: reward
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: ema
  notes: think_gain = max(0, tanh(ema_dpo_think - ema_dpo_base)); multiplicative on absolute via quality
    = absolute*(0.60 + 0.40*think_gain). A memorizer (think_gain~0) reaches only 60% of absolute. Not
    an additive signal -> weight null.
- name: flow
  metric_kind: risk_adjusted_ratio
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.15
  normalization: ema
  notes: 'W_FLOW=0.15. Sharpe ratio of the EMA trend of dpo_think_margin (or CE fallback): sharpe = mu_delta/(sigma_delta
    + flow_eps), flow = max(0, tanh(sharpe)). Requires >= MIN_FLOW_EPOCHS=10 records.'
- name: side_quest_accuracy
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: ema
  notes: W_SQ=0.10. EMA of side-quest accuracy (fraction of deterministic math side-quest answers correct,
    binary per quest, 2 quests per sample).
- name: per_miner_scale
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: ema
  notes: 'Multiplicative final scale in [0,1]: miner_scale = W_IMPROVEMENT*improvement + W_PROXIMITY*proximity
    (0.3/0.7). improvement = max(0, tanh(long_ema - short_ema)); proximity = min(1, global_best_long_ema/long_ema).
    raw_score is multiplied by miner_scale.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Per-epoch challenge rows drawn deterministically (epoch seed committed on-chain) from the
    ACTIVE_DATASETS HuggingFace dataset(s); ground-truth instruction/response pairs used as the teacher-forced
    target for CE and as the reference T in DPO margins.
  source_identifier: evolai/universal_qa
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: ACTIVE_DATASETS default 'evolai/universal_qa'; N_EVAL=10 rows per dataset.
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: 'Side-quest turns: deterministic math questions generated from block_hash + sample index;
    answer-matching is binary (0 if correct answer appears in output, else counted via check_side_quest_answer).'
  source_identifier: evolai.validator.side_quests.generate_side_quests
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: SIDE_QUEST_N=2 per sample; turn order shuffled deterministically by block_hash+index.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: STAGNATION_BURN_UID=0. Burn UID weight = max(0, 1 - effective_budget), where effective_budget
      = miner_budget * emission_scale. emission_scale is 1.0 when any miner is improving or near-frontier,
      else decays exp(-EMISSION_LAMBDA*staleness_days) floored at EMISSION_FLOOR=0.2. miner_budget = min(1,
      DAILY_TAO_EMISSION/(alpha_price*DAILY_ALPHA_EMISSION)), defaulting to 1.0 when price unavailable.
  notes: 'Per-track: per-miner final score is raised to WEIGHT_EXPONENT=2.0, normalized within the track
    (sum to 1), then scaled by an equal _share = 1/num_active_tracks across tracks, then multiplied by
    effective_budget. Final weight vector normalized to sum to 1 on-chain.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Background weight-updater thread runs every _WEIGHT_INTERVAL_S=600s. For each active track,
    score**WEIGHT_EXPONENT normalized to sum 1, multiplied by equal track share and effective budget;
    burn UID gets the residual. set_weights called with wait_for_inclusion=True.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 600 seconds (weight worker); EPOCH_BLOCKS=360 for eval epochs
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: PROGRESS_EMA_ALPHA=0.10 used as the per-signal EMA over epoch history (_ema). Short/long EMAs
      for improvement/proximity use alpha = 2/(N+1) with EMA_SHORT_ROUNDS=140, EMA_LONG_ROUNDS=1800. Loss/score
      history capped at HISTORY_EPOCHS=1800 records.
anti_gaming:
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Model name must contain 'evolai' and be a public HuggingFace model; supported tracks transformer/mamba2.
    VALID_PARAM_RANGES_B gates allowed parameter counts to three bands (~0.45-0.48B, 1.5-1.8B, 3.5-3.8B).
- kind: plagiarism_detection
  enforcement: elimination
  description: 'copy_gaming: model_fingerprint.py flags a UID whose model fingerprint matches another
    UID''s (exact weight hash, structural clone, or near-copy); EMA score forced to 0 and evaluation skipped
    (score_zero_copy_gaming). Note: enforcement lives in the interview/scoring.py path.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Anti-memorization: absolute quality uses DPO-base margin only (not think margin), so a
    miner degrading base quality while faking thinking traces gets absolute=0. Special-token literals
    (<|...|>) are stripped from miner decodes to block injected fake turn boundaries.'
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Epoch seed is committed on-chain one epoch ahead (generate_seed/commit_epoch_seed) and
    used to derive eval rows the NEXT epoch, so miners cannot precompute on the live eval set.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Answer-loop detection: LOOP_WARNING_PENALTY=0.1 per warning; identical response repeated
    >= LOOP_CIRCUIT_BREAKER=5 forces score 0 (answer_loop_circuit_break). (scoring.py path.)'
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: transformer
    role: main
    advancement_rule: ''
  - name: mamba2
    role: main
    advancement_rule: ''
  count: 2
  notes: Two parallel tracks (transformer, mamba2) evaluated and weighted independently; each active track
    receives an equal emission share (1/num_active_tracks).
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - cumulative_score
  - registration_age
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Per-UID epoch history (deque maxlen=HISTORY_EPOCHS=1800) wiped on hotkey/coldkey change;
    coldkey:hotkey history archived and restorable within COLDKEY_ARCHIVE_TTL_DAYS=7. Loss history reset
    when a miner's model_name changes.
  notes: ProgressTracker persists EpochRecord history (loss, thinking_loss, base_loss, sq_accuracy, dpo_think_margin,
    dpo_base_margin, model_revision) per UID to progress_tracker.json.
documentation:
  status: code_authoritative
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - VALIDATOR_INTERVIEW_EVALUATION.md (referenced by scoring.py docstring)
  completeness: medium

docs.title: Bittensor SDK (core library, not a subnet)
docs.text:
Subnet opentensor__bittensor netuid None Archetype opaque Language python Mechanism status dormant Task No subnet task is defined in this repository. The SDK provides Python packages for interacting with the Bittensor ecosystem, including the extrinsics a subnet validator uses to set weights on chain (set_weights_extrinsic, commit_timelocked_weights_extrinsic). Miner production and the rewarded commod DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CommitReveal"}}:::ov
  AGG["aggregate: Undocumented"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> G
  G --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: bittensorSdkCoreLibraryNotASubnet

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: dormant
    submission: signals

    @guards {
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }

    Opaque {
        aggregate: Undocumented {
            composition: other
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: undocumented
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: dormant
mechanism_status_notes: This is the opentensor/bittensor SDK package, not a subnet incentive mechanism.
  It exposes set_weights and commit-reveal extrinsics for validators to publish weights, but contains
  no reward/scoring function. The README states scoring is an off-chain subnet competition and that on-chain
  Yuma Consensus determines TAO rewards. No incentive mechanism is live in this repo.
subnet:
  name: Bittensor SDK (core library, not a subnet)
  owner_repo: opentensor__bittensor
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: No subnet task is defined in this repository. The SDK provides Python packages for interacting
    with the Bittensor ecosystem, including the extrinsics a subnet validator uses to set weights on chain
    (set_weights_extrinsic, commit_timelocked_weights_extrinsic). Miner production and the rewarded commodity
    are defined by individual subnets, not here.
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals: []
ground_truth_sources: []
aggregation:
  method: undocumented
  composition: other
  composition_other: no signal composition; repository defines no scoring aggregation
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: ''
  notes: No reward aggregation exists in this SDK. The only normalization present is L1 normalization
    of the published weight vector itself (convert_and_normalize_weights_and_uids), not an aggregation
    of scoring signals.
weight_setting:
  cadence: undocumented
  method: Validators call set_weights_extrinsic to publish a (uids, weights) vector for a subnet via the
    SubtensorModule.set_mechanism_weights chain call, or commit_timelocked_weights_extrinsic for a commit-reveal
    (timelocked) submission. uids and weights are converted and L1-normalized before submission. Cadence/tempo
    is a per-subnet on-chain hyperparameter read from chain, not fixed here.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: null
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No final-reward EMA in this repo. Temporal smoothing of emissions/consensus happens on the
      subtensor chain (Yuma Consensus), which is not part of this SDK source.
anti_gaming:
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Provides commit-reveal (timelocked) weight submission via commit_timelocked_weights_extrinsic
    with a commit_reveal_version default of 4, plus optional MEV-shield encrypted extrinsic submission
    to protect against front-running/MEV. These are transport-level protections, not a subnet anti-gaming
    scoring rule.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []

docs.title: Patrol
docs.text:
Subnet patrol_subnet netuid 81 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status active Task A validator selects a target wallet (and target block) randomly from the Bittensor chain and sends it to each miner. Each miner builds a subgraph of relational on-chain data (nodes = wallets, edges = transaction/staking relationships, plus supporting evidence) tracing the target out to N degrees of DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, Deduplication"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: volume"]
  M1["Metric: latency"]
  M2["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  SM["smooth: Sma"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: patrol

    netuid: 81
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: signals

    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: volume
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: content_quality
                specific: novelty
                raw: "novelty_uniqueness"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Sma {
            window: 20
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "blocks_since_last_update > tempo (chain tempo)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Validator computes per-miner overall scores (Volume 90%, Responsiveness 10%),
  takes a moving average, normalizes scores to sum-to-one and calls set_weights. Weight setting is gated
  on ENABLE_WEIGHT_SETTING (default "1" => enabled) and is due once blocks_since_last_update > tempo.
  No burn allocation is present in the code. Novelty score is documented but unimplemented (returns 0.0).
subnet:
  name: Patrol
  owner_repo: patrol_subnet
  netuid: 81
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: A validator selects a target wallet (and target block) randomly from the Bittensor chain and
    sends it to each miner. Each miner builds a subgraph of relational on-chain data (nodes = wallets,
    edges = transaction/staking relationships, plus supporting evidence) tracing the target out to N degrees
    of separation, and submits it. The validator verifies the subgraph against on-chain events (pass/fail)
    and then scores the miner on the volume of valid data submitted (90%) and responsiveness/latency (10%).
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: volume
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.9
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Sigmoid of total_items = len(nodes) + len(edges): 1 / (1 + exp(-STEEPNESS * (total_items - INFLECTION_POINT)))
    with STEEPNESS=0.005 and INFLECTION_POINT=1000. ~0.5 at 1000 items.'
- name: responsiveness
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: none
  notes: RESPONSE_TIME_HALF_SCORE / (response_time + RESPONSE_TIME_HALF_SCORE) with RESPONSE_TIME_HALF_SCORE=2
    seconds. Lower latency yields a higher score.
- name: novelty
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: novelty_uniqueness
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Documented as "coming soon"; calculate_novelty_score is a placeholder returning 0.0 and novelty_score
    is stored as None. Not in overall_score.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: On-chain Bittensor events fetched and processed by the validator. Each miner-submitted
    edge must match a processed chain event (exact key match on coldkey source/destination/owner, category,
    type, rao_amount, block_number, etc.); unmatched edges within fetched blocks fail validation.
  source_identifier: wss://archive.chain.opentensor.ai:443
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: 'Pass/fail verification: empty payload, single node, target-not-in-graph, disconnected graph,
    out-of-range blocks, or mismatched edges => score 0.'
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn allocation present in the weight-setting code.
  notes: overall_score = volume_score*0.9 + responsiveness_score*0.1 per submission. A moving average
    is taken over the latest scores divided by moving_average_denominator (default 20). At weight time,
    last average overall scores for metagraph miners are summed and each divided by the sum (overall_weights
    = v / sum_of_scores) to produce sum-to-one weights.
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Normalize last-average overall scores to sum-to-one across miners present in the metagraph,
    then submit via subtensor.set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: blocks_since_last_update > tempo (chain tempo)
  smoothing:
    kind: sma
    alpha: null
    window: 20
    notes: overall_score_moving_average = (sum(previous_overall_scores) + overall_score) / moving_average_denominator,
      with moving_average_denominator default 20 (previous fetched as denominator-1 latest scores).
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Pass/fail validation of the submitted subgraph against on-chain events; failures (empty/null
    payload, single node, target not in graph, disconnected graph, duplicate node/edge, blocks outside
    [LOWER_BLOCK_LIMIT, current_block], or edges not matching on-chain events) yield an ErrorPayload and
    a score of 0.0.
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Duplicate nodes (by id) and duplicate edges (by source/destination/ category/type/rao_amount/block_number
    key) raise PayloadValidationError, failing the submission.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: Per-(hotkey, uid) overall scores are persisted in a repository; a moving average over the latest
    scores (denominator default 20) is maintained and used at weight-setting time.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - docs/incentive.md
  - README.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:

    source_path: src/patrol/validation/config.py

However, the on-chain incentive code rewards no miners: the validator is a "co DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov

  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: full_burn
mechanism_status_notes: The validator does not score miners at all. It forces 100% of weight onto a single
  hardcoded treasury hotkey every epoch, directing all miner emissions to the Treasury Wallet. Miner code
  is explicitly deprecated. There is no per-miner scoring/reward path.
subnet:
  name: Enigma
  owner_repo: qbittensor-labs__enigma
  netuid: 63
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Enigma is a decentralized challenge platform: participants compete to break cryptographic
    systems, AI safeguards, and post-quantum protocols, submitting verified exploit solutions (code +
    exploits) for funded prize pools. However, the on-chain incentive code rewards no miners: the validator
    is a "constant-weight" validator that always assigns 100% weight to a single treasury hotkey, and
    miner code is deprecated. Challenge prizes and rewards are paid off-chain/via treasury governance
    (validator proposals and votes), not via subnet scoring.'
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals: []
ground_truth_sources: []
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: other
  composition_other: No per-miner aggregation. Scores vector is zeroed and a single entry (the treasury
    UID) is set to 1.0, then L1-normalized to weights.
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: 5DCLafsAKaLeZwm9hjMHvrQNjtucSwBhKyTLYnYmMvhxF2Uc
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: 100% of weight is forced onto the treasury hotkey UID. All miner emissions are directed to
      the Treasury Wallet, which accumulates SN63 Alpha tokens.
  notes: self.scores = np.zeros(metagraph.n); self.scores[uid] = 1.0 where uid is the treasury hotkey
    index. Base set_weights then L1-normalizes (norm = ||scores||_1) so the single non-zero entry becomes
    weight 1.0.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: 'Constant-weight validator: every epoch it locates the treasury hotkey in the metagraph, zeros
    the score vector, sets that single UID to 1.0, and calls the base set_weights. If the treasury hotkey
    is None (passive mode) or not yet in the metagraph, no weights are set.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (epoch_length default; 12s blocks)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: 'The base update_scores EMA (moving_average_alpha default 0.1) exists in the inherited code
      but is unused: the constant-weight validator overrides set_weights to overwrite self.scores directly
      each epoch rather than accumulating an EMA over rewards.'
anti_gaming: []
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Documentation describes multiple challenges (Treasury Wallet, Q-Day cryptography, AI security)
    but these are off-chain prize challenges, not encoded sub-competitions in the incentive code.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: The validator persists step/scores/hotkeys to state.npz, but scores are overwritten to a fixed
    one-hot treasury vector each epoch, so no meaningful per-miner state is maintained.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: Enigma-Treasury-v1.1-20260505
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - VALIDATORS.md
  - VOTING.md
  - treasury/README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '5'
    quote: '# **Enigma** (SN 63)'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 20-22
    quote: 'import time

      import numpy as np

      import bittensor as bt'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '101'
    quote: '**Note**: Miner code is deprecated. Only validators are currently supported. The validator
      automatically sets weights to the treasury wallet.'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 29-33
    quote: 1. **Challenges are posted** with funded prize pools in SN63 Alpha. ... 3. **Validators** score
      and verify solutions. A dedicated validator sets weights to the treasury wallet to distribute emissions
      and rewards.
    confidence: high

docs.title: Quantum Compute
docs.text:
Subnet qbittensor-labs__quantum-compute netuid 48 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners own or operate physical gate-based quantum computers (or are partnered with an operator) and execute OpenQASM 2.0/3.0 quantum circuits dispatched by validators on behalf of OpenQuantum end users. A miner is rewarded in proportion to the total confirmed execution cost of the quantum-compute jo DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: OrganicTraffic"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: volume"]
  M1["Metric: uptime"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: quantumCompute

    netuid: 48
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: compute_service

    @burn {
        fraction: 0.9
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: volume
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: OrganicTraffic {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "30 minutes"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: README documents a 90% burn with 10% of alpha used to incentivize quantum compute.
  The constant BURN_PERCENTAGE = 0.90 is defined in WeightSetter.py but is NOT referenced in the live
  _get_weights() weight computation; the live code distributes weights purely proportional to miner execution
  cost over a 14-day lookback, plus a small maintenance incentive. The 90% burn is described as applied
  at the emissions level, not enforced in the on-chain weight vector shown here.
subnet:
  name: Quantum Compute
  owner_repo: qbittensor-labs__quantum-compute
  netuid: 48
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners own or operate physical gate-based quantum computers (or are partnered with an operator)
    and execute OpenQASM 2.0/3.0 quantum circuits dispatched by validators on behalf of OpenQuantum end
    users. A miner is rewarded in proportion to the total confirmed execution cost of the quantum-compute
    jobs it successfully completed over the lookback window.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: execution_cost_share
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Each miner's weight is its summed execution cost (over completed/cost-confirmed jobs in the 14-day
    lookback) divided by the network-wide sum of those costs. Cost is fetched per execution from the job
    server's cost endpoint and stored in the successful_job table.
- name: maintenance_incentive
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Onboarded miners present in the metagraph but with no cost-bearing jobs receive a flat maintenance
    allocation: TOTAL_MAINTENANCE_INCENTIVE (0.01) split evenly across all such miners. The cost-proportional
    weights are scaled by (1 - TOTAL_MAINTENANCE_INCENTIVE) when any miner needs maintenance.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: organic_traffic
  description: Quantum computing requests (circuits) from OpenQuantum end users, dispatched by the validator
    to miners; success is determined by the miner-reported execution status (COMPLETED/FAILED).
  source_identifier: OpenQuantum job server
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Job completion status comes from the miner's synapse response (ExecutionStatus). Execution cost
    is confirmed by querying the job server's executions/{id}/cost endpoint.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: 0.9
    dynamic: false
    notes: README states emissions begin with a 90% burn and 10% available to incentivize quantum compute.
      The literal BURN_PERCENTAGE = 0.90 exists in WeightSetter.py but is not applied in _get_weights;
      DISTRIBUTION_KEY_UID = 220 and get_burn_uid()/_get_burn_uid() are defined but unused in the computed
      weight vector. The address/uid of the burn target is not resolvable from the live weight code.
  notes: weights[uid] = proportion(miner) * (1 - TOTAL_MAINTENANCE_INCENTIVE) for cost-bearing miners;
    weights[uid] = TOTAL_MAINTENANCE_INCENTIVE / N for the N onboarded-but-idle miners.
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: 'Every 30 minutes a Timer fires _set_weights(): fetch onboarded miner hotkeys from the job server,
    compute per-hotkey cost proportions over a 14-day lookback, assign weights, and publish via set_weights
    over the full uid range.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 30 minutes
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of the final weight vector; weights are recomputed from a rolling 14-day
      SQL aggregation of confirmed execution costs each cycle.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Validator verifies miner responses: synapse.success false or rate_limited causes the job
    to be rejected/retried; only COMPLETED executions are recorded as successful_job rows that count toward
    cost-proportional weight. Rate limiting pushes the circuit back on the queue.'
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Miners signal rate_limited in the synapse; rate-limited requests are not credited and are
    re-queued for retry.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Confirmed-cost rows in successful_job older than MAX_DATA_AGE (30 days) are deleted; weight
    computation only sums costs from the trailing LOOKBACK_PERIOD (14 days).
  notes: Per-miner cumulative execution cost is accumulated in the successful_job SQLite table (miner_hotkey,
    execution_id, cost, created_at) and summed per hotkey at weight time.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - qbittensor/validator/validator.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '1'
    quote: '# Quantum Compute (Bittensor Subnet 48)'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/weights/WeightSetter.py
    line_ref: 1-3
    quote: 'from datetime import timedelta, datetime, timezone

      import bittensor as bt

      from typing import Dict, List, Tuple'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '31'
    quote: miners of the Quantum Compute subnet must own or operate a physical quantum computer capable
      of executing gate-based quantum circuits using OpenQASM 2.0/3.0, or have a direct and documented
      partnership with an owner/operator.
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/validator.md
    line_ref: '9'
    quote: The validator then intelligently distributes these jobs to registered miners in the network.
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/weights/WeightSetter.py
    line_ref: 87-91
    quote: 'hotkey: str = entry[0] miner_total_cost: int = entry[1] proportion: float = miner_total_cost
      / sum hotkey_proportion_dict[hotkey] = proportion'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/weights/WeightSetter.py
    line_ref: 62-68
    quote: SELECT miner_hotkey, SUM(cost) as total_cost FROM successful_job WHERE created_at > ?  AND
      cost IS NOT NULL  GROUP BY miner_hotkey
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/weights/WeightSetter.py
    line_ref: 113-115
    quote: "if len(keys_needing_maintenance) > 0:\n    maintenance_amount = TOTAL_MAINTENANCE_INCENTIVE\
      \ / len(keys_needing_maintenance)\n    non_maintenance_multiplier = 1 - TOTAL_MAINTENANCE_INCENTIVE"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/weights/WeightSetter.py
    line_ref: 118-122
    quote: "if hotkey in proportions:\n    final_proportion: float = proportions[hotkey] * non_maintenance_multiplier\n\
      \    weights[uid] = final_proportion"
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/weights/WeightSetter.py
    line_ref: '14'
    quote: BURN_PERCENTAGE = 0.90
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '100'
    quote: Emissions will slowly scale up on the subnet, beginning with a 90% burn and 10% of alpha available
      to incentivize quantum compute.
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/weights/WeightSetter.py
    line_ref: '114'
    quote: maintenance_amount = TOTAL_MAINTENANCE_INCENTIVE / len(keys_needing_maintenance)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/weights/WeightSetter.py
    line_ref: '38'
    quote: 'self.timer: Timer = Timer(timedelta(minutes=30), self._set_weights, run_on_start=True)'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/weights/WeightPublisher.py
    line_ref: 28-35
    quote: "success, message = st.set_weights(\n    wallet=self.wallet,\n    netuid=self.metagraph.netuid,\n\
      \    uids=uids,\n    weights=weights,"
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/weights/WeightSetter.py
    line_ref: 49-58
    quote: 'onboarded_miner_hotkeys = self._get_onboarded_miner_hotkeys() weights: List[float] = self._get_weights(onboarded_miner_hotkeys)'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/weights/WeightSetter.py
    line_ref: '15'
    quote: LOOKBACK_PERIOD = timedelta(days=14)
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/reward/score.py
    line_ref: 41-53
    quote: "if not synapse.success:\n    ... self._patch_job_rejected(synapse.execution_id, msg)\nif synapse.rate_limited:\n\
      \    self._patch_job_rejected(synapse.execution_id, \"Miner is rate limiting\")"
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/reward/score.py
    line_ref: 88-89
    quote: "if execution.status == ExecutionStatus.COMPLETED:\n    completed_execution_ids.append(execution.execution_id)"
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/notes
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/reward/score.py
    line_ref: '110'
    quote: 'query: str = """INSERT OR IGNORE INTO successful_job (miner_hotkey, execution_id, created_at)
      VALUES (?, ?, ?)"""'
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/reward/cost.py
    line_ref: '10'
    quote: 'MAX_DATA_AGE: timedelta = timedelta(days=30)'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/notes
    source_path: qbittensor/validator/reward/cost.py
    line_ref: 59-63
    quote: 'endpoint: str = f"executions/{execution_id}/cost" params: dict = {"miner_hotkey": miner_hotkey}
      return self.request_manager.get(endpoint, params=params, ignore_codes=[404])'
  - claim_field: /documentation/primary_docs
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 98-102
    quote: '## Incentive Emissions will slowly scale up on the subnet, beginning with a 90% burn and 10%
      of alpha available to incentivize quantum compute.'
  unresolved:
  - 'Burn target address/UID: DISTRIBUTION_KEY_UID = 220 and get_burn_uid() exist but are not used in
    the live weight vector, so the actual burn recipient is not resolvable from the weight code.'
  - Whether the 90% burn is enforced on-chain (owner take / emission split) vs. via the weight vector
    — code path shown applies no burn fraction to weights.
  - REG_MAINTAINENCE_INCENTIVE = 0.001 is defined but unused in _get_weights; only TOTAL_MAINTENANCE_INCENTIVE
    = 0.01 is applied.
composition:
  shape: pipeline

docs.title: Gradients on Demand (G.O.D)
docs.text:
Subnet rayonlabs__G.O.D netuid None Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners compete in AutoML tournaments to fine-tune models. A miner submits a trained model repo for a given task (instruct-text, DPO, GRPO, chat, image, or environment/RL). The validator downloads each submission and evaluates it on held-out test data; for most task types lower test loss is better, w DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, DeterministicCheck, CodeInspection"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: loss"]
  M1["Metric: reward"]
  M2["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: TournamentBracket"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: gradientsOnDemandGOD

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: model_weights

    @burn {
        uid: "5GU4Xkd3dCGTU3s8VLcHGc5wsD5M8XyxDca5yDQhYm1mVXFu"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, cumulative_score, performance_ledger }

    Multiplex<tournament> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: loss
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: rank
            },
            Metric {
                kind: reward
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: rank
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: TournamentBracket {
            composition: tournament
            normalization: sum_to_one
            decay_rate: 0.3
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "WeightsSetRateLimit blocks (chain-defined); ~25 block sleep after set"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: tournament
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: 100% tournament-based. Base tournament allocations are small (text 0.15, image
  0.10, environment 0.10) with the large remaining weight routed to a dedicated burn hotkey (base ~0.65
  plus all undistributed tournament weight). Champions can boost their pool up to caps (0.48/0.32/0.20)
  on strong performance; weak performance => more burn.
subnet:
  name: Gradients on Demand (G.O.D)
  owner_repo: rayonlabs__G.O.D
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners compete in AutoML tournaments to fine-tune models. A miner submits a trained model repo
    for a given task (instruct-text, DPO, GRPO, chat, image, or environment/RL). The validator downloads
    each submission and evaluates it on held-out test data; for most task types lower test loss is better,
    while GRPO and environment tasks reward higher scores. Within a task pool the best submission is ranked
    1st; tournament bracket winners advance and the overall champion of each tournament type (text, image,
    environment) earns the boosted emission pool, with remaining weight burned.
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
scoring_signals:
- name: test_loss
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: rank
  notes: Per-task evaluation metric. calculate_miner_ranking_and_scores ranks miners by test_loss (lower
    is better for instruct/DPO/chat/image). Top miner gets FIRST_PLACE_SCORE=3, bottom 25% get SCORE_PENALTY=-1
    when pool > 8.
- name: grpo_environment_score
  metric_kind: reward
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: rank
  notes: For GRPO and ENVIRONMENT tasks, higher value is better; ranked_results sorted in reverse. Stored
    in the same test_loss field but ranking direction is inverted.
- name: tournament_rank_weight
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: 'exponential rank decay normalized to sum to 1: weight[rank] = 0.3^(rank-1) / sum,
    via exponential_decline_mapping / tournament_scores_to_weights.'
  notes: Within-tournament distribution. Participants ranked, weights distributed by exponential decay
    base TOURNAMENT_SIMPLE_DECAY_BASE=0.3 (1st=1.0, 2nd=0.3, ...), normalized so they sum to 1.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Held-out test split evaluated in a Docker/Basilica sandbox via run_evaluation_basilica_text
    / run_evaluation_basilica_image. Loss computed against task.test_data; environment tasks use a proxy
    dataset with a fetched eval_seed.
  source_identifier: validator/evaluation/scoring.py:_evaluate_submissions
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Miner submissions fetched from HF under RAYONLABS_HF_USERNAME='gradients-io-tournaments'. Repos
    matching the original model_id are flagged non-finetuned (score 0).
aggregation:
  method: tournament_bracket
  composition: tournament
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: 0.3
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: 5GU4Xkd3dCGTU3s8VLcHGc5wsD5M8XyxDca5yDQhYm1mVXFu
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: burn_weight = 1.0 - text_tournament_weight - image_tournament_weight - environment_tournament_weight,
      plus all undistributed tournament weight added to the burn node. With base allocations 0.15/0.10/0.10
      the documented base burn is ~0.65. EMISSION_BURN_HOTKEY is the fixed burn account.
  notes: Each tournament type (text/image/environment) gets a base weight that can be boosted by performance
    up to a max cap, then capped; champion uses boosted pool, non-champions use base pool, both distributed
    by exponential rank decay (base 0.3). decay_rate=0.3 is the within-aggregation rank decay (TOURNAMENT_SIMPLE_DECAY_BASE),
    not an EMA.
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: set_weights_periodically loops, checks blocks since LastUpdate against WeightsSetRateLimit;
    when due, builds tournament audit data, computes per-node weights via get_node_weights_from_tournament_audit_data,
    and calls set_node_weights. Sleeps 25 blocks between successful sets.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: WeightsSetRateLimit blocks (chain-defined); ~25 block sleep after set
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of the final weight vector found; weights are recomputed each cycle from
      latest completed tournament data. Time-based decay of champion emission boost exists (EMISSION_DAILY_TIME_DECAY_RATE=0.0033/day)
      but acts on the boost, not as a reward EMA.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: _evaluate_submissions deduplicates identical submission repos; duplicate submissions get
    score 0 / SCORE_PENALTY. group_by_losses groups identical losses.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Submissions whose repo equals the original model_id are marked is_finetune=False and scored
    0 ("Non-finetuned submission"). NaN/invalid test loss => 0 then penalty.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Tournament tasks only evaluate miners with training status SUCCESS; training is run and
    audited. Winning AutoML scripts are open-sourced after tournaments complete.
- kind: other
  kind_other: champion emission-boost time decay and per-type max caps
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Champion emission boost decays over time (EMISSION_DAILY_TIME_DECAY_RATE=0.0033 per day
    from EMISSION_TIME_DECAY_START_DATE 2025-11-26) to prevent indefinite reign; MAX caps (0.48/0.32/0.20)
    prevent a single tournament type from dominating.
sub_competitions:
  structure: tournament
  stages:
  - name: Text tournament
    role: knockout
    advancement_rule: Bracket rounds; per-round winners earn round_number * type_weight points; champion
      of final round earns the boosted text pool (base 0.15, max 0.48).
  - name: Image tournament
    role: knockout
    advancement_rule: Separate bracket; champion earns boosted image pool (base 0.10, max 0.32).
  - name: Environment tournament
    role: knockout
    advancement_rule: Separate bracket; higher score is better; champion earns boosted environment pool
      (base 0.10, max 0.20).
  count: 3
  notes: Text, image, and environment tournaments are calculated separately and independently. Tournaments
    run 4-7 days, new one starts 72h after the previous ends (README).
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - cumulative_score
  - performance_ledger
  status_values:
  - SUCCESS
  - PREEVALUATION
  - FAILURE
  reset_policy: Per-task evaluation status drives whether a miner is scored; consecutive championship
    wins and first-championship time are tracked across tournaments to compute emission boost decay (count_champion_consecutive_wins,
    get_tournament_where_champion_first_won).
  notes: Champion reign tracked by first_championship_time; consecutive wins counted; tournament results
    persisted and used for both weight setting and auditing.
