docs.title: Chutes
docs.text:
Subnet rayonlabs__chutes netuid None Archetype opaque Language python Mechanism status unknown Task Chutes is a serverless GPU compute platform on Bittensor. Miners provide GPU hardware that runs "chutes" (FastAPI-style applications built on docker images); each "cord" is a single function/route executed on the miners' GPUs. Miners are validated for authentic GPU hardware (via the GraVal library) DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: HardwareProbe"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: HardwareValidation"}}:::ov
  AGG["aggregate: Undocumented"]
  PUB["publish: Unknown"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> G
  G --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: chutes

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: compute_service

    @guards {
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: other
        }
    }

    Opaque {
        groundTruth: HardwareProbe {
            trust_model: adversarial
        }
        aggregate: Undocumented {
            composition: other
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: Unknown {
            cadence: undocumented
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: unknown
mechanism_status_notes: No validator weight-setting or emission code is present in this SDK/CLI repo;
  the README states the validator/API code and miner code are hosted in separate repositories (chutes-api,
  chutes-miner). The operative on-chain reward/burn behaviour cannot be determined from this tree.
subnet:
  name: Chutes
  owner_repo: rayonlabs__chutes
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Chutes is a serverless GPU compute platform on Bittensor. Miners provide GPU hardware that
    runs "chutes" (FastAPI-style applications built on docker images); each "cord" is a single function/route
    executed on the miners' GPUs. Miners are validated for authentic GPU hardware (via the GraVal library)
    and serve user invocations of deployed applications. The reward/scoring logic itself is not in this
    SDK repo. Inferred from the README description of the platform.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals: []
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: 'GraVal ("graphics card validation library") is used to help ensure the GPUs miners claim
    to be running are authentic/correct: VRAM capacity checks, matrix multiplications seeded by device
    information, filesystem checks, device-info challenges and pings, run as middleware within the chute.'
  source_identifier: graval
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Described in the README glossary only; the validation runs against miner-claimed GPUs to detect
    spoofing. No scoring formula is given in this repo.
aggregation:
  method: undocumented
  composition: other
  composition_other: not present in this repo (validator/API code is in a separate repo)
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No emission/burn logic present in this SDK/CLI repo.
  notes: No aggregation/scoring code exists in this tree; the README points to chutes-api for the validator/API
    code.
weight_setting:
  cadence: undocumented
  method: ''
  on_chain_call: unknown
  tempo_or_interval: null
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No weight-setting call site exists in this SDK/CLI repo.
anti_gaming:
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: other
  enforcement_other: authenticity validation of miner GPUs (consequence not specified in this repo)
  description: GraVal middleware performs VRAM capacity checks, device-information-seeded matrix multiplications,
    filesystem checks, device-info challenges and pings to verify that miners are running the GPUs they
    claim. Described in README glossary; the enforcement consequence is not specified in this repo.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: ''
documentation:
  status: docs_only
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/dev.md
  completeness: low
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: low
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 1-3
    quote: '# Chutes!  This package provides the command line interface and development kit for use with
      the chutes.ai platform.'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: setup.py
    line_ref: 1-1
    quote: from setuptools import setup, find_packages
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 5-5
    quote: The miner code is available [here](https://github.com/rayonlabs/chutes-miner), and validator/API
      code [here](https://github.com/rayonlabs/chutes-api).
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 24-28
    quote: A chute is essentially an application that runs on top of an image, within the platform. Think
      of a chute as a single FastAPI application. ... A cord is a single function within the chute. In
      the FastAPI analogy, this would be a single route & method.
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 343-343
    quote: Any function that you decorate with `@chute.cord()` becomes a function that runs within the
      chute, i.e. not locally - it's executed on the miners' hardware.
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/kind
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 30-32
    quote: '### ✅ graval  GraVal is the graphics card validation library used to help ensure the GPUs
      that miners claim to be running are authentic/correct. The library performs VRAM capacity checks,
      matrix multiplications seeded by device information, etc.'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 35-35
    quote: it runs as middleware within the chute to decrypt traffic from the validator and perform additional
      validation steps (filesystem checks, device info challenges, pings, etc.)
  - claim_field: /documentation/status
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 5-5
    quote: The miner code is available [here] ... and validator/API code [here].
  unresolved:
  - 'No scoring_signals: this repo contains no reward/scoring function (validator code is in the separate
    chutes-api repo).'
  - 'aggregation.method/composition/normalization unknown: no aggregation code in this tree.'
  - 'weight_setting.cadence/method/on_chain_call/tempo_or_interval unknown: no set_weights call site in
    this repo.'
  - 'Emission/burn allocation unknown: no on-chain emission logic present.'
  - 'netuid unknown: not declared anywhere in this SDK/CLI repo.'
  - GraVal anti_gaming enforcement consequence (elimination/penalty/rejection) not specified in this repo.
  - Whether GPU hourly pricing (README pricing section) feeds miner emissions is not determinable from
    this repo.
composition:
  shape: opaque
  overlays:
  - guards
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Reboot Subnet (Robotics)
docs.text:
Subnet reboot-org__reboot-subnet netuid 47 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status full_burn Task As documented, a miner receives a RobotSynapse (action sequences + a validator camera image) and returns a rendered/predicted camera image (img_b64); the reward function scores the structural similarity between the miner's image and the validator's image. In the actual running validator, however, no DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: Other"]):::in
  M0["Metric: similarity"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: rebootSubnetRobotics

    netuid: 47
    lang: python
    status: full_burn
    submission: api_response

    @burn {
        uid: "5D5Yw1NzfxLDQDP8D9jthkTNnJRKBfKSRTwnVBvg6RQymonx"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Pipeline {
        score: Metric {
            kind: similarity
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: Other {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: other
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: full_burn
mechanism_status_notes: The documented scoring (camera image similarity via pixelmatch) is implemented
  in reboot/validator/reward.py but is NOT invoked by the live validator forward pass. reboot/validator/forward.py
  initializes every miner reward to zero and assigns a hardcoded score of 1000 to a single hardcoded subnet-owner
  hotkey (5D5Yw1NzfxLDQDP8D9jthkTNnJRKBfKSRTwnVBvg6RQymonx), then sets weights from those scores. All
  emission is therefore directed to the owner; competitive scoring is dormant.
subnet:
  name: Reboot Subnet (Robotics)
  owner_repo: reboot-org__reboot-subnet
  netuid: 47
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'As documented, a miner receives a RobotSynapse (action sequences + a validator camera image)
    and returns a rendered/predicted camera image (img_b64); the reward function scores the structural
    similarity between the miner''s image and the validator''s image. In the actual running validator,
    however, no miner work is scored: rewards are zeroed and a fixed subnet-owner hotkey is hardcoded
    to receive the full reward.'
  submission_format:
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: camera_image_similarity
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: DOCUMENTED/DEAD-CODE signal only. reward() returns the pixelmatch similarity score (1 - mismatch/(w*h));
    scores below 0.95 are floored to 0. This function is defined in reward.py but the live forward.py
    never calls get_rewards()/reward(); it is not part of the operative mechanism.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: other
  kind_other: validator_rendered_camera_image
  description: The validator's own camera image bytes, compared pixel-by-pixel against the miner's returned
    image. (Documented path only; not used by the live validator.)
  source_identifier: validator_image_bytes
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Used only inside the unused reward()/get_rewards() code path.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: other
  composition_other: 'Hardcoded override: all miner rewards set to 0, a single hardcoded owner hotkey
    set to 1000; no combination of signals occurs.'
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: 5D5Yw1NzfxLDQDP8D9jthkTNnJRKBfKSRTwnVBvg6RQymonx
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: Not a literal burn-to-null; rather a full diversion of emission to the hardcoded subnet-owner
      hotkey. Every other miner receives reward 0 in forward.py, so after L1 normalization in set_weights
      the owner receives effectively all weight.
  notes: set_weights() L1-normalizes self.scores (raw_weights = scores / ||scores||_1) before process_weights_for_netuid.
    Since only the owner has a non-zero score, the owner gets weight ~1.0.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: self.scores updated each forward via EMA (update_scores), then L1-normalized and emitted via
    subtensor.set_weights with version_key=spec_version.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: update_scores applies scores = alpha*rewards + (1-alpha)*scores with alpha = config.neuron.moving_average_alpha
      (default 0.1). Applied to the hardcoded owner reward in the live path.
anti_gaming: []
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: A miner's score is zeroed when its hotkey is replaced in the metagraph (resync_metagraph);
    state (step, scores, hotkeys) is persisted to state.npz.
  notes: self.scores is an EMA moving-average array over miner UIDs, but in the live mechanism it is dominated
    entirely by the hardcoded owner override.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: 0.1.1
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/quickstart.md
  completeness: low
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '88'
    quote: '**Network Stats** – [Taostats Subnet 47](https://taostats.io/subnets/47/chart)'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: reboot/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 22-23
    quote: 'import numpy as np

      import bittensor as bt'
  - claim_field: /documentation/source_version
    source_path: reboot/__init__.py
    line_ref: '20'
    quote: __version__ = "0.1.1"
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: reboot/protocol.py
    line_ref: 22-31
    quote: "class RobotInput(bt.Synapse):\n    action_seqs: typing.List[str] = []\n    validator_image_b64:\
      \ str = \"\"\n\nclass RobotOutput(bt.Synapse):\n    img_b64: str"
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind
    source_path: reboot/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 27-29
    quote: "def calculate_image_similarity(img1_bytes: bytes, img2_bytes: bytes) -> float:\n    \"\"\"\
      \n    Calculate similarity between two images using structural similarity index (SSIM)."
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: reboot/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 51-57
    quote: "mismatch = pixelmatch(img1_resized, img2_resized, img_diff)\n\n        score = float(1-(mismatch/(min_width*min_height)))\n\
      \        \n        if score < 0.95:\n            score = 0\n        return score"
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: reboot/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: '94'
    quote: final_reward = similarity_score
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: reboot/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 81-82
    quote: "validator_camera_bytes = validator_image_bytes\n        miner_camera_bytes = base64.b64decode(synapse.output.img_b64)"
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: reboot/validator/forward.py
    line_ref: 32-49
    quote: "rewards = np.zeros(len(miner_uids), dtype=np.float32)\n    bt.logging.info(f\"Initialized\
      \ all miner rewards to zero: {rewards}\")\n    \n    # Try to get subnet owner and set their score\
      \ to 1\n    subnet_owner_address = \"5D5Yw1NzfxLDQDP8D9jthkTNnJRKBfKSRTwnVBvg6RQymonx\"\n    ...\n\
      \                rewards[owner_index] = 1000"

docs.title: RESI (Real Estate AI) — RESI-models
docs.text:
Subnet resi-labs-ai__RESI-models netuid 46 Archetype gated Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners train and submit ONNX real-estate price-prediction models (committed on-chain and uploaded to HuggingFace). Validators download the models, run them in sandboxed Docker inference against fresh validation data (properties listed/sold within the last 30 days, and committed at least ~30 days pri DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, DataAugmentation, CodeInspection"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: regression_error"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: resiRealEstateAiResiModels

    netuid: 46
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [model_weights, commitment_hash]

    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        DataAugmentation {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { registration_age }

    Gated {
        score: Metric {
            kind: regression_error
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: MarketData {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: gated
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "361 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: 'Live scoring is active: a daily evaluation runs models on fresh unseen data,
  scores by 1 - MAPE, picks a winner (threshold + earliest commit), and distributes 99% winner / 1% non-winners
  / 0% cheaters. A burn path exists (_apply_burn) but burn_amount is hardcoded to 0.0 in validator.py,
  so no emissions are burned in this revision.'
subnet:
  name: RESI (Real Estate AI) — RESI-models
  owner_repo: resi-labs-ai__RESI-models
  netuid: 46
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners train and submit ONNX real-estate price-prediction models (committed on-chain and uploaded
    to HuggingFace). Validators download the models, run them in sandboxed Docker inference against fresh
    validation data (properties listed/sold within the last 30 days, and committed at least ~30 days prior),
    and reward the single best-generalizing model. Score = 1 - MAPE (Mean Absolute Percentage Error) on
    predicted vs actual sale prices.
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: mape_score
  metric_kind: regression_error
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Score = 1 - MAPE, clamped to [0,1] via max(0.0, 1.0 - mape). MAPE = mean(|y_true - y_pred|/y_true).
    Lower error => higher score. Score is the sole ranking metric (mape_to_score in metrics.py).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: Fresh real-estate validation data (recently listed/sold property sale prices) fetched daily
    from the RESI validator dashboard API. Used as y_true for MAPE scoring against model predictions.
  source_identifier: https://resi-validator-api.vercel.app
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: 'Temporal separation enforced: models must be committed on-chain ~30 days before evaluation,
    and evaluation uses properties listed/sold within the last 30 days (data that did not exist at submission
    time).'
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: gated
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: '238'
    fraction: 0.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: '_apply_burn() supports allocating a fraction of emissions to burn_uid (default 238), but burn_amount
      is hardcoded to 0.0 in validator.py ("Hardcoded: 0% burn"), so no burn is applied.'
  notes: 'Winner-takes-most. Winner set = all valid models within score_threshold (0.005) of best score;
    within that set the earliest on-chain commit wins (innovation/pioneer reward). Distribution: winner_share=0.99
    to winner, remaining 0.01 split proportionally by score among valid non-winners, cheaters (copiers,
    memorizers, rejected) get 0. Validator normalizes scores so weights sum to 1.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Daily evaluation produces an IncentiveWeights mapping (hotkey -> weight); validator writes it
    into self.scores, normalizes by total, and submits via Pylon. A separate weight-setting loop fires
    once elapsed blocks exceed epoch_length.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 361 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of the final reward. Scores are overwritten each evaluation (self.scores.fill(0.0)
      then set). Weight setting gated by epoch_length (361 blocks).
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: elimination
  description: 'Duplicate prediction detection: models producing identical predictions (within similarity_threshold=1e-6)
    are grouped; the earliest committer is the "pioneer", later committers are "copiers" and receive 0
    weight.'
- kind: data_augmentation
  enforcement: elimination
  description: Generalization (memorization) detection via perturbation. Models re-evaluated on globally
    perturbed features (global_noise_pct=0.01) and spatially perturbed coords (spatial_noise_std=0.005).
    Flagged as memorizer if global_ratio < global_threshold (0.70) or spatial_ratio < spatial_threshold
    (0.95); memorizers get 0 weight. Perturbation seed is the decentralized commit-reveal randomness seed.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: ModelInspector inspects submitted ONNX models; rejects models with too many price-like
    constants (price_count_threshold=50000, i.e. lookup/memorization) and, when configured, models with
    unused initializers (reject_unused_initializers=True).
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Decentralized randomness: validators timelock-commit a random value (~360 blocks reveal)
    and harvest a shared seed used for perturbation, with a mid-epoch snapshot of committers to reject
    late commits (min_quorum=2). Also a temporal commit-cutoff: commitments must be at least min_commitment_age_blocks
    (216000, ~30d) old to be eligible.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - registration_age
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Score zeroed when a UID's hotkey changes (deregistration/replacement). Winner selection
    uses on-chain commit block_number; minimum commitment age (~30d) gates eligibility.
  notes: No persistent per-miner ledger; per-cycle scores are recomputed and overwritten. Commit block/age
    is the main per-miner state used (earliest-commit tie-break + eligibility gate). An optional ATH (all-time-high)
    mode (ath_enabled, default false) would reward the persisted ATH winner 99% / 1% to others until beaten,
    but it is disabled by default in this revision.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md

docs.title: RESI - Real Estate Super Intelligence
docs.text:
Subnet resi-labs-ai__resi netuid 46 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status full_burn Task Miners build custom scrapers to collect US real estate listing data (e.g. sold properties for assigned zipcodes from sources like Zillow, Redfin, county records), conform it to a required property schema, and upload it to S3. Validators assign miners zipcodes per epoch and score submissions on quant DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: OrganicTraffic, DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, DeterministicCheck, HardwareValidation"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: volume"]
  M1["Metric: accuracy"]
  M2["Metric: accuracy"]
  M3["Metric: latency"]
  M4["Metric: reward"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: resiRealEstateSuperIntelligence

    netuid: 46
    lang: python
    status: full_burn
    submission: content_link

    @burn {
        uid: "238"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: volume
                direction: target_band
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: reward
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            OrganicTraffic {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: gated
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "1200 blocks (4 hours) per cycle"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: full_burn
mechanism_status_notes: 'The documented incentive (per-zipcode competitive scoring with 55/30/10/5 reward
  distribution) is implemented in rewards/zipcode_competitive_scorer.py, but the live weight-setting call
  site in neurons/validator.py hardcodes 100% of emission to a single burn UID (238) and zeros all miner
  weights. The competitive scorer path is therefore dormant in production: scores are computed but never
  drive on-chain weights.'
subnet:
  name: RESI - Real Estate Super Intelligence
  owner_repo: resi-labs-ai__resi
  netuid: 46
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners build custom scrapers to collect US real estate listing data (e.g. sold properties for
    assigned zipcodes from sources like Zillow, Redfin, county records), conform it to a required property
    schema, and upload it to S3. Validators assign miners zipcodes per epoch and score submissions on
    quantity, quality/schema compliance, and spot-check accuracy against the source. The documented mechanism
    runs a per-zipcode competition rewarding the earliest valid submissions.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  submission_format_other: ''
scoring_signals:
- name: tier1_quantity
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: target_band
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Tier 1 gate: actual listing count must fall within +/-15% of the expected count from the zipcode
    server (expected_min = expected_count*0.85, expected_max = expected_count*1.15). Pass/fail gate, not
    an additive weight.'
- name: tier2_quality_epoch_compliance
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Tier 2 gate: quality and epoch-compliance validation of entities (schema completeness, etc.).
    Pass/fail; failing here drops the miner to participant status.'
- name: tier3_spot_check
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Tier 3 gate: randomly selected entities are re-scraped from the source and verified for accuracy.
    Passing all three tiers makes a miner eligible to be a top-3 winner for that zipcode.'
- name: submission_earliness
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Submissions are sorted by submission_timestamp earliest-first; the first three miners to pass
    all tiers take 1st/2nd/3rd place for the zipcode.
- name: zipcode_reward_rank
  metric_kind: reward
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Per-zipcode reward by rank: 1st=0.55, 2nd=0.30, 3rd=0.10; remaining 0.05 split equally among
    non-winner participants. Each zipcode''s contribution is weighted by that zipcode''s share of total
    listings found across the epoch.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: organic_traffic
  kind_other: ''
  description: Real estate listing source websites (e.g. Zillow) are re-scraped by validators in Tier
    3 spot-check validation to verify miner-submitted listing accuracy.
  source_identifier: zillow / real estate websites
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Expected listing counts and zipcode assignments come from the RESI API server (api.resilabs.ai);
    accuracy verification scrapes the original listing source.
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  kind_other: ''
  description: Per-epoch zipcode assignments and expected listing counts served by the RESI API define
    the ground-truth target quantity for Tier 1.
  source_identifier: RESI API (get_current_zipcode_assignments)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: ''
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  method_other: ''
  composition: gated
  composition_other: ''
  normalization: sum_to_one
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '238'
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: Live set_weights() hardcodes final_weights[0] = 1 for burn UID 238 (100% to burn) and sets
      remaining_weight = 0, so every miner weight is zeroed. The competitive scorer's normalized per-miner
      scores never reach the chain.
  notes: 'Documented aggregation: each zipcode is a winner-take-(top-3) competition gated by a 3-tier
    pass/fail check; per-zipcode rewards are weighted by zipcode listing share and normalized to sum to
    1.0 (calculate_epoch_proportional_weights). This documented path is bypassed by the burn in the live
    call site.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  cadence_other: ''
  method: should_set_weights() triggers when the validator enters a new 1200-block (4-hour) cycle; set_weights()
    then routes 100% weight to burn UID 238 and calls subtensor.set_weights().
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 1200 blocks (4 hours) per cycle
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    kind_other: ''
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: ''
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: are_entities_unique() runs a 4-layer duplicate check (zpid, URI-zpid consistency); a submission
    with duplicates fails pre-tier (failed_at_tier 0) and is rejected.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: 'Tier 1 quantity gate: listing count must be within +/-15% of the API-provided expected
    count, preventing under/over-reporting.'
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: rejection
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: Tier 3 spot-check re-scrapes randomly selected entities from the original source to detect
    synthetic or fabricated listing data.
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  enforcement_other: ''
  description: DeterministicConsensus computes a consensus hash over final scores and an epoch nonce so
    all validators reach identical results for the epoch.
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  structure_other: ''
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Each assigned zipcode is an independent competition (per-zipcode ranking and 55/30/10/5 reward
    split), aggregated across all zipcodes in the epoch weighted by listing share.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: Per-zipcode validation produces transient WINNER/PARTICIPANT status within an epoch result dict,
    but no persistent cross-epoch per-miner ledger was found in the scoring code.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/validator.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8

docs.title: Ridges
docs.text:
Subnet ridgesai__ridges netuid None Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners submit an agent.py (source code) coding agent to the Ridges platform. Each agent is run against software-engineering problems (SWE-bench-style fail_to_pass / pass_to_pass test suites) inside the Harbor execution sandbox by screeners and validators. An agent's score is the fraction of problems DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CodeInspection, StakeWeighting, ChallengePeriod"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: ridges

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: source_code

    @burn {
        uid: "OWNER_HOTKEY"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @guards {
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        ChallengePeriod {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<tiered> {
        score: Metric {
            kind: accuracy
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "SET_WEIGHTS_INTERVAL_SECONDS (env-configured, value not in source)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: tiered
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: The platform computes a live consensus score and assigns 100% of weight to the
  single top approved agent (winner-take-all). However, a runtime BURN env flag, when set, routes 100%
  of emissions to the owner hotkey instead, and if no eligible weight-receiving agent exists the validator
  also falls back to burning to the owner hotkey. Burn is a toggle/fallback, scoring is otherwise active.
subnet:
  name: Ridges
  owner_repo: ridgesai__ridges
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners submit an agent.py (source code) coding agent to the Ridges platform. Each agent is
    run against software-engineering problems (SWE-bench-style fail_to_pass / pass_to_pass test suites)
    inside the Harbor execution sandbox by screeners and validators. An agent's score is the fraction
    of problems it solves under validator consensus; the single highest-scoring approved agent receives
    100% of the subnet weight (winner-take-all).
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: consensus_solve_fraction
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: final_score = (count of validator-set problems where every validator agreed the agent solved
    them) / total problem count in the set. Computed in SQL function refresh_agent_scores_for_agent /
    populate_agent_scores.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: SWE-bench-style coding problems with fail_to_pass and pass_to_pass test suites; an agent's
    patch is run in the Harbor sandbox and marked 'solved' when tests pass.
  source_identifier: evaluation_sets / problems (set_group='validator')
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Multiple independent validators each evaluate; a problem counts toward score only when solved
    by all validators (consensus per problem).
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: OWNER_HOTKEY
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'Burn is conditional, not always-on: when config.BURN is true OR when no registered weight-receiving
      agent exists, 100% of weight is assigned to the owner hotkey. The fraction of 1.0 applies in the
      burn branch only.'
  notes: 'The /scoring/weights endpoint returns a single {hotkey: 1.0} mapping. The validator set_weights_from_mapping
    asserts exactly one hotkey with weight 1 and sets uids=[uid], weights=[1] on-chain. Pure winner-take-all;
    the top agent is ranked by final_score DESC, then lowest avg running seconds, then earliest created_at.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Validator polls the platform's /scoring/weights endpoint every SET_WEIGHTS_INTERVAL_SECONDS
    and calls subtensor.set_weights with uids=[top_uid], weights=[1] (single non-zero weight).
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: SET_WEIGHTS_INTERVAL_SECONDS (env-configured, value not in source)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No on-chain EMA smoothing in the repo; weight is set directly to the current leader. The leader
      query restricts to agents approved within the last 12 hours.
anti_gaming:
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Pre-screening LLM judge (pre_screening_jobs / pre_screening_results) returns a verdict
    of pass, fail, or needs_review on a submitted agent before it can be scored.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: elimination
  description: 'Banned hotkeys table: get_banned_hotkey looks up a miner_hotkey in banned_hotkeys; banned
    miners are excluded.'
- kind: challenge_period
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Multi-tier screening pipeline: agents must clear screener-1 and screener-2 thresholds
    (SCREENER_1_THRESHOLD, SCREENER_2_THRESHOLD) and avoid the PRUNE_THRESHOLD before reaching validator
    evaluation; thresholds are env-configured (literal values not in source).'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Consensus requirement: a problem only counts toward an agent''s final_score when solved
    by ALL validators (solved_validator_count = validator_count), and at least 2 validators must have
    evaluated (validator_count >= 2).'
sub_competitions:
  structure: tiered
  stages:
  - name: screener_1
    role: qualifier
    advancement_rule: score >= SCREENER_1_THRESHOLD (env-configured)
  - name: screener_2
    role: qualifier
    advancement_rule: score >= SCREENER_2_THRESHOLD (env-configured)
  - name: validator
    role: main
    advancement_rule: consensus final_score; top agent wins all weight
  count: 3
  notes: EvaluationSetGroup has screener_1, screener_2, and validator groups; agents progress through
    screener tiers before validator scoring. Only the validator group feeds the consensus final_score
    and weight assignment.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - cumulative_score
  status_values:
  - approved
  - pass
  - fail
  - needs_review
  reset_policy: Weight eligibility requires approval within a rolling 12-hour window (approved_at between
    NOW()-12h and NOW()) and the latest evaluation set.
  notes: agent_scores table tracks per-agent final_score, approved flag, approved_at, validator_count
    and set_id. Pre-screening verdicts are pass/fail/needs_review.
documentation:
  status: code_authoritative
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - miners/README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: validator/set_weights.py
    line_ref: 1-9
    quote: from bittensor.core.async_subtensor import AsyncSubtensor
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: miners/README.md
    line_ref: 1-9
    quote: ridges/ is the CLI + Python toolkit for testing agent.py ... ridges upload --file agent.py
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: alembic/versions/2026_05_07_consensus_agent_scores.py
    line_ref: 100-103
    quote: ( COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE cbp.solved_validator_count = vc.validator_count)::float / spc.problem_count
      ) AS final_score
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description
    source_path: models/problem.py
    line_ref: 9-19
    quote: pass_to_pass = "pass_to_pass" ... fail_to_pass = "fail_to_pass" ... PASS = "pass"
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/notes
    source_path: alembic/versions/2026_05_07_consensus_agent_scores.py
    line_ref: '80'
    quote: COUNT(DISTINCT ve.validator_hotkey) FILTER (WHERE erh.solved IS TRUE) AS solved_validator_count
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: api/endpoints/scoring.py
    line_ref: 31-34
    quote: 'weight_receiving_hotkey = await get_weight_receiving_agent_hotkey() ... return {weight_receiving_hotkey:
      1.0}'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: queries/scores.py
    line_ref: '28'
    quote: ORDER BY ass.final_score DESC, rt.avg_running_secs ASC NULLS LAST, ass.created_at ASC
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/enabled
    source_path: api/endpoints/scoring.py
    line_ref: 24-26
    quote: 'if config.BURN: # When burning, we assign 100% of emissions to the owner hotkey. return {config.OWNER_HOTKEY:
      1.0}'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: api/endpoints/scoring.py
    line_ref: 36-38
    quote: '# If no weight-receiving agent is found, assign 100% of emissions to the # owner hotkey (to
      burn). return {config.OWNER_HOTKEY: 1.0}'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: api/config.py
    line_ref: 37-39
    quote: OWNER_HOTKEY = os.getenv("OWNER_HOTKEY")
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status_notes
    source_path: api/config.py
    line_ref: 45-48
    quote: BURN = os.getenv("BURN") ... BURN = BURN.lower() == "true"
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: validator/set_weights.py
    line_ref: 31-38
    quote: success, message = await subtensor.set_weights( wallet=config.VALIDATOR_WALLET, netuid=config.NETUID,
      uids=[weight_receiving_uid], weights=[1], ... )
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/cadence
    source_path: validator/main.py
    line_ref: 95-104
    quote: weights_mapping = await get_ridges_platform("/scoring/weights", quiet=1) ... await asyncio.sleep(config.SET_WEIGHTS_INTERVAL_SECONDS)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes
    source_path: queries/scores.py
    line_ref: 24-25
    quote: AND ass.approved_at <= NOW() AND ass.approved_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '12 hours'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: db/models/pre_screening_judge.py
    line_ref: '14'
    quote: PRE_SCREENING_VERDICTS = ("pass", "fail", "needs_review")
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: queries/banned_hotkey.py
    line_ref: 10-12
    quote: SELECT * FROM banned_hotkeys WHERE miner_hotkey = $1
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: api/endpoints/scoring.py
    line_ref: 63-65
    quote: screener_1_threshold=config.SCREENER_1_THRESHOLD, screener_2_threshold=config.SCREENER_2_THRESHOLD,
      prune_threshold=config.PRUNE_THRESHOLD,
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/3/description
    source_path: alembic/versions/2026_05_07_consensus_agent_scores.py
    line_ref: 111-113
    quote: HAVING vc.validator_count >= 2 AND COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE cbp.solved_validator_count = vc.validator_count)
      > 0
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/stages
    source_path: api/endpoints/scoring.py
    line_ref: 66-68
    quote: EvaluationSetGroup.screener_1 ... EvaluationSetGroup.screener_2 ... EvaluationSetGroup.validator
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/state_kinds
    source_path: db/models/agent.py
    line_ref: 89-93
    quote: 'final_score: Mapped[float] = mapped_column(sa.Float, nullable=False) ... sa.Index("idx_agent_scores_final_score",
      "final_score")'
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: queries/scores.py
    line_ref: 23-26
    quote: ass.approved AND ass.approved_at <= NOW() AND ass.approved_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '12 hours'
      AND ass.set_id = (SELECT MAX(set_id) FROM evaluation_sets)
  unresolved:
  - Numeric values of SCREENER_1_THRESHOLD, SCREENER_2_THRESHOLD, PRUNE_THRESHOLD, SET_WEIGHTS_INTERVAL_SECONDS,
    and NETUID are env-configured and not present as literals in the source.
  - Whether BURN is currently enabled in production (runtime env state) cannot be determined from the
    repo.
  - Exact problem dataset identity/origin (e.g. SWE-bench version) is not pinned in the read files.
composition:
  shape: multiplex
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: SAFE SCAN (cancer-ai)
docs.text:
Subnet safe-scan-ai__cancer-ai netuid 76 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners submit ONNX cancer-detection (skin lesion classification) models referenced on-chain; validators download each model and evaluate it on a held-out dataset pushed to HuggingFace. Models are scored by classification quality (accuracy + risk-category-weighted F1) plus a model-size efficiency ter DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, Deduplication"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: composite"]
  M1["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: RankBased"]
  SM["smooth: Sma"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: safeScanCancerAi

    netuid: 76
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: model_weights

    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, performance_ledger }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: RankBased {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: Sma {
            window: 5
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "180 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: The UID-0 burn override in set_weights() is commented out (lines 303-309 of base_validator.py),
  so weights are emitted from real competition scores. Rewards are distributed to the top-10 miners per
  competition via SCORE_DISTRIBUTION with minimal participation rewards beyond rank 10.
subnet:
  name: SAFE SCAN (cancer-ai)
  owner_repo: safe-scan-ai__cancer-ai
  netuid: 76
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners submit ONNX cancer-detection (skin lesion classification) models referenced on-chain;
    validators download each model and evaluate it on a held-out dataset pushed to HuggingFace. Models
    are scored by classification quality (accuracy + risk-category-weighted F1) plus a model-size efficiency
    term. Per competition, the top-10 miners (ranked by moving-average score) receive a fixed reward split,
    and the winner is the highest moving-average model.
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
scoring_signals:
- name: prediction_quality
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.9
  normalization: none
  notes: prediction_score = ACCURACY_WEIGHT*accuracy + WEIGHTED_F1_WEIGHT*weighted_f1 with ACCURACY_WEIGHT=0.5
    and WEIGHTED_F1_WEIGHT=0.5; contributes to final score with PREDICTION_WEIGHT=0.9. weighted_f1 is
    computed over risk categories with CATEGORY_WEIGHTS HIGH_RISK=3.0, MEDIUM_RISK=2.0, BENIGN=1.0.
- name: efficiency
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: min_max
  notes: 'efficiency_score linearly interpolated from model size in MB: 1.0 at MIN_MODEL_SIZE_MB=50, 0.0
    at MAX_MODEL_SIZE_MB=150; contributes with EFFICIENCY_WEIGHT=0.1. final_score = 0.9*prediction_score
    + 0.1*efficiency_score.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Held-out labeled skin-lesion image dataset (with age/gender/location metadata) released
    by organizations to HuggingFace and detected by the validator's monitor_datasets loop; evaluation
    is deterministic (rounded metrics, fixed preprocessing).
  source_identifier: HuggingFace dataset repos (data_package.dataset_hf_repo / dataset_hf_filename)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: competition_id e.g. tricorder-3; datasets pushed for evaluation.
aggregation:
  method: rank_based
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: A UID-0 burn block exists in set_weights() but is fully commented out (raw_weights[0]=1.0;
      raw_weights[1:]=0.0), so no burn is applied.
  notes: Per competition, hotkeys are ranked by average score (descending); top-10 get fixed fractions
    from SCORE_DISTRIBUTION (1st 0.50, 2nd 0.17, 3rd 0.10, 4th 0.07, 5th 0.05, 6th 0.04, 7th 0.03, 8th
    0.02, 9th 0.01, 10th 0.005) scaled by the per-competition weight; miners beyond top-10 share BEYOND_TOP_10_TOTAL_SCORE=0.005
    via a linear-rank formula. Competition weights come from a competition_weights.yml file on HuggingFace.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: self.scores accumulated across competitions in update_scores(); set_weights() L1-normalizes
    scores (raw_weights = scores/norm), processes via process_weights_for_netuid, then calls subtensor.set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 180 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: sma
    alpha: null
    window: 5
    notes: Per-competition per-hotkey score is a moving average over the last MOVING_AVERAGE_LENGTH=5
      scores (update_average_score); score history capped at HISTORY_LENGTH=10. epoch length default 180
      blocks (config neuron.epoch_length).
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Dataset/metadata validation in BaseTricorderCompetitionHandler: if more than MAX_INVALID_ENTRIES=2
    entries are invalid the competition is cancelled (raise ValueError). Metrics rounded to 6 decimals
    and preprocessing fixed for deterministic scoring across hardware.'
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: get_comparable_result builds a sha256 signature over sampled top-class/prob predictions
    to group duplicate model submissions (anti-copy of predictions).
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Multiple independent competitions run concurrently, each keyed by competition_id (e.g. tricorder-3);
    per-competition weights are read from competition_weights.yml. Reward pool per competition = total
    miner emission divided by number of competitions (per README).
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - performance_ledger
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Per-hotkey score history kept to last HISTORY_LENGTH=10 entries; dead hotkeys (not in
    metagraph) removed via delete_dead_hotkeys; replaced hotkeys zeroed in resync_metagraph.
  notes: CompetitionResultsStore holds score_map {competition_id:{hotkey:[ModelScore]}}, average_scores,
    and current_top_hotkeys; persisted to state.json.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - DOCS/COMPETITIONS.md
  completeness: medium

docs.title: Score Vision (SN44 / GSR)
docs.text:
Subnet score-technologies__score-vision netuid 44 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners perform Game State Recognition on football video: per-frame object detection and tracking (players, goalkeepers, referees, ball) plus pitch keypoint detection. Validators download a challenge video, select frames, and score the miner's returned detections/ keypoints. Reward combines a quality DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: ReferenceModel"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: LivenessCheck, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: vlm_judgment"]
  M1["Metric: composite"]
  M2["Metric: latency"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: scoreVisionSn44Gsr

    netuid: 44
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: signals

    @guards {
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, cumulative_score }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: vlm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: ReferenceModel {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "WEIGHTS_INTERVAL = 30 minutes"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Validator computes a real per-miner final_score (quality 0.65 + speed 0.35) and
  sets on-chain weights as final_score ** (3 * ALPHA_SCORING_MULTIPLICATOR), normalized to sum to 1. No
  burn / zeroing logic was found in set_weights.py; if all scores are zero the validator distributes weight
  evenly across nodes.
subnet:
  name: Score Vision (SN44 / GSR)
  owner_repo: score-technologies__score-vision
  netuid: 44
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners perform Game State Recognition on football video: per-frame object detection and tracking
    (players, goalkeepers, referees, ball) plus pitch keypoint detection. Validators download a challenge
    video, select frames, and score the miner''s returned detections/ keypoints. Reward combines a quality
    score (CLIP-based bbox confidence + homography/keypoint consistency) with a processing-time-based
    speed score.'
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: bbox_score
  metric_kind: vlm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Average CLIP-based bounding-box confidence score over selected frames (evaluate_bboxes / evaluate_frame
    in bbox_clip.py). Forms half of quality_score. Also acts as a gate: bbox_score <= 0.2 forces speed_score
    to 0.'
- name: keypoints_final_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: none
  notes: Keypoint/homography quality score (calculate_final_score_keypoints), divided by 100. Weighted
    blend of keypoint_score 0.25, mean_on_line 0.4, mean_inside 0.2, keypoint_stability 0.05, player_final_score
    0.1, then multiplied by kpts_stability_weighted_avg and (mean_scale+scale_valid)/2. Returns 0 if mean_on_line<=0.2.
    Forms half of quality_score.
- name: speed_score
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.35
  normalization: min_max
  notes: 'Exponential decay of normalized processing_time: exp(-5 * normalized_time) where normalized_time
    = (processing_time - min_time)/(MAX_PROCESSING_TIME - min_time). Zeroed if bbox_score<=0.2 or processing_time>=MAX_PROCESSING_TIME
    (15.0s).'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: reference_model
  description: CLIP (openai/clip-vit-base-patch32) used for semantic verification of bounding-box class
    labels, plus geometric/homography consistency checks (RANSAC homography to a fixed SoccerPitchConfiguration)
    for keypoints. No external labeled ground-truth dataset; quality is assessed via model + geometric
    plausibility checks.
  source_identifier: openai/clip-vit-base-patch32
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: 'Keypoint scoring is reference-free (self-consistency: reprojection error, stability, on-line/inside
    fractions). bbox scoring uses CLIP as the judge model.'
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn found. If total weight is 0 (no positive scores), weights are distributed evenly across
      all nodes rather than burned.
  notes: 'quality_score = bbox_score*0.5 + keypoints_final_score*0.5; final_score = quality_score*0.65
    + speed_score*0.35. On-chain weight per node = final_score ** (3 * ALPHA_SCORING_MULTIPLICATOR) with
    ALPHA_SCORING_MULTIPLICATOR=4 (exponent 12), then normalized so weights sum to 1.0. Composition is
    hybrid: quality signals are additive among themselves but speed gating/zeroing is multiplicative/gated.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: set_weights() reads per-miner final_score from the DB (last 24h), raises each to the power (3*ALPHA_SCORING_MULTIPLICATOR),
    normalizes to sum 1.0, and calls fiber weights.set_node_weights on chain with wait_for_inclusion/finalization.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: WEIGHTS_INTERVAL = 30 minutes
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of the final reward in code. Scores are pulled from the DB over the last
      24 hours; raising to a high power (exponent 12) sharpens the distribution toward top miners.
anti_gaming:
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Validators select random frames (select_random_frames) from a downloaded challenge video
    to validate against, and enforce MAX_PROCESSING_TIME (15.0s); responses with bbox_score<=0.2 or processing_time>=MAX_PROCESSING_TIME
    get speed_score 0.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Geometric plausibility / self-consistency checks penalize implausible outputs: RANSAC
    homography requiring >=4 valid keypoints, player movement plausibility (max_speed_pixels), scene-transition
    detection, keypoint stability, and scoreboard-zone / min-size bbox filtering. keypoints_final_score
    returns 0 when mean_on_line<=0.2.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: set_weights uses get_miner_scores_with_node_id over the last 24 hours of stored task scores;
    miners with no scores default to final_score 0.0.
  notes: Per-response scores (quality_score, speed_score, final_score, processing_time) are stored in
    the validator DB and aggregated per node for weight setting.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - validator/README.md
  - miner/README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '92'
    quote: mainnet deploy on netuid 44 week commencing 13 January
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: validator/config.py
    line_ref: 1-5
    quote: import os / from datetime import timedelta / from pathlib import Path / from dotenv import
      load_dotenv

    line_ref: 34-36
    quote: Each frame undergoes processing for object detection and tracking ... validators verify miners'

      stability and plausibility, while object detections undergo semantic verification
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/name
    source_path: validator/evaluation/calculate_score.py
    line_ref: 50-57
    quote: bbox_score = result['validation_result'].score ... quality_score= (bbox_score*0.5) + (keypoints_final_score*0.5)
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes

    line_ref: 968-998
    quote: 'weights = { ''keypoint_score'': 0.25, ''mean_on_line'': 0.4, ''keypoint_stability'': 0.05,
      ''player_final_score'': 0.1, ''mean_inside'': 0.2 }'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: validator/evaluation/evaluation.py
    line_ref: '225'

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes

    line_ref: 60-63

      = calculate_speed_score(processing_time, min_time, max_time)'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: validator/evaluation/calculate_score.py

    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: validator/evaluation/bbox_clip.py
    line_ref: 26-27
    quote: clip_model = CLIPModel.from_pretrained("openai/clip-vit-base-patch32").to("cpu")
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: validator/evaluation/keypoint_scoring.py
    line_ref: 964-965
    quote: 'if round(mean_on_line, 2) <= 0.2: return 0.0'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: validator/evaluation/set_weights.py
    line_ref: 76-91
    quote: miner_scores = db_manager.get_miner_scores_with_node_id() ... final_score=score_data['final_score']
    confidence: high
  unresolved:
  - Documented GS-HOTA metric (README) is not used in the code scoring path; actual reward uses CLIP bbox
    + homography keypoint scores.
  - Whether the live mainnet validator burns or zeroes emissions could not be confirmed from source; no
    burn logic present in set_weights.py.
  - Exact mapping of validator-stored scores to the 24h aggregation window (get_miner_scores_with_node_id
    implementation in db/operations not read).
  extractor_notes: Code is authoritative for scoring. README describes a GS-HOTA metric and a more elaborate
    miner/validator reward split than the implemented per-miner final_score path. Weight sharpening via
    exponent 12 (3*ALPHA_SCORING_MULTIPLICATOR) is the dominant winner-favoring mechanism.
composition:
  shape: pipeline
  overlays:
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Brainplay
docs.text:
Subnet shiftlayer-llc__brainplay-subnet netuid None Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners deploy AI model endpoints on Targon (TVM) and commit the endpoint UID on-chain. Validators query those endpoints to make the models compete in games (Codenames, 20 Questions, SuperMario). Miners are rewarded for winning / performing best in the games; the single top-scoring miner per competit DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: LlmJudgment, DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: StakeWeighting, LivenessCheck, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: win_rate"]
  M1["Metric: accuracy"]
  M2["Metric: vlm_judgment"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  SM["smooth: Sma"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: brainplay

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: [compute_service, commitment_hash]

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: 0.9
    }
    @guards {
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: win_rate
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: vlm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: oracle
            },
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: l1
            min_weight_floor: 0.001
        }
        smooth: Sma {
            window: 86400
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "1 days"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Every weight update mixes 90% burn (UID 0) with 10% real distribution via --burn_ratio
  default 0.90 in game/base/validator.py::_set_weights. In addition, whole weight groups fall back to
  a 100% burn vector (all weight to UID 0) when scores are stale (>1h), there are <30 games, no scores,
  a non-positive or tied top score, or the winner is missing from the metagraph. The documented winner-
  take-all scoring is live but only ever expresses through the 10% non-burn share.
subnet:
  name: Brainplay
  owner_repo: shiftlayer-llc__brainplay-subnet
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners deploy AI model endpoints on Targon (TVM) and commit the endpoint UID on-chain. Validators
    query those endpoints to make the models compete in games (Codenames, 20 Questions, SuperMario). Miners
    are rewarded for winning / performing best in the games; the single top-scoring miner per competition
    window takes essentially all of that competition's non-burn weight.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: codenames_team_win
  metric_kind: win_rate
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Legacy Codenames reward (game/validator/reward.py::get_rewards): winning team''s members get
    1.0, losers 0.0. Aggregated as a window-average win rate per hotkey before winner selection.'
- name: twentyq_solve
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: '20 Questions per-attempt score: 0.0 if unsolved or question_index<=0; 1.0 if solved within 20
    questions; for index in 21..30 a linear penalty 1.0 - 0.1*(index-20); 0.0 beyond 30 questions.'
- name: supermario_progress
  metric_kind: vlm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: 'Round-relative SuperMario score: best valid run gets 1.0; other valid runs scale by progress_from_start
    / best_progress (clamped to [0,1]); failed or invalid runs (no artifacts, error, invalid_response)
    get 0.0.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: 20 Questions referee yes/no/unknown answers are produced by an LLM judge provider (chutes.ai,
    default model openai/gpt-oss-120b-TEE), with a deterministic local heuristic fallback when credentials
    are absent.
  source_identifier: https://llm.chutes.ai/v1
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: ''
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: SuperMario uses an isolated Mario benchmark environment; progress and level-completion
    are measured from the run artifacts/env_score returned by the miner endpoint, with validator-side
    image-hash verification.
  source_identifier: 4b9ba675ef3c8ca8b8e41dfe7636b5c72c507711befe76562d18326572efcfef
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: ''
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: 0.001
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 0.9
    dynamic: false
    notes: Fixed 0.90 burn_ratio mixed into every published weight vector (_set_weights). Separately,
      the entire vector becomes a pure burn vector (UID 0 = 1.0) under stale/insufficient/no-score/tie/missing-winner
      conditions.
  notes: 'Per competition, scores are averaged over a rolling window per hotkey (default 1 day). Only
    hotkeys meeting the stake requirement and with games >= record_count_limit (int(0.9*median game count))
    are eligible. The single unique top-scoring hotkey gets raw weight 1.0; every other hotkey with score
    >0 gets a 0.001 floor; the vector is L1-normalized, then 90% burn is mixed in. Weight groups combine
    competitions by ratio (llm group: codenames 0.5 / twentyq 0.5 when both present; otherwise 1/count).'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: set_weights per weight group per publish_mechid. llm group (codenames+twentyq) publishes on
    mechid=0; vision group (supermario) on mechid=1. Snapshots must be fresh (TTL 3600s); missing fresh
    snapshots keep last publication or burn.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 1 days
  smoothing:
    kind: sma
    alpha: null
    window: 86400
    notes: Scoring window is a rolling simple average of per-hotkey scores over scoring.interval (default
      '1 days'); not an EMA. Set via window_average_scores_by_hotkey over [since_ts, end_ts].
anti_gaming:
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Only hotkeys with metagraph stake S[uid] >= neuron.minimum_stake_requirement are counted
    toward scores/weights (default 0).
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: If the latest synced score is older than 1 hour the validator switches the whole competition
    to a burn vector; competitions with <30 games in the window are skipped (burned).
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: record_count_limit = int(0.9 * median eligible game count) excludes actors with few games
    (e.g. new registrations) from winner eligibility.
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: rejection
  description: For SuperMario, validators verify the deployed miner container image hash against the canonical
    hash before using the endpoint.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: codenames
    role: main
    advancement_rule: weight_group llm, publish_mechid 0; window-average win-rate winner-take-all
  - name: twentyq
    role: main
    advancement_rule: weight_group llm, publish_mechid 0; window-average score winner-take-all
  - name: supermario
    role: main
    advancement_rule: weight_group vision, publish_mechid 1; round-relative progress winner-take-all
  count: 3
  notes: Three competitions across two weight groups / two mechids. llm group shares mechid 0 (codenames
    0.5 + twentyq 0.5); vision group is mechid 1.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: 'Rolling-window based: scores are summed/averaged only over the configured scoring window
    (default 1 day); older records age out of the window.'
  notes: Per-hotkey window aggregates (avg_scores, total_scores, counts, win/loss counts, observer counts)
    are maintained in the score/generic stores keyed by competition and validator hotkey.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: 2.4.1
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/running_on_mainnet.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '5'
    quote: '# Brainplay'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '95'
    quote: '**Python Version** (Python 3.10 + recommended)'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 18-22
    quote: Uses TVM (Targon) for miner model submission and validator-side querying ... Miners deploy
      models via Targon; validators query server endpoints miners deployed via TVM
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 240-242
    quote: Deploy a serverless model endpoint on Targon. ... Commit that endpoint UID on-chain so validators
      can discover and query it.
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: game/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 63-68
    quote: 'if winner == "red": rewards = np.array([1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0]) elif winner == "blue": rewards
      = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 1.0, 0.0]) else: rewards = np.array([0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0])'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: game/plugins/twentyq/scoring.py
    line_ref: 4-14
    quote: 'if not solved: return 0.0 ... if question_index <= 20: return 1.0 if question_index > 30:
      return 0.0 bonus_used = question_index - 20 return max(0.0, 1.0 - 0.1 * bonus_used)'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes

    line_ref: 35-45
    quote: 'if index == 0: scores[participant.hotkey] = 1.0 ... score = max(0.0, min(1.0, float(participant.progress_from_start)
      / best_progress))'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes

  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier

docs.title: Sparket Subnet
docs.text:
Subnet sparket-ai__sparket-ai netuid None Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status mixed Task Miners submit sports-betting odds and event outcomes. Two task tracks: Odds Origination (miner decimal odds / implied probabilities scored against a consensus closing line via CLV, CLE, MES) and Outcome Verification (miner probabilities scored against the realized result via Brier, LogLoss, PSS). Pe DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData, RealEngagement"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, Deduplication, RateLimit"}}:::ov

  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0

  G --> M1

  G --> M2

  G --> M3

  M6 --> AGG

  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
