  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: sparketSubnet

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: mixed
    submission: signals

    @burn {
        uid: "subnet owner / burn hotkey UID (get_subnet_owner_hotkey)"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RiskLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, performance_ledger }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: probabilistic_forecast
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: probabilistic_forecast
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: pnl
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: zscore
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: probabilistic_forecast
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: zscore
            },
            Metric {
                kind: predictive_contribution
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: predictive_contribution
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            MarketData {
                trust_model: oracle
            },
            RealEngagement {
                trust_model: oracle
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            window: 30
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: 'Active per-submission scoring pipeline (Cobb-Douglas SkillScore over 5 pillars)
  feeds on-chain weights. Burn allocation is configurable: the live set_weights.py path reads burn_rate
  from scoring_params.py where the default is 0.0 (no burn), but the ledger compute_weights.py and auditor
  weight_verification.py paths default burn_rate to 0.9 (90% to subnet owner). When all miner scores are
  zero, 100% is allocated to the burn hotkey. Hence the operative burn fraction depends on which code
  path / config is live and cannot be pinned to a single value from source.'
subnet:
  name: Sparket Subnet
  owner_repo: sparket-ai__sparket-ai
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners submit sports-betting odds and event outcomes. Two task tracks: Odds Origination (miner
    decimal odds / implied probabilities scored against a consensus closing line via CLV, CLE, MES) and
    Outcome Verification (miner probabilities scored against the realized result via Brier, LogLoss, PSS).
    Per-submission metrics are aggregated over a rolling window with time decay and shrinkage, then combined
    into a multiplicative Cobb-Douglas SkillScore across five pillars (Accuracy, Edge, Timeliness, Uniqueness,
    Marginal).'
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: forecast_quality
  metric_kind: probabilistic_forecast
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.6
  normalization: min_max
  notes: FQ = 1 - 2*brier_mean, mapped [-1,1] -> [0,1] via (FQ+1)/2. Combined with calibration into ForecastDim
    (Accuracy pillar). w_fq default 0.6.
- name: calibration
  metric_kind: probabilistic_forecast
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.4
  normalization: none
  notes: CAL = 1 / (1 + |b-1| + |a|) from logit regression fit; clipped to [0,1]. w_cal default 0.4. ForecastDim
    = w_fq*FQ_norm + w_cal*CAL.
- name: economic_edge
  metric_kind: pnl
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.7
  normalization: zscore
  notes: ES_adj = es_mean/es_std (Sharpe-like, from CLE = O_miner*p_close - 1), normalized via zscore_logistic
    (or percentile when <10 miners). w_edge 0.7.
- name: market_efficiency
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: none
  notes: MES = 1 - min(1, |CLV_prob|); clipped to [0,1]. w_mes default 0.3. EconDim = w_edge*ES_norm +
    w_mes*MES (Edge pillar).
- name: relative_skill_pss
  metric_kind: probabilistic_forecast
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: zscore
  notes: PSS = 1 - (miner_score/baseline_score), time-adjusted, normalized via zscore_logistic. Feeds
    SkillDim and Timeliness pillar (0.5*SkillDim + 0.5*LEAD).
- name: lead_ratio
  metric_kind: predictive_contribution
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: LEAD = Moves_led / Moves_matched; clipped [0,1]. Feeds Timeliness pillar (0.5*SkillDim + 0.5*lead_norm).
- name: uniqueness
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Uniqueness pillar. Composite SOS (market+crowd+cluster) from Shapley/correlation jobs, with bootstrap
    fallback SOS = 1 - |correlation|. Cobb-Douglas exponent 1.5 (highest, strong anti-sybil incentive).
- name: marginal_contribution
  metric_kind: predictive_contribution
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Marginal pillar = leave-one-out Shapley contribution to crowd forecast; bootstrap fallback 0.5
    (neutral). Cobb-Douglas exponent 1.0.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: Consensus closing line per market and side, built from ingested provider (sportsbook) odds
    via inverse-variance weighting with bias adjustment. Benchmark for both economic edge and outcome-relative
    (PSS) scoring.
  source_identifier: sparket/providers (sportsdataio); consensus closing line
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: min_books_for_consensus default 2; time-matched snapshots every snapshot_interval_hours (default
    6).
- kind: real_engagement
  description: Realized settled event outcomes (one-hot y_k) recorded when events settle, used for Brier
    / LogLoss / PSS outcome accuracy scoring.
  source_identifier: settled event outcomes
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Outcome accuracy track.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: subnet owner / burn hotkey UID (get_subnet_owner_hotkey)
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: 'burn_rate default differs by code path: scoring_params.WeightEmissionParams default 0.0 (live
      set_weights.py); ledger/compute_weights.py and auditor weight_verification.py default 0.9. burn_rate
      fraction goes to burn_uid, remaining (1-burn_rate) scaled across miners. If all scores zero -> 100%
      burn.'
  notes: 'Per-submission metrics aggregated over rolling window (default 30 days) with exponential time
    decay (half_life default 10 days; w_i = exp(ln(0.5)*age/half_life)) and Bayesian shrinkage toward
    population mean (k default 200). Within-dimension combination is additive (weighted_average); across-pillar
    combination is multiplicative Cobb-Douglas: SkillScore = Accuracy^0.5 * Edge^1.0 * Timeliness^0.5
    * Uniqueness^1.5 * Marginal^1.0. Pillars clamped to [epsilon=0.01, 1.0]. Final SkillScores are L1-normalized
    across miners before chain emission.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Load latest per-miner skill_score from miner_rolling_score DB table, fill score array by UID
    (zeros for missing), replace NaN with 0, L1-normalize, apply burn_rate redistribution, process_weights_for_netuid
    (max_weight_limit / min_allowed_weights), convert to uint16, subtensor.set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: null
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: null
    window: 30
    notes: No EMA on the final emitted weight itself; temporal smoothing is upstream via exponential time-decayed
      rolling aggregates over rolling_window_days (default 30) with half_life_days (default 10). alpha
      not expressed as a literal constant.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: elimination
  description: 'Hard accuracy floor: SkillScore set to 0 if brier_mean > floor_threshold (default 0.30),
    regardless of other pillars.'
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Cluster/sub-window correlation detection penalizes near-identical (sybil / copy-trading)
    submissions via the Uniqueness pillar (composite SOS with cluster_correlation_threshold default 0.85).
    Super-linear exponent (1.5) makes low uniqueness collapse the total score.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Per-miner daily submission caps: max_submissions_per_market_day default 200, max_outcomes_per_event_day
    default 50; odds/outcome time-bucket dedup.'
- kind: risk_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Time-to-close weighting: late correct predictions get reduced credit (floor_factor default
    0.1), early bad predictions clipped (early_penalty_clip default 0.7) to discourage last-minute copy-trading.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Security bounds reject invalid inputs: odds in (1.01, 1000], probabilities in [0.001,
    0.999], CLE clamped, max_odds_ratio_for_cle default 2.0 excludes extreme-odds gaming from economic
    edge.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages: []
  count: 2
  notes: 'Two task tracks: Odds Origination (vs closing line) and Outcome Verification (vs realized result).
    Both feed the same composite SkillScore rather than separate weight pools.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - performance_ledger
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Rolling window (default 30 days) with exponential time decay; old submissions decay out.
  notes: Per-miner rolling aggregates stored in miner_rolling_score (skill_score and component dimensions,
    score_version). Effective sample size n_eff tracked; metrics shrunk toward population mean for low-sample
    miners.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - docs/im.md

docs.title: Sportstensor
docs.text:
Subnet sportstensor__sn41 netuid 41 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status unknown Task Miners are sports-prediction traders. They place real money trades on Polymarket prediction markets (routed through the Almanac front end / proxy wallet) and their completed trades are scored as predictions against real market outcomes. The validator pulls each miner's 30-day trading history from Al DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, DeterministicCheck, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: pnl"]
  M1["Metric: throughput"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: sportstensor

    netuid: 41
    lang: python
    status: unknown
    submission: api_response

    @burn {
        uid: "210"
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: pnl
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: MarketData {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            normalization: sum_to_one
            decay_rate: 0.9
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "hourly (scoring/set_weights at a random minute 10-25 UTC each hour); 24h epoch window"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
subnet:
  name: Sportstensor
  owner_repo: sportstensor__sn41
  netuid: 41
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners are sports-prediction traders. They place real money trades on Polymarket prediction
    markets (routed through the Almanac front end / proxy wallet) and their completed trades are scored
    as predictions against real market outcomes. The validator pulls each miner's 30-day trading history
    from Almanac's backend API, computes profitability (ROI = PnL / volume) and qualified (winning, after-fee)
    volume over daily epochs, gates out unprofitable/inactive traders, and runs a two-phase convex optimizer
    that funds qualified volume within an epoch budget while redistributing payout toward higher-ROI traders.
    Rewards (Alpha token emissions) flow to consistently profitable, high-volume traders.
  submission_format:
  - api_response
  submission_format_other: ''
  extensions:
    notes: Miners do not submit signals directly to the validator; their "submission" is the set of completed
      Polymarket trades retrieved from the Almanac trading-history API. Identity is bound by committing
      a (partial) Polymarket profile id to chain metadata.
scoring_signals:
- name: roi_trailing
  metric_kind: pnl
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Trailing ROI = total_profit / total_volume across the rolling 30-day window. Used as an eligibility
    gate (roi_trailing >= ROI_MIN=0.0) and as the per-miner signal strength that Phase 2 redistributes
    payout toward. Exact relative weight vs qualified volume is set implicitly by the convex optimizer,
    not a fixed scalar.
- name: qualified_volume
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Qualified volume = volume from winning trades after fee deduction (qualified = volume - actual_fees
    when is_correct). Phase 1 maximizes routed qualified volume fundable within budget. metric_kind 'throughput'
    is the closest enum for trading volume; this is a stress point (no 'volume' metric kind).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: Real Polymarket prediction-market trade outcomes (is_correct, pnl, volume, fees) for each
    miner's completed trades, retrieved per-epoch from Almanac's trading-history API. The "correct answer"
    is whether each market resolved in the trader's favor, plus realized PnL.
  source_identifier: https://api.almanac.market/api/v1/trading/trading-history
  trust_model: trusted
  extensions:
    notes: Authenticated (HTTPBasicAuth with hotkey signature). Validator trusts the Almanac backend as
      the authoritative source of trade outcomes and PnL.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  normalization: sum_to_one
  decay_rate: 0.9
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '210'
    fraction: null
    notes: General-pool (non-registered-miner) weight is always assigned to BURN_UID=210, and all excess/unallocated
      weight is also burned to BURN_UID (EXCESS_MINER_TAKE_PERCENTAGE=0 means none is diverted to an excess
      UID). The burn fraction is not a fixed constant; it is the residual 1.0 - total_allocated_weight
      plus the general-pool weight, computed per epoch.
  notes: Per-miner token allocations come from a two-phase CVXPY optimization (Phase 1 maximizes fundable
    qualified volume; Phase 2 redistributes toward higher ROI). Final per-UID weight = miner_tokens /
    total_epoch_budget, miner-pool weights boosted by MINER_POOL_WEIGHT_BOOST_ PERCENTAGE=1 (100%), then
    the whole vector is normalized to sum to 1.0. VOLUME_DECAY=0.9 drives a decaying volume memory (v_memory
    = 0.9*v_memory + epoch_volume).
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Validator runs scoring + set_weights once per hour (at a random UTC minute), computing the previous
    24h epoch. Builds a weight per UID from optimized token allocations divided by the total epoch budget
    (alpha_price_usd * TOTAL_MINER_ALPHA_PER_DAY=2952), boosts miner-pool weights, assigns general-pool
    + excess weight to BURN_UID, normalizes to sum to 1.0.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: hourly (scoring/set_weights at a random minute 10-25 UTC each hour); 24h epoch window
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Build-up eligibility gates: a trader must have traded in at least MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_ ELIGIBILITY
    epochs and have at least MIN_PREDICTIONS_FOR_ELIGIBILITY predictions, plus pass roi_trailing >= ROI_MIN
    and qualified volume >= VOLUME_MIN, to receive any reward. (In this snapshot MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_ELIGIBILITY=0,
    MIN_PREDICTIONS_FOR_ELIGIBILITY=1, ROI_MIN=0.0, VOLUME_MIN=1.)'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Trade-integrity checks mark a trade as not reward-eligible if actual volume exceeds expected
    volume by more than a $5 buffer (position added outside Almanac), or if actual fees are less than
    90% of expected fees (underpaid fees).
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Diversity cap: Phase 1 constrains each miner''s funded payout to rho_cap*B (RHO_CAP=0.1,
    i.e. <=10% of budget) to prevent any single trader from dominating.'
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Identity binding: miners commit a (partial) Polymarket profile id to on-chain metadata
    (subtensor.get_commitment). The validator zeroes a miner''s weight if the Almanac-reported profile
    id has no/multiple values, has no chain metadata, or the chain-committed polymarket id is not contained
    in the reported profile id (miners_to_penalize -> weight 0).'
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: miner_pool
    role: main
    advancement_rule: Registered miners (UID + matching hotkey) whose trades are reward-eligible. Scored
      in their own pool with its own budget; eligible for budget/weight boosts.
    multipliers:
      early_stage_min_fee_multiplier: 1.2
      miner_pool_weight_boost: 1
  - name: general_pool
    role: other
    advancement_rule: Non-registered (general-pool) Polymarket profiles, not the 'main' registered-miner
      pool. Scored separately; their total weight is always assigned to BURN_UID rather than to the traders.
    multipliers:
      early_stage_min_fee_multiplier: 1.2
  count: 2
  notes: 'Dual-pool system: registered miners vs a general pool of traders. Each pool gets a separate

docs.title: SN108 Internet of Intelligence (Agent Launchpad)
docs.text:
Subnet subnet108__internet-of-intelligence netuid 108 Archetype multiplicative Language python Mechanism status active Task A miner runs a server (queried at the config endpoint) that reports its GPU inventory and the uptime/status of its running containers ("pods"). The validator queries a random sample of miners, verifies the reported identity (ip/port/coldkey/hotkey/nonce/signature/timestamp), then scores each miner o DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: HardwareProbe"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: LivenessCheck, Deduplication, HardwareValidation"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: hardware_spec"]
  M1["Metric: uptime"]
  M2["Metric: uptime"]
  M3["Metric: hardware_spec"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: sn108InternetOfIntelligenceAgentLaunchpad

    netuid: 108
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [compute_service, api_response]

    @guards {
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Multiplicative {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: hardware_spec
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: hardware_spec
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: HardwareProbe {
            trust_model: adversarial
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: multiplicative
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: The live validator scores miners on GPU hardware inventory and container uptime
  (a proof-of-resources mechanism) in evaluate_miners.get_rewards, normalizes to sum=1, and EMAs into
  on-chain weights. The README markets an aspirational "AI Agent Launchpad / TAOPump" economy with agent
  tokens, creators and users, but none of that scoring is present in code — only hardware/uptime scoring
  is implemented. No burn UID is present.
subnet:
  name: SN108 Internet of Intelligence (Agent Launchpad)
  owner_repo: subnet108__internet-of-intelligence
  netuid: 108
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: A miner runs a server (queried at the config endpoint) that reports its GPU inventory and the
    uptime/status of its running containers ("pods"). The validator queries a random sample of miners,
    verifies the reported identity (ip/port/coldkey/hotkey/nonce/signature/timestamp), then scores each
    miner on (a) its share of total GPUs, (b) its share of total container uptime, and (c) a bonus for
    long-running containers, all multiplied by a GPU-model rarity weight. There is no AI/agent task being
    evaluated in code; reward is hardware capacity and liveness.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: gpu_count_share
  metric_kind: hardware_spec
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: min_max
  notes: score_a = len(r["gpu"]) / total_gpus across all valid responses; weighted by GPU_CORES_SCORE
    = 0.5.
- name: container_uptime_share
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: min_max
  notes: score_b = sum of uptimes of containers with status==1, divided by total uptime across all valid
    responses; weighted by POD_RUN_TIME_SCORE = 0.3.
- name: long_running_bonus
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: none
  notes: score_c = 1/total_long_run if a miner's average container uptime exceeds POD_RUN_TIME_AVG_DAY
    (604800s = 7 days); weighted by POD_RUN_TIME_AVG_SCORE = 0.1.
- name: gpu_model_rarity_multiplier
  metric_kind: hardware_spec
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: gpu_weight = sum of GPU_MODEL_RATES per GPU model; applied as a multiplicative (1 + gpu_weight)
    factor on the additive score, not an additive term. Rates literal in const.py (e.g. H100 80GB HBM3
    = .10).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: Self-reported GPU inventory and container uptime returned by the miner's local server,
    attested with an Ed25519/RSA-style signature verified against a baked-in public.key plus identity
    checks (ip/port/coldkey/hotkey, nonce, timestamp freshness within 10s).
  source_identifier: neurons/validator/src/config/public.key
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Values are self-reported by miners; validator only verifies signature and identity fields, not
    the actual presence of the hardware.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: sum_to_one
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn UID or burn fraction present in code.
  notes: total_score = (0.5*score_a + 0.3*score_b + 0.1*score_c) * (1 + gpu_weight); then scores_result
    = scores_result / total_sum so the vector sums to 1.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: update_scores EMAs the per-step normalized rewards into self.scores (alpha*new + (1-alpha)*old);
    set_weights L1-normalizes self.scores, runs process_weights_for_netuid (quantile/max-weight-limit
    clamp), converts to uint16 and calls subtensor.set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: moving_average_alpha default 0.1 in add_validator_args; applied in update_scores as alpha*scattered_rewards
      + (1-alpha)*self.scores.
anti_gaming:
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Responses are dropped (scored 0) unless status is true and the reported ip/port/coldkey/hotkey
    match the metagraph axon, nonce matches, signature verifies against public.key, and timestamp is within
    10000 ms of now.
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: If more than one active axon shares the same IP (active_ip_count[ip] > 1), that miner's
    response is invalidated (set to None / score 0).
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: barrier
  description: GPU_MODEL_RATES whitelists rewarded GPU models; unknown models contribute 0 to gpu_weight.
    Only containers with status==1 count toward uptime.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: On resync_metagraph, scores[uid] is zeroed when a hotkey is replaced; scores persisted
    to state.npz and reloaded on start.
  notes: self.scores is an EMA moving average per uid (cumulative_score); no debt ledger or status enum.
documentation:
  status: sparse
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - neurons/validator/README.md
  - neurons/miner/README.md
  completeness: low

  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: neurons/utils/config.py
    line_ref: '75'
    quote: parser.add_argument("--netuid", type=int, help="Subnet netuid", default=108)
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes

    against the netuid's on-chain tempo).

    floor/cap (values read from chain at runtime, not literal in repo).
composition:
  shape: multiplicative
  overlays:
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Minotaur — Agentic Intent Execution Platform
docs.text:
Subnet subnet112__minotaur_subnet netuid 112 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status full_burn Task Miners write and continuously update a "Solving Engine" (solver) that turns user/agent App Intents (e.g. swaps) into execution plans. Validators run the highest-scoring submitted solver inside isolated Docker containers, simulate its plans on an Anvil fork, and double-score each plan against both a DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData, DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CodeInspection, CommitReveal, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  M1["Metric: accuracy"]
  M2["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: minotaurAgenticIntentExecutionPlatform

    netuid: 112
    lang: python
    status: full_burn
    submission: [source_code, compute_service]

    @burn {
        uid: "subnet owner hotkey (SUBNET_OWNER_HOTKEY / OWNER_HOTKEY env)"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @guards {
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, cumulative_score }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                raw: "on_chain_constraint_score_bps"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            MarketData {
                trust_model: oracle
            },
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: sum_to_one
            decay_rate: 0.6
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "epoch (README default 5 minutes; ChampionWeights epoch_seconds default 60)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: single
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: full_burn
mechanism_status_notes: _build_weights_mapping and build_bootstrap_or_champion_weights route 100% of emissions
  to the subnet-owner hotkey (burn) until a "real miner-backed champion" exists (is_real_miner_hotkey
  returns False for the genesis/empty hotkey). The documented scored/ranked decay path only activates
  once a real miner champion has been adopted, so the operative default is a full burn to the owner hotkey.
subnet:
  name: Minotaur — Agentic Intent Execution Platform
  owner_repo: subnet112__minotaur_subnet
  netuid: 112
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners write and continuously update a "Solving Engine" (solver) that turns user/agent App
    Intents (e.g. swaps) into execution plans. Validators run the highest-scoring submitted solver inside
    isolated Docker containers, simulate its plans on an Anvil fork, and double-score each plan against
    both a JavaScript scoring module and an on-chain constraint score. Submissions are benchmarked; the
    highest benchmark_score becomes the "champion" engine, and weights are emitted toward champion-eligible
    miners (decay-ranked) — or burned to the owner hotkey before a real champion exists.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: plan_quality_js_score
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.6
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Plan is scored by the app''s JavaScript scoring module via the JS engine (PlanScorer.score /
    ScoringEngine._score_via_js). When no JS engine is available, compute_mock_score returns a deterministic
    mock in [0.3,1.0]. In run_benchmark this term is combined with plan_quality_weight=0.6. Marked llm_judgment
    as the closest enum to an app-defined scoring program (see provenance.unresolved: metric_kind is a
    stress point).'
- name: auto_trigger_accuracy
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.4
  normalization: none
  notes: For auto-triggered intents, trigger_score is 1.0 if the solver's trigger_decision matches trigger_ground_truth,
    else 0.0. Combined with auto_trigger_weight=0.4 in run_benchmark. Only applied when the intent is
    auto-triggered and ground truth is available; otherwise the composite is just the plan JS score.
- name: on_chain_constraint_score
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: on_chain_constraint_score_bps
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Gating signal (not additive). simulation.on_chain_score must be >= threshold (app_def.config.on_chain_threshold,
    default 5000 BPS) or the proposal is rejected (ON_CHAIN_SCORE_BELOW_THRESHOLD). Also the JS local_score
    must be >= score_threshold (default 0.5) or rejected (SCORE_BELOW_THRESHOLD).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: Plans are re-simulated on an Anvil fork (mainnet/Base/Bittensor-EVM) of real chain state;
    simulation produces gas_used, token_transfers and an on_chain_score used to gate and score solver
    plans.
  source_identifier: Anvil fork (ANVIL_RPC_URL / BASE_RPC_URL / ETHEREUM_RPC_URL)
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: 'README: "Execute the solver software ... within isolated Docker containers to simulate plans
    on an Anvil fork." Followers independently re-simulate (FOLLOWER_PROPOSAL_RESIMULATE default "1").'
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Benchmark scenarios with trigger_ground_truth (correct auto-trigger decision per intent
    app_id) used to score trigger accuracy.
  source_identifier: run_benchmark trigger_ground_truth
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Validator-supplied ground-truth trigger decisions per intent.
aggregation:
  method: exponential_decay_ema
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: 0.6
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: subnet owner hotkey (SUBNET_OWNER_HOTKEY / OWNER_HOTKEY env)
    fraction: 1.0
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'build_bootstrap_or_champion_weights returns {owner: 1.0} (100% burn) when no real miner champion
      exists; once a real champion exists, burn stops and weights go to champion-eligible miners.'
  notes: 'Champion-eligible scored submissions are sorted by benchmark_score descending; raw weight =
    weight_decay**rank (rank 0 = champion, weight_decay default 0.6), then normalized to sum to 1.0. Composition
    is hybrid: per-plan benchmark_score itself is a weighted sum (0.6*JS_score + 0.4*trigger_score) gated
    by JS and on-chain thresholds, while the cross-miner weight distribution is exponential rank decay.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Epoch loop (default 5-minute windows per README; ChampionWeights epoch default 60s) builds a
    hotkey→weight mapping via _build_weights_mapping, then WeightsEmitter.emit_async maps hotkeys→UIDs
    and calls subtensor.set_weights. Only the leader (highest-stake validator) emits (metagraph_sync.is_leader).
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: epoch (README default 5 minutes; ChampionWeights epoch_seconds default 60)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of the final emitted weights found; weights are rebuilt and normalized
      each epoch.
anti_gaming:
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: is_submission_champion_eligible rejects inline-source/subprocess submissions (non-None
    solver_path or repo_url starting 'source://'), requires an image_tag and an immutable image_id starting
    'sha256:'.
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Signed provenance required by default (REQUIRE_SIGNED_PROVENANCE and REQUIRE_ASYMMETRIC_PROVENANCE
    default True). verify_signed_provenance checks the submission's provenance against allowed signers
    / expected image_id; failure makes the submission not champion-eligible.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Consensus proposals must carry a valid EIP-191 proposer_signature from a registered validator
    (verify_proposer_signature); followers independently re-simulate plans (FOLLOWER_PROPOSAL_RESIMULATE
    default 1) and re-score via JS rather than trusting the leader's reported score.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Leader-based OrderBook: only the highest-stake validator maintains the OrderBook and emits
    weights (metagraph_sync.is_leader); consensus requires a quorum (QUORUM_BPS, default 8000).'
sub_competitions:
  structure: single
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: 'Single champion competition: submissions ranked by benchmark_score; the top eligible submission
    becomes the champion engine run across all validators.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - cumulative_score
  status_values:
  - SCORED
  - ADOPTED
  reset_policy: StateStore persists watermarks, last scores and last emitted block per validator; recoverable
    across restarts via JSON snapshot + .backup.
  notes: Submissions carry a status (only SCORED/ADOPTED are champion-eligible) and a benchmark_score;
    champion submission tracked with submission_id, image_id, hotkey, activated_round_id/epoch. The active
    champion can be re-benchmarked (_refresh_incumbent_score) when scenarios change.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/validator/README.md
  - docs/miner/README.md
  - docs/code-verified-runtime.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: minotaur_subnet/validator/weights_emitter.py
    line_ref: '35'
    quote: 'netuid: int = 112,'
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '1'
    quote: '# Minotaur — Agentic Intent Execution Platform'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '7'
    quote: Miners compete to write the best Solving Engine; validators run it, simulate plans, double-score
      results, and reach off-chain consensus.
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '78'
    quote: Miners provide and continuously update their Solving Engine software. The highest-scoring engine
      becomes the Champion and runs across all validators.
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: minotaur_subnet/harness/orchestrator.py
    line_ref: '569'
    quote: 'plan_quality_weight: float = 0.6'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/metric_kind
    source_path: minotaur_subnet/validator/scoring_engine.py
    line_ref: 250-251
    quote: "score_result = await self.js_engine.score(app_id, plan, simulation, state)\n        return\
      \ score_result.score"
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/weight
    source_path: minotaur_subnet/harness/orchestrator.py
    line_ref: '568'
    quote: 'auto_trigger_weight: float = 0.4'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: minotaur_subnet/harness/orchestrator.py
    line_ref: 723-728
    quote: trigger_correct = (br.trigger_decision == gt) br.trigger_score = 1.0 if trigger_correct else
      0.0 br.score = (config.auto_trigger_weight * br.trigger_score + config.plan_quality_weight * score_result.score)
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: minotaur_subnet/validator/scoring_engine.py

    quote: 'threshold = (app_def.config.on_chain_threshold ... else 5000) ... if simulation.on_chain_score
      < threshold: return {"approved": False, ... ON_CHAIN_SCORE_BELOW_THRESHOLD ...}'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes

    quote: 'if local_score < score_threshold: return {"approved": False, "reason": f"Score {local_score:.4f}

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes

  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/description

  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/notes

    line_ref: 285-288
    quote: resimulate_proposals = os.environ.get("FOLLOWER_PROPOSAL_RESIMULATE", "1",).strip().lower()

  - claim_field: /aggregation/method

  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/3/description

  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/status_values
    source_path: minotaur_subnet/epoch/manager.py

    line_ref: '564'
    quote: 'if submission.benchmark_score is None or submission.benchmark_score <= 0:'
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy

    quote: Runtime state (watermarks, last scores, last emitted block) is stored under the validator's
      data directory (StateStore).

    source_path: README.md

  unresolved:

    (app-defined scoring program). compute_mock_score is only the fallback.'

    defaults to 60; SOLVER_ROUND_EPOCH_SECONDS env can override. No single authoritative constant.'
  - version_key default 6 and max_attempts default 10 in WeightsEmitter are commit-reveal tuning params,

docs.title: Neza
docs.text:
Subnet subnet99__Neza netuid 99 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners run open-source video-generation models (e.g. WAN2.1) through ComfyUI, executing workflows dispatched by validators, then upload the generated video to public cloud storage. Validators score each output on quality (ImageBind similarity against a benchmark/reference render) and speed (completi DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: ReferenceModel, OrganicTraffic"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: LivenessCheck, RateLimit, StakeWeighting"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: similarity"]
  M1["Metric: latency"]
  M2["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Sma"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: neza

    netuid: 99
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [content_link, api_response]

    @guards {
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: other
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, cumulative_score, cooldown }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            ReferenceModel {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            OrganicTraffic {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Sma {
            window: 6
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: continuous
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Emission control (burn) is configurable but disabled by default (emission_control.enabled=False).
  The dynamic auto-burn path in effective_emission_control only activates when the computed emission_percentage
  < 1.0, but with default config emission_min=emission_max=1.0, so calculate_emission_percentage always
  returns 1.0 and no burn occurs by default. Scoring (quality via ImageBind + speed + API model usage)
  is the live mechanism.
subnet:
  name: Neza
  owner_repo: subnet99__Neza
  netuid: 99
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners run open-source video-generation models (e.g. WAN2.1) through ComfyUI, executing workflows
    dispatched by validators, then upload the generated video to public cloud storage. Validators score
    each output on quality (ImageBind similarity against a benchmark/reference render) and speed (completion
    time vs a baseline), plus an API path that scores miners on model-call cost x success rate (with a
    time factor for sora-2 models). Higher quality and faster generation yield higher rewards.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: quality_score
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.6
  normalization: none
  notes: ImageBind similarity between validator reference render and miner output, averaged over files.
    Weighted by verification.quality_score_weight (default 0.6). If avg quality < quality_threshold (0.9),
    the speed score is forced down to low_quality_speed_score (0.2).
- name: speed_score
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.4
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Speed score in [0,1]: 1.0 if completion_time <= verification_time_estimate baseline (300s),
    else max(0, 1 - excess_time/baseline). Weighted by speed_score_weight (default 0.4). Combined task
    score = quality*0.6 + speed*0.4.'
- name: api_model_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: API task score = sum over models of (total_cost * success_rate * model_weight), with a sora-2
    time_factor multiplier. Normalized across miners via percentile (5th/95th) min-max into [0,1]. Combined
    with the comfy task score as task_avg*comfy_task_weight (0.2) + api_avg*api_task_weight (0.8).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: reference_model
  description: Validator renders a reference output and uses the ImageBind model (imagebind_huge, pretrained)
    to compute similarity between the validator render and the miner-generated video/image/audio.
  source_identifier: imagebind_model.imagebind_huge(pretrained=True)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: VideoVerifier in neza/validator/verify.py.
- kind: organic_traffic
  description: Organic workflows from real user requests pushed into the database and dispatched to miners;
    API-path scoring uses real model-call stats (call_count, success_count, total_cost) fetched from the
    API.
  source_identifier: get_api_miner_stats
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: API scoring path in process_api_scoring.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: l1
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: emission_control disabled by default. When enabled (or when computed emission_percentage <
      1.0), a burn fraction = 1.0 - emission_percentage is routed to UID 0 via emission_control_scores.
      With default emission_min=emission_max=1.0 the percentage is always 1.0 so no burn occurs.
  notes: Per-miner final_weight = (history_avg*history_weight(0.4) + combined_score*current_weight(0.6))
    * miner_factor (stake-based, default range 1.0-1.0). combined_score = task_avg*comfy_task_weight(0.2)
    + api_avg*api_task_weight(0.8). Validator scores are then blended 50/50 with an external consensus
    score (consensus_score_ratio 0.5, local_score_ratio 0.5). Final on-chain raw_weights = scores / L1-norm
    in base set_weights.
weight_setting:
  cadence: continuous
  method: Block-callback driven. set_weights computes API scores, updates base scores via calculate_weights,
    blends 50/50 with consensus scores, applies optional emission control, then the base BaseValidatorNeuron.set_weights
    normalizes by L1 norm and calls subtensor.set_weights with version_key=spec_version.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: null
  smoothing:
    kind: sma
    alpha: null
    window: 6
    notes: Historical scores keep a sliding window of max_history=6 epoch averages; history contributes
      history_weight=0.4 of the base weight. Epoch scores moved to history every history_move_blocks=6000
      blocks (~20h). task/api sliding_window=30.
anti_gaming:
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Comfy support queried every 100 blocks; miners that no longer support ComfyUI have their
    task_scores reset to [0.0]. API miners not in the active cache get api_scores reset to [0.0].
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'PenaltyManager: consecutive failure_threshold=2 triggers penalty_duration 5min, doubling
    (penalty_multiplier=2) up to max_penalty_duration 30min.'
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: other
  enforcement_other: score multiplier
  description: calculate_miner_factor scales final weight by a stake-based segmented-exponential factor
    (default min/max both 1.0, so neutral by default).
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Hotkey-change detection clears all score records for a UID when its hotkey changes; coldkey
    blacklist and blacklisted_uids zero out scores.
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: 'Two task types contribute to the combined score: ComfyUI/synthetic+organic video tasks (comfy_task_weight
    0.2) and API model-call tasks (api_task_weight 0.8). Not a formal tournament/ladder.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - cumulative_score
  - cooldown
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Scores cleared on hotkey change. Historical scores capped at max_history=6; task/api scores
    capped at sliding_window=30. Penalty cooldowns reset on success.
  notes: score_cache.json persists historical_scores, current_scores, task_scores, api_scores, and miner_hotkeys
    across cycles, versioned (cache_version=1).
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md

docs.title: SN121 — sundae_bar Generalist Agent
docs.text:
Subnet sundae-bar__bittensor-subnet netuid 121 Archetype multiplex Language none_docs_only Mechanism status unknown Task Developers (miners) submit fully open-source generalist AI agents to SN121 validators. Validators benchmark each agent with the Agent Eval Test Suite (AETS) — datasets, targets, rubric, and graders derived from structured "Generalist Challenges" across multiple domains. Agents are ranked by an aggre DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: CodeInspection, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: composite"]
  M1["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  M2["Metric: accuracy"]
  M3["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: Unknown"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: sn121SundaeBarGeneralistAgent

    netuid: 121
    lang: none_docs_only
    status: unknown
    submission: source_code

    @burn {}
    @guards {
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: oracle
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: Unknown {
            cadence: continuous
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: unknown
mechanism_status_notes: Docs-only repo (README only, no validator/reward source code). The README describes
  a winner-takes-all emissions model with a pre-revenue "controlled low emissions; unused emissions burned"
  mode and a post-revenue revenue-backed mode, but no on-chain code is present to confirm what the live
  validator actually does. Status genuinely unknown from the available source.
subnet:
  name: SN121 — sundae_bar Generalist Agent
  owner_repo: sundae-bar__bittensor-subnet
  netuid: 121
  implementation_lang: none_docs_only
task:
  summary: Developers (miners) submit fully open-source generalist AI agents to SN121 validators. Validators
    benchmark each agent with the Agent Eval Test Suite (AETS) — datasets, targets, rubric, and graders
    derived from structured "Generalist Challenges" across multiple domains. Agents are ranked by an aggregated
    consensus performance score and emissions are awarded winner-takes-all to the single top-performing
    agent for the evaluation window.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: task_completion
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.35
  normalization: none
  notes: Sample rubric metric, weight 35%, evaluated by deterministic graders validating schema-correct,
    actionable outputs. Rubric weights are described as relative and summing to 100%; this sample is illustrative,
    not a fixed network constant.
- name: reasoning_quality
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.25
  normalization: none
  notes: Sample rubric metric, weight 25%, LLM judge evaluates coherence, logical steps, and justification.
- name: retrieval_accuracy
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.2
  normalization: none
  notes: Sample rubric metric, weight 20%, exact-match + semantic-match scoring against ground-truth datasets.
- name: tool_use_efficacy
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.2
  normalization: none
  notes: Sample rubric metric, weight 20%, ability to call tools correctly, avoid errors, and meet constraints.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: 'AETS specification per Generalist Challenge: datasets + targets + rubric + graders, including
    business documents, structured tables, web-style passages, and tool-access stubs. Used for exact-match/semantic-match
    retrieval scoring and deterministic graders.'
  source_identifier: Agent Eval Test Suite (AETS)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: No dataset URL or repo path is given in the README; AETS specs are said to be published publicly
    after evaluation.
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: LLM-based judges used where appropriate within the rubric (e.g. reasoning-quality coherence
    and justification scoring).
  source_identifier: AETS LLM judge
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: ''
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'Pre-revenue operating mode: "Controlled low emissions; unused emissions burned". No numeric
      burn fraction is given in the README.'
  notes: Per-validator rubric scores are aggregated statistically into a single consensus performance
    score per agent; agents are ranked and emissions awarded to the top performer only (winner-takes-all).
    Rubric metrics combine as a weighted sum (weights sum to 100%). No aggregation temperature, decay,
    or floor constants are documented.
weight_setting:
  cadence: continuous
  method: 'Rolling challenge cycle: agents can be submitted at any time; the top-ranked agent for each
    evaluation window earns emissions. No explicit on-chain weight-setting code or cadence (epoch/tempo)
    is present in the repo.'
  on_chain_call: unknown
  tempo_or_interval: null
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: ''
anti_gaming:
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: All agents must be fully open-source; "No closed-source submissions are permitted." Every
    submission is archived and publicly inspectable.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Rubric Pass/Fail Conditions: "minimum qualification thresholds to prevent poor-quality
    submissions from scoring highly." No threshold value is documented.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Multiple Generalist Challenges across domains (reasoning, retrieval, tool use, information synthesis),
    each converted into an AETS spec; new challenges added on a rolling basis. Scores aggregate into a
    single consensus score, so this is multi-domain evaluation rather than separate emission pools.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: No per-miner persistent state (ledger, cooldown, reputation) is documented; evaluation is per-window
    and winner-takes-all.
documentation:
  status: docs_only
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: low
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: low
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '7'
    quote: SN121 is an incentivised economy for the creation of a single generalist AI agent.
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '27'
    quote: 'SN121 is owned and operated by Sundae Bar Plc (AIM: SBAR), a London-listed company.'
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '57'
    quote: Developers ("miners") submit public generalist agents, with all agent code fully open-source.
      No closed-source submissions are permitted.
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '15'
    quote: Validators benchmark agents using the Agent Eval Test Suite (AETS), and emissions are awarded
      to the top-performing generalist agent.
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '144'
    quote: '| **Task Completion** | 35% | Deterministic graders validate schema-correct, actionable outputs
      |'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/weight
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '145'
    quote: '| **Reasoning Quality** | 25% | LLM judge evaluates coherence, logical steps, and justification
      |'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/weight
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '146'
    quote: '| **Retrieval Accuracy** | 20% | Exact-match + semantic-match scoring against ground-truth
      datasets |'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/3/weight
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '147'
    quote: '| **Tool-Use Efficacy** | 20% | Ability to call tools correctly, avoid errors, and meet constraints
      |'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '59'
    quote: 'Each Generalist Challenge is converted into an AETS specification: datasets + targets + rubric
      + graders.'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '65'
    quote: Generalist agents are ranked by their aggregated performance score, and emissions are awarded
      to the top-performing agent for that evaluation window. This winner-takes-all structure ensures
      that miner rewards reflect real improvements.
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '61'
    quote: Metrics are aggregated statistically into a single consensus performance score for each submitted
      generalist agent.
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '49'
    quote: 'Pre-Revenue: Controlled low emissions; unused emissions burned; optional use of sundae_bar
      reserve for early stability.'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/cadence
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '91'
    quote: SN121 operates on a continuous evaluation cadence ... allowing for iterative, ongoing improvement
      rather than fixed submission cycles.
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '57'
    quote: No closed-source submissions are permitted. Every agent submitted is archived and available
      for community inspection.
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '121'
    quote: Pass/Fail Conditions – minimum qualification thresholds to prevent poor-quality submissions
      from scoring highly.
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/structure
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '55'
    quote: 'sundae_bar defines Generalist Challenges: structured, multi-domain datasets that represent
      the real workflows businesses need automated.'
  - claim_field: /documentation/status
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '1'
    quote: Incentivizing The Creation and Benchmarking of A Generalist Commercial AI Agent
  unresolved:
  - No validator/reward source code in repo — aggregation temperature, decay, weight floors, and on-chain
    set_weights call/tempo unknowable.
  - Burn fraction in pre-revenue mode not numerically specified (README says unused emissions are burned
    but gives no fraction).
  - Live mechanism_status (active/burn/dormant) cannot be confirmed without validator code.
  - Rubric metric weights (35/25/20/20) are explicitly an illustrative sample, not a fixed network constant.
  - Emission cap '70% of revenue' is a worked dollar example, not a confirmed protocol constant, so not
    encoded as a numeric field.
composition:
  shape: multiplex
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Swarm — Autonomous Drone Navigation
docs.text:
Subnet swarm-subnet__swarm netuid 124 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task A miner submits a trained neural-network flight policy. The model receives a 128x128 depth image plus a flight state vector at 50 Hz and outputs 5D velocity commands ([dir_x, dir_y, dir_z, speed, yaw]). It has 60 seconds to navigate a drone to a landing platform and land safely across procedurally g DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, CodeInspection, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: latency"]
  M2["Metric: similarity"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: swarmAutonomousDroneNavigation

    netuid: 124
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: model_weights

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: 0.95
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
            count: 6
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Validators burn a fixed 95% of emissions to UID 0 (BURN_EMISSIONS=True, BURN_FRACTION=0.95);
  the remaining 5% is distributed by backend-supplied weights computed from the documented flight-reward
  scoring. So scoring is live but most emission is burned.
subnet:
  name: Swarm — Autonomous Drone Navigation
  owner_repo: swarm-subnet__swarm
  netuid: 124
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: A miner submits a trained neural-network flight policy. The model receives a 128x128 depth
    image plus a flight state vector at 50 Hz and outputs 5D velocity commands ([dir_x, dir_y, dir_z,
    speed, yaw]). It has 60 seconds to navigate a drone to a landing platform and land safely across procedurally
    generated worlds (city, open, mountain, village, warehouse, forest). Each mission is scored on success,
    time efficiency, and obstacle safety; the model is ranked by average score across 1,000 seeds per
    epoch.
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
scoring_signals:
- name: success
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.45
  normalization: none
  notes: 1 if the mission reaches its goal (lands on platform), 0 otherwise. REWARD_W_SUCCESS = 0.45.
- name: time_efficiency
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.45
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Based on minimum theoretical time (distance/SPEED_LIMIT + HOVER_SEC) with a 6% buffer; full credit
    at/under target time, linearly decaying to 0 at the horizon. REWARD_W_TIME = 0.45.
- name: safety
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: min_max
  notes: Based on minimum obstacle clearance during flight; 1.0 at >= safe distance (SAFETY_DISTANCE_SAFE=1.0m,
    0.6m for forest type 6), 0.0 at/under danger distance (SAFETY_DISTANCE_DANGER=0.2m), linear in between;
    collision forces 0. REWARD_W_SAFETY = 0.10.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Procedurally generated 3D worlds keyed by seed, evaluated deterministically in a PyBullet
    physics simulation inside Docker containers. 1,000 seeds per epoch; seeds rotate every 7 days (Monday
    16:00 UTC). Each validator generates its own seeds.
  source_identifier: swarm/validator/task_gen.py; SCREENING_TEMPLATE / random_task seeds
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: No external dataset; tasks are simulation seeds. Backend (swarm124.com) coordinates which models/seeds
    are evaluated and publishes seeds.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 0.95
    dynamic: false
    notes: BURN_EMISSIONS=True, BURN_FRACTION=0.95, UID_ZERO=0. When applying backend weights, distributed
      weights are rescaled to KEEP_FRACTION (=1-0.95=0.05) and UID 0 is prepended with weight BURN_FRACTION.
      If no backend weights are present, UID 0 gets full weight 1.0.
  notes: Per-mission score = 0.45*success + 0.45*time + 0.10*safety, clamped to [0,1]. Model ranked by
    average score across 1,000 seeds. WINNER_TAKE_ALL=True (winner gets all available emissions). Validator
    scores are normalized by L1 norm before being emitted as on-chain weights.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Validator applies backend-supplied per-UID weights to its scores vector (with the 95% burn split
    applied), normalizes by L1 norm, then calls subtensor.set_weights. A separate burn_validator variant
    sets weight 1.0 on UID 0 only. Eligibility gated by neuron.epoch_length blocks since last update.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (neuron.epoch_length default)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: 'update_scores performs an exponential moving average: scores = alpha*scattered_rewards + (1-alpha)*scores,
      with neuron.moving_average_alpha default 0.1. Note: when backend weights are applied via _apply_backend_weights_to_scores
      the scores vector is reset and overwritten rather than EMA-blended.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: New models must pass a screening gate (must score above the top/champion model by SCREENING_MIN_IMPROVEMENT=0.015)
    before running the full benchmark; screening can early-fail at checkpoints.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Models evaluated inside sandboxed Docker containers with a pip-package whitelist (DOCKER_PIP_WHITELIST),
    memory/CPU limits, and a legitimacy verification check (legitimate_model flag); illegitimate models
    that fail receive 0 instead of the 0.01 base reward.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Per-step RPC timeouts and hardware-fair calibrated compute budgets (MINER_COMPUTE_BUDGET_SEC,
    RPC_STEP_TIMEOUT_SEC, strikes per seed) bound miner inference time; model size capped at MAX_MODEL_BYTES.
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages: []
  count: 6
  notes: Six challenge types (city, open, mountain, village, warehouse, forest), each sampled with probability
    1/6 (CHALLENGE_TYPE_DISTRIBUTION). Per-type average scores are tracked, but the final ranking uses
    a single average across all seeds, so this is one mechanism with task-type variety rather than separate
    emission pools.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Scores are EMA-tracked per UID and reset to 0 when a hotkey is replaced; seeds rotate
    and re-evaluation occurs every 7-day epoch.
  notes: Validator keeps a per-UID scores vector (moving average); resync_metagraph zeroes scores for
    replaced hotkeys.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: v4.0.0
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/validator.md
  - swarm/validator/reward.py
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: '101'
    quote: '"subnet": 124,'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang

    quote: '# swarm/validator/reward.py'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 62-66
    quote: Your model gets a **128×128 depth image** and a **state vector** ... It outputs **velocity
      commands** to fly the drone ... It has **60 seconds** to navigate to a landing platform
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: swarm/constants.py
    line_ref: '150'
    quote: 'MODEL_DIR = Path("miner_models_v2")     # Directory for storing miner model files'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: swarm/constants.py
    line_ref: '226'
    quote: 'REWARD_W_SUCCESS = 0.45                 # Weight for success term in reward calculation'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/weight
    source_path: swarm/constants.py
    line_ref: '227'
    quote: 'REWARD_W_TIME = 0.45                    # Weight for time efficiency term in reward calculation'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/weight
    source_path: swarm/constants.py
    line_ref: '228'
    quote: 'REWARD_W_SAFETY = 0.10                 # Weight for safety term in reward calculation'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: swarm/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 55-56
    quote: "min_time = (distance / SPEED_LIMIT) + HOVER_SEC\n    return min_time * 1.06"
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: swarm/validator/reward.py
    line_ref: 66-70
    quote: 'if min_clearance >= safe: return 1.0 ; if min_clearance <= SAFETY_DISTANCE_DANGER: return
      0.0 ; return (min_clearance - SAFETY_DISTANCE_DANGER) / (safe - SAFETY_DISTANCE_DANGER)'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes

  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization

docs.title: SoundsRight
docs.text:
Subnet synapsec-ai__soundsright-subnet netuid 105 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners fine-tune speech-enhancement models (denoising and dereverberation, at 16 kHz and 48 kHz) and upload them to HuggingFace. Validators download each model, run it on a freshly generated TTS + noise/reverb dataset, and benchmark output quality with metrics (PESQ, ESTOI, SI_SDR, SI_SIR, SI_SAR). DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, DeterministicCheck, DataAugmentation"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: similarity"]
  M1["Metric: similarity"]
  M2["Metric: similarity"]
  M3["Metric: similarity"]
  M4["Metric: similarity"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: soundsRight

    netuid: 105
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: model_weights

    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        DataAugmentation {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, performance_ledger }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: other
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "350 blocks (last_updated_block + 350)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
