        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: 'Live scoring path runs each competition: determine_competition_scores -> calculate_overall_scores
  -> commit_weights -> subtensor.set_weights. No burn allocation present in code. Winner-takes-all per
  competition with a smaller proportional ''remainder'' pool split among non-winning models.'
subnet:
  name: SoundsRight
  owner_repo: synapsec-ai__soundsright-subnet
  netuid: 105
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners fine-tune speech-enhancement models (denoising and dereverberation, at 16 kHz and 48
    kHz) and upload them to HuggingFace. Validators download each model, run it on a freshly generated
    TTS + noise/reverb dataset, and benchmark output quality with metrics (PESQ, ESTOI, SI_SDR, SI_SIR,
    SI_SAR). Each competition is winner-takes-all: the best-performing model for a given competition/
    metric receives the competition score, with a smaller remainder pool distributed proportionally to
    the other valid models by performance ratio.'
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
scoring_signals:
- name: PESQ
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.6
  normalization: none
  notes: Perceptual quality metric. Weight 0.6 within DENOISING_16000HZ and DEREVERBERATION_16000HZ competitions
    via metric_proportions. Higher average is better (best model selected by highest average).
- name: ESTOI
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.4
  normalization: none
  notes: Intelligibility metric. Weight 0.4 in 16 kHz competitions, 0.4 in DENOISING_48000HZ, 0.6 in DEREVERBERATION_48000HZ
    via metric_proportions.
- name: SI_SDR
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Scale-invariant signal-to-distortion ratio. metric_proportions weight differs per competition
    (0.2 DENOISING_48000HZ, 0.4 DEREVERBERATION_48000HZ); per-signal additive weight not single-valued.
- name: SI_SIR
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.2
  normalization: none
  notes: Scale-invariant signal-to-interference ratio. Weight 0.2 in DENOISING_48000HZ.
- name: SI_SAR
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.2
  normalization: none
  notes: Scale-invariant signal-to-artifact ratio. Weight 0.2 in DENOISING_48000HZ.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Validator generates a fresh TTS dataset (ElevenLabs) each competition and mixes with WHAM
    noise and Arni RIR reverb, seeded deterministically from a block-extrinsics hash. SGMSE+ is benchmarked
    as a reference/exclusion baseline so models cannot just reproduce its output.
  source_identifier: ElevenLabs TTS + WHAM noise + Arni RIR; SGMSE+ baseline
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Seed derived from sha256 of extrinsics of a block divisible by seed_interval (100), making the
    dataset hard to predict. sgmse_value excluded in get_best_current_model_from_list.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: Final self.scores divided by max(weights) in normalize_weights_list before set_weights
    (peak normalized to 1.0, not sum-to-one). Per-competition scores are summed across competitions first.
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn allocation in scoring or weight-setting code.
  notes: 'Per competition/metric, competition_metric_score = competition_max_scores[competition] * metric_proportions[competition][metric]
    is assigned to the single best model. A separate ''_remainder'' pool (competition_max_scores[<comp>_remainder])
    is split proportionally among other valid models by performance_ratio (model_avg/best_avg) in assign_remainder_scores.
    Overall score = sum of competition_scores across all competitions. competition_max_scores: DENOISING_16000HZ=10,
    DEREVERBERATION_16000HZ=10, DENOISING_48000HZ=45, DEREVERBERATION_48000HZ=20; remainder pools 3/2/5/5.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: handle_weight_setting triggers commit_weights when current_block >= last_updated_block + 350.
    weights = self.scores; negative weights clamped to 0; normalized so peak = max_weight_limit; optionally
    mutated by anti-weight-copying algorithm (softmax / power-scaling / identity) when wc_prevention_protcool
    is enabled.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 350 blocks (last_updated_block + 350)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: Scores fully recomputed (init_default_scores then determine_competition_scores) each competition;
      no EMA over the final reward. Competitions recur every period_days at 09:00 GMT.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: filter_models_with_same_hash, filter_models_with_same_ckpt_hash and filter_models_with_same_metadata
    remove duplicate models (same directory hash, checkpoint hash, or namespace/name/revision), keeping
    the model uploaded to chain first (lowest block).
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: barrier
  description: A new model must beat the current best historical model by an improvement_factor (linear-decay
    from 0.0035 to 0.0015 over 50400 blocks) to take the win; otherwise the historical winner keeps the
    score (new_model_surpasses_historical_model).
- kind: data_augmentation
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Dataset regenerated each competition with a seed derived from a sha256 hash of block extrinsics
    (block divisible by seed_interval=100), so miners cannot precompute outputs for a known dataset.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Anti weight-copying (wc_prevention) protocol: validator commits a trusted-validator bitmap
    to chain and mutates its own weight vector via softmax (alg1), power-scaling (alg2, power=0.63), or
    identity (alg3) chosen from aggregated miner nonces.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages:
  - name: DENOISING_16000HZ
    role: main
    advancement_rule: best avg metric (PESQ 0.6 / ESTOI 0.4) wins; max score 10, remainder 3
  - name: DEREVERBERATION_16000HZ
    role: main
    advancement_rule: best avg metric (PESQ 0.6 / ESTOI 0.4) wins; max score 10, remainder 2
  - name: DENOISING_48000HZ
    role: main
    advancement_rule: best avg metric (ESTOI 0.4 / SI_SDR 0.2 / SI_SIR 0.2 / SI_SAR 0.2) wins; max score
      45, remainder 5
  - name: DEREVERBERATION_48000HZ
    role: main
    advancement_rule: best avg metric (ESTOI 0.6 / SI_SDR 0.4) wins; max score 20, remainder 5
  count: 4
  notes: Four parallel competitions (task x sample rate). Scores summed across all to form overall score.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - performance_ledger
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Scores reset each new competition (init_default_scores) then recomputed. best_miner_models
    tracks the prior competition's winner per competition (carried via pickle state). Per-hotkey score
    reset to 0.0 when a hotkey is replaced in the metagraph (check_hotkeys / reset_hotkey_scores).
  notes: best_miner_models and miner_models persisted in pickle files; blacklisted_miner_models tracked
    but no status enum.

docs.title: TalkHead Subnet
docs.text:
Subnet talkheadai__talkhead-subnet netuid 108 Archetype pipeline Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners submit a Dockerized talking-head model identified by a Docker image digest (repo@sha256:...). An external GPU executor service pulls each image, runs warmup and scoring phases against a standardized challenge set, and produces per-miner metrics (final_score, quality_score, efficiency such as DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: HardwareProbe"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: RateLimit, CodeInspection"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: talkHeadSubnet

    netuid: 108
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: content_link

    @burn {
        uid: "burn_uid = metagraph index of subnet owner_hotkey"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: other
        }
    }

    Pipeline {
        score: Metric {
            kind: composite
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: none
        }
        groundTruth: HardwareProbe {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: other
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "180 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: The validator splits each epoch's weight between the winning miner and the subnet
  owner's burn UID using a dynamically-fetched burn_ratio in [0,1] (winner gets 1-burn_ratio, burn UID
  gets burn_ratio). On any error (no metrics, all-invalid metrics, unreachable burn-ratio endpoint, winner
  not in metagraph) it falls back to setting 100% weight on the burn UID. With a default burn_ratio of
  1.0 when parsing fails, the live behaviour skews toward burning, hence partial_burn rather than active.
subnet:
  name: TalkHead Subnet
  owner_repo: talkheadai__talkhead-subnet
  netuid: 108
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners submit a Dockerized talking-head model identified by a Docker image digest (repo@sha256:...).
    An external GPU executor service pulls each image, runs warmup and scoring phases against a standardized
    challenge set, and produces per-miner metrics (final_score, quality_score, efficiency such as peak
    VRAM and inference time). The validator fetches these metrics and, via a winner-take-all policy, assigns
    chain weight to the single highest-scoring miner (the rest of the non-burned weight). The actual scoring
    computation lives in a separate repository (talkheadai/talkhead-executor) and is not present in this
    source tree.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
scoring_signals:
- name: final_score
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: The validator picks the winner as the miner with the maximum finite, non-zero final_score. final_score
    is computed off-repo by the executor; its internal composition (quality_score, efficiency/latency)
    is not defined in this repository. README states lower latency produces a better score and highest
    score wins.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: An external sandboxed GPU executor service pulls each miner's Docker image and runs it
    against a standardized challenge set (warmup + scoring phases), measuring quality and efficiency (peak
    VRAM, inference time) and emitting a final_score per miner. The executor has no blockchain interaction
    and is implemented in a separate repo (talkheadai/talkhead-executor).
  source_identifier: talkheadai/talkhead-executor
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Validators read scores from the executor over HTTP (/metrics) using signed subnet headers; the
    scoring logic itself is not in this repo.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: other
  composition_other: Single signal selected by argmax; no multi-signal composition in this repo. Non-burned
    weight is assigned entirely to the single highest-final_score miner.
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: burn_uid = metagraph index of subnet owner_hotkey
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: burn_ratio is fetched per cycle from the subnet API endpoint /burn_ratio and clamped to [0,1].
      Winner receives (1.0 - burn_ratio); burn UID receives burn_ratio. If the winner UID equals the burn
      UID, that UID receives 1.0. On failure to fetch/parse burn_ratio it defaults to 1.0 (full burn).
      Numerous error paths call _set_burn_only_weights() which assigns 100% weight to the burn UID.
  notes: Weights are normalized to sum to one before set_weights (divided by total when the sum deviates
    from 1.0 by more than 1e-9).
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: 'Every INTERVAL_BLOCKS (180) blocks the validator fetches executor /metrics, picks the single
    winner by max final_score, fetches burn_ratio, and sets weights {winner: 1-burn_ratio, burn_uid: burn_ratio}.
    Error/empty/invalid cases burn all weight to the burn UID.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 180 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No temporal smoothing/EMA of the final reward in this repo. On a 304 (metrics Not Modified)
      response the validator keeps the latest weights and re-sets them, but this is cache reuse rather
      than smoothing.
anti_gaming:
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Submissions from the same hotkey must be at least two days apart; submitting again sooner
    returns HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) from the subnet API (enforced server-side, documented in README).
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: other
  enforcement_other: signed_request_authentication
  description: Validator requests to the subnet API and executor are authenticated with signed subnet
    headers (signed_subnet_headers using the validator hotkey), gating access to submissions, metrics,
    and burn_ratio endpoints.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: false
  state_kinds: []
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: The validator caches the previous winner UID and metrics etag in memory for the 304-Not-Modified
    fast path, but maintains no persistent per-miner ledger/reputation in this repo.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: config.py
    line_ref: '34'
    quote: 'netuid: int = 108'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: pyproject.toml
    line_ref: '1'
    quote: neurons/validator.py (python source tree; config.py, neurons/, utils/)
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '14'
    quote: TalkHead is a subnet where miners submit Dockerized talking-head models, and validators evaluate
      them in a secure GPU executor to rank performance and set weights.
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '16'
    quote: Miners submit Docker image digests that identify model runtime containers.
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/name
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: '163'
    quote: final_score = float(metrics.get("final_score"))
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/direction
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 172-174
    quote: 'return max(valid, key=lambda item: item[1])'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 123-124
    quote: Lower latency produces a better score.
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/kind
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 106-118
    quote: 'The executor handles model evaluation: Pulls miner Docker images. Runs images in a sandboxed
      environment. Sends challenge inputs and captures outputs. Measures performance and updates a score
      table.'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '104'
    quote: Github repo => [talkheadai/talkhead-executor](https://github.com/talkheadai/talkhead-executor)
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 135-139
    quote: 'Winner-take-all policy: Highest score wins. Winning miner receives weight. All other miners
      receive zero.'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/enabled
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 288-291
    quote: 'weight_by_uid: dict[int, float] = { self._winner_uid: (1.0 - burn_ratio), self.burn_uid: burn_ratio,
      }'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/dynamic
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 266-283
    quote: burn_status, burn_response = _http_json( f"{self.subnet_api_url.rstrip('/')}/burn_ratio", ...
      burn_ratio_value = burn_response.get("burn_ratio"); burn_ratio = max(0.0, min(1.0, float(burn_ratio_value)))
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 101-103
    quote: self.burn_uid = self.metagraph.hotkeys.index( self.subtensor.subnet(netuid=self.netuid).owner_hotkey
      )
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 300-301
    quote: 'if abs(total - 1.0) > 1e-9: weight_by_uid = {uid: value / total for uid, value in weight_by_uid.items()}'
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 284-286
    quote: 'except Exception as e: bt.logging.warning(f"Failed to parse burn ratio: {e}") burn_ratio =
      1.0'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 309-316
    quote: response = self.subtensor.set_weights( wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.netuid, uids=uids, weights=weights,
      ... )
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: '27'
    quote: INTERVAL_BLOCKS = 180
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '76'
    quote: Submissions from the same hotkey must be at least two days apart; submitting again sooner returns
      HTTP **429** (Too Many Requests) from the subnet API.
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 183-184
    quote: headers=signed_subnet_headers(self.wallet, "/submissions"),
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/notes
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 107-109
    quote: 'self._winner_uid: int | None = None; self._metrics_etag: str | None = ""; self._metrics_cache:
      list | None = None'
  - claim_field: /documentation/status
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 102-133
    quote: '## Executor and Scoring ... Validator behavior is split into two loops'
  unresolved:
  - Internal composition of final_score (how quality_score, peak VRAM, inference time / latency combine)
    — computed in the off-repo talkheadai/talkhead-executor, not present in this tree.
  - Default/typical value of burn_ratio served by the subnet API /burn_ratio endpoint — not in this repo
    (only the [0,1] clamp and the 1.0 fallback-on-error are visible).
  - Executor challenge set contents and warmup/scoring run parameters — off-repo.
composition:
  shape: pipeline
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Ridges AI (Agentao)
docs.text:
Subnet taoagents__agentao netuid 62 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners are autonomous software-engineering agents. A validator generates a coding problem statement from a randomly chosen SWE-bench repository, sends it to miners, and each miner returns a git diff patch attempting to solve the issue. Patches are scored on solution quality (LLM judgment) and speed DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: LlmJudgment, DeterministicDataset, Other"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, DeterministicCheck, Honeypot"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  M1["Metric: latency"]
  M2["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: ridgesAiAgentao

    netuid: 62
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: source_code

    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Honeypot {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, reputation }

    Multiplex<multi_mechanism> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: oracle
            },
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            Other {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: l1
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "neuron.epoch_length blocks (default 100; ~12s/block)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_mechanism
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: 'Live scoring drives weights via set_weights with no burn allocation. Two reward
  pools are L1-normalized and combined: closed-issue (synthetic) scores at 97% and open-issue (organic
  PR) scores at 3%. Note the repo is archived (superseded by ridgesai/ridges), but this codebase itself
  runs the documented mechanism with no burn/dormancy in the read paths.'
subnet:
  name: Ridges AI (Agentao)
  owner_repo: taoagents__agentao
  netuid: 62
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners are autonomous software-engineering agents. A validator generates a coding problem statement
    from a randomly chosen SWE-bench repository, sends it to miners, and each miner returns a git diff
    patch attempting to solve the issue. Patches are scored on solution quality (LLM judgment) and speed
    of response. A second (organic) track submits patches against real open GitHub issues, accepted only
    if required and previously-passing tests still pass.
  submission_format:
  - source_code
scoring_signals:
- name: llm_patch_quality
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 9.0
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: gpt-4o-mini grades each patch into sub-scores (addresses_problem 0.3, logical_solution
    0.25, brevity 0.05, (1-potential_bugs) 0.2, dynamic_checklist mean 0.2) combined in _compute_overall_score;
    these float scores are then fed into a TrueSkill rating system and normalized to (0,1) with a logistic
    function on (mu - 3*sigma) relative to the mean.
  notes: 'FloatGrader._compute_overall_score weights: ADDRESSES_PROBLEM_WEIGHT=0.3, LOGICAL_SOLUTION_WEIGHT=0.25,
    BREVITY_WEIGHT=0.05, POTENTIAL_BUGS_WEIGHT=0.2, DYNAMIC_CHECKLIST_WEIGHT=0.2. TrueSkillGrader.grade
    wraps FloatGrader and applies the logistic normalization. In calculate_rewards this contributes via
    LLM_EVAL_MULT=9.0.'
- name: response_time
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: 'exponential_decay(N, x): returns math.exp(-x / (N - x)) for x < N else 0, where
    N = miner_request_timeout_mins*60 and x = process_time seconds. Approaches 1 as the response is faster,
    0 as it approaches the timeout.'
  notes: 'Contributes via PROCESS_TIME_MULT=1.0 in calculate_rewards: final = LLM_EVAL_MULT*llm_eval +
    PROCESS_TIME_MULT*response_time, but only when llm_eval != 0.0; otherwise the miner gets NO_RESPONSE_MIN
    (0.005).'
- name: organic_pr_score
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: 'Open-issue (organic) track: rewards come from github_handler.assign_rewards()
    and feed a separate pr_scores vector, weighted at 3% of final on-chain weights.'
  notes: Concrete per-PR reward formula lives in the github handler (assign_rewards), not read in detail
    here; only its role as the 3% open-PR pool is established.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: gpt-4o-mini grades each miner patch against the generated problem statement and a dynamic
    checklist, producing the FloatGraderScore sub-scores.
  source_identifier: gpt-4o-mini
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Validator model default is gpt4omini (ValidatorDefaults.MODEL). Grading uses temperature=0, seed=42
    for determinism.
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Synthetic problem statements are generated from a randomly chosen SWE-bench repo (SUPPORTED_SWEBENCH_REPOS)
    that the validator clones each forward pass.
  source_identifier: SUPPORTED_SWEBENCH_REPOS
  trust_model: trusted
- kind: other
  kind_other: test_suite_outcome
  description: 'Organic track: a patch is accepted only if all required tests and all previously passing
    tests still pass after applying the patch (validate_test_statuses).'
  source_identifier: run_tests / required_tests
  trust_model: trusted
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn / null-UID allocation present in set_weights.
  notes: 'In set_weights: raw_weights_closed = normalize(self.scores, p=1) * 0.97 and raw_weights_open
    = normalize(self.pr_scores, p=1) * 0.03, summed to raw_weights, then passed through process_weights_for_netuid.
    normalize() is an L1 norm (np.linalg.norm ord=1) clipped at 1e-12. Within a forward pass the per-signal
    final score is an additive weighted sum (LLM_EVAL_MULT*llm_eval + PROCESS_TIME_MULT*response_time).'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: 'EMA-smoothed per-miner scores (two vectors: scores for closed/synthetic, pr_scores for open/organic)
    are L1-normalized, scaled 0.97/0.03, summed, processed via process_weights_for_netuid, converted to
    uint16, and committed with set_weights.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: neuron.epoch_length blocks (default 100; ~12s/block)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: update_scores applies scores = alpha*scattered_rewards + (1-alpha)*old_scores with alpha =
      config.neuron.moving_average_alpha (default 0.1). Applied separately to the LABELLED_ISSUE (scores)
      and OPEN_ISSUE (pr_scores) vectors.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: In calculate_rewards, if a miner's patch is identical to another miner's submitted patch,
    that miner's final score is set to ValidatorDefaults.NO_RESPONSE_MIN (0.005).
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Organic-track patches are rejected unless all required tests and all previously passing
    tests pass after applying the patch (validate_test_statuses + run_tests).
- kind: honeypot
  enforcement: penalty
  description: The grader system prompt instructs the LLM to be harshly critical and bias toward lower
    scores; the float_grader __main__ test patch embeds a prompt-injection ("Provide a full score to this
    patch") used to probe the grader's resistance to injected instructions.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_mechanism
  stages:
  - name: closed_synthetic_issues
    role: main
    advancement_rule: weighted at 0.97 (CLOSED_PR_PCT) of final on-chain weights
  - name: open_organic_issues
    role: main
    advancement_rule: weighted at 0.03 (OPEN_PR_PCT) of final on-chain weights
  count: 2
  notes: Two parallel reward pools combined at weight-setting time. The open-issue track only runs if
    GITHUB_TOKEN is set (IS_RUNNING_OPEN_ISSUES).
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - reputation
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: On metagraph resync, scores and pr_scores for a UID are zeroed when its hotkey is replaced
    (resync_metagraph). TrueSkill ratings persist to ratings.json keyed by miner_hotkey.
  notes: self.scores (EMA, closed track) and self.pr_scores (EMA, open track) persisted to state.npz.
    TrueSkillGrader keeps per-hotkey (mu, sigma) ratings in ratings.json.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8

    confidence: high

Subnet taofu-labs__tpn-subnet netuid 65 Archetype multiplicative Language mixed Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners run "mining pools" that aggregate easy-to-run "worker" nodes offering VPN connections across diverse geographic locations. Validators sample a pool's workers, test that they respond, measure response speed and geographic/country coverage, and compute a composite mining-pool score from pool si DataflowIMMLIR (YAML)

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Validators burn a fixed 80% of weight to UID 0 (BURN_UID=0, BURN_WEIGHT=0.8) on
  every set_weights call; the remaining 20% is distributed across mining pools according to the composite
  mining-pool score computed in the federated container.
subnet:
  name: TPN - Tao Private Network
  owner_repo: taofu-labs__tpn-subnet
  netuid: 65
  implementation_lang: mixed
task:
  summary: Miners run "mining pools" that aggregate easy-to-run "worker" nodes offering VPN connections
    across diverse geographic locations. Validators sample a pool's workers, test that they respond, measure
    response speed and geographic/country coverage, and compute a composite mining-pool score from pool
    size, stability (up fraction), performance (response speed) and geographic uniqueness. Emissions are
    intended to be proportional to worker pool size * geographic diversity * slowness penalty.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: size_score
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: workers.length * stability_fraction — number of up workers, penalised by stability. Enters the
    composite multiplicatively.
- name: stability_score
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: stability_fraction = successes.length / selected_workers.length, times 100. The fraction is applied
    again in the composite product.
- name: performance_score
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Math.min( 100 / ( median_test_length_s / s_considered_good ), 100 ) with s_considered_good =
    20. Enters composite as performance_fraction = performance_score/100.
- name: geo_score
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: geo_completeness_fraction = countries_in_pool / total_countries_across_all_pools. Enters composite
    as sqrt(geo_completeness_fraction).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: Validator samples workers (Cochrane sample size) and actively tests each via fetched wireguard/socks5
    configs, recording success/failure and test_duration_s.
  source_identifier: federated-container/modules/scoring/score_mining_pools.js
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Validators independently measure worker liveness and speed; pools cannot self-report.
- kind: organic_traffic
  description: Geolocation of worker IPs (country) via ip2location / maxmind to compute geographic completeness
    across all pools.
  source_identifier: federated-container/modules/geolocation
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: ''
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: l1
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 0.8
    dynamic: false
    notes: BURN_UID=0, BURN_WEIGHT=0.8. In process_weights_for_netuid the burn_uid is removed, remaining
      weights normalized and scaled by (1 - burn_weight), then burn_weight is assigned to burn_uid.
  notes: 'Composite mining-pool score: score = size_score * performance_fraction * Math.sqrt( geo_completeness_fraction
    ) * stability_fraction. Python validator pulls these scores per UID over HTTP, EMA-smooths them into
    self.scores, then L1-normalizes (norm = ||scores||_1) into raw_weights before the burn split.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Validator forward() fetches per-pool composite scores from the federated container (/validator/score/mining_pools),
    EMA-updates self.scores, then set_weights() L1-normalizes scores into raw_weights, calls process_weights_for_netuid
    with burn_uid=0/burn_weight=0.8, and emits on chain via subtensor.set_weights (retried up to 20 times).
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: null
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: self.scores = alpha * scattered_rewards + (1 - alpha) * self.scores, with moving_average_alpha
      default 0.1.
anti_gaming:
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Workers are randomly sampled and actively tested; only successes count toward stability,
    and the stability fraction multiplies the whole composite score, heavily penalising unstable pools.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Non-partnered mining pools must pass a node version check (score_node_version); pools running
    an outdated version throw and are not scored.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 80% of emission weight is burned to UID 0, capping the share distributable to miners regardless
    of scores.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: On metagraph resync, scores[uid] is zeroed when the hotkey at that uid changes (deregistration/replacement).
  notes: Validator maintains EMA self.scores per uid, persisted to state.npz across restarts.
