docs.title: Hippius (thebrain / theNerveLab)
docs.text:
Subnet thenervelab__thebrain netuid 75 Archetype multiplex Language rust Mechanism status partial_burn Task A miner runs a Hippius storage node (IPFS-backed) that stores file shards and serves bandwidth. Validators report per-node quality metrics (stored bytes, served bandwidth, uptime, strikes, integrity failures) on-chain; miners cannot self-report. Only StorageMiner-type nodes earn non-zero weight. Per DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: HardwareProbe"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: StakeWeighting, LivenessCheck, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: throughput"]
  M1["Metric: throughput"]
  M2["Metric: uptime"]
  M3["Metric: other"]
  M4["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: hippiusThebrainTheNerveLab

    netuid: 75
    lang: rust
    status: partial_burn
    submission: compute_service

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { reputation, cumulative_score, registration_age, status_enum, performance_ledger }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                raw: "validator-issued strike penalty"
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: classification_quality
                specific: data_integrity
                raw: "data-integrity failure penalty"
                direction: lower_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: HardwareProbe {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: other
            decay_rate: 0.8
            min_weight_floor: 10
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.3
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: continuous
            tempo: "BittensorCallSubmission interval (offchain worker); rewards every BlocksPerEra = HOURS*6 (~3600 blocks / 6h)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Storage-miner weights are actively scored on-chain (bandwidth/storage/uptime +
  family aggregation), but a dynamically computed burn_percentage of the 65,535 weight budget is routed
  to UID 0 (the validator) rather than to miners. When alpha emissions exceed storage cost, the residual
  is burned to UID 0; the burn fraction is computed each cycle, not fixed.
subnet:
  name: Hippius (thebrain / theNerveLab)
  owner_repo: thenervelab__thebrain
  netuid: 75
  implementation_lang: rust
task:
  summary: A miner runs a Hippius storage node (IPFS-backed) that stores file shards and serves bandwidth.
    Validators report per-node quality metrics (stored bytes, served bandwidth, uptime, strikes, integrity
    failures) on-chain; miners cannot self-report. Only StorageMiner-type nodes earn non-zero weight.
    Per-node scores are aggregated per family (SS58 owner + linked child nodes), smoothed, and converted
    to Bittensor subnet-75 weights.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: bandwidth_served
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.7
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: log2(1+x) concave fixed-point (8.8) scoring
  notes: bw_score = log2(bandwidth_bytes + 1); blended at NodeBandwidthWeightPermille=700 (70%).
- name: shard_storage
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: log2(1+x) concave fixed-point (8.8) scoring
  notes: st_score = log2(shard_data_bytes + 1); blended at NodeStorageWeightPermille=300 (30%).
- name: uptime
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: permille multiplier (uptime_permille / 1000)
  notes: 'Multiplicative on the blended score: score *= uptime_permille/1000. Not additive.'
- name: strikes_penalty
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: validator-issued strike penalty
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Subtracted directly: penalty = strikes * StrikePenalty(50). node_weight = max(0, score - penalty).'
- name: integrity_fails_penalty
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: data-integrity failure penalty
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Subtracted directly: integrity_fails * IntegrityFailPenalty(100).'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: Validators observe each storage node and submit NodeQuality metrics on-chain via submit_node_quality();
    miners cannot self-report. Family placement/ping reliability is tracked off-chain (miner_ping_stats)
    for assignment eligibility.
  source_identifier: submit_node_quality / NodeQuality (pallets/arion-pallet)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: 'Reported metrics: shard_data_bytes, bandwidth_bytes, uptime_permille, strikes, integrity_fails.'
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: proportional to family share of total active-family weight, scaled into miners_pool
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: 0.8
  min_weight_floor: 10
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: uid_zero_pool = 65535 * burn_percentage, where burn_percentage = max(0, emissions-cost)/emissions
      (defaults to 0.2 if emissions == 0). emissions = alpha_price * EMISSION_PERIOD(50); cost = storage_gb
      * price_per_gb. miners_pool = 65535 - uid_zero_pool. UID 0 weight is capped so total <= 65535.
  notes: 'Family raw weight = sum over top FamilyTopN(10) node weights with geometric rank decay FamilyRankDecayPermille=800
    (factor 0.8^rank). Newcomer floor NewcomerFloorWeight=10 applied within NewcomerGraceBuckets=24. EMA
    smoothing FamilyWeightEmaAlphaPermille=300 (30% new, 70% prev), then delta clamp +/- MaxFamilyWeightDeltaPerBucket=100.
    Per-miner weight = (family_arion_weight / total_arion_weight) * miners_pool. Caps: MaxNodeWeight=50000,
    MaxFamilyWeight=65535.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: continuous
  method: Validator offchain_worker periodically calls calculate_weights_for_nodes(), saves rankings to
    the ranking pallet (instances 1=storage, 3=validator), then constructs and signs a Bittensor set_weights
    extrinsic submitted to the Finney chain for subnet 75. An external Python submitter (vali-weights-submitter/weight_submitter.py)
    is an alternative path that reads RankedList and submits via SubtensorModule.set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: BittensorCallSubmission interval (offchain worker); rewards every BlocksPerEra =
    HOURS*6 (~3600 blocks / 6h)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.3
    window: null
    notes: FamilyWeightEmaAlphaPermille=300 => smoothed = prev*0.7 + raw*0.3, applied to family weight
      before delta clamp.
anti_gaming:
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Family diversity: at most one replica per family (family = main_node_id or node_id), so
    many linked nodes in one family cannot multiply rewards. Family weight uses only top-10 nodes with
    0.8 rank decay, favouring few high-quality nodes over many low-quality ones.'
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Offline detection: if last heartbeat exceeds the offline buffer, weight is set to 0 (storage
    miners 3000 blocks ~5h; other types 300 blocks ~30min). Ping reliability >= 0.8 (last10_success_ratio)
    gates placement eligibility.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Strikes (StrikePenalty=50 each) and integrity failures (IntegrityFailPenalty=100 each)
    are subtracted directly from a node's score. Metrics are validator-reported, not self-reported.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Newcomer/ramp-up controls: new families floored at weight 10 for NewcomerGraceBuckets=24
    buckets; EMA smoothing + per-bucket delta clamp (+/-100) prevent sudden weight jumps; family age gate
    (>=3 days) and ~7-day age penalty slow new-cluster dominance in placement ranking.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Weights are computed per miner type via separate functions (storage, storage-S3, validator, GPU,
    compute) and the ranking pallet uses separate instances per type. Only storage (instance 1) and validator
    (instance 3) are Active; compute (2), GPU (4), and storage-S3 (5) instances are disabled / commented
    out.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - reputation
  - cumulative_score
  - registration_age
  - status_enum
  - performance_ledger
  status_values:
  - Active
  - Inactive
  reset_policy: Inactive miners have ping history purged and miner_ping_stats deleted; pings older than
    ~7 days trimmed. FamilyWeight persists and is EMA-smoothed across buckets.
  notes: 'On-chain: NodeWeightByChild, FamilyWeight (EMA-smoothed), FamilyWeightRaw, FamilyFirstSeenBucket
    (registration age for newcomer grace), NodeRankings/RankedList. MIN_BLOCKS_REGISTERED=1000 grace (non-storage
    types reduced 80% for first 1000 blocks).'
documentation:

  source_version: commit dd671787e9db7ce62cebe3f1ceb8744a38ef7c22

docs.title: ALPHA — Company Intelligence / Verifiable On-Chain Treasury Data
docs.text:
Subnet tigerinvests-com__sn107-alpha netuid 107 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task A miner ("agent") answers company-intelligence queries: given a ticker and an analysis_type (crypto, financial, sentiment, or news), it returns an IntelligenceResponse with company data (e.g. crypto holdings, market cap, share price, sentiment, news). Validators score each response against JSON-sche DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, Deduplication, StakeWeighting"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: composite"]
  M1["Metric: accuracy"]
  M2["Metric: latency"]
  M3["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: Softmax"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: alphaCompanyIntelligenceVerifiableOnChainTreasuryData

    netuid: 107
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: api_response

    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, performance_ledger }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: MarketData {
            trust_model: oracle
        }
        aggregate: Softmax {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: softmax
            temperature: 2
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "epoch_length * 12 seconds (default epoch_length=100)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
            count: 4
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: No burn logic present. The validator computes per-miner EMA scores and sets softmax-normalized
  weights on-chain every epoch via subtensor.set_weights. Scoring depends on an external private validation
  API (CRYPTO_HOLDINGS_URL), so live behavior cannot be confirmed from the repo alone, but the code path
  is wired and active.
subnet:
  name: ALPHA — Company Intelligence / Verifiable On-Chain Treasury Data
  owner_repo: tigerinvests-com__sn107-alpha
  netuid: 107
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'A miner ("agent") answers company-intelligence queries: given a ticker and an analysis_type
    (crypto, financial, sentiment, or news), it returns an IntelligenceResponse with company data (e.g.
    crypto holdings, market cap, share price, sentiment, news). Validators score each response against
    JSON-schema structure and against an external authoritative API, plus response time and confidence
    alignment.'
  submission_format:
  - api_response
scoring_signals:
- name: structure_validation
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Tier 1: JSON-schema structural/completeness validation of the response. Contributes with structure_weight
    (default 0.3). Acts as a gate: if structure_score < 0.3 the response is rejected with that score.'
- name: api_validation
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.7
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Tier 2: field-by-field accuracy of response data vs external API (CRYPTO_HOLDINGS_URL). Contributes
    with api_validation_weight (default 0.7). Per analysis_type, enhanced adjustments are applied (crypto/financial/
    sentiment/news field weightings and quality bonuses). Gate: if api_score < 0.5 the final score is
    min(structure_score, api_score).'
- name: response_time
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.15
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Piecewise time score: 1.0 if <=2s, decreasing in bands up to >20s -> 0.05. Added into final_score
    with a literal 0.15 coefficient.'
- name: confidence_alignment
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.15
  normalization: none
  notes: Scores how well the miner's reported confidenceScore aligns with success (e.g. success & confidence>0.6
    -> 1.0). Added into final_score with a literal 0.15 coefficient.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  description: External authoritative validation API for company / crypto-holdings data, queried per (ticker,
    analysis_type, miner_data). Returns valid flag, per-field scores, overall score, freshness/completeness
    scores.
  source_identifier: CRYPTO_HOLDINGS_URL (env, default https://api.example.com)
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Endpoint and API key are supplied via environment; not in repo. Tier-2 scoring fully depends
    on this external client (ExternalAPIClient.validate_company_data).
aggregation:
  method: softmax
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: softmax
  temperature: 2.0
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn logic in the repo.
  notes: 'Per-miner EMA score (alpha=0.1) is the input. calculate_weights places each miner''s EMA score
    into a vector, then applies softmax with temperature=2.0: exp(weights/temperature)/sum(...). If all
    scores are 0, weights are uniform (1/N). The per-response final_score itself is a weighted sum of
    the four signals (structure*0.3 + api*0.7 + time*0.15 + confidence*0.15), gated by the structure<0.3
    and api<0.5 short-circuits.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: 'Each loop iteration: forward() gathers validation results, update_scores applies the EMA, calculate_weights
    softmax-normalizes, then subtensor.set_weights(uids, weights, wait_for_inclusion=True).'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: epoch_length * 12 seconds (default epoch_length=100)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: miner_scores[uid] = (1 - alpha)*current + alpha*new_score, with moving_average_alpha default
      0.1. Per-uid score_history capped at 1000 entries.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Tier-1 JSON-schema structure validation; structure_score < 0.3 short-circuits to that score
    (response effectively rejected before API validation).
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: barrier
  description: get_available_miners deduplicates miners by IP, keeping the lowest port, and requires axon.ip
    != 0.0.0.0, port > 0, and stake > 0 to be queried/weighted.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Only miners with total_stake > 0 are considered available for querying and weight setting.
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages: []
  count: 4
  notes: Four analysis_type values (crypto, financial, sentiment, news) drive type-specific API score
    adjustments in _calculate_enhanced_api_score, but all feed a single shared per-miner score/weight
    vector — not separate weight pools.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - performance_ledger
  status_values: []

docs.title: CliqueAI
docs.text:
Subnet toptensor__CliqueAI netuid 83 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners receive a graph (LambdaGraph) and must return a set of vertices forming a maximum clique. A response is validated as a maximal clique, then scored on optimality (relative clique size with a penalty for being beaten by larger cliques) and on diversity (uniqueness of the solution among miners). DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, Deduplication, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: similarity"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: cliqueAi

    netuid: 83
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: signals

    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: min_max
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.01
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "361 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Fully implemented scoring + EMA + sigmoid/power weight-setting pipeline. set_weights
  combines sigmoid weights with LAMBDA_WEIGHTS=1.0, so weights[0] = (1.0 - 1.0)*sum = 0; no burn allocation
  to UID 0. No partial/full burn observed in source.
subnet:
  name: CliqueAI
  owner_repo: toptensor__CliqueAI
  netuid: 83
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners receive a graph (LambdaGraph) and must return a set of vertices forming a maximum clique.
    A response is validated as a maximal clique, then scored on optimality (relative clique size with
    a penalty for being beaten by larger cliques) and on diversity (uniqueness of the solution among miners).
    The final per-problem reward combines optimality scaled by (1 + difficulty) plus diversity.
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: optimality
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: min_max
  notes: omega = val * exp(-pr/rel) where val=valid maximal clique (0/1), rel = size/max_size, pr = fraction
    of miners with strictly larger valid size. Normalized by dividing by max omega. In aggregation it
    is scaled by (1 + difficulty), so it has no plain additive weight.
- name: diversity
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: min_max
  notes: delta = val * unq, where unq = 1 / (number of miners whose solution is exactly identical). Normalized
    by dividing by max delta. Added directly to the scaled optimality term.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Graphs retrieved from CliqueAI's distributed problem database via get_graph; validity and
    maximality of returned cliques are checked deterministically against the graph's adjacency list (is_valid_maximum_clique).
  source_identifier: CliqueAI/graph/client.py:get_graph; CliqueAI/scoring/clique_scoring.py:is_valid_maximum_clique
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Difficulty levels documented as Medium (0.2), Hard (0.4), Very Hard (1) in docs/mechanism.md.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: min_max
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: weights[0] = (1.0 - LAMBDA_WEIGHTS) * sum(sigmoid_weights) with LAMBDA_WEIGHTS=1.0, i.e. zero;
      no burn to UID 0.
  notes: Per-problem score f(m,p) = optimality_normalized * (1 + difficulty) + diversity_normalized (rewards
    = optimality * (1 + difficulty) + diversity in clique_scoring.py). Optimality and diversity are each
    min-max normalized by their own max before combination.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: 'Per-miner debiased EMA scores are min-max normalized to [0,1], then passed through a sigmoid
    (midpoint=median of nonzero, steepness=IQR clamped to >=0.1), then power-scaled: gamma is found by
    bisection (up to POWER_MAX_GAMMA=32.0) so the top half of miners by rank receive POWER_TARGET_TOP_HALF_SHARE=0.80
    of total weight. Final weights = LAMBDA_WEIGHTS(=1.0) * sigmoid_weights.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 361 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.01
    window: null
    notes: 'Debiased EMA on per-problem scores: ema = alpha*reward + (1-alpha)*ema; debiased by dividing
      by (1 - (1-alpha)^step_count). ema_alpha default 0.01 (config), half-life ~69 samples per docs/mechanism.md.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: is_valid_maximum_clique rejects empty sets, duplicates, out-of-range nodes, non-cliques,
    and non-maximal cliques (val=0 => zero optimality and diversity contribution).
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Diversity uses unq = 1/(count of identical solutions), penalizing miners who submit the
    same clique as others.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Validator shuffles the graph vertices (random permutation of node ids) before sending the
    synapse, preventing miners from memorizing problems by graph identity.
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages: []
  count: 3
  notes: 'Three documented problem difficulty types: Medium (difficulty=0.2, 290<=|V|<=300), Hard (0.4,
    490<=|V|<=500), Very Hard (1, 690<=|V|<=700), selected with equal probability. Not separate weighting
    tracks; difficulty modulates scoring and miner-selection probability.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ema_scores, scores, and ema_step_count for a UID are reset to 0 when its hotkey is replaced
    in the metagraph (resync_metagraph).
  notes: 'Per-UID arrays: ema_scores (raw EMA), scores (debiased EMA), ema_step_count (sample count for
    debiasing). Persisted via save_validator_state / load_state.'
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - docs/mechanism.md
  - README.md
  - docs/wandb_logging.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '27'
    quote: --subtensor.network finney --netuid 83
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: CliqueAI/validator.py
    line_ref: 1-19
    quote: import asyncio ... from CliqueAI.scoring.clique_scoring import CliqueScoreCalculator
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: docs/mechanism.md
    line_ref: 98-99
    quote: A `solution` is a set of vertices $G = \{v_1, …, v_N\}$, where $N$ is the size of the clique.
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: CliqueAI/scoring/clique_scoring.py
    line_ref: 77-86
    quote: omega[i] = np.exp(-pr[i] / rel[i]) ... omega_normalized = omega / max_omega
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: CliqueAI/scoring/clique_scoring.py
    line_ref: 99-111
    quote: unq = np.array([1 / counts[sol] for sol in canonical_responses]) ... delta = val * unq ...
      delta_normalized = delta / max_delta
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: CliqueAI/scoring/clique_scoring.py
    line_ref: '122'
    quote: rewards = optimality * (1 + self.difficulty) + diversity
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: common/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 303,343-344
    quote: LAMBDA_WEIGHTS = 1.0 ... weights = LAMBDA_WEIGHTS * sigmoid_weights ; weights[0] = (1.0 - LAMBDA_WEIGHTS)
      * sum(sigmoid_weights)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: common/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 324-335
    quote: midpoint = np.median(nonzero_normalized) ... sigmoid_weights, power_gamma = self._power_weight(sigmoid_scores,
      normalized, self.POWER_TARGET_TOP_HALF_SHARE)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: common/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 32-33
    quote: 'POWER_TARGET_TOP_HALF_SHARE: float = 0.80 ; POWER_MAX_GAMMA: float = 32.0'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: common/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 350-358
    quote: result, msg = self.subtensor.set_weights(wallet=self.wallet, netuid=self.config.netuid, uids=uids,
      weights=weights, ... version_key=validator_int_version,)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: common/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 66-71
    quote: --neuron.epoch_length ... how often we set weights, measured in 12 second blocks ... default=361,
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/alpha
    source_path: common/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 165-170
    quote: --neuron.ema_alpha ... Exponential moving average alpha parameter ... default=0.01,
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes
    source_path: common/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 442-451
    quote: self.ema_scores[uids_array] = (alpha * rewards + (1 - alpha) * self.ema_scores[uids_array])
      ; correction = 1 - np.power(1 - alpha, self.ema_step_count) ; self.scores = np.divide(self.ema_scores,
      correction, ...)
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: CliqueAI/scoring/clique_scoring.py
    line_ref: 29-53
    quote: 'if len(node_set) == 0: return False ... if node_set.issubset(self.graph.adjacency_list[candidate]):
      return False  # Clique can be extended, so it''s not maximum'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: CliqueAI/validator.py
    line_ref: 119-129
    quote: random.shuffle(new_vertices) ; vertex_map = dict(zip(old_vertices, new_vertices)) ... graph.adjacency_list
      = new_adjacency_list
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/notes
    source_path: docs/mechanism.md
    line_ref: 47-49
    quote: 'Medium (difficulty=0.2): general graph with $290≤|V|≤300$ ... Very Hard (difficulty=1): general
      graph with $690≤|V|≤700$'
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: common/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 374-379
    quote: 'if hotkey != self.metagraph.hotkeys[uid]: ... self.ema_scores[uid] = 0 ; self.scores[uid]
      = 0 ; self.ema_step_count[uid] = 0'
  - claim_field: /documentation/primary_docs
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 20-22
    quote: '- [Mechanism](docs/mechanism.md) – Detailed explanation of our mechanism.'
  unresolved:
  - Exact miner-selection probability constants are documented (r_ref=1.5) only in docs/mechanism.md;
    MinerSelector source not read to confirm literal values.
composition:
  shape: multiplex
  overlays:
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Basilica
docs.text:
Subnet tplr-ai__basilica netuid 39 Archetype multiplex Language rust Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners operate GPU machines (assumed 8 GPUs per node) that are validated for hardware authenticity via attestation/SSH automation. Earnings are denominated in USD via two ledgers fetched from the Basilica API: Compute Units (CU), a capacity signal priced per GPU-hour per GPU category and diluted by DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: HardwareProbe, OrganicTraffic"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: HardwareValidation, CodeInspection, Deduplication"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: hardware_spec"]
  M1["Metric: volume"]
  M2["Metric: hardware_spec"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: basilica

    netuid: 39
    lang: rust
    status: partial_burn
    submission: compute_service

    @burn {
        uid: "204"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: hardware_spec
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: volume
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: hardware_spec
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            HardwareProbe {
                trust_model: adversarial
            },
            OrganicTraffic {
                trust_model: oracle
            }
        ]
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "360 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Scoring is live (compute_incentive_pool drives set_weights), but the shipped validator.toml.example
  sets forced_burn_percentage = 95.0, so 95% of emission capacity is removed before the dynamic pool,
  and any further unmet capacity becomes additional dynamic burn to burn_uid (default 204). The forced
  fraction is a config value, not a hardcoded constant, so the operative split depends on deployed config.
subnet:
  name: Basilica
  owner_repo: tplr-ai__basilica
  netuid: 39
  implementation_lang: rust
task:
  summary: 'Miners operate GPU machines (assumed 8 GPUs per node) that are validated for hardware authenticity
    via attestation/SSH automation. Earnings are denominated in USD via two ledgers fetched from the Basilica
    API: Compute Units (CU), a capacity signal priced per GPU-hour per GPU category and diluted by category
    supply, and Revenue Units (RU), the organic rental revenue a miner''s hardware actually earned, paid
    at a revenue-share percentage. Per-epoch USD payouts are converted to weights against the epoch''s
    alpha emission capacity; any shortfall and any forced burn go to a burn UID.'
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
  submission_format_other: ''
scoring_signals:
- name: compute_units_capacity
  metric_kind: hardware_spec
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: 'Per-GPU-category dilution: effective_price = min(row_price_usd, category_capacity_budget
    / category_cu_supply); payout = vested_fraction * cu_amount * effective_price. Capacity budget = target_count*8
    * window_hours * price_per_gpu_usd.'
  notes: CU rows priced per GPU-hour (price_per_gpu_cents). target_count assumes 8 GPUs/node. Diluted
    so that an over-provisioned category cannot earn above its capacity budget.
- name: revenue_units_organic
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: payout = vested_fraction * ru_amount * revenue_share_pct/100. Not subject to per-category
    dilution.
  notes: RU = realized organic rental revenue; paid additively with CU at a revenue-share fraction.
- name: hardware_attestation
  metric_kind: hardware_spec
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: verification_score = valid_count/total_count over node validations where is_valid && attestation_valid;
    tracked in GPU profile. The CU/RU ledger (server-side) is the basis for weight payouts; on-chain attestation
    gates whether a node's units count (is_slashed filter).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: Node validation via attestation / ssh_automation producing GPU model, count, memory; stored
    as VerificationLog and used to build the miner GPU profile.
  source_identifier: VerificationLog.verification_type in {attestation, ssh_automation}
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: attestation_valid requires verification_type attestation/ssh_automation AND success.
- kind: organic_traffic
  description: CU/RU ledgers fetched from the Basilica API (get_cus / get_rus / get_incentive_config),
    reflecting metered GPU capacity and realized rental revenue. Token prices (alpha/tao USD) also fetched
    from the API to size emission capacity.
  source_identifier: 'BasilicaApiClient: /v1/incentive/* CU and RU ledger rows'
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: Off-chain server-side ledgers; validator trusts the API for CU/RU/prices/config.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '204'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: 'Two burn sources. (1) Forced: forced_burn_percentage (config; 95.0 in validator.toml.example)
      removes that fraction of usd_emission_capacity up front and adds forced_pct*u16::MAX weight to burn_uid.
      (2) Dynamic residual: burn_rate = max(0, 1 - usd_required_epoch/effective_usd_capacity); when required
      < capacity the unmet share routes to burn_uid. If no payouts or zero capacity, all weight (u16::MAX)
      goes to burn_uid.'
  notes: Per-miner USD payouts summed, scaled down by effective_usd_capacity/required if over capacity,
    then each uid share = payout/effective_usd_capacity; shares normalized to u16::MAX via largest-remainder
    rounding. burn_uid receives the residual.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Every weight_set_interval_blocks the validator computes the incentive pool from CU/RU ledgers
    and submits NormalizedWeights via set_weights_payload + submit_extrinsic. version_key increments each
    set to prevent replay. Retries up to 3x at the compute and submission layers.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 360 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA on the final weights; instead each ledger row vests linearly over its window_hours (vested_fraction
      = epoch/window overlap), spreading a row's value across epochs.
anti_gaming:
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: rejection
  description: CU/RU rows are only counted if not is_slashed, the hotkey is in the metagraph, and (for
    CU) the gpu_category is configured. GPU profile score depends on attestation_valid node validations.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: penalty
  description: slash_node POSTs /v1/incentive/slash with slash_pct (config slash_pct, e.g. 100); slashed
    ledger rows are excluded from payouts (is_slashed filter), zeroing that node's contribution.
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: CU dilution caps a category's total payout at its capacity budget (target_count*8 GPUs),
    so over-provisioning a category dilutes per-CU price rather than increasing total category payout.
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages:
  - name: 'CU compute-capacity pool (per GPU category: A100, H100, H200, B200, B300)'
    role: main
    advancement_rule: per-category capacity-budget dilution; payout = vested * cu_amount * min(price,
      budget/supply)
  - name: RU organic-revenue pool (revenue-share, not category-diluted)
    role: main
    advancement_rule: payout = vested * ru_amount * revenue_share_pct/100
  count: null
  notes: Two payout ledgers (CU, RU) combine additively per miner. CU is further partitioned by GPU category,
    each with its own target_count, price_per_gpu_cents, and capacity budget.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: 'Per-epoch: payouts recomputed each epoch from ledger rows overlapping the epoch window;
    vesting spreads a row across epochs. GPU profile (total_score, last_successful_validation) upserted
    per validation; only last GPU_CATEGORY_CUTOFF_HOURS=3 hours of validations considered for scoring
    refresh.'
  notes: GpuProfileRepository stores MinerGpuProfile (total_score, gpu_counts); WeightSetEpochRepository
    tracks epoch windows (period_start/end, attempts, success). CU/RU ledgers held server-side via API.
documentation:
  status: code_authoritative
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:

  extractor_notes: 'SN39 Basilica is a GPU-rental subnet: weights derive from off-chain USD ledgers (CU

docs.title: GRAIL
docs.text:
Subnet tplr-ai__grail netuid None Archetype gated Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners generate GRPO rollouts in a Triton-kernel code-generation environment. Each rollout produces token completions whose hidden states are bound by a cryptographic GRAIL proof. Validators verify a sample of rollouts through a 9-stage pipeline and the only metric that drives weight is the number o DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: ProofOfWork, Deduplication, PlagiarismDetection"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: volume"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  SM["smooth: Sma"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  M0 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: grail

    netuid: -
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: signals

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        ProofOfWork {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        PlagiarismDetection {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, cumulative_score }

    Gated {
        score: Metric {
            kind: volume
            direction: higher_is_better
            normalization: other
        }
        groundTruth: DeterministicDataset {
            trust_model: adversarial
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: gated
            normalization: other
        }
        smooth: Sma {
            window: 12
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "360 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: 90% of emissions always go to the burn UID (UID 0); the remaining 10% is split
  among miners proportionally to cap achievement, with any unallocated portion also going to burn. Scoring
  is active (capped mode is the current default), but a fixed 90% base burn means most emission is burned
  regardless of miner output.
subnet:
  name: GRAIL
  owner_repo: tplr-ai__grail
  netuid: null
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners generate GRPO rollouts in a Triton-kernel code-generation environment. Each rollout
    produces token completions whose hidden states are bound by a cryptographic GRAIL proof. Validators
    verify a sample of rollouts through a 9-stage pipeline and the only metric that drives weight is the
    number of UNIQUE rollouts (distinct SHA-256 of completion tokens) a miner produces per window. Task
    correctness / reward is a verification gate, not the scored quantity.
  submission_format:
  - signals
scoring_signals:
- name: unique_rollouts
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: 'Capped mode (current default): min(unique, period_cap) then normalized against
    period_cap^exponent. Uncapped mode: divided by sum of all scores.'
  notes: A rollout is unique if its completion tokens (after the prompt) have a distinct SHA-256 hash.
    Counted from a verified sample then extrapolated via estimated_unique = unique_in_sample / sample_rate.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Triton-kernel code-generation environment (CURRENT_ENV_ID = "triton_kernel"). Generated
    code is compiled and executed against reference tests; rewards are deterministic given (completion,
    context). Validator recomputes the GRAIL proof, logprobs and reward independently and never trusts
    miner-supplied data.
  source_identifier: triton_kernel
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Reward gate uses sigmoid R = sigmoid(1{correct} + min(speedup, clip) - delta) with SIGMOID_DELTA=1.8,
    SPEEDUP_CLIP=3.0; compared to miner claim with REWARD_REL_TOL=0.02. This only gates rollout validity,
    it is not the scored signal.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: gated
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: 'Capped mode: cap_relative = score / (period_cap^exponent), then if total>1.0 share
    proportionally capped at 1.0, else miners keep their cap-relative share and the remainder burns. Uncapped
    mode: score / sum(all_scores).'
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 0.9
    dynamic: true
    notes: GRAIL_BURN_PERCENTAGE = 90.0 base to GRAIL_BURN_UID = 0. In capped mode the "missing" weight
      from underproduction also goes to burn rather than being redistributed (effective_miner_fraction
      = min(remaining, remaining*total_cap_relative)). Burn UID receives 1.0 - sum(miner weights).
  notes: 'Superlinear scoring: superlinear_score = capped_unique ** SUPERLINEAR_EXPONENT with SUPERLINEAR_EXPONENT
    = 4.0, amplifying differences and making sybil splitting unprofitable (k identities yield k^(1-4)
    of reward).'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Validators process the previous complete window (30 blocks), verify a sample, score miners by
    unique rollouts over a rolling 12-window history, apply superlinear + cap-proportional scoring and
    90% burn, then call set_weights.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 360 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: sma
    alpha: null
    window: 12
    notes: WEIGHT_ROLLING_WINDOWS = WEIGHT_SUBMISSION_INTERVAL_BLOCKS(360)/WINDOW_LENGTH(30) = 12. Metrics
      are summed over the rolling 12-window submission interval; weights submitted once per 360-block
      interval.
anti_gaming:
- kind: proof_of_work
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'GRAIL proof: validator picks CHALLENGE_K=32 positions, extracts last-layer hidden activations,
    selects PROOF_TOPK=16, log-buckets them, computes a random linear sketch mod Mersenne prime PRIME_Q=2147483647
    and compares to the miner commitment. Hard check; a single failure rejects the rollout.'
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Only unique completions count toward weight; duplicate completions (same SHA-256 of completion
    tokens) within or across problems give no extra weight.
- kind: plagiarism_detection
  enforcement: elimination
  description: Copycat detection hashes completion tokens across miners. Per-window overlap > COPYCAT_WINDOW_THRESHOLD=0.05
    of the smaller miner, or per-interval overlap > COPYCAT_INTERVAL_THRESHOLD=0.03, flags both miners;
    flagged metrics are zeroed. Any failure in the last 14 windows yields zero weight.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 9-stage verification pipeline (schema, token, environment prompt, GRAIL proof, termination
    at MIN_EOS_PROBABILITY=0.02, environment evaluation, reward match, logprob match — all hard; distribution
    heuristic — soft). Soft failures gate a miner only if > STOCHASTIC_CHECK_FAILURE_THRESHOLD=0.51 of
    checked rollouts fail.
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: elimination
  description: 'Failure gating: no rollout files in recent windows, any verification/copycat failure in
    the last FAILURE_LOOKBACK_WINDOWS=14 windows, or never being selected for validation all result in
    zero weight.'
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Superlinear exponent SUPERLINEAR_EXPONENT=4.0 makes sybil splitting unprofitable: splitting
    into k identities yields k^(1-4) of the total reward.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Single environment (triton_kernel). Two scoring MODES (capped / uncapped) are hardcoded and switched
    network-wide, not parallel sub-competitions.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Failure flag and copycat flag persist over a 14-window lookback (FAILURE_LOOKBACK_WINDOWS);
    any failure in that window zeroes weight.
  notes: Validator maintains rolling 12-window availability and selection history (deques of maxlen WEIGHT_ROLLING_WINDOWS)
    plus per-hotkey per-window inference metrics (estimated_unique, had_failure) summed over the interval.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: v5
  primary_docs:
  - docs/incentive-mechanism.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: docs/incentive-mechanism.md
    line_ref: 11-12
    quote: The only metric that matters for your weight is **unique rollouts**. A rollout is unique if
      its completion tokens (the part after the prompt) have a distinct SHA-256 hash.
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/name
    source_path: grail/scoring/scorer.py
    line_ref: 46-52
    quote: '# Count unique (by hashing completion tokens) ... digest = MinerScorer._compute_rollout_hash(r)
      ... unique_hashes.add(digest)'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: grail/scoring/scorer.py
    line_ref: 54-59
    quote: '# Extrapolate from sample to estimate total ... estimated_unique = int(len(unique_hashes)
      / sample_rate)'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: grail/protocol/constants.py
    line_ref: 193-195
    quote: CURRENT_ENV_ID = "triton_kernel"
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/notes
    source_path: grail/protocol/constants.py
    line_ref: 125-133
    quote: '# Sigmoid formulation: R = sigmoid(1{correct} + min(speedup, clip) - delta) ... SIGMOID_DELTA
      = 1.8 ... SPEEDUP_CLIP = 3.0 ... REWARD_REL_TOL = 0.02'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: grail/protocol/constants.py
    line_ref: 138-141
    quote: '# Superlinear weighting exponent for sybil resistance. w_i proportional to s_i^p. Splitting
      into k identities yields k^(1-p) * s^p < s^p. ... SUPERLINEAR_EXPONENT = 4.0'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: grail/scoring/weights.py
    line_ref: 117-120
    quote: '# Apply superlinear scoring ... superlinear_score = base_score**self.superlinear_exponent'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization_other
    source_path: grail/scoring/weights.py
    line_ref: 122-140
    quote: 'max_score = period_cap**self.superlinear_exponent ; cap_relative = [score / max_score for

docs.title: templar (τemplar)
docs.text:
Subnet tplr-ai__templar netuid 3 Archetype multiplicative Language python Mechanism status dormant Task Miners perform distributed pre-training of a shared language model: each miner trains on assigned data shards and submits compressed (TopK/DCT-quantized) pseudo-gradients each window. Validators gather peer gradients, and for each evaluated miner apply that miner's gradient to the model and measure DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: ReferenceModel"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, DeterministicCheck, LivenessCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: loss"]
  M1["Metric: other"]
  M2["Metric: similarity"]
  AGG["aggregate: RankBased"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: templarEmplar

    netuid: 3
    lang: python
    status: dormant
    submission: signals

    @burn {
        uid: "1"
        fraction: 0.5
    }
    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { reputation, performance_ledger, cooldown, status_enum }

    Multiplicative {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: loss
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: rank
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: classification_quality
                specific: overfitting_indicator
                raw: "binary overfitting indicator: +1 if relative improvement on the miner's own data exceeds that on random data, else -1; EMA-smoothed into a binary moving average"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: ema
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: ReferenceModel {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: RankBased {
            composition: multiplicative
            normalization: sum_to_one
            decay_rate: 0.5
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.05
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_round
            tempo: "every 3 sync windows (windows_per_weights); blocks_per_window=115; burn.py every 360 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: dormant
mechanism_status_notes: 'The main README states "Templar is dormant ... Templar''s decentralized training
  protocol is not currently active". docs/README.md states "100% Burn Period Active: Miners are not being
  evaluated and no emissions are being distributed." A dedicated neurons/burn.py sets 100% weight to the
  burn UID (UID 1) every 360 blocks. Subnet 3 now runs a different mechanism (Crusades). The documented
  scoring pipeline below (validator.py, burn_rate=0.5) is the designed-but-dormant mechanism; the live
  reality is full burn to UID 1. netuid is ambiguous: validator.py/burn.py default to --netuid 268, while
  README states the subnet is netuid 3.'
subnet:
  name: templar (τemplar)
  owner_repo: tplr-ai__templar
  netuid: 3
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners perform distributed pre-training of a shared language model: each miner trains on assigned
    data shards and submits compressed (TopK/DCT-quantized) pseudo-gradients each window. Validators gather
    peer gradients, and for each evaluated miner apply that miner''s gradient to the model and measure
    the loss reduction on a random data batch. The per-miner gradient score is the relative loss improvement
    (L_before - L_after)/L_before on random data. Scores feed an OpenSkill (PlackettLuce) skill rating;
    the final per-miner score multiplies the OpenSkill ordinal by a binary-overfitting moving average
    and a model-sync score. Weights are then split (designed) between a burn UID and the top "gather"/"reserve"
    peers. As of the captured source the mechanism is dormant / in full-burn mode.'
  submission_format:
  - signals
  submission_format_other: ''
scoring_signals:
- name: gradient_loss_improvement
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: rank
  notes: gradient_scores[uid] = (loss_before_random - loss_after_random) / loss_before_random, i.e. the
    relative reduction in model loss on a random batch after applying the miner's gradient. This raw score
    is fed (as scores, higher-is-better) into an OpenSkill PlackettLuce rate() call which produces per-miner
    mu/sigma; the OpenSkill ordinal is what enters the final score, so the effective normalization is
    rank/skill-rating based.
- name: binary_overfitting_indicator
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: 'binary overfitting indicator: +1 if relative improvement on the miner''s own data
    exceeds that on random data, else -1; EMA-smoothed into a binary moving average'
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: ema
  notes: 'binary_indicator_scores[uid] = 1 if improvement_own > improvement_random else -1. Smoothed:
    binary_moving_averages = (1 - binary_score_ma_alpha)*old + binary_score_ma_alpha*indicator, with binary_score_ma_alpha
    = 0.05. In the final score the BMA is clamped to max(0, bma); after bma_warmup_windows (10) a bma_threshold
    (0.10) gate zeroes BMAs below threshold. Multiplicative gate on the final score (no additive weight).'
- name: model_sync_score
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: sync_score = max(0, (1 - x/3)^2.5) where x = min(avg_steps_behind, 3)
  notes: Measures how well-synchronized a miner's model is with the validator's. sync_score = max(0.0,
    (1.0 - x / 3.0) ** 2.5) where x is the average number of steps the miner is behind, capped at 3. Multiplicative
    factor in the final score (no additive weight).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: reference_model
  description: The validator's own copy of the collaboratively-trained model is the reference. A miner's
    gradient quality is judged by the change in this model's cross-entropy loss on data batches before
    vs. after applying the miner's submitted gradient (own-data and random-data batches).
  source_identifier: neurons/validator.py gradient evaluation (loss_before/after_random)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Data comes from sharded pre-training corpora (DCLM / FineWeb-edu per docs) managed by ShardedDatasetManager;
    loss is computed deterministically by the validator on its model.
aggregation:
  method: rank_based
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: sum_to_one
  normalization_other: ''
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: 0.5
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '1'
    fraction: 0.5
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'Designed mechanism (validator.py update_weights): self.weights[self.burn_uid] = burn_rate
      with hparams burn_rate = 0.5 and burn_uid = 1; non-burn weights are rescaled so they sum to (1 -
      burn_rate). LIVE reality is fuller: docs declare a "100% Burn Period" and neurons/burn.py sets weights=[1.0]
      to UID 1, i.e. effective fraction 1.0 while dormant. decay_rate 0.5 is the reserve_decay_ratio geometric
      base used to taper reserve-peer weights (k=0.5 per rank).'
  notes: Final per-miner score = openskill_ordinal * bma * sync_score (multiplicative composition of the
    three signals). update_weights then allocates (1-burn_rate)*gather_share (0.75) to the top gather_peer_count
    (20) positive-scored peers on a linear profile (w1 = gather_top_ratio=2.0 x wn), and (1-burn_rate)*(1-gather_share)
    to the next reserve_peer_count (10) peers on a geometric decay (reserve_decay_ratio=0.5). burn_rate
    (0.5) to burn_uid; final vector rescaled to sum to 1.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_round
  method: 'Designed: validator sets weights every windows_per_weights (3) sync windows via self.comms.subtensor.set_weights
    over positive-weighted UIDs (plus burn UID). Live/dormant: neurons/burn.py independently sets weights=[1.0]
    to burn UID 1 every 360 blocks.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: every 3 sync windows (windows_per_weights); blocks_per_window=115; burn.py every
    360 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.05
    window: null
    notes: The binary overfitting indicator is EMA-smoothed with binary_score_ma_alpha = 0.05 (new_avg
      = (1-alpha)*old + alpha*new). OpenSkill ratings provide additional temporal smoothing of skill across
      windows. There is no single EMA over the final reward vector itself.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Index-similarity slashing: instantiate_slashing_multiplier() returns multipliers applied
    to final_score by gradient index overlap — 0.5 when similarity high, 0.0 when similarity >= 95% ("max"),
    0.0 when similarity == 100% ("mega"). Catches miners copying others'' sparse gradient indices.'
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Overfitting detection via the binary indicator: a miner scoring better on its own data
    than on random data gets indicator -1, dragging down its binary moving average (a multiplicative gate
    on the final score).'
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Inactivity / missing-gradient slashing. inactivity_slash_rate = 0.25 per window for inactive
    peers; missing_gradient_slash_rate = 0.75 (final_score *= 0.75) for missing gradients; missing_gradient_penalty_score
    = -99.0. Consecutive negative evaluations (>= 3, exclude_negative _peers) exclude a peer from gathering;
    >50% negative in last 8 evals slashes final_score by 75%.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Only the highest-stake validator (S.argmax) posts the canonical start_window; weights are
    set via standard Bittensor stake-weighted set_weights consensus.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Single collaborative pre-training task. Weight schedule distinguishes "gather" peers (top 20)
    and "reserve" peers (next 10) but this is a ranking tier within one mechanism, not separate competitions.
    An optional anneal_mode switches dataset shards but not the scoring rule.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - reputation
  - performance_ledger
  - cooldown
  - status_enum
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: reset_peer(uid) zeroes final/gradient/sync/binary scores, OpenSkill rating and history.
    Positive evaluation resets consecutive_negative_count and can re-include an excluded peer.
  notes: 'Per-miner persistent state includes: OpenSkill ratings (mu/sigma), binary_moving_averages, final_scores,
    peer_eval_history (last 20 negative/positive evals), consecutive_negative_count, missing_gradient_history
    (last 8), excluded_from_gather set with exclusion_start_window, and naughty_peers (timeout 20). Inactive
    peers tracked in inactive_scores. State persisted to validator-state-<version>.pt.'
documentation:

  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
