  - Live burn fraction is effectively 1.0 (full-burn period per docs / burn.py weights=[1.0]); the designed
    validator burn_rate is 0.5. burn_allocation.fraction records the designed 0.5 with the full-burn reality
    noted.
composition:
  shape: multiplicative
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 1

docs.title: TrajectoryRL
docs.text:
Subnet trajectoryRL__trajectoryRL netuid 11 Archetype gated Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners submit content-addressed "packs" (PolicyBundles) whose payload is a SKILL.md — instructions/strategies teaching an AI agent (Hermes) to solve Terminal-Bench-format shell scenarios. Validators run the agent inside each scenario container with the miner's SKILL.md, then a fresh verifier contain DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: Deduplication, PlagiarismDetection, CommitReveal"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  AGG["aggregate: WinnerTakeAll"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: trajectoryRl

    netuid: 11
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: [content_link, commitment_hash]

    @burn {
        uid: "74"
        fraction: 0.95
    }
    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        PlagiarismDetection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, cooldown, cumulative_score }

    Gated {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WinnerTakeAll {
            composition: gated
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "100 blocks (weight_interval_blocks default)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: single
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: INCENTIVE_MECHANISM.md documents winner-take-all (weight[winner]=1.0). The actual
  validator code (trajectoryrl/base/validator.py) sets the winner's weight to 1.0 - BURN_FRACTION = 0.05
  and routes BURN_FRACTION = 0.95 to OWNER_UID = 74 on every weight write, and burns 100% to the owner
  when no winner is seated or the winner cache is stale.
subnet:
  name: TrajectoryRL
  owner_repo: trajectoryRL__trajectoryRL
  netuid: 11
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners submit content-addressed "packs" (PolicyBundles) whose payload is a SKILL.md — instructions/strategies
    teaching an AI agent (Hermes) to solve Terminal-Bench-format shell scenarios. Validators run the agent
    inside each scenario container with the miner's SKILL.md, then a fresh verifier container scores the
    agent's output. Reward goes (per docs) to the single seated winner pack; in the live code the winner's
    emission is 95% burned to the owner UID.
  submission_format:
  - content_link
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: scenario_correctness
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Per-scenario quality = passed_tests / total_tests parsed from the verifier's ctrf.json (continuous
    in [0,1]); final score is the equal-weighted sum across scenarios (range [0, N]). Falls back to the
    binary reward (0 or 1) when the ctrf payload is unparseable. Season 1 docs (EVALUATION_S1.md) describe
    an alternative agent-judge quality score with a hard qualification gate (qualified must be true on
    all episodes).
- name: qualified_gate
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Stake-weighted majority qualified flag. consensus_qualified is true when qualified-reporting
    stake > 0.5 of valid stake; only qualified votes contribute to the consensus score. A non-qualified
    miner cannot seat as winner. Cited in winner_state.aggregate_challenger_score and EVALUATION_S1.md
    qualification gate.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Fixed set of Terminal-Bench-format shell-verifier scenarios (SANDBOX_SCENARIOS), each scored
    by a one-shot pytest verifier container producing ctrf.json.
  source_identifier: trajrl-bench (ghcr.io/trajectoryrl/sandbox-agent)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Scenarios + episodes-per-scenario are deliberately non-configurable so all validators run the
    same set and scores are comparable.
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: Season 1 agent-judge that SSHes into the sandbox, reads a per-scenario JUDGE.md rubric,
    inspects sandbox state, and emits a structured quality score. Also a server-side Phase-1 LLM-as-judge
    integrity pre-eval gate.
  source_identifier: JUDGE.md per scenario / Pre-Eval Gate
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Judge never sees the miner SKILL.md (prompt-injection isolation). EVALUATION_S1.md.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: gated
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '74'
    fraction: 0.95
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'BURN_FRACTION = 0.95 in trajectoryrl/base/validator.py: when a winner is seated, weights =
      [1.0 - BURN_FRACTION, BURN_FRACTION] on [winner_uid, OWNER_UID]; the winner receives 0.05 and the
      owner UID 74 receives 0.95. When no winner is seated, or the cache is stale (> WINNER_FALLBACK_TTL),
      100% is burned to OWNER_UID.'
  notes: 'Per docs: server-coordinated stake-weighted aggregation. consensus_score = Σ(stake_i × score_i)/Σ(stake_i)
    over qualified-reporting validators; consensus_qualified = stake-weighted majority (>0.5). Winner
    Protection (delta = 0.03, docs) requires a challenger to beat the seated winner''s frozen score by
    the multiplicative margin. NOTE: the in-repo ValidatorConfig still carries the legacy v5.x score_delta
    = 0.10 and quorum_threshold = 0.5; the documented v6.0 delta of 0.03 is not present as a literal constant
    in the read code.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: Tempo-gated weight loop. Reads the locally-derived seated winner (cached from GET /api/v2/winner/current
    via derive_winner_state) and calls set_weights for [winner_uid, OWNER_UID] = [0.05, 0.95], or burns
    100% to OWNER_UID when no winner / stale cache. Always sets weights every tempo to avoid deregistration.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100 blocks (weight_interval_blocks default)
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No local EMA on the final reward; on-chain YC3 + Liquid Alpha bond dynamics handle cross-validator
      smoothing (yuma_version 3, liquid_alpha_enabled true, bonds_moving_avg 900000 per docs).
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Pack ownership lock (pack_first_seen): server records the first hotkey per pack_hash;
    later submissions of the same pack_hash from a different hotkey are rejected at queue admission (eval_status=''failed'').
    No succession; eviction by-active with EVICTION_GRACE_WINDOWS = 7.'
- kind: plagiarism_detection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: NCD (Normalized Compression Distance) similarity via zlib; SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = 0.80
    in trajectoryrl/utils/ncd.py (is_similar returns True when similarity >= threshold). Note v6.0 docs
    state NCD self-checks were removed from the validator daemon; the module remains in the repo.
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Winner Protection: challenger must beat the seated winner''s frozen consensus score by
    a multiplicative margin (docs delta = 0.03; code ValidatorConfig.score_delta = 0.10) to dethrone;
    resists copy-paste and trivial undercutting.'
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Server-coordinated stake-weighted aggregation + quorum gate (QUORUM_FRACTION / quorum_threshold
    = 0.5) and MIN_VALIDATOR_STAKE = 10000.0; dilutes single-validator manipulation and gates finalize
    on reporting stake share.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Content-addressed packs: validators verify sha256(canonical_json) == pack_hash independently;
    tampered bytes/URLs invalidate the commitment.'
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'Channel A: one chain commitment per ~100 blocks per hotkey. Channel B: MINER_SUBMIT_COOLDOWN_SECONDS
    = 3600. Owner ban after failed_pack_count > 3 (30-day ban, escalating to ~99 years).'
sub_competitions:
  structure: single
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Per-season benchmark; one challenger pack evaluated per challenge epoch. Season-defined scoring
    (Season 1 = Self-Learning Agents). Steady-state winner-take-all with a < 10-miner bootstrap phase
    (70/20/10 split, per docs).
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - cooldown
  - cumulative_score
  status_values:
  - pending_pre_eval
  - pending_eval
  - failed
  - completed
  - exhausted
  - blacklisted
  reset_policy: eval_status advances through the submission lifecycle per pack_hash; quorum abort moves
    the submission to 'exhausted' (no retry). winner_state is not reset on SPEC_NUMBER bump.
  notes: Server holds winner_state / winner_history / validator_activity; miner cooldown via MINER_COOLDOWN_EPOCHS
    = 5; validators marked inactive after INACTIVE_THRESHOLD_EPOCHS = 3 consecutive misses. State is primarily
    server-side in v6.0; the validator daemon is intentionally thin.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: v6.0 (INCENTIVE_MECHANISM.md, 2026-05-07); SPEC_NUMBER = 10
  primary_docs:
  - docs/INCENTIVE_MECHANISM.md
  - docs/EVALUATION_S1.md
  - README.md
  - trajectoryrl/base/validator.py
  - trajectoryrl/utils/config.py
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: trajectoryrl/utils/config.py
    line_ref: '82'
    quote: 'netuid: int = 11'
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: trajectoryrl/base/validator.py
    line_ref: '1'
    quote: '"""TrajectoryRL Validator — v6.0 winner-challenger daemon.'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '112'
    quote: Mining means writing a SKILL.md — instructions and strategies that teach an AI agent how to
      handle operational scenarios. The validator runs the agent (Hermes) inside each scenario's container
      ...; a fresh container of the same image runs `pytest` against the agent's output for the score.
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: docs/INCENTIVE_MECHANISM.md
    line_ref: 34-36
    quote: Channel A — On-Chain Commitment. Miners upload pack.json to any publicly accessible HTTP(S)
      URL ... and submit metadata on-chain via Bittensor's set_commitment extrinsic.
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: trajectoryrl/utils/sandbox_harness.py
    line_ref: 77-79
    quote: 'Continuous correctness in [0.0, 1.0]: passed_tests / total_tests from the verifier''s ctrf.json.
      Falls back to ``float(reward)`` (binary 0 or 1) when ctrf isn''t parseable.'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: trajectoryrl/utils/sandbox_harness.py
    line_ref: 110-126
    quote: Final score is the equal-weighted sum ... self.final_score = sum(scores)
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: trajectoryrl/utils/winner_state.py
    line_ref: 133-135
    quote: qualified_subs = [s for s in valid if s.get("challenger_qualified")] ... consensus_qualified
      = (qualified_stake / total_stake) > 0.5
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/notes
    source_path: trajectoryrl/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 170-174
    quote: 'Scenarios + episodes-per-scenario are not configurable on purpose: every validator runs the
      same set so scores are comparable across validators / windows / SPEC_NUMBER bumps.'
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/1/description
    source_path: docs/EVALUATION_S1.md
    line_ref: 37-39
    quote: Scoring uses an agent judge that independently explores the sandbox environment and grades
      the testee agent's work. The judge reads a natural-language rubric (`JUDGE.md` per scenario) and
      produces a quality score.
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: docs/INCENTIVE_MECHANISM.md
    line_ref: 97-99
    quote: weight[winner] = 1.0 / weight[others] = 0.0
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: docs/INCENTIVE_MECHANISM.md
    line_ref: 331-336
    quote: consensus_score = Σ(stake_i × score_i) / Σ(stake_i), summed over qualified-reporting validators
      only
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction
    source_path: trajectoryrl/base/validator.py
    line_ref: '72'
    quote: 'BURN_FRACTION = 0.95  # 95% of miner emissions burned via owner UID'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: trajectoryrl/base/validator.py
    line_ref: '71'
    quote: OWNER_UID = 74
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/notes
    source_path: trajectoryrl/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 1245-1250
    quote: 'if winner_uid == OWNER_UID: uids = [OWNER_UID]; weights = [1.0] else: uids = [winner_uid,
      OWNER_UID]; weights = [1.0 - BURN_FRACTION, BURN_FRACTION]'
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: trajectoryrl/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 1166-1172
    quote: 'if not self._winner_state.is_fresh(...): ... "refusing set_weights and burning to owner UID"
      ... await self._set_burn_weights(reason="winner_cache_stale")'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: trajectoryrl/utils/config.py
    line_ref: '135'
    quote: 'weight_interval_blocks: int = 100'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: trajectoryrl/base/validator.py
    line_ref: 1195-1202
    quote: result = self.subtensor.set_weights(netuid=self.config.netuid, wallet=self.wallet, uids=uids,
      weights=weights, ...)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: docs/INCENTIVE_MECHANISM.md
    line_ref: 114-118
    quote: Validators always call `set_weights` every tempo, never skip — failure to set weights leads
      to deregistration.
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: docs/INCENTIVE_MECHANISM.md
    line_ref: '281'
    quote: A later submission with the same `pack_hash` but a different hotkey is treated as a copy and
      rejected before queue admission
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: trajectoryrl/utils/ncd.py
    line_ref: '19'
    quote: SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = 0.80
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: trajectoryrl/utils/config.py
    line_ref: '98'
    quote: 'score_delta: float = 0.10  # Winner Protection: challenger must score 10% higher'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/3/description
    source_path: trajectoryrl/utils/config.py
    line_ref: 113-114
    quote: 'min_validator_stake: float = 10000.0 ... quorum_threshold: float = 0.5 ... aggregate only
      when submitted stake share > threshold'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/4/description
    source_path: docs/INCENTIVE_MECHANISM.md
    line_ref: '260'
    quote: Validators verify `sha256(canonical_json) == pack_hash` independently.
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/5/description
    source_path: docs/INCENTIVE_MECHANISM.md
    line_ref: '64'
    quote: 'Per-miner cooldown: 60 min (configurable via `MINER_SUBMIT_COOLDOWN_SECONDS`)'
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/status_values
    source_path: docs/INCENTIVE_MECHANISM.md
    line_ref: 638-640
    quote: miner_submissions row inserted with eval_status = 'pending_pre_eval' ... failed → eval_status
      = 'failed' ... all checks pass → eval_status = 'pending_eval'
  - claim_field: /documentation/source_version
    source_path: trajectoryrl/utils/config.py
    line_ref: '40'
    quote: SPEC_NUMBER = 10
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/notes
    source_path: docs/INCENTIVE_MECHANISM.md
    line_ref: 103-110
    quote: When active miners < `bootstrap_threshold` (default 10) ... weight[1st] = 0.70 / weight[2nd]
      = 0.20 / weight[3rd] = 0.10
  unresolved:
  - '/aggregation Winner Protection delta: docs v6.0 state delta = 0.03, but the only literal in the read
    code is ValidatorConfig.score_delta = 0.10 (legacy v5.x value). The 0.03 constant is not present in
    the source read, so the documented value is not code-confirmed.'
  - '/scoring_signals active scoring method: two scoring descriptions coexist — sandbox_harness.py computes
    passed_tests/total_tests from ctrf.json (Terminal-Bench verifier), while EVALUATION_S1.md describes
    an agent-judge quality_score. Which is live for the current season is not unambiguously resolvable
    from the read sources.'
  - '/aggregation config vs docs divergence: config.py reflects the v5.x consensus-CAS model (IPFS, consensus_epsilon,
    window_publish_pct, disable_winner_protection default true) while INCENTIVE_MECHANISM.md and validator.py
    reflect v6.0 server-coordinated derivation; scoring/__init__.py confirms the v5.x scorer was removed.
    The config dataclass is partly stale relative to the live daemon.'
composition:
  shape: gated
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: Distil — SN97 (GLM-5 / Kimi-K2.6 distillation)
docs.text:
Subnet unarbos__distil netuid 97 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners distill the teacher model (moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6, ~1T total / ~32B active MoE) into a smaller student (<=33B total params, Kimi-family DeepseekV3 architecture, vocab 163,840), upload it to HuggingFace, and commit the repo link on-chain (one permanent commitment per hotkey). Validators score ev DataflowIMMLIR (YAML)

Mechanism {

    netuid: 97
    lang: python

    @guards {
        Deduplication {
            enforcement: rejection
        }

            enforcement: rejection

    }
    @state { cumulative_score, status_enum, cooldown }

    Multiplex<tournament> {

                kind: composite

            Metric {
                kind: composite

            Metric {
                kind: composite

                normalization: none
            },

                direction: higher_is_better

            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [

                trust_model: trusted
            },

                trust_model: trusted
            },

        }

            structure: tournament
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Live king-of-the-hill emission. Per service.py the default MULTI_KING_PAYOUT_ENABLED=1
  splits emission equally across up to 5 most-recent distinct kings; with that env unset it falls back
  to strict winner-take-all (king=1.0). No documented burn allocation.
subnet:
  name: Distil — SN97 (GLM-5 / Kimi-K2.6 distillation)
  owner_repo: unarbos__distil
  netuid: 97
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners distill the teacher model (moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6, ~1T total / ~32B active MoE) into a
    smaller student (<=33B total params, Kimi-family DeepseekV3 architecture, vocab 163,840), upload it
    to HuggingFace, and commit the repo link on-chain (one permanent commitment per hotkey). Validators
    score every committed model on a multi-axis composite (composite.final = alpha*worst_K_mean + (1-alpha)*weighted)
    anchored on procedurally-generated v31 axes plus distillation/judge/discipline tiers, crown the highest
    composite.final as "king", and set on-chain weights to that king (winner-take-all, optionally split
    across recent kings).
  submission_format:
  - model_weights
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: composite_final
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 1.0
  normalization: none
  notes: Ranking key. composite.final = COMPOSITE_FINAL_BOTTOM_WEIGHT*worst_K_mean + (1-COMPOSITE_FINAL_BOTTOM_WEIGHT)*weighted.
    Code default alpha=0.85, K (WORST_3_MEAN_K) default 3; README states live values alpha=0.85, K=5 (env-overridden).
- name: worst_K_mean
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Mean of the bottom-K non-broken per-axis values (K default 3). Anti-Goodhart pressure; dominates
    final via alpha=0.85. Surfaced as worst_3_mean in API.
- name: weighted
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Convex combination Sum(w_i*axis_i)/Sum(w_i) over AXIS_WEIGHTS and tier weights. Each axis normalized
    to [0,1].
- name: kl_divergence
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: One distillation axis (top-20 sparse KL vs teacher); KL > maxKlThreshold (2.0) disqualifies.
    KL alone never crowns; reward=exp(-kl) only in the legacy distillation/validator/reward.py path.
- name: on_policy_rkl
  metric_kind: loss
  direction: lower_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Distillation tier axis, AXIS_WEIGHTS weight 0.30 (highest single axis weight).
- name: judge_probe
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Quality tier; AXIS_WEIGHTS judge_probe 0.20 per README.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: reference_model
  description: Teacher model moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6 (multimodal wrapper, DeepSeek-V3 MoE inner). Teacher
    logprobs/continuations are the distillation target; top-20 sparse logprobs returned per position.
  source_identifier: moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: subnet-config.json teacher.model; vocabSize 163840, served via OpenRouter per README.
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Block-hash-seeded prompts drawn from ClimbMix-400B and 11 procedurally generated v31 axes
    (math/code/reasoning/long-context/knowledge/etc.); every item regenerated per round from the on-chain
    block seed.
  source_identifier: karpathy/climbmix-400b-shuffle
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: evalPromptsH2h 300 / evalPromptsFull 60; no static answer key (anti-Goodhart).
- kind: reference_model
  description: Always-in reference baseline (UID -1, Qwen/Qwen3.5-4B) run every round so the asymmetric
    reference-broken-axes filter can drop axes the base model cannot pass.
  source_identifier: Qwen/Qwen3.5-4B
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: subnet-config.json validator.referenceModel / referenceUid=-1.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn allocation found in source.
  notes: 'King = highest composite.final from state/composite_scores.json. Emission weight is winner-take-all
    (king=1.0) unless MULTI_KING_PAYOUT_ENABLED (default 1) splits 1.0/N equally across up to RECENT_KINGS_MAX=5
    most-recent distinct kings. Dethronement requires challenger.final > incumbent.final*(1+margin), SINGLE_EVAL_DETHRONE_MARGIN
    default 0.03 (README states 5% live). Composition is hybrid: signals combine additively within the
    composite but the final on-chain allocation is a winner/recent-kings selection, and a per-axis quality
    floor (KL>2.0, axis==0) gates to score 0.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Each epoch (~10 min / tempo default 360s) the validator computes the composite for newly-evaluated
    commitments (single-eval per commitment, king re-evaluated every round), selects the king, builds
    the emission-weight vector (winner-take-all or recent-kings split), and calls set_weights on-chain
    immediately.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: '360'
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: 'eval/scoring.py docstring: "No EMA — models are permanently committed, scores converge naturally".
      README step 8: "Raw scores, no EMA smoothing". The legacy distillation/base/validator.py update_scores
      path does apply an EMA (moving_average_alpha default 0.1) but is not the operative v31.2 path.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deduplication
  enforcement: rejection
  description: SHA256 hash of safetensors weights; identical weights to an existing model are blacklisted
    (COPY DQ) for that commitment. Earlier commitment by block number owns the hash.
- kind: plagiarism_detection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Logit/activation fingerprinting: models with cosine similarity > 0.99999 (activationCopyThreshold)
    on per-position vectors over the first 2 prompts are flagged as functional copies even when hashes
    differ.'
- kind: data_augmentation
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Block-hash-seeded procedural v31 axes (no static answer key), Isomorphic Perturbation Testing
    (name rotation in v31_consistency_paraphrase), and GSM-NoOp topical-distractor injection (v31_math_robustness)
    defeat memorised canonical wordings.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Architecture/integrity precheck (no GPU): <=33B total params (MoE-aware counting), Kimi
    tokenizer vocab 163840, no quantization (GPTQ/AWQ/FP8 rejected), model must stay public and unchanged
    on HuggingFace (revision-pinned git-SHA integrity); failure = INVALID/REMOVED DQ.'
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Per-UID failure counts (failures.json); is_stale at max_failures default 3. thinking_collapse_probe
    / reasoning_density axis penalise models that loop without answering.
sub_competitions:
  structure: tournament
  stages:
  - name: precheck
    role: qualifier
    advancement_rule: No-GPU architecture/integrity/duplicate checks every epoch; models that fail are
      never sent to GPU.
  - name: per_uid_composite_eval
    role: main
    advancement_rule: Single-eval per commitment (FIFO by commit_block, capped at SINGLE_EVAL_MAX_PER_ROUND
      default 10) plus always-in reference baseline; compute composite.final.
  - name: king_dethronement
    role: boss
    advancement_rule: King = max composite.final; challenger dethrones only if challenger.final > incumbent.final*(1+SINGLE_EVAL_DETHRONE_MARGIN)
      (default 0.03; README 5%). King re-evaluated every round for paired fairness.
  count: null
  notes: King-of-the-hill architecture.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - status_enum
  - cooldown
  status_values:
  - COPY
  - REMOVED
  - INVALID
  reset_policy: 'DQ is per-hotkey (since 2026-05-04): once a hotkey is DQ''d a new on-chain commit on
    the same hotkey does not clear it; the miner must register a new hotkey. Failure counts reset on a
    successful eval (reset_failures) or model update. README also describes per-commit DQ keyed hotkey:commit_block.'
  notes: State persisted to ./state/ (scores.json, disqualified.json, failures.json, composite_scores.json,
    score_history.json, recent_kings). recent_kings queue (max 5) drives multi-king emission split.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: v31.2
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/MINER_FAQ.md
  - docs/AUTONOMOUS_OPERATIONS.md

    which the README/docs describe as live, and a simplified/legacy KL-winner-take-all path (distillation/validator/reward.py,
    distillation/base/validator.py, eval/scoring.py). Fields are extracted from the operative path; the

    constants differ from README "live" values (K=3 code vs 5 README; dethrone margin 0.03 code vs 5%
    README) — both are cited; the code literal is treated as authoritative for numeric fields.'
  evidence:

docs.title: tau (unarbos)
docs.text:
Subnet unarbos__tau netuid 66 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners submit an editable single-file SWE agent (agent.py defining solve(repo_path, issue, model, api_base, api_key)) via a PR to the public unarbos/ninja harness, committed on-chain as a github-pr commitment. The validator runs a king-of-the-hill duel: the challenger agent and the reigning king age DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: DeterministicDataset, LlmJudgment"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: PlagiarismDetection, ChallengePeriod, CodeInspection"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: similarity"]
  M1["Metric: llm_judgment"]
  AGG["aggregate: TournamentBracket"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: tauUnarbos

    netuid: 66
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: [source_code, commitment_hash]

    @burn {
        uid: "uid-0"
        fraction: dynamic
    }
    @guards {
        PlagiarismDetection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        ChallengePeriod {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        CodeInspection {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        StakeWeighting {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { status_enum, registration_age }

    Multiplex<tournament> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: llm_judgment
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            DeterministicDataset {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            LlmJudgment {
                trust_model: oracle
            }
        ]
        aggregate: TournamentBracket {
            composition: tournament
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: "360 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: tournament
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: King-of-the-hill duel tournament is live and active. Validator weight is distributed
  across a rolling window of the last 5 kings (1/5 each). Empty bootstrap slots or slots pointing at a
  deregistered hotkey roll their share to burn UID 0, so emissions are partially burned only until/unless
  the window fills with live registered kings. This is a fallback burn, not a dormant documented mechanism,
  hence status active.
subnet:
  name: tau (unarbos)
  owner_repo: unarbos__tau
  netuid: 66
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners submit an editable single-file SWE agent (agent.py defining solve(repo_path, issue,
    model, api_base, api_key)) via a PR to the public unarbos/ninja harness, committed on-chain as a github-pr
    commitment. The validator runs a king-of-the-hill duel: the challenger agent and the reigning king
    agent each solve the same mined GitHub-commit repair tasks inside Docker. Each round is scored as
    a blend of changed-line similarity to a Cursor baseline solution plus an LLM diff judgment. The challenger
    that wins more decisive rounds than the king (and then wins a separate retest pool) is promoted to
    king and receives validator weight.'
  submission_format:
  - source_code
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: cursor_baseline_similarity
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: none
  notes: Per-round changed-lines-only similarity (difflib SequenceMatcher) of the agent's patch to the
    saved Cursor baseline solution. Cursor is only the measuring stick. Contributes cursor_weight = 1.0
    - _DIFF_JUDGE_WEIGHT = 0.5 to the combined round score.
- name: llm_diff_judgment
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: none
  notes: Per-round LLM diff judge using openai/gpt-5.4 via OpenRouter at temperature 0, medium reasoning
    effort, 16000-token output cap. Scores king and challenger patches against the task/reference context.
    Neutral fallback score 0.5 when unavailable. _DIFF_JUDGE_WEIGHT = 0.5.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: 'Each task is mined from a real GitHub commit: task/original is the repo before the commit,
    task/reference is the repo after, task/reference.patch filters out tiny tasks. Solutions are scored
    against the Cursor baseline.'
  source_identifier: mined GitHub commits; solutions/baseline (Cursor)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Cursor baseline solution (solutions/baseline) is the per-round scoring target for the similarity
    signal.
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: LLM diff judge reviewing king vs challenger patches against the task/reference context.
  source_identifier: openai/gpt-5.4 via OpenRouter
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: temperature 0, medium reasoning, 16000-token cap.
aggregation:
  method: tournament_bracket
  composition: tournament
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: uid-0
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: Burn UID is _BURN_KING_UID = 0. Weight is split into `window` slots of 1/window each (window
      = validate_king_window_size = 5, slot = 1/5). Each slot maps to one of the last N kings; empty (bootstrap)
      slots or slots whose king hotkey is deregistered/not in neurons add their slot share to the burn
      UID. Burn fraction is therefore a dynamic residual of unfilled slots, not a fixed constant.
  notes: Winner-take-window distribution. Within a duel, the challenger wins iff wins > losses + margin
    (margin = validate_win_margin = 0); ties ignored. Per-round combined score = 0.5*cursor_similarity
    + 0.5*llm_score; round winner is whichever of king/challenger has the higher combined score. Promotion
    additionally requires winning a separate retest pool. On promotion the new king is prepended to the
    rolling recent_kings window (capped at window length).
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: _maybe_set_weights distributes weight across the last N kings (rolling window). slot = 1.0/window
    per king; the same hotkey can hold multiple slots if it reclaimed the throne (shares accumulate).
    Unfilled or deregistered slots roll to burn UID 0. Skips if current_block - last_weight_block < validate_weight_interval_blocks
    unless forced.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 360 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing of the final reward in the validator. Temporal smoothing instead comes
      from the rolling king window (last 5 kings), not an exponential average.
anti_gaming:
- kind: plagiarism_detection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Copy detection: a duel is rejected when mean king-vs-challenger patch similarity >= _COPY_MEAN_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD
    = 0.90, or when at least _COPY_NEAR_EXACT_MIN_ROUNDS = 3 rounds have similarity >= _COPY_NEAR_EXACT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD
    = 0.98.'
- kind: challenge_period
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Promotion requires winning the primary 50-task duel pool AND a separate 50-task retest
    pool before the king changes (confirmation retest).
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Miner PRs to unarbos/ninja must pass required CI checks 'PR Scope Guard' (rejects edits
    outside agent.py, breaking the solve contract, or forbidden provider/sampling control) and 'OpenRouter
    PR Judge' (gpt-5.4 review requiring a score above JUDGE_MIN_SCORE) before being queued.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: 'A hotkey may submit only once per registration period: any prior on-chain commitment at
    or after the hotkey-spent cutoff block (default 8,104,340, VALIDATE_HOTKEY_SPENT_SINCE_BLOCK) spends
    the current registration; a hotkey must be freshly registered to resubmit.'
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Commitment must come from a registered subnet hotkey; PR title must start with the exact
    committing hotkey, base must be unarbos/ninja:main, PR open and not draft, and committed SHA must
    match the current PR head.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Managed inference proxy forwards solver calls to OpenRouter and enforces the validator-selected
    model, temperature=0.0, top_p=1.0, strips miner-controlled sampling fields (top_k, seed, penalties,
    logit_bias, logprobs) and enforces request/token/cost budgets. Solver Docker containers run with network
    disabled, resource limits, and reach the proxy via a local socket bridge.
sub_competitions:
  structure: tournament
  stages:
  - name: primary_duel
    role: main
    advancement_rule: Challenger vs king over validate_duel_rounds (50) tasks from the primary pool; challenger
      advances iff wins > losses + win_margin (0).
  - name: confirmation_retest
    role: final
    advancement_rule: Challenger must also win a separate 50-task retest pool to be promoted to king;
      otherwise the king is retained.
  count: 2
  notes: 'King-of-the-hill: continuously draining queued PR candidates in queue order; each duel runs
    up to 25 round workers, challenger timeout 600s, and 5 consecutive round timeouts abandon the challenger.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - registration_age
  status_values:
  - king
  - challenger
  - burn
  reset_policy: On promotion, _record_king_transition sets current_king and prepends to recent_kings (capped
    at window=5, burn excluded). On king change the task pool is flushed and the winning PR is auto-merged
    into unarbos/ninja main. A hotkey's submission eligibility resets only on fresh registration.
  notes: recent_kings is a rolling window of the last 5 kings used to distribute weight; the same hotkey
    may appear multiple times if it reclaims the throne. Burn king (uid 0, hotkey 'burn-uid-0') is the
    fallback occupant of unfilled/deregistered slots.

docs.title: Constantinople
docs.text:
Subnet unconst__ConstantinopleFront netuid 97 Archetype pipeline Language none_docs_only Mechanism status unknown Task Miners serve always-on, OpenAI-compatible LLM inference (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct) on decentralized GPUs. Alongside each inference response, a miner commits the model's hidden state at a validator-chosen layer/position so the validator can verify the miner runs the genuine model. Miners are rewarded DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: ReferenceModel, OrganicTraffic"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: ChallengePeriod, CommitReveal, RateLimit"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: throughput"]
  M1["Metric: similarity"]
  M2["Metric: uptime"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  PUB["publish: CommitReveal"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: constantinople

    netuid: 97
    lang: none_docs_only
    status: unknown
    submission: [api_response, commitment_hash]

    @guards {
        ChallengePeriod {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            ReferenceModel {
                trust_model: adversarial
            },
            OrganicTraffic {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: weighted_sum
            normalization: none
        }
        smooth: None {}
        publish: CommitReveal {
            cadence: per_epoch
        }
        tracks {
            structure: none
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: unknown
mechanism_status_notes: Docs-only frontend; no validator code in this repo. The site renders live weights/scores
  from an external status API but contains no scoring logic, so whether the documented mechanism is active,
  burning, or dormant on-chain is not determinable here.
subnet:
  name: Constantinople
  owner_repo: unconst__ConstantinopleFront
  netuid: 97
  implementation_lang: none_docs_only
task:
  summary: Miners serve always-on, OpenAI-compatible LLM inference (Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct) on decentralized
    GPUs. Alongside each inference response, a miner commits the model's hidden state at a validator-chosen
    layer/position so the validator can verify the miner runs the genuine model. Miners are rewarded for
    inference speed, hidden-state verification pass rate, and consistency/uptime.
  submission_format:
  - api_response
  - commitment_hash
scoring_signals:
- name: speed
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.4
  normalization: none
  notes: '"Speed (40%) ... Tokens per second and time to first token. Faster miners earn more." Measured
    wall-clock at the gateway; self-reported metrics are capped.'
- name: verification
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.4
  normalization: none
  notes: '"Verification (40%) ... Hidden state commitment pass rate and cosine similarity." Validator
    recomputes the reference hidden state and compares via cosine similarity (verification passes around
    cosine ~1.0; manifest cites threshold 0.995).'
- name: consistency
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.2
  normalization: none
  notes: '"Consistency (20%) ... Uptime and reliability over time. Miners that maintain steady performance
    with low variance get rewarded."'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: reference_model
  description: The validator runs its own copy of Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, independently computes the
    reference hidden state at the randomly chosen layer/position, and compares it to the miner's commitment
    via cosine similarity. Honest miners on the correct model produce near-identical hidden states (cosine
    ~1.0).
  source_identifier: Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Random layer/position selection plus per-request, per-miner nonce binding (cryptographic hash)
    is described as anti-relay; miners cannot predict what is checked.
- kind: organic_traffic
  description: Speed and consistency are measured from real client inference requests routed through the
    gateway (wall-clock TPS / TTFT, uptime).
  source_identifier: https://api.constantinople.cloud/v1
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Self-reported throughput is clamped against gateway wall-clock measurements.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: none
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn described or implemented in this docs-only repo; not determinable on-chain from this
      tree.
  notes: 'Documented as a three-factor weighted score: Speed 40% + Verification 40% + Consistency 20%.
    Weights stated as percentages in prose; the precise combination formula and any normalization/penalty
    multipliers are not given in source.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Validator sets per-miner weights on-chain based on the three-factor performance score; at each
    tempo boundary Bittensor's Yuma Consensus distributes emissions proportional to weights.
  on_chain_call: commit_reveal
  tempo_or_interval: null
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA/SMA smoothing constant given in source. SKILL.md lists "On-chain weight setting via
      commit-reveal"; StatusPage references a commit_reveal hyperparameter but no numeric tempo is hard-coded
      in this repo.
anti_gaming:
- kind: challenge_period
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Hidden-state commitment protocol: validator demands the hidden state at a random layer
    and position each request; fabricated/cached/wrong-model states yield low cosine similarity and "trigger
    immediate score penalties."'
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Each commitment is bound to a per-request, per-miner nonce via cryptographic hash to prevent
    relay/proxy attacks (forwarding to another miner's hardware or an external API is caught by nonce
    verification).
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: '"TPS inflation -- Self-reported metrics are validated against wall-clock measurements
    at the gateway. Inflated numbers are automatically clamped."'
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Commitment evasion (dropping/ignoring commitment requests) is tracked; high miss rates
    draw "progressive weight penalties that compound." Intermittent availability lowers consistency score
    over time. Penalties are described as "layered, compounding."
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: ''
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: ''
  notes: Docs imply per-miner tracking over time (pass rate, miss rate, consistency/variance, uptime;
    StatusPage shows per-UID avg_cosine, passed/failed challenges, reliability, weight). No explicit state
    machine or reset policy is documented in source.
documentation:
  status: docs_only
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - src/components/MinerDocsPage.tsx
  - public/SKILL.md
  - public/.well-known/inference.json
  - src/components/DocsPage.tsx
  completeness: low
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8
  extracted_at: '2026-06-06'
  confidence_overall: low
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: public/.well-known/inference.json
    line_ref: '5'
    quote: '"subnet": 97,'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: package.json
    line_ref: '1'
    quote: React/Vite/TypeScript frontend landing site; no validator or scoring code present in repo
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: public/SKILL.md
    line_ref: 124-136
    quote: Decentralized GPU Miners (serve inference + hidden states) -> Audit Validator (samples requests,
      verifies hidden states) -> Scoring Engine (speed x verification x consistency)
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format
    source_path: src/components/MinerDocsPage.tsx
    line_ref: 63-67
    quote: the validator tells the miner to commit the hidden state at a randomly selected layer and position.
      The miner returns this commitment alongside the inference response.
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: src/components/MinerDocsPage.tsx
    line_ref: 110-115
    quote: Speed (40%) ... Tokens per second and time to first token. Faster miners earn more. We measure
      wall-clock speed from the gateway -- self-reported metrics are capped
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/weight
    source_path: src/components/MinerDocsPage.tsx
    line_ref: 116-120
    quote: Verification (40%) ... Hidden state commitment pass rate and cosine similarity. Miners running
      the correct model honestly will consistently pass at cosine ~1.0.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/weight
    source_path: src/components/MinerDocsPage.tsx
    line_ref: 121-125
    quote: Consistency (20%) Uptime and reliability over time. Miners that maintain steady performance
      with low variance get rewarded.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/kind
    source_path: src/components/MinerDocsPage.tsx
    line_ref: 68-74
    quote: The validator then independently computes the reference hidden state using its own copy of
      the model and compares it against the miner's commitment via cosine similarity.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /ground_truth_sources/0/source_identifier
    source_path: public/.well-known/inference.json
    line_ref: 9-11
    quote: '"name": "Qwen/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct", ... "verification": "hidden_state_cosine_similarity_0.995"'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /aggregation/method
    source_path: src/components/MinerDocsPage.tsx
    line_ref: 107-108
    quote: 'Your weight is determined by three factors:'
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: src/components/MinerDocsPage.tsx
    line_ref: 101-106
    quote: The validator sets weights on-chain for each miner based on their performance. At each tempo
      boundary, Bittensor's Yuma Consensus distributes emissions proportional to these weights.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: public/SKILL.md
    line_ref: '132'
    quote: On-chain weight setting via commit-reveal
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: src/components/MinerDocsPage.tsx
    line_ref: 212-215
    quote: Fake hidden states -- Returning fabricated or cached hidden states from a different model will
      produce low cosine similarity and trigger immediate score penalties.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: src/components/MinerDocsPage.tsx
    line_ref: 75-78
    quote: Each commitment is also bound to a per-request, per-miner nonce via cryptographic hash. This
      prevents relay attacks where a miner forwards requests to someone else's hardware.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: src/components/MinerDocsPage.tsx
    line_ref: 234-235
    quote: TPS inflation -- Self-reported metrics are validated against wall-clock measurements at the
      gateway. Inflated numbers are automatically clamped.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/3/description
    source_path: src/components/MinerDocsPage.tsx
    line_ref: 227-228
    quote: Commitment evasion -- Dropping or ignoring commitment requests is tracked. Miners with high
      miss rates receive progressive weight penalties that compound with other scores.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/tracked
    source_path: src/components/StatusPage.tsx
    line_ref: '123'
    quote: 'uid: number; avg_cosine: number; passed_challenges: number; failed_challenges: number;'
    confidence: medium
  - claim_field: /documentation/status
    source_path: src/components/MinerDocsPage.tsx
    line_ref: 55-57
    quote: Everything you need to start mining on SN97 -- hardware requirements, setup instructions, and
      how the verification and incentive system works.
    confidence: high
  unresolved:
  - 'Exact aggregation formula: whether the three factors combine additively (weighted_sum) or multiplicatively
    (SKILL.md flow writes ''speed x verification x consistency''); chosen weighted_sum from the percentage
    framing but this is ambiguous.'
  - 'Cosine similarity pass/fail threshold used in scoring: docs say ''cosine ~1.0'' and manifest string
    ''0.995''; no scoring-code constant to confirm it is a hard threshold vs a continuous score input.'
  - Per-signal normalization, any penalty multipliers, and how compounding penalties are numerically applied
    -- not given in source.
  - weight_setting cadence/tempo (blocks), commit-reveal interval, and any EMA/SMA smoothing alpha --
    not hard-coded in this repo (StatusPage reads them from a live API).
  - mechanism_status (active vs burn vs dormant) -- no on-chain weight-setting code in this frontend repo
    to determine operative reality.
  - Burn allocation existence/fraction -- not mentioned in docs and no code to confirm.
  extractor_notes: Repo is the Constantinople (SN97) marketing site, not the subnet implementation. Actual
    validator/miner source is referenced as github.com/unconst/Constantinople (not in this tree). All
    scoring weights (40/40/20) and the cosine reference come from human-readable docs, not code; numeric
    thresholds beyond those literal percentages and the '0.995' manifest string were left unresolved rather
    than guessed.
composition:
  shape: pipeline
  overlays:
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 0

docs.title: FlameWire
docs.text:
Subnet unitone-labs__FlameWire netuid 97 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status active Task Miners operate decentralized blockchain RPC/archive nodes (currently full-archive Bittensor nodes) across regions (us/eu/as). Validators probe each node's data correctness (events-storage hash match against a validator-controlled reference archive), uptime (passed health checks / total), and latency DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: HardwareProbe"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, LivenessCheck, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: accuracy"]
  M1["Metric: uptime"]
  M2["Metric: latency"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: flameWire

    netuid: 97
    lang: python
    status: active
    submission: compute_service

    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, cumulative_score }

    Multiplex<multi_asset> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: accuracy
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: uptime
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: min_max
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: HardwareProbe {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            composition: hybrid
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.1
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_round
            tempo: "verification_interval=480 seconds (default)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_asset
            count: 3
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: active
mechanism_status_notes: Validator computes performance scores (correctness/uptime/latency), smooths them
  with EMA, and sets chain weights pro-rata to miner scores after each verification cycle. No burn/recycle
  logic is present in the validator code; only the README mentions a "default buyback-and-burn of ALPHA"
  as economic policy, not implemented in the weight-setting path.
subnet:
  name: FlameWire
  owner_repo: unitone-labs__FlameWire
  netuid: 97
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners operate decentralized blockchain RPC/archive nodes (currently full-archive Bittensor
    nodes) across regions (us/eu/as). Validators probe each node's data correctness (events-storage hash
    match against a validator-controlled reference archive), uptime (passed health checks / total), and
    latency. Miners are rewarded for serving correct, available, low-latency archive RPC data, with regional
    diversity and balancing factors applied.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: correctness
  metric_kind: accuracy
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.4
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Binary hard gate: 1.0 if node.data_verified (events-storage sha256 matches the reference block
    hash for all sampled blocks), else 0.0. If correctness is 0 the whole node score is forced to 0. DEFAULT_METRIC_WEIGHTS[''correctness'']=0.4.'
- name: uptime
  metric_kind: uptime
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: none
  notes: node.health.success_rate() = passed health checks / total checks, accumulated into validator-local
    cumulative history across cycles. DEFAULT_METRIC_WEIGHTS['uptime']=0.3.
- name: latency
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: min_max
  notes: 'Relative latency score in [0,1]: fastest node 1.0, slowest 0.0, linear in-between via (max-latency)/(max-min).
    DEFAULT_METRIC_WEIGHTS[''latency'']=0.3. Lower raw latency yields a higher score (direction recorded
    on the derived score).'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: Validator-controlled archive RPC endpoint (REFERENCE_RPC_URL) supplies reference blocks.
    For three random blocks (old/middle/new intervals) the validator fetches the system events storage
    and compares the miner node's sha256 events hash against the reference; also checks system_health
    (isSyncing) and chain_getHeader (head >= network_head) for liveness.
  source_identifier: REFERENCE_RPC_URL / config.validator.reference_rpc_url
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Truth comes from a validator-operated archive node, not chain consensus directly. Gateway (gateway-dev.flamewire.io)
    brokers node lookups and RPC calls to miners.
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  composition: hybrid
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: false
    address_or_uid: null
    fraction: null
    dynamic: false
    notes: No burn implemented in code. README economic model mentions "default buyback-and-burn of ALPHA
      unless governance decides otherwise" but this is not present in the scoring/weight-setting path.
  notes: 'Per-node total = weighted sum of correctness/uptime/latency (gated to 0 if correctness fails).
    Per miner+region, node scores are sorted descending and combined with diminishing returns (node N
    contributes score*(1/N)). Regional score = base * regional_multiplier, where multiplier = clamp(target_share/actual_share,
    0.5, 2.0) with target_share = 1/3 per region. Raw miner score = sum of regional scores * diversity_bonus
    (1.0/1.1/1.2 for 1/2/3 regions covered). Final weights are pro-rata: raw_scores / sum(raw_scores).
    The 1/N diminishing-returns base and the diversity-bonus multipliers are not free-text decay constants
    and are captured in extensions.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_round
  method: After each full verification cycle, EMA-smoothed scores are written via update_scores, then
    set_weights() sets weights = scores / (scores.sum() + 1e-8) over all UIDs. Triggered immediately post-cycle
    (should_set_weights() returns False; weights set explicitly in verify()), not on a fixed epoch tempo.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: verification_interval=480 seconds (default)
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.1
    window: null
    notes: 'apply_reward_ema: smoothed = (1-alpha)*previous + alpha*reward, applied only for active (verified)
      miners; non-active UIDs zeroed immediately. DEFAULT_VALIDATOR_EMA_ALPHA=0.1, overridable via VALIDATOR_EMA_ALPHA
      / --validator.ema_alpha.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Data correctness verified by comparing the miner node's sha256 of system-events storage
    against the validator's reference archive block hash. Any mismatch sets data_verified=False; failed
    correctness forces node score to 0 (zero-tolerance gate).
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Health probe per node: system_health must report not syncing and chain_getHeader head
    must be >= validator network_head. Failures reduce uptime success_rate. Miners with no active/verified
    nodes this cycle have their EMA score zeroed immediately.'
- kind: other
  kind_other: regional_rebalancing
  enforcement: barrier
  enforcement_other: rebalancing
  description: Regional multiplier clamp(target_share/actual_share, 0.5, 2.0) discourages crowding a single
    region; diversity bonus (up to 1.2x) rewards covering multiple regions; per-region diminishing returns
    (1/N) limit gains from stacking many nodes in one region.
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_asset
  stages: []
  count: 3
  notes: Scoring is partitioned across three fixed regions (SUPPORTED_REGIONS = us, eu, as), each with
    its own multiplier and diminishing-returns aggregation before summation. Not a tournament/ladder;
    regions act as parallel scoring buckets per miner.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - cumulative_score
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: Validator-local node health (CheckStats total/passed per node_id) is persisted to node_health_stats.json
    and accumulated across cycles. EMA scores persisted to scores.npy. ema_initialized_uids tracked and
    persisted. Scores zeroed when a hotkey is replaced in the metagraph or when a miner has no active
    nodes in a cycle.
  notes: Cumulative local uptime history (_merge_local_health) feeds uptime scoring; EMA state carries
    previous reward forward per active UID.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: SN97
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: flamewire/utils/config.py
    line_ref: '154'
    quote: parser.add_argument("--netuid", type=int, help="Subnet netuid", default=97)
  - claim_field: /subnet/name
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '2'
    quote: '# SN97 - FlameWire: Decentralized Infrastructure'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md

    quote: 'Validators on FlameWire: - Probe miners for uptime, correctness, and latency - Aggregate scores

  - claim_field: /task/submission_format

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: flamewire/utils/scoring.py
    line_ref: 12-16
    quote: 'DEFAULT_METRIC_WEIGHTS = {"correctness": 0.4, "uptime": 0.3, "latency": 0.3,}'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes

docs.title: SN106
docs.text:
Subnet v0idai__SN106 netuid 106 Archetype pipeline Language typescript Mechanism status unknown Task Miners provide concentrated liquidity to wAlpha/wTAO trading pairs on Solana (Raydium CLMM), receive a liquidity-position NFT, and stake that NFT to the SN106 program on Solana. The validator reads each staked NFT position (tickLower/tickUpper/liquidity) and the pool's current tick from chain, then DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: MarketData"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: other"]
  M1["Metric: other"]
  M2["Metric: other"]
  AGG["aggregate: Proportional"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  AGG --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: sn106

    netuid: 106
    lang: typescript
    status: unknown
    submission: other

    @burn {
        uid: "0"
        fraction: 1
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
    }
    @state { other }

    Pipeline {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: financial_pnl
                specific: liquidity_range_width
                raw: "liquidity_range_width_penalty"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: financial_pnl
                specific: tick_distance
                raw: "distance_from_pool_current_tick"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: other
                family: financial_pnl
                specific: liquidity_position
                raw: "position_liquidity"
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: MarketData {
            trust_model: trusted
        }
        aggregate: Proportional {
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: other
            tempo: "40 minutes (setTimeout fixed interval)"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: other
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
subnet:
  name: SN106
  owner_repo: v0idai__SN106
  netuid: 106
  implementation_lang: typescript
task:
  summary: 'Miners provide concentrated liquidity to wAlpha/wTAO trading pairs on Solana (Raydium CLMM),
    receive a liquidity-position NFT, and stake that NFT to the SN106 program on Solana. The validator
    reads each staked NFT position (tickLower/tickUpper/liquidity) and the pool''s current tick from chain,
    then scores positions: narrower (more concentrated) ranges, proximity to the current market tick,
    and larger liquidity score higher; out-of-range positions score zero. Emissions are split chain ->
    pool -> NFT position -> miner. NOTE: the active validator entry point (runValidator) currently runs
    in 100% burn mode (all weight to UID 0); the position-scoring emissions path (runValidatorWithEmissions)
    is present but "kept for reference" and not invoked.'
  submission_format:
  - other
  submission_format_other: Staked Solana liquidity-position NFT (Raydium CLMM position) registered to
    the SN106 program; the validator reads on-chain position state rather than a direct miner-to-validator
    submission.
scoring_signals:
- name: range_concentration
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: liquidity_range_width_penalty
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: widthPenalty = 1 / Math.pow(width, 1.2), where width = tickUpper - tickLower. Narrower (more
    concentrated) ranges score higher. Part of the reference emissions path only (runValidatorWithEmissions),
    not the active burn path.
- name: market_proximity
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: distance_from_pool_current_tick
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: centerWeight = 1 / (1 + distanceFromCenter), distanceFromCenter = |center - currentTick|, center
    = (tickLower + tickUpper) / 2. Closer to current tick scores higher. Out-of-range positions (currentTick
    outside [tickLower, tickUpper]) score 0.
- name: liquidity_amount
  metric_kind: other
  metric_kind_other: position_liquidity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Final score = widthPenalty * centerWeight * position.liquidity. Larger liquidity scales the score
    linearly.
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: market_data
  kind_other: ''
  description: 'On-chain Solana state: staked NFT position parameters (tickLower, tickUpper, liquidity)
    decoded from SN106 program stake records, and each pool''s current tick decoded from Raydium CLMM
    pool state.'
  source_identifier: solana_raydium_clmm_pool_state
  trust_model: trusted
aggregation:
  method: proportional
  normalization: sum_to_one
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: 1.0
    notes: The active entry point runValidator() calls burnAllWeightsOnSubtensor, allocating 100% (65535
      u16 units) to UID 0. The reference emissions path (runValidatorWithEmissions) sets burnPercentage
      = 0 (burn disabled), so when that path is used the burn fraction is 0 and 100% goes to miners. As
      shipped, the invoked path is full burn.
  notes: 'Reference path: per-NFT emission = (score / totalScore) * poolReward, where poolReward = poolWeights[pool]
    * totalReward; per-miner weight is the sum of that miner''s NFT emissions, then normalized to sum
    to 1.0. Pool weights use reserved fixed shares (85% to subnet-0 pools, 15% to subnet-106 pools) per
    calculatePoolWeightsWithReservedPools. An EMA smoothing (alpha default 0.3, configurable) is applied
    to eligible miners when CONFIG.VALIDATOR.USE_EMA is set. These apply only to the reference path, not
    the active burn path.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: other
  cadence_other: 'Fixed wall-clock timer: validator re-runs every 40 minutes (scheduleNextRun, setTimeout).
    README text says every 20 minutes; code uses 40. Not tied to chain epoch/tempo.'
  method: u16 largest-remainder scaling to 65535 total, then api.tx.subtensorModule. setWeights(netuid,
    uids, scaled, versionKey) signed by the validator hotkey. versionKey is the current chain block number.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 40 minutes (setTimeout fixed interval)
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  kind_other: ''
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Out-of-range positions get zero score (isInRange = currentTick in [tickLower, tickUpper]);
    invalid/degenerate positions (non-finite tick, liquidity <= 0, width <= 0) also score 0. In setWeights,
    if all non-zero weights are exactly equal it is treated as likely error and all weights are forced
    to zero; empty/invalid weight sets are submitted as all-zero (no uniform fallback).
sub_competitions:
  structure: other
  structure_other: 'Two-tier reward structure: subnet-level allocation (reserved pool shares by subnetId)
    and individual NFT-position competition within each pool. Not a ladder/tournament; positions compete
    continuously within their pool''s reward slice.'
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: 'README: "two-tier reward system: Subnet Performance ... Individual Performance". Reserved shares:
    85% subnet-0 pools, 15% subnet-106 pools.'
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - other
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: EMA state lives in global.__sn106EmaWeights, persisted on module scope across scheduled
    runs in-process; reset on process restart. Only updated for miners with positive (in-range) emissions.
  notes: Exponentially-weighted moving average of per-miner weights (EMA_ALPHA * current + (1-EMA_ALPHA)
    * previous), applied only in the reference emissions path when USE_EMA is enabled. Not a debt/status
    ledger.
  extensions:
    state_kind_detail: ema_weight_smoothing_in_memory
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - docs/TECHNICAL_IMPLEMENTATION.md
  - docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
  - docs/QUICK_REFERENCE.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract / claude-opus-4-8
  extracted_at: '2026-06-05'
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '111'
    quote: NETUID=106
    confidence: high

    source_path: validator/index.ts
    line_ref: 1-16
    quote: import { setWeightsOnSubtensor, burnAllWeightsOnSubtensor } from '../utils/setWeights';
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 5-13
    quote: SN106 is a specialized Bittensor subnet that rewards miners for providing concentrated liquidity
      to wAlpha/wTAO trading pairs on Solana.
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /task/submission_format_other

    quote: Stake your liquidity position NFT to the SN106 smart contract on Solana
    confidence: high

    line_ref: 49-53
