      || []; const subnet106Pools = poolsBySubnet[106] || [];
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: utils/setWeights.ts
    line_ref: '183'
    quote: const tx = api.tx.subtensorModule.setWeights(netuid, uids, scaled, versionKey);
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/cadence_other
    source_path: validator/index.ts
    line_ref: 231-243
    quote: // Fixed interval of 40 minutes (in milliseconds) const intervalMinutes = 40;
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/method
    source_path: utils/setWeights.ts
    line_ref: 165-167
    quote: const versionKey = header.number.toNumber();
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description
    source_path: utils/setWeights.ts
    line_ref: 43-49
    quote: const allEqual = nonZeroWeights.every(w => Math.abs(w - firstWeight) < 1e-10); if (allEqual)
      { ... floatWeights = Array(uids.length).fill(0); }
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/structure_other
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 9-13
    quote: 'SN106 operates on a two-tier reward system: 1. Subnet Performance ... 2. Individual Performance:
      NFT liquidity positions compete within pools'
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/notes
    source_path: validator/index.ts
    line_ref: 50-62
    quote: next[k] = EMA_ALPHA * safeCurr + (1 - EMA_ALPHA) * safePrev;
    confidence: high
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: validator/index.ts
    line_ref: 41-48
    quote: if (!global.__sn106EmaWeights) { global.__sn106EmaWeights = {} as Record<string, number>; }
    confidence: high
  unresolved:
  - '/aggregation/decay_rate: EMA_ALPHA default is 0.3 in validator/index.ts but 0.8 in docs/TECHNICAL_IMPLEMENTATION.md;
    it is config-driven (CONFIG.VALIDATOR.EMA_ALPHA) and only used in the inactive reference path, so
    left null rather than commit to a constant.'
  - 'scoring_signals/weight: individual signal weights are not numeric coefficients; score is a multiplicative
    composite (widthPenalty * centerWeight * liquidity) with no per-signal weight, so weights left null.'
  - 'min_weight_floor: no explicit floor; EMA_EPSILON thresholds tiny weights to 0 but is a config value
    with no literal in source.'
  - 'reserved-share constants: poolWeights default signature uses 0.85/0.15 (matching index.ts call),
    but the function default header and docs cite 0.25; only the index.ts call-site values (0.85 subnet-0,
    0.15 subnet-106) are the actually-passed constants.'
  - 'Active-vs-reference ambiguity: runValidator (full burn) is the invoked entry point; runValidatorWithEmissions
    (position scoring) is shipped but never called. Whether burn is a temporary launch state or permanent
    is not stated in source.'
  extractor_notes: 'TypeScript/blockchain-centric subnet that does not fit the standard bittensor reward-template
    vocabulary. Miners do not submit models/code to validators; they provide Raydium CLMM concentrated
    liquidity on Solana and stake position NFTs, which the validator reads from chain. The scoring is
    a DeFi market-making quality function (concentration + price proximity + liquidity), unlike loss/accuracy
    tasks. Two control-flow paths exist: full-burn (active) and position-emissions (reference). I recorded
    the active burn path in burn_allocation while capturing the reference scoring mechanism in scoring_signals/aggregation,
    flagging the ambiguity in unresolved. No config/ constants file was found by locate.py; numeric constants
    come from call sites and function-default parameters, which conflict with the docs in several places
    (EMA alpha, reserved shares, 20- vs 40-minute interval).'
composition:
  shape: pipeline
  overlays:
  - burn
  - guards
  - state
  extern_count: 3

docs.title: Verathos
docs.text:
Subnet verathos-ai__verathos netuid 96 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners serve LLM inference (vLLM) with ZK-inspired sumcheck proofs over Merkle-committed weights, registered on a Bittensor EVM MinerRegistry. Each epoch (~72 min / 360 blocks) the validator sends canary tests through the same /chat endpoint as organic traffic, optionally verifies proofs, and collec DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: CommitReveal, OrganicTraffic, HardwareProbe"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: ProofOfWork, CommitReveal, DeterministicCheck"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: composite"]
  M1["Metric: throughput"]
  M2["Metric: latency"]
  M3["Metric: throughput"]
  M4["Metric: volume"]
  M5["Metric: composite"]
  M6["Metric: composite"]
  AGG["aggregate: WeightedAverage"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  G --> M4
  GT --> M4
  G --> M5
  GT --> M5
  G --> M6
  GT --> M6
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  M4 --> AGG
  M5 --> AGG
  M6 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT
  ST --> AGG
  classDef in fill:#e6f0ff,stroke:#5b8;
  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: verathos

    netuid: 96
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: compute_service

    @burn {
        uid: "subnet owner UID (_burn_uid, resolved at startup)"
        fraction: 0.5
    }
    @guards {
        ProofOfWork {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        CommitReveal {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        HardwareValidation {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
        LivenessCheck {
            enforcement: elimination
        }
    }
    @state { performance_ledger, status_enum, cooldown }

    Multiplex<multi_asset> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: latency
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: volume
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            },
            Metric {
                kind: composite
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: none
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            CommitReveal {
                trust_model: adversarial
            },
            OrganicTraffic {
                trust_model: adversarial
            },
            HardwareProbe {
                trust_model: adversarial
            }
        ]
        aggregate: WeightedAverage {
            composition: multiplicative
            normalization: sum_to_one
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.2
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: per_epoch
            tempo: "360 blocks"
        }
        tracks {
            structure: multi_asset
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Scoring is live (utility × throughput² × ttft × speed × demand × tee), but a default
  50% emission burn (emission_burn_bps=5000) redirects half of all weight to the subnet owner (burn) UID
  before set_weights. The burn fraction is read from on-chain SubnetConfig.ScoringParams at runtime, so
  it can change.
subnet:
  name: Verathos
  owner_repo: verathos-ai__verathos
  netuid: 96
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners serve LLM inference (vLLM) with ZK-inspired sumcheck proofs over Merkle-committed weights,
    registered on a Bittensor EVM MinerRegistry. Each epoch (~72 min / 360 blocks) the validator sends
    canary tests through the same /chat endpoint as organic traffic, optionally verifies proofs, and collects
    signed service receipts. Miners are scored per (miner, model) entry on model utility, weighted-token
    throughput (squared), peer-relative latency (TTFT and decode speed), an organic-demand bonus, and
    a TEE bonus; the entry scores are EMA-smoothed and summed additively across a miner's models.
  submission_format:
  - compute_service
scoring_signals:
- name: utility
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: base_utility = log2(active_params_b)^1.8 × log2(ctx/1024) × quant_quality × generation_quality.
    Multiplicative base factor of the entry score (scoring.py compute_epoch_entry_score). quant_quality
    default 0.80 when quant unknown; QUANT_QUALITY maps bf16/fp16=1.0 ... int4/nf4=0.90.
- name: throughput
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: throughput_score = (total_weighted_tokens / 1_000_000) ** throughput_power (default power 2.0).
    Output tokens weighted 3× input. Squaring is explicit sybil defense. Per-receipt output caps (canary
    300, organic 4096) and a per-miner canary budget bound inflation.
- name: ttft_factor
  metric_kind: latency
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: min(1.3, sqrt(model_median_ttft / miner_median_ttft)) — peer-relative, soft-capped at 1.3. Multiplicative
    factor (compute_ttft_factor).
- name: speed_factor
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: min(1.3, sqrt(miner_median_tps / model_median_tps)) — peer-relative decode speed, soft-capped
    at 1.3 (compute_speed_factor). Combined ttft×speed uplift bounded at 1.69×.
- name: demand_bonus
  metric_kind: volume
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: 1.0 + (demand_bps/10000) × demand_bonus_max (default max 0.20). Per-model organic demand = sqrt(request_count
    × generated_tokens), normalized to 0-10000 bps (compute_model_demand / compute_demand_bonus).
- name: tee_bonus
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Multiplier tee_bonus (default 1.10 = 1 + tee_bonus_bps/10000) applied when the entry has at least
    one verified TEE attestation this epoch; else 1.0.
- name: proof_integrity
  metric_kind: composite
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: null
  normalization: none
  notes: Gate, not additive. Any proof verification failure, TEE attestation failure, or own-receipt integrity
    failure forces the epoch score to 0.0 (hard penalty, decays EMA). No receipts at all -> None (EMA
    unchanged).
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: commit_reveal
  description: Sumcheck-based ZK-inspired proofs over Merkle-committed quantized model weights anchored
    on-chain; SHA-256 output commitment bound via Fiat-Shamir. Validator verifies proofs on CPU. Canary
    prompts are deterministic (seeded on epoch, miner_address, test_index).
  source_identifier: neurons/canary.py + verallm sumcheck proof system
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: 'README: ''Correct computation: Sumcheck protocol ... Output integrity: SHA-256 commitment over
    full output, bound to the proof via Fiat-Shamir.'''
- kind: organic_traffic
  description: Real (non-canary) inference receipts from gateway/proxy traffic feed the throughput, demand,
    and latency signals alongside canaries.
  source_identifier: ServiceReceipt (neurons/receipts.py)
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: Organic receipts capped per-receipt (4096 output tokens) to bound gaming.
- kind: hardware_probe
  description: Optional TEE attestation (TDX / SEV-SNP) verified against on-chain codeMeasurement allowlist;
    grants the TEE bonus.
  source_identifier: _verify_code_measurement (neurons/validator.py)
  trust_model: adversarial
  notes: TEE verification gated by subnet feature flag is_tee_enabled_on_subnet.
aggregation:
  method: weighted_average
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: subnet owner UID (_burn_uid, resolved at startup)
    fraction: 0.5
    dynamic: false
    notes: 'emission_burn (default emission_burn_bps=5000 -> 0.50) read from on-chain SubnetConfig.ScoringParams.
      At weight-setting: weights[uid] *= (1-burn), weights[burn_uid] += burn. Fraction is a fixed on-chain
      param (can change).'
  notes: Per-entry epoch score = product of all signal factors; entry EMA (alpha 0.2) summed additively
    across a miner's model entries (aggregate_score); per-UID raw scores normalized to sum to one (get_weights).
    Within an entry, signals COMBINE multiplicatively; across entries they sum.
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: Every set_weights_epoch_blocks (default 360, = epoch). get_weights() returns normalized per-UID
    weights; blacklisted UIDs zeroed and re-normalized; emission burn applied; scaled to uint16 (v/max*65535)
    and submitted via subtensor.set_weights with version_key=spec_version.
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 360 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.2
    window: null
    notes: 'Per-entry EMA: ema = alpha*epoch_score + (1-alpha)*ema, alpha default 0.2 (ema_alpha / ema_alpha_bps=2000).
      First scored epoch seeds ema directly.'
anti_gaming:
- kind: proof_of_work
  enforcement: penalty
  description: ZK sumcheck proof verification on a sampled fraction of canaries (canary_proof_sample_rate
    default 0.30). Any failed proof -> epoch score 0.0 and probation entry.
- kind: commit_reveal
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Output integrity via SHA-256 commitment bound to proof (Fiat-Shamir); input commitment
    recomputed validator-side from tokenizer; receipt-set hash for tamper evidence across validators.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Receipt integrity: if found own_receipts < expected-1 (and >=5 expected) -> score 0.0.
    Canaries indistinguishable from organic traffic; seeds hash (epoch, miner_address, test_index) so
    all validators issue identical prompts.'
- kind: hardware_validation
  enforcement: rejection
  description: Identity challenge filters hijacked endpoints (excluded from epoch + reportOffline). TEE
    attestation cross-checked against on-chain codeMeasurement allowlist.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'throughput squared (power 2.0): N sybil UIDs splitting fixed demand each get (1/N)^2,
    total 1/N of honest. Per-receipt output caps (canary 300, organic 4096) and a per-miner canary token
    budget bound max_tokens inflation.'
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: elimination
  description: 'Probation after proof failure: forced 0.0 score (EMA decays) until 3 consecutive clean
    epochs; EMA halved per failure; after 5 epochs on probation, reportOffline escalation.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: multi_asset
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Per (miner, model) entry scored independently (additive across a miner's models). Peer comparison
    is per-model (model-level median of miner medians). Not a formal staged/tournament structure.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - performance_ledger
  - status_enum
  - cooldown
  status_values:
  - normal
  - probation
  reset_policy: Probation lifted after probation_required_passes (default 3) consecutive clean epochs;
    any failure resets the pass counter. EMA persisted in SQLite validator DB; probation persisted to
    /tmp/verathos_probation.json.
  notes: Per-entry EMA (ModelEntryScore), ProbationTracker keyed on (miner_address, model_index), busy-skip
    / canary-error counters reset each epoch.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: Subnet 96 (README)
  primary_docs:
  - README.md
  - neurons/scoring.py module docstring
  - https://verathos.ai/docs
  completeness: high
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: high
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 646-652
    quote: 'emission_burn = self._scoring.emission_burn; if emission_burn > 0: for uid ...: weights[uid]
      *= (1.0 - emission_burn); weights[self._burn_uid] = ... + emission_burn; bt.logging.info(f"Emission
      burn: {emission_burn:.0%} to UID {self._burn_uid}")'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction
    source_path: verallm/chain/types.py
    line_ref: '88'
    quote: 'emission_burn_bps: int = 5000            # 50%'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 546-557
    quote: 'def _resolve_burn_uid(self) -> int: ... Resolve subnet owner hotkey to UID on this subnet
      (= burn target). ... self._burn_uid = self._resolve_burn_uid()'
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: '15'
    quote: Verathos is a decentralized compute network on Bittensor (Subnet 96)
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: neurons/scoring.py
    line_ref: 52-66
    quote: 'from __future__ import annotations

      import hashlib

      import math

      ... import bittensor as bt'
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: neurons/scoring.py
    line_ref: 1-12
    quote: 'Epoch-based composite scoring for the Verathos Bittensor subnet. Weight composition: WEIGHT(uid)
      = normalize( AGGREGATE ); AGGREGATE = Σ entry_ema(model_index)'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: neurons/scoring.py
    line_ref: 329-336
    quote: utility = math.log2(max(quality_params, 1.0)) ** 1.8; ctx_value = math.log2(max(outcome.max_context_len
      / 1024, 1)); quant_q = QUANT_QUALITY.get(outcome.quant, 0.80); base_utility = utility * ctx_value
      * quant_q * gen_q
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: neurons/scoring.py
    line_ref: '346'
    quote: throughput_score = (total_tokens / 1_000_000) ** throughput_power
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: neurons/scoring.py
    line_ref: '181'
    quote: return min(1.3, math.sqrt(model_median_ttft_ms / miner_median_ttft_ms))
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/3/notes
    source_path: neurons/scoring.py
    line_ref: '206'
    quote: return min(1.3, math.sqrt(miner_median_tps / model_median_tps))
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/4/notes
    source_path: neurons/scoring.py
    line_ref: 464-465
    quote: clamped = max(0, min(demand_score_bps, 10000)); return 1.0 + (clamped / 10000) * demand_bonus_max
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/5/notes
    source_path: neurons/scoring.py
    line_ref: 369-371
    quote: tee_multiplier = tee_bonus if outcome.tee_verified else 1.0; return base_utility * throughput_score
      * ttft_factor * speed_factor * demand_bonus * tee_multiplier
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/6/notes
    source_path: neurons/scoring.py
    line_ref: 255-267
    quote: 'if outcome.proof_tests > 0 and outcome.proof_failures > 0: ... return 0.0 ... if not outcome.all_receipts:
      return None'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/composition
    source_path: neurons/scoring.py
    line_ref: 144-147
    quote: 'def aggregate_score(self) -> float: \"\"\"Sum of all entry EMAs — additive multi-model aggregation.\"\"\"
      return sum(e.ema_score for e in self.entries.values())'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization
    source_path: neurons/scoring.py
    line_ref: 661-664
    quote: 'total = sum(raw.values()); if total <= 0: return {uid: 0.0 for uid in raw}; return {uid: s
      / total for uid, s in raw.items()}'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/alpha
    source_path: neurons/config.py

    quote: 'ema_alpha: float = 0.2  # EMA smoothing for scores'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/smoothing/notes
    source_path: neurons/scoring.py

    quote: entry.ema_score = (self.ema_alpha * epoch_score + (1 - self.ema_alpha) * entry.ema_score)
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/tempo_or_interval
    source_path: neurons/config.py
    line_ref: 28-30
    quote: 'epoch_blocks: int = 360  # ~72 min at 12s/block ... set_weights_epoch_blocks: int = 360  #
      weight-setting interval (= epoch by default)'
  - claim_field: /weight_setting/on_chain_call
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 2874-2880
    quote: self._subtensor.set_weights(wallet=self._wallet, netuid=self.config.netuid, uids=uid_tensor,

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/1/notes
    source_path: neurons/config.py
    line_ref: '25'
    quote: 'throughput_power: float = 2.0  # superlinear exponent (sybil resistance)'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/5/notes
    source_path: verallm/chain/types.py

  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/0/description

    line_ref: '35'
    quote: 'canary_proof_sample_rate: float = 0.30  # probability of ZK proof verification on small canaries'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: neurons/scoring.py
    line_ref: 246-249

  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/4/description
    source_path: neurons/scoring.py

    quote: 'N sybil UIDs splitting fixed demand: each gets 1/N tokens, score per UID = (1/N)², total =

  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/5/description
    source_path: neurons/scoring.py

    source_path: neurons/scoring.py

      measurement not in on-chain allowlist")'

  - Exact live emission_burn value on-chain (default 50%, but read from SubnetConfig at runtime; may differ).
  - Live netuid at runtime (config default 0; README states Subnet 96 — assumed authoritative).

composition:

docs.title: Vidaio Subnet
docs.text:
Subnet vidaio-subnet__vidaio-subnet netuid 85 Archetype multiplex Language python Mechanism status partial_burn Task Miners perform AI-driven video processing in two task types: upscaling (enhance a downscaled video back to high resolution) and compression (reduce file size while maintaining quality above a VMAF threshold). Validators send synthetic and organic video challenges; miners return a processed-video URL DataflowIMMLIR (YAML) 
flowchart TD
  SUB(["submission"]):::in
  GT(["groundTruth: ReferenceModel, OrganicTraffic"]):::in
  G{{"@guards: DeterministicCheck, RateLimit, Other"}}:::ov
  M0["Metric: similarity"]
  M1["Metric: throughput"]
  M2["Metric: similarity"]
  M3["Metric: similarity"]
  AGG["aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma"]
  SM["smooth: Ema"]
  BURN{{"@burn: redirect a fraction"}}:::ov
  PUB["publish: SetWeights"]
  OUT(["weights on-chain = final score"]):::out
  ST(["@state: carried across rounds"]):::note
  SUB --> G
  G --> M0
  GT --> M0
  G --> M1
  GT --> M1
  G --> M2
  GT --> M2
  G --> M3
  GT --> M3
  M0 --> AGG
  M1 --> AGG
  M2 --> AGG
  M3 --> AGG
  AGG --> SM
  SM --> BURN
  BURN --> PUB
  PUB --> OUT

  classDef out fill:#e6ffe6,stroke:#3a3;
  classDef ov fill:#fff3d6,stroke:#caa;
  classDef note fill:#f3f3f3,stroke:#bbb;
Mechanism {
    id: vidaioSubnet

    netuid: 85
    lang: python
    status: partial_burn
    submission: content_link

    @burn {
        uid: "subnet owner UID (SubnetOwnerHotkey -> get_burn_uid)"
        fraction: 0.8
    }
    @guards {
        DeterministicCheck {
            enforcement: rejection
        }
        RateLimit {
            enforcement: barrier
        }
        Other {
            enforcement: penalty
        }
    }
    @state { cumulative_score, performance_ledger, reputation }

    Multiplex<per_task_type> {
        score: [
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: throughput
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            },
            Metric {
                kind: similarity
                direction: higher_is_better
                normalization: other
            }
        ]
        groundTruth: [
            ReferenceModel {
                trust_model: trusted
            },
            OrganicTraffic {
                trust_model: trusted
            }
        ]
        aggregate: ExponentialDecayEma {
            composition: multiplicative
            normalization: other
            decay_rate: 0.75
        }
        smooth: Ema {
            alpha: 0.25
        }
        publish: SetWeights {
            cadence: rate_limited
            tempo: 100
        }
        tracks {
            structure: per_task_type
            count: 2
        }
    }
}

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: partial_burn
mechanism_status_notes: Validators set real weights derived from accumulated per-miner scores, but burn_proportion
  = 4/5 (80%) of miner emissions is redirected to the subnet owner UID on every weight-set. Only the remaining
  20% is distributed across miners, split by task type and tier. Documented exponential upscaling final-score
  formula (S_F = 0.1 * e^(6.979*(S_pre-0.5))) does not match the implemented log10-based calculate_final_score(),
  so docs and code diverge.
subnet:
  name: Vidaio Subnet
  owner_repo: vidaio-subnet__vidaio-subnet
  netuid: 85
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: 'Miners perform AI-driven video processing in two task types: upscaling (enhance a downscaled
    video back to high resolution) and compression (reduce file size while maintaining quality above a
    VMAF threshold). Validators send synthetic and organic video challenges; miners return a processed-video
    URL. Upscaling is scored on PIE-APP perceptual quality gated by a VMAF threshold plus a content-length
    term; compression is scored on compression ratio plus VMAF margin above threshold.'
  submission_format:
  - content_link
scoring_signals:
- name: pieapp_quality_score_upscaling
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: 'sigmoid then log10-based transform: calculate_final_score maps PIE-APP into a
    0-1 quality score; gated to 0 when VMAF below threshold.'
  notes: QUALITY_WEIGHT = 0.5. S_Q is the PIE-APP final score; set to 0 if vmaf_score/100 < vmaf_threshold
    (default vmaf_threshold 0.5).
- name: content_length_score_upscaling
  metric_kind: throughput
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.5
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: 'logarithmic: S_L = log(1+content_length)/log(1+320) (per docs)'
  notes: LENGTH_WEIGHT = 0.5. Rewards longer processed content length (currently 5s or 10s supported).
- name: compression_rate_score
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.8
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: ((compression_ratio-1.25)/18.75)^0.9 above threshold, with log bonus beyond 20x;
    normalized by factor 1.12. compression_ratio = 1/compression_rate.
  notes: COMPRESSION_RATE_WEIGHT (w_c) = 0.8 in config; calculate_compression_score default compression_weight
    = 0.70. Zeroed when compression_rate >= 0.80 (< 1.25x).
- name: vmaf_quality_margin_compression
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.2
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: 0.7 + 0.3*min(1, vmaf_excess/(100-threshold)) above threshold
  notes: 'COMPRESSION_VMAF_WEIGHT (w_vmaf) = 0.2 in config; default quality_weight = 0.30 in calculate_compression_score.
    Hard cutoff: score 0 if VMAF below threshold-5.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: reference_model
  description: VMAF (harmonic mean over sampled frames) and PIE-APP perceptual-difference metrics compute
    objective quality between the validator's reference video and the miner's processed output. Reference
    video is held by the validator (trim/permuted source).
  source_identifier: services/scoring (vmaf_metric.py, pieapp_metric.py)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: VMAF uses 5 random frames; PIE-APP uses sampled frames. Scoring runs on validator-side scoring
    service over HTTP.
- kind: organic_traffic
  description: Real user-submitted videos routed via the organic gateway are also processed and a subset
    randomly selected for scoring.
  source_identifier: neurons/validator.py score_organics_upscaling / score_organics_compression
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: 1 upscaling pair and up to 5 compression pairs randomly sampled per organic round.
aggregation:
  method: exponential_decay_ema
  composition: multiplicative
  normalization: other
  normalization_other: 'Per-task tier allocation: weights within top-10 and rank 11-50 tiers are score-proportional
    and scaled to fixed allocation shares (comp 0.30/0.30, up 0.20/0.20); then 80% burned and 20% kept,
    ultimately normalized on-chain by process_weights_for_netuid.'
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: 0.75
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: subnet owner UID (SubnetOwnerHotkey -> get_burn_uid)
    fraction: 0.8
    dynamic: false
    notes: self.burn_proportion = float(4/5). scores scaled by (1 - burn_proportion); owner UID appended
      with burn_proportion * total_scores.
  notes: 'accumulate_score is an EMA: accumulate_score = accumulate_score*decay_factor + score_with_multiplier*(1-decay_factor),
    where score_with_multiplier = s_f * total_multiplier and total_multiplier = bonus * penalty_f * penalty_q
    (upscaling) or bonus * penalty_f (compression). decay_factor = 0.75.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: rate_limited
  method: 'WeightSynthesizer loops every 1200s calling set_weights; weights only emitted when current_block
    > last_update + SUBNET_TEMPO (100). Miner weights come from MinerManager.weights: split 60/40-style
    tiered allocation across compression/upscaling top-10 and rank-11-50 tiers, with 80% of emission burned
    to the owner UID.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 100
  smoothing:
    kind: ema
    alpha: 0.25
    window: null
    notes: EMA on accumulate_score with decay_factor 0.75; effective new-sample weight (1 - decay_factor)
      = 0.25.
anti_gaming:
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: 'Compression: score zeroed if compression_rate >= 0.80 (< 1.25x, suspected return of original)
    or if VMAF more than 5 points below threshold. Upscaling: quality score zeroed if VMAF below threshold.'
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: barrier
  description: ServingCounter rate limits per-validator requests by stake-normalized share (build_rate_limit);
    create_miner_batches caps per-miner performance records (max_performance_records=10) and skips recently-updated
    capped miners.
- kind: other
  kind_other: historical_performance_multiplier
  enforcement: penalty
  description: 'Rolling 10-round historical multipliers: bonus up to +15% (BONUS_MAX 0.15), S_F penalty
    up to -20% (PENALTY_F_MAX 0.20), S_Q penalty up to -25% (PENALTY_Q_MAX 0.25) applied multiplicatively
    to the per-round score.'
sub_competitions:
  structure: per_task_type
  stages:
  - name: upscaling
    role: other
    advancement_rule: top_10_and_rank_11_to_50_get_emission
  - name: compression
    role: other
    advancement_rule: top_10_and_rank_11_to_50_get_emission
  count: 2
  notes: Miners declare their task type via TaskWarrantProtocol. Emission allocation splits compression
    (0.30 top / 0.30 mid) and upscaling (0.20 top / 0.20 mid); a missing category's share is redistributed
    to the other.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - cumulative_score
  - performance_ledger
  - reputation
  status_values: []
  reset_policy: On hotkey change for a UID, MinerPerformanceHistory and MinerMetadata rows are deleted
    (refresh_miner_manager). accumulate_score, bonus/penalty multipliers and counts reset to defaults
    on certain step paths.
  notes: SQL-backed MinerMetadata (accumulate_score, bonus_multiplier, penalty_f/q_multiplier, counts,
    total_rounds_completed) and MinerPerformanceHistory (rolling 10-round window, s_f/s_q records) in
    video_subnet_validator.db.
documentation:
  status: docs_and_code
  source_version: null
  primary_docs:
  - docs/incentive_mechanism.md
  - README.md
  completeness: medium
provenance:
  extracted_by: im-extract (Claude Opus 4.8)
  confidence_overall: medium
  evidence:
  - claim_field: /subnet/netuid
    source_path: vidaio_subnet_core/base/config.py
    line_ref: '29'
    quote: parser.add_argument("--netuid", type=int, help="Subnet netuid", default=85)
  - claim_field: /subnet/implementation_lang
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: '9'
    quote: import bittensor as bt
  - claim_field: /task/summary
    source_path: README.md
    line_ref: 46-47
    quote: Handle video upscaling and compression requests from validators and end-users. Process both
      upscaling tasks (enhancing video quality) and compression tasks (reducing file size while maintaining
      quality).
  - claim_field: /mechanism_status
    source_path: vidaio_subnet_core/validating/managing/miner_manager.py
    line_ref: '31'
    quote: 'self.burn_proportion = float(4/5)   # 80% of miner emissions burnt'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/fraction
    source_path: vidaio_subnet_core/validating/managing/miner_manager.py
    line_ref: 1314-1318
    quote: scores = [x * (1 - self.burn_proportion) for x in scores]; uids.append(owner_uid); scores.append(self.burn_proportion
      * total_scores)
  - claim_field: /aggregation/burn_allocation/address_or_uid
    source_path: vidaio_subnet_core/validating/managing/miner_manager.py
    line_ref: 1188-1203
    quote: 'def get_burn_uid(self): sn_owner_hotkey = self.subtensor.query_subtensor( "SubnetOwnerHotkey",
      params=[self.config.netuid]) ... return sn_owner_uid'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/decay_rate
    source_path: vidaio_subnet_core/configs/score.py
    line_ref: '5'
    quote: 'decay_factor: float = Field(default=0.75)'
  - claim_field: /aggregation/notes
    source_path: vidaio_subnet_core/validating/managing/miner_manager.py
    line_ref: 334-341
    quote: applied_multiplier = miner.total_multiplier; score_with_multiplier = s_f * applied_multiplier;
      miner.accumulate_score = (miner.accumulate_score * CONFIG.score.decay_factor + score_with_multiplier
      * (1 - CONFIG.score.decay_factor))
  - claim_field: /aggregation/normalization_other
    source_path: vidaio_subnet_core/validating/managing/miner_manager.py
    line_ref: 1244-1248
    quote: 'alloc_comp_top, alloc_comp_mid = 0.30, 0.30; alloc_up_top, alloc_up_mid = 0.20, 0.20; TOP_N
      = 10  # boundary between "top" and "mid" tiers'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/weight
    source_path: vidaio_subnet_core/validating/managing/miner_manager.py

  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/weight
    source_path: vidaio_subnet_core/validating/managing/miner_manager.py
    line_ref: 81-82
    quote: 'self.COMPRESSION_RATE_WEIGHT = 0.8  # w_c; self.COMPRESSION_VMAF_WEIGHT = 0.2  # w_vmaf'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/0/notes
    source_path: services/scoring/video_similarity_score_sythetic.py
    line_ref: 360-364
    quote: 'if vmaf_score / 100 < VMAF_THRESHOLD: ... pieapp_scores.append(0.0); reasons.append(f"vmaf
      score is too low ...") scores.append(0.0)'
  - claim_field: /scoring_signals/2/notes
    source_path: services/scoring/scoring_function.py

  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/description
    source_path: vidaio_subnet_core/validating/managing/miner_manager.py
    line_ref: 63-69

  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/2/enforcement

    source_path: neurons/validator.py

  - claim_field: /sub_competitions/structure
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 295-298
    quote: 'if response.warrant_task == TaskType.UPSCALING: upscaling_miners.append((axon, uid)); elif
      response.warrant_task == TaskType.COMPRESSION: compression_miners.append((axon, uid))'
  - claim_field: /per_miner_state/reset_policy
    source_path: neurons/validator.py
    line_ref: 239-253
    quote: 'if miner.hotkey != latest_hotkey: delete_uids.append(uid) ... delete_stmt = delete(MinerPerformanceHistory).where(MinerPerformanceHistory.uid.in_(delete_uids))'
  - claim_field: /anti_gaming/1/description
    source_path: vidaio_subnet_core/configs/score.py

    source_path: docs/incentive_mechanism.md

  unresolved:
  - Documented upscaling final-score S_F = 0.1*e^(6.979*(S_pre-0.5)) is not present in code; the implemented

    scoring service source read.
  - vmaf_threshold default in config is 0.5 (fraction) but docs/compression use 85/89/93; the live threshold

composition:

schema_version: 1.2.0
instance_kind: extracted
mechanism_status: mixed
mechanism_status_notes: Full scoring pipeline is implemented (GPT-4o-audio trait extraction, weighted
  element scoring, binary pass-threshold win-rate, stake-weighted global consensus, winner-take-all weight
  setting). However the validator burns (weight 1.0 on UID 0) whenever there are too few active validators,
  too few readable buckets, no usable cross-validator data, or no eligible miner clears THRESHOLD_MARGIN
  — so the live network frequently burns while the documented scoring is dormant.
subnet:
  name: Vocence
  owner_repo: vocence-78__vocence
  netuid: 78
  implementation_lang: python
task:
  summary: Miners deploy PromptTTS voice-intelligence models on Chutes exposing a single /speak endpoint
    (text + voice-trait instruction -> WAV). Validators derive a task spec from real source audio via
    GPT-4o-audio, call each miner's /speak, and score the generated audio on content (WER), audio naturalness
    (pairwise judge), and prompt/voice-trait adherence (gender, pitch, speed, age_group, emotion, tone,
    accent). Rewards go winner-take-all to the single globally-best miner.
  submission_format:
  - api_response
  - model_weights
scoring_signals:
- name: script
  metric_kind: similarity
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.3
  normalization: none
  notes: 1 - WER(spec.transcription, miner.transcription), clamped to [0,1]. WER is Levenshtein distance
    over lowercase word tokens. Highest-weighted element.
- name: naturalness
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.15
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Pairwise GPT-4o-audio judge: source vs miner audio, presentation order randomized; 1.0 if miner
    judged more natural else 0.0.'
- name: gender
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Exact enum match (male/female/neutral): 1.0 if equal else 0.0.'
- name: speed
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Ordinal enum (slow/normal/fast): exact=1.0, off-by-one=0.5, else 0.0.'
- name: emotion
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Exact enum match over 8 emotion values: 1.0 if equal else 0.0.'
- name: age_group
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.1
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Ordinal enum (child/young_adult/adult/senior): exact=1.0, off-by-one=0.5, else 0.0.'
- name: pitch
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.05
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Ordinal enum (low/mid/high): exact=1.0, off-by-one=0.5, else 0.0.'
- name: accent
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.05
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Exact enum match (us/uk/au/in/neutral/other): 1.0 if equal else 0.0.'
- name: tone
  metric_kind: llm_judgment
  direction: higher_is_better
  weight: 0.05
  normalization: none
  notes: 'Exact enum match over 6 tone values: 1.0 if equal else 0.0.'
ground_truth_sources:
- kind: llm_judgment
  description: GPT-4o-audio-preview (or GPT_AUDIO_MODEL) used pointwise to extract the source task spec
    and the miner-audio traits, and pairwise to judge naturalness. Temperature 0.0 for both calls.
  source_identifier: gpt-4o-audio-preview
  trust_model: oracle
  notes: The "spec" (transcription + 7 voice traits) is derived from real source audio, so the judge is
    also the ground-truth labeler for each evaluation.
- kind: deterministic_dataset
  description: Source audio clips pulled from the shared Hippius corpus bucket (LibriVox-sourced, 20-25s
    clips) used to derive each task spec.
  source_identifier: audio-corpus-bucket (AUDIO_SOURCE_BUCKET)
  trust_model: trusted
  notes: Validator selects a random .wav avoiding recently-used items; duration-gated 20-25s.
aggregation:
  method: winner_take_all
  composition: weighted_sum
  normalization: sum_to_one
  temperature: null
  decay_rate: null
  min_weight_floor: null
  burn_allocation:
    enabled: true
    address_or_uid: '0'
    fraction: null
    dynamic: true
    notes: When no miner is eligible / no consensus, validator sets weight 1.0 on burn UID 0. BURN_UID
      = 0, OWNER_UID = 0. Burn is a full-cycle residual, not a fixed fraction.
  notes: 'Per-evaluation score = sum(element_weight * element_score) / sum(weights), in [0,1]; raw element
    weights are renormalized to sum to 1.0. Each evaluation becomes a binary win (generated_wins) when
    score >= PASS_THRESHOLD (0.9); win_rate = wins/total is the ranking primitive (continuous score is
    diagnostic only). Per-validator win_rates are combined into a global stake-weighted mean: sum(sqrt(stake_v)*win_rate_v)/sum(sqrt(stake_v)),
    falling back to equal weights if all stakes are zero. The single global winner that clears every earlier
    eligible miner by THRESHOLD_MARGIN gets weight 1.0; all other valid miners get 0.0.'
weight_setting:
  cadence: per_epoch
  method: 'Winner-take-all: every CYCLE_LENGTH (150) blocks at offset 15, validator fetches valid miners
    + active validators from owner API, reads recent MAX_EVALS_FOR_SCORING (50) evals from each active
    validator bucket, computes stake-weighted global win_rates, applies eligibility (>40 evals in >=3
    buckets) + threshold-margin (0.02) + deterministic tie-breaks, and sets weight 1.0 on the winner UID
    (0.0 on others). Burns to UID 0 if no eligible winner.'
  on_chain_call: set_weights
  tempo_or_interval: 150 blocks
  smoothing:
    kind: none
    alpha: null
    window: null
    notes: No EMA on the final reward; weights are set fresh each cycle (1.0 winner / 0.0 others, or 1.0
      burn UID). A recency window of the most recent 50 evals (MAX_EVALS_FOR_SCORING) bounds the per-validator
      score history, but this is a fixed-count window, not an EMA/SMA smoother.
anti_gaming:
- kind: plagiarism_detection
  enforcement: elimination
  description: detect_duplicates groups valid miners by model_hash; for each duplicate hash only the earliest
    commit block is kept valid, later miners marked invalid (duplicate_model). The owner base model weights
    hash is pinned (BASE_MODEL_WEIGHTS_HASH) so miners copying the base model are caught.
- kind: stake_weighting
  enforcement: barrier
  description: Each contributing validator's influence on the global score is weighted by sqrt(stake)
    from the current metagraph (validator_weight = sqrt(stake)); zero/unavailable stakes fall back to
    equal weights.
- kind: code_inspection
  enforcement: rejection
  description: check_wrapper_integrity verifies the deployed chute source matches the canonical Vocence
    wrapper; chute name must contain the magic word "vocence" (CHUTE_NAME_MAGIC_WORD). Failing either
    marks the miner invalid.
- kind: rate_limit
  enforcement: elimination
  description: A hotkey making more than MAX_POST_CUTOVER_COMMITS (2) on-chain commits at/after COMMIT_LOCK_BLOCK
    is marked invalid (cap disabled when COMMIT_LOCK_BLOCK=0).
- kind: liveness_check
  enforcement: rejection
  description: A validator only counts as active (and only its bucket is read) if it submitted evaluation
    data within ACTIVE_VALIDATOR_WINDOW_HOURS (24h) and is present in the local VALIDATOR_BUCKETS_JSON.
    Too few active validators -> whole cycle burns.
- kind: deterministic_check
  enforcement: penalty
  description: Pairwise naturalness presentation order randomized to neutralize position bias; trait extractor
    drift coerced to closed enum via alias map; judge temperature pinned to 0.0; ELEMENT_WEIGHTS and PASS_THRESHOLD
    must be identical across honest validators for consensus.
sub_competitions:
  structure: none
  stages: []
  count: null
  notes: Single PromptTTS task (Q1 focus). The owner base model is injected as a synthetic participant
    (uid 0, BASE_MODEL_COMMIT_BLOCK 1000) that miners must beat by THRESHOLD_MARGIN.
per_miner_state:
  tracked: true
  state_kinds:
  - status_enum
  - performance_ledger
  - registration_age
  status_values:
  - valid
  - invalid
  - duplicate_model
  - eligible
  - winner
  reset_policy: Win rates recomputed each cycle from the most recent 50 evals per validator bucket (no
    carryover EMA). Validity recomputed from owner API each cycle.
  notes: Eligibility uses commit block (registration_age proxy) for ordering ("later miners must beat
    earlier baselines") and counts of evals per validator bucket. is_valid/invalid_reason carried per
    participant from the owner API.
